facts: DOE announced a $68 million investment to design, develop, and demonstrate innovative electric vehicle (EV) charging sites near key ports, distribution hubs, and major corridors.
The projects are selected and funded through DOE’s SuperTruck Charge initiative.
The initiative aims to accelerate deployment of large-scale public EV charging infrastructure for medium-and heavy-duty (MHD) EVs to improve grid resiliency and reliability.
DOE’s SuperTruck Program launched in 2009 with four industry participants successfully demonstrating new, efficient technologies for Class 8 tractor trucks.
SuperTruck 2 followed with five industry participants more than doubling Class-8 tractor truck efficiency while significantly improving engine brake thermal efficiency.
SuperTruck 3 projects, scheduled to be completed in 2027, will reduce MHD truck greenhouse gas and air pollution emissions by 75% (on a lifecycle basis) and reduce the total cost of truck ownership.

Jeff Marootian, principal deputy assistant secretary for the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, stated that the SuperTruck program has significantly advanced energy-efficient technologies for freight trucks and the new projects will demonstrate replicable models for cost-effective, high-power charging for electric trucks.
Each SuperTruck Charge project targets large-scale, replicable, high-power charging installations to serve MHD electric fleets with tens to hundreds of vehicles.

The projects will develop and demonstrate high-capacity charging infrastructure to serve MHD electric trucks for long-haul use cases (more than 500 miles per day) along major corridors and rural regions where grid capacity is limited.

Pearl Street Property Co. (Terawatt Infrastructure) in San Francisco, California received $20 million for the 'Supercharging the Southwest' project to demonstrate innovative grid and load management strategies at a charging site along the I-10 corridor in either Goodyear or Tucson, Arizona.

The Supercharging the Southwest project provides 10 pull-through truck charging stalls with megawatt charging systems (MCS) compatible chargers, solar canopies, and 3 megawatts (MW) of battery electric storage systems.

Greenlane Infrastructure, LLC in Santa Monica, California received $26 million for the 'Mega Charging the I-15 Corridor' project to support multiple generations of MHD EV charging on freight corridors.

Greenlane will deploy a publicly accessible 10+ MW MHD EV charging station at Greenlane Center in Barstow, California, featuring distributed energy resource systems including solar arrays and energy storage.
The Mega Charging project site will offer combined charging systems and be futureproofed with scalable MCS direct current fast charging MHD EV chargers.
Utah State University in Logan, Utah received $22 million for the 'SUPERCHARGE' project to establish a reliable, replicable, and scalable charging infrastructure blueprint implementable nationwide.
The SUPERCHARGE project will demonstrate a facility with 9 MW of max concurrent charging capability and 12 MW of installed EV charging equipment while drawing less than 4.5 MW from the grid.
SuperTruck Charge is administered by DOE’s Vehicle Technologies Office.
Connected Communities 2.0 focuses on advancing grid-edge solutions for resilient communities, including demonstrating urban, suburban, and rural-use cases for smart charge management.
The Joint Office of Energy and Transportation’s Communities Taking Charge Accelerator invests more than $43 million in projects to fill gaps in public charging infrastructure.
Selection for award negotiations is not a commitment by DOE to issue an award or provide funding before the negotiation process.
Full funding for these projects is subject to future congressional appropriations.
source_title: DOE Invests $68 Million in Innovative Heavy-Duty Electric Vehicle Charging Solutions | Department of Energy

entity_name: EV Charger Reliability and Accessibility Accelerator Program
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Transportation’s (DOT) Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) EV Charger Reliability and Accessibility Accelerator Program
facts: The U.S. Department of Transportation’s (DOT) Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) EV Charger Reliability and Accessibility Accelerator Program offers funding for the repair and replacement of existing, non-operational publicly accessible Level 2 and direct current (DC) fast chargers.
Funding is available for up to 80% of eligible project costs.
Eligible applicants include State departments of transportation and local governments.
The program references Public Law 117-58.
The jurisdiction for the program is Federal.
The program type is classified as Incentives.
The administering agency is the U.S. Department of Transportation.
Supported technologies include EVs and PHEVs.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger Reliability Grants

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy announced its intent to issue notices of funding opportunities totaling nearly $1 billion to advance and scale mining, processing, and manufacturing technologies across key stages of the critical minerals and materials supply chains.
The proposed funding will accelerate the growth of the U.S. critical minerals and materials sector.
The funding announcements were issued in accordance with President Trump’s Executive Order Unleashing American Energy.

The initiative aims to ensure a more secure, predictable, and affordable supply of critical minerals and materials foundational to American energy dominance, national security, and industrial competitiveness.
U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright stated that the United States has long relied on foreign actors for critical materials essential to modern life and national security.
Secretary Wright noted that the Energy Department will play a leading role in reshoring the processing of critical materials and expanding domestic supply under President Trump’s leadership.
The Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office expects to release a NOFO of up to $50 million early this fall through the Critical Minerals and Materials (CMM) Accelerator program.
The CMM Accelerator promotes technology maturation to unlock capital investments and facilitate domestic commercialization.

The CMM Accelerator NOFO addresses processes in the rare-earth magnet supply chain, refining and alloying gallium, gallium nitride, germanium, and silicon carbide for semiconductors, cost-competitive technologies for direct lithium extraction and separation, and critical-material separation technologies for co-producing useful products from byproducts and scrap.

The Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management is announcing intent to issue a NOFO to support approximately $250 million of financial assistance for American industrial facilities producing valuable mineral byproducts from existing industrial processes.

This program requires piloting technologies at an industrial scale in an industrial facility where material feedstocks can be processed to derisk technical uncertainty and financial risk for commercial deployment.
The proposed NOFO addresses topic areas pertaining to both industry at large and the coal-based industry.
The Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains (MESC) is announcing intent to issue a NOFO of up to $135 million to enhance domestic supply chains for rare earth elements (REEs).

The REE initiative aims to reduce America’s dependence on foreign sources by demonstrating commercial viability of methods for domestically refining and recovering REEs from mine tailings, deleterious material, and waste streams.
An academic partner is required as part of the project team for the REE initiative, and awards require a cost-share of at least 50% by the recipient.
MESC is announcing intent to issue a NOFO of up to $500 million to expand U.S. critical mineral and materials processing and derivative battery manufacturing and recycling.

This funding supports demonstration and/or commercial facilities processing, recycling, or utilizing for manufacturing critical materials including traditional battery minerals such as lithium, graphite, nickel, copper, aluminum, and other minerals contained within commercially available batteries like rare earth elements.
Awards for the battery materials program require a cost-share of at least 50% by the recipient.
The Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) is planning to announce project selections early this fall for its $40 million RECOVER program.

The RECOVER program aims to develop technologies to recover critical minerals from industrial wastewater, enabling the U.S. to reduce dependence on imports and replace them with secure domestic sources.
Significant amounts of critical minerals exist in domestic wastewater systems, untreated and discarded.
RECOVER technologies complement traditional mining operations to access materials and potentially meet a significant portion of America’s needs using supplies that might otherwise go to waste.
source_title: Energy Department Announces Actions to Secure American Critical Minerals and Materials Supply Chain | Department of Energy

facts: U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) recognizes Volvo Group North America for certification to the Superior Energy Performance 50001 (SEP 50001) program.
The certification applies to three U.S. facilities operated by the company.
One facility is the New River Valley plant located in Dublin, Virginia.
A second facility is Mack Trucks Inc. located in Macungie, Pennsylvania.
A third facility is Volvo Powertrain North America located in Hagerstown, Maryland.
source_title: Volvo Group North America - SEP 50001 | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

facts: The Volvo Group is one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, construction equipment and marine and industrial engines.
In the U.S., its subsidiary Volvo Group North America (which includes Mack Trucks) has committed to improving competitiveness through energy efficiency.
The Group operates two remanufacturing plants in the U.S. that prepare engines, transmissions, and other parts for a second life.
The company met its first goal in the Better Plants Challenge in 2014, five years ahead of schedule.
The company set an ambitious new goal of achieving 25% more energy savings by 2024.
Volvo has leveraged the Better Plants program's resources through four in-plant trainings and multiple IAC assessments.
In May of this year, the Volvo Group organized its Energy Network North America Meeting (VENNA) at Oak Ridge National Lab (ORNL).
The VENNA meeting brought energy management staff from 8 Volvo manufacturing facilities and 6 Prevost service centers to ORNL.
The meeting focused on Volvo’s 2017 Better Plants annual energy performance reporting activities, baselining analysis, and plant-level performance tracking.
ORNL staff scientists Sachin Nimbalkar and Thomas Wenning introduced Volvo staff to DOE programs, including 50001 Ready for establishing energy management systems and self-attesting to ISO 50001.
During the meeting, Volvo members shared their energy management status and discussed their 2018 energy efficiency action plan.
Oak Ridge National Lab (ORNL) is the largest science and energy national laboratory in the DOE system by surface area and annual budget.
ORNL is home to the world's most powerful supercomputer and pioneers new energy sources, technologies, materials, and scientific research.
The Volvo Group team toured the Distributed Energy Communications and Controls (DECC) Laboratory and the Building Technologies Research & Integration Center (BTRIC).

At the DECC Lab, ORNL scientists demonstrated testing of dynamic voltage and power factor controls from Distributed Energy resources and expanded capabilities for microgrid control and communications testing.
The BTRIC tour covered multi-zone environmental chambers, building envelope research, ultrasonic drying technology, and advanced refrigerant research.
ORNL provided a tour of the X-10 Graphite Reactor, the world's second artificial nuclear reactor and the first designed for continuous operation.
The Better Plants program operates as a broad partner community committed to sharing energy reduction solutions.
While in Tennessee, ORNL staff facilitated a tour of an Arconic plant (formerly part of ALCOA) for the Volvo team.
Adam Vucelich, Director of Arconic’s Energy Services, presented Arconic’s Energy Intelligence System, which tracks energy performance at both facility and corporate levels.
Other Arconic staff shared environmental, safety, and health best practices with the visiting Volvo team.
Similar energy network meetings are available to all Better Plants members through their Technical Account Managers.
source_title: Volvo Group Heads to ORNL for Energy Network North America | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

canonical_name: Internal Revenue Code Section 30D Clean Vehicle Tax Credit
facts: A clean vehicle tax credit of up to $7,500 is available under Internal Revenue Code Section 30D for new qualified plug-in electric vehicles purchased in 2022 or before.
The credit equals $2,917 for a battery capacity of at least 5 kWh plus $417 for each additional kWh over 5 kWh.
The maximum credit is $7,500 and is nonrefundable, meaning taxpayers cannot receive more back than they owe in taxes or apply excess credits to future tax years.
The credit is available to individuals and businesses purchasing a qualified new car or light truck for their own use primarily in the U.S.
Qualified vehicles must have an external charging source, a gross vehicle weight rating of less than 14,000 pounds, and be made by a manufacturer that has not sold more than 200,000 EVs in the U.S.
Vehicles purchased between August 17, 2022, and December 31, 2022, must undergo final assembly in North America to qualify.
Buyers who entered a written binding contract after December 31, 2021, but before August 16, 2022, may elect to claim the credit under prior rules if delivery occurs on or after August 16, 2022.
A written binding contract is generally enforceable under state law and does not limit damages for breach, often indicated by a significant non-refundable deposit or down payment.
Taxpayers must file Form 8936 with their 2022 tax return to claim the credit for vehicles taken possession of in 2022, including providing the vehicle's VIN.
The credit for qualified 2-wheeled plug-in electric vehicles expired in 2022, though those purchased in 2021 but placed in service during 2022 may still be eligible.
source_title: Credits for new electric vehicles purchased in 2022 or before | Internal Revenue Service

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced $11 million in awards to five U.S. companies to develop and license new or modified transportation packages for high-assay low-enriched uranium (HALEU).
The announcement was made on December 8, 2025, during Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s visit to Idaho National Laboratory (INL) in Idaho Falls, ID.
The visit marked the final stop in Secretary Wright’s ongoing tour of all 17 DOE National Laboratories.
The selections advance President Trump’s executive orders and commitment to rebuild the Nation’s nuclear fuel cycle.
The initiative aims to strengthen domestic enrichment and fabrication capabilities.
The initiative aims to accelerate the deployment of advanced reactors to usher in a new American nuclear renaissance.
Secretary Chris Wright stated: “From critical minerals to nuclear fuel, the Trump administration is fully committed to restoring the supply chains needed to secure America’s future.”

Secretary Chris Wright stated: “Thanks to President Trump, the Energy Department is operating at record speeds to unleash the next American Nuclear Renaissance and to deliver more affordable, reliable, and secure energy for American families and businesses.”
DOE’s $11 million in awards will support industry-led efforts to design, modify, and license transportation packages through the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC).
The investments will help establish long-term, economical HALEU transport capabilities that better serve domestic reactor developers.
The investments aim to strengthen the U.S. nuclear supply chain.

Five companies were selected for Topic Area 1 to develop new package designs that can be licensed by the NRC: NAC International, Westinghouse Electric Company, Container Technologies Industries, LLC, American Centrifuge Operating, and Paragon D&E.
NAC International was also selected for Topic Area 2 to modify existing design packages for NRC certification.
Projects under Topic Area 1 will have performance periods of up to three years.
The Topic Area 2 project will have a performance period of up to two years.
Funding is provided through DOE’s HALEU Availability Program, established under the Energy Act.
The program supports civilian research, development, demonstration, and commercial use of HALEU.

links.context: More information on the HALEU Availability Program

source_title: Energy Department Announces $11 Million in Awards to Develop HALEU Transportation Packages | Department of Energy

facts: The Manufacturing Deployment Office (MDO) serves as the frontline of America's energy manufacturing deployment and supply chain security.
MDO ensures America’s resilient energy future through direct investments in manufacturing capacity and workforce development.
MDO's mission is to enhance economic and national security by eliminating vulnerabilities in the United States' energy supply chains.
MDO catalyzes processing and manufacturing for energy resilience and independence.
MDO reinvigorates the manufacturing workforce through education and training.
MDO guides investment decisions and policy formulation through data-backed supply chain insights.
MDO is currently funding more than 375 projects focused on workforce training and manufacturing expansion.

These funded efforts span batteries, grid components, critical minerals processing and recycling, energy generation, nuclear power, buildings and energy efficiency, transportation and industrial materials.
MDO focuses on Manufacturing Capacity by investing in catalyzing processing and manufacturing capacity in critical junctures of the supply chain to enable resilience and energy independence.
MDO focuses on Workforce Deployment by supporting workforce education and training through programs at universities, community colleges, and trade schools.
MDO focuses on Analysis & Strategic Investment by providing data-driven insights on supply chain vulnerabilities and opportunities to inform investment decisions and the national security mission.
The Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains contact address is 1000 Independence Ave SW, Washington, D.C. 20585.
source_title: Manufacturing Deployment Office (MDO) | Department of Energy

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on June 2, 2026, that Authority Brands will establish its new headquarters, known as the Franchisee Success Center, in Cobb County.
The relocation project will create 390 new jobs.
Authority Brands will invest $13 million over the next several years.
Authority Brands is a leading home services franchisor with 15 brands operating in more than 2,700 territories and served by over 1,000 franchise owners.
The company’s portfolio spans essential residential services including electrical, plumbing, HVAC, pool, house cleaning, lawn care, pest control, in-home caregivers, and junk removal.

Authority Brands and its family of franchise brands earned recognition on the 2025 and 2026 lists of Entrepreneur’s Franchise 500, Franchise Business Review’s 2026 Top Franchises awards, Franchise Times, and Inc. Magazine.
Jay Caiafa serves as the CEO of Authority Brands.
The new 48,000-square-foot Franchisee Success Center will be located at 200 Galleria Parkway in Cobb County.
The majority of the 390 new roles will be concentrated in operations, IT, finance, and marketing.
Sharon Mason is the president and CEO of the Cobb Chamber.
Katie Kirkpatrick is the president and CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber.
Assistant Director of Statewide Projects John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development on this competitive project.
The initiative was completed in partnership with Select Cobb, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, the University System of Georgia, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Authority Brands grew from just six employees to more than 300 in Georgia prior to this expansion.

Authority Brands' portfolio includes 15 home service franchise brands: America’s Swimming Pool Company, Benjamin Franklin Plumbing, The Cleaning Authority, DoodyCalls, DRYmedic Restoration Services, Homewatch CareGivers, The Junkluggers, Lawn Squad, Mister Sparky, Monster Tree Service, Mosquito Squad, One Hour Heating & Air Conditioning, Screenmobile, Service Team of Professionals Restoration, and Woofie’s.
These brands provide home services through more than 2,700 territories operated by more than 1,000 franchise owners.
Interested individuals can learn more about Authority Brands via its official website.
source_title: Authority Brands Relocates HQ, Creates 390 Jobs in Cobb Co. | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that YKK AP America Inc. will invest up to $125 million in an expansion that will include a new manufacturing facility and additional machinery and equipment in Bibb County.
The new facility will create 100 new jobs in addition to the 250 jobs YKK AP currently supports in Macon, Georgia.
YKK AP specializes in architectural products for commercial and residential buildings.
YKK AP entered the U.S. market in 1991 with a vertically integrated, commercial products factory in Dublin, Georgia.
YKK AP maintains its headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia.
YKK AP and its Ontario-based subsidiary Erie Architectural Products employ over 1,200 employees in North America, including nearly 900 within the State of Georgia.

The new facility will expand the company's workforce, introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production, and allow for enhanced efficiencies to manufacture high-quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.

YKK AP President Oliver Stepe stated the investment builds toward a long-term vision to be the leader in the residential window and door market and positions the company to expand its business in and beyond the Southeast region.
The new state-of-the-art facility will be located at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site.
After completion, the company will relocate its operations and employees from its current site on Ocmulgee Boulevard to the new facility.
The Macon facility will become home to the company’s residential division, which manufactures vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners.

The facility will serve as a model plant for future North American manufacturing facilities, featuring new technologies, advanced sustainable manufacturing processes, and enhanced work environments for employees.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in early 2024.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller noted that YKK AP is one of the county's longest-running business partners and highlighted the trust placed in Georgia as a place to do business.

Senior Project Manager Candice Scott represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project in partnership with the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and Georgia Power.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that YKK AP opened its first branch in Atlanta in 1986 and established its U.S. manufacturing presence in middle Georgia five years later, playing a key role in bringing jobs to Georgia.
Companies in Georgia's advanced manufacturing industry created the second most new jobs in the state last year, according to GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Through the GRAD program, Georgia has more than 60 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
All building products are manufactured in world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.

links.context: Press contact for Governor Kemp's office
Deputy press contact for Governor Kemp's office

source_title: YKK AP America Expands Residential Window Production in Bibb County | Georgia

facts: Jim Shorkey Kia Gainesville is a dedicated Kia dealer located at 2359 Browns Bridge Road, Gainesville, GA 30504.
The dealership serves drivers across North Georgia, including neighbors in Cumming, GA, Buford, GA, Flowery Branch, GA, and Oakwood, GA.
The inventory includes new Kia vehicles such as the Telluride SUV, Sportage, Carnival MPV, Seltos, and K5 sedan.
The dealership also offers a wide selection of high-quality used cars and Certified Pre-Owned Kia models.
Jim Shorkey Kia Gainesville provides financing through a network of banks and lenders for new vehicles, hybrids, electric vehicles, and used cars.
The dealership maintains a growing Kia hybrid and electric lineup available for purchase.
A dedicated Kia Service Center employs certified technicians who specialize in genuine Kia auto repair and maintenance using only OEM Kia parts.
Customers can schedule service online or by phone, with options to wait in customer lounges or rent vehicles while their car is serviced.
The dealership offers trade-in and direct sell options for vehicles, including a 'We Come To You' appraisal and pickup service for the local area.
source_title: Jim Shorkey Kia Gainesville | Kia Dealer in Gainesville, GA

facts: Q4 GAAP net income was $18M.
Q4 diluted EPS was $0.22.
Q4 Adjusted diluted EPS was $0.52.
Q4 Adjusted EBITDA was $226M.
Q4 Adjusted EBITDA margin was 6.1%.
For full FY25, Adient generated $204M of free cash flow and returned $125M to shareholders via share repurchases, representing ~7% of shares outstanding at the beginning of the fiscal year.
At September 30, 2025, gross debt totaled ~$2.4B and net debt totaled ~$1.4B.
At September 30, 2025, cash and cash equivalents totaled $958M.
For FY26, Adient expects improved business performance to be offset by lower customer production volumes and increased growth investments.
Adient's president and chief executive officer is Jerome Dorlack.
Adient's executive vice president and chief financial officer is Mark Oswald.
Adient has ~70,000 employees in 29 countries.
Adient operates ~200 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's expertise spans every step of the automotive seat-making process from complete seating systems to individual components.
Adient takes products from research and design to engineering and manufacturing into millions of vehicles every year.

Adient identifies business risks including volatile currency exchange rates, U.S. trade policy and tariffs, automotive production levels and schedules, manufacturer concentration, joint venture risks, supply chain disruptions, wage inflation, restructuring execution, debt service requirements, global tax reform, climate change/sustainability goals, and leadership retention.

Non-GAAP measures tracked include Adjusted EBIT, Adjusted EBITDA, Adjusted net income, Adjusted effective tax rate, Adjusted EPS, Adjusted equity income, Adjusted interest expense, Free cash flow, and Net debt.
Adient's key performance indicator is defined as period-over-period Adjusted EBITDA excluding production volume/mix, equity income, foreign exchange, and net commodity pricing.

links.context: Investor events page for full press release and earnings presentation

source_title: Adient reports solid fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results; provides full-year FY26 outlook

entity_name: Industrial Efficiency and Decarbonization Funding Opportunity Announcement
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Industrial Efficiency and Decarbonization Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA DE-FOA-0002804)
facts: On June 15, 2023, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced $135 million in funding for 40 projects to reduce the carbon footprint of the industrial sector and move the U.S. toward a net-zero emissions economy by 2050.
The selected projects are primarily funded through DOE’s Industrial Technologies Office (ITO).
The projects will support research, development, and pilot-scale demonstrations to reduce energy usage and emissions from high-emitting industrial subsectors.
Award and cost share amounts are subject to change pending negotiations.

Topic 1: Decarbonizing Chemicals includes 9 projects with an award amount of $38.3 million, focusing on unit operations like advanced separations, advanced reactors, alternative production, and process heating technologies.

Topic 2: Decarbonizing Iron and Steel includes 10 projects with an award amount of $31.9 million, collaboratively funded with the Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office, focusing on decarbonization in ore-based or scrap-based iron and steelmaking operations and converting ancillary/thermal processes to clean fuels or electricity.

Topic 3: Decarbonizing Food and Beverage Products includes 3 projects with an award amount of $11.4 million, focusing on innovative technologies for process heating operations within the food and beverage sector.

Topic 4: Decarbonizing Cement and Concrete includes 5 projects with an award amount of $16.4 million, focusing on next-generation cement formulations, process routes, and carbon capture/utilization technologies to address process-related and energy-related CO2 emissions.

Topic 5: Decarbonizing Paper and Forest Products includes 6 projects with an award amount of $16.2 million, focusing on increasing renewable energy share, electrification, carbon capture, energy efficiency, novel paper/wood drying technologies, and innovative pulping/paper forming technologies.

Topic 6: Cross-sector Decarbonization Technologies includes 7 projects with an award amount of $20.4 million, focusing on innovations like industrial heat pump technologies and power generation from low temperature waste heat.
The page directs users to sign up for the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) email list and newsletter to stay current with funding opportunities and ITO news.
source_title: Funding Selections: Industrial Efficiency and Decarbonization FOA | Department of Energy

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy's Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation selected 16 projects that will enhance the capability of the U.S. National Laboratories to develop technologies that improve the efficiency and competitiveness of American industry.
Funding amount is $155 million.
Expanded lab capabilities will drive process and technological innovation, reduce costs, and increase prosperity for American workers and consumers.
Selected projects address critical industrial challenges in energy-intensive industries such as iron and steel, cement, chemicals, forest products, and food and beverage.
Selected projects address cross-sector technologies including industrial process heating, membrane separations, load flexibility, and advanced computing.

Data Center Cooling Collaborative is a Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory project that will form a data center cooling testbed network to meet varied cooling technology needs and reduce the time and cost to bring new technologies to market.

Burner Laboratories to Advance Fuel UtiliZation for Thermal Energy (BLAZE) involves the National Energy Technology Laboratory and partners establishing industry-accessible testbed facilities to accelerate development of innovative industrial combustion systems.

Center for Industrial Modeling and Simulation involves Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory and partners bringing together lab modeling and simulation experts with industry and academia stakeholders to accelerate adoption of high-performance computing tools to improve industrial efficiency.

Idaho National Laboratory will develop a National Library for Iron Ore and Scrap resource center to deliver characterized lab-and pilot-scale samples of various iron ore and steel scrap material for cutting-edge innovations.

Assistant Secretary of Energy (EERE) Audrey Robertson stated that the projects will help National Laboratories advance cutting-edge technologies that make American industry the global standard in excellence and innovation.
source_title: Energy Department Announces $155 Million to Advance American Industrial Innovation | Department of Energy

facts: The page provides approximate light-duty vehicle registration counts derived by the National Laboratory of the Rockies with data from Experian Information Solutions.
Counts are rounded to the closest 100 vehicles and reflect the total number of light-duty registered vehicles through the selected year (2023).
Fuel types are based on vehicle identification numbers (VINs), which do not reflect aftermarket conversions to use different fuels or power sources.
Georgia had approximately 92,400 registered electric vehicles (EVs) in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 22,700 registered plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 175,300 registered hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 92,500 registered biodiesel vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 719,400 registered ethanol/flex (E85) vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 300 registered compressed natural gas (CNG) vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 200 registered propane vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had zero registered hydrogen and methanol vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 8,256,400 registered gasoline vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 206,300 registered diesel vehicles in 2023.
Georgia had approximately 76,900 registered vehicles with unknown fuel types in 2023.
For a map comparing the density of light-duty vehicles per state and charts comparing registration counts over time, users can refer to TransAtlas.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Vehicle Registration Counts by State

facts: Clean Cities and Communities (CC&C) is a U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) partnership that fosters collaboration and innovation to advance transportation energy choices nationwide.
More than 75 DOE-designated CC&C coalitions work in urban, suburban, and rural areas to deliver objective technical expertise based on a unique understanding of local markets.

As partners with DOE’s Transportation Technologies Office, coalitions build bridges between national priorities and local needs to create transportation energy systems that are affordable, reliable, and secure.
Coalition activities create compounding impacts nationwide that support locally driven energy choices and benefit regional economic development and job growth.
The report summarizes the success and impact of partnership activities based on data and information provided in their annual reports.
Report authors include Singer, M.; Johnson, C.; Wilson, A.; Reichelt, L.; Abdullah, M.
Report publication date is 6/2/2026.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Publications

facts: Economic information is of interest to job seekers, students, workforce planners, employers, researchers, and educators.
The provided data offers a snapshot of Georgia’s economy.

Georgia economic indicators include employment by industry, weekly manufacturing earnings, unemployment insurance data, new business starts, motor vehicle sales, construction permits, and state revenues.
Local Employment Dynamics (LED) and Quarterly Workforce Indicators (QWI) provide new, current, and local information on earnings and employment dynamics.
Interactive mapping is available to show where people work and where workers live.
Industry Focus indicators provide new, current, and local information on industries.
Detailed industry information includes the top industries for a local area and its workers.
Industry Focus data shows how a particular industry ranks among top industries.
Industry Focus data provides characteristics of workers in a specific industry.
source_title: Obtain Economic Information | Georgia Department of Labor

facts: The New Clean Vehicle Credit, Previously-Owned Clean Vehicle Credit, and Qualified Commercial Clean Vehicle Credit are not available for vehicles acquired after Sept. 30, 2025.
A vehicle must be placed in service to claim the credit.
If a vehicle is placed in service after Sept. 30, 2025, it must have been acquired on or before Sept. 30, 2025 to remain eligible for the credit.
Acquisition eligibility can be demonstrated by entering into a binding written contract and making a payment on the vehicle on or before Sept. 30, 2025.
A vehicle is considered placed in service when the buyer takes possession of it.
Sellers must provide buyers with information about an eligible new or previously-owned clean vehicle's qualifications upon taking possession.
Sellers are required to register online and report vehicle qualification information to the IRS, or the vehicle will be ineligible for the credit.
The page outlines changes to tax credits under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill.
The New Clean Vehicle Credit under section 30D is available only for vehicles acquired on or before Sept. 30, 2025.
Details regarding the credit for new vehicles were updated on Jan. 1, 2023.
The Previously-Owned Clean Vehicle Credit applies exclusively to vehicles acquired on or before Sept. 30, 2025.
The Previously-Owned Clean Vehicle Credit is available only to individuals.
Installing an electric vehicle charger at home can simplify charging, increase convenience, and potentially raise property value.
A tax credit is available to reduce the costs of EV charging equipment and its installation.
Qualified vehicle refueling and recharging property installed at a home and placed in service before July 1, 2026, may qualify for the Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Tax Credit.
The Treasury Department and IRS issued FAQs addressing the accelerated termination of several energy provisions under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill (OBBB).
Additional FAQs were published regarding modifications to sections 25C, 25D, 25E, 30C, 30D, 45L, 45W, and 179D under Public Law 119-21, 139 Stat. 72 (July 4, 2025).
source_title: Clean vehicle tax credits | Internal Revenue Service

facts: Constellium N.V. announced the grand opening of its new plant in White, Georgia, on May 11, 2017.
The facility is dedicated to the production of advanced aluminium automotive structural components and crash management systems.
The plant occupies 135,000 sq. ft. of space.
The facility is strategically located to supply automakers in the Southeast U.S.
The plant may be expanded to 220,000 sq. ft. in the future to meet customers' supply needs.
Constellium expects to have 150 employees at the White, Georgia location by 2019.

Paul Warton, President of Constellium's Automotive Structures & Industry business unit, stated that being closer to customers' assembly plants will allow the company to better serve automakers in making vehicles lighter and safer.
Paul Warton noted the plant responds to the industry's growing demand for aluminium structural parts.

Eric Krepps, Vice President and General Manager of Constellium Automotive Structures North America, thanked the State of Georgia, Bartow County, local partners, and employees for their strong support in establishing the White, Georgia plant.
Aluminium is a material of choice in the automotive industry to reduce vehicle weight, improve fuel economy, lower emissions, and increase safety.
Constellium develops innovative, value-added aluminium products for aerospace, automotive, and packaging markets.
Constellium generated €4.7 billion of revenue in 2016.

The press release notes potential risks related to achieving expected synergies with Wise Metals and slower or lower than expected growth in the North American market for Body-in-White aluminium rolled products.
source_title: Constellium celebrates the grand opening of its new plant for advanced aluminium automotive structures in White, Georgia

facts: Liberty Tire Recycling is committed to partnering with industry leaders to help inspire a more sustainable planet.
The company strives to find new and better ways to reclaim, recycle, reuse, and repurpose the more than 190 million end-of-life tires it collects.
Liberty Tire Recycling participates in the Better Plants Challenge under the Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative.
The partner profile page was published on 10/14/2022.
source_title: Liberty Tire Recycling, LLC | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy’s Alternative Fuels and Feedstocks Office (AFFO)
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Alternative Fuels and Feedstocks Office (AFFO) enables research, development, and demonstration (RD&D) to accelerate technology commercialization for the production, storage, delivery and use of alternative fuels, fertilizers, chemicals, and feedstocks.
AFFO aims to enhance the security and reliability of energy networks and lower energy costs for Americans.

Through collaboration with National Laboratories, academia, and industry, AFFO advances energy independence by establishing a secure domestic supply chain, lowering production costs, invigorating a strong U.S. workforce, and fostering energy growth through diverse, domestic production pathways.
As part of the Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation, AFFO integrates legacy efforts in the Bioenergy Technologies Office and Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office under one office.
AFFO focuses on cost-effective conversion of feedstocks to affordable chemicals and strategic fuels.
AFFO focuses on fuel-flexible fuel cells to provide prime and backup power for U.S. data centers and other growing electrical loads.
AFFO focuses on production of performance-enhanced chemicals to advance U.S. manufacturing dominance.
AFFO focuses on reduction of imported fertilizer by providing cost-effective domestic alternatives from hydrogen and biomass.
AFFO focuses on leveraging growing use of artificial intelligence to spur innovation, advance technology readiness levels, and enable commercialization.

The Analysis, Codes, and Standards program conducts analysis to inform direction, prioritization, and decision-making, conducts R&D to develop technologies that enable the safe use of alternative fuels and feedstocks, and conducts underlying research to inform common-sense regulations, codes, standards, and workforce training.

The Feedstocks Technologies R&D program develops reliable and cost-effective technologies for producing, harvesting, storing, processing, transporting, and mobilizing abundant domestic biomass and waste feedstocks for conversion into alternative fuels, fertilizers, hydrogen, performance-enhanced chemicals, and high-value bioproducts that will advance national security and energy dominance.

The Fuel Cell Technologies program develops fuel cells that are fuel-flexible and cost-competitive with incumbent and emerging technologies for use as stationary power (e.g. for data centers) and other applications.
These efforts support data center expansion, nuclear-hydrogen synergies, and alternatives to critical minerals used in fuel cell technologies.
The Infrastructure R&D program advances infrastructure technologies to reduce the cost of transporting, storing, and dispensing alternative fuels and feedstocks.
Cost-effective bulk hydrogen storage and transport can support efforts such as improving data center reliability and enhancing nuclear plant efficiency.

The Production Technologies program reduces costs and enables expanded, resilient options for fuels, chemicals, and hydrogen using a variety of energy resources, feedstocks, and conversion technologies.
This work includes R&D aimed at adding to domestic primary energy sources and enabling the conversion of nuclear and other energy resources to valuable chemicals.

It also supports improving supply chain and production efficiency to support U.S. energy and manufacturing, as well as boosting American-made fertilizer production by enabling more ways to improve ammonia.
The Systems Development and Integration program integrates and validates technologies through first-of-a-kind demonstrations.
This includes integration of technologies for the production, transportation, and end-uses of alternative fuels, fertilizers, chemicals, and feedstocks at various scales.
This work reduces technical and operational uncertainty, mitigates risk, and enables industry-led scale-up and commercialization.

The Multi-Year Program Plan (MYPP), published in May 2024, set forth the Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office's mission, goals, and strategic approach relative to broader clean energy priorities of the U.S. Department of Energy.

The Regional Biomass Resource Hub Initiative (RBRH) is a new initiative from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) designed to accelerate the mobilization of purpose-grown energy crops to create clean fuels and products.
AFFO 2027 Peer Review Meeting starts April 19th, 2027 at 9:00 AM EDT.
source_title: Alternative Fuels and Feedstocks Office | Department of Energy

facts: The Build America, Buy America Act was enacted as part of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act on November 15, 2021.
It established a domestic content procurement preference for all federal financial assistance obligated for infrastructure projects after May 14, 2022.
The Act was enacted under Division G, Title IX of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA), Public Law 117–58.

BABA established a domestic content procurement preference requirement to increase a resilient domestic supply chain and manufacturing supply for critical materials for emerging and existing industries in the United States.
The requirement applies to three product categories purchased under financial assistance awards: iron or steel products, manufactured products, and construction materials.
The IIJA supports maximizing use of domestically produced goods consistent with Executive Order 14005.

Per Section 70914 of the IIJA, an agency may not obligate funds for an infrastructure project unless all iron, steel, manufactured products, and construction materials are produced in the United States or a waiver is applied.
On April 18, 2022, OMB issued Memorandum M-22-11 providing initial implementation guidance on applying the BABA Requirement to federal financial assistance programs.
OMB amended 2 CFR by adding a new part 184 to assist with BABA implementation.
On August 23, 2023, OMB finalized 2 CFR 184 as the primary guidance for agencies implementing BABA for federally funded financial assistance.
The guidance in part 184 aims to improve consistency in implementing BABA requirements across the federal government.
OMB also amended 2 CFR 200.322 to clarify existing domestic preference provisions within part 200.
On October 25, 2023, OMB rescinded Memorandum M-22-11 and issued updated guidance via OMB Memorandum M-24-02.
Memo M-24-02 replaces Memo M-22-11 to remove direct conflicts between it and 2 CFR part 184.
Memo M-24-02 summarizes aspects of 2 CFR part 184 and provides supplemental guidance for infrastructure projects subject to BABA requirements.
DOE may issue further guidance to recipients and stakeholders on DOE financial assistance programs.
OMB may issue additional guidance in the future based on stakeholder feedback.
The Department of Energy will continue to work with OMB’s Made in America Office to coordinate implementation and compliance of Buy America Requirements.
All DOE FOAs issued on or after May 14, 2022, must contain the Buy America Requirement for Infrastructure Projects provision.

The requirement applies to all new and renewal DOE Financial Assistance awards and any funding modifications made on or after May 14, 2022, to existing awards containing public infrastructure projects.

Any funding modification (continuation award, supplemental award, or other incremental/additional funding) made on or after May 14, 2022, to an existing award must incorporate the Buy America Requirement term and condition.
When included in an award, the Buy America Requirement flows down to all sub-awardees regardless of entity type, including For-Profit Entities.
The requirement applies to Non-Federal Entity prime recipients that are States, Local Governments, Indian Tribes, Territories, Institutions of Higher Education (IHEs), and Non-Profit Organizations.
For-Profit Entities are not included in the definition of Non-Federal Entity when applying the statutory Buy America Requirement to prime recipients.
Tribal Consultation is required prior to applying the requirement to Indian Tribe prime recipients.

The requirement applies to a For-Profit Entity if it is a sub-recipient or sub-awardee under an award containing the requirement, or if it voluntarily chooses to use domestically sourced materials by stating so in its application.
If a For-Profit Entity states compliance in its application and is selected, its award will contain the Buy America Requirement term and condition.

The prime recipient is responsible for flowing the requirement down to all sub-awards, contracts, subcontracts, and purchase orders for work under the project, including to For-Profit Entities when they are sub-recipients or sub-awardees.
DOE prime recipients must ensure that absent a waiver, none of the funds (federal share or recipient cost-share) may be used for infrastructure unless specific domestic content criteria are met.
All iron and steel used must be produced in the United States, meaning all manufacturing processes from initial melting through coating application occurred in the U.S.

All manufactured products must be produced in the United States, with the cost of components mined/produced/manufactured in the U.S. greater than 55 percent of the total component cost (unless another standard applies).
Component costs for manufactured products are determined in accordance with 2 CFR 184.5.
All construction materials must be manufactured in the United States, meaning all manufacturing processes occurred in the U.S.
The requirement only applies to articles, materials, and supplies consumed in, incorporated into, or permanently affixed to an infrastructure project.
It does not apply to tools, equipment, and supplies like temporary scaffolding brought to and removed from the construction site.
It does not apply to equipment and furnishings like movable chairs, desks, and portable computer equipment used at the finished project but not integral to the structure.
The requirement must flow down to all sub-awards, contracts, subcontracts, and purchase orders for work under the infrastructure project.
Prime recipients must maintain certifications or equivalent documentation proving compliance that covered articles, materials, and supplies are produced in the United States.
Certifications must be provided by suppliers or manufacturers and flow up from sub-awardees, contractors, and vendors to the prime recipient.
Prime recipients must keep these certifications with award/project files and produce them upon request from DOE, auditors, or the Office of Inspector General.
Recipients should contact the cognizant DOE Contracting or Grants Officer for questions about applying the Buy America Requirement in an award.
Prime recipients may apply for and DOE may grant project-specific waivers from the Buy America Requirement when necessary.
Waiver requests must be submitted in writing by the prime recipient to DOE per award terms and conditions.
DOE may request additional information, which the recipient must provide.
Waiver requests are subject to review by DOE and OMB, plus a public comment period of no less than 15 calendar days.
Recipient waiver requests will be made publicly available on DOE’s and OMB’s websites.
DOE may reject or grant waivers in whole or part based on review, analysis, and feedback.
DOE’s final determination on approval or rejection cannot be appealed.
Waiver requests may take up to 90 calendar days to process.
Recipients should contact the cognizant DOE Contracting or Grants Officer for questions about requesting waivers.
The Act requires all proposed waivers to be publicly available on DOE’s and OMB’s websites for comment for at least 15 calendar days before issuance.
Requests to modify or renew a DOE General Applicability waiver require a 30-calendar day public comment period.
Only comments received prior to the closing date will be reviewed.
DOE posts all approved waivers on its Buy America Requirement Waiver page.
source_title: Build America, Buy America | Department of Energy

entity_name: DOE Bipartisan Infrastructure Law Battery Materials Processing and Manufacturing Funding Opportunity Announcement
canonical_name: Bipartisan Infrastructure Law - Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing Funding Opportunity Announcement
facts: The Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains (MESC) and Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) are issuing Funding Opportunity Announcement No. DE-FOA-0002678.
The FOA is entitled Bipartisan Infrastructure Law - Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing Funding Opportunity Announcement.
The FOA supports the Bipartisan Infrastructure Legislation goals of delivering more clean energy, creating new good-paying jobs, and lowering costs for American families and workers.
The initiative aims to guide the nation towards a one hundred percent carbon pollution-free electricity sector by 2035 and a net-zero economy by 2050.
This amendment changes the Letter of Intent requirement from mandatory to strongly encouraged.
The amendment clarifies the content and process for a Letter of Intent.
The amendment clarifies the requirements for Areas of Interest (AOIs) 3, 4, 5, and 10.
The amendment corrects language in Criterion 2a, Section V.A.
Reviewer comments were emailed to the Business Point of Contact and Technical Point of Contact on August 24, 2022.
Replies to reviewer comments are only accepted in Exchange from August 24, 2022, at 5:00 PM ET until September 1, 2022, at 5:00 PM ET.
The deadline for submitting replies to reviewer comments will not be extended and cannot be submitted through any other methods.
Providing a reply to reviewer comments is not a mandatory requirement.
Submissions must conform to content and form requirements, including maximum page lengths described in Section IV.E of the FOA.
source_title: DOE: CMEI eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy Hydrogen Program
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Hydrogen Program
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy Hydrogen Program is led by the Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office (HFTO) within the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE).

The program conducts research and development in hydrogen production, delivery, infrastructure, storage, fuel cells, and multiple end uses across transportation, industrial, and stationary power applications.
The program includes activities in technology validation, manufacturing, analysis, systems development and integration, safety, codes and standards, education, and workforce development.
On May 22, 2025, the Energy Department announced updates to remove barriers for American energy producers and unleash investment in domestic hydrogen.
On April 16, 2025, an H2IQ Hour webinar was held on geologic hydrogen, covering its energy potential and current engineering efforts to bring it from underground to the surface.
On December 20, 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy unveiled an updated Hydrogen Program Plan that incorporates new input from multiple DOE offices as a strategic framework for hydrogen innovation.
source_title: Program Records | Hydrogen Program

entity_name: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC
canonical_name: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC will invest $9 million in expanding their seating manufacturing operations in West Point, Georgia.
The expansion will create more than 150 jobs in Troup County.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System manufactures seats and seat foam pads for the Hyundai Santa Fe, KIA Sorento, and other models.
In addition to providing seating structures for automobile manufacturers, the company also supplies seats to leading electric vehicle makers.
This expansion will take Hyundai TRANSYS’ employment total in Georgia to more than 1,890 jobs.
The company is continuing to hire for careers in production seat assembly, foam, quality, and logistics.

Senior Project Manager Amanda Fields represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on this competitive project in partnership with the West Point Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Hyundai TRANSYS is an automotive parts company that produces critical automobile parts, including transmissions, axles, and seats.
The company is the only company in the world specializing in transmissions.
Hyundai TRANSYS produces all types of automatic transmissions, manual transmissions, dual clutch transmissions (DCT), and continuously variable transmissions.

Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities and remains a national leader in advanced manufacturing, outpacing the United States in 10-year GDP growth in the manufacture of products including machinery, electrical equipment and components, and fabricated metals.
West Point Development Authority Chairman Kevin Patrick stated that the company's automotive and existing industries are the backbone of their economic success.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Hyundai TRANSYS contributes more than $3 billion to Georgia’s economy.
source_title: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC Expands Operations | Georgia

entity_name: Hydrogen from Next-generation Electrolyzers of Water (H2NEW)
canonical_name: Hydrogen from Next-generation Electrolyzers of Water (H2NEW)
facts: H2NEW is a consortium of nine U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) national laboratories focused on making large-scale electrolyzers more durable, efficient, and affordable.

The consortium will address components, materials integration, and manufacturing R&D to overcome technical barriers and enable manufacturable electrolyzers that meet required cost, durability, and performance targets simultaneously.
H2NEW aims to enable $2/kg hydrogen by 2026.
The consortium conducts research activities in low-temperature electrolysis (LTE) and high-temperature electrolysis (HTE).
source_title: Home | Hydrogen from Next-generation Electrolyzers of Water (H2NEW)

Four companies are expanding in Right to Work Georgia. These include Purple Innovation and ASOS as well as InFlex, and Woodgrain.
Three companies that are locating to Right to Work Georgia include Freshly, Semperit Group, and Plug Power. These will create 759 new jobs!
Three businesses bringing new location to Right to Work Georgia include Brightmark, Duckyang, and IC Biomedical. These will create new jobs all over the state!
FanDuel Group, Domtar Corporation, Heliox, and Wittek Industries are all investing in Right to Work Georgia, creating a total of 1,095 new jobs!

facts: Duckyang, a South Korean-based company, will open its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The company is an electric parts supplier of automotive battery modules.
Governor Brian Kemp announced that Duckyang will invest $10 million to open the manufacturing plant in Jackson County.
The investment is expected to create an estimated 285 jobs.
The new facility will be 230,000 square feet.
The facility will be located in Braselton at 984 Broadway Avenue.
Duckyang is looking to hire for production, maintenance, logistics, and warehouse management positions.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the project creates high-paying, advanced automotive manufacturing jobs for Georgians in Jackson County.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia has the best workforce training programs in the nation and has remained the number one state for business for the past eight years.
Duckyang supplies cockpit modules to customers worldwide, including Hyundai, Kia, Renault Samsung Motors, Genesis, and Mobis.
Cockpit modules are auto parts made of more than 130 subcomponents found near the driver’s seat of a vehicle.
The new Georgia facility will allow Duckyang to better serve automotive customers within the region.

Dong-in Son, CEO of Duckyang Ind. Co. Ltd., stated that by entering the U.S. electric vehicle battery market with SKBA, the company will cooperate with other automotive companies like Hyundai and Kia Motors that already have a U.S. manufacturing base.

Dong-in Son expected business expansion opportunities to supply electric vehicle parts and interior parts, including cockpit modules, door trim panel modules, and other flagship parts, to global automakers based in the United States.
source_title: S. Korean-based company to open first-of-its kind manufacturing plant - LocalNews8.com - KIFI

facts: The Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) held its Grand Opening on March 26.
At the time of the article, the project was described as the largest economic development project in the history of Georgia.
Land clearing for the facility began in 2022 along Interstate-16.
HMGMA refers to its employees as 'Meta Pros.'
Employee Julisa Donahue, previously a Trane technician for a Georgia HVAC company, has been employed at the plant since September 2023 and commutes daily from Reidsville.
The facility employs over 1,200 Meta Pros.
South Korean natives onsite provide training for the robotics and assembly line systems.
Chief Administrative Officer Brent Stubbs discussed Hyundai's cultural training elements in an interview in January.
Last year, the company sent over 250 of its Meta Pros to Korea for a culture exchange experience to learn about the history of Hyundai and Korea.
The workforce includes homegrown Georgia southerners, employees newly arrived from the West Coast, and 56 South Korean expatriates employed by HMGMA.
HMGMA provides free Korean cultural education and language classes on site for interested employees.
The company teaches English as a Second Language (ESL) on site to foster global literacy among workers.
The plant produces Hyundai Ioniq 5 models.
Employee Charlene Toole, previously a dispatcher at a local HVAC company, joined the plant in October 2023 as employee number 300.
All Meta Pros know their hire number, which serves as a point of pride for being part of the facility's foundation team.
The facility utilizes Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMR) and Automated Guided Vehicles (AGV) to transport car parts and assist in manufacturing.
Boston Dynamics Spots are used by robots to scan vehicles as part of the quality control process.
The plant has thousands of robots, including over 450 welding robots that will increase to almost 1,000 with the second line.
There are over 200 AMRs at the facility, compared to under 50 in other assembly plants.
The plant was designed with humans in mind, featuring skylights and lamps that emulate natural light for worker benefit.
AMRs and AGVs handle repetitive or intensive tasks that could strain or endanger human workers.
Break areas called team centers are stationed throughout the plant just off the assembly line.
Meta Pros can order lunches through an app for delivery to the team centers.
HMGMA intends to build a second battery plant as part of a joint venture with LG Solutions.
The company plans to expand its own production by 200,000 units per year.
The timeline for increased production is unclear and depends on fully staffing the first shift and bringing on a second full shift.
Hyundai Motor Company President and CEO José Muñoz stated that production expansion would happen 'the sooner the better' but did not commit to a specific timeline.
The need for a second battery plant and added hybrid vehicle production is expected to lead to 5,500 new jobs on site.
HMGMA Assistant Manager of Communications and Public Relations Bianca Johnson stated that how additional jobs are distributed among HMGMA and onsite affiliates remains to be seen.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Executive Director of Communications Marie Gordon stated via email on Tuesday that there has been no request to further amend the economic development agreement.
source_title: More than meets the eye awaits Hyundai Meta Pros at its Savannah region metaplant

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) provides support for businesses across the state through its online resources, assistance with regulatory requirements, tax-incentives for business development, and connections to industry experts.
The Center of Innovation is a division of GDEcD that serves as a strategic resource hub to help businesses across the state accelerate growth through innovation.
The Center of Innovation provides connections and resources in aerospace, AgTech, IT, logistics, and manufacturing.
GDEcD maintains an Innovation and Entrepreneurship Support Network that lists entrepreneurship hubs it works with across Georgia.
A statewide hub is Georgia Power, with contacts Elyse Davis, Scott Purvis, and Vanessa Wagner.
