facts: Korean company Enchem partnered with Carroll Daniel to build its first U.S. location.
Enchem is a Korean technology leader specializing in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes for rechargeable batteries and electrostatic double-layer capacitors (ELDCs).
The facility occupies an existing 283-acre complex that includes six buildings.
Construction on the campus encompassed renovations to the existing 87,500-square-foot Main Accessory Building.
Construction included building a new three-story process tower.
Construction included installing a NMP tower containment dike and a tank farm containment dike.
U.S. codes and standards differed from Korean codes and standards, which was identified as a proposed threat to design, schedule, and overall project success.

The Carroll Daniel team traveled to Poland and Korea in February 2020 to visit two of Enchem’s facilities to understand requirements and how U.S. codes would affect construction, operation, and production.
The project was delivered as an award-winning design-build project with a smooth process for the client.
source_title: Enchem America

facts: Enchem America Inc. officials announced the company will invest $152.5 million to establish electrolyte manufacturing operations in Haywood County, Tennessee.
The Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development (TNECD) categorizes Haywood County as an “economically distressed” county.
Brownsville, Tennessee will be the site of Enchem America Inc.'s second U.S. facility.
The new facility will be located at the I-40 Advantage Industrial Park in Brownsville.
The electrolyte manufacturer is expected to create 190 new jobs and invest $150M+ in Haywood County.
Enchem America Inc. is a subsidiary of South Korea-based Enchem Co., Ltd.
According to TNECD Commissioner Stuart McWhorter, South Korea is one of Tennessee’s top 10 foreign direct investment partners.
The new facility will manufacture electrolytes for electric vehicle (EV) batteries as well as provide other EV battery-related services.
Jung Kang Oh, CEO and founder of Enchem Co. Ltd., expressed gratitude to Haywood County, the City of Brownsville, and professionals who assisted with the expansion.
Jung Kang Oh noted that the growth of the EV market has been remarkable and expects it to continue at an accelerated pace over the coming decade.

Jung Kang Oh stated confidence that the company is well positioned to capitalize on EV market growth and looks forward to exploring innovation and partnership opportunities with Haywood County and the City of Brownsville.
TNECD categorizes Haywood County as a Tier-4, At-Risk, economically distressed county, which is one ranking away from being classified as a distressed county (the worst rating).
Data by TNECD shows Haywood County’s poverty rate is 20.7 percent.
The three-year average unemployment rate in Haywood County stands at 6.4 percent.
Haywood County’s per capita market income is $25,529.

links.context: TNECD tweet link regarding the investment and job creation announcement

source_title: Enchem America Inc. Announces $152.5 Million Investment Project in Tennessee - Tennessee Star

facts: Tennessee Gov. Bill Lee, Department of Economic and Community Development Commissioner Stuart McWhorter and Enchem America Inc. officials announced the company will invest $152.5 million to establish electrolyte manufacturing operations in Brownsville, Tennessee.
The new facility is located at the I-40 Advantage Industrial Park, a Select Tennessee Certified Site.
The Brownsville facility will be Enchem’s second U.S. plant.
The project will create 190 new jobs in Tier 4, At-Risk Haywood County.
The Brownsville facility will operate similarly to Enchem’s U.S. headquarters in Commerce, Georgia.
The facility will manufacture electrolytes for electric vehicle (EV) batteries as well as other EV battery-related services.
Enchem America Inc. is a subsidiary of Enchem Co., Ltd., which is based in South Korea.
The company specializes in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes and other additives for rechargeable batteries.
With the additional Tennessee operations, Enchem will be able to further support its EV partners across the Southeast.
Since 2019, TNECD has supported more than 50 economic development projects in the Southwest Tennessee region, resulting in nearly 9,300 job commitments and $7.2 billion in capital investment.

Gov. Bill Lee stated that Enchem’s decision to invest more than $150 million is a testament to Tennessee’s unmatched business climate, highly skilled workforce and position as a leader in the automotive industry.

TNECD Commissioner Stuart McWhorter noted that South Korea is one of Tennessee’s top 10 foreign direct investment partners and that Enchem is boosting the EV supply chain across Tennessee by investing in an At-Risk county.
Enchem Co. Ltd. CEO and founder Jung Kang Oh thanked Haywood County, the City of Brownsville and all professionals that assisted Enchem in expanding its presence in North America.
Jung Kang Oh stated that the growth of the EV market in recent years has been truly remarkable and indications suggest the trend will continue at an accelerated pace over the coming decade.

Jung Kang Oh expressed confidence that Enchem is well positioned to further capitalize on the growth of the EV market and looks forward to exploring new avenues of innovation and partnership opportunities with Haywood County and the City of Brownsville.
Haywood County Mayor David Livingston stated that Enchem’s economic opportunity will help transform Haywood County and all West Tennessee with great, new high paying jobs.

Brownsville Mayor Bill Rawls said the announcement brings to fruition years of work and investment in I-40 Advantage Industrial Park fulfilling the goal of bringing high quality job opportunities to the city.
Bill Rawls stated that Brownsville looks forward to working with Enchem to continue to build the advanced manufacturing workforce of the future right here in Brownsville, Tennessee.

TVA senior vice president of economic development John Bradley stated that TVA and Brownsville Energy Authority are proud to partner with HTL Advantage, Brownsville-Haywood County Industrial Development Corporation, City of Brownsville and TNECD to support companies creating jobs and investment in the region.

Sen. Page Walley (R-Savannah) stated that Enchem’s investment will bring many jobs, boost the regional economy, and further establish Haywood County as a hub of innovation at the forefront of this rapidly growing industry.

Rep. Johnny Shaw (D-Bolivar) stated that BlueOval City lit the fuse for an economic explosion in Brownsville, Haywood County and West Tennessee, citing the more than $152 million investment as an example of unprecedented growth.
source_title: TN: Enchem America Inc. to Locate U.S. Manufacturing Operations in Brownsville, Creating 190 Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Enchem announced on June 30 that it is experiencing clear revenue growth in the second quarter of this year, fueled by a sharp increase in electrolyte shipments primarily to major North American customers.
The company expects its operating loss to narrow, aided by cost efficiency measures and tariff refund effects.
Electrolyte supply volume is increasing in line with production expansion at major North American customers.

Particularly in the second quarter, shipments to North American clients are driving revenue growth, resulting in a significant expansion of the company's top line compared to the same period last year.
The volume scheduled for supply in June has already been produced, and most shipments have been completed according to customer delivery schedules.
Second-quarter revenue is expected to increase substantially compared to the same period last year.
Specific revenue and profit figures will be finalized after final shipments, customer inspections, accounting closing procedures, and reviews by external auditors.
The revenue expansion is based on an actual increase in electrolyte supply volume, rather than merely reflecting a market price increase driven by rising lithium salt prices.

Electrolyte shipments are growing as the production expansion of North American customers aligns with the rising utilization rate at Enchem's local production base, its Georgia plant in the U.S., which is gradually easing the fixed cost burden.

The company anticipates that its operating loss will narrow compared to the previous quarter, reflecting the fixed cost dilution effect from higher revenue, alongside efficiencies in production, purchasing, and logistics costs, strengthened inventory and working capital management, and tariff refund effects related to its North American business.

Enchem is continuously reviewing its cost structure and management resource operations, focusing on utilization rates by plant and profitability by customer, to ensure that increased production and revenue translate into tangible profit improvements.

A company official stated that in the second quarter, they are seeing a trend of significantly expanded revenue scale compared to the same period last year, as actual supply volumes increase, centered on major North American and Chinese customers.

The official added that with rising utilization rates and cost efficiency effects occurring simultaneously, they expect a meaningful improvement in their profit structure compared to the previous quarter.

Enchem expects the normalization of production at major North American customers and expanded supply to major battery companies in China to act as additional growth drivers for the second half of the year.
If factory operations at certain customers normalize, electrolyte shipments to those clients are expected to resume in phases.

Progress is being made in the Chinese market as Enchem pushes to transition to mass production supply for new customers with whom it has completed sample supply, technical verification, quality evaluation, supplier registration, and factory audits.
This transition is interpreted as an important stepping stone to diversify Enchem's revenue structure, which is currently skewed toward North America.
Enchem expects that if the trend of top-line growth and narrowing operating losses in the second quarter continues into the second half, it could approach a quarterly breakeven point (BEP).

The actual timing of achieving BEP may vary depending on several variables, including customer production plans, order volumes, delivery and inspection schedules, raw material prices, exchange rates, and the timing of tariff refund applications.

A company official emphasized that in the second half of the year, they expect the normalization of production at major customers and the expansion of supply to new customers to be reflected sequentially.
The official added that Enchem will focus on managing utilization rates and profitability by customer so that revenue expansion leads to substantial profitability improvements.
Enchem was founded in 2012 and is a specialized manufacturer and seller of electrolytes and additives for secondary batteries and EDLCs.
The company listed on the Kosdaq market in 2021.
Enchem is pursuing the recycling of waste NMP generated after secondary battery manufacturing as a new business venture.
source_title: Enchem Q2 North America Electrolyte Shipments Surge; Eyes Breakeven This Year — BigGo Finance

facts: Enchem is a secondary battery material and electrolyte company.
Enchem announced on July 29 that it has expanded its supply to Ultium Cells, the joint venture between LG Energy Solution and General Motors (GM).
Enchem's electrolyte market share in North America is estimated to exceed 50% this year.
Enchem established its Georgia plant in 2019.
Enchem began supplying electrolytes, a core material for batteries, to global customers in 2022.
Ultium Cells has built its first and second plants in Ohio and Tennessee, respectively.
Ultium Cells operations commenced in 2022 and last year.
GM announced in its second-quarter earnings conference that it will begin producing lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries at the Ultium Cells Tennessee plant starting in 2027.
GM stated it will develop lithium-rich manganese (LMR) batteries through Ultium Cells by 2028.

GM has formalized its plans to make strategic investments in battery production within the United States and to strengthen the proportion of key materials such as lithium, graphite, and manganese sourced from North America.
Price competitiveness has emerged as a critical factor in the battery market.
Ultium Cells is accelerating LFP battery production due to price competitiveness.
Enchem is increasing its share of electrolyte supply for LFP batteries.
The "One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA)" aims to exclude Chinese battery and material companies.
Enchem, as one of the first battery material companies to enter the U.S. market, expects to see increased sales as joint ventures move to expand domestic production.
Even though Enchem's electrolyte market share in the United States already exceeds 50%, the company continues to further expand its market share.
An Enchem official stated, "With the passage of OBBBA, the North American supply chain is rapidly being reorganized."
The same Enchem official added, "As relationships between K-battery companies and North American automaker joint ventures deepen, this will also act as a positive factor for Enchem."
source_title: "Enchem Achieves Over 50% Electrolyte Market Share in North America, Replacing Chinese Materials with Korean Supply" - The Asia Business Daily

facts: South Korea’s electrolyte maker Enchem Co. aims to expand its annual production capacity in the US to 300,000 tons by 2026.
The expansion aims to meet increasing demand for electrolytes from the US as well as Korean electric vehicle battery giants LG Energy Solution Ltd. and SK On Co.
Enchem plans to increase the annual manufacturing capacity of its existing plant in Georgia to 140,000 tons by 2024.
Enchem plans to start producing up to 160,000 tons of electrolyte in 2026 at factories under construction in four other states.
Enchem aims to manufacture 60,000 tons and 20,000 tons of the key battery material at Michigan-based and Ohio-based factories, respectively.
The Michigan and Ohio plants are jointly invested in by LG Energy Solution and General Motors Co.
Enchem plans to produce 40,000 tons of electrolytes from Kentucky-based facilities.
The Kentucky facilities are invested in by SK On and Ford Motor Co.
Enchem plans to produce another 40,000 tons from three Tennessee-based factories.

facts: EnChem America, Inc. is a U.S. corporation engaged in the development, manufacture, and sale of battery electrolyte materials.
EnChem America, Inc. is a U.S. subsidiary of EnChem Co., Ltd., a corporation organized under the laws of the Republic of Korea.
On May 28, 2026, The GrowHub Limited (Nasdaq: TGHL) entered into a binding summary term sheet with EnChem America, Inc. for a proposed business combination.
Under the proposed merger, EnChem America will merge with a newly formed, wholly-owned subsidiary of GrowHub and become the surviving company post-merger.
Immediately upon closing, EnChem America's pre-closing equity holders will receive 85% of the equity of GrowHub on a fully diluted basis.
Pre-closing equity holders of GrowHub will retain 15% of the equity of GrowHub on a fully diluted basis immediately following closing.
The agreed minimum equity value of EnChem America for purposes of the merger is USD 400,000,000.
Post-closing ownership allocation reflects 142,848,176 shares for EnChem (85.0%) and 25,299,810 shares for TGHL existing holders (15.0%), totaling 168,147,986 total shares.
If the closing VWAP is less than USD 4.00 per share, EnChem America has a walk-away right to terminate the agreement without liability within five business days of the VWAP determination date.
The closing VWAP is defined as the volume-weighted average trading price of TGHL Common Stock on Nasdaq for the twenty consecutive trading days ending three trading days prior to the closing date.
The parties anticipate one or more capital raising transactions in connection with or following the closing.
Upon becoming F-3 eligible, GrowHub shall file a shelf registration statement registering an amount not less than USD 200,000,000.

Post-closing, the combined company may conduct capital raises using follow-on offerings, Equity Lines of Credit on form F-1 or S-1, PIPEs, registered direct offerings, confidentially markets public offerings, at-the-market programs, or convertible debt offerings.

Conditions to closing include execution of merger documents, no governmental orders prohibiting the transaction, regulatory approvals (HSR, CFIUS if applicable), Nasdaq approval for listing of Merger Shares, requisite stockholder approvals, and SEC effectiveness of required registration statements.
Public company stockholders must approve the issuance of Merger Shares pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(a).
Private company shareholders must approve the merger under applicable corporate law.
A joint registration statement on Form S-4 or Form F-4, including a proxy statement, will be filed with the SEC as soon as reasonably practicable following execution of the Merger Agreement.
EnChem America shall deliver PCAOB audited financial statements prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP to GrowHub.

Upon closing, all incumbent directors of GrowHub shall resign, and all post-closing board seats (5 to 7 directors) will be filled by individuals designated by EnChem America, subject to a minimum of three satisfying Nasdaq director independence requirements.
All senior executive officers of GrowHub (including CEO, CFO, and other C-suite positions) shall tender their resignations effective at the closing.
The post-closing senior management team will be designated by EnChem America.
Upon closing, all Class B shares will convert to Class A on a 1:1 basis, constituting a full transfer of economic ownership and operational control to EnChem America.
An Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation will eliminate Class B Common Stock and re-designate all shares as a single class of Common Stock.
All Merger Shares issued to pre-closing equity holders of EnChem America shall be subject to lock-up restrictions for 180 days following the closing date.

GrowHub shall execute a Registration Rights Agreement to register the resale of Merger Shares under the shelf or a new registration statement upon expiration of the lock-up period, maintaining such registration for not less than 36 months following lock-up expiration.
The exclusivity period runs from execution through the earlier of 90 days or mutual written termination, with automatic 30-day renewal periods unless non-renewal notice is given.

During exclusivity, GrowHub cannot solicit, initiate, or encourage inquiries for alternative merger or business combination transactions, except for bona fide superior proposals considered in good faith by the board.

The transaction may be terminated by mutual consent, if closing has not occurred within 12 months of the Merger Agreement date, upon a final non-appealable governmental order, failure of stockholder approvals, or material uncured breach with a 30-day cure period.
Each party shall bear its own transaction costs and expenses, with shared costs allocated equally.
The definitive Merger Agreement will contain customary representations, warranties, covenants, conditions, and indemnification provisions.

Ancillary documents may include Lock-Up Agreements, Registration Rights Agreement, Voting Agreements, Officer and Director Indemnification Agreements, and Amended Certificate of Incorporation and Bylaws.
The term sheet and exchanged information are strictly confidential, with exceptions for advisors, SEC/Nasdaq rules, regulatory approvals, and required KOSDAQ disclosures in Korea.
The transaction is governed by Delaware law, with disputes subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware, waiving jury trial rights.
All terms become legally binding and enforceable simultaneously with formal signing and sealing by both parties.
source_title: GrowHub plans reverse merger with EnChem America | TGHL SEC Filing - Form 6-K

facts: Enchem is a company specializing in electrolyte materials for secondary batteries.
Enchem received approximately 26 billion KRW in Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (AMPC) last year.
Enchem expects to receive more than 17 billion KRW in AMPC this year.
Among domestic battery material companies, Enchem is the only one to benefit from the AMPC in the United States.
On August 6, Enchem announced that it has been receiving AMPC tax credits since establishing its Georgia subsidiary in the United States in 2019, based on its electrolyte supply.
The cumulative amount of AMPC benefits is expected to reach about 43 billion KRW by the end of this year.
The AMPC is a key policy introduced by the United States to foster domestic clean energy manufacturing.
With the recent passage of the "One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBA)," the AMPC application period has been extended from the original 2031 to 2032.

The AMPC provides a cash-type tax credit equivalent to 10% of manufacturing costs to companies that produce and sell batteries, solar, wind power components, and critical minerals within the United States.
Enchem's electrolyte produced at its Georgia plant has been officially recognized by the U.S. Internal Revenue Service (IRS) as an "electrode active material," making it eligible for the AMPC.
Enchem operates its Georgia plant with a production capacity of 150,000 tons as of this year.
The company is leading its "localization strategy" in North America.
The company is considering expanding production bases near major battery customers' plants in states such as Indiana and Tennessee.

An Enchem official stated that the secondary battery industry is highly sensitive to global policies, so they are closely monitoring tariffs and industrial policies in major countries, including the United States.

The official added that Enchem is implementing a flexible business strategy to respond to policies promoted by various countries, such as energy storage systems (ESS) and the defense industry, in preparation for a prolonged stagnation in electric vehicle demand.
source_title: Enchem Expects Over 17 Billion KRW in U.S. Tax Benefits This Year; Only K-Battery Material Firm to Qualify - The Asia Business Daily

The company provides Body in White frame assembly brackets to some of the world’s leading OEMs and tier one suppliers.
GSC Steel Stamping, LLC offers competitively priced metal stamping, engineering, welded assemblies, and tooling services.
In June 2023, GSC acquired Dixien, LLC’s assets.
The acquisition continues a 43-year history of automotive parts stamping at the company's facility in Lake City, GA.
The facility spans 200,000 sq. ft.
CEO Vincent Henderson previously purchased and led the turnaround of two award-winning automotive stamping operations in Michigan.
Those Michigan operations were regularly recognized by customers for excellence in overall performance, quality, and on-time delivery.
The company continuously re-evaluates ways to improve its processes, invests in advanced technology, and hires top-notch staff.
GSC has a very experienced and dedicated staff.
The plant operations management team alone possesses over 200 years of combined stamping experience.
The management team takes pride in consistently delivering high-quality, precision parts on time.
GSC Steel Stamping, LLC is classified as a private company.

The company operates across multiple industries including Aerospace & Defense, Automotive & Transport, Automotive Manufacturing, Automotive Metal Stamping, Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing, Forging & Stamping, and Transportation Equipment Manufacturing.
source_title: GSC Steel Stamping Company Profile - PR.com

facts: The global automotive metal stamping market size was valued at USD 105.13 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow from USD 110.45 billion in 2026 to USD 163.99 billion by 2034 at a CAGR of 5.06% during the forecast period 2026-2034.
Automotive metal stamping transforms flat sheet metal into automotive components including body panels, chassis components, brackets, and structural elements using a stamping press.
Blanking dominates the technology segment, hot stamping dominates the process segment, and Asia-Pacific is the highest shareholder in the global market.
Growing automotive production serves as a fundamental driver, with new vehicle sales/registrations reaching 18.8 million units in Q1 2021, representing a growth of over 26% compared to Q1 2020.
Emerging markets like India, China, and Brazil are experiencing rapid industrialization and urbanization, further propelling automotive production and demand for stamped metal components.

Technological disruptions like 3D printing and composite materials pose restraints by offering alternatives to conventional metal stamping with advantages such as greater design flexibility and reduced material waste.
The advent of electric vehicles (EVs) presents a significant opportunity as they require specialized components including lightweight yet durable body structures and battery enclosures.
Global EV sales exceeded 11 million in 2022, with forecasts predicting a 35% growth in 2023 to reach a total of 14 million units.
The proportion of the global vehicle market allocated to electric cars rose from around 4.0% in 2020 to 14% in 2022 and further to 18% in 2023.

Hot stamping, also known as press hardening, involves heating high-strength steel to around 900°C and rapidly stamping it into a die for lightweight yet strong safety components like car body reinforcements.
Commercial vehicles require durable and precisely engineered stamped parts such as chassis, body panels, brackets, and structural parts to withstand rigorous use and ensure safety standards.
Asia-Pacific is expected to expand substantially, driven by established automotive manufacturing hubs in China, Japan, and South Korea and rising EV demand.
China led the electric vehicle industry in 2022 with an 82% surge in EV sales, accounting for almost 60% of global EV purchases.
China currently possesses nearly 50% of the total number of electric vehicles worldwide and represented 35% of global EV exports in 2022.
North America represents a dynamic market fueled by robust manufacturing activities in the United States and Mexico, with established OEMs and a dense network of suppliers.
The U.S. automotive industry accounts for approximately 3-3.5% of the country's GDP, with 15.8 million units sold in 2021, making it the second-largest automobile market globally.
In August 2023, Willis & Smith Capital and its affiliates officially announced the acquisition of Performance Stamping, LLC, a prominent metal stamping company based in Carpentersville, Illinois.

Performance Stamping serves domestic and international original equipment manufacturers in sectors including automotive, appliances, water treatment, oil & gas, utilities, industrial services, and HVAC.

Key players in the market include Aro Metal Stamping Co., Kenmode Precision Metal Stamping, Martinrea International Inc., Shiloh Industries, Inc., Acro Metal Stamping Co., Manor Tool & Manufacturing Company, American Industrial Company, Wisconsin Metal Parts, Inc., and Clow Stamping Co.
In July 2023, ArcelorMittal Europe - Flat Products entered into a contract with Gestamp Metal Forming GmbH to manage the processing of Gestamp's ferrous scrap metal.
In January 2024, GSC Steel Stamping, LLC acquired the assets of Dixien, LLC.
GSC Steel Stamping operates a facility in Lake City, GA, spanning 200,000 square feet, making it the largest automotive stamping plant owned by a minority group worldwide.
In March 2024, Tenral, a Chinese metal stamping company, plans to enhance its capacity and elevate its metal stamping operations.

Research Associate Abhijeet Patil specializes in evaluating industry automation trends, mobility innovations, and supply chain shifts with over 3 years of experience in Automation & Process Control and Automotive & Transportation sectors.
source_title: Automotive Metal Stamping Market Size, Share, Growth, Analysis, 2034

facts: GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson was named the 2023 “Georgian of the Year” by Georgia Trend.
The award recognizes Commissioner Wilson as the top salesperson for the state and commends his work promoting the advantages of Georgia to attract investments and create jobs.
Wilson credits the state’s success with the team approach Georgia takes when conducting business.
Wilson maintains a personal focus on keeping relationships strong at every level, including with partners and businesses across the state and the globe.

In the six years since Wilson was appointed as economic development Commissioner by former Governor Nathan Deal, Georgia maintained its place as the No. 1 state in the nation to do business, earning this recognition for the ninth consecutive year in 2022.

Former Georgia Governor Sonny Perdue credited Wilson with much of the credit for Georgia being one of the best places to grow a business by communicating all the great things that the Georgia governor and legislature have done during those years.
Wilson’s leadership and focus on relationship-building helped Georgia play the long game in attracting business and fostering its growth.

In August 2022, GDEcD announced that in FY22, investments in expansions and new locations totaled more than $21.2 billion and would create 51,132 direct new jobs in the state, setting new records for Georgia.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s work providing solutions for companies and keeping communication open were two key factors that helped Georgia land two of its largest-ever investments in FY22.
Wilson expressed passion for selling Georgia and gratitude for the opportunity to work with governors who gave him the chance to do this.

GDEcD teammates Lee Thomas, Director of the Georgia Film Office, and David Nuckolls, Executive Director of the Center of Innovation, were also recognized in Georgia Trend’s lists for their contributions to the state.
GDEcD acts as the “quarterback” that brings partners across the state together to make business happen.

links.context: Learn more about why business wants what Georgia has

source_title: GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson Named ‘Georgian of the Year’ by Georgia Trend - Savannah CEO

facts: Georgia legislature passed House Bill 1181 to enhance tax incentive strategy and improve fiscal management, bringing major changes effective January 1, 2025.
HB 1181 modifies the duration and applicability of credit carryforward periods and institutes sunset provisions for certain credit programs.
The legislation shortens carryforward periods to encourage more immediate utilization of credits, injecting dynamic activity into the state's economy.
Sunset provisions establish predefined expiration dates after which specific credits will no longer be available, aiming to periodically assess program effectiveness.
Taxpayers are advised to accelerate tax credit generation before January 1, 2025, as pre-2025 credits retain their original carryforward periods.
Starting in 2026, the Notice of Intent (NOI) for the Jobs Tax Credit Program must be submitted electronically via the Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) website by June 30, 2026.
The NOI locks in a specific county or census tract tier ranking for the Jobs Tax Credit for three years.
For most income tax credits in Georgia, the maximum carryforward period for unused credits generated on or after January 1, 2025, is reduced from ten years to five years.
The Electric Vehicle Charger Credit (HB 1181 Section 1-26) has its carryforward period reduced from five years to three years, with a sunset date of December 31, 2029.
The Clean Energy Property credit (HB 1181 Section 1-6) maintains a three-year carryforward period.
The Business Enterprise Vehicle Credit (HB 1181 Section 1-28) has its carryforward period reduced to three years, with a sunset date of December 31, 2029.

Credits for Investment in Expanding Existing Manufacturing Facilities and Enhancements for High-Impact Aerospace Defense Projects (HB 1181 Section 1-29) have their carryforward period reduced from fifteen years to ten years.
HB 1181 also establishes sunset dates for various sales and use tax and excise tax exemptions.
source_title: Georgia's Tax Credits Update: What to Know in 2025 and 2026 | Cherry Bekaert

facts: Astemo Americas Inc. will close its auto-parts plant in Tallapoosa.
The closure will leave about 60 people out of work.
The facility was considered one of the town's manufacturing anchors and is being stripped from the local economy with barely a whisper of public notice.
The shutdown follows the termination of a principal customer contract covering parts for four vehicle models.
The plant is located at 550 Honda Parkway in Tallapoosa, placing it side by side with Honda’s transmission operations.
The Tallapoosa site is listed as one of several U.S. plants operated by Astemo Americas.
Tallapoosa has roughly 3,200 residents.
Losing about 60 manufacturing jobs represents a meaningful shock to the local job base and dependent small businesses.
Exact shutdown timelines, severance or transition support details for employees, and plans for moving equipment to other Astemo sites were not included in initial coverage.
The closure highlights how tightly auto suppliers can be hitched to one automaker’s programs and how sourcing shifts can suddenly impact hometown factories.
source_title: Astemo Tallapoosa Plant Closing Displaces 60 Workers

facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. and Light have begun “proof of concept” development of a forward-facing perception system for use with advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS).
The two companies will begin field testing the new multi-view, camera-based sensing technology later in July 2021.
Light’s Clarity™ depth perception platform integrates seamlessly into Hitachi Astemo’s ADAS and engine control unit (ECU) applications to work directly with other ADAS systems.
John Nunneley serves as senior vice president of Design Engineering at Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc.
Light is based in Santa Clara, California.
Light’s Clarity platform uses cameras to detect 3D structures on the road from 10 centimeters to 1,000 meters away.
The Clarity platform offers three times the range and 20 times the detail of best-in-class LiDAR sensors.

The technology enables safer automatic emergency braking, evasive steering control for a wider variety of objects, adaptive suspension adjustment for road hazards such as potholes, and smoother adaptive cruise-control and lane-keeping systems.
Dave Grannan is co-founder and CEO of Light.
Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle control systems for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Hitachi Astemo adheres to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards in its manufacturing and marketing operations.
Light combines computational imaging breakthroughs with multi-camera calibration to provide accurate real-time depth perception at near and far distances.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Americas And Light Collaborate On Development Of Adas Technology: Hitachi Global

facts: Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. has had a long involvement in the development of electric motors with high output ratio for use as electric drive motors for vehicles.
The company adopted the IPM (interior permanent magnet) synchronous motor for use as an electric drive motor for vehicles.
IPM synchronous motors combine small size, light weight, high output and high efficiency.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. is in production on a "hybrid" vehicle.
The company produces an electric traction motor housing that sits between the internal combustion engine and the gear.
The traction motor can provide main or supplemental power to the vehicle.
Hitachi produces an auxiliary electric motor which provides starting ability in internal combustion engines and electrical power generation to charge batteries.
Hitachi produces an electric motor that drives the CVT transmission oil pump providing power to control the transmission ratio (drive).
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. is in production of and provides complete system compatibility for EV and PHEV applications.
The company's other motor and inverter is used in a small Nissan full electric vehicle (Hyper-Mini).

facts: A Massachusetts-based manufacturer is committing $40.2 million to expand its Floyd County operation, creating 25 new jobs at the location.
Hollingsworth & Vose is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications.

The family-owned company traces its history back to a 1728 legislative act encouraging paper production and has evolved over nearly 300 years to focus on materials for filters (clean rooms, hospitals, computers, commercial buildings, homes, cars, trucks, heavy duty equipment) and batteries used mainly in telecom and hybrid vehicle applications.
The expansion in Floyd County will add 28,000 square feet to the H&V facility at 365 Christiansburg Pike Northeast to accommodate new production equipment to meet increased demand.
H&V currently employs more than 200 workers in Floyd County.
Josh Ayer, CEO of H&V, stated the company has been part of the Floyd, Va., community since 1976 and considers the facility essential to serving global and domestic customers.
The company chose Virginia for this expansion due to its positive business environment and strong support from the Commonwealth of Virginia and Floyd County.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Floyd County on the economic incentives package.
Gov. Glenn Youngkin approved a $558,700 grant from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund as part of that incentive package.

Gov. Glenn Youngkin stated that Hollingsworth & Vose has generated positive economic impact and job opportunities in Floyd County for over four decades, and this investment further solidifies the company's commitment to Virginia.

Gov. Glenn Youngkin noted that businesses with a long history of expansion in the Commonwealth offer powerful testimonials on why a Virginia location is a foundation for success, expressing a desire for continued partnership with H&V.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose expanding Floyd County operation, creating 25 new jobs

facts: East Walpole, Mass.-based advanced materials manufacturer Hollingsworth & Vose Co. is investing $40.2 million to expand one of its Floyd, Va., facilities.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose invests $40M in Virginia expansion - Plastics News

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose (H&V), a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications, will invest $40.2 million to expand its operation in Floyd County, Virginia.
The expansion includes adding over 28,000 square feet to H&V's facility at 365 Christiansburg Pike Northeast to accommodate new production equipment for increased demand.
Virginia successfully competed with Georgia for this project, which will create 25 new jobs.
Governor Glenn Youngkin announced the investment and stated that H&V has generated positive economic impact and job opportunities in Floyd County for more than four decades.
Governor Glenn Youngkin noted that businesses with a long history of expansion in Virginia offer powerful testimonials on why a Virginia location is a foundation for success.
Virginia Secretary of Commerce and Trade Caren Merrick stated that H&V has played a role in the success of Virginia’s advanced materials industry over the past 46 years.
Secretary Caren Merrick thanked H&V for creating high-quality jobs in Floyd County, citing the New River Valley’s strategic location and business-friendly environment.
H&V CEO Josh Ayer stated that the company has been part of the Floyd, Virginia community since 1976 and considers the facility essential to serving global and domestic customers.
CEO Josh Ayer explained that Virginia was chosen for the expansion due to its positive business environment and strong support from the Commonwealth of Virginia and Floyd County.
Floyd County Board of Supervisors Chairman Joe Turman stated that H&V's new investment is over three times bigger than the largest private investment in the county's 191-year history.
Delegate Marie March stated that the expansion will provide crucial jobs for local families and enable them to keep working at home.
Delegate Marie March welcomed the investment, noting that private enterprise ventures reward and benefit the local community.
Hollingsworth & Vose was incorporated in 1892 and is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts.
H&V's advanced materials contribute to a cleaner world through products providing clean air, clean liquids, and energy storage.
The company has been family-owned for seven generations.
H&V established its first Virginia facility in Floyd County in 1976 and currently employs over 200 workers.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Floyd County to secure the project for Virginia.
Governor Youngkin approved a $558,700 grant from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund to assist Floyd County with the project.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose to Expand and Create 25 Jobs in Floyd County - Virginia's New River Valley

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company has been awarded the 2026 BCI Innovation Award by Battery Council International for its development of a unique glass fibrous matrix electrode.

The innovation, known as Glass Electrode Material (GEM), helps generate and support a unique microporous structure within the lead active mass of a battery to enhance chargeability performance and cycle life reliability without impacting water loss.
GEM technology improves recharge efficiency through enhanced charge acceptance, reducing fuel-related emissions while improving battery performance, durability, and cycle life reliability.
With approximately three times higher Dynamic Charge Acceptance (DCA), GEM enables next-generation start-stop systems that supports fuel economy gains of up to 5%.
Its fast recharge response also makes it well suited for advanced 12-V architectures in hybrid (HEV, PHEV) and electric vehicle auxiliary systems.
The technology platform is applicable across multiple energy storage applications, including automotive batteries, motive power for forklifts, and stationary backup power solutions.
Hollingsworth & Vose developed GEM in partnership with ArcActive.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International annually presents this award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
source_title: Breaking news: BCI names Hollingsworth & Vose 2026 Innovation Award winner – Batteries International
