facts: Isidro Arellano logged 60-hour-plus weeks at a Kia assembly line in West Point, Georgia.
Arellano is one of nine Mexican engineers who are plaintiffs in a federal racketeering lawsuit against KIA, an affiliate of South Korea-based Hyundai Motor Group, and Hyundai Mobis.
The Mexican nationals allege they were lured to the companies’ assembly lines by Korean-led labor brokers dangling professional jobs that didn’t exist.
Last year Georgia gave its largest incentive package ever, $1.8 billion, to Hyundai for an EV plant near Savannah, promising 8,100 direct jobs and thousands more from suppliers.
Benjamin Botts, law director for Centro de los Derechos del Migrante, asked why companies engaged in deceptive practices in existing facilities while making large promises to the state.

According to the lawsuit, staffing companies misled both the engineers and the US State Department by coaching them on how to obtain TN visas and providing employer letters detailing fictitious US jobs.
The plaintiffs were among hundreds of Mexican nationals brought to West Point under the visa program by labor brokers under contract to supply workers to the automakers.
Federal officials do not track TN visa holders in the US, making the exact number elusive.
The plaintiffs ended up doing manual labor for less pay and longer hours than their domestic counterparts, the suit alleges.
Kia and Hyundai MOBIS both strongly deny the lawsuit allegations.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp set a torrid pace of recruiting EV projects, making promised jobs a centerpiece of his 2022 reelection campaign.
The ceremonial groundbreaking for the $5.5 billion Hyundai EV plant came weeks before Governor Kemp's November victory.
Since October, Kemp has announced 1,500 jobs from Hyundai MOBIS; 630 from Joon Georgia Inc., a subsidiary of Ajin USA; 740 from Seoyon E-HWA and 456 from Ecoplastic.
There are an additional 3,500 new jobs at a battery manufacturing plant, a joint venture from Hyundai Motor Group and SK On, a spinoff of Seoul-based SK Innovations.
In March, Kemp announced 400 more jobs from a Hyundai supplier.
Georgia’s economic development community announced more than 1,900 new jobs for hardworking Georgians in a single month, most related to the Hyundai plant.
State economist Jeffrey Dorfman warned there may not be enough Georgians to fill the jobs.
Georgia’s unemployment rate was 3.1% in March, consistently lower than the US average.
Nationwide, unemployment in transportation manufacturing stood at 1.8% while the national average averaged 3.3%.
Plants are located in rural areas with a far-flung labor pool and lack a dense, experienced manufacturing workforce around them.

Legally hiring foreign line workers is difficult because there is not a manual labor visa for those jobs; non-agricultural manual labor visas are for temporary seasonal work, capped at 66,000 annually.

The TN visa is granted by the US State Department, typically applied for by workers instead of companies, has no annual cap, and lacks worker protections such as work contracts or prevailing wage requirements.
There have been more TN visa workers in the US than any other category of temporary visa holder every year except one since 2012.
The TN visa is for Mexicans and Canadians in specific fields including engineers and scientific technicians, but no agency systematically checks to ensure they work in their specified jobs.
According to the lawsuit, Mexican engineers learned of Georgia opportunities through LinkedIn, job fairs and universities, with a recruiting company coaching them through the application process.

Luis Salazar, an engineer with seven years of experience, was supposed to be a quality control engineer at Hyundai MOBIS but ended up on an assembly line installing bumpers, suspension systems and speakers.
Veronica Olan, an engineer for six years, was supposed to develop and improve operations processes but was put on a KIA assembly line tightening door screws and pouring antifreeze.

Arellano was supposed to be a scientific production technician making $40,000 annually with two weeks of paid vacation and a year’s free housing, but ended up doing manual labor in a two-bedroom apartment with three other engineers.
Neither state nor local officials would comment on the specific allegations or future implications for Georgia’s labor supply.

Officials said they were taking steps to ensure an adequate workforce, including a labor shortage action plan from the Savannah Joint Development Authority, a state rural housing initiative and continued investment in training.
Garrison Douglas, a spokesman for Kemp, said Georgia is leading the way in growing and developing its workforce to fill jobs created by Kia and Hyundai.
An estimated 4 million manufacturing jobs will open over the decade, of which 2.1 million will remain unfilled, according to a 2021 report by Deloitte and the Manufacturing Institute.
Laine Mears, a professor of automotive engineering at Clemson University in South Carolina, called labor among the biggest challenges for EV manufacturing.
The lawsuit raises questions about automaker responsibility for labor violations when workers are recruited and paid by outside firms.
When Alabama fined eight companies in Hyundai’s supply chain late last year for child labor violations, seven were third-party staffing companies.
Third-party staffing companies have become a staple of the US manufacturing industry.
The Obama administration broadened the definition of responsibility to include companies where violations occur along with third-party recruiters due to high coordination between them.
Former President Donald Trump reversed the provision, and it was restored by President Joe Biden.
David Weil, a Brandeis economist who headed the US Department of Labor’s Wage and Hours Division under Obama, noted similar problems arose with the J-1 visa program.
source_title: Mexican Engineers Hired by Kia, Hyundai in US for Jobs that ‘Didn’t Exist’ | Mexico News

facts: The Georgia Association of Manufacturers (GAM) announced the development of “Manufacturing Georgia” on February 27, 2025.
Manufacturing Georgia is the first-ever digital platform designed to help Georgia manufacturers engage directly with the young generation.
GAM is developing the platform through a multi-year strategic partnership with Unmudl, a leading Skills-to-Jobs® Marketplace.
The platform will feature dynamic, social media-like capabilities to connect manufacturers with future workers, especially younger audiences.
The initiative's goals are improving public awareness and understanding of modern manufacturing and increasing the availability of skilled manufacturing workers within the next five years.
Manufacturing Georgia will be available to all manufacturers in the state.
Lloyd Avram serves as GAM’s President and CEO.
GAM formed a focus group in late 2023 to brainstorm ways to engage the public, especially the younger generation.
Unmudl was selected as the platform partner after a competitive review process.
Stephanie Scearce is GAM’s Vice President of Workforce Innovation.
Jim Lechleitner is Head of Strategy at Unmudl.
The platform will feature real-time job postings, youth apprenticeships, work-based learning opportunities, training programs, and insights into modern manufacturing.
Patrick Lenz, President and COO of Toyo Tires NA Manufacturing, voiced support for the initiative.
Stuart Countess, President and CEO of Kia Georgia in West Point, expressed gratitude for GAM’s commitment to combating the stigma that manufacturing is dull, dirty, dangerous, and dead end.

Brian Cooksey, Director of Workforce Development at Shaw Industries in Dalton, stated hope that the platform will engage young people with accurate information about advanced manufacturing career opportunities.
Smaller manufacturers make up approximately 67 percent of Georgia’s manufacturing sector.
Grenzebach emphasized that its team members are its greatest asset and that connecting with the next generation is key to manufacturers in Georgia.
The platform will launch in phases, starting with a pre-screening event for stakeholders in March.
A soft rollout to select manufacturers and education institutions will occur in April.
Manufacturing Georgia will publicly launch in July.
The initiative is supported by the Manufacturers Education Foundation and contributions from Toyo Tires, Kia Georgia, Shaw Industries, and Grenzebach.
Manufacturers across the state will have free access to the platform in 2025.
An affordable subscription fee for the platform will start in 2026, with users enrolling for free.
source_title: GAM and Grenzebach Partner with Unmudl to Launch Innovative Digital Platform: Manufacturing Georgia

facts: Kia Manufacturing Georgia plant in West Point, Georgia reached a cumulative production milestone of 5 million vehicles on Feb 24 (local time).
On the same day, the Kia Manufacturing Georgia plant began production of the 2027 All-new Telluride.
The facility marked the full-scale start of mass production for the Kia Telluride Hybrid.
The vehicle commemorating the 5 million-unit milestone is a hybrid model of the All-new Telluride.
It is the first hybrid vehicle ever produced in Georgia.
An on-site ceremony was attended by Governor Brian Kemp, local government officials, community leaders, and Kia employees.
Kia Georgia began production at the facility in 2009.
Kia Georgia has grown into a leading local employer since its inception.
Kia Georgia is established as a key manufacturing hub for the automotive industry in the southeastern United States.
The Kia Telluride produced at the West Point plant is a strategic model developed around the needs of North American consumers and U.S. road conditions.
It is widely viewed as a symbol of Kia’s growth in the U.S. market.

The first-generation Telluride received major accolades in 2020, including the North American Car, Truck and Utility Vehicle of the Year Awards (NACTOY), World Car of the Year (WCOTY), and MotorTrend SUV of the Year.
Seungkyu Yoon, Head of Kia North America and Head of Kia America, stated that Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that produces the Telluride.
Seungkyu Yoon added that the start of this production run carries significance that goes beyond simply launching a new model.
Seungkyu Yoon noted that reaching 5 million units produced and building the first hybrid vehicle in Georgia demonstrates Kia’s technological progress and future strategy direction.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that since its launch in 2019, the Telluride has been produced exclusively in Georgia and has become one of the most successful models in Kia’s lineup.
Governor Brian Kemp assessed that this achievement is the result of a close partnership between the local community and the state government.
source_title: Kia Georgia Reaches 5 Million Vehicles Produced, Begins Mass Production of Kia Telluride Hybrid - MK

facts: RACEMARK International is a 100% family-owned and operated business founded in 1972 by Bob Bailey and Mark Donohue.
The company was the first in the world to create a custom-fit carpet car mat.
The company operates manufacturing locations in the United States, Germany, Hungary, and Switzerland.
Its North American headquarters is located in Calhoun, Georgia, USA.
Its European headquarters is located in Empfingen, Germany.
RACEMARK International has a company size of 201-500 employees.
The company specializes in floor mats, trunk mats, automotive OEM supply, Tier 1 supply, manufacturing, and design.
RACEMARK delivers just-in-time products to customer assembly plants to provide the shortest lead-times possible at a competitive price.
The company's customers include Porsche, Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Tesla, Acura, Audi, Bentley, Chrysler, GM, Honda, Hyundai, Kia, MINI, Mitsubishi, Toyota, and Volkswagen.
RACEMARK aims to become the global leader in sustainable automotive floor protection and design by 2023.
The company's mission is to create innovative and sustainable automotive floor products that protect and enhance car interiors.
The company's core values are Respect, Integrity, Initiative, Creativity, Leadership, and Sustainability.
With a strong presence in North America and Europe, the company follows international market trends of the highest quality brands in the automotive and textile industries.
The company operates within the automotive, motorcycles, and bicycles industry.

facts: RACEMARK was founded in 1964 by Bob Bailey, a Porsche of America team world-class race car driver.
Mark Donohue partnered with Bob Bailey and joined RACEMARK in 1972.
The company's philosophy is to design and engineer innovative interior trim parts to meet and exceed the styling expectations of international OEM customers.
In 1973, RACEMARK became the first company in its industry to create a custom-fit carpet car mat.
RACEMARK is recognized as the reference point for innovative and stylish car mats in the automotive market.
High material standards, competitive manufacturing processes, and dedication to quality result in optimized product life cycle cost.
Capabilities include high volume production as well as premium and luxury designs for customized products supporting a wide range of vehicle segments.
Manufacturing plants are located in the United States, Germany, Hungary, and Switzerland.
The company follows international market trends of the highest quality brands in the automotive and textile industries.
RACEMARK delivers features and product enhancements to achieve “best in class” parts for the global automotive industry.
The company expanded globally to supply just-in-time delivery to customer assembly plants.

Plants are dedicated to producing automobile floor mats for premier brands in the automotive industry.

facts: Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd Nw Suite 400 Atlanta, GA 30327.
The company has over 1,000 employees.
Superior Essex reports an estimated annual revenue of over $500 million.
The company's primary industry classification is NAICS 332216 Saw Blade & Handtool Manufacturing.
Additional NAICS codes for the company include 3314 Nonferrous Metal Production & Processing and 332 Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing.

Superior Essex holds trademarks for electric cables and wires, electrical and optical cables, fiber optic or optical fiber cables, insulated electrical wire and cable, power cables, coaxial cables, magnetic wire, and winding wire.

The company manufactures cabinets specially adapted to protect telecommunications equipment in the nature of fiber optic cables, as well as fiber optic enclosures for connecting and splicing fiber optic cables.
Superior Essex produces cable connectors including RJ45 connectors, F-type connectors, RCA-type connectors, MTRJ fiber connectors, SC fiber connectors, and ST fiber connectors.
The company manufactures jumper booster cable sets comprised of battery jumper cables.
Superior Essex produces electric wire harnesses used in automotive applications.
The company's magnet wire is utilized to transfer power in household appliances, automotive applications, industrial motors, and power generation/distribution.

Peer companies headquartered in Atlanta, GA include BWAY ($100 million revenue), Spectra Metal Sales ($35 million), Fencing Supply Group Acquisition ($25 million), Central Can ($22 million), YKK AP America ($22 million), Merchants Metals ($9 million), Kingspan Insulation ($8 million), PHX Holdings ($8 million), and Tydenbrooks ($7 million).
In a separate peer comparison table, Superior Essex is listed with $44 million in revenue.

Financial data tables for Superior Essex P&L (Revenue, Cost of Goods Sold, Gross Profit, Operating Expenses, Advertising, Salaries and wages, Other Operating Expenses, Total Operating Expenses, Operating Income, EBITDA, EBIT, Net Profit) are present but contain no specific numerical values.
source_title: Annual Report on Superior Essex's Revenue, Growth, SWOT Analysis & Competitor Intelligence - IncFact

facts: Essex Solutions, the US magnet wire unit of South Korea’s LS Group and wholly owned subsidiary of Superior Essex Inc. (SPSX), is seeking an initial public offering next year with an estimated corporate value of 2 trillion won ($1.4 billion).
Essex Solutions plans to send requests for proposals to South Korean and foreign brokerage houses by early 2025 to select managers for a listing in either the US or Korea.
The company's enterprise value is estimated at over 2 trillion won, given expectations of a recovery in the electric vehicle market.
Essex Solutions supplies magnet wire products for EV transaction motors and other components to major global EV manufacturers.

Formerly known as Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, the company was founded in 2020 as a 61:39 joint venture between SPSX and Japan’s Furukawa Electric Co., with SPSX taking over Furukawa’s stake in March and changing its name to Essex Solutions.
The company aims to increase its EV magnet wire market share to 70% in North America and 50% in Europe in the next three to five years with $300 million in investment by 2028.

Essex Solutions separately seeks to raise $200 million before the planned listing through a rights offering to a consortium of the private equity unit of South Korea’s Mirae Asset Global Investments Co. and PE firm KCGI Co.
Parent company SPSX reported a net profit of 100.5 billion won in the first half, up 789.3% from a year earlier, as sales rose 5.3% to 2.1 trillion won.
source_title: LS Group’s US magnet wire unit to go public with $1.4 bn value - KED Global

facts: In 2024, electric car sales rose by 25% to 17 million globally.
Annual battery demand surpassed 1 terawatt-hour (TWh) in 2024.
The average price of a battery pack for a battery electric car dropped below USD 100 per kilowatt-hour in 2024.
Lithium prices dropped by more than 85% from their peak in 2022.
Global battery manufacturing capacity reached 3 TWh in 2024.
The next five years could see another tripling of production capacity if all announced projects are built.
Markets have shifted from regionalised and small to global and very large, with technological approaches giving way to standardisation.
Economies of scale, partnerships along the supply chain, manufacturing efficiency, and speed to market will be crucial for competition.
Greater consolidation across the sector is likely, reshaped by government-driven efforts to geographically diversify battery supply chains.
China produces over three-quarters of batteries sold globally.
In 2024, average battery prices in China dropped by nearly 30%, faster than anywhere else.
Batteries in China were reported to be cheaper than in Europe and North America by over 30% and 20%, respectively.
Declining battery prices are a major reason why many EVs in China are now cheaper than conventional counterparts.
Over 70% of all EV batteries ever manufactured were produced in China, creating extensive manufacturing know-how.
Giant manufacturers such as CATL and BYD have centralised expertise in the battery sector and driven innovation.
These companies have scaled up production faster and more efficiently than competitors and achieved higher manufacturing yields.
Supply chain integration, through acquisitions and close cooperation among leading firms, has supported faster innovation and declined manufacturing costs.
The Chinese battery ecosystem covers all steps of the supply chain, from mineral mining and refining to production of equipment, precursors, components, final batteries, and EVs.
Access to below-market prices for critical minerals supports cost declines in China.
Chinese producers have prioritised lithium-iron phosphate (LFP), a cheaper battery chemistry.
LFP batteries now cover nearly half the global EV market after more than tripling their share within the past five years.
LFP batteries are about 30% less expensive than lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide (NMC) batteries while offering competitive ranges.
Fierce domestic competition has shaped the Chinese battery market, which is home to almost 100 producers.
The number of companies producing batteries in China is likely to fall amid tough competition and shrinking margins.
Certain producers will acquire greater influence and pricing power.
China is expected to remain the largest battery manufacturer by some distance in the medium-term.
Many battery producers in Europe are postponing or cancelling expansion plans due to uncertainty about future profitability.
Production costs in Europe are about 50% higher than in China.
The European battery supply chain ecosystem is still relatively weak and lacks specialised workers.
Northvolt, Europe’s largest investment in a homegrown battery maker, went bankrupt.
Smaller manufacturers in Europe are struggling to scale up production and reach sufficient yields.
Clear policy signalling continued demand growth and reducing investment risks is essential for building a competitive European battery industry.
Efforts to produce cheaper LFP batteries in Europe are beginning to expand.
Korean manufacturers lost almost one quarter of their market share in the European Union over the past two years, dropping from nearly 80% in 2022 to 60% in 2024.
The drop in Korean market share is partly due to the increased success of LFP batteries made in China.
Some Korean companies have started investing in making LFP batteries in Europe to compete with Chinese producers.
Chinese battery makers are likely to keep expanding their European footprint through partnerships.
The joint venture between Stellantis and CATL could speed up the uptake of LFP batteries in Europe, improve the ecosystem, and reduce the cost gap with China.
Korea and Japan are major players in the global battery industry with strong expertise in NMC batteries.
Both countries have limited domestic battery production but host established manufacturers with significant overseas investments.
Korean companies lead in overseas manufacturing capacity with nearly 400 gigawatt-hours (GWh).
Japan’s overseas manufacturing capacity is 60 GWh, and China’s is 30 GWh.
Korean producers supplied over one-fifth of global electric car battery demand in 2024.
Japanese producers covered nearly 7% of global electric car battery demand in 2024.
These producers have strong innovation track records and are racing to develop new technologies such as solid-state batteries.
In the United States, battery manufacturing capacity doubled since 2022 following the implementation of tax credits for producers.
US battery manufacturing capacity reached over 200 GWh in 2024.
Nearly 700 GWh of additional manufacturing capacity is under construction in the United States.
Around 40% of existing US capacity is operated or developed by established battery makers in close collaboration with automakers.
Developing domestic capacity for manufacturing battery components in the US has progressed more slowly, so most anode and cathode demand is still satisfied by imports.
Battery demand for stationary applications in the US has increased by over 60% annually for the past two years.
Southeast Asia and Morocco are emerging as potential production hubs for batteries and their components.
Southeast Asia has attracted significant Chinese investment, which could speed up technology and innovation transfer.
In Indonesia, home to half the world’s mined nickel, the first EV battery manufacturing and graphite anode plants began production in 2024.
Morocco has the largest reserves of phosphate, a mineral essential for LFP batteries.
Morocco has an established car manufacturing industry and free trade agreements with the European Union and the United States.
These factors contributed to over USD 15 billion in announced investments in battery and components manufacturing in Morocco in 2022.
The degree of concentration in battery supply chains has raised security concerns among governments.
China recently proposed export limitations on battery cathode and lithium processing technologies.
Diversifying battery production requires time, investment to bolster domestic manufacturing, build expertise, and reduce production cost gaps relative to China.
Sufficient and sustained battery demand is required, with electric vehicle sales accounting for 85% of the battery market.
Strategically deploying automation, digitalisation and innovation can help reach sufficient production yields to compete with Chinese production.
Collaboration with incumbent battery producers through joint ventures or technology licensing agreements can decrease time and investments required to onshore battery production.
International collaboration is needed as many individual markets may not be sufficiently large to justify necessary investments.
Cooperation with resource-rich countries in South America, Africa, Australia, and Indonesia may be necessary.
The IEA will continue to monitor these trends and publish a special report focused on the car industry later this year, including new analysis on battery supply chains.
source_title: The battery industry has entered a new phase – Analysis - IEA

facts: Essex Brownell provides custom fabrication and in-house capabilities to turn materials into application-ready components.
The company offers a comprehensive product portfolio ranging from advanced materials to custom-engineered solutions.
Essex Brownell is a source for magnet wire, insulation, tapes, varnishes, and related materials.
The company maintains an inventory of over 100,000 SKUs.
Essex Brownell operates a trusted network of more than 450 supplier partners.
The company delivers precision conversion and fabrication services to meet unique application needs.
Fabrication services include die cutting, laminating, rapid prototyping, and kitting.
An expert team transforms materials into ready-to-use components designed to improve performance, efficiency, and reliability.
Products and services are categorized under Essex Active & Flexible Insulation, Liquid Insulation, Motors & Drives, Parts Equipment & Accessories, Wire & Cable, and Rigid Insulation.
The company serves multiple markets including Automotive, Industrial, Commercial & Residential, Military & Aerospace, Electronics, Motor Repair, Energy, and Mobility.
source_title: Essex Brownell Homepage

facts: Essex Furukawa released its second annual sustainability report today in collaboration with Vision 2030: A Sustainable Future, Our Driving Force.
The 29-page document highlights the company's increased use of renewable energy and successes in reducing carbon dioxide emissions.
It shows progress toward 'Zero Waste to Landfill' in global production and outlines future initiatives regarding Scope-3 emissions, supply chain data, and ESG projects.
Austin Robinson, Global Corporate Sustainability Manager at Essex Furukawa, believes the results were very encouraging.
Robinson and her team compiled the data with help from company leaders across the organization and external verification partners.
Robinson stated that what they achieved is only possible through strong commitment, noting consistent changes across the company and ongoing exchange/implementation of best practices.
Robinson noted a noticeable increase in social sustainability as more employees become aware of their role for the future of the environment and local communities.

Essex Furukawa developed its holistic sustainability program with three core values: disruptive innovation, environmentally friendly production, and agile digitalization, which serve as pillars to drive change and innovate the magnet wire industry without losing sight of environmental impacts.
The report presents last year's results and shows how they impact Essex Furukawa's progress in implementing its holistic Vision 2030 program.
The document addresses goals for zero waste to landfill, reducing energy consumption, renewable energy, and CO2 reduction regarding environmentally friendly production.
The report covers how Essex Furukawa prioritizes agile digitalization goals while collaborating with customers and their R&D leaders to drive disruptive innovations in the magnet wire industry.
Daniel Choi, CEO of Essex Furukawa, said that a leading role in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) requires the transparency provided by the report.
Choi stated that the ESG field is constantly evolving and they are committed to stakeholders to remain flexible and accountable, claiming success across most perspectives and metrics.
Choi added that the company wants to set the standard rather than just follow it in ESG practices.
Superior Essex is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.
source_title: Essex Furukawa veröffentlicht jährlichen Nachhaltigkeitsbericht, kündigt Kohlendioxidreduzierung an – Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: On February 19, 2020, Superior Essex Communications announced it has joined GeSI, an organization focused on enabling digital sustainability.

Annie Bevan, Global Head of Sustainability for Superior Essex Communications, stated that joining GeSI required significant time and demonstrates the company's support for global sustainability efforts.
Superior Essex Communications is the only cable manufacturer to achieve Living Product Challenge certification.
It is the only company to offer externally verified EPD, HPD, and multi-attribute labels for over 55 product families.
Many of its products are manufactured in a Zero Waste to Landfill certified facility.
Its products are designed to help customers achieve sustainability goals and contribute to green building certifications including Living Building Challenge, WELL, and LEED v2009 and v4.
Superior Essex is a leading cable supplier supporting Power-over-Ethernet systems, which result in energy savings and increased occupant productivity and well-being.
Will Bryan, VP of Broadband Sales, stated that the company considers its GeSI membership a way to participate in and facilitate global sustainability vision discussions.

GeSI is described as a globally recognized thought leader and proactive driver of the ICT sustainability agenda, with members focusing on climate change, energy/resource efficiency, e-waste management, responsible supply chain practices, labor rights, and public policy.
Luis Neves, CEO of GeSI, welcomed Superior Essex Communications as a member, noting its commitment strengthens GeSI's work to apply digital solutions across the industry value chain.

Superior Essex International LP produces a wide portfolio of wire and cable products and accessories, including copper and fiber optic cables for premises, outside plant (OSP), and DAS systems serving commercial, industrial, and service markets.
The company practices high levels of environmental sustainability in its manufacturing processes, conducting operations safely and responsibly from an environmental perspective.
The company's corporate address is listed as 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Superior Essex Communications Joins GeSI - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: The Georgia Transportation Alliance, an affiliate of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, selected Joseph Santoro to serve as its new Executive Director.
Joseph Santoro starts his new position on December 6.
Prior to joining the Georgia Transportation Alliance, Santoro worked for four years at the Council for Quality Growth as Director of Policy & Government Affairs.

Over the last four years at the Council for Quality Growth, Santoro worked on local, regional, and state policies related to infrastructure planning, legislative initiatives, regulatory issues, land use, transportation, and environmental planning impacting the development and growth industry.
Santoro has over 10 years of government affairs experience working for membership-based organizations in metro Atlanta evaluating local, state, and federal policy priorities.
Santoro earned a B.S. in Public Policy from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Santoro has been a Georgia Chamber Government Affairs Committee member since 2014 through various roles.
Santoro is a 2023 graduate of the Atlanta Regional Commission Regional Leadership Institute.
Nick Juliano, Partner at Impact Public Affairs and Chair of the Georgia Transportation Alliance, commented on the announcement.

The Georgia Transportation Alliance is the state’s only organization focused solely on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions specifically connected to economic development and job creation.
The Georgia Transportation Alliance works closely with the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Department of Transportation, and other key stakeholders in Georgia and Washington, D.C.
The goal of the Georgia Transportation Alliance is ensuring an efficient and safe statewide multi-modal transportation system.

Chris Clark, CCE, President and CEO of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, stated that Georgia has extensive surface transportation networks, two world-class ports, and one of the largest railroad systems in the country.
Chris Clark noted that Georgia’s transportation and logistics industry is an essential driver of record-breaking economic success.
source_title: Economic Development Expert Tapped to Lead Georgia Transportation Alliance - Augusta CEO

facts: Gov. Brian Kemp accepted the Korea Society’s top bilateral award on behalf of Georgia.

U.S.-based executives from Kia and Hyundai highlighted an explosion of Korean investment in Georgia, totaling some $23.6 billion over a decade, recently driven by auto makers responding to the industry’s electric revolution.
Stuart Countess serves as president of Kia Georgia, which operates a manufacturing plant in West Point that triggered an influx of Korean auto suppliers more than 15 years ago.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) was selected for the Gen. James A. Van Fleet Award for shepherding an ever-growing parade of Korean investors.
The award was also presented to the Korea International Trade Association (KITA) and accepted by its chairman, Christopher Koo.
The event took place at The Plaza Hotel in New York City on Sept. 20.
Gov. Kemp has supported the Kia plant since early in his first term, blessing the launches of the Telluride and Sportage models, with the latter becoming the brand’s top-selling vehicle in the U.S.
Kia is investing $200 million to upgrade its West Point assembly line for the EV9, which will be Kia’s first U.S.-made electric model.
The state’s Electric Mobility Innovation Alliance initiative, championed by Gov. Kemp, brings government, business, and academia together to make Georgia the e-mobility capital of the U.S.
SK On’s catalytic investment in Commerce in 2018 helped spur the Electric Mobility Innovation Alliance initiative.
Hyundai Motor America President and Group COO Jose Muñoz discussed how Georgia rolled out the red carpet for its Metaplant project.
The Hyundai Metaplant, including battery investment, will bring $7.59 billion and more than 8,500 jobs to Ellabell.
The Metaplant is Hyundai’s first dedicated electric-vehicle plant in the U.S.
Earlier in the week, a partnership was inaugurated between Hyundai and Georgia Tech to work on hydrogen fuel commercial vehicles and skill up engineers in the state.
North America now accounts for a quarter of Hyundai Motor’s global sales.
Gov. Kemp highlighted that Georgia hosts the third-largest Korean population in the United States, contributing work ethic and commitment to free enterprise.
A Korean consulate general was established in Atlanta in 1976, and Georgia opened a trade and investment office in Seoul in 1985.
SK Group established the first major Korean factory in Georgia in 1996, producing photo films in Covington.
SK recently inaugurated a new chip substrate plant under the Absolics brand at the former Covington property.
Korea was the first country Gov. Kemp visited on a trade mission as governor, shortly after SK Battery signaled its potential as an investment source.
Multi-billion dollar Korean investors mentioned include Hanwha Q Cells and LG.
LG is partnering with Hyundai in a 50-50 joint venture on the Metaplant battery operation.
Korea has been the top investor in Georgia by dollar amount and job creation for three consecutive years.
A total of 144 Korean facilities have established operations in Georgia.
The Van Fleet Award typically honors individuals, but this year focused on organizations driving trade and investment as trade is increasingly framed as a national security issue.
Thomas Byrne, president and CEO of The Korea Society, stated the awardees are enhancing trade and investment, creating high-paying jobs, and securing supply chains in critical technologies.

Gov. Kemp noted that Korean companies view Georgia as a “good bet” amid global economic challenges, citing quality workers, low cost of doing business, high quality of life, and speed-to-market solutions.
Georgia’s growing ports have direct links with Korea, and Delta and Korean Air fly nonstop to Seoul.
The first new nuclear reactor installed in the U.S. in 30 years came online in March at Plant Vogtle, providing reliable clean energy for companies seeking to lower carbon emissions.
Gov. Kemp publicly acknowledged Yoonie Kim, director of Korean investment for GDEcD, for crafting the strategy to welcome and settle new Korean firms in the state.

A major Georgia contingent attended the event, including GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson, Chief Marketing Officer Mark Jaronski, Yoonie Kim, Georgia Power CEO Kim Greene, Delta Air Lines Executive Vice President Peter Carter, Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison, Savannah Area Chamber President and CEO Bert Brantley, and former U.S. Sen. Saxby Chambliss with his wife Julianne.
New Korean Consul General Suh Sangpyo was unable to attend but had recently met with the governor.
Korean companies with a presence in Georgia scheduled to attend included Kumho Tire, LG, and Superior Essex.
Former U.S. Ambassador to Korea Kathleen Stephens introduced KITA chairman Christopher Koo, noting that KITA now has 73,000 member companies.
source_title: Georgia Honored for Korean Investment Wave Credited With Bolstering Bilateral Ties - Global Atlanta

facts: Superior Essex Communications achieved a 98% landfill waste diversion rate at its Hoisington, Kansas production facility for copper data cables in 2013.
The facility recycled or reused over 4 million pounds of waste instead of sending it to landfills in the past year.
All communication cable products fully comply with RoHS 2 directive and REACH restricted substances list.

While compliance with RoHS 2 and REACH is not mandated by US or Canadian law, these directives are used by North American authorities and companies to ensure environmental standards for purchased products.
Lindsay Allen, Vice President Marketing at Superior Essex International LP, stated the achievement reflects high environmental goals and world-class performance levels.
Matt Wheeler serves as Marketing Director.
Superior Essex International LP is a leading manufacturer and provider of cable, wire, and accessory products for the communications industry, energy sector, and related distribution markets.

Product lines include copper and fiber optic data cables for indoor and outdoor use, DAS cables and accessories for distributed antenna systems, FTTP closures, coaxial cables, fire alarm/security cables, and low/medium voltage power cable products and accessories.
Superior Essex International LP is a subsidiary of Superior Essex Inc.
Corporate address is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.
source_title: Superior Essex Communications Achieves 98% Landfill Waste Diversion Rate - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex successfully completed the acquisition of the remaining minority interest in Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire from Furukawa Electric Co., Ltd.
The transaction became effective on April 30, 2024.
The completion establishes Superior Essex as the sole owner of the brand, assets, and operations previously conducted by the global joint venture with Furukawa Electric.
Superior Essex plans to relaunch its magnet wire business under a new company name later in 2024.
The company will continue to operate all current facilities of the Essex Furukawa joint venture worldwide.
These facilities include magnet wire operations in Japan and Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur) that were contributed by Furukawa Electric in October 2020.
Daniel Choi serves as the CEO of Superior Essex.

Choi stated, “Over the last several years we have been able to accomplish many great things working with the team from Furukawa Electric. I am proud of what was created and thankful for their contributions.”
The acquisition solidifies Superior Essex's control over global operations and reinforces its commitment to delivering products and services to customers worldwide.
Superior Essex plans to continuously respond to market demands in the xEV automotive industry, power, electrification, and renewable energy markets.
The company will strengthen its position by actively pursuing decarbonization and sustainability initiatives.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, Essex Energy Italy, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
The company employs over 3,000 people across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is the leading global provider of magnet wire and is at the forefront of communications fiber application and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Furukawa Electric was established in 1884 and comprises 127 group companies with approximately 50,000 employees worldwide.

Furukawa Electric provides technologies, products, and services for infrastructure fields such as information and energy, automotive systems, and electronics using core technologies of metals, polymers, photonics, and high frequency.
source_title: Superior Essex Completes Buyout in Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire

The rebrand follows the company's recent buyout of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire.
Essex Solutions will encompass all current Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire locations worldwide.
Essex Energy in Italy will now be included in the new organization, reinforcing the company’s global reach and capabilities.
Related businesses Lacroix + Kress, IVA Insulations, and Hi-Wire will join the Essex Solutions organization while continuing to operate under their existing brand identities.

The company aims to proactively shape the xEV automotive industry, as well as power, electrification, and renewable energy markets.

Essex Solutions will unveil a new logo in the near future to reflect its evolution and commitment to innovation.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Solutions, Superior Essex Communications, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees in 11 countries on three continents.

The company creates disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while setting the pace on sustainability.

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. (SMAC) became a 100% subsidiary of Suzuki Motor USA, LLC (SMO) effective March 1, 2024.
SMAC was founded in 2001 and is based in Rome, Georgia.
SMAC serves as Suzuki’s All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) manufacturing facility.
For over two decades, all Suzuki adult KingQuad ATVs have been manufactured at the Rome, Georgia location.
Suzuki ATVs are sold in the U.S., Canada, and nearly 25 countries worldwide.

Prior to this change, SMAC stock was owned by Suzuki Motor Corp. (SMC) at 20% and Suzuki Motor of America, Inc. (SMAI) at 80%, with SMAI owned by SMC as the holding company for Suzuki’s U.S. operations.
The organizational change consolidates ATV sales and production arms to strengthen Suzuki’s overall ATV business in the U.S. and globally.

Masami Haga, president of SMO, stated that a primary goal is to expand commitment to the growth of the entire powersports business and build on relationships with Suzuki dealers and SMAC vendor partners.
Strengthening the supply chain and purchasing policies for locally sourced parts are objectives of the move.
Integrated production and sales functions are expected to improve responsiveness to the ATV market by more accurately linking production to sales demand.
SMO aims to improve inventory management for both the company and its dealer body to protect sales margins, flooring costs, and profitability.
ATV industry sales are generally steady throughout the calendar year with demand typically increasing in the fall months.
The change will result in better, more direct communication between SMO and SMAC, improving staff interaction between the two companies.
A more collaborative environment will be created between the production and sales groups of Suzuki ATVs, fostering better connection between the Rome- and Brea-based teams.
Suzuki’s U.S. distribution headquarters are located in Brea, California, where the company relocated in 1981 after first entering the U.S. market in 1963.
SMAC becoming a subsidiary of SMO sets the stage for new Suzuki ATV offerings and enhancements to existing products in the future.
source_title: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. Becomes Subsidiary of Suzuki Motor USA, LLC - Motorcycle & Powersports News

facts: Georgia Foreign-Trade Zone, Inc., grantee of FTZ 26, submitted a notification of proposed production activity to the FTZ Board on behalf of Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC).
SMAC operates Subzone 26L at its facilities located in Rome, Jonesboro and Cartersville, Georgia.
The notification conforming to the requirements of the regulations of the FTZ Board (15 CFR 400.22) was received on May 21, 2014.
SMAC already has authority to produce all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) and related components including carriers, footrests, fuel tanks, grips/handle bars, frames, and rear box assemblies.
The current request would add certain foreign components to the scope of authority for production under FTZ procedures.
Production under FTZ procedures could exempt SMAC from customs duty payments on the foreign status components used in export production.
On domestic sales, SMAC would be able to choose the duty rate during customs entry procedures that applies to ATVs and related components at a rate of 2.5% for the noted foreign status inputs.
Customs duties also could possibly be deferred or reduced on foreign status production equipment.

Components and materials sourced from abroad include plastic resins, chip plates, rubber hoses with/without fittings, rubber v-belts, tires and tubes, steel tubing, runners/banners, printed books/manuals/brochures, chain cam/oil pump drives, fasteners of steel and aluminum, aluminum gaskets, mountings and brackets, fenders, oil strainers, crankshaft bearings and assemblies, bearing housings, metal gaskets, stainless o-rings, batteries, spark plugs, starter motors, capacitive discharge ignition (CDI) units, magnetos, compact disc sets, copper and paper gaskets, gear shift assemblies, light bulbs, KD parts of ATVs, gear/fork shafts, therma-coupler nozzles, speedometers, and battery plates.
The duty rate ranges from free to 9.0% for the sourced components and materials.
Public comment is invited from interested parties with a closing period for receipt on July 22, 2014.

A copy of the notification will be available for public inspection at the Office of the Executive Secretary, Foreign-Trade Zones Board, Room 21013, U.S. Department of Commerce, 1401 Constitution Avenue NW., Washington, DC 20230-0002.
Andrew McGilvray served as Executive Secretary of the Foreign-Trade Zones Board and dated the notice June 6, 2014.

facts: As of March 1, Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) became a 100 percent subsidiary of Suzuki Motor USA, LLC (SMO).
SMAC was founded in 2001 and is based in Rome, Georgia.
SMAC serves as Suzuki’s ATV manufacturing facility where all Suzuki adult KingQuad ATVs have been manufactured for over two decades.
Prior to the change, SMAC stock was owned by Suzuki Motor Corporation (SMC) at 20 percent and Suzuki Motor of America, Inc. (SMAI) at 80 percent.
SMAI is owned by SMC and acts as the holding company for Suzuki companies operating in the United States.
The organizational change consolidates ATV sales and production arms to strengthen Suzuki’s overall ATV business in the United States and globally.
A primary goal of the change is to expand commitment to the growth of Suzuki’s entire powersports business, according to Masami Haga, president of SMO.
The move aims to build on relationships with Suzuki dealers and SMAC vendor partners.
Strengthening the supply chain and purchasing policies for locally sourced parts are objectives of the organizational change.
Integrating production and sales functions is expected to improve responsiveness to the ATV market by more accurately linking production to sales demand.
SMO aims to improve inventory management for both the company and its dealer body.
Improved inventory management will help protect sales margins, flooring costs, and profitability for both groups.
ATV industry sales are generally steady throughout the calendar year with demand typically increasing in the fall months.
The change enables more direct communication between SMO and SMAC, resulting in improved staff interaction between the two companies.
Suzuki will create a more collaborative environment between production and sales groups while fostering better connection between Rome-based and Brea-based teams.
Suzuki’s United States distribution headquarters are located in Brea, California.
Suzuki relocated its US headquarters to Brea in 1981 after first coming to the United States in 1963.
Suzuki introduced the first four-wheel ATV in 1982 with the Suzuki LT125, creating an all-new category of off-road recreational and utility vehicles.
Suzuki currently offers a full line of ATV vehicles with the KingQuad 750AXi as its flagship model.
SMAC becoming a subsidiary sets the stage for new Suzuki ATV offerings and enhancements to existing products in the future.
source_title: Suzuki Manufacturing of America now subsidiary of Suzuki Motor

Suzuki Motor Corporation remains a leading manufacturer and distributor of automobiles globally.
Over half of the staff at Suzuki Motor USA, LLC has been together ten years or more.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) has announced the launch of the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5.
Hyundai is investing $7.59 billion in its new Metaplant production facility in Georgia.
The first vehicle to roll off the new $7.59 billion Metaplant facility will be the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5.
Production of the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5 is scheduled to begin in the fourth quarter of this year.
The 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5 will serve as the flagship model manufactured at the new plant.
The new plant is expected to create 8,500 direct jobs in the region.
Oscar Kwon serves as the president and CEO of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
HMGMA is nearing completion of its state-of-the-art facility.
The Hyundai IONIQ 5 model has already won accolades including Best Electric SUV and Best EV for the Money.
The carmaker’s Meta-Factory concept leverages advanced technologies such as AI and 5G.
Hyundai’s investment in the Metaplant is being called a strategic move to help bolster the local economy.

facts: Suzuki Motor Corporation announced a technology strategy for the next 10 years aimed at realizing "technology that minimizes energy consumption" from manufacturing to recycling.
The company aims for a carbon-neutral world while providing transportation globally.
Suzuki will further evolve its light-weight and safety body "HEARTECT" to reduce CO2 emissions during use, resources, and production emissions.
Suzuki will work to minimize energy consumption through weight reduction technology.

Suzuki will develop electric vehicles that minimize energy consumption by combining components embodying "Sho-Sho-Kei-Tan-Bi" such as a small and efficient electric unit, and a small and light battery.
The goal is to provide customers with the most energy-efficient electric vehicles based on the renewal energy ratio and usage conditions in each country and region.
In 2023, Suzuki developed a high-efficiency engine (the Z12E Engine) that achieved a maximum thermal efficiency of 40%.
Suzuki plans to extend this high-efficiency engine worldwide and minimize energy consumption by carbon-neutral fuel and next-generation hybrids.
Suzuki will develop and provide "SDV right," an affordable software-defined vehicle system that minimizes energy consumption with "Sho-Sho-Kei-Tan-Bi."
The SDV system will use the best mix of wired and wireless (OTA) updates to make software easier to update.
Suzuki will share hardware to reduce component costs and reuse software to reduce development costs in its SDV development.
Suzuki aims to achieve minimal energy consumption with a circular economy by designing products for easy disassembly, recycling, and reuse, moving away from the linear economy model.
source_title: Suzuki Announces Technology Strategy for 10 Years Ahead | GLOBAL NEWS | Global Suzuki

facts: Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has decided to proceed with the relocation of its U.S. subsidiary (YMUS) and the sale of all fixed assets owned by YMUS in Cypress, including land, offices and warehouses.
This initiative is positioned as one of the Company's key measures aimed at improving asset efficiency and enhancing profitability in the United States.
The Cypress facility has served as the headquarters of YMUS for approximately 50 years, since Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. acquired the land in 1978 and established the office in 1979.
YMUS Marine Business was relocated to Kennesaw, Georgia in 1999 to serve the customer better.
YMUS Motorsports Business was relocated to Kennesaw, Georgia in 2019.
The Cypress facility currently primarily houses corporate functions and the Financial Services business.
The total site area of the Cypress facility is approximately 25.1 acres (approximately 102,000 square meters).
The Company plans to utilize a sale-and-leaseback arrangement for a certain period in order to ensure business continuity and to facilitate a smooth relocation to Kennesaw.
Details such as the sale price, purchaser, and timing of the transaction are currently under review.

Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is undertaking structural reforms aimed at improving the profitability of its U.S. operations in response to cost increases resulting from U.S. tariffs and changes in the market environment.
The Company is implementing cross-business cost reduction initiatives alongside these structural reforms.

Over the medium to long term, the Company seeks to build a profit structure that is not solely dependent on top-line growth, thereby transforming itself into a more resilient and robust organization capable of adapting to change.
source_title: Announcement Concerning the Relocation of Our U.S. Subsidiary and the Sale of Fixed Assets - News releases | Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd.

facts: Suzuki Motor USA, LLC is celebrating 20 years of manufacturing KingQuad ATVs at the Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) facility.
KingQuad ATVs first rolled off the assembly line in Rome, Georgia in May 2002 and have continued to be built exclusively in the United States ever since.
Suzuki has been building the award-winning KingQuad ATVs at the SMAC facility since 2002.
The SMAC facility is a multi-million dollar, 35-acre manufacturing site located in Rome, Georgia.

The facility's location in the center of the biggest ATV market in the world allows access to real-world rider feedback for product refinement and timely delivery of KingQuad ATVs to Suzuki dealers worldwide.
Suzuki founded its engineering commitment in 1909 and led the industry by creating the first ever four-wheel ATV in 1983.

Kerry Graeber, Vice President and Division Manager MC/ATV Sales and Marketing at Suzuki, stated that building KingQuad ATVs in America for the past 20 years is an extremely important milestone representing the commitment, hard work, and dedication of the Rome, Georgia facility's workforce.
The SMAC facility is a key component to the continued success of Suzuki’s global ATV business, with KingQuad ATVs shipped to U.S. Suzuki dealers and nearly 30 countries worldwide.
KingQuad ATVs are built in the USA using both U.S. and imported parts.

facts: Local management isn’t mentioning an official date, but it is confirming the closure of Emporia’s Thermal Ceramics plant.
The Emporia plant has had the Thermal Ceramics brand since the 1980s.
The plant is closing this year as its parent company, Morgan Advanced Materials, consolidates local operations with a plant in Augusta, Georgia.
The closure impacts 35 employees.
Impacted employees have the option of moving to the consolidated facility or taking severance packages.
Separation package negotiations involve conversations with Boilermakers Local 83 based in Kansas City, Missouri, and discussions have not finished as of late Wednesday afternoon.
Thermal Ceramics makes chemical components for furnaces.

Parent company Morgan Advanced Materials creates thermal products for insulation, heat shields, so-called fired refractory shapes and other high-temperature settings involving aerospace, automotive and marine environments.
Morgan Advanced Materials is headquartered in the United Kingdom.
Morgan Advanced Materials has 17 manufacturing and/or sales locations in the United States, along with nearly 70 other locations worldwide.

Thermal Ceramics becomes the latest production business with a local connection to close its operations, following the Tyson Foods announcement in December and closure announcements from Holiday Resort and Bling and Buckle since early December.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and GDEcD announced that through Q3 of FY2022 (July 1, 2021 - March 31, 2022), job creation and investments from economic development projects surpassed FY2021 year-end totals.
251 project locations supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team will result in the creation of more than 35,400 new jobs and $12.9 billion in investment for the state.
Job totals account for a 48% increase compared to three-quarters through the previous fiscal year and a 6% increase compared to the prior year’s overall total.
Total investment saw a recorded 54% increase compared to the same time period last year.
Nearly 78% of both projects and investments were located outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area, with over half of the jobs created also outside this region.
Key industry sectors driving growth include automotive, advanced manufacturing, logistics and distribution, software and technology, and food processing.
Georgia’s electric vehicle (EV) and sustainability ecosystems have grown tremendously over the past year.
The state is continuing to recruit the entire EV supply chain to ensure an effective, efficient, and affordable environment for EV production.
Rivian plans to develop a $5 billion campus at Stanton Springs North.
Rivian also plans an electric vehicle service center in Atlanta that will provide training on EVs.

SK Battery America has partnered with American lithium-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup Ascend Elements to recycle cell and module lithium-ion battery manufacturing scrap from SK’s Jackson County facility.
Ascend Elements announced its first commercial-scale battery recycling plant in Covington in January.
Aurubis will invest $340 million in a ‘first-of-its-kind in the U.S.’ state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta.
Aspen Aerogels announced plans to locate a new 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Statesboro.
Aspen Aerogels will produce PyroThin materials that provide thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems at its new Statesboro facility.
Georgia Quick Start, the No. 1-ranking workforce training program, helped secure numerous projects across key industries.
The Georgia Quick Start program has provided specialized training tailored specifically to business needs for more than 40 years and is administered through the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) deepwater and regional ports projects have played a key role in securing diverse projects, supporting logistics and supply chain infrastructure.
For the past 19 consecutive months, the GPA has been reporting record-breaking numbers as the port expedites plans for long-term growth.

The Savannah Harbor Expansion Project (SHEP), a joint state and federal project, was recently completed, deepening a 40-mile stretch between the port and the Atlantic Ocean to accommodate super-sized freighters from the expanded Panama Canal.
Inland ports are attracting investments, such as the Appalachian Regional Port driving Roper Corporation’s decision to invest $118 million in expanding its facilities in Walker County.
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport and the state’s extensive rail network serve as key assets driving Georgia’s growth.
The Georgia Department of Transportation maintains more than 1,200 miles of highway connecting companies to 80% of the country in two days or less of driving time.
This transportation infrastructure was integral to landing Ryder System Inc.’s new facility in Henry County that will create more than 250 new jobs.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that targeting new strategic industries and supporting existing business expansions are central to the department's mission to prove Georgia is where companies want to be.
source_title: New Georgia Economic Development Records Set for Jobs, Investment in Just Nine Months - Savannah CEO

facts: Georgia passed a highway-funding bill that killed its $5,000 income-tax credit for the purchase of battery-electric vehicles.
Illinois suspended its own EV rebates to close a budgetary hole.
Texas may suspend its EV rebates as well.
Georgia residents have less than three months to take advantage of the state's EV tax credit before it expires.
The Georgia EV tax credit will no longer be effective as of July 1st.
State sales of electric cars in Georgia are expected to soar for April, May, and June, then decline thereafter.
The extent of the sales decline is unknown, though data from British Columbia may prove instructive.
Georgia added a $200 registration fee for battery-electric vehicles.
The $200 fee exceeds the $165 in state gasoline taxes a Georgia driver in a 25-mpg vehicle would pay for driving 15,000 miles per year at $0.0275 per gallon.
Georgia becomes only the second state among 50 (after Virginia) where EV owners pay more in taxes for driving than gasoline and diesel car owners.
The state rushed through a hasty tax break for employees of Mercedes-Benz who lease cars from the company.
Under the new tax break, Mercedes-Benz will pay only a small application fee and a small charge for specialized license plates instead of its employees paying the standard car tax.
The total cost of the Mercedes-Benz tax break to Georgia will exceed $1 million, according to an analysis by the Fiscal Research Center of Georgia State University.
Mercedes-Benz North America is relocating its operations from New Jersey to Georgia.
Mercedes-Benz states its goal in relocating is to be closer to its large SUV assembly plant in Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
Industry observers suggest Mercedes-Benz will lose a majority of its current employees during the relocation.
Not all current MBNA employees were invited to relocate, and some invited may decline the offer.
The tax break for driving cars registered to the company (possibly on manufacturer or "MFR" plates) replicates one in New Jersey that relocating employees were used to.

Political commentators note Georgia may be shuffling various tax incentives to contribute to the rehabilitation of a former General Motors assembly plant in Doraville, which is set to become film studios.
The Georgia tax break for Mercedes still requires signing by Governor Nathan Deal.

According to an Associated Press report, the tax break's legality may be questionable because it was passed four minutes after the legislature's midnight deadline and may have violated a rule requiring lawmakers at least two hours to review new legislation.
The electric-car tax credit is conclusively dead.
Plug-in car owners in Georgia will start paying the $200 annual fee later this year.
source_title: Insult To Injury? GA Kills Electric-Car Incentive, Adds Last-Minute Luxury-Car Tax Break

facts: On March 7, 2022, Advantage Capital announced a $2.75 million investment in Thomson-based plastic injection molder Thomson Plastics, Inc.
The funding was made in connection with the Georgia Agribusiness and Rural Jobs Act (GARJA) program.
The investment will provide working capital necessary to execute on growth opportunities and continue expanding the company's business in Thomson, Georgia.

CEO Steve Dyer stated that the investment comes at an opportune time for the company's growth trajectory and provides flexibility to take advantage of growth opportunities in the large tonnage molding market.
Thomson Plastics designs, manufactures, and assembles custom plastic injection molded products for large industrial customers.
The company specializes in high tonnage molding using specially designed models, gas assisted technology, and injection molding presses.
Thomson Plastics currently employs 167 people in the surrounding community.
This marks Advantage Capital's seventh investment under the GARJA program.
Through the GARJA program, Advantage Capital has now invested more than $20 million in small businesses across Georgia to spur new job creation and economic growth in rural communities.
Principal Tim Perusek noted that Thomson Plastics is exactly the type of business they look to partner with and highlighted the program's success in working with high-growth companies.
Each GARJA investment offers a customized financing solution designed to position companies for future growth and enable job expansion in their local communities.

The GARJA program was first passed in Georgia in 2017 to provide access to capital for small businesses and stimulate economic expansion in rural areas that have not seen the same prosperity as urban areas.
State Senator Max Burns stated that the program's goal is to drive capital into traditionally overlooked areas of the state, bringing opportunity and prosperity to communities and households alike.
The plastic injection molding industry is expected to grow to $7.2 billion in the next five years.
Industry revenue was projected to rise 6.5% in 2021 alone as the economy rebounds from the COVID-19 pandemic.
Thomson Plastics operates a 180,000 square foot facility in Thomson dedicated to the manufacturing, assembly, and warehousing of plastic products.
Advantage Capital has invested more than $3.8 billion since 1992 across diverse industry sectors and helped support more than 50,000 jobs.

Underwood Mold Co maintains a full in-house tool shop for PM & repair and offers a wide range of press sizes for assembly and secondary operations.
The Rodon Group specializes in custom plastic injection molding offering various thermoplastics such as ABS, LDPE, HDPE, PC, nylon, HIPS, and PP.
Jabil Atlanta offers thermoplastic polymer solutions for diverse packaging applications.
Jabil Atlanta capabilities include accelerated product introduction, high cavitation and precision injection molding, design and in-mold labeling, and sustainable packaging options.
Jabil leverages a global network of 250,000 professionals across more than 100 strategic sites worldwide for supply chain management and sourcing.
Thomson Plastics features broad press capability ranging from 66 tons to 4400 tons of clamp force.
Thomson Plastics specializes in producing medium and large-sized parts with Class-A, high gloss, and clear acrylic surface finishes.
Thomson Plastics offers gas-assisted injection molding for large, dense parts with hollow interiors to achieve weight reduction and cycle time reduction.
Thomson Plastics recent investments have been made to enhance high pressure gas control equipment.

facts: Amara Industries announced the acquisition of Thomson Plastics, a recognized leader in large tonnage injection molding in the Southeastern United States.
The strategic acquisition enhances Amara Industries’ manufacturing footprint, automation capabilities, and technical depth.
The acquisition strengthens Amara Industries' position as a key player in the North American injection molding industry.

Thomson Plastics is known for its scale, precision, and ability to meet the demands of major customers across multiple facilities.

facts: ABC Technologies announced a recommended all-cash offer for the acquisition of the entire issued and to be issued ordinary share capital of TI Fluid Systems plc by ABC Technologies Acquisitions Limited.
TI Fluid Systems is a London-listed, market-leading global manufacturer of thermal and fluid system solutions for the full range of current and developing vehicle architectures.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 27 countries and serves all major automotive manufacturers.
The combined group would create an industry leader with an extensive global product portfolio and financial strength to support long-term growth objectives.
The transaction will provide access to a broader and more diversified range of customers, including some of the largest and most recognizable automotive OEMs and Tier One suppliers worldwide.

Terry Campbell, President and CEO of ABC Technologies, stated that the transaction is a transformative strategic opportunity which unlocks value for all stakeholders and provides a platform for further growth.
Under the terms of the transaction, shareholders of TI Fluid Systems will be entitled to receive 200 pence per share.
The offer values TI Fluid Systems at an enterprise value of approximately £1,831 million.
The acquisition is currently expected to complete in H1 2025, subject to shareholder and other relevant legal and regulatory approvals.
Lazard acted as lead financial advisor to ABC Technologies for the transaction.
Citi, TD Securities, and Scotiabank also acted as financial advisers on the deal.
Kirkland & Ellis International acted as legal advisor to ABC Technologies.
Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison acted as legal advisor in respect of regulatory and financing matters.
ABC Technologies is a leading global manufacturer and supplier of custom, highly engineered, technical plastics, and light-weight innovations to the North American light vehicle industry.
ABC Technologies serves more than 25 major original equipment manufacturer customers in 8 countries.
ABC Technologies operates through a skilled workforce of over 11,000 team members.
ABC Technologies is majority-owned by certain affiliated funds of Apollo Global Management, Inc. and its subsidiaries.
Funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management, L.P. own a minority equity interest in ABC Technologies.
source_title: ABC TECHNOLOGIES ANNOUNCES RECOMMENDED OFFER TO ACQUIRE TI FLUID SYSTEMS

facts: Terry Campbell will become President & Chief Executive Officer of the combined ABC Technologies and TI Fluid Systems group following the acquisition.

ABC Technologies' acquisition of TI Fluid Systems remains on track to complete during the first half of 2025.
Terry Campbell became President & CEO of ABC in 2022 after previously holding senior leadership roles at Johnson Controls, Magna International, and The Woodbridge Group.
Campbell brings over thirty years of experience in the global automotive sector to his new role.
Alexander De Bock was appointed CFO of TIFS in 2023 after spending over 15 years in the industry, including finance leadership roles at WABCO and ZF Group.
The acquisition will be implemented by way of a court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement under Part 26 of the Companies Act 2006.

The acquisition is expected to complete in the first half of 2025 following receipt of remaining regulatory and judicial clearances.
TI Fluid Systems will announce the anticipated completion timetable through a Regulatory Information Service and its website.

facts: TI Fluid Systems has started high volume production of new thermal products, assemblies and technology for the new Volkswagen ID.3 and ID.4 battery electric vehicles.
The ID.3 is a compact hatchback and the first production model based on the modular electric drive matrix (MEB) from Volkswagen.
The release of the ID.4, an electric compact SUV, will follow.
Bill Kozyra, President and CEO of TI Fluid Systems said that they were excited to announce the launch of these new battery electric vehicle components.
With this product line, TI Fluid Systems took another important step to its future of becoming an integrated thermal management supplier for the battery electrical vehicle market.

This new application of heat pump technology using R744 (also called CO2) refrigerant provides both heating and cooling for the cabin comfort and operates at a higher efficiency in certain conditions for significant battery range extension.
TI Fluid Systems assembles the heat pump module with the electronic valves, accumulator, bracket, and chiller with associated high pressure aluminum lines.
The heat pump valve assembly are supplied from TI Fluid System’s Jablonec Plant in the Czech Republic.
TI Fluid Systems will also be supplying the Chassis Thermal Coolant Loop Line Assemblies that are manufactured in various materials including lightweight engineered polymers.
TIFS has commissioned a new plant in Tangier, Morocco for the battery electric vehicle market.
This plant supplies the finished hoses to Tauste, Spain for final assembly and verification test.
TI Fluid Systems is the Tier 1 Global Supplier for these BEV products.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems Provides Industry-First Thermal Technology To Volkswagen's Battery Electric Vehicles - EMobility+

facts: Ford recognised the importance of EV battery temperature in relation to performance, range, and battery life.
Ford implemented DDM measurement sensors to monitor the flow of coolant.
The implementation of DDM measurement sensors aims to ensure EV battery lifetime and long-term sustainability.
Electric vehicles have witnessed remarkable growth in recent years, driven by environmental concerns, technological advancements, and government incentives.
As the world transitions toward sustainable transportation, EVs play a pivotal role in reducing greenhouse gas emissions and dependence on fossil fuels.
Ensuring optimal performance and range in EVs requires addressing a critical factor: battery temperature control.

facts: Julien Plenchette joined TI Fluid Systems as President – Americas, effective 08 April 2024.
TI Fluid Systems is a leading global supplier of automotive fluid systems technology.
Plenchette has over 20 years’ experience in the industry, including senior positions with other leading tier-one automotive suppliers such as ZF/Wabco, LS Automotive and Valeo.
He was latterly Managing Director and Vice President of Aptiv’s Active Safety and User Experience Business Unit in North America.
The appointment completes TI Fluid Systems’ regional leadership team, joining President for Asia Pacific, Henri Tsang, and President for Europe, Middle East, and Africa, Steve Vercauteren.

The Group’s regional structure has been in place since 1 January 2024, improving alignment with customers, increasing flexibility to shift resources between product lines and creating synergy opportunities.
The structure enhances regional leaders’ agility to respond to changes in their markets, particularly the transition to electrification which is occurring at different speeds in regions.

Stefan Rau will focus on his role as Chief Operating Officer, leading the Group’s operational efficiency and productivity programmes to drive progress towards TI Fluid Systems’ mid-term targets and ensure long-term cost competitiveness.
CEO and President Hans Dieltjens stated the company has built a strong, well-balanced leadership team with a broad range of skills and experience.
Dieltjens thanked Stefan Rau for taking on the role of President for the Americas over the last few months in addition to his COO role.
Dieltjens noted productivity and efficiency have always been in TI Fluid Systems’ DNA and they are well on track with implementing planned initiatives.
Dieltjens highlighted their 'Taking-the-Turn strategy' is delivering and their propulsion agnostic portfolio means they are ideally positioned for the industry transition.

TI Fluid Systems operates across 26 countries with a commitment to improving efficiency, performance and sustainability worldwide.
