canonical_name: Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Grant Ecosystem
facts: Georgia offers $2.4B+ in annual business incentives across 65+ programs.
The state utilizes an aggressive Tier system ranking counties from Tier 1 (most distressed) to Tier 4 (least distressed).
Businesses creating new full-time jobs in Tier 1 counties qualify for a Job Tax Credit of $750 to $4,000 per job per year for 5 years.
No equity is required for the Job Tax Credit or Georgia Quick Start programs.
In Tier 1 counties, Job Tax Credits can offset 100% of state income tax liability and excess credits can be applied against payroll withholding taxes.
Job creation thresholds for the credit vary by tier, ranging from 2 to 25 jobs.
Strategic industries eligible include manufacturing, telecommunications, broadcasting, tech, headquarters operations, and processing.
Form IT-CA must be filed with the annual state corporate income tax return to claim Job Tax Credits.
Credits can offset 50% to 100% of state income tax liability depending on the county tier.
Georgia provides an Apprenticeship Program Tax Credit of up to $25,000 per apprentice per year, capped at $100k per employer.
The apprenticeship credit requires a registered apprenticeship with the US Dept of Labor or state agency and focuses on manufacturing and high-demand trades.
Georgia Quick Start offers free customized workforce training valued at thousands of dollars per employee for companies creating 15 or more new jobs.
Quick Start eligibility targets manufacturing, biotech, distribution, or HQ operations requiring complex skills.
The state builds custom mobile training centers and supplies instructors to train initial workforces entirely for free through Quick Start.
A high-wage Job Tax Credit offers $2,500 to $5,000 per job per year for 5 years for positions paying 110% to 200% of the local average county wage.
The high-wage credit requires creating 50 or more jobs within 12 to 24 months and usually requires an investment threshold.
A mega-project statutory credit offers $5,250 per job per year for 5 years for transformational investments of $450 million or more that create 1,800 new jobs.
The mega-project credit is designed for automotive and major manufacturing anchor projects.
The Georgia Film Tax Credit provides up to a 30% transferable tax credit on qualified local production spend of $500,000 or more.
Productions receive a 20% base tax credit plus a 10% uplift for including the Georgia logo in credits.
Transferable film credits can be sold to corporations for 85 to 90 cents on the dollar to generate immediate cash.
Hyundai is constructing a $5.5B EV plant near Savannah that promises 8,100 jobs.
Georgia is attracting $5B+ megaplants including those from Hyundai and Rivian to become the EV capital of the South.
Kia previously established a presence in the state's automotive sector.
The state processes 70% of all US card swipes through its Transaction Alley cybersecurity and fintech sector.
OneGeorgia Authority provides rural grants to support economic development outside major metros.
Georgia SBIC offers seed funding for early-stage businesses.
Standard grant processing times in Georgia are 30 to 45 days, while large enterprise incentives can take 6 to 12 months.
Micro-grants for early-stage businesses range from $5,000 to $25,000, while standard growth programs offer $50,000 to $500,000.
Large enterprise grants for major job creation or facility expansion can exceed $1 million.
Retailers and restaurants are generally ineligible for state job tax credits unless located in the top 10 poorest counties or designated Rural Zones/Opportunity Zones.
Companies must maintain E-Verify compliance and proper NAICS code classification to qualify for most incentives.
Payroll records (GDOL-4) and investment receipts/invoices are required documentation for applications.
A detailed training plan is required specifically for Quick Start program applications.
Georgia has been ranked the #1 State for Business for over a decade straight.
The state heavily rewards building in rural or distressed zones while penalizing locations in affluent areas like North Fulton.
Success rates for Georgia grants typically range from 25% to 35%.
Grants are generally considered taxable income at the federal level, but tax credits directly reduce state tax liability dollar-for-dollar.
Grant funds used for specific purposes like equipment, R&D, or payroll may be deductible as business expenses.
Georgia business grants are available to for-profit businesses registered and operating within the state.
Most programs require businesses to be incorporated in Georgia, employ Georgia residents, and be in good standing with state tax authorities.
Key eligibility factors include employee count (many require 500 or fewer employees) and industry sector priority.
Priority sectors for funding include Logistics & Supply Chain, Electric Mobility, Film & Media, manufacturing, tech, and headquarters operations.
Some programs have citizenship requirements while others are open to all legal residents.
Federal programs like the SBA's Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Federal Contracting Program provide access to set-aside contracts.
Many Georgia state programs provide bonus scoring or dedicated tracks for women-owned businesses.
Average funding for women entrepreneurs ranges from $5,000 to $250,000 depending on the program.
The SBA's 8(a) Business Development Program provides 9 years of sole-source federal contracting access for minority-owned businesses.
State-level minority business enterprise (MBE) certification unlocks set-aside procurement opportunities in Georgia.
Funding for minority entrepreneurs typically ranges from $10,000 to $500,000 based on program and business size.
Startup grants typically target businesses under 3 years old focusing on proof-of-concept and MVP development with amounts ranging from $10,000-$150,000.
Small business grants target established companies (3+ years) emphasizing expansion and job creation with amounts ranging from $50,000-$500,000.
Startups should target innovation-focused programs like SBIR Phase I while established SMBs benefit more from state economic development grants.
You can legally stack state grants with federal programs like SBIR/STTR for maximum funding.
Government grants are non-repayable but highly competitive, limited to specific uses, and require significant application time.
SBA loans (7(a), 504, microloans) have higher approval rates (50-70% for qualified applicants), offer flexible use of funds, and provide larger amounts up to $5M, but require repayment with interest.
Best strategy is to apply for grants first for funding without debt, then use SBA loans for remaining capital needs.
The Georgia Small Business Development Center provides free guidance on both grant and loan options.
Grant application success follows a proven process: Research & Match (2-4 weeks), Prepare Foundation (1-2 weeks), Write Proposal (2-4 weeks), Submit & Respond, and Award & Comply.
Most grants want an executive summary, project description, timeline, budget, and impact metrics in the proposal.
Current average processing time in Georgia is 30-45 days.
Success rate improves dramatically with complete, professional applications.
Rolling admission programs accept applications year-round as funds deplete over time.
Quarterly deadline programs have cycles in March, June, September, and December.
Annual competitions typically have single deadlines in Q1 or Q4.
Federal pass-through programs follow federal fiscal year timing from October to September.
Pro tip is to start applications 6-8 weeks before deadlines to avoid rushing and weak proposals.
Check the Georgia Economic Development Agency website monthly for new announcements.
Set up Grants.gov email alerts for your industry and join Georgia business associations for early deadline notifications.
Avoid grant scams that charge upfront fees because legitimate programs never charge to apply.
Savannah port plus Atlanta airport provides global access, with incentives supporting distribution centers and cargo tech.
Quick Start is a discretionary program where project manager evaluation determines approval before the team designs and delivers training curriculum completely free.
Credits can offset 50-100% of state income tax liability depending on tier, with excess offsetting withholding tax in Tier 1 counties.
Most Georgia grants are actually tax credits, and only direct cash grants like REBA or EDGE are taxable income.
Retail generally does not qualify for state job tax credits in affluent areas like North Fulton, only in the most distressed Tier 1 counties.
source_title: Georgia Business Grants 2026: Georgia Job Tax Credit

facts: Bartow County is a northern Atlanta exurb located 25 miles northwest of I-285.
Bartow County has a population of 112,816 according to the July 2022 census.
Highland 75 is a 782-acre master-planned corporate/industrial park located a mile east of the I-75 Cassville-White Road exit in Cartersville.
Highland 75 is certified under the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Program, which puts construction projects on a fast track.

The Bartow-Cartersville Joint Development Authority was formed in 2004 by Cartersville and the county to split expenses and revenues between the city, the county, and the city and county school systems.
Bartow is one of only seven sole commissioner counties in Georgia, which allows for quick decision-making that helps economic development.
Steve Taylor has served as a commissioner for 13 years as of the article's publication.
The Cartersville-Bartow County Chamber of Commerce hired a workforce development director to work with companies and posts information for employers on its website.
The chamber works with Georgia Highlands College on business internships and Chattahoochee Technical College on a Project Purpose program to train high school seniors in technical skills.
In December, Hyundai Motor Group/SK On announced a joint venture to build an electric vehicle battery manufacturing facility on 600 acres several miles west of I-75 between Cartersville and Rome.
The Hyundai Motor Group/SK On joint venture project is valued at nearly $5 billion and will create 3,750 jobs.
Both Hyundai Motor Group/SK On and Qcells began site work for their facilities in the spring.
In January, Qcells announced it would build a $2.5 billion manufacturing facility on 381 acres in Highland 75, creating 2,500 jobs.
The Qcells facility is expected to open in 2025.
In March, Hanwha Advanced Materials Georgia, Inc. (HAGA) announced it would build a $147 million manufacturing facility creating 160 jobs in the park to supply the Qcells facility.
The HAGA plant is projected to open next summer.

In the last seven or eight years, about 20 million square feet of logistics/manufacturing space (Class A Industrial) has been built or planned in Adairsville, Emerson, the Cassville White corridor and Cartersville.
Approximately 10,000 jobs have been added in Bartow County over the last 10 years.
Melinda Lemmon is the executive director of the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development.
Drivers of industrial growth include proximity to Metro Atlanta, interstate access, and the nearby Appalachian Regional Port in Murray County.
In 2019, The Foxfield Co. paid about $10.6 million for 800-plus acres next to the Anheuser-Busch plant at I-75 and Cassville White Road.
Anheuser-Busch undertook a $150 million expansion in 2020.
Duluth Trading Company was scheduled to open a facility in Adairsville in the summer of 2023.
Highland 75 includes Voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc., headquartered in Linz, Austria, which supplies parts to Mercedes, BMW and Volkswagen.
Highland 75 also includes Constellium Automotive Structures N.A.
source_title: Cartersville | Bartow County: Boom Time in Bartow - Georgia Trend Magazine

It is the Voestalpine Group’s 17th production site in the United States.
As a global partner to the automotive industry, their goal is to provide optimum support for innovative light-weight solutions.

voestalpine AG executives Peter Bernscher, Oliver Kasser, Herbert Eibensteiner and Philipp Schulz posed with the first items to roll off the new high tech press at the voestalpine plant in Cartersville on Tuesday.

facts: voestalpine Automotive Components Nagold is a company of the Metal Forming Division of the voestalpine Group.
The company was acquired in 2005 and currently employs around 130 people.

Based in Germany, the company develops and produces stamped and formed parts for the automotive industry, such as radiator components, side member reinforcements and other pressed parts and assemblies.
The site also produces inline bonded stator and rotor stacks for electric motors.
The voestalpine Group has decided to sell voestalpine Automotive Components Nagold after 18 years as part of the Group for purely strategic reasons.
The sale takes into account customer expectations regarding coverage of the complete technology portfolio in electrical steel processing, which is guaranteed by the buyer.
Negotiations have been held with potential buyers for the Automotive Components plant in Nagold.
Tempel Steel (the electrical steel operations of Worthington Steel) was found as an ideal buyer for the Nagold plant to secure the site in the long term.
For Tempel Steel, the acquisition represents a strategic expansion of the portfolio.
All employees and existing technologies at the site were taken over by the buyer.
The Livingstone Duesseldorf office advised voestalpine Group on this transaction.
The terms of the transaction have not been disclosed.

Jochen Hense, Partner at Livingstone commented that they are optimistic that the ideal partner has been found and that teaming up with Tempel Steel offers a great long-term perspective for the employees, the management and the customers of voestalpine Automotive Components Nagold.
source_title: voestalpine Automotive Components Nagold finds an ideal strategic partner in Tempel Steel - Livingstone Partners

facts: voestalpine Automotive Components showcases the phs-door ring, a groundbreaking structural component combining advanced production methods, diverse materials, and engineering expertise.
The phs-door ring is a large, complex component measuring nearly two meters in length and over one meter in height.
It brings together multiple elements—such as the sill, A-pillar, B-pillar, and roof frame—into a single, fully functional module.
The design uses tailor-welded blanks with varying sheet thicknesses and localized reinforcements precisely adapted to different load zones.
voestalpine now offers a phs-scalefree coating variant for its press-hardened steels.

Customers can choose from a full range of coatings—galvanized, AlSi-coated, and scalefree—allowing maximum flexibility to balance corrosion protection, formability, and thermal resistance depending on specific design and performance requirements.
The phs-door ring's optimized material distribution significantly reduces overall vehicle weight.
Weight reduction helps automakers improve fuel efficiency and vehicle performance without compromising safety.
This lightweight design contributes to sustainability goals by minimizing material usage and lowering the product’s overall CO₂ footprint.
The component enhances crash safety through the strategic use of materials with varying strengths and ductility.
Each section of the door ring is tailored to the specific loads it must withstand, ensuring that energy is absorbed efficiently during a collision.
Integrating multiple parts into a single functional unit reduces the number of joining steps and simplifies logistics throughout the assembly process.
The A-pillar area utilizes 1000 MPa steel with high ductility for improved energy absorption.
A 2000 MPa patch plate in the roof frame enhances rigidity where it’s needed most.
Press-hardened door rings are gaining significant traction globally—especially in China and the United States—and are now entering European series production.

voestalpine's Metal Forming Division serves as its center of excellence for high-precision sections, tubes, and strip steel products, as well as for ready-to-install system components made from pressed, stamped, and roll-formed parts.
Within the voestalpine Automotive Components Group, close collaboration across all sites ensures that each location contributes its specialized skills and experience.
The phs-door ring merges safety, efficiency, and intelligent material use into a single structural element, embodying voestalpine’s strength in advanced body construction.
source_title: The phs-door ring: Redefining Automotive Body Engineering | Machine Maker - Latest Manufacturing News | Indian Manufacturing News - Latest Manufacturing News | Indian Manufacturing News - Machine Maker

facts: Volvo Cars broke ground on its first US production plant in 2015.
The S60 began rolling off the line in Ridgeville, South Carolina, in 2018.
The flagship fully electric SUV, the EX90, is assembled in the US by over 2,000 local employees for export around the world.
Two additional models will be added to production at the Ridgeville plant soon.
Quinnshona ‘Meka’ Sumpter was one of the first 100 hires when Volvo Cars began recruiting for its first US manufacturing plant.
Three of Meka Sumpter's children work alongside her across different areas of the business at the plant.
Volvo retailers employ over 10,000 people in their local communities across the United States, in addition to the over 2,500 employed by Volvo Cars’ corporate and manufacturing presence.
$1,300,000,000 has been invested in US manufacturing.
The state-of-the-art plant in Ridgeville, South Carolina, is designed to produce up to 150,000 cars per year for electric, hybrid, or gas vehicles.
In September 2025, Volvo announced it will build two new models in the US: a next-generation hybrid and the XC60.
The XC60 is now Volvo Cars’ best-selling model of all time.
The first electric Volvo came to the US in 2020 as the XC40 Recharge.
In March 2025, Volvo Cars sold its 5 millionth car in the US, an XC60 delivered in Illinois.
There are 281 Volvo retailers in 48 states.
City Safety autonomous emergency braking (AEB) technology became standard in all new Volvo models by 2016.
From 2029, the NHTSA will require AEB in all new cars sold in the US.
source_title: Celebrating Volvo in the United States

facts: Volvo aims for 200,000 U.S. retail sales by 2030 amid a product strategy shift.
With its sales in China falling sharply and Europe under pressure from a surge of Chinese imports, Volvo Cars is relying on the U.S. for growth.
Volvo Cars Americas President Luis Rezende stated, “We don’t see the U.S. as one country; we see the U.S. as 50 different countries.”
The automaker plans a hyper-regional approach to supply, incentives and marketing in the U.S.
source_title: Volvo seeks U.S. revival by targeting 60% retail sales gain - Automotive News

facts: Volvo is discontinuing its EX30 electric subcompact SUV in America after just two model years.
The decision comes as automakers adapt to tariffs, production costs and shifting EV policies.
Many car brands are focusing on more profitable gas and hybrid models as consumer interest in those vehicles grows.
Volvo will continue to sell the EX30 globally but has decided to discontinue the EV after the 2026 model year.
The EX30 joins a slew of electric vehicles sentenced to death as tariffs and production costs force automakers to adapt production strategies to a new automotive landscape.
Between the current administration's stance on electric vehicles, a lack of federal incentives and tariffs that have devastated some of the world's largest automakers, many EVs won't survive 2026.
Electric vehicles may have made an impact on the overall American market in recent years, but not all EVs are equal.
Some giants like the Tesla Model Y and 3 have become household names and daily drivers for thousands of Americans.
Other electric vehicle nameplates that haven't seen the same level of success are dead weight to many automakers as they struggle to survive an ultra-competitive market.
Volvo sold around 5,400 EX30 EVs in 2025.
Hyundai sold over 47,000 Ioniq 5 SUVs in 2025.
Tesla sold over 192,000 Model 3 electric sedan units in 2025, eclipsing the sales of competitors by thousands of units.
The 2026 Volvo EX30 starts at $40,345.
It produces 268 horsepower and 253 pound-feet of torque.
Volvo's electric SUV has up to 261 miles of standard driving range.
Volvo offers a Twin Motor Performance trim for $46,345 that produces 422 horsepower and 400 pound-feet of torque.
The Twin Motor Performance trim has 253 miles of driving range.
Volvo's EX30 is pricier than some larger electric SUVs.
Several more affordable models offer better standard driving range.
The EX30 may appeal to a small niche of EV fans, but it doesn't make enough impact to be worthwhile for the brand to continue producing.
Volvo follows several brands including Ford in discontinuing an electric vehicle nameplate that had promise, but ultimately was not sustainable to produce.
Many car brands are giving up on electric vehicle production because President Donald Trump has rolled back Biden-era EV mandates.

Since brands aren't required to produce electric vehicles, they can turn their efforts to more profitable and sustainable gas and hybrid nameplates that can be more cost-effective to produce and more appealing to American buyers.
Toyota recently made two of its best-selling models, the Camry and RAV4 standard hybrid models.
The Japanese automaker's decision to completely eliminate gas-only Camry midsize sedans and RAV4 small SUVs echoes the buying trends of consumers.
American car buyers are purchasing affordable fuel-efficient vehicles.
Tesla's sales numbers are an anomaly compared to EV sales from other brands.
Volvo's EX30 is unlikely to be the last casualty of a rapidly evolving market.
source_title: Volvo kills the EX30 electric SUV continuing EV reversal

facts: Volvo Cars' new top executive said on Thursday that the company would produce more cars in the U.S. while ramping up its regionalization efforts.
The announcement comes as a 25% U.S. tariff on global autos takes effect.

European companies are scrambling to find solutions as President Donald Trump's 25% duties on foreign cars became effective and a bigger-than-expected wall of import tariffs was unveiled on Wednesday.
Volvo Cars made an unexpected decision on Sunday to replace its CEO with former CEO Hakan Samuelsson.
The company has struggled lately, with its share price falling to record lows amid a slowdown in demand for electric vehicles and global uncertainty.

During the company's annual general meeting in Gothenburg on Thursday, investors awaited plans from Samuelsson, who led the company for more than 10 years until 2021, to revive the share price and improve the company.
Samuelsson stated that the share price has not developed as shareholders expected, indicating they are not fully invested in Volvo Cars.
He said he would look at ways to cut costs and improve strategy, which would include ramping up regionalization efforts with hubs in China, the U.S., and Europe.
Samuelsson noted that while Volvo is well prepared in China and Europe, it needs to be better in the U.S. to get around import tariffs.
He expects to ramp up production at its Charleston plant in South Carolina.
His comments mirrored those made by former CEO Jim Rowan to Reuters in March.
The company currently produces a pricier electric SUV, the EX90, in the South Carolina factory.
The South Carolina factory also produces Polestar's Model 3.
Volvo's cheaper model, the EX30, will start production soon in Europe.
Samuelsson said he thinks the next car should be some car with a high volume in the U.S., though he does not know exactly what that car would be.
He added that what might be a best-selling car today would perhaps not be the same in five years, so they have to think carefully.
Samuelsson told investors he would take some time to look at what had already been done in his absence.
He expects to return in a month or two with a turnaround plan for the company and a strategy focused on continued electrification, profitability, and regionalization.
He told investors that producing one type of car in one place and sending it across the world is a thing of the past.
source_title: Volvo Cars' new CEO vows to produce more cars in US

facts: Volvo Car USA and Canada reported sales of 138,647 cars for the full year 2024, down 2.1 percent compared with last year.
Electrified models, defined as those with a fully electric or plug-in hybrid powertrain, accounted for 35.4 percent of sales during 2024, up 22.3 percent compared to last year.
In December, Volvo Car USA and Canada sold 15,131 cars, up 2.1 percent over the same period last year.
Electrified models represented 35.7 percent of December sales, an increase of 44.5 percent compared with the same period last year.
For the full year 2024, sales in the US reached 125,243 cars, down 2.7 percent compared with the previous year.
Electrified models accounted for 34.3 percent of US sales during the year, up 20.3 percent compared to last year.
December sales in the US reached 14,077 cars, up 3.4 percent over the same period last year.
Sales of electrified models represented 33.9 percent of US December sales, an increase of 47.6 percent compared to the same period last year.
In 2024, Canada set a new annual sales record with 13,404 cars sold, up 4.2 percent compared with the previous record-setting year.
Sales of electrified models accounted for 46.1 percent of Canadian sales during the year, up 38.2 percent compared to last year.
December sales in Canada were down 12.7 percent over the same period last year, with a volume of 1,054 cars.
Sales of electrified models accounted for 59.6 percent of Canadian December sales, an increase of 24.1 percent compared to the same period last year.
In the US, the XC60 compact luxury SUV was the top-selling model with a record-setting 5,245 cars sold during December.
The XC90 midsize luxury SUV followed as the second best-selling US model in December with 5,006 cars sold.
The XC40 small luxury SUV was the third best-selling US model in December with 2,591 cars sold.
Mike Cottone, President of Volvo Car USA and Canada, stated that demand continued through the end of the year, especially for electrified models.
Volvo Car USA LLC is headquartered in Mahwah, New Jersey.
Volvo Car USA LLC is a subsidiary of Volvo Car Group of Gothenburg, Sweden (Nasdaq Stockholm VOLCAR B).
Volvo Car USA provides sales, marketing, parts, service, technology, and training support to Volvo automobile retailers in the United States since the 1950s.
Operations include a manufacturing campus in Ridgeville, SC that underlines the company’s long-term commitment to the U.S. as a key market.
VCUSA maintains offices in California and parts distribution and training centers across the country.
Volvo Car Canada Ltd. is a subsidiary of Volvo Car Group of Gothenburg, Sweden.
Volvo Car Canada provides marketing, sales, parts, service, technology, and training support to 37 Volvo automobile retailers across Canada.
For the full year 2023, Volvo Car Group recorded a record-breaking core operating profit of SEK 25.6 billion.
Revenue in 2023 amounted to an all-time high of SEK 399.3 billion.
Global sales for Volvo Car Group reached a record 708,716 cars in 2023.
Volvo Cars was founded in 1927 and sells to customers in more than 100 countries.
Volvo Cars is listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm exchange under the ticker “VOLCAR B”.
Volvo Cars' ambition is to become a fully electric car maker with a commitment to be a climate-neutral company by 2040.
As of December 2023, Volvo Cars employed approximately 43,400 full-time employees.
Volvo Cars' head office, product development, marketing, and administration functions are mainly located in Gothenburg, Sweden.
Volvo Cars' production plants are located in Gothenburg, Ghent (Belgium), South Carolina (US), Chengdu, Daqing, and Taizhou (China).
The company has R&D and design centres in Gothenburg and Shanghai (China).
source_title: Volvo Car USA and Canada reports sales of 138,647 in 2024, with strong electrified results | Volvo Cars Media US

facts: A decade after beginning manufacturing in South Carolina, Volvo Cars is on an aggressive timeline to add a fourth car to production and double its workforce.
The Sweden-based company rolled its first car off the Lowcountry assembly line in September 2018.
Volvo aims to add a new, yet-to-be-named hybrid vehicle to its lineup by 2030.
The new hybrid will be the fourth product manufactured in South Carolina, joining the fully electric EX90, the Polestar 3, and the midsize hybrid XC60 (announced in July).
Production of the S60, the first car assembled in Ridgeville, ended last summer.
With the hybrid's addition, Volvo aims to supply 50 percent of the U.S. volume of its sales from this plant.
Currently, the North America plant supplies less than 5 percent of Volvo's U.S. volume.

Francesca Gamboni, chief industrial operations officer of Volvo Cars, stated that aligning local manufacturing to customer needs benefits everyone by getting cars into customers' hands faster and more efficiently.
Gamboni noted that local manufacturing prioritizes American customers and increases flexibility to adjust volumes based on local needs.
Gamboni added that the strategy supports more local jobs and brings greater stability and consistency to the local workforce.
Volvo’s 10-year celebration and media event took place in Ridgeville on Sept. 23.

Event attendees included Gamboni, Volvo CEO Håkan Samuelsson, Luis Rezende (president of Americas for Volvo), David Stenstrom (vice president of manufacturing for the U.S.), Gov. Henry McMaster, and U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-Charleston).
The S.C. plant has a capacity to manufacture 150,000 vehicles a year but has yet to hit that milestone.
Today, vehicles take about 1.5 days to build across 240 stations.
Total current output is 100 vehicles a day, which equals about 26,000 vehicles a year.
Gamboni said the new XC60 should bring the plant to about 50-60 percent capacity.
The addition of the new hybrid will take plant capacity utilization to 100 percent.
Production expansion would double the workforce from 2,000 to 4,000 employees.
With Volvo's goal to be fully electric by 2040, the plan includes localizing more partners and suppliers to increase production and investing in technology.
CEO Håkan Samuelsson stated that investment plans reinforce Volvo's long-term commitment to the U.S. market and manufacturing operations in South Carolina.
The company is celebrating 70 years of Volvo Cars presence in the United States.
Volvo has sold over 5 million cars in the U.S. and plans to sell many more in the future.
David Stenstrom announced he will step down at the end of the week, succeeded by Miguel Tavera, the current chief supply chain officer.
To-date, Volvo has invested $1.3 billion into operations in South Carolina over the past 10 years.
Vehicles manufactured stateside are sold in the U.S. and overseas, mainly Europe.
The Charleston plant currently provides about 20 percent of vehicles for Volvo’s global sales revenue.

facts: Volvo Trucks North America recently confirmed that 11 dealership locations in the U.S. and Canada have completed sales and service training and facility upgrades to become Certified Electric Vehicle Dealerships.
The certification of Kriete Truck Centers in Wisconsin and Transport Equipment in Montana adds two new states to Volvo Trucks’ North American dealer support network for battery-electric trucks.
Volvo Trucks now has 47 certified dealership locations across 22 states and three Canadian provinces, with some locations yet to be announced publicly.
The announcement was made at the American Trucking Associations’ (ATA) 2023 Management Conference & Exhibition in Austin, Texas.

Jared Ruiz, regional vice president – West for Volvo Trucks North America, stated that the growth of the Certified EV Dealership network underscores the company's commitment to providing customers with resources to adopt battery-electric vehicles beyond just selling the truck.
Earlier this year, Volvo announced it had tripled the number of certified dealerships in just one year.
The rapid growth of the dealership network is ongoing with these 11 new locations and more than 45 others in the process of completing certification.

New certified dealership locations include Coffman Truck Sales in Aurora, Illinois; Gerry’s Truck Centre in London, Ontario; McMahon Truck Centers in Columbus, Ohio; M&K Truck Centers in Summit, Illinois; Mid-Ontario Truck Center in Maple, Ontario; Nextran Truck Center in Kansas City, Missouri; Northwest Equipment Sales in Boise, Idaho; Kriete Truck Centers in Madison, Wisconsin; S&S Truck Sales in Lima, Ohio; TransEdge Truck Centers in Allentown, Pennsylvania; and Transport Equipment in Missoula, Montana.
The Certified EV Dealership program ensures a robust sales and service ecosystem required to support customers with the commercial deployment of Class 8 battery-electric trucks.

Each dealership location boasts a team of highly trained sales professionals who assist customers with vehicle configuration selection, securing available incentive funding, and identifying ideal routes for electrification.

Sales teams at Volvo Trucks Certified EV Dealers have access to route planning and connected technology tools designed to help customers maximize the performance capabilities and benefits of the zero-tailpipe emission Volvo VNR Electric model.

Service technicians at certified locations are fully trained to perform battery-electric truck maintenance and repairs using diagnostics tools and personal protective equipment necessary for high-voltage systems.
Certified EV Dealers support the Volvo Gold Contract, a premier turnkey service offering for the Volvo VNR Electric model designed to ensure peak vehicle uptime, performance, and productivity.
Dealers maintain a stock of key parts and components for the Volvo VNR Electric model to minimize service times and quickly return customers to operation.

The certified dealer network spans 22 U.S. states (Alabama, California, Delaware, Florida, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Missouri, Montana, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, and Wisconsin) and three Canadian provinces (British Columbia, Ontario, and Quebec).
Volvo Trucks is on track to certify 46 additional dealerships in 2023 and 2024, which will bring the total network to 37 U.S. states and four Canadian provinces.

entity_name: Volvo XC90 & Federal EV Tax Credit Policy
facts: The federal EV/PHEV tax credit expired for vehicles purchased after September 30, 2025.
Before expiration, the XC90 Recharge/T8 plug-in hybrid historically qualified for a partial or full federal credit of up to $7,500 in some years.
The gas/mild-hybrid XC90 (B5/B6) never qualified for the EV credit because it is not a plug-in vehicle.
Buyers financing a new, U.S.-assembled model may qualify for a deduction on up to $10,000 in annual car loan interest.
The Volvo XC90 is assembled in South Carolina, which qualifies it for the new car loan interest deduction.
Only buyers with auto loans can claim the interest deduction; leases or cash purchases do not qualify.
The purchase must be after December 31, 2024, and the deduction phases out starting at $100,000 modified adjusted gross income for single filers or $200,000 for joint filers.
A 2026 buying-guide analysis estimated that a buyer financing over $50,000 at 5%-plus interest could see more than $2,500 in tax savings from the interest deduction alone.
Volvo upgraded the XC90 Recharge’s battery to 18.8 kWh with up to 35 miles of electric-only range starting with the 2023 model year.
Most XC90s were historically assembled in Torslanda, Sweden, which complicated eligibility under the North American assembly requirement that applied to the EV credit before its expiration.
Some states still offer their own EV/PHEV rebates independent of federal policy, though amounts and eligibility vary widely.
States like California, New York, and Colorado have offered rebates for plug-in hybrids independent of federal policy.
Section 179 business deduction is separate from the EV credit and is based on GVWR, not powertrain.
source_title: Does The Volvo XC90 Qualify For A Tax Credit ?

facts: Swedish truckmaker Volvo expects trucking demand in North America to remain under pressure into next year.
Weak freight activity and U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs weigh on the North American market.
The Gothenburg-based group also makes construction equipment and engines.
Volvo lowered its forecast for the North American truck market while keeping its European view unchanged.
Volvo expects heavy truck deliveries of around 265,000 in North America this year, 10,000 fewer than previously envisioned.
Volvo expects to deliver some 290,000 vehicles in Europe.
The company’s shares fell 8% by 0935 GMT, touching their lowest price in six months.
The North American long-haul freight market remains in recession, with lower freight volumes and prices.
Customers are holding back orders amid uncertainty over new emissions rules and tariffs.
CEO Martin Lundstedt stated transport buyers are in a wait-and-see mode as uncertainty clouds market conditions.
Europe truck demand is largely replacement-driven, supported by stable fleet utilisation.
Equity analysts from Jefferies see a potential pickup later in 2026 due to normalized inventory levels and back-end-loaded demand.
Volvo’s adjusted operating profit fell around 17% to 11.7 billion Swedish crowns ($1.2 billion) in the third quarter, matching the average forecast in an LSEG poll of analysts.
Trump’s move to slap a 25% tariff on imported heavy trucks has deepened trade uncertainty across North America.
The USMCA trade agreement still shields most manufacturers from fresh duties.
Volvo Group is best positioned among European truckmakers to weather trade barriers because it builds all trucks destined for the U.S. market domestically, along with most of its components.
Volvo runs 16 manufacturing and remanufacturing sites across the U.S., Canada and Mexico.
Volvo makes vehicles under brands such as Mack Trucks and Renault as well as its own name.
source_title: Volvo Group expects North America weakness through 2026 amid freight slump - SRN News

entity_name: Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Corridor Development Authority
canonical_name: Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Corridor Development Authority
facts: The Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Corridor Development Authority filed an application with the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers for a 1,904.5-acre project site to construct a mega-site manufacturing facility at Highway 280 and I-16.
The site application requests authorizations from both the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers and the state of Georgia.
The application is believed to be part of a bid to secure Volvo’s first U.S. auto plant.
Volvo, described as a Swedish-based, Chinese-owned car maker, announced in late March that it would spend $500 million to build a manufacturing facility in the U.S.
The Atlanta Journal-Constitution reported that Volvo is considering both the Georgia coast and South Carolina for the site.
Last month, Georgia lawmakers approved more than $17 million to build a manufacturing training center in Pooler.

The application to the Savannah District of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers was filed by Joint Development Authority representative Hugh “Trip” Tollison through agent Alton Brown Jr. of Resource and Land Consultants.
Bryan County Development Authority Chairman Derrick Smith declined to comment on the application or whether Volvo is considering Bryan County.
Derrick Smith serves on the regional authority alongside County Commission Chairman Jimmy Burnsed.
The joint corridor development authority includes members from Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham counties.
The authority was formed in the wake of the announced deepening of the Savannah Harbor.
The authority's reported aim is to work on projects of a regional nature too big for any single county-sized authority to handle.
A spokesman for the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers stated there is no timeline on permit approval.
source_title: 1,900-acre manufacturing facility sought for I-16 megasite - Coastal Courier

facts: Wabash National L.P. operates a facility in Griffin, GA.
The facility's parent company is Wabash National Corp.
The site operates in the Transportation Equipment sector under NAICS code 336211.
The facility files annual Form R disclosures to the EPA Toxics Release Inventory under EPCRA Section 313.
Cumulative toxic releases reported across the facility from 2019 through 2023 total 54 lbs across 4 distinct chemicals.
One of the four reported chemicals, Nickel, is classified as a carcinogen, representing 25% of the facility's chemical profile.
Air releases account for 100% of the facility's total toxic releases, totaling 54 lbs.
Reported release totals decreased by 38% between 2019 and 2023, dropping from 21 lbs to 13 lbs.

In 2019, the facility reported 21 lbs of releases across 4 chemicals; in 2020, 7 lbs across 4 chemicals; in 2021, 8 lbs across 4 chemicals; in 2022, 5 lbs across 4 chemicals; and in 2023, 13 lbs across 4 chemicals.
Manganese accounted for the highest cumulative release volume at 34 lbs between 2019 and 2023.
Chromium accounted for 14 lbs of cumulative releases between 2019 and 2023.
Nickel accounted for 6 lbs of cumulative releases between 2019 and 2023.
Diisocyanates accounted for 0 lbs of cumulative releases between 2019 and 2023.
TRI Form R values are self-reported estimates rather than measured stack emissions and treat every pound of reported chemical equally regardless of toxicity.

Inclusion in the EPA Toxic Release Inventory database does not establish a health risk to nearby residents but documents annual reporting compliance under federal community right-to-know requirements.
The facility's reported pounds and chemical counts are 100% less than one comparison metric and 85% less than another when stacked up against nearby facilities or typical reporters.
source_title: Wabash National L.P. EPA Record - Griffin, GA | Toxic Releases & Compliance

facts: Wabash National Corp. received orders exceeding $70 million from J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. for 6,000 53-foot DuraPlate domestic containers and a smaller number of container chassis.
The 53-foot domestic containers feature a new design utilizing Wabash National's proprietary DuraPlate technology.
The stackable, high-cube design includes an uninterrupted sidewall and interior dimensions that closely resemble conventional trailers.
These will be the first 53-foot units in the J.B. Hunt fleet with sufficient strength to be positioned on the bottom of a "double-stack" on railcars.
Richard E. Dessimoz, acting CEO of Wabash, stated pleasure that J.B. Hunt selected the DuraPlate container for intermodal operations alongside the DuraPlate trailer for over-the-road operations.
Wabash said the containers incorporate features designed to reduce maintenance costs and increase durability.
These will be the first production domestic containers to use the DuraPlate sidewall material.
The DuraPlate material is composed of a polyethylene plastic core sandwiched between two sheets of pre-painted high-strength galvanized steel.
Wabash claims the resulting panel is efficient, lightweight, and incredibly strong, providing advantages over aluminum plate and traditional sheet and post sidewall designs.

Kirk Thompson, president & CEO of J.B. Hunt Transportation Services, expressed delight with the performance and quality of their DuraPlate van trailers and noted the opportunity to convert their intermodal container fleet to exclusively 53-foot equipment by replacing 48-foot equipment with stackable 53-foot DuraPlate containers.

Thompson stated that J.B. Hunt’s intermodal customers will have access to North America's largest fleet of exclusively 53-foot containers, while rail partners can stack them on top in traditional configurations or at the bottom of 53-foot rail cars.
With the addition of DuraPlate domestic containers, Wabash now offers a complete line of intermodal equipment including domestic containers, piggyback trailers, and bi-modal equipment.

facts: Wabash, a leader in end-to-end supply chain solutions for the transportation, logistics and infrastructure markets, announced a significant expansion of its network in the Southeast.

Wabash is strengthening customer access and service in the greater Atlanta region with three strategic growth moves: opening a new Parts & Service center, expanding its dealer relationship with Fleetco and adding CS Truck and Trailer to its Preferred Partner Network.
Dave Hill, vice president of Parts & Services at Wabash, said these moves reflect how Wabash is building an extensive partner ecosystem that puts parts, service and support closer to customers.
Hill stated that together, the Atlanta expansions mean faster turnaround, more coverage and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina and Tennessee.
Wabash’s new Atlanta-area Parts and Services center is strategically located at the heart of the Southeast’s trucking corridor.
The center provides faster access to parts, service and truck body upfitting.
It will offer Wabash’s Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications with rapid turnaround times for in-stock chassis.
The Atlanta site joins Wabash service centers in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania and Texas, with additional locations on the way.
Doors to the new Atlanta Parts & Service center opened September 18, 2025.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market with trailer sales.
This expansion broadens Wabash’s dealer footprint in the Southeast and gives customers additional access to new and used equipment through a trusted partner.
Bill Tirrill, president at Fleetco Inc., said they are proud to grow their existing partnership with Wabash in the greater Atlanta market.

Tirrill noted that with more than 14 years of experience working closely with Wabash, the expansion allows Fleetco to expand its sales force across the region and provide clients immediate access to new and used trailers.
Fleetco will improve the expertise and commitment clients expect from the Fleetco/Wabash team and create more opportunities to serve clients.
Wabash added three CS Truck and Trailer locations to its Preferred Partner Network in Georgia.
The addition brings expert service, maintenance and Wabash Genuine Parts to more customers through authorized third-party dealerships.
The Preferred Partner Network model is central to Wabash’s strategy of making high-quality parts more accessible through a seamless national network.
Hill added that expanding the dealer and partner network is critical to delivering the speed and service customers expect as their customer base grows.

Atlanta serves as an example of how Wabash is executing its strategy by combining service centers, dealer partners, and the Preferred Partner Network to increase coverage and elevate the customer experience.

Wabash (NYSE: WNC) is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana, and designs, manufactures, and services dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
Wabash enables customers to thrive by providing insight into tomorrow and delivering pragmatic solutions today to move everything from first to final mile.

facts: Wabash (NYSE: WNC) has been named to Newsweek’s list of America’s Most Responsible Companies for the third year.
Wabash is the only trailer or truck body manufacturer on the 2025 list.
Newsweek and market research company Statista compile this annual list to recognize companies committed to making a positive global impact.
Companies are ranked on the list based on their scores in the three pillars of ESG: environmental, social and corporate governance.
Wabash President and CEO Brent Yeagy stated that fostering safe and inclusive workplaces, driving innovation for sustainable transportation, and giving back to communities are core company values.
In 2024, Wabash received two Plant Safety Awards from the Truck Trailer Manufacturers Association for its manufacturing operations in San José Iturbide, Mexico, and Cadiz, Kentucky.
At its Cadiz, Kentucky facility, a commitment to the "Respect for People" ethos helped lead to an 81 percent reduction in the facility’s total recordable incident rate from 2022 to 2024.
Wabash is leveraging targeted automation at its Lafayette, Indiana facility to optimize safety and the overall worker experience.
The Lafayette, Indiana facility reopened in 2023 after a significant renovation.
Wabash is the only trailer and truck body manufacturer supporting a new U.S. Department of Energy project that aims to help decarbonize commercial transportation.
The DOE project will use Wabash’s proprietary EcoNex™ Technology, a composite material designed to enhance thermal efficiency and reduce energy consumption.
The technology aims to integrate high-efficiency solar energy into refrigerated trailers and truck bodies.
Wabash designs, manufactures, and services an extensive range of products including dry and refrigerated trailers, truck bodies, platform trailers, tank trailers, and structural composites.
Through the Wabash Marketplace and Wabash Parts, customers gain access to a nationwide parts and service network.
Wabash offers Trailers as a Service (TaaS)℠ and advanced tools designed to streamline operations.
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.

facts: Wabash National Corp. is acquiring Supreme Industries Inc. (NYSE: STS), a supplier of commercial vehicles and related components, for approximately $364 million.
The deal is intended to advance Wabash National into the final mile, or the last leg, of the delivery process.
Supreme was founded in 1974 and is a U.S. manufacturer of light and medium-duty truck bodies.
Supreme is based in Goshen, Indiana, and operates seven facilities throughout the United States.
Supreme is one of the largest U.S. truck body suppliers, with reported sales of $299 million in 2016.
Supreme serves a range of customers including national rental fleets, national and regional leasing companies, truck dealers, and fleet operators.

Wabash National CEO Dick Giromini stated: “This is a great opportunity for both companies to combine our strengths to provide an enhanced customer experience within the growing final mile delivery space.”
Wabash National is based in Lafayette, Indiana, and manufactures semi-trailer and liquid transportation systems.
Wabash National sells its products across North America.
With Supreme, Wabash National will accelerate organic growth and offer a broader conventional product offering.
Wabash National has been closely monitoring the transportation landscape as e-commerce growth continues to change the logistics model.

Other recent truck and auto-related deals include Federal Signal Corp.’s agreement to buy Truck Bodies and Equipment International, Accuride Corp.’s purchase of parts manufacturer KIC LLC, Monroe Truck Equipment Inc.’s acquisition of equipment maker Towmaster Inc., and W.W. Williams Co. LLC’s deal to buy parts provider Auto Safety House LLC.
Baird is serving as financial adviser to Supreme.
Haynes and Boone LLP is acting as legal counsel for the transaction.

facts: Wabash National Corporation reported a challenging first quarter on May 1, 2026, with significant operating losses and revenue declines that exceeded analyst expectations for weakness.
The trailer and truck body manufacturer posted an adjusted loss per share of $(1.17), missing the consensus forecast of $(1.01) by nearly 16%.
Revenue of $303 million fell short of the $319 million estimate.
Shares plunged 15.88% to $7.32, approaching the 52-week low of $6.90.
The stock had already declined 2.99% in premarket trading following the earnings release.

Management expressed building confidence in the outlook, citing anticipated favorable shifts in industry dynamics from Section 232 tariff calculation changes and anti-dumping/countervailing duty rulings expected in late 2026 and into 2027.
Wabash reported consolidated revenue of $303 million for the first quarter.
New shipments totaled 5,378 trailers and 1,527 truck bodies, down from 6,290 and 3,000 units respectively in the prior-year period.
The company recorded a GAAP operating loss of $52.4 million and an operating margin of negative 17.3%.
On a non-GAAP adjusted basis, which excludes facility idling costs and purchase accounting gains, the operating loss widened to $55.5 million with a margin of negative 18.3%.
Net loss attributable to common stockholders reached $45.2 million on a GAAP basis, or $47.5 million adjusted.
CEO Brent Yeagy highlighted five key takeaways in the presentation, acknowledging near-term headwinds while emphasizing strategic positioning for recovery.
The company pointed to Parts & Services revenue growth exceeding 4% year-over-year as evidence of its counter-cyclical infrastructure enhancing resiliency across market cycles.
Revenue in the Transportation Solutions segment declined 28% year-over-year to $250 million.
The adjusted operating margin for the Transportation Solutions segment deteriorated sharply to negative 13.8% from negative 2.8% in the prior-year quarter.
Parts & Services revenue grew modestly to $54 million from $52 million, but experienced dramatic margin compression.
The Parts & Services segment’s adjusted operating margin fell to negative 4.1% from a healthy positive 13.3% in Q1 2025.
New trailer shipments were down 14.5% and truck body shipments plunged 49% year-over-year.
The company used $33.7 million in operating activities compared to just $0.3 million in the prior-year period.
Free cash flow was negative $37.3 million versus negative $29.1 million in Q1 2025.
Capital allocation during the quarter totaled just $7.6 million, with $3.4 million directed to traditional capex, minimal spending on revenue-generating assets, and $3.5 million in dividends.
Share repurchases were suspended entirely given the challenging operating environment.

Management emphasized strategic initiatives designed to position the company for recovery amid anticipated changes to Section 232 tariff calculations and favorable anti-dumping/countervailing duty rulings expected in the second half of 2026 and into 2027.

CEO Yeagy highlighted the company’s focus on digital enablement and operational efficiency, stating they are focused on scaling digital capabilities and expect significant opportunities from recent advancements in AI technology.
The company achieved new safety and efficiency benchmarks in Q1 2026 despite volume challenges.

The expansion of the upfit business represents another strategic priority as Wabash seeks to capture more value-added services and diversify revenue streams beyond traditional trailer and truck body manufacturing.
Wabash provided guidance for the second quarter calling for sequential improvement, with revenue expected in the range of $380 million to $400 million.
The Q2 revenue midpoint of $390 million represents nearly 29% growth from Q1 levels.
The company anticipates an operating margin of approximately negative 5% for Q2 2026, a substantial improvement from the negative 18.3% adjusted margin in Q1.
Adjusted earnings per share are projected between $(0.40) and $(0.60), with a midpoint of $(0.50).
Management expects continued improvement in Q3 and Q4 2026 as production volumes increase, operational efficiencies take hold, and the benefits of favorable trade policy changes begin to materialize.
Analysts maintain a consensus full-year 2026 EPS forecast of $(1.27), suggesting expectations for continued losses throughout the year.
The company’s outlook assumes traditional capex spending of $4 million to $7 million in Q2, with SG&A expenses of $40 million to $45 million and an effective tax rate of 25%.
Analysts expressed skepticism about the company’s ability to achieve its guidance amid persistent market softness during the Q&A session.
Questions from analysts centered on supply chain challenges, the timeline for demand recovery, and execution risks associated with strategic initiatives.
Analyst price targets range from $11 to $18, suggesting potential upside of 50% to 146% from current levels around $7.32.
The consensus view is that Wabash will not return to profitability in 2026, with recovery likely pushed into 2027 as industry conditions normalize and trade policy benefits materialize.
The company’s position near its 52-week low has attracted some value-oriented interest, though concerns about the depth and duration of the current industry downcycle continue to weigh on sentiment.
Substantial year-over-year comparisons are complicated by a $342 million Missouri legal matter gain recorded in Q1 2025, which distorts operating performance metrics.
Investors will closely monitor shipment volumes, margin trends, and evidence of anticipated sequential improvements in coming quarters.
The company’s ability to maintain its dividend at $0.08 per share while burning cash will face scrutiny as the year progresses.
source_title: Wabash Q1 2026 slides: heavy losses deepen, tariff relief eyed By Investing.com

facts: WIKA USA’s relationship with one of the largest energy providers in the country has grown from gas analyzers to a future calibration lab for SF6 testing equipment.
A major utility company in the South was seeking a reliable vendor for gas analyzers.
The utility's parent company already had an excellent relationship with WEgrid Solutions, a wholly owned WIKA subsidiary.
The utility uses WIKA's full-trailer solution: GPU-S-3000 gas cart, adapters, gas analyzers, hoses, and leak detectors.
Utility executives recommended that the utility reach out to WIKA regarding its issues.
WEgrid proposed stocking spare parts for the GA11 gas analyzer for as long as the utility uses this model.
WEgrid proposed giving plenty of advance notice before upgrading the technology.
WEgrid proposed that existing customers will receive preferential pricing when a new model is introduced.
The electric utility company switched to WEgrid in November 2019.
The GA11 and GIR-10 leak detector satisfied all of the customer’s requirements for performance.
WEgrid Services provided maintenance, repairs, and spare parts as necessary.
WIKA USA’s Lawrenceville (Georgia) headquarters rearranged its production floor to accommodate a large space for calibrating and repairing SF6 testing equipment.
The state-of-the-art calibration facility is expected to be operational in the near future.
The parent company previously had to send its SF6 testing equipment to Europe for calibration, which takes four to six weeks.
Having a lab nearby would greatly reduce downtime.
WEgrid’s partnership with this major utility holding company is based on high-quality SF6 gas equipment and superior after-sale support.
Some of the country’s largest electric utilities rely on WEgrid Solutions for quality SF6 equipment and trusted service.
The new US-based calibration lab will cut turnaround time in half compared to sending equipment to Europe.

facts: A record 830,000 automobiles moved through the Port of Brunswick south of Savannah in the 2024 fiscal year.
The combined number of auto and heavy machinery units handled by Brunswick and the Port of Savannah topped 876,000 in the fiscal year ending June 30.
That volume represents an increase of 21% over the same period a year ago.
Georgia Ports Authority CEO Griff Lynch called it “a great year for us.”
The number of cars and light trucks being shipped through the Port of Brunswick has increased significantly in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.
U.S. auto sales saw their biggest increase in a decade in 2023.
Georgia was investing $262 million in upgrades and expansions in Brunswick to make room for growth.
The Brunswick upgrade projects are almost complete and should be finished by fall.
Griff Lynch predicted last October that automobile volumes at Brunswick would surpass the Port of Baltimore by 2026.
Port officials in Maryland reported that Baltimore handled 847,000 auto imports and exports in the 2023 calendar year.
Baltimore’s shipping channel shut down completely for weeks following the deadly collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge on March 26.
The waterway reopened in phases before being fully cleared in June.

Maryland Port Administration spokesperson Richard Sher stated total automobile volumes for the Port of Baltimore over the past 12 months are not yet available because some auto terminals at the port are privately operated and do not report volumes until the end of the calendar year.
When the bridge collapse forced auto shipments to be diverted from Baltimore, the Port of Brunswick received about 14,000 of those cars and trucks in April and May.

Griff Lynch noted Georgia’s big gains in the past year largely resulted from automakers shifting their business to Brunswick from other neighboring ports such as Charleston, South Carolina, and Jacksonville, Florida.
Georgia’s push to become a Southern hub for electric vehicle production could send more autos across Brunswick’s docks.
Hyundai plans to open its first U.S. plant dedicated to EVs west of Savannah before the year ends.
Griff Lynch expects the Hyundai factory to focus initially on vehicles for the U.S. market and noted that in the early years they would not be exporting a lot of cars.
The Port of Savannah handled 5.25 million container units in the latest fiscal year, down 2.3% from fiscal 2023.
The Port of Savannah is the fourth-busiest U.S. port for cargo shipped in containers.
Container volumes lagged in the last six months of 2023 as retailers with overstuffed inventories scaled back new orders, but started to rebound in recent months.
source_title: A Georgia seaport is closing the gap with Baltimore, the top US auto port | AP News

facts: WIKA USA established the Ursula Wiegand Scholarship Fund in 2002 to recognize the accomplishments of its employees’ children.
The scholarship is awarded annually to high academic achievers and community leaders who are or will be enrolled in full-time undergraduate studies.
In 2019, 26 exemplary students received the Ursula Wiegand Scholarship.
Professional development, training, and ongoing education for its entire workforce are key values at WIKA USA.
The scholarship is merit-based and extends WIKA USA’s commitment to learning to employees’ families.
Former WIKA President Michael Gerster established the Ursula Wiegand Scholarship Fund in 2002.
Recipients are selected based on their academic record and community involvement, requiring supporting documentation that includes a personal statement and teacher recommendations.
The scholarship is named after Ursula Wiegand, one of WIKA’s founders and the first woman to receive the Business Owner of the Year award in Germany in 1984.
Each year, WIKA USA provides a one-time award ranging from $500 to $1,000 per person to high-achieving students.
Applicants must be children of WIKA USA employees and enrolled or planning to enroll in a full-time undergraduate program at an accredited two-year, four-year, or technical postsecondary institution.
The application process begins in the spring, with winners announced in the summer.
The award is intended to help students cover costs for tuition, books, lab fees, or other educational expenses.
Since its inception 17 years ago, more than 300 students have received the Ursula Wiegand Scholarship.
Employees’ children are permitted to apply each year of their undergraduate studies, and several have received the scholarship multiple times.
No WIKA USA employees participate in selecting winners; an independent organization handles all application and selection phases.
The 2019 recipients represented 18 different technical schools, community colleges, colleges, and universities.
Recipients’ fields of study ranged from business and online marketing to cybersecurity and materials science engineering.
Twelve scholarship winners, along with their families and WIKA management, attended a July 24 awards luncheon.
Brent Shadix serves as Executive Vice President Human Resources at WIKA USA.
Brent Shadix described the scholarship as an investment in future leaders for both the company and society, aiming to support tomorrow’s workforce across all industries.
The program also recognizes WIKA employees at its Lawrenceville (Georgia) and Pasadena (Texas) locations who have raised outstanding children.

facts: The Vehicle Foams Market constitutes a critical segment within the broader automotive materials landscape, encompassing foam-based solutions engineered for vibration damping, thermal insulation, acoustic absorption, and interior cushioning.

The proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs) and autonomous driving technologies introduces new design paradigms that demand specialized foam materials with superior thermal management and electromagnetic shielding properties.
Regulatory frameworks aimed at reducing vehicle weight for fuel efficiency and emissions compliance are compelling manufacturers to adopt lightweight foam composites.
Companies like BASF and Covestro have pioneered bio-based polyurethane foams that meet stringent environmental standards while delivering superior damping properties.
Major corporations like Dow, BASF, and Huntsman dominate the market through strategic acquisitions, joint ventures, and technological leadership.

The transition toward electric vehicles necessitates foams with enhanced thermal management and electromagnetic interference shielding, prompting material scientists to develop multifunctional composites.
Stricter emissions and recyclability standards are compelling manufacturers to adopt bio-based and recyclable foam formulations, aligning with global sustainability agendas.
Generative AI is being deployed to rapidly simulate and optimize foam formulations tailored to specific vehicle requirements, drastically reducing R&D cycles and time-to-market.
Key companies profiled in the market include Woodbridge Foam Corp, Dow Chemical Co, Adient Plc, Recticel, Caligen Foam, Saint-Gobain SA, BASF SE, Lear Corp, Zotefoams Plc, and Bridgestone Corp.
The market is segmented by vehicle type including Passenger Cars, Commercial Vehicles, Electric Vehicles (EVs), Luxury Vehicles, and Heavy-Duty Trucks.
Automakers like Tesla and Nissan are investing heavily in specialized foam solutions that prevent thermal runaway and improve battery longevity for EVs.
Luxury brands like Mercedes-Benz and BMW are deploying high-density viscoelastic foams in door panels and seat cushions to enhance acoustic insulation.
OEMs like Waymo and Uber are investing in vehicle architectures that incorporate specialized foams to protect sensitive electronics and enhance passenger safety for autonomous vehicles.
Ford’s recent launch of bio-based seat cushions demonstrates the industry’s move toward greener materials.
BMW’s use of lightweight foam composites in interior panels has contributed to a 10% reduction in vehicle weight.
Companies like 3M and BASF are pioneering conductive and piezoelectric foams embedded with sensors for real-time structural health monitoring.

The market faces restraints including raw material volatility, high manufacturing costs, stringent environmental regulations, and technological challenges associated with developing multifunctional, sustainable foams.
Environmental policies aimed at reducing VOC emissions, phasing out halogenated flame retardants, and promoting recyclability impose strict constraints on foam formulations.

The Vehicle Foams Market is characterized by a complex interplay of technological innovations, evolving regulatory landscapes, shifting consumer preferences, and supply chain dynamics that collectively influence its trajectory from 2026 through 2034.
Polyurethane foam is highlighted as a key segment within the market classification.
source_title: Global Vehicle Foams Market Size, Share, Industry Trends & Global Forecast 2026-2034

facts: Inoac Corporation develops urethane, rubber, plastics, and composite materials.
The company delivers automotive interior and exterior decorative parts, industrial materials for interior products, cosmetic containers, and housing materials.
The company has established joint ventures with Woodbridge Foam based in Canada, and a Chinese supplier Ningbo Huaxiang Electronic Company.
Inoac Corporation is an unlisted company.
Products include interior parts such as instrument panels, energy absorption parts, engine covers, molded ceilings, air-intake ducts, and pillar garnishes.
Seat parts produced include armrests, seat cushions, and head rests.
Molding parts include window moldings, roof moldings, aero spats, side mudguards, mudguards, and rear spoilers.

Functional parts include air intake ducts/resonators, sound deadening parts for resonator, steering column hole covers, hood seals/door weatherstrip, sealing, door cushions, plug holes/packing, and Cellasto (Suspention).
The company provides material solutions for EV battery.
In 1926, the company established the Inoue Gomu Seizousyo to produce bicycle tire tubes.
In 1934, it started overseas operations by exporting the IRC Brand tire tube to Southeast Asia.
In 1938, it incorporated Inoue Rubber Co., Ltd.
In 1954, it established MTP Kasei, becoming the first company in Japan to manufacture and sell polyurethane foam.
In 1955, it adopted ceiling materials and heat insulating materials for engine compartments.
In 1960, it started to produce vacuum formed polyvinyl chloride sheet products.
In 1968, it adopted plastic molds for automobiles.
In 1979, it started to produce plastic bumpers (PP).
In 1980, it established Inoue MTP Co. Ltd. through a merger of Inoue Rubber and MTP Kasei, launched the "INOAC" brand, and established Inoue Cellotechs as a joint venture with Jim Walter Inc.
In 1984, it adopted instrument panels using powder slash molds.
In 1987, it started to produce blow molded spoilers.
In 1988, it started to produce Beneron sheets domestically by entering into a technical partnership with Benecke-Kaiko Group.
In June 1990, the company changed its name to Inoac Corp.
In 1994, it started to produce instrument panels using HPI Technology.
In 1995, it established Kansai Inoac and Chita Inoac.
In 1996, it started to produce instrument panels integrated with airbag doors.
In 1997, Seino Inoac was renamed Inoac Elastomer Co., Ltd.
In 1998, it established IW foam corporation, a joint venture company with Woodbridge of Canada, to manufacture automobile cushions.
In 1999, it established BASF Inoac polyurethane.
In 2000, it established SAI Automotive SAI Japan KK, a joint venture company, with Faurecia of France.

In 2001, it established a joint venture company with RECTiCEL S.A. of Belgium to manufacture automobile interior parts, and established Inoac Tokai (Thailand) Co., Ltd. to produce medium-pressure and low pressure hoses in Thailand.

In 2004, it established Tianjin Inoac Polymer Co., Ltd. in China to manufacture and sell automotive components, and established Inoac Automotive (Thailand) Co., Ltd. in Thailand to manufacture automotive resin products.

In 2005, it established the Toyohashi Plant in Aichi Prefecture as an integrated production base for headrests with a seamless production structure including cutting of headrest cover through finishing.

In 2006, it established Dongguan Inoac Polymer Co., Ltd. in China to manufacture high-performance urethane foam, and established Inoac (Thailand) Co., Ltd. in Thailand to supervise the Group's affiliated companies in Asia.

In Nov. 2007, the company and Woodbridge Foam of Canada signed a joint-venture contract to collaborate in the automotive seats urethane foam business, with Woodbridge Foam acquiring 50 percent of the share capital in Kyusyu Inoac Co., Ltd., while the company obtained 50 percent of the share capital in Saltillo Interior Trim S.A. de C.V.
In Nov. 2007, it established the Kitakami Plant in Iwate Prefecture as an integrated production base for automotive interior and exterior components.
In 2008, it established Saltillo Interior Trim S.A. de C.V. in Mexico to manufacture foam products.
In 2009, it established Tianjin Inoac Huaxiang Automobile Produces in Tianjin, China.
In 2011, it acquired 100% of the shares in Inoac Exterior Products LLC based in North America to enhance its automotive spoiler business.
In 2012, it established ABC Inoac Exterior Systems in USA and Canada to produce automotive exterior components.
In 2013, it started series production at Inoac Polymer Lanka (PUT) Ltd. in Sri Lanka, initially producing urethane form for the apparel business in India.
In 2014, it established the general foundation named the "Polyurethane International Technology Promotion Foundation".
In 2015, it started volume production on a new foaming line at INOAC (THAILAND) CO., LTD.
In 2016, it started volume production on foaming lines at INOAC VIETNEM CO., LTD. and PT. INOAC POLYTECHNO INDONESIA, and established INOAC LIVING VIETNAM CO., LTD.
In 2017, it established Inoac Ryukyu Co., Ltd. as the group's first company in Okinawa prefecture.
In 2021, it built a new production plant at PT Inoac Polytechno Indonesia.
source_title: Inoac Corporation - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Woodbridge, a key supplier of automotive foam, will spend $100-million on a modernization project and centralize its global research and development at a facility in Ontario.

Woodbridge Foam Corp. of Mississauga, Ont., will receive up to $20 million from the federal government’s Strategic Innovation Fund to help pay for the upgrades at facilities in the Ontario towns of Woodbridge and Blenheim.
The R&D centre will be located in Woodbridge.
Minister of Innovation, Science and Economic Development Navdeep Bains announced the funding on Tuesday morning.

The supplier will modernize two of its plants with state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and create a Centre of Excellence to centralize global R&D activities in advanced materials and product design of molded foam products.
The molded foam products include car seats and armrests.
Woodbridge says the investments will help the company better meet market demand for products and secure new business.
The upgrades and R&D centre will create 110 jobs and maintain 727 jobs.
Woodbridge CEO Charles Daly stated: “This significant investment will result in world-class manufacturing capabilities and accelerated innovation in our technology advantage.”
Charles Daly added: “We view this as an investment in our global competitiveness…as we prepare for the future of a rapidly changing global automotive marketplace.”
Woodbridge Foam Corp. started with a single Canadian plant in 1978.
The company now operates 67 facilities around the world.
The company specializes in the manufacturing of foam polymers and molding for the auto industry.
While its corporate headquarters are in Mississauga, its automotive headquarters are in Troy, Mich.
source_title: Woodbridge Upgrades - Everchem Specialty Chemicals

The announcement was made jointly by the company and the Canadian government.
The announcement date is July 11, 2019.
