facts: An $18 million dollar investment will bring 130 new jobs to Laurens County.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced Woory Industrial Company will build a new automotive parts manufacturing facility in Dublin.
Governor Brian Kemp stated, “We're proud to welcome Woory Industrial as the latest job creator to help us on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation,”

Governor Brian Kemp noted that the emerging industry continues to bring historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities all over the state, thanks to a highly-skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network.
Governor Brian Kemp expressed confidence that Woory will have a long and beneficial partnership with Dublin-Laurens County.
Woory was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the automotive industry.
Woory maintains traditional product lines but is also concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated that the company is facing a revolution in the automobile industry for the first time in 100 years.
Jungwoo Kim noted that the transition to electric vehicles is the future and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.
Jungwoo Kim stated that over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
Jungwoo Kim said Georgia will be a good opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development.

Jungwoo Kim expressed confidence that Woory's experience and technology will contribute to the prosperity of the community and appreciated the partnership of the State of Georgia and the City of Dublin.
The new facility will be located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin and will be the company's first U.S. manufacturing facility.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that fostering quality and sustainable growth is a goal of the City of Dublin.

Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight noted that Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps to promote more infill development while creating new career paths for the community.
Woory will be hiring for positions in personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Operations are expected to begin in November 2023.
source_title: EV parts manufacturer to build first U.S. facility in Laurens County creating 130 jobs

facts: South Korea-based auto parts maker Woory Industrial Co. will build its first U.S. manufacturing plant in Georgia.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the project on Wednesday.
Woory Industrial Co. will invest an estimated $18 million in a facility in Dublin.
The new facility will create more than 130 jobs.
The company specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components for both electric and internal combustion vehicles.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated: “We’re proud to welcome Woory Industrial as the latest job creator to help us on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.”

Gov. Brian Kemp added: “This emerging industry continues to bring historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities all over the state, thanks to our highly skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network.”
Unlike internal combustion engines, EVs do not have a heating source, making heaters required for cabin heating and maintaining battery temperature.
Pioneering development by Woory has made the company a leader in EV heating technology.
The company is also working to develop other new products for EVs as well as hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
Jungwoo Kim, Woory’s CEO, stated: “We are facing a revolution in the automobile industry for the first time in 100 years.”
Jungwoo Kim added: “The transition to electric vehicles is the future, and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automobile markets.”
Woory will be hiring for positions from all fields necessary for an automotive parts plant.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team worked with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and the Technical College System of Georgia’s Quick Start program to land the project.
The new plant is expected to begin operations in November.
source_title: EV parts supplier to build plant in Georgia - Rough Draft Atlanta

facts: A South Korean company will open an $18 million plant in Georgia to build heaters for electric vehicles.
Woory Industrial Co., based in Yongin, South Korea, said Wednesday that it would open the plant in Dublin, Georgia, in November, hiring 130 people.
The company says it will make electric heaters and control units for electric and internal combustion automakers.
It’s Woory’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The company has existing factories in South Korea, China, Thailand, Vietnam, India, Slovakia and Mexico.
Woory says it has been developing electric heaters over the last 10 years with hopes of becoming a leader in the market.
Heaters are needed in cold weather not only to keep passengers warm, but to keep electric batteries warm for better performance.
Woory is the ninth supplier to locate in the region after Hyundai said in 2022 that it would build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia.
The site could grow to 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Suppliers have pledged to invest more than $2 billion and to hire nearly 4,700 people.
Woory could qualify for $2.6 million in state income tax credits, at $4,000 per job over five years, as long as workers make at least $31,300 a year.
The state will also pay to train workers.
The company could qualify for other incentives, including property tax breaks from Dublin and Laurens County.
source_title: South Korean company to invest $18 million in Georgia plant to make heaters for electric vehicles | AP News

facts: Honda honored 54 North American suppliers for outstanding performance in supplying parts and materials during 2021.
Honda purchased $27 billion in parts from mass production suppliers in North America in 2021.
Honda purchased more than $1 billion in parts from Service Parts suppliers in North America.
The supplier purchases supported the sales of more than 1.3 million Honda and Acura vehicles in North America in 2021.
Honda recognized its suppliers with awards for over 35 years during its annual Honda Supplier Conference, which was conducted as a virtual event in 2022 for the second consecutive year.
Honda recognized 35 suppliers for outstanding performance in providing parts and materials for mass production operations in 2021.
Mass Production Supplier Awards were given in four categories: quality & delivery, value, innovation, and sustainability.
For the first time, Honda recognized one supplier with an award for Distinguished Performance.

Mike Lapham, general manager of procurement at Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, stated that working together as one team with suppliers has been critical to Honda in the face of industrywide supply chain disruptions during the past year.
Mike Lapham noted that supplier teamwork will serve as a springboard to future success as they enter the electrified era.
DENSO International America, Inc. of Southfield, Michigan received the Distinguished Performance Award for exceptional performance in cost, quality, and delivery.

Aisin World Corporation of America of Marion, Illinois received the Supplier Sustainability Award for commitment to social responsibility, compliance, ethics, environmental governance, diversity, health and safety, and trade compliance.

Honda does business with 692 OEM suppliers across North America which provide parts to the company’s 18 plants in the region that produce Honda and Acura cars and light trucks and the engines and transmissions that power them.
Honda honored 19 suppliers for excellent performance in providing service parts for customers after purchasing Honda or Acura products.
Service Parts supplier awards reflect a supplier’s combined performance in quality, cost, and delivery.

Mark Wiles, assistant vice president of the Parts Division at American Honda Motor Co., Inc., stated that nothing is more important to Honda than serving customer needs and appreciates supplier efforts during the challenging business environment.
Mark Wiles noted they are working more closely with suppliers than ever before to overcome supply issues and meet customer needs.

Honda does business with nearly 1,200 service parts suppliers in North America to provide service and accessory parts for more than 20 million Honda and Acura automobiles in operation in the United States.

Honda manages a national network of parts and distribution facilities that deliver high-quality and value service parts to Honda and Acura dealers nationwide as well as supporting global parts operations.
Honda established operations in North America in 1959.

Today, the company employs more than 40,000 associates in the region engaged in development, manufacturing, sales and service support of Honda and Acura automobiles, Honda power equipment, Honda powersports products and the HondaJet Elite S advanced lightjet.

Based on its longstanding commitment to build products close to the customer, Honda operates 18 major manufacturing plants in North America, working with nearly 800 suppliers in the region to produce a diverse range of quality products.
Honda has built automobiles in North America for 40 years and, in 2021, more than 95% of all Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were produced in North America.
Honda also operates 21 North American research and development facilities that fully design, develop and engineer many of the company’s North American-made products.

facts: The Yamaha manufacturing facility in Newnan, GA, is featured in a new episode of “Small Town, Big Deal,” one of the most popular and widely viewed shows airing on the RFD-TV network.
The episode featuring Newnan and Yamaha first aired on Thursday, June 11, at 8:30 p.m. Central Standard Time.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) serves as Yamaha’s worldwide hub of off-highway vehicle (OHV) manufacturing.
All of the company's SxS and the majority of their ATV vehicles are produced at the Newnan facility for national and international distribution.
The facility in Newnan covers 280 acres with more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing facilities.
The facility has approximately 1,473 employees.
Yamaha has invested more than $250 million into U.S. manufacturing over the past 15 years, creating a world-class facility in Newnan.
Mike Chrzanowski is the executive vice president of YMMC.
The facility produces ATV or side-by-side vehicles, golf cars, and personal watercraft.

Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation strives to maintain the highest production quality and environmental standards, while giving back to the community through comprehensive corporate social responsibility programs.

Yamaha does not sell or service its products directly; other Yamaha-affiliated companies support customers and dealers for sales, service, and marketing purposes.
The Newnan facility has been manufacturing recreational vehicles for over 30 years.

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) is based in Newnan Georgia.
YMMC broke ground yesterday on a new, advanced, and innovative "SMART" Warehouse.

source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation breaks ground on inno...

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC), based in Newnan Georgia, broke ground on a new SMART Warehouse on June 9, 2022.
The facility is the first-of-its-kind SMART warehouse for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The new warehouse will be 200,000 square feet and utilizes state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The facility will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will be capable of retrieving and putting away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small part automated system will be capable of handling 2400 containers per hour, in comparison to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
The new systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklifts by 50 percent using automatic conveyors and tuggers to move parts through the SMART center to the factories.

Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated that the warehouse will improve overall operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines to get product to dealers and customers faster.
Chrzanowski noted that demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at this factory continues to exceed supply.
The significant investment underscores Yamaha’s long-term commitment to YMCC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The SMART Warehouse will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
The facility will include an observation platform for groups such as local school students to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
Due to strong product demand and continuous growth, Yamaha is currently hiring.
Most careers at Yamaha start as contract-to-perm positions.
YMUS has corporate offices in California, two in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Breaks Ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan Georgia | Yamaha

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the announcement that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company is relocating out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
This decision follows a meeting between Governor Kemp and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co. Ltd. is a manufacturer of ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
Yamaha already has a strong presence in Georgia, employing over 2,300 Georgians.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin this year and is expected to continue through 2028.
Governor Brian Kemp stated: “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.”

Governor Brian Kemp added: “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we'll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
The Yamaha Motor Company currently employs thousands of people across various facilities in Georgia.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999, expanding the facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to the existing facilities.
source_title: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia - Metro Atlanta CEO

facts: Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. announced plans to relocate the headquarters of Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A. from Cypress, California, to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The transition is expected to take place in phases from late 2026 through 2028.
As part of the plan, Yamaha will sell its 25.1-acre Cypress campus, which has served as its U.S. home base for nearly five decades.
The company described the decision as part of broader structural reforms designed to centralize operations and make more efficient use of corporate assets.
Yamaha purchased the Cypress property in 1978 and opened its offices there in 1979.
The Southern California campus has long housed corporate leadership and financial services operations.
Much of Yamaha's U.S. business has already shifted to Georgia over the years.
The company's Marine division moved to Kennesaw in 1999, followed by its Motorsports division in 2019.
The relocation of remaining corporate functions will consolidate key operations in one state.
Yamaha plans to use a sale and leaseback arrangement during the transition.
Under that structure, the company would sell the California property while temporarily leasing it back to maintain operations as teams gradually move east.
source_title: Yamaha announces major US headquarters relocation

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) is a manufacturing facility located in Newnan, Georgia.
YMMC assembles recreational vehicles like WaveRunners, Side-by-Sides, ATVs and golf carts.
On Friday, March 26 an EF4 tornado struck Newnan with a path that passed right over Newnan High School.
Dr. Joshua Boyd, Director of Bands, stated the tornado leveled their building, deemed it a total loss, and they lost every instrument, uniform, and piece of band history.
The roof shifted and water poured in for three weeks, leaving woodwind instruments sitting in water to rot.
Band students volunteered to help clear debris for the community; 30 kids showed up initially, and all 130 showed up three days later.
A tornado-impacted student told Dr. Boyd that band was critical emotional support because they had lost their home and belongings.
YMMC local leadership connected Newnan High School's band to Yamaha Corporation of America (YCA), based in Southern California near Yamaha Motor's Corporate Headquarters.
Dr. Boyd aimed to get the Marching Band back up and running by fall to help students deal with the emotional roller coaster caused by the tornado.
Over several weeks, YMMC staff worked in unison with a YCA team to develop a plan to assist the band for the fall opening of school and marching season.
David Jewell, Partnerships and Alliances Manager for YCA, immediately asked what his company could do to help upon hearing about the tornado destruction in Newnan.
Insurance for the school was not yet settled after the March storm, and instrument ordering typically requires a six-month lead time due to complex manufacturing and international shipping.

Yamaha acquired and loaned marching brass, low reeds, and marching drums, coordinating inventory from across the country to arrive together at YMMC in Newnan because packages could not be received at the high school yet.
The entire leadership team from YMMC personally delivered the instruments and unboxed them with the band.
Band member Diego Sanchez described the delivery as hopeful, encouraging, and exciting, comparing unboxing to Christmas day.
YMMC President Takahiro Imanishi helped with the unboxing and stated that 'Two Yamahas, One Passion' reflects how the two companies work together to make passion for music and memories possible.
Dr. Boyd observed instrument division staff collaborating with ATV manufacturing staff to secure the band's return to the field.
Approximately 60% of YMMC staff live in Coweta County, with many being Newnan High School graduates or having children who attended.
YMMC is Coweta County's largest private employer and continues to grow as demand for products is high.

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) and Yamaha Motor Corp., USA, (YMUS) celebrated the production of 4 million vehicles with the assembly of a new 2021 Wolverine RMAX2 1000 Side-by-Side at their U.S. manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.
The milestone celebration and employee recognition event took place at the Newnan factory on August 12.
YMMC serves as Yamaha’s hub for 4-wheel off-highway vehicle manufacturing, producing all of the company’s Side-by-Sides, full-size ATVs, WaveRunners, and Golf Cars for worldwide distribution.
Over the past 20 years, Yamaha has invested more than $380 million into U.S. manufacturing to create a world-class facility in Newnan.

Taka Imanishi, president of YMMC, stated that YMMC strives to maintain the highest quality of production and environmental standards while giving back to the community through comprehensive corporate social responsibility programs.
Since its founding in 1988, YMMC has made a significant impact within its community.
The city of Newnan named May 18th “Yamaha Day” in 2011 to celebrate the company’s positive economic impacts and ongoing job creation.
The facility covers 280 acres with more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing facilities.
Between the YMMC facility and YMUS corporate offices in Marietta and Kennesaw, Georgia, Yamaha now employs over 2,000 people in the state of Georgia alone.
This Georgia workforce encompasses more than half of the 4,000+ individuals the company directly employs in the United States.
The company’s impact on Newnan includes recent disaster relief donations and countless ongoing community engagement programs.

Many of these messages were highlighted in the season 19 premiere of “Modern Marvels: Machines” on the HISTORY® Channel, which went behind-the-scenes to spotlight the Yamaha factory and its employees.

Mike Martinez, YMUS vice president of motorsports, said that Yamaha continues to add personnel and strengthen operations in Georgia as the Side-by-Side line expands with popular new models like the Wolverine RMAX 1000.
With increasing demand for outdoor recreation, Yamaha expects to see continued growth of its operations in Georgia and across the country.

The YMMC facility has the highest certifications for quality, safety, and environmental manufacturing in producing golf cars, personal watercraft, Side-by-Side vehicles, and all-terrain vehicles (ATVs).
The factory is ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System certified.
YMUS has a corporate office in California, two corporate offices in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company (YMSC) with divisions Bennett Marine (Florida) and Kracor Systems (Wisconsin), Skeeter Boats (Texas) with division G3 Boats (Missouri), and Yamaha Precision Propeller (Indiana).

entity_name: State Electric Vehicle Tax Credits & Rebates Guide
canonical_name: State Electric Vehicle Tax Credits & Rebates Guide
facts: The federal electric clean vehicle tax credits were prematurely ended in 2025 with OBBBA legislation, making any EV/PHEV purchased after 9/30/25 ineligible for the federal credit.
The Inflation Reduction Act (signed in 2022 and effective in 2023) renamed PHEV and electric vehicle federal tax credits to the "Clean Vehicle Tax Credit".

Major vehicle requirements for federal credits include a battery capacity of at least 7 kilowatt hours, manufacture by a qualified manufacturer, final assembly in North America, meeting battery and minerals sourcing criteria, and a maximum MSRP of $80,000 for vans/SUVs/trucks or $55,000 for other vehicles.
The Inflation Reduction Act removed automaker vehicles-sold volume phaseouts and added taxpayer modified adjusted gross income (MAGI) eligibility caps.
Used electric vehicles became potentially eligible for up to a $4,000 tax credit under the new federal rules.
Starting in 2024, dealerships were authorized to offer the value of the federal tax credit up front to consumers.
A "Clean Commercial Vehicle Credit" was created, allowing auto lessors to pass along a $7,500 EV lease tax credit to consumer lessees.
California proposed $200 million in new funds for EV tax credits in 2026 to backfill for the expired federal EV tax credits.

Colorado extended its electric vehicle tax credits with a phaseout schedule: 2025 offers $3,500 on vehicles up to $80,000 plus an additional $2,500 if under $35,000 for both purchases and leases; 2026 reduces the base to $750 with the same $2,500 low-price add-on.

Connecticut's Hydrogen and Electric Automobile Purchase Rebate (CHEAPR) program offers rebates for residents purchasing or leasing new eligible electric, fuel cell, or plug-in hybrid vehicles up to a maximum MSRP of $50,000.

Connecticut expanded "Rebates+" credits are available for applicants residing in Environmental Justice communities, distressed municipalities, participating in qualifying income programs, or earning less than 300% of the Federal Poverty Level.
Connecticut allocated its rebate funds on a first-come, first-served basis and lowered base amounts beginning on 8/1/25.
Delaware offers electric vehicle cash rebates totaling up to $2,500 depending on vehicle type, requiring applications within 90 days of purchase.

The Illinois Electric Vehicle Rebate Program, funded by the Illinois Environmental Protection Agency, provides rebates for residents purchasing new or used all-electric vehicles from licensed dealers.
Illinois approved a funding round from 10/28/25 to 1/41/26 offering a $4,000 rebate for low-income applicants, $2,000 for non-low-income applicants, and $1,500 for all-electric motorcycles.
Illinois vehicle rebates are capped at vehicles with a selling price not exceeding $80,000.

Maine suspended electric vehicle rebates starting November 16, 2024, except for low-income residents, though previously listed enhanced rebates included $7,500 for new EVs, $3,000 for new PHEVs, and $2,500 for used EVs/PHEVs.

Maryland offered an excise tax credit of $3,000 on vehicles $50,000 and less through 2027 for qualifying zero-emission plug-in or fuel cell vehicles, though funding appeared inactive as of early 2026.
Massachusetts MOR-EV program is funded through 2027 and offers rebates for new or used electric and fuel-cell vehicles.

Massachusetts provides a $3,500 rebate for new vehicle purchases/leases under $55,000, an additional $1,500 (MOR-EV+) for below-specified income levels, and an extra $1,000 for trading in a vehicle at least 12 years old.
New Jersey offers a $1,500 "Charge Up" point-of-sale incentive on eligible EVs and a $4,000 pre-approved "Charge Up+" incentive for below-specified income levels.
New Jersey also provides a $250 in-home EV charger incentive.
New York offers tax rebates for new electric vehicles based on range ($2,000 for >200 miles, $1,000 for 40-199 miles, $500 for <40 miles) and an additional $500 rebate for MSRPs greater than $42,000.
Oregon's "Charge Ahead Rebate" provided up to $7,500 for new EVs and $5,000 for used EVs but was suspended on 9/9/25, with new funding anticipated in 2026.

Pennsylvania offers EV rebates for new and used vehicles with a final purchase price of $45,000 or less: $3,000 for battery electric vehicles, $1,500 for plug-in hybrids, and $500 for other alternative fuel/electric motorcycles.
Pennsylvania limits household income to 300% of the federal poverty level for standard rebates, with an additional $1,000 available for lower-income applicants meeting the 150% FPL threshold.

Rhode Island's DRIVE EV Rebate program offers up to $3,000 for new BEVs/fuel-cell vehicles and up to $2,000 for new PHEVs (under $75,000), plus up to $2,500 for used BEVs/fuel-cell and up to $1,750 for used PHEVs (under $55,000).

Rhode Island provides rebates of up to $800 or 100% of the purchase price for Level 2 Residential EV Chargers, and up to $1,000 or 50% of installation costs, with higher amounts for income-qualified applicants.
Rhode Island offers an additional rebate of up to $1,500 for applicants in state or federal income-qualified programs (DRIVE+), awarded on a first-come, first-served basis.

Federal energy tax credits and rebates related to solar panels, electrical panels, battery storage, and EV charging were updated via the Inflation Reduction Act for 2025 but were mostly ended prematurely with OBBBA legislation for 2026 and beyond.
source_title: List of State Electric Vehicle Tax Credits & Rebates in 2025 & 2026

facts: YKK AP America Inc. is an innovation-driven manufacturer of commercial façade systems and residential windows and doors.
The company operates a subsidiary named ERIE Architectural Products.
Founder Tadao Yoshida established the “Cycle of Goodness” philosophy as the central tenet of all business activities.
The company adheres to 25 Fundamental Behaviors to operationalize its corporate values.
YKK AP designs and manufactures high-quality fenestration solutions for commercial and residential markets.
Products are utilized on buildings across the United States.
The STUDIO 101 Design Center provides hands-on product demonstrations and training events, with locations in downtown Atlanta, GA and Rancho Cucamonga, CA.
YKK AP Inc. (Japan) focuses on Architectural Products including windows, doors, curtain walls, and exterior products.

Open positions are available in sales, estimating, customer service, marketing, application/design/mechanical engineering, operations/production, and business support areas like IT, HR, finance, and purchasing.
YKK AP America Inc. is the North American arm of the global YKK AP Inc. (Japan).
Products are proudly made in the USA and Canada.

The company’s philosophy centers on creating mutual benefit for customers, partners, employees, and society, guided by Tadao Yoshida’s principle that “no one prospers without rendering benefit to others.”
Sustainability is integrated into responsible sourcing, transparent manufacturing, fair employee treatment, and customer value delivery.
The company aligns with the YKK AP Group’s Evolution 2030 global initiative and the YKK Group Environmental Vision 2050 to reduce carbon intensity and environmental footprint.
YKK AP controls every stage of manufacturing from billet to finished product across ISO 14001-certified facilities in the U.S. and Canada.

The company produces its own aluminum billets, cuts its own dies, operates multiple extrusion lines, and applies advanced anodizing, painting, and thermal break technologies, including one of the industry’s only vertical anodizing lines in the U.S.
YKK AP introduced BetterBillet™, a proprietary aluminum billet made from recycled scrap and low-carbon primary aluminum, integrated into most commercial products.
To support Western U.S. demand, YKK AP established the Los Angeles Sales and Manufacturing Center in Rancho Cucamonga, California, adding engineering support.

A 400,000-square-foot greenfield facility manufactures residential windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners throughout the Southeast, integrating vinyl extrusion, fabrication, and glazing under one roof.
Tim Thomas was promoted to President of Erie Architectural Products, leveraging over 38 years in the glass and glazing industry.
A new showroom dubbed STUDIO 101 was opened at the downtown Atlanta HQ facility, housing corporate staff, engineering, and a training center for product demos.
A second regional manufacturing center in Cincinnati, Ohio, opened in 2019 in over 100,000 square feet to increase local production capabilities and finished goods inventory for the Midwest.
Pearson advanced from a temp agency receptionist to a top sales representative, becoming the first female recipient of the company’s President’s Award.
In 2019, YKK AP acquired high-performance facades solution provider Erie Architectural Products to grow its offering for prefabricated, pre-assembled products.
In 2018, the company appointed its first female vice president, who created an organizational excellence department focused on employee engagement, talent acquisition, and human capital technology.
In 2017, YKK AP led a local business challenge to complete Martin Luther King, Jr. Monument Park in Dublin, GA, and recognized Martin Luther King, Jr. Day as a paid company holiday.
Oliver Stepe was promoted to President of YKK AP America Inc. in 2016 after joining in 1992; he appointed the company’s first minority director and expanded business to the west coast.

In 2015, YKK AP became the first fenestration manufacturer to obtain third-party certified environmental product declarations for its entire portfolio, offering seven categories meeting LEED v4 requirements.
Also in 2015, a direct freight program was launched to reduce damage and increase scheduling predictability for commercial customers coast-to-coast.

In July 2014, the company opened its first regional manufacturing facility in Coppell, Texas, a 105,000-square-foot site serving as a key supply and distribution center for south central and western regions.
In 2013, YKK AP debuted the “I am an Architect” video series at AIA Denver, which gained two million views and built an AEC Facebook community of over 64 thousand people.
The company hosted its first “Chillin’ with YKK AP” event in Las Vegas to build relationships with customers and industry partners.
In 2011, at AIA New Orleans, the company demonstrated ProTek® impact-resistant glazing systems by having pitcher Trey Shields throw pitches at an estimated 80 mph.
In 2010, YKK AP debuted myThermalAssistant, a thermal performance calculator enabling building professionals to calculate energy-saving metrics using AAMA 507 standard based on NFRC methods.
In 2009, the company launched enerGfacade, the industry’s first energy-efficient line of framing systems under a single brand name, using patented technologies for advanced thermal performance.
In 2008, ThermaShade® Sun Control system was launched, winning an IDEA design award in 2009, featuring ease-of-installation with a hanger bolt and slotted receptor design.
In 2007, the company diversified into another market and began plans for a vinyl window manufacturing facility in Macon, GA, which remains the hub of its residential business.

In 2006, YKK AP held a groundbreaking ceremony for phase 1 of an $80M expansion of its Dublin plant, opening nearly 200 jobs, and was named one of the industry’s top ten employers by US Glass Magazine.
In 2005, YKK AP officially entered the residential market with the first shipment of vinyl windows to Quality Craft, Inc.
In 2004, YKK AP achieved $100 million in sales for the first time.
The Dublin commercial manufacturing plant is ISO 14001 certified and maintains a 73% recycling rate.
In 2002, a grand opening was held for a new fabrication plant next to the Dublin manufacturing plant to increase capacity for commercial entrances and windows.
In 2001, YKK AP began attending national trade shows starting with NGA (now Glassbuild) and has frequently won Best In Show at AIA Conference, including in 2015.
In 2000, the company received the Region 9 Existing Industry Award from the Georgia Economic Developers Association for exceptional community and state contributions.

In 1999, YKK AP released the first version of Ycalc® estimating software, which evolved into Ycalc® X5 powered by Logikal® for structural design, shop drawings, material optimization, and fabrication utilities.

In 1998, YKK AP partnered with the City of Dublin, GA to establish a “Sister Cities” relationship and student exchange program with Osaki Japan, alongside supporting initiatives like Dublin FIRST Robotics Competition and river cleanups.

In 1997, YKK AP was granted a patent for its ProTek® entrance design’s water resistance, later expanding the line into blast mitigation; additional patents cover StyleView® residential window sash and ThermaShade® Sunshades.
In 1996, the company opened its Cincinnati branch (sixth satellite location) and expanded the Dublin manufacturing facility by 100,000 square feet; it now operates ten branches coast-to-coast.
In 1995, YKK AP launched the “Project Center” team to support medium to large-scale projects with estimating, engineering, and project management services.
Following Hurricane Andrew, YKK AP developed ProTek®, a complete integrated entrance and framing solution for impact resistance, later expanded into blast mitigation.
The company employs Anodized Plus®, a unique vertical anodizing process meeting AAMA 612 standards for enhanced durability.
The Dublin, GA manufacturing plant opened in September 1992 as a vertically integrated operation handling melting, casting, die manufacturing, extrusion, finishing, fabrication, and shipping.

YKK AP entered the U.S. market with aluminum products from YKK AP Japan in 1984, opened an Atlanta branch in 1986, and incorporated YKK AP America Inc. on April 24, 1991, choosing Dublin, GA as its manufacturing base.
High-performance aluminum facade, window, and door systems are supported by design tools and technical expertise for architects and glaziers.
Premium residential windows and patio doors serve the Southeastern U.S. through a trusted dealer network for homebuilders and homeowners.
Erie AP delivers custom unitized curtain wall solutions via its ENVIRO|FACADES® line, combining design assist, performance testing, and advanced manufacturing.
source_title: About YKK AP and Our Building Solutions Guide | YKK AP

facts: YKK AP America Inc. has begun operating from its new state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Macon, Ga.
The 400,000 square foot purpose-built facility manufactures residential windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners throughout the Southeast region.
The facility is located at 100 YKK AP Way, in the heart of Macon’s industrial district.
The opening of the facility follows more than 18 months of thoughtful design and planning.
With a growing residential market, the new facility provides YKK AP with the ability to enhance customer satisfaction by doubling production capacity.
The facility sets the stage for the upcoming new and upgraded StyleView® Classic series that will be produced on new manufacturing lines.
All employees have been relocated from the previous manufacturing facility.
Oliver Stepe, president and CEO of YKK AP America, stated that the new manufacturing facility is an investment in their people and customers.

The facility integrates vinyl extrusion, fabrication, and glazing while improving operational efficiencies by bringing all processes under one roof, a strategic floor layout, and investment in new equipment.
The company plans to add automated Goods-To-Person (GTP) technology to reduce the manual movement of heavy windows across the plant.
To enhance the employee experience, the facility was designed with increased natural light, updated furnishing and outdoor spaces including a walking trail and basketball/pickleball court.
The facility is 100 percent climate controlled.
YKK AP America Inc. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
All building products are manufactured in the company’s world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.
Together with its subsidiary brand, Erie Architectural Products (Erie AP), the company serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
source_title: YKK AP Begins Operations at New Residential | YKK AP

facts: YKK AP America Inc. will invest up to $125 million in an expansion that will include a new residential window and door manufacturing facility and additional machinery and equipment in Macon, Ga.
The facility will serve as a model plant for the company’s future North American manufacturing plants, featuring advanced sustainable manufacturing and enhanced work environments for employees.
YKK AP’s residential products business has been headquartered in Macon since it was established in 2007.
The company currently employs more than 250 employees in Macon who will relocate to the new facility once complete.
With this expansion, the company will also add over 100 new jobs over the next few years.
Oliver Stepe, president of YKK AP America Inc., stated that strong demand for single and multi-family residential window products is driving the investment to better serve customers.

The investment will expand the workforce, introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass and window production, and create enhanced efficiencies to manufacture the highest quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.

YKK AP aims to offer purpose driven product solutions to enhance the built environment and sees this investment as another step toward further expansion in the architectural products market in the U.S.
In addition to the residential business expansion in Macon, the company is currently expanding capacity for its commercial products business at its manufacturing facility in Dublin, Ga.
YKK AP plans to break ground on the new facility in Oct. 2022.
JE Dunn Construction will serve as the construction manager for the Macon manufacturing facility and SSOE Group will serve as architects and engineers.

Scott Bodden, Project Director at JE Dunn Construction, stated they look forward to collaborating with SSOE Group to support YKK AP’s growth and create jobs, noting the facility will be built on innovation in project delivery.

Mike Nelson, Senior Project Manager at SSOE Group, stated SSOE is delighted to design a world-class facility for YKK AP to support continued growth and noted the opportunity this creates to further develop the Macon-Bibb County economy aligns with SSOE’s vision.
The facility is scheduled to be operational by Q1 of 2024.
YKK AP America Inc. is a leading manufacturer of award-winning commercial facade systems and residential windows and doors, headquartered in Atlanta, Ga.
All building products are manufactured in world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga. and Lakeshore, Ont.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
Erie Architectural Products (Erie AP) is a unitized curtain wall business subsidiary located in Lakeshore, Ont.

SSOE Group has earned a reputation over its 70-year history for providing quality project solutions to high-tech and general manufacturing clients in the semiconductor, automotive, battery, food, chemical, and glass industries.

links.context: SSOE Group website and career opportunities

source_title: YKK AP Announces up to $125 Million Investment in | YKK AP

facts: YKK USA expanded its product offerings to reinforce the domestic supply chain that will help manufacturers throughout the United States manufacture products essential to fighting the COVID-19 pandemic.
YKK offers a wide variety of metal wire, hook & loop, injection molded plastic parts, webbing, snaps, and buttons that can be used in COVID-19 relief applications.
Applications for YKK’s POWERHOOK® extruded hook include Foley Catheter Holders, DVT compression sleeves, endotracheal tube holders, arm boards, blood pressure cuffs, and radial compression devices.
Manufacturers of surgical gowns can make use of SOFIX®, which incorporates plastic snaps injected onto tape, as well as POWERHOOK® and YKK’s polyester tape.
Medical curtain manufacturers can use YKK’s plastic snaps, stainless SNAPET®, and gypsy stud snaps.
YKK is manufacturing back to back hook and loop fasteners, SOFIX®, plastic detachable clips, and plastic cord stoppers for face mask ear savers.
For those manufacturing face masks, YKK has 3.0 mm and 4.0 mm aluminum wire and 3.0 mm and 5.0 mm elastic tape available.
For medical face shield manufacturers, YKK is producing plastic and metal snaps, hook and loop fasteners, and plastic cord stoppers.
YKK’s PROSEAL® airtight and watertight zipper can be used in isolation chambers, tent shelters, and Level B Hazmat suits.
YKK’s AquaGuard® water repellent zipper can be used for medical mattresses.
YKK’s AquaSeal® waterproof zipper can be used for body bags.
YKK USA has 86 years of manufacturing experience and four manufacturing plants in the United States.

YKK’s core competencies include wire manufacturing and forming, metal stamping, metal die casting, metal finishing and plating, weaving and knitting, textile dyeing, plastic extrusion, plastic injection molding, fabrication and assembly, and custom machinery and support.
Jim Reed serves as President of YKK Corporation of America.
source_title: YKK USA Expands Product Offerings to Support COVID-19 Relief - PCIAW®

facts: Atlanta-based YKK AP America, a subsidiary of Tokyo-based YKK AP, plans to invest up to $125 million to expand its middle Georgia facility.
The expansion will create 100 additional jobs at the Bibb County manufacturing facility.
As is typical for jobs classified as in progress, state officials declined to divulge whether the state offered any incentives to entice the expansion or what it might cost Georgia taxpayers.
Georgia Department of Economic Development communications specialist Jessica Atwell confirmed that the project is located in a Tier 1 tax credit zone.
Under the Tier 1 credit, companies that create at least two jobs are eligible for a $4,000 tax credit per new job.
Companies may use 100% of that credit toward their tax liability.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that YKK AP has a strong history of growth and success in Georgia.
YKK AP opened its first branch in Atlanta in 1986.
YKK AP America chose middle Georgia to establish its manufacturing presence in the U.S. five years after opening the Atlanta branch.
The company has since expanded its presence in Georgia and built valuable relationships with local communities.
Companies in advanced manufacturing, like YKK AP, have played a key role in bringing jobs and opportunities to Georgia, creating the second most new jobs in the state last year.
The company plans to add machinery and equipment as part of its expansion at the facility located at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park.
The company currently supports 250 employees in Macon.
YKK AP America’s president Oliver Stepe said the new facility will help introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production.
The expansion will allow the company to create enhanced efficiencies to manufacture the highest quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.
source_title: Incentives remain unknown as Georgia officials announce expansion at Bibb County's YKK AP facility | Georgia | thecentersquare.com

facts: YKK AP will build a new state-of-the-art factory at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park in Macon, Ga., according to a release from the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority.
The new plant is part of an expansion that will include additional machinery and equipment in Bibb County.
YKK AP plans to add 100 jobs in addition to its 250 jobs currently located in Macon.
Governor Brian Kemp announced the deal on Tuesday, which includes up to a $125 million investment from YKK in the new location.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that being the No. 1 state for business means prioritizing ensuring companies already operating in Georgia can grow and thrive alongside attracting new investments.
Governor Brian Kemp said he is excited YKK AP recognizes the advantages of doing business in middle Georgia and has chosen to expand its relationship with the Macon community through 100 new jobs.
After completion, YKK AP will relocate its operations and employees from its current site on Ocmulgee Boulevard to the new factory.
The company’s residential division, which manufactures vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners, will be located at the new Macon location.

Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller stated it is an especially great day in Macon-Bibb County when one of their longest-running business partners decides to make another investment in the community and bring an additional 100 jobs to the area.

Mayor Lester M. Miller noted that the new state-of-the-art facility will be used as a blueprint for future facilities, illustrating trust in Macon-Bibb County and Georgia as the best place to do business.
YKK AP expects operations at the new site to begin in early 2024.
source_title: YKK AP Plans New Factory in Macon, 100 New Jobs Created | Choose Macon

facts: YKK AP America announces key leadership appointments to position the organization for future growth across North America.
Mike Turner has been appointed as president of the company’s commercial business group, effective April 1.
Josh Jensen has been appointed as president of the company’s residential business group, effective April 1.
The commercial business group includes the YKK AP brand division and the company’s subsidiary Erie Architectural Products.
Tim Thomas leads Erie Architectural Products as its president.
Turner and Jensen will report to North American Group president and CEO Oliver Stepe.
These appointments reflect continuous growth in the company’s commercial façade systems and residential windows and doors business.
YKK AP is committed to continued investment in its manufacturing and sales operations in the State of Georgia and across the United States.
Turner currently serves as senior vice president of sales and marketing and has worked for YKK AP for more than 30 years.
Turner oversaw consolidated revenue growth and a substantial increase in business complexity since 2016 when he assumed leadership of sales and marketing.
Josh Jensen joined the company in January 2024 and brings 25 years of experience in the windows and doors industry.
Jensen has progressed from sales management up to general manager and president.
Jensen is an accomplished strategic leader with comprehensive knowledge and experience across operations, sales, marketing, quality assurance, finance, procurement, and human resources.
Oliver Stepe, president and CEO of YKK AP America, states that the appointments reflect a commitment to business growth and executive expertise.
YKK AP is ramping up operations at its brand new, state-of-the-art $125 million residential windows and doors manufacturing facility in Macon, Georgia.

Josh Jensen’s leadership of the residential business group will be instrumental as YKK AP works to achieve its vision to become a global leading company through the evolution of architectural products.
YKK AP America Inc. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
All building products are manufactured in world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.
Together with its subsidiary brand Erie Architectural Products, the company serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
source_title: YKK AP Makes Key Leadership Appointments to | YKK AP

facts: ZF opened a manual transmission plant in the North Georgia city of Gainesville in 1987.
ZF's initial presence has expanded into four facilities, with two located in Gainesville and two in Oakwood.
ZF employs more than 1,000 people across its Hall County facilities.
ZF owns Kelsey-Hayes Co., a braking systems manufacturer with a presence in Oakwood, following its 2015 acquisition of U.S. rival TRW Automotive.
In 2011, Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal inaugurated a new $100 million factory in Gainesville producing gear boxes for wind turbines.
The wind turbine gear boxes produced at the 2011 plant weigh 16 tons each and are transported one per semi-truck.
The 2011 Gainesville plant was ZF’s first in the U.S. for the wind turbine gear box line of business, with initial plans to expand to about 250 workers.
Thirty years after its initial investment, ZF retains 14 of its original 88 Gainesville employees.
ZF's manufacturing team now includes employees representing 28 different nationalities.
At least one employee on ZF's Georgia team relocated from one of the company's six plants in Mexico.
Economic development leaders emphasize that winning new greenfield investment is only the beginning, as most new jobs come from supporting existing companies and helping them expand.
Gainesville Mayor Danny Dunagan and Oakwood Mayor Lamar Scroggs led city and county leaders to the ZF facility last month to celebrate the company’s 30th anniversary in Hall County.
Sept. 23 was designated “ZF Day” through a joint declaration by the governments of Oakwood, Gainesville, and Hall County.

facts: Honda honored 61 North American suppliers for their performance and perseverance in supplying parts and materials during 2020.
Suppliers earned awards for supporting Honda with mass production parts and materials, as well as service parts that serve customers after purchasing a Honda or Acura product.
Honda purchased $27 billion in parts for mass production from North American suppliers in 2020.
These purchases supported the sales of more than 1.3 million Honda and Acura vehicles in North America in 2020.
Honda purchased approximately $1 billion in parts from its Service Parts suppliers in North America.
Honda recognizes suppliers with awards during its annual Honda Supplier Conference, held each spring for more than 35 years.
The 2020 conference was conducted as a virtual event due to the COVID-19 pandemic's focus on health and safety.

Mark Willoughby, vice president of procurement at Honda Development and Manufacturing of America, stated that supplier effort helped keep Honda plants running to meet customer needs during the pandemic.
Mark Wiles, parts division assistant vice president at American Honda Motor Co., Inc., thanked service parts suppliers for their support despite supply chain challenges.
OEM suppliers provide Honda with parts and materials for mass production.
Honda recognized 39 OEM suppliers in 2020 across three categories: quality and delivery, value, and sustainability.
The Supplier Sustainability Award recognizes a company committed to social responsibility, compliance, ethics, environmental governance, diversity, health and safety, and trade compliance.
East Penn Manufacturing of Lyons Station, Pennsylvania, received the 2020 Supplier Sustainability Award.
Honda does business with 743 OEM suppliers across North America.
These OEM suppliers provide parts to Honda’s 16 plants in the region that produce Honda and Acura cars, light trucks, engines, and transmissions.
American Honda Motor Co., Inc. honored 22 service parts suppliers for excellent performance in providing post-purchase service parts.
Service Parts supplier awards reflect combined performance in quality, cost, and delivery.
American Honda does business with nearly 1,200 service parts suppliers in North America.
These suppliers provide service and accessory parts for more than 20 million Honda and Acura automobiles in operation in the United States, as well as for powersports and power equipment products.
American Honda manages a national network of parts and distribution facilities delivering high-quality service parts to dealers nationwide and supporting global parts operations.
Honda established operations in North America in 1959.
Honda employs more than 40,000 associates in the region engaged in development, manufacturing, sales, and service support.
Honda operates 18 major manufacturing plants in North America.
Honda works with nearly 800 suppliers in the region to produce its diverse product range.
In 2020, more than 95 percent of Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were produced in North America using domestic and globally sourced parts.
Honda operates 21 research and development facilities in North America that fully design, develop, and engineer many products made in the region.
Nidec Elesys Americas Corporation in Atlanta, Georgia, received an OEM Supplier Award for Excellence in Quality and Delivery.
ACM Georgia LLC in Warrenton, Georgia, received a Service Parts Supplier Award.

facts: Adient’s Q3 showcased the operational leverage and strategic value of its North American footprint as tariff-driven onshoring unlocked $150M–$200M in new revenue opportunities, with minimal incremental investment.
Margin expansion and cash generation remain central as EMEA restructuring and Asia mix shifts play out, while management raises full-year guidance and signals continued capital returns.
Adient’s local production base is converting tariff disruption into net new business wins.
Adient delivered a 12% year-on-year increase in adjusted EBITDA to $226 million, with margins expanding by 60 basis points to 6.0%.
The Americas region outperformed industry volume, benefiting from operational efficiencies, automation, and a lighter launch calendar compared to the prior year.
Net tariff headwinds moderated to $4 million in Q3, down from $9 million in Q2, reflecting effective mitigation actions and customer negotiations.
Free cash flow reached $115 million, supporting $50 million in share buybacks for the quarter and bringing year-to-date repurchases to $75 million, or about 4% of shares outstanding.
Liquidity remains robust with $1.7 billion available.
Tariff exposure reduced to $4 million monthly, with further recovery expected in Q4.
APAC margins expanded 150 basis points, offsetting lower China sales with growth in other Asian markets.
Adient’s U.S. production footprint covers 75% of North American output, compared to 55% for its nearest competitor.

Management quantified the onshoring opportunity at 600,000 annual vehicles, representing $150M–$200M in incremental revenue for Adient beginning in 2026, with no corresponding asset downsizing elsewhere.
Minimal incremental investment is required to convert the onshoring opportunity into high-margin revenue.
Recent wins include Nissan and an Asia-based OEM relocating production from Canada to the U.S.
Adient expects EMEA EBITDA margins to trough in 2025 at 2.5–3%, then climb to 5–6% over the next two to three years as underperforming contracts expire and higher-margin business ramps.
New business awards with Mercedes-Benz and Volvo support improved regional profitability in Europe.
Component wins with BYD and new business with Toyota position Adient for growth as local OEMs globalize.
Management expects a return to market parity in China by 2026–2027, contingent on software and ADAS readiness at local OEMs.
Adient has repurchased nearly 15% of its shares since initiating its buyback program.
Adient’s operational model is anchored in just-in-time (JIT), foam, and trim vertical integration, enabling high service levels and switching cost advantages.
Automakers are seeking partners who can deliver on accelerated onshoring timelines and complex product launches.
Management raised full-year 2025 revenue guidance to approximately $14.4 billion and adjusted EBITDA guidance to approximately $875 million.
Free cash flow guidance for full-year 2025 is maintained at $150–$170 million.
Management emphasized that guidance does not contemplate further tariff changes and that U.S. onshoring wins are expected to drive incremental gains into 2026.
Ongoing changes to U.S. trade policy could alter the magnitude and pace of onshoring benefits.
Achieving targeted EMEA margin uplift depends on timely contract roll-offs and successful ramp of new business.
Volume recovery in APAC is contingent on local OEMs’ software readiness and EV adoption rates.
Continued pressure from input costs and customer reimbursement timing could impact near-term profitability.
Sustained free cash flow and prudent buybacks will be watched as restructuring and growth investments continue.
Peers with less developed North American footprints may face share loss or require significant investment to compete as U.S. onshoring accelerates.
source_title: Adient (ADNT) Q3 2025: Onshoring Wins Unlock $150M–$200M Incremental Revenue, Margin Expansion in Focus | EarningsIQ

The global automotive seat manufacturer officially announced plans to make the Marquette Building its new home for 500 employees.

facts: Adient PLC is a global leader in automotive seating.
Adient operates roughly 238 manufacturing and assembly plants in 34 countries.
Adient shares rose more than 10% on Monday afternoon following a broader auto-parts stocks rally and an analyst upgrade.
SORL Auto posted fourth-quarter revenue of $123.2 million, exceeding analysts' estimates by $18.35 million.
Robert W. Baird upgraded Adient from underperform to neutral with a price target of $13.22.
Adient's stock traded between $13.46 and $14.57 per share on Monday afternoon, suggesting potential downside from the analyst's price target.
Adient was spun off from Johnson Controls in late 2016.
Adient holds nearly 40% market share in the North American and European automotive seating markets.
Adient holds a 45% market share in the Chinese automotive seating market.
Adient's stock trades at 4.4 times the consensus estimate for forward earnings.
Global automakers need suppliers that can deliver consistently on short notice to match demand.

facts: Adient is a leading global automotive seating supplier supporting all major automakers.
The company reported more than $15 billion in sales in FY2025.
Adient employs more than 65,000 people across 29 countries.
The company operates approximately 200 manufacturing and assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all vehicle classes and major OEMs.
The company's capabilities span from research and design to engineering and manufacturing.
Adient is one of the largest suppliers of just-in-time seating in China.
In China, Adient operates through seven joint ventures and approximately 38 manufacturing locations across 24 cities.
Partnerships with major auto groups in China have given Adient broad market penetration relative to its seating competitors.

facts: Rivian announced an increase in the initial capacity of its upcoming Georgia assembly plant by 50% to 300,000 units.
Construction of the Georgia assembly plant is scheduled to start in 2026.
The Department of Energy (DOE) loan commitment for the Georgia facility was reduced from $6.6 billion to around $4.5 billion.
Rivian will begin drawing on the DOE loan in 2027, with a production start goal of late 2028.
The previous $6.6 billion loan commitment had Rivian tapping the funding in 2028, making the new deal allow earlier access to government funds.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated that accessing the DOE dollars sooner and faster will help secure more capacity and volume sooner.
Renegotiating the Department of Energy loan was done specifically to facilitate increasing the first phase capacity from 200,000 units to 300,000 units.
After the first phase is operational, Rivian's total capacity will exceed 500,000 units, which would support positive free cash flow.
Rivian reported Q1 revenue of $1.38 billion, up 11% year-over-year, against Bloomberg consensus estimates of $1.39 billion.
Rivian posted a Q1 loss per share of $0.33 versus the expected $0.72.
Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was a loss of $472 million, wider than last year, but gross profit reached $119 million for the third consecutive quarter.
The automotive segment posted a gross profit loss, while the software and services segment posted a $180 million gross profit, nearly a 60% gain from a year ago.
Rivian maintained its full-year 2026 adjusted EBITDA loss projection in a range of $1.80 billion to $2.10 billion.
Full-year 2026 capital expenditures are projected between $1.95 billion and $2.05 billion.
Rivian reaffirmed its 2026 vehicle delivery guidance of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles.
Last month, Rivian unlocked an additional $1 billion in investment from joint venture partner Volkswagen.
The JV with Volkswagen successfully tested the first vehicle from their partnership, the VW ID.EVERY1, which utilizes Rivian software and platform technology.
In March, Rivian announced a robotaxi deal with Uber involving $1.25 billion in investment for up to 50,000 autonomous R2 EVs.
Rivian ended Q1 with $4.83 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, and $5.39 billion in total liquidity.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated that Volkswagen and Uber investments, plus the DOE loan and existing cash, provide $13.6 billion of accessible liquidity to fund ramp-up activities over the next several years.
source_title: Rivian DOE loan for Georgia plant shrinks but available sooner; reports smaller Q1 loss than expected and reaffirms outlook

facts: Adient announced its latest breakthrough innovation, ModuTec, on January 23, 2026.
ModuTec is a modular seat design solution intended to greatly simplify the seat build process and enable higher levels of automation.
ModuTec was developed after in-depth analysis of the needs of automakers, manufacturing locations and consumers.
With the ModuTec assembly process, build of the seat module happens offline and is then seamlessly sequenced into the main JIT line, reducing assembly time from minutes to seconds.
The ModuTec solution can help achieve higher efficiency, lower costs, and enable automation in ways the industry has not yet seen.
Traditional manufacturing of vehicle seats relies on complex and integrated processes that are labor-intensive.
ModuTec’s use of modularity revolutionizes this approach to enhance assembly efficiency.
ModuTec simplifies production workflows for automotive seating.
ModuTec enables automation and reduces floor space requirements.
ModuTec enhances seat comfort.
ModuTec enables modularity and component integration.
ModuTec allows for craftsmanship and warranty improvements.
ModuTec enables easier serviceability and dealer upgrades.

Pierre Alain Caillaut, Adient’s Vice President of Engineering EMEA, stated that ModuTec introduces a modular seating approach that significantly improves assembly efficiency while driving overall cost optimization.

Caillaut noted that by standardizing interfaces and clearly structuring individual assembly steps, ModuTec creates a scalable, automation-ready manufacturing concept that reduces process complexity and can integrate OEM-specific features in a controlled and cost-effective way.
Adient is a global leader in automotive seating with more than 65,000 employees in 29 countries.
Adient operates approximately 200 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's expertise spans every step of the automotive seat-making process, from research and design to engineering and manufacturing.
Adient delivers its products into millions of vehicles every year.
source_title: Adient announces breakthrough ModuTec innovation

facts: Adient is a global leader in automotive seating.
Q1 GAAP net loss and EPS diluted were $(22)M and $(0.28), respectively.
Q1 Adj.-EPS diluted was $0.35.
Q1 Adj.-EBITDA was $207M, a $11M y-o-y improvement.
Adj.-EBITDA margins increased from 5.6% to 5.7% y-o-y.
Gross debt and net debt totaled ~$2.4B and ~$1.5B, respectively, at December 31, 2025.

The company returned $25M to its shareholders in Q1 FY2026, repurchasing ~1.2M shares.
Adient raised its FY26 guidance for revenue, Adj.-EBITDA and FCF to $14.6B, $880M, and $125M, respectively.
The raised guidance is underpinned by an improved vehicle production outlook and the company’s expectation of continued positive business performance.
Adient has more than 65,000 employees in 29 countries.
Adient operates ~200 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs, ranging from complete seating systems to individual components.
Jerome Dorlack is Adient’s president and chief executive officer.
Mark Oswald is Adient’s executive vice president and chief financial officer.

Adient is a leading seating supplier to the automotive industry with about a midteens share of the global market, including unconsolidated joint venture business.
Adient's market share in China is around 20%, down from about 45% following the sale of its main joint venture there at the end of fiscal 2021.
Unconsolidated revenue from Adient's joint ventures was about $3.5 billion in fiscal 2025.
Consolidated China revenue for Adient was $1.3 billion.
The company is headquartered in Ireland but has corporate offices in the Detroit area.
Fiscal 2025 consolidated revenue, which excludes joint venture sales, was $14.5 billion.
