facts: Georgia’s economy is projected to grow by 2.4% in 2025, a slowdown from the 3.1% the state saw in 2024.
The state’s GDP is projected to grow faster than the nation’s, which forecasters project to grow by 1.6% in 2025.
The probability Georgia will slip into recession is 25%.
The number of jobs will rise by 1% in 2025, slower than the 1.5% gain estimated for Georgia in 2024 but higher than the national job growth rate of 0.6%.
Georgia’s unemployment rate is poised to increase from 3.7% to 4%, lower than the 4.3% expected at the U.S. level.
The increase in unemployment will be due to slowed hiring rather than layoffs.
Health care and housing will lead Georgia’s economic growth in 2025.

Homebuilders will ramp up construction of single-family homes in the coming year because of slightly lower mortgage rates, favorable demographic trends, and a housing shortage that has been apparent since the pandemic.
Georgia’s homebuilders will increase the number of single-family homes built in 2025 by 9%.
Home prices — which have increased 65% since before the COVID-19 pandemic — are expected to hold steady.
Georgia is likely to see job losses in retail and information jobs due to advances in technology and competition from online retailers.
Georgia has broken records in the number of new jobs and industries attracted to the state, and that will slow this year due to the slowdown of the national and global economy.
With the build-out of existing projects, Georgia is poised to see thousands of new jobs created over the next few years.

Advantages for economic development include a favorable mix of industries, supportive demographics, excellent transportation, logistics infrastructure, a competitive tax climate, and low costs of living and doing business.
source_title: Uga’s economic outlook for Georgia in 2025

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), in cooperation with the Golden Isles Development Authority, announced that the Innovation Isles Aerospace Park has earned a “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification with “Select” status.
The site’s new “GRAD Select” status provides an additional seal of approval that makes it especially attractive for future industrial development.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated the park is a key addition to the GRAD site portfolio due to its strategic location.
The park is adjacent to the Brunswick Golden Isles Airport, which offers daily service to and from Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The park is located only a short distance to the Port of Brunswick, a national Ro/Ro hub.
Combined with the speed-to-market advantages of a GRAD site, the park is positioned as a prime location for manufacturers and aerospace service or R&D companies.
The Innovation Isles Aerospace Park spans 650 acres.
The site is located off Innovation Drive and Jetport Road in Brunswick.
The site is less than 30 minutes away from the Port of Brunswick, which is one of two facilities that make Georgia Ports Authority the second busiest in the nation for total Ro/Ro trade.

The site is just over five miles from Interstate 95, described as a major east coast artery from Miami to the Houlton–Woodstock Border Crossing between Maine and the Canadian province of New Brunswick.
Innovation Isles Aerospace Park is less than 10 minutes from Brunswick’s central business district.
Existing road access through the adjacent North Glynn Commerce Park will be expanded to become the primary entrance.
Golden Isles Development Authority CEO and President Ryan Moore called the GRAD certification a significant milestone that underscores their commitment to economic growth in the Golden Isles.
Ryan Moore emphasized that workforce strategies must evolve in tandem with business attraction efforts as they market the site for investment.
In 2020, the Golden Isles Development Authority and the Brunswick–Golden Isles Chamber of Commerce launched a community-wide talent development strategy for Brunswick and the Golden Isles.
The talent development strategy was facilitated by the Carl Vinson Institute of Government at the University of Georgia.
Initiatives from these efforts include a teacher externship program that allows middle through high school educators to step out of the classroom and into local industries.
The teacher externship program equips participants with firsthand exposure to guide students toward fulfilling careers in their community.
Student industry tours are led by the CEO of the Golden Isles College and Career Academy and the Talent Development Strategy Leader.
Completing the GRAD certification program is a hands-on way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development in their regions.

To qualify for GRAD status, available sites must meet the program’s due diligence standards, be reviewed by a third party, and earn final approval from a board of advisors comprised of public and private sector economic development professionals.
The GRAD Select status indicates that a site has met or exceeded more rigorous certification requirements.

The Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Program offers more than 60 industrial certified sites ready for fast-track industrial projects due to extensive prior planning and investment into site readiness.

links.context: Aerial views of GRAD certified sites and property details

source_title: Innovation Isles Aerospace Park Certified ‘Ready for Accelerated Development’ - AllOnGeorgia

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced that Archer Aviation Inc., an aerospace company advancing sustainable urban air mobility, will create 1,000 jobs and invest $118 million over 10 years in a new state-of-the-art electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL) manufacturing facility located alongside the Covington Municipal Airport in Newton County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia has made fostering innovation a priority as they work with companies to bring the jobs of the future to the No. 1 state for business.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Archer Aviation Inc. is based in Santa Clara, CA, and is currently developing and manufacturing eVTOL aircraft for use in Urban Air Mobility networks.
Archer Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein announced the company's decision to locate its first manufacturing facility in Covington after many months of discussion and evaluation.

Adam Goldstein stated that Archer's goal of creating Urban Air Mobility networks across major cities nationwide requires ramping up aircraft production, and the new facility represents a huge step toward meeting this challenge.
Archer Vice President of Manufacturing Glen Burks described the new manufacturing facility as a crucial next step in bringing their vision for eVTOL travel to life.
Glen Burks expressed excitement over Covington as a location and partner, noting they look forward to breaking ground in the coming months.
Archer plans to construct a new aircraft manufacturing facility adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport in Newton County, which was established in 1963 as part of a long-term airport master plan.
The site is strategically accessible by Georgia’s robust interstate system via I-20, and large adjacent parcels of land were set aside for future development.
The new Archer facility will use one of these adjacent parcels.
Archer’s new facility, located at the Covington Municipal Airport, will consist of three buildings across more than 96 acres.
Construction is expected to begin in December 2022 and follow a phased buildout of eVTOL aircraft production, with eventual capacity able to produce up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Hiring is expected to begin in late 2023, and the facility is slated to open in the first half of 2024.
Covington Mayor Steve Horton expressed excitement for Archer Aviation to join the city's business community.

Mayor Steve Horton noted that the Covington Municipal Airport is a point of pride for the city and that Archer's presence within the airport plan is another distinction, continuing Covington's trend on the cutting edge for quality jobs and new technology.
Newton County Industrial Development Authority Chairman Lanier Sims expressed excitement for Archer to join the community.

Lanier Sims stated that Archer fits perfectly with Newton County's current industry mix, will be an incredible asset to the Covington airport, and emphasized the Development Authority's commitment to ensuring the workforce is prepared for outstanding jobs from Archer Aviation.
Lanier Sims expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their leadership in keeping Georgia the number one state for business.
Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick welcomed Archer Aviation to metro Atlanta, citing community strength to help ensure the success of their new facility.

Katie Kirkpatrick highlighted Metro Atlanta’s expertise in the aviation industry coupled with support from academic, business, civic and government partners as making the region the perfect place to invest in a new facility.

The Global Commerce Industrial Projects team represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) on this competitive project in partnership with the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Center of Innovation’s Aerospace industry team, Georgia EMC, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Tech.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that technology and innovation continue to drive change across strategic industries, and Archer Aviation is on the forefront of a technological evolution in the aviation industry using an innovative, state-of-the art eVTOL aircraft design to sustainably tackle mobility issues in urban centers.

Pat Wilson expressed thrill over Archer's decision to invest in Georgia and highlighted the Center of Innovation’s specialized Aerospace team's involvement in fostering Georgia’s aerospace and defense industries.

Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies and maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline, with a dozen high schools that offer training in the field, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.

The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has been cultivating an ecosystem of opportunities for innovative companies like Archer Aviation for more than 10 years by building and connecting Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) networks for the commercialization of Advanced Air Mobility and UAS Systems.
source_title: GA: Electric Vertical Aircraft Manufacturing Company Archer to Create 1,000 Jobs in Covington | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Newton County continues to grow, attracting retail shops and industrial and manufacturing plants.
The products made here range from the fibers used to make disposable diapers to dry cereals to golf balls.
Many of the plants are considered state-of-the-art.
Last year, Michelin Tread Technologies upgraded its site in Covington to allow burn off of volatile organic compounds (VOC), making the plant more environmentally friendly.
VOCs released in to the air can combine with nitrogen oxides to form ground-level ozone.
The new technology Pactiv Corporation, Covington, was opened in 1984 and employees almost 500 people.
Pactiv Corporation makes packaging for food service industries, and grocery and convenience stores.
Bard Urological Division, Covington, was established in 1967 and employs over 500 people.
Bard Urological Division manufactures health care products for urological procedures such as catheters, trays and other tools.
Specialty Yarn and Converting in Porterdale has been in operation since 1917.
Specialty Yarn and Converting was recently purchased by Continental tires.
Specialty Yarn and Converting engineers textiles such as rubber thread and yarns.
The Specialty Yarn and Converting plant employs over 200 people.
Oldcastle Glass, Covington, opened in 1981 and employs over 100 people.
Oldcastle Glass creates custom-engineered curtain and window walls, architectural windows, storefront systems, doors, skylights and architectural glass.
Guardian Automotive, Covington, was opened in 1969 and is responsible for creating decorative automotive trims and insulated glass.
Guardian Automotive employs 310 people.
FiberVisions, Covington, employs 225 people and opened in 1967.
FiberVisions makes the fibers, called polypropylene staple fibers, that go in to the making of disposable mops and dust cloths, disposable diapers and feminine products.
General Mills is celebrating its 150th anniversary this year.
The General Mills Newton County plant makes dry cereal and employs 400 people.
The General Mills Newton County plant opened here in 1989.
International Storage, Covington, makes metal shelving and storage products at its Covington plant.
International Storage opened in 1986 and employs 150 people.
SKC, Inc., Covington makes polymer and polyester film used in packaging, industrial, imaging, electrical, bio-compostable and solar applications.
Production at the SKC Newton County plant began in the spring of 1999, using cutting edge robotics and technology designed and engineered to transition into the 21 Century.
The SKC Newton County plant employs 331 people.
Clarion Metals Corporation, Covington, is a full-service metal stamping plant with tool and die, engineering, subassembly and special packaging capabilities.
Clarion Metals Corporation opened in 1989 and employs 180 people.
Bridgestone Sports, Covington, makes golf balls and assembles golf clubs at its Newton County plant.
The Bridgestone Sports plant opened in 1989 and employs 172 people.
Beaver Manufacturing in Mansfield opened in 1971 and makes yarns used to reinforce hoses.
Beaver Manufacturing employs 140 people.
SGD Glass, based in France, has a branch plant in Newton County where they make and decorate luxury glass bottles for cosmetics and perfumes.
The SGD Glass Covington branch opened in 1996 and employs 290 people.
Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc., Covington, is a state-of-the-art facility that opened in 1997.
The 325 employees at the Nisshinbo Automotive plant make molded disc pads for braking system.
Rock Tenn in Covington opened in 1972 and employs 110 people.
Rock Tenn makes corrugated and fiber boxes.
Michelin Tread Technologies, Covington, was upgraded to allow burn off of VOCs before being released into the air.
The Michelin Tread Technologies plant opened in 1999 and manufactures tires.
Leftover rubber tread at the Michelin Tread Technologies plant is recycled.

Contract Packaging, part of Kelly Products, manufactures and packages for chemical companies by formulating small package production and distribution of insecticides, pool and spa cehcmicals, and wildlife scents and attractants.
source_title: Manufacturing in Newton County employs thousands - The Covington News

facts: The facility will support Archer’s planned production, with Stellantis, of up to 650 Midnight aircraft annually.
Archer Aviation Inc. provided an update at the end of July on the construction progress of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Ga.
The company reaffirmed an expected 2024 completion date for the facility in a release.
Archer is the designer and developer of Midnight, its electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft that is said to represent the potential for “a significant step change for aviation.”
Midnight is built around Archer’s proprietary electric powertrain and is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation.
Earlier this year, Archer completed the first build phase for the facility, including grading and foundation work.
As of July 31, the company was nearing completion of phase two, the erection of the exterior walls and roof.
Phase three (interior fit out) and phase four (equipment load-in and power on) are on track to be completed in the coming months.
Once complete, the facility will measure nearly 400,000 square feet.
The completed facility would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aviation industry.
Archer’s buildout is taking place in close partnership with the automaker Stellantis to mass-produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft together.
Adam Goldstein, CEO and founder of Stellantis, stated that the company is tracking towards completing this manufacturing facility by the end of the year.
Once complete, Archer and Stellantis will focus on ramping production up to 650 aircraft a year, aiming to be the first in the eVTOL industry to achieve scaled manufacturing.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to travel at speeds up to 150 mph, turning hour-long ground commutes into minutes in the air.
It is designed for back-to-back flights of 20-50 miles, with minimal charge time in between flights.
Midnight is described as “100 times quieter than a helicopter at cruising altitudes,” making it “nearly inaudible from the streets below.”
The aircraft can carry a pilot, up to four passengers, and carry-on luggage.
Midnight is designed with redundant systems across the aircraft, allowing Archer to target similar levels of safety as commercial liners.
In preparation for the facility’s completion, Archer showcased the Midnight aircraft at Atlantic Aviation’s new terminal at DeKalb–Peachtree Municipal Airport.

Guests at the showcase included government officials from Georgia’s Office of the Governor, Georgia’s Congressional Offices, the City of Atlanta, Georgia’s State Legislature, the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), and other local governments.
source_title: Archer Aviation Nears Completion Of High-Volume Manufacturing Facility In Georgia | Design 2 Part Magazine

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. is a developer of electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
Archer closed agreements with Synovus Bank and Evans General Contractors LLC for the financing and development of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
Construction is already underway on the first phase of the Covington facility build-out.
The first phase covers approximately 350,000 square feet to support production of up to 650 aircraft per year.
This capacity would make it the world’s largest eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility by volume.
The agreement secures at least $65 million in financing, which represents a substantial majority of the cost of construction for the first phase.

The announcement advances Archer’s strategy of creating the most capital efficient path to scaled manufacturing, in conjunction with previously announced incentives from the state of Georgia and Archer’s partnership with Stellantis.
Archer partners with top tier aerospace suppliers, such as Honeywell and Safran, for a majority of Midnight’s components.
Partnering with these suppliers streamlines the manufacturing and assembly process dramatically while allowing for high-volume output.
Phase one of the high-volume manufacturing facility is on target to be completed in 2024.
Midnight’s planned entry into service is in 2025.
Phase two of the facility provides the capability to expand the operation to approximately 900,000 square feet.
The phase two expansion supports long-term production targets of more than 2,000 aircraft per year.
Adam Goldstein is Archer’s founder and CEO.
Goldstein stated that the ability to manufacture eVTOL aircraft at scale is critical to building a successful business in the industry.
He noted that securing the design-build and financing agreements for the world’s first and largest by volume eVTOL manufacturing facility marks a major step forward on their manufacturing buildout.
Goldstein added that they achieved attractive terms consistent with a capital efficient strategy to get to commercialization.
He emphasized that these agreements, along with the partnership with Stellantis, provide flexibility to ramp up capacity and become the leading scale manufacturer of eVTOL aircraft globally.

Archer selected Synovus Bank as its financing partner and Evans General Contractors LLC as its development partner for the Covington project after considering companies with expertise in high-volume manufacturing facilities in the southeast United States.

Goldstein stated that Archer is committed as a long-term partner to the Covington, Georgia community and is thrilled to work with companies sharing their vision for a state-of-the-art U.S. manufacturing facility.

Archer aims to transform urban travel by replacing 60-to-90 minute commutes by car with estimated 10-to-20 minute electric aircraft flights that are safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost competitive with ground transportation.
Archer’s Midnight is a piloted, four passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
The Midnight aircraft will be manufactured at the Covington, Georgia facility before being deployed to different customers and markets in the U.S. and abroad.
Archer's team is based in Santa Clara, California.
source_title: Archer closes financing to complete eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility - Aerospace Manufacturing and Design

facts: Archer Aviation is an electric vehicle company planning to go public via a SPAC merger with Atlas Crest Investment Corp (ACIC).
The company came out of stealth mode less than a year ago relative to the article's publication date.
Archer differentiates its offering as the first eVTOL backed by a major airline.
United Airlines will contribute expertise in airspace management to assist Archer with developing battery-powered, short-haul aircraft.
Once the aircraft meet United's operating and business requirements, United together with Mesa Airlines would acquire a fleet of up to 200 electric aircraft.
United Airlines is an investor in Archer Aviation.
Archer expects to open its first manufacturing facility in 2022.
First sales are expected in 2024, bringing in $42 million in revenues.
Revenues are projected to jump +2,375% to over a billion dollars in 2025.
The SPAC transaction is expected to close in Q2-2021, after which the ticker will change from ACIC to ACHR.
Archer unveiled its all-new eVTOL production aircraft named the Midnight at an open house event in Palo Alto, California.
The Midnight is a pilot-plus-four-passenger electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft.
The Midnight represents the evolution of Archer's demonstrator eVTOL aircraft, the Maker, which validated proprietary twelve-tilt-six configuration and key enabling technologies.
The new Midnight model is expected to have a payload of over 1,000 pounds and carry four passengers plus a pilot.
It will be optimized for back-to-back short distance trips of 20-miles, with a charging time of approximately 10 minutes in-between.
Archer is working to certify its new Midnight model with the FAA by late-2024 and launch in 2025.
The company aims to make about 250 battery-electric air taxis in 2025 and scale up production in the following years, after setting a goal of getting its aircraft certified by the end of 2024.
Stellantis announced a partnership with Archer Aviation to build the all-electric Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
Stellantis plans to provide $150 million of equity capital for Archer to use at its discretion in 2023 and 2024.
Stellantis intends to increase its shareholding in Archer by purchasing stock in the open market.
Stellantis will aid in the manufacture of Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft by contributing personnel, technology, and engineering know-how to the venture.
source_title: Invest in Urban Air Mobility with Archer Aviation Stock - Nanalyze

facts: Archer Aviation announced that it received the certificate of occupancy for its approximately 400,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
The company plans to begin initial production in early 2025.
Archer broke ground on the facility in March 2023.
The company is finalizing its agreement with global automaker Stellantis, which will become its exclusive contract manufacturer.
Archer aims to manufacture two aircraft per month by the end of next year.
By 2030, it plans to produce 650 electric takeoff and landing aircraft annually.
On a Nov. 7 investor call, Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein named New York City, Los Angeles, the San Francisco Bay Area and Miami as the company’s first U.S. markets.
The company has interest from the United Arab Emirates and Japan.
Archer plans to deploy a network of air taxis in Southern California in time for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which will be held in Los Angeles.
Archer worked closely with Stellantis to construct the Covington facility.
The facility will produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft, which is optimized to fly routes of 20 to 50 miles at up to 150 mph, with a quick recharge between flights.
The air taxi can carry up to four passengers and their carry-on luggage, plus the pilot.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf stated that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for the two companies.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp said in a statement that today’s announcement reaffirms the state’s commitment to working with innovative companies like Archer to create quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.
The incoming Trump administration could prove friendly to air taxi developers as they work toward commercialization.
President-elect Donald Trump expressed support for advanced air mobility technology such as air taxis in a 2023 campaign speech.
source_title: Archer Aviation completes Georgia air taxi factory | Smart Cities Dive

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. will invest $118 million over the next 10 years to build a new assembly plant in Covington, GA.
The facility will produce electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
The new plant will be located alongside the Covington Municipal Airport.
The facility is expected to create 1,000 jobs.
Archer Aviation Inc. is based in Santa Clara, CA.
Archer is currently developing and manufacturing eVTOL aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s founder and CEO, announced the decision to locate the company's first manufacturing facility in Covington as it progresses toward commercialization.
Goldstein stated that creating urban air mobility networks across major cities nationwide requires ramping production of their aircraft.
Archer’s new facility will consist of three buildings across more than 96 acres.
Construction is expected to begin in December 2022 and follow a phased buildout.
At full capacity, the plant will be able to produce up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Hiring is expected to begin in late 2023.
The facility is slated to open in the first half of 2024.

Glen Burks, Archer’s vice president of manufacturing, stated that the new manufacturing facility is a crucial next step in bringing their vision for eVTOL travel to life and expressed happiness with Covington as a location and partner.
source_title: Aircraft Assembly Plant to Open in Georgia, Create 1,000 Jobs | ASSEMBLY

facts: California-based Archer Aviation Inc. was founded in 2018 and is building next-gen eVTOL aircraft to redefine urban air mobility.
Archer has a $5.94 billion market cap and is advancing both commercial and defense applications.
Archer's ride-share model, Archer Direct, targets quick rollout by using existing airport and helipad infrastructure.
Archer Aviation’s stock has been up by a staggering 213% over the past year and 77.6% in just six months.
Investor enthusiasm has been fueled by steady progress toward FAA certification and major production milestones.
Archer Aviation soared 22.9% last Tuesday on the Olympics win, and another 9.1% surge came in Friday’s trading session.
Cantor Fitzgerald issued a bullish outlook for Archer Aviation.
Archer Aviation reported fiscal Q1 2025 results released on May 12.
The electric air taxi maker reported a net loss of $93.4 million.
Its loss per share narrowed significantly to $0.17, halving from the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA dropped further into the red at negative $109 million, compared to a $86.8 million loss a year ago.
Non-GAAP operating expenses landed at just $113.1 million.
Archer remains well-capitalized with over $1 billion in cash and equivalents.
Archer has plans to launch operations in the UAE later this year.
Archer plans to build out a New York City air taxi network.
CEO Adam Goldstein highlighted strong progress on both the civil and defense fronts, stating the team continues to deepen strategic partnerships while gearing up for commercialization.

Archer announced a partnership with Palantir Technologies (PLTR), tapping into AI and data analytics to streamline development and enhance efficiency, and potentially accelerating its go-to-market timeline.
Archer expects a Q2 adjusted EBITDA loss between $100 million and $120 million.
Analysts anticipate losses to shrink by 30% year over year to $0.99 per share in fiscal 2025.
Archer Aviation was named the official air taxi partner for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.
Over 15 million visitors are expected at the Games, offering Archer a global showcase for its Midnight aircraft.
The plan is to fly VIPs, athletes, and fans across LA using a fleet of electric air taxis, which are quieter than helicopters and built with aviation-grade safety systems.
Midnight’s routes will connect Olympic hot spots like SoFi Stadium, LAX, and Santa Monica, operating from purpose-built vertiports.
This deal positions Archer as a transportation partner and as a symbol of innovation and sustainability.
It may also grease the regulatory wheels for FAA certification, fast-tracking Archer’s commercial ambitions in the U.S. and beyond.
Cantor Fitzgerald’s Andres Sheppard reaffirmed his “Overweight” rating and $13 price target on the news, which he sees as a game-changer.
The company aims to launch its air taxi service by Q4 2025, with the UAE likely as its first stop.
Archer has a deep bench of partners, such as Anduril, car manufacturer Stellantis (STLA), the U.S. Department of Defense, and United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (UAL).
ACHR stock has a consensus “Moderate Buy” rating overall.

The Street-high price target is $13.50.

facts: Archer Aviation received an additional $55 million investment from Stellantis under the companies’ strategic funding agreement following the achievement of its transition flight test milestone last month.
This latest investment builds on Stellantis’ series of open market purchases of 8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock in March of this year that was previously announced.
During 2023, Stellantis invested $110 million in Archer through a combination of open market stock purchases and investments under the companies’ strategic funding agreement.
Archer remains on track to complete construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Georgia later this year.
This first phase of the build out is a ~350,000 square foot facility on an ~100 acre site designed to support production of up to 650 aircraft annually.
The facility would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer’s goal with this facility remains to establish a factory that can support its planned commercial ramp up by leveraging the expertise of Stellantis as its contract manufacturer.

Carlos Tavares, Stellantis CEO, stated: “Few things rival the excitement of seeing a dream take flight. I applaud the innovation, expertise and hard work of the engineering and manufacturing teams from Stellantis and Archer.”
Carlos Tavares added: “With this additional investment in Archer, we remain on course for a future where freedom of mobility extends beyond today’s roads.”

Adam Goldstein, Archer CEO, stated: “The commitment by Stellantis to Archer has been unrivaled, from its foresight to provide the manufacturing expertise and capital needed to accelerate Archer’s business objectives, to the strategic vision and steadfast support from Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares.”

Adam Goldstein added: “Together, we’re working to redefine urban transportation, opening new worlds of opportunity for citizens across the globe by providing more efficient access to people, places, and events across the regions they live in.”
Stellantis has been a strategic partner to Archer since 2020 through various collaboration initiatives.
Stellantis has been an investor in Archer since 2021.
During this time, Archer has leveraged Stellantis’ deep manufacturing, supply chain, and design expertise in connection with Archer’s efforts to design, develop, and commercialize its eVTOL aircraft.

Archer’s goal is to transform urban travel, replacing 60–90 minute commutes by car with estimated 10–20 minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.
Archer’s Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
source_title: Stellantis invests additional $55 million in Archer following recent flight test milestone - JEC

facts: Archer Aviation has been officially designated as the Air Taxi Provider for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic and Paralympic Games.

The company plans to deploy its state-of-the-art Midnight eVTOL (Electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing) aircraft to transport VIPs, enhance security operations, and facilitate travel for fans among the city’s key Olympic venues.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced operating and financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ended December 31, 2024.

Archer announced a comprehensive “Launch Edition” commercialization program for its Midnight aircraft to deploy commercially in dozens of early adopter markets in advance of FAA type certification.
Abu Dhabi Aviation (ADA) will be Archer’s first Launch Edition customer, with plans to deploy an initial fleet of Midnight aircraft beginning later this year.
Archer plans to provide ADA with a team of pilots, technicians and engineers to support the initial operational ramp.
In December, Archer announced an exclusive partnership with Anduril to jointly develop a hybrid vertical take off and landing (VTOL) aircraft for critical defense applications.
Archer believes the potential demand for the Anduril joint development aircraft is stronger than expected.
Archer is working closely with Anduril on future vertical lift use cases for defense, expecting sizable programs of record without FAA certification.
Archer is now starting production of its first Midnight aircraft at its ARC facility in Covington, GA.
Archer is planning to build up to 10 Midnight aircraft this year to support ongoing certification testing programs and deployments with key partners.

Archer’s spending in Q4’24 remained within its guided range and stayed nearly flat for the second straight quarter.
The company's liquidity includes a Q4’24 ending cash balance of $835M and $302M raised through an equity offering in February.
Archer’s founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that with aircraft production starting at ARC, they are on track to deliver their first revenue-generating Midnight aircraft later this year.
Archer is designing and developing key enabling technologies and aircraft necessary to power the future of aviation.

facts: Archer Aviation has raised an additional $850 million following the White House’s recent announcement of an Executive Order by President Trump to implement an eVTOL Integration Pilot Program in the United States.
The eVTOL Integration Pilot Program is focused on accelerating the deployment of electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft in the U.S.

Archer intends to closely coordinate with the White House, Department of Transportation, and the Federal Aviation Administration on integrating the program into its plans to ramp operations in the U.S. ahead of the LA 28 Olympic Games.
Archer will serve as the official air taxi provider for the LA 28 Olympic Games and Team USA.
Archer believes cross-industry collaboration will be key to the success of the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program and the U.S. achieving its goal of “dominance” within this new category of aircraft.

Archer is showcasing its Midnight aircraft at the Paris Air Show and hosting delegations from more than 20 countries, including leadership from partner organizations in the U.A.E., which is Archer’s first target “Launch Edition” market.
Archer CEO and founder Adam Goldstein is set to meet with United States Secretary of Transportation Sean P. Duffy and acting FAA administrator Chris Rocheleau at the Paris Air Show.
The new capital bolsters Archer's balance sheet, cementing a pro forma liquidity position of approximately $2 billion.
Archer is strategically positioned to execute across its engineering, certification, and commercialization efforts in the United States and abroad.
Financing was provided for the purchase and sale of 85,000,000 shares of Archer’s Class A common stock at $10 per share through a registered direct offering.

Net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, focusing on expanding commercial capabilities, particularly infrastructure to support recently announced initiatives in the U.S. and “Launch Edition” markets, and developing an AI-based aviation software platform.

The Class A common stock was offered pursuant to an automatic shelf registration statement on Form S-3ASR (File No. 333-284812) filed with the SEC on February 11, 2025, which became automatically effective upon filing.
Moelis & Company is acting as the exclusive placement agent in connection with this offering.
source_title: Archer Raises $850 Million Amid eVTOL Push by White House | Travel Agent Central

facts: Archer Aviation and United Airlines announced plans to launch the first air taxi route in Chicago between O’Hare International Airport (ORD) and Vertiport Chicago from 2025.
Archer designs and develops electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
The pair are seeking to provide a “safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost-competitive alternative to ground transportation.”

The initial point to point route using Archer’s eVTOL aircraft will reduce the journey from O’Hare International Airport (ORD) and Vertiport Chicago to ten minutes, down from over one hour at rush hour.

Michael Leskinen, President of United Airlines Ventures said both Archer and United are committed to decarbonising air travel and leveraging innovative technologies to deliver on the promise of the electrification of the aviation industry.
Once operational, United is excited to offer customers a more sustainable, convenient, and cost-effective mode of transportation during their commutes to the airport.

Illinois Governor JB Pritzker highlighted the sustainability potential from the partnership, stating it paves the way for a more sustainable future and helps achieve the goal of transitioning to 100 per cent clean energy by 2050 while saving Illinoisans money and creating thousands of good paying jobs.
Archer and United’s early launch routes will focus on airport to city centre transportation aka. “trunk” routes before expanding to “branch” routes connecting the surrounding communities.
source_title: United and Archer's first commercial electric air taxi route

facts: Archer Aviation and Stellantis are ramping up production of their "Midnight" eVTOL air taxi to deploy "hundreds or thousands" of aircraft for the 2028 LA Olympics.
Archer Aviation, a Silicon Valley-based electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) vehicle developer, is moving at full speed toward full-scale production by the 2030s.
The company and its main investor Stellantis unveiled the "Midnight" four-seater air taxi at this year's Paris air show.
Archer's Midnight air taxi has a range of up to 87 nautical miles (161 kilometers) but is specifically optimized for flights between 17 and 43 nautical miles.
The aircraft is engineered to reach speeds of up to 130 knots (241 kilometers per hour).
Archer CEO and co-founder Adam Goldstein aims to have "hundreds or thousands" of eVTOL aircraft flying over the Los Angeles Olympic Games in 2028.
According to a June 19th statement by Goldstein, operations will transition from only a few test flights per day to thousands per month.
Stellantis has increased its strategic shareholding in Archer by purchasing Archer stock in the open market.
The world's first high-volume eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility is currently being constructed on an approximately 100-acre campus in Covington, Georgia.
Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares stated that both teams are moving full speed ahead to bring scalable manufacturing of Archer's aircraft online in mid-2024.
Tavares noted the partnership is setting the standard for cross-industry collaboration and remains a key pillar of Stellantis' strategy to lead global mobility.
Stellantis personnel are embedded across all areas of Archer's operations, including manufacturing, engineering, supply chain, quality, facilities, and human resources.
Experienced personnel from Stellantis are working full-time with the Archer team to support operational readiness, focusing on manufacturing automation and component sourcing.
The manufacturing facility is set to go online in mid-2024.
Archer's manufacturing operations can produce 650 air taxis annually, with potential for expansion to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft annually.
Both companies aim to become the world's top aircraft manufacturing facility in terms of volume.

Tavares and Goldstein believe the necessary technology, public approval, and updated air traffic control infrastructure to integrate the aircraft into the National Airspace System will be available by the target timeline.
Goldstein emphasized that high-volume manufacturing is critical to commercialization at scale and that partnering with Stellantis helps realize the opportunity to redefine urban transportation.
source_title: Archer Aviation wants 'hundreds' of eVTOLs in the air by 2028 Olympics

facts: Archer Aviation is pioneering electric vertical takeoff and landing vehicles (eVTOLs) with a market cap of just $4.8 billion.
Morgan Stanley analysts believe the eVTOL industry could be worth $1.5 trillion by 2040.
The company plans to launch its own air taxi service alongside manufacturing, utilizing vertical integration to expand revenue opportunities and unlock economies of scale.
Archer Aviation teamed up with global automotive giant Stellantis to take advantage of expertise in large-scale production and supply chains.
Archer Aviation and Stellantis created a 400,000-square-foot facility (called ARC) in Georgia.
The ARC facility aims to produce 650 units of Archer Aviation's flagship Midnight eVTOL aircraft annually by 2030.
Fourth-quarter sales were just $300,000 (up from zero in the prior year period), mainly coming from early-stage partnership agreements instead of recurring operating revenue.
The Midnight eVTOL has not secured approvals from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) necessary for commercial use.
Operating losses reached almost $234.4 million as the company continues to pour cash into research and development to meet government testing requirements.
Archer Aviation holds just over $1 billion in cash and equivalents on its balance sheet.
The company will likely rely on continued equity dilution (issuing and selling more stock shares) to fund operations.
Management believes the company can deliver piloted flights later this year and provide bona fide air taxi services during the 2028 Olympic Games.
FAA approval timelines are outside management's direct control because government agencies are not known for rushing.

facts: Construction is complete on Archer Aviation's high-volume manufacturing facility at the Covington Municipal Airport.
The roughly 400,000-square-foot facility, codenamed ARC, is located in Covington, Georgia.
The facility has received its certificate of occupancy.
Tooling load-in will begin ahead of initial production efforts slated to start in early 2025.
Archer's partner Stellantis plans to contribute manufacturing expertise, capital investment, and an experienced workforce to the facility.
The goal is ramping production to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
The development will bring increased employment opportunities, supply-chain development, and enhanced regional prominence in high-tech sectors to Newton County.
The timing and scale of the facility align with the goals of the Newton County Industrial Development Authority (NCIDA).
The facility aims to provide high-quality jobs in production, engineering, and maintenance roles.
The facility aims to expand the local supplier ecosystem to support global-scale manufacturing operations.
The facility aims to elevate Newton County's infrastructure, talent base, and reputation for innovation.
Governor Brian Kemp called this development “the next step in that journey” for Newton County’s economic growth.
In early 2025, tooling load-in will transition toward manufacturing of Archer’s all-electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft, the Midnight model.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. has reaffirmed an expected 2024 completion date of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA.
In preparation for the facility's completion, Archer showcased its Midnight aircraft at Atlantic Aviation’s new terminal at DeKalb–Peachtree Municipal Airport.

Guests at the showcase included government officials from Georgia’s Office of the Governor, Georgia’s Congressional Offices, the City of Atlanta, Georgia’s State Legislature, the Federal Aviation Administration, and other local governments.
Archer completed the first build phase earlier this year, inclusive of grading and foundation work.
Archer is nearing completion of phase two, which involves the erection of the exterior walls and roof.
Phase three (interior fit out) and phase four (equipment load in and power on) are on track to be completed in the coming months.
Once complete, the facility will be nearly 400,000 square feet.
The facility will support Archer’s planned production of up to 650 Midnight aircraft annually.
This production volume would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aviation industry.
Archer’s buildout is taking place in close partnership with Stellantis.
Archer and Stellantis are continuing to progress their plans to mass-produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft together.

Archer’s goal is to be the first in the eVTOL industry to achieve scaled manufacturing.
Midnight is Archer’s electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, representing the potential for a significant step change for aviation.
Built around Archer’s proprietary electric powertrain, Midnight is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation.

facts: Archer Aviation is a frontrunner in the fledgling electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft sector.
Archer Aviation formed a strategic alliance with Soracle, which is a joint venture between Japan Airlines (JAL) and Sumitomo Corporation.
Soracle has obtained the right to purchase up to 100 of Archer’s Midnight aircraft, with a potential value reaching $500 million.
The deal structure includes milestone-based pre-delivery payments, which de-risks the financial outlook for Archer Aviation.
Sumitomo Corporation’s extensive business network within Japan offers support for establishing necessary infrastructure and navigating regulatory complexities.
Archer Aviation reported non-GAAP operating expenses of $96.8 million for the third quarter of fiscal year 2024.
Archer Aviation reported a net loss of $115.3 million (non-GAAP) for the third quarter of fiscal year 2024, which was in line with expectations.
The company's current cash and cash equivalents stand at $501.7 million to provide a cushion for near-term operational requirements.
Archer Aviation projects non-GAAP operating expenses of $95 million to $110 million for the fourth quarter of 2024.
Archer Aviation completed its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
The Covington, Georgia manufacturing facility has a capacity of 650 aircraft per year.
Plans call for production to commence at the Georgia facility in early 2025, aiming for a two-aircraft-per-month output by year-end.
The FAA released the final Special Federal Aviation Regulation (SFAR), which provides clarity and streamlines the certification process for eVTOL operations in the US.
Archer Aviation delivered the first Midnight aircraft to the US Air Force under its AFWERX contract.
Archer Aviation has a partnership with Stellantis that provides up to $400 million.
Battery technology limitations currently affect flight range and charging infrastructure needs for eVTOL aircraft.
Obtaining FAA Type Certification is crucial but complex, potentially subject to unforeseen delays that could significantly impact commercialization timelines.
Scaling operations demands significant capital, which could require further funding through debt or equity, potentially diluting existing shares.
Archer Aviation is focusing on successfully completing the FAA certification process and scaling production to capitalize on early market wins.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. was founded in 2018 by Brett Adcock and Adam Goldstein.
The company's headquarters are located in San Jose, California, having initially been based in Palo Alto.
Archer's flagship product is the four-passenger, one-pilot Midnight electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
The Midnight aircraft targets speeds of up to 150 mph and a range optimized for 20 to 50 miles.
Archer went public on September 17, 2021, through a SPAC merger with Atlas Crest Investment Corp., trading on the NYSE under ticker ACHR.
The IPO transaction provided approximately $1.1 billion in gross proceeds, including a $600 million PIPE investment.
United Airlines became a major strategic partner in 2021 by placing a conditional order for 200 Archer electric aircraft.
In August 2022, United Airlines paid Archer a $10 million deposit for the aircraft order.
In November 2022, Archer and United Airlines announced plans to launch the first electric air taxi route in the U.S.
The Midnight production aircraft was officially unveiled on November 17, 2022.
Stellantis became Archer's exclusive contract manufacturer in January 2023, providing up to $150 million in equity.
In August 2023, Archer secured a $215 million equity investment from Stellantis, Boeing, and United Airlines.
The Midnight aircraft received its FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate in August 2023.
Piloted flight tests for the Midnight began in early 2024.
In December 2024, Archer launched Archer Defense and partnered with Anduril Industries to develop hybrid-propulsion VTOL aircraft for military applications.
In August 2025, Archer acquired a patent portfolio and key employees from Overair to strengthen its intellectual property.
In August 2025, Archer acquired composite manufacturing assets from Mission Critical Composites to bring fabrication in-house.
The Midnight aircraft completed its longest piloted flight of 55 miles in 31 minutes in August 2025.
Archer was selected as the Official Air Taxi Provider for the LA28 Olympic Games in May 2025.
In June 2025, Archer partnered with Jetex for urban air mobility infrastructure development.
In September 2025, Soracle (a joint venture of Japan Airlines and Sumitomo Corporation) selected the Midnight aircraft for air taxi services in Osaka, Japan.
Archer announced a $30 million agreement with Ethiopian Airlines in March 2025.
As of late September 2025, Archer's total liquidity stood at approximately $1.73 billion.
Archer completed a 400,000-square-foot high-volume manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The company aims to ramp up Midnight production to two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer targets producing 650 Midnight aircraft annually by 2030.
Archer partnered with Palantir to integrate AI-driven aviation software for optimized production and competitive advantage.
The Midnight features a vectored-thrust lift+cruise design with six tiltrotors, 12 electric motors, and six independent battery packs.
The aircraft prioritizes quick 10-minute charging capabilities for rapid operational turnarounds.
Adam Goldstein serves as the CEO and Chairman of the Board of Directors.
Priya Gupta assumed the role of Acting Chief Financial Officer in July 2025.
Tom Muniz has served as Chief Technology Officer since March 2024.
Archer reported a net loss of $206.0 million for Q2 2025, up from $106.9 million in Q2 2024.
Operating expenses increased by 45.3% year-over-year to $176.1 million in Q2 2025, primarily driven by R&D expenditures.
Total debt stood at $81.6 million as of June 2025, with a low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.05.
Cash and cash equivalents were reported at $1.72 billion as of June 30, 2025.
Archer completed an $816.8 million registered direct offering in June 2025 to bolster its liquidity.
The company's market capitalization ranged between approximately $5.96 billion and $6.22 billion as of September 30, 2025.
Joby Aviation held the largest eVTOL commercial passenger market share at 22% as of early 2025, with Archer closely following at 18%.
The global eVTOL market is projected to grow from approximately $2.14 billion in 2025 to over $170 billion by 2034.
Industry supply chain vulnerabilities include bottlenecks for electric motors, advanced avionics, lightweight composites, and current battery technology limitations.
Rising costs for raw materials like lithium and cobalt impact component expenses across the eVTOL sector.
Infrastructure gaps currently exist for vertiports, charging stations, and maintenance networks in the UAM sector.
Archer expects to achieve FAA Type Certification for the Midnight aircraft by late 2025.
Only approximately 15% of final FAA Type Certification stage (Stage 4) compliance verification documents were approved as of early 2025.
A U.S. executive order in June 2025 created an eVTOL pilot program to accelerate regulatory frameworks.
Archer actively participates in the White House's eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP).
source_title: Archer Aviation: Soaring Ambitions in Urban Air Mobility | FinancialContent

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. recently completed construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.

The approximately 400,000-square-foot aircraft manufacturing facility is referred to as ARC and serves as the planned production site for Archer’s all-electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, Midnight.
Archer received the certificate of occupancy for the ARC facility and held a commemorative ribbon cutting ceremony in December.
At the time of writing, Archer was working to complete tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line at the facility.
The facility is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia.
Archer is planning to begin production of its all-electric, vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft at the Covington facility early this year.
Production is planned to begin in early 2025.
Archer aims to ramp production to a rate of two aircraft per month by the end of the year.
Archer built ARC in close partnership with auto manufacturer Stellantis.

The two companies are working to finalize an agreement whereby Stellantis would work with Archer as its exclusive contract manufacturer to mass produce Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft at the facility.
As part of the partnership, Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, and experienced personnel.
Archer stated a goal to scale the facility to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s founder and CEO, stated that the completion of the facility testifies to the industry shifting from R&D into commercialization.
Goldstein noted that with construction on ARC now complete, the team is focused on the start of production planned for early next year and emphasized execution and scaling.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.

Arnaud Deboeuf, Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer, stated that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp offered congratulations to Archer, the Covington community, and all the hardworking Georgians who will find meaningful employment at the campus.
Governor Kemp noted that when they announced Archer’s choice to build this new manufacturing facility in Newton County, it was just the start of an incredible relationship.

Governor Kemp stated that today’s announcement marks the next step in that journey, reaffirming Georgia’s commitment to working with innovative companies like Archer to create quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) announced plans to locate its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia at a site adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer plans to initially build out a 350,000 square-foot facility on a 96-acre site.
The planned eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024.
Initial production is expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expansion is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential sites across the United States to select the location for its first manufacturing facility.

Key considerations for site selection included the availability of talent in the local labor market, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
Archer will receive an incentive package that includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
Archer anticipates that financing for the project will be provided by Synovus, the largest bank headquartered in the state.
Adam Goldstein serves as Archer’s CEO.
Adam Goldstein stated, “After many months of discussion and evaluation, we’re excited to select Georgia as the location for our manufacturing facility.”
Adam Goldstein noted that the U.S. has long led the world in aerospace technology and Georgia has played a vital role in that industry.

Adam Goldstein said Archer’s eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Adam Goldstein expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp along with Bob Kosek, Sara Gershon, Serra Hall, Frank Turner, and their teams for their work on the project.
Governor Brian P. Kemp commented on the decision to locate the facility in Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated, “Georgia has made nurturing innovation a priority as we work with businesses to define the future.”
Governor Brian P. Kemp added, “Our established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.”
Archer can take advantage of Georgia's signature workforce training program Quick Start to help fill over 1,000 jobs in Covington in the long term.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies and maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline.

The local education pipeline includes a dozen high schools that offer training in the field, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.
The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace is a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace has been cultivating an ecosystem of opportunities for innovative companies like Archer for more than 10 years.
source_title: HeliHub.com : Archer Selects Site in Georgia for Manufacturing Facility - HeliHub.com

facts: Archer Aviation Inc., an aerospace company advancing sustainable urban air mobility, will invest $118 million over 10 years in a new state-of-the-art electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL) manufacturing facility.
The new facility will be located alongside the Covington Municipal Airport in Newton County.
Archer’s new facility at the Covington Municipal Airport will consist of three buildings across more than 96 acres.
Construction is expected to begin in December 2022 and follow a phased buildout of eVTOL aircraft production.
The facility will have an eventual capacity able to produce up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Archer Aviation Inc. will create 1,000 jobs and invest $118 million over 10 years in the new facility.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia has made fostering innovation a priority to bring the jobs of the future to the No. 1 state for business.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia’s established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Archer is based in Santa Clara, CA.
Archer is currently developing and manufacturing eVTOL aircraft for use in Urban Air Mobility networks.

Adam Goldstein, Archer’s Founder and CEO, stated that after many months of discussion and evaluation, they are excited to announce the decision to locate its first manufacturing facility in Covington as they progress towards commercialization.
Adam Goldstein noted that creating Urban Air Mobility networks across major cities nationwide requires ramping production of their aircraft.
Glen Burks, Archer’s Vice President of Manufacturing, stated that the new manufacturing facility is a crucial next step in bringing their vision for eVTOL travel to life.
Glen Burks expressed happiness with Covington as a location and partner for this significant next step in the company’s development and growth.
Archer plans to construct the facility adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport in Newton County, which was established in 1963 as part of a long-term airport master plan.
The site is strategically accessible by Georgia’s robust interstate system via I-20.
Large adjacent parcels of land were set aside for future development, and the new Archer facility will use one of these adjacent parcels.
Hiring is expected to begin in late 2023.
The facility is slated to open in the first half of 2024.
Mayor Steve Horton stated that City of Covington Council Members and himself are very excited for Archer Aviation to join their business community.
Mayor Steve Horton noted that the Covington Municipal Airport is one of many points of pride for Covington, and Archer’s presence within the airport plan is another distinction.
Mayor Steve Horton stated that Covington has always been on the cutting edge for quality jobs and new technology.
Lanier Sims, Chairman of the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, stated they could not be more excited for Archer to join their community.
Lanier Sims noted that Archer fits in perfectly with their current industry mix and will be an incredible asset to the beautiful airport in Covington.
Lanier Sims stated that the Development Authority is committed to ensuring their workforce is prepared for the community’s future and ready for the outstanding jobs to come from Archer Aviation.
Lanier Sims expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their leadership and continuation of keeping Georgia the number one state for business.

Katie Kirkpatrick, Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO, stated they welcome Archer Aviation to metro Atlanta where the strength of their community will help ensure the success of their new facility.

Katie Kirkpatrick noted that Metro Atlanta’s expertise in the aviation industry coupled with support from academic, business, civic and government partners makes the region the perfect place to invest in a new facility.
The Global Commerce Industrial Projects team represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) on this competitive project.

GDEcD partnered with the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Center of Innovation’s Aerospace industry team, Georgia EMC, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Tech.

Pat Wilson, GDEcD Commissioner, stated that technology and innovation continue to drive change across strategic industries, and Archer Aviation is on the forefront of a technological evolution in the aviation industry.
Pat Wilson noted that utilizing an innovative, state-of-the art eVTOL aircraft design, the company will look to sustainably tackle mobility issues in urban centers.

Pat Wilson stated that GDEcD’s Center of Innovation’s specialized Aerospace team has been uniquely involved in this project and is proud of their continued efforts to foster Georgia’s aerospace and defense industries.
Home to more than 800 aerospace companies, Georgia maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline.
The pipeline includes a dozen high schools that offer training in the field, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.
The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace is a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace has been cultivating an ecosystem of opportunities for innovative companies like Archer Aviation for more than 10 years.
The center builds and connects Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) networks for the commercialization of Advanced Air Mobility and UAS Systems.
Archer (NYSE: ACHR) is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer’s mission is to unlock the skies, freeing everyone to reimagine how they move and spend time.
Archer’s team is based in Santa Clara, CA.
source_title: Electric Vertical Aircraft Manufacturing Company Archer to Create 1,000 Jobs in Covington - Location Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp, in conjunction with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), announced that Georgia sustained momentum through economic development projects in key industries during the first half of Fiscal Year 2023 (July 1 through December 31, 2022).
The 218 locations or expansions supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce division are expected to create approximately 17,500 new jobs and more than $13 billion in investment for the state.
Both the number of locations and total investment amount increased over the same period last year.
Areas outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region accounted for 85 percent of jobs created and 92 percent of investments from economic development projects during the period.
Four of Georgia’s largest industries – advanced manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, and food processing – accounted for nearly 75 percent of new jobs and 84 percent of investments.
Planned investments resulting from these projects increased by more than 38 percent compared to the same time last year.
The automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment, after advanced manufacturing.

In October, state and local officials joined company representatives, including Korean Ambassador to the U.S. Tae-yong Cho, for the groundbreaking of the new Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) EV facility.
The HMGMA EV facility will support more than 8,100 direct jobs in Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham Counties.
Three off-site HMGMA suppliers announced locations following HMGMA’s groundbreaking, swiftly surpassing the initial $1 billion in investment predicted to come from such additions.
International companies have been valued partners in creating a closed-loop electric mobility supply chain and accounted for 23 percent of state projects since July 1, 2022.
Historic project examples include the partnership between SK On and Hyundai Motor Group in Bartow County and the next-generation developer FREYR Battery’s Coweta County facility.
Both the SK On/Hyundai Motor Group partnership and the FREYR Battery facility are in the top five largest projects by investment in the state’s history.
Battery recyclers Sung-Il Hitech and Denkai America also joined Georgia’s growing electric mobility ecosystem.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson cited proven solutions such as the best-in-class job training program Georgia Quick Start and GRAD certified sites that support accelerated project timelines.

Advanced manufacturing is a cornerstone industry sector in Georgia’s middle and coastal regions, with major investments or expansions announced thus far in FY 2023 including YKK AP in Macon and Beretta Holding S.A.’s new facility in Bryan County through its subsidiary Norma Precision Inc.
The advanced manufacturing sector has announced more than 7,700 new jobs in the state since July 1, 2022.

Germany-based Haering Precision celebrated the grand opening of its Lavonia facility in November with a two-day event including a “Future Day” for approximately 400 local high school students and members of the community.
source_title: Key Industries Sustain Momentum for State's Economic Development - Columbus CEO

facts: Archer Aviation plans to invest $118 million to construct a plant near Atlanta.
The company will eventually hire up to 1,000 people.
Archer Aviation is based in Santa Clara, California.
The plant will be built adjoining an airport in Covington, Georgia.
The aircraft is a battery-powered vertical takeoff and landing craft with six propellers that hold four passengers and a pilot.
The propellers would pivot to allow the aircraft to take off and land like a helicopter and fly like a plane.
United Airlines plans to fly the craft from downtown Manhattan to its hub at Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey, cutting a congested car trip of one hour to 10 minutes.
United Airlines put down a $10 million deposit to purchase 100 aircraft from Archer for $1 billion.
After building a prototype weighing 3,330 pounds (1,510 kilograms), the company plans to unveil a production model dubbed "Midnight" on Wednesday.
The company aims for the "Midnight" aircraft to enter service in 2025.
The U.S. Air Force is evaluating the company’s aircraft for possible use.
Archer is seeking Federal Aviation Administration certification and says it could receive it in 2024.
The company aims to produce 650 aircraft per year at the Georgia site, possibly scaling up to 2,300 per year.
Automotive firm Stellantis, owner of Dodge, Chrysler, Jeep and Ram, is providing manufacturing and engineering help.
Archer expects to receive a roughly $40 million incentive package from Georgia and local governments.
The incentive package includes the donation of 96 acres (39 hectares) of land at Covington Municipal Airport.
The package also includes a cash grant from the state and other tax incentives.
A property tax break and a job tax credit could be worth $15 million to Archer over five years, contingent on employees making at least $31,300.
State and local officials did not immediately respond to questions about the decision to incentivize a startup in an unproven industry.
The company chose the Georgia location for locally available workers, the ability to conduct test flights, affordable construction costs, and good highway, rail, and air connections.

Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated: "Our eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute and this factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing hobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity."
Archer expects to borrow money from Georgia-based Synovus Financial Corp. for the plant’s construction.
Plant construction is expected to begin and conclude in 2024.
The company went public last year, raising about $850 million.
Archer lost $91 million in the quarter ended Sept. 30, reporting $600 million in cash on hand.
Georgia economic development officials have been focusing on recruiting electric car companies, landing major plants from Rivian Automotive and Hyundai Motor Group last year.
Norwegian batter startup Freyr announced a $2.6 billion plant in Georgia on Friday, joining an already-constructed electric battery plant owned by South Korean firm SK Innovation.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson said economic recruiters focused on aerospace had worked with Archer.
Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that "technology and innovation continue to drive change across strategic industries."
source_title: Electric air taxi company Archer plans $118M Georgia factory | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced that earlier this week it received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC.
ARC is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia.
Archer is now working to complete the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line at this facility.
Production is planned to begin in early 2025.
The goal is to ramp to a rate of two aircraft per month by the end of the year.
Archer built ARC in close partnership with Stellantis.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise and experienced personnel with the goal of scaling this facility to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp commented that Archer’s choice to build the new manufacturing facility in Newton County marks the start of an incredible relationship and reaffirms Georgia’s commitment to creating quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.

Congressman Mike Collins of Georgia, who sits on the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, stated that the completion of the facility highlights Georgia’s leadership in innovation, advanced manufacturing and job creation, and will drive economic benefits for the state.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s founder and CEO, noted that construction on ARC is complete, production is planned for early next year, and the team got it done fast and on budget.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf commented that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.
ARC is where Archer plans to produce its all-electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft, Midnight.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.
source_title: Archer Aviation Completes Georgia Air Taxi Factory - Augusta CEO

facts: Archer Aviation announced plans to locate its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
The facility site is located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
The location is 40 minutes east of Atlanta.
Archer plans to initially build a 350,000 square-foot facility on a 96-acre site.
The initial facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024.
Initial production is expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility can be expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expansion is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential manufacturing sites across the United States before selecting Georgia.
Key site selection considerations included local labor market talent availability, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
Archer will receive an incentive package that includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
Archer anticipates that financing for the project will be provided by Synovus.
Synovus is the largest bank headquartered in Georgia.
Adam Goldstein, Archer's CEO, stated that after many months of discussion and evaluation, they selected Georgia for their manufacturing facility.
Adam Goldstein noted that the U.S. has long led the world in aerospace technology and Georgia has played a vital role in that industry.
Adam Goldstein stated that their eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs.
Adam Goldstein expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp, Bob Kosek, Sara Gershon, Serra Hall, Frank Turner, their teams, and his colleagues at Archer for pioneering innovative aviation technologies.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia has made nurturing innovation a priority while working with businesses to define the future.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Governor Kemp highlighted that Archer can take advantage of Georgia's signature workforce training program, Quick Start, to help fill over 1,000 long-term jobs in Covington.
source_title: Archer Aviation Selects Georgia Site for Manufacturing Facility | Aviation Maintenance Magazine
