facts: The 2017 Arising Industries was an all-electric vehicle.
All trims featured a dual-motor electric all-wheel-drive system providing excellent traction and a balanced driving experience.
Core and Elevate trims delivered a combined output of approximately 250 horsepower.
Apex trim boosted horsepower to around 320 horsepower for more exhilarating acceleration.
All models were equipped with a 70 kWh battery pack.
Estimated EPA range was about 270 miles on a full charge, varying slightly based on driving conditions and climate control usage.
Core and Elevate models achieved 0-60 mph in roughly 6.8 seconds.
Apex trim achieved 0-60 mph in 5.9 seconds.
Towing capacity was rated up to 1,800 pounds when properly equipped.

Core trim included a 10-inch touchscreen infotainment system, LED lighting, 18-inch alloy wheels, panoramic glass roof, automatic emergency braking, lane-keeping assist, and sustainable cloth upholstery.

Elevate trim added 20-inch alloy wheels, premium synthetic leather upholstery, heated front seats, a 12.3-inch infotainment display with enhanced navigation, adaptive cruise control, and a 360-degree camera system.

Apex trim offered an exclusive performance tune, 21-inch aero wheels, genuine leather seating with ventilation, a premium sound system, head-up display, advanced park assist, and unique exterior styling elements.
The vehicle was manufactured in the United States, specifically in Hazlehurst.
Early models occasionally experienced software glitches with the infotainment system and connectivity issues.
Some owners reported less-than-ideal rear visibility due to a sloped roofline.
Long-term parts availability and service network coverage were initial concerns for potential buyers as it was a new brand.
Minor build quality inconsistencies included slight panel gaps and occasional interior trim rattles.
Inconsistent range estimates were reported in extreme weather conditions, partially addressed through subsequent over-the-air software updates.

One notable recall was issued in late 2017 (code: AI-2017-001) addressing a battery management system software anomaly that could theoretically lead to an inaccurate display of remaining charge under specific charging conditions.
The recall was rectified via a mandatory software update performed at dealerships or remotely.
Long-term reliability concerns focused on the longevity of its proprietary battery technology and availability of specialized service centers.
Standard safety technologies included automatic emergency braking with pedestrian detection, lane-keeping assist, and a rearview camera.

Higher trims added adaptive cruise control, blind-spot monitoring with rear cross-traffic alert, 360-degree surround-view camera system, advanced park assist, and semi-autonomous highway driving assist on the Apex trim.
Passive safety features included a high-strength steel and aluminum frame, eight airbags, and advanced seatbelt pretensioners.
The manufacturer highlighted rigorous in-house testing aiming for top safety scores.
On the used market, 2017 models typically range from $18,000 to $28,000 depending on mileage, battery health, trim level, and overall condition.
Depreciation was steeper than some established luxury EVs due to it being a first-generation product from a nascent brand.
Insurance premiums were initially slightly higher but settled over time.
Electricity fueling costs were significantly cheaper than gasoline, offering substantial savings especially with home charging.
Routine maintenance was notably lower than ICE vehicles, with no oil changes and reduced brake wear due to regenerative braking.
Out-of-warranty repairs for specialized EV components like the battery pack or advanced electronics could be costly.
Performance was competitive with the base Model 3 and superior to the Bolt EV.
Its 270-mile range outclassed the Bolt's 238 miles but fell short of some higher-tier Tesla configurations.
It offered a more comprehensive suite of standard driver-assistance technologies compared to rivals.
Software glitches were more frequent than in a Leaf or Bolt, and its service network was less extensive than Tesla's at the time.
Starting price positioned it as a premium offering, above the Bolt EV but generally below higher-end Model 3 trims.
source_title: 2017 ARISING INDUSTRIES Arising Industries | Read Owner Reviews, Prices, Specs

facts: One of the world’s largest copper recyclers will bring about 125 jobs to the Augusta area after a local production plant begins construction next summer.
Hamburg, Germany-based Aurubis AG plans to build a $340 million recycling and secondary-smelting facility on 150 acres in Augusta Corporate Park off Mike Padgett Highway.
Secondary smelting is the process by which copper scrap is reformed into material used in producing finished products.
source_title: Aurubis AG Copper recycling plant to bring 125 jobs to Augusta area

facts: Aurubis AG adopted a comprehensive investment package of around €530 million approved by its Supervisory Board on December 20, 2022.
The investment accelerates the expansion of the modular recycling system and doubles processing capacity at the Aurubis Richmond site in Georgia, USA.
Additional investments target the Complex Recycling Hamburg (CRH) project for processing complex recycling materials and intermediate products.
A third component involves doubling the output of the largest national and in-house photovoltaic plant at the Pirdop site in Bulgaria.
The three strategic projects are projected to deliver an annual earnings contribution of around €130 million (EBITDA).
The Supervisory Board also approved a comprehensive modernization project of the company’s IT landscape, including migration to the S/4HANA SAP platform.

CEO Roland Harings stated that Aurubis is accelerating growth in recycling with the next expansion stage at its US plant and strengthening its core business by investing in innovative metallurgy in Hamburg.
A second recycling module with an investment volume of around €250 million will be added to the Aurubis Richmond plant currently under construction in Georgia.
An additional €90 million was approved for a capital expenditure adjustment covering infrastructure requirements and inflation for the Georgia project.
Production at the expanded Aurubis Richmond facility is scheduled to begin in 2026.
Recycling in the USA is growing by 5% every year, resulting in rapid increases in the availability of complex recycling materials.
The expansion will double the throughput volume from a previously planned 90,000 tons to 180,000 tons of complex recycling materials per year.
The additional plant will be built in accordance with the highest environmental standards to allow for environmentally friendly recycling of input materials.
The expansion will create around 80 additional jobs in the region.
Once both recycling modules are completed, Aurubis anticipates employing around 200 people at the Richmond site.
Starting in 2025/26, Aurubis anticipates additional EBITDA totaling around €90 million per year from the approved expansion phase.
The Richmond plant will benefit from attractive local electricity and natural gas prices that will positively impact cost efficiency.
Construction is already underway on the first plant at the Richmond site.
Total anticipated investments for the Richmond site, including the planned expansion, amount to €640 million.
The Richmond plant is expected to provide an additional contribution to EBITDA of around €170 million starting in fiscal year 2026/27.
Aurubis plans to finance the approved projects primarily out of current cash flow without needing a capital increase.
The company projects an operating EBT between €400 and €500 million for the current fiscal year 2022/23.
The Supervisory Board and Executive Board will recommend a dividend of €1.80 per share, the highest in the company’s history.
Aurubis secured an investment of around €190 million for the CRH project at the Hamburg site.
The CRH project will add capacity to process around 30,000 more tons of recycling material and internal complex smelter intermediary products.
COO Heiko Arnold noted that the CRH process retains much more value creation in-house and highlights the company’s research and development expertise.
The CRH project creates a foundation for additional strategic projects at the plant and represents a clear commitment to the Hamburg site.
Once the CRH project is ramped up in 2025, the company forecasts an annual earnings contribution of around €40 million.
An initial investment volume of around €12 million will be used to increase the Pirdop solar park’s output from 14 MW to 24 MW.
Solar panels for future enlargement of the Pirdop solar park will also be purchased for €8 million.
The current expansion employs stationary, double-sided modules that produce more than conventional PV modules.
The expanded Pirdop plant is scheduled to be commissioned as early as 2024.
Once complete, the expanded solar park will generate electricity equivalent to powering 9,000 households or a small city every year.
Compared to coal-fired power generation, the expanded solar park will save 34,000 tons of CO2 emissions per year.
Over the planned operating period of 15 years, the project will save over 500,000 tons of CO2 emissions.
The in-house solar park supports Aurubis’ sustainability goal of a 50% reduction of Scope 1 + 2 emissions by 2030.
For the outstanding 2021/22 result, the company reported an operating EBT of €532 million.
A comprehensive commentary on the fiscal-year result will accompany the publication of the 2021/22 Annual Report on December 21, 2022.
source_title: Press release: Aurubis adopts € 530 million investment package to accelerate growth ... | Presseportal

facts: In the US state of Georgia, Aurubis is building the country’s first plant for complex recycling materials with a processing capacity of 180,000 tons of input material per year.
Aurubis is investing in new recycling technology at its two Belgian plants to keep strategically crucial, high demand raw materials in the cycle.
At the Hamburg site, a pilot plant for recycling the lithium-ion batteries from electric cars went into operation last year.
Findings have shown that more 95 percent of the materials contained in the used batteries were recovered.
Aurubis has invested more than € 780 million in protecting the environment and the climate since 2000, with € 47 million in 2021 alone.
The company is aiming for carbon-neutral production well before 2050.
Today, the company already processes more than one million tons of recycling material, and the recycled content in each copper cathode is almost 45 percent.

For a number of years now, all business partners have undergone a comprehensive Business Partner Screening based on the principles of the Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD) to fulfil due diligence for a responsible and sustainable supply chain.
In the past fiscal year, the Aurubis plants in Hamburg, Lünen and in Bulgarian Pirdop were certified under 'The Copper Mark'.

The external audit was conducted to the exacting standards of ‘The Copper Mark’, an industry initiative to verify the sustainability of copper production sites, including mines, smelters and refineries.
Christian Hein, Head of Sustainability at Aurubis, stated that The Copper Mark stands for a voluntary commitment to responsibility along the entire value chain and covers 32 sustainability criteria.
The certification process is currently underway at the Olen plant.

source_title: Enabling tomorrow: Aurubis releases new Sustainability Report

facts: Aurubis held its Annual General Meeting in Hamburg on February 16, 2023.
The company is focusing on securing and strengthening its core business in accordance with its strategy "Metals for progress: Driving sustainable growth".
Over the next three to four years, Aurubis will invest about €1.1 billion to implement its strategic agenda.
Decided growth projects are expected to contribute around €260 million to the Group's operating result (EBITDA) starting in fiscal year 2026/27.
The new Aurubis Richmond US site is located in the state of Georgia and serves as a central building block for future growth.
Total investment for the Aurubis Richmond US site is €640 million.
The Georgia facility intends to capitalize on a recycling market growth of more than 5% and increased availability of complex recycling materials.
Roland Harings, Chairman of the Executive Board, emphasized the company's extremely solid financial position and commitment to investing in sustainable growth.
Aurubis is expanding tankhouse capacity at its Bulgarian site in Pirdop by 50% with an investment of about €120 million.

Approximately €190 million will be invested in the "CRH - Complex Recycling Hamburg" project to process about 30,000 tons of additional recycling material and complex smelter intermediates starting in 2025.
The company aims to achieve climate-neutral production well before 2050 through continuous process optimization and decarbonization initiatives.
Aurubis is expanding its solar park at the Pirdop site by an additional 17 MWp with an investment of about €20 million.

Roland Harings stated that the growth strategy focuses on consolidating Aurubis's pioneering role in sustainability and its position as the most efficient and sustainable metallurgical network globally.
Prof. Dr. Fritz Vahrenholt, Kathrin Dahnke, Gunnar Groebler, Prof. Dr. Markus Kramer, Dr. Stephan Krümmer, and Dr. Sandra Reich were elected to Aurubis AG's Supervisory Board during the meeting.
Prof. Dr. Fritz Vahrenholt was appointed Chairman of the Supervisory Board.
source_title: Growth course confirmed at Aurubis Annual General Meeting --  Trade Fair

facts: A German company intends to invest $340 million and create 125 jobs by building a recycling and smelting facility in Augusta.
Aurubis is a recycler of copper and other metals.
Aurubis plans to produce materials at the plant that will be used in electronic products including lithium-ion batteries.
The project represents the first multi-metal recycling operation of its kind in the United States.
It is the largest single investment by a German company in Georgia, according to Gov. Brian Kemp’s office.
Constrained supplies of crucial metals needed for batteries in electric cars and other products have raised worries in industry and government.
Aurubis plans to build at the Port of Augusta Industrial Park.
The facility will be located on a former military base.
The plant will be the second largest metals recycler operation in the world.
State officials stated that Aurubis will be able to attract more than 60% of its workforce from the local community.
source_title: Germany's Aurubis to bring jobs to Augusta, Georgia with planned large metals recycling plant.

facts: The press release announcing the start of production was dated September 24, 2025.
Aurubis Richmond is located in Augusta, Georgia.
Aurubis Richmond has started production as the first US multimetal recycling plant.
The facility recovers strategic metals including copper, nickel, tin, and precious metals from complex recycling materials.
The recovered metals are intended for American energy infrastructure, data centers, AI applications, and the defense industry.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
American industry currently requires approximately 1.8 million tons of copper per year.
Experts estimate that demand for strategic metals like copper will increase nearly 30% over the next five years.
The US currently imports about half of the copper it processes.
Multimetal recycling is positioned to quickly close the domestic supply gap by processing complex recycling materials.

The “First Melt” celebration attendees included US Representative Rick W. Allen for Georgia’s 12th District, German Ambassador to the United States Jens Hanefeld, and high-profile guests from politics and business.
Aurubis CEO Dr. Toralf Haag stated that Aurubis Richmond makes the company the frontrunner in multimetal recycling in the US, matching its position in Europe.
Dr. Toralf Haag indicated plans to continue expanding expertise in producing key strategic raw materials in the US due to high availability of recycling material and favorable local conditions.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp stated in a video message that Aurubis Richmond is now a significant employer for Georgia and plays a major role in the American multimetal supply chain.
Governor Kemp described the facility as a testament to American manufacturing, independence, and resiliency.
The project involves an investment of about $800 million.
Aurubis is creating more than 240 jobs in Georgia.
The plant will process up to 180,000 tons of complex recycling material annually.
An expansion stage for the facility is scheduled to begin in 2026.
Input materials for the plant include printed circuit boards, copper cable, and other metal-bearing products.
Aurubis Richmond recovers critical metals on-site, effectively closing the value chain locally.
The facility will be supplied with recycling materials from local sources to keep valuable raw materials in the domestic market.
US Representative Rick W. Allen emphasized that the facility reinforces America’s ability to supply itself with metals for national security, infrastructure, and technology leadership.
Representative Allen noted that Aurubis exemplifies how international partnerships can solidify America’s industrial foundation and strengthen economic independence.
David Schultheis, President and Managing Director of Aurubis Richmond, stated the facility is now an important player in the North American multimetal supply chain.
Schultheis thanked Georgia policymakers and governmental authorities for strong support, smooth approval processes, and enabling quick settlement.
The new plant utilizes cutting-edge technology that meets the latest environmental standards enacted by the state of Georgia and federal authorities.
Facility operations are designed with high consideration for water, air, and soil protection.
Aurubis Richmond is designed as a flexible “one-stop shop” to receive a broad range of metal-bearing recycling materials from US suppliers.

facts: Aurubis is building a multimetal recycling plant in the US state of Georgia.
The Georgia facility will be the country’s first plant for complex recycling materials.
The processing capacity of the new Georgia plant will be 180,000 tons of input material per year.
Aurubis is investing in new recycling technology at its two Belgian plants to keep strategically crucial, high demand raw materials in the cycle.
A pilot plant for recycling lithium-ion batteries from electric cars went into operation at Aurubis's Hamburg site last year.
Findings from the Hamburg pilot project showed that more than 95 percent of the materials contained in used batteries were recovered.
Aurubis currently processes more than one million tons of recycling material annually.
The recycled content in each copper cathode produced by Aurubis reaches almost 45 percent.
The company is aiming for carbon-neutral production well before 2050.
Aurubis has invested more than €780 million in environmental and climate protection since 2000, including €47 million in 2021 alone.
The CO2 footprint of Aurubis's copper cathodes has fallen by 36 percent since 2013.
All business partners undergo comprehensive Business Partner Screening based on OECD principles for responsible and sustainable supply chain due diligence.
Aurubis plants in Hamburg, Lünen, and Pirdop were certified under 'The Copper Mark' initiative to verify the sustainability of copper production sites.
The company aligned its 2023 Sustainability Report with the Global Reporting Initiative Standard (GRI) and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosure (TCFD).
At its site in Pirdop, Bulgaria, Aurubis constructed an in-house solar park that powers the plant with 13,500 megawatt hours of electricity and avoids 22,400 tons of CO2 annually.
The expansion target for the Pirdop solar park is a total of 24,000 MWh from solar power by 2024.
Since 2023, Aurubis's plant in Olen, Belgium has drawn 90 percent of its energy needs from a Belgian offshore wind park.
The Belgian offshore wind integration avoids 42,000 tons of CO2 annually.
Preparations are underway for the second stage of the Industrial Heat project in Hamburg, which has been in operation since 2018.
In cooperation with public utility Hamburger Energiewerke, up to 20,000 additional households will receive heat from the plant starting in the 2024/25 heating period.
The expanded Industrial Heat project is the largest of its kind in Germany and will prevent an additional 100,000 tons of CO2 per year.
A pilot project at Aurubis's Hamburg headquarters used blue ammonia as an energy source to advance the decarbonization of copper production.

facts: German multi-metal provider and recycling company Aurubis was drawn to Georgia for its pro-business environment, spirit of innovation, and focus on building out a world-class electric mobility value chain.
Aurubis announced in November 2021 that it would build a recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta, Georgia.
Leaders from the Georgia Department of Economic Development were at company headquarters in Hamburg, Germany, when the investment was announced.
Aurubis announced in December 2022 that it would increase its investment total to €640 million.
Aurubis projects it will double its throughput volume from the previously announced 90,000 tons of complex recycling materials to 180,000 tons per year.
The facility is expected to create 200 jobs.
The announcement marked the single-largest investment of a German company in Georgia.
It is the first facility of its kind in the U.S.
This is also the first greenfield project in the U.S. for Aurubis.
Aurubis plans to produce materials that can be used in lithium-ion batteries and other electronics valuable in the EV industry.

Not long after the December 2022 announcement, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp and state leaders were again on the ground in Germany to celebrate the company’s commitment to Georgia, the e-mobility and renewable energy ecosystems, and the jobs it would bring.
Governor Brian P. Kemp said the project was attracted by Georgia's ports system, highly skilled workforce, and pro-business environment that prioritizes partnership and innovation.
Aurubis will now join more than 500 German companies with operations in Georgia.
Roland Harings, CEO of Aurubis, said today’s high recycling demand cannot be met by existing facilities.
Roland Harings stated the world needs metals such as copper, which can be recycled without loss of quality or performance, for cars, mobile phones, batteries, cables, and computers.
Roland Harings added that Aurubis's increased commitment to the U.S. will be a game-changer for the environment, manufacturers, and the transition to a renewable economy.
Aurubis broke ground on the recycling plant in June 2022.
The first phase is expected to come online in mid-2024.
The expansion is expected to be completed in 2026.
The company was able to quickly start work on the site due to its status as a “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified” site.
Cal Wray, president of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, expressed excitement over the speed at which Aurubis is building the facility and executing further expansions.
Cal Wray noted that Aurubis will provide great jobs and be a wonderful addition to the Augusta community.
Germany is one of Georgia’s top three investment partners.
Germany was Georgia’s second-largest job-creating country for Foreign Direct Investment in FY22.
At least 513 German facilities operate in Georgia, employing more than 25,000 Georgians.
Georgia is celebrating its 50th year of continuous representation in Germany in 2023.

facts: Europe's biggest copper smelter Aurubis will invest around $700 million to build a smelter for complex recycling materials in North America.
The commissioning of the secondary smelter in Augusta, Georgia, is planned for the second half of 2024.
On Wednesday, U.S. first lady Jill Biden toured the facility.
An expansion of the plant is already under way.
The expansion will allow the site to process 180,000 tons of complex recycling materials, such as printed circuit boards and insulated copper cable, from 2026.
Aurubis is creating long-term, secure jobs for more than 200 people here in an industry that is strategically important to U.S.

facts: Total planned investments in Georgia’s e-mobility and battery ecosystem topped $27.3 billion in the five years between 2018-2023.
The first Georgia-made electric car, the EV9, prepares to roll off the line at Kia Georgia in West Point this year (2024).
Georgia is historically a top destination for job seekers looking to reduce living expenses.
Georgia works with employers to identify opportunities to attract and train the local labor pool.
Georgia Quick Start is helping the state meet the demands of the clean energy boom.
Georgia Quick Start recently unveiled plans to support the training of more than 8,500 Georgians for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
CNBC ranked Georgia as the No. 1 state for infrastructure in the U.S. in 2023.
Georgia has two deepwater ports, one of which is ranked among the fastest growing and the other a top port for automotive Ro/Ro in the country.
Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport is located near the center of the state and is the busiest airport in the world.
Cities across Georgia are connected by a seamless network of interstates, highways, and rail lines.
Reliable energy grids are diversifying Georgia’s energy mix through renewable options, including solar energy and Plant Vogtle.
The state is supporting the availability of low-cost energy, including solar and hydrogen.
Georgia is bringing together energy technology thought-leaders to foster innovative ecosystems.
Georgia’s cutting-edge mobility ecosystem consists of original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) Kia, HMGMA, Rivian, and Archer Aviation.
Battery manufacturers in the ecosystem include SK Battery America and LG Energy Solution.
Metal and battery-material recyclers in the ecosystem include Aurubis AG and Ascend Elements.
Hydrogen fuel cell manufacturer Plug Power is part of Georgia’s mobility ecosystem.
Synthetic graphite manufacturer Anovion Technologies is part of Georgia’s mobility ecosystem.
At CES 2024 in Las Vegas, Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) unveiled plans for the Clean Logistics Project.
The Clean Logistics Project involves HMGMA deploying XCIENT Fuel Cell tractors to manage logistics at the new Metaplant in coastal Georgia.
GDEcD can be connected with at trade shows including NAATBatt, InterBattery, and SelectUSA Investment Summit.

facts: Aurubis AG achieved operating earnings before taxes (EBT) of €349 million for the 2022/23 fiscal year as at the balance sheet date of September 30.
Operating return on capital employed (ROCE) amounted to 11.3% in the reporting period, compared to 19% in the previous year.
Deviations from the record result in fiscal year 2021/22 were mainly attributable to the financial impact of criminal activities directed against Aurubis.
IFRS consolidated earnings before taxes (EBT) were €165 million for the period, compared to €935 million in the previous year.

CEO Roland Harings stated the result is the third-best in company history and highlighted potential due to global developments like electrification, the circular economy, and sustainability transitions.

The Supervisory and Executive Boards will propose a dividend per share of €1.40 at the Annual General Meeting on February 15, 2024, corresponding to a payout ratio of 23% of operating consolidated net income.
Treatment charges for concentrates and recycling materials were key earnings drivers for the past fiscal year.
Higher wire rod revenues with increased product surcharges and a significantly higher copper premium positively influenced earnings.
A considerably lower metal result at decreased prices for industrial metals copper, tin, and zinc had an opposite effect on earnings.
Lower sales prices for sulfuric acid cut revenues from the Aurubis by-product, and demand for flat rolled products also dropped.
Inflation-related higher Group costs and launching expenses for strategic projects currently in implementation lowered earnings.

The financial impact from criminal activities resulted in a €169 million metals shortfall as of September 30, 2023, which was €16 million less than the assessed inventory difference of €185 million disclosed on August 31, 2023.
Insurance reimbursements provided a compensating effect of €30 million against the criminal activity impacts.

CEO Roland Harings emphasized that security levels have been significantly increased and comprehensive investments are being made in new facilities with tighter, multistage security areas at the Hamburg site.

Aurubis established immediate measures and long-term improvements to increase security, including more frequent spot checks of personnel and vehicles, intensified surveillance of risk areas, and additional approval levels in raw material purchasing.
A new precious metals processing plant (PMR) will come online at the end of 2026 with a €300 million investment, mapping out the entire precious metal processing chain in a closed security area.
The newly developed PMR process will raise efficiency, considerably reduce throughput times for materials containing precious metals, and lower operating costs by around 15%.
Around €1.7 billion is currently approved for strategic projects to further develop the smelter network.

Strategic projects include the Precious Metals Refinery, the large-scale growth project Aurubis Richmond in Georgia, the expansion of recycling capacity with the Complex Recycling Hamburg project, and the extension of the Industrial Heat project in Hamburg.
Over the next three to five years, the strategic projects will contribute a total of around €260 million to EBITDA per year.

CEO Roland Harings emphasized commitment to investing in the US and Europe for growth, strength, security, safety, and sustainability, contributing to meeting growing demand for metals essential to the energy and mobility transition.
Aurubis anticipates an operating EBT of between €380 and €480 million for the current 2023/24 fiscal year.
The company expects an operating ROCE of between 10% and 14% for the 2023/24 fiscal year.

Aurubis AG produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually, along with wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys, precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, zinc, sulfuric acid, and iron silicate.
Aurubis has around 7,200 employees, production sites in Europe and the US, and an extensive distribution network around the world.
source_title: EQS-News: Aurubis achieves good full-year result of € 349 million despite extraordinary effects caused by criminal activities

facts: Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
The company processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic and inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into metals of the highest quality.
Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually.
From its copper cathodes, Aurubis manufactures wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys.
The company also produces precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc, along with by-products such as sulfuric acid and iron silicate.
CEO Roland Harings stated that high market demand for the company's products demonstrates its potential amid global developments like electrification and the transition to sustainability.
Roland Harings emphasized that Aurubis is making an important contribution to meeting the growing demand for metals essential to the energy and mobility transition.
Aurubis has a large-scale growth project named Aurubis Richmond located in Georgia.
Around €1.7 billion is currently approved for strategic projects, including Aurubis Richmond in Georgia, the Complex Recycling Hamburg project, and the Industrial Heat project in Hamburg.
Over the coming three to five years, these strategic projects will contribute a total of around €260 million to EBITDA per year.
For the 2023/24 fiscal year, Aurubis anticipates an operating earnings before taxes (EBT) between €380 million and €480 million.
The company expects an operating return on capital employed (ROCE) of between 10% and 14% for the current fiscal year.
Aurubis achieved operating EBT of €349 million for the 2022/23 fiscal year ending September 30, 2023.
IFRS consolidated earnings before taxes for the same period were €165 million.
The Supervisory and Executive Boards will propose a dividend per share of €1.40 at the Annual General Meeting on February 15, 2024.
Aurubis has approximately 7,200 employees and operates production sites in Europe and the US.
CEO Roland Harings stated that the company is committed to investing in the US and Europe to drive growth, security, safety, and sustainability.
Criminal activities directed against Aurubis resulted in a €169 million metals shortfall as of September 30, 2023.
Insurance reimbursements for the criminal activity impact totaled €30 million.
A new Precious Metals Refinery (PMR) plant at the Hamburg site will require a €300 million investment and is scheduled to come online by the end of 2026.
The PMR project will lower operating costs by approximately 15% and significantly reduce throughput times for materials containing precious metals.
In response to security threats, Aurubis implemented additional approval levels for raw material purchasing and increased spot checks of personnel and vehicles.
A virtual press conference detailing the results was scheduled for December 20, 2023, at 10 a.m. CET.
An English-language webcast for analysts, investors, and journalists was offered at 2 p.m. CET on December 20, 2023.
source_title: Aurubis achieves good full-year result of € 349 million despite extraordinary effects caused by criminal activities | Valuespectrum.com

Aurubis AG reported an operating ROCE of 11.5% for the 2023/24 fiscal year.
The company successfully completed the ribbon-cutting ceremony for Aurubis Richmond, its first secondary smelter for multimetal recycling in the US.
Aurubis advanced its decarbonization roadmap by installing hydrogen-ready anode furnaces at the Hamburg site.
Aurubis is expanding its Industrial Heat project to supply CO2-free heat to up to 28,000 households.
The Group expanded its solar park in Bulgaria.
The Group completed Copper Mark certification for all major smelter sites.
Aurubis AG has a net zero target for 2050.
Aurubis AG employs 5,000 people.

facts: Aurubis Richmond is a nearly $800 million recycling facility located in Augusta, Georgia.
The Augusta plant began smelting last week according to a news release.
The facility aims to play a critical role in recycling metals for fast-growing electronic and battery uses, including EVs, data centers, and energy infrastructure.
The Augusta plant was first announced four years ago.
It is the first facility of its kind in the U.S., smelting both common and rare metals for high-tech sectors.
Toralf Haag, Aurubis CEO, stated that 'With Aurubis Richmond, we are the front-runner in multimetal recycling in the U.S.'
Aurubis is a German company.
The new Augusta facility is turning up the heat on Georgia’s fast-growing energy sector and electric vehicle supply chain.

facts: On Thursday, Governor Brian Kemp signed an executive order extending the temporary suspension of the state gas tax and the supply chain state of emergency.
Solar energy giant QCells will create 470 new jobs through an expansion of its facilities in Whitfield County.

The governor announced the purchase of a more than 1,100-acre economic development site in partnership between the state of Georgia and the Development Authority of Peach County, designated as the Middle Georgia Megasite to position the community for significant investment and job creation.
On June 17, Governor Brian Kemp joined officials from Aurubis and state, local, and federal leaders to break ground on a recycling and copper smelting facility in Augusta.
The Aurubis facility is the first-of-its-kind in the U.S. and represents the largest investment by a German company in Georgia.
The Aurubis recycling and copper smelting facility will create 125 jobs for the Augusta community.

facts: AWS announced plans to invest an estimated $11 billion to expand its infrastructure in Georgia.
The investment is intended to support cloud computing and AI technologies.
The investment is expected to create at least 550 new high-skilled jobs.
Generative AI is driving increased demand for advanced cloud infrastructure and compute power.
AWS’s investments will support the future of AI from data centers in Georgia.
AWS data centers are flexible enough to efficiently run GPUs for traditional workloads or AI and machine learning models.
The deployment of cutting-edge AI infrastructure will strengthen Georgia’s position as an innovation hub.
Amazon has been a driving force behind Georgia's economic growth for more than a decade.
Since 2010, Amazon has contributed $20.1 billion to the state's gross domestic product.
Amazon supports 34,000 full- and part-time jobs in Georgia.
Amazon supports 44,700 indirect jobs in Georgia.
AWS’s planned investment is expected to create hundreds of new, direct AWS jobs in technical roles.
Direct job roles include data center engineers, network specialists, engineering operations managers, security specialists, and many more.
The investment is expected to support thousands of local construction jobs.
The investment is expected to support positions in the data center supply chain.
Through its Disaster Relief Hub in Atlanta, Amazon's teams have worked with local organizations to deliver essential supplies after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
Since the storms made landfall, Amazon has delivered more than 40 truckloads of over 2.3 million essential items supporting 32 relief organizations.
The majority of items were delivered within 72 hours from the request.
Amazon’s on-the-ground Response team used AWS technology to set up energy systems during disaster response.
Amazon’s on-the-ground Response team established connectivity during disaster response.
Amazon’s on-the-ground Response team conducted drone mapping of more than 3,000 acres in remote and heavily impacted areas.
The drone mapping aided response operations and search-and-rescue missions.
Amazon has made significant investments in renewable energy.
Amazon has eight solar farms, including the Bulldog Solar farm.
The Bulldog Solar farm was built in part by local solar technicians.
Once fully operational, these projects will generate enough carbon-free energy to power an estimated 123,000 U.S. homes each year.
Amazon MGM Studios regularly films series in Georgia.
Series filmed in Georgia include The Bondsman, The Pickup, and I Want You Back.

"AWS's ongoing infrastructure investments across the United States demonstrate our relentless commitment to powering our customers' digital innovation through cloud and AI technologies," said Roger Wehner, vice president of economic development at AWS.

"We are delighted to expand our infrastructure into Georgia with this planned multibillion-dollar investment, which we expect to create more than 550 well-paying jobs and drive significant economic growth for the state."
Roger Wehner noted that state and local leaders have cultivated an environment enabling companies like AWS to make bold investments.
"This is a historic day for Douglas County," said Dr. Romona Jackson Jones, Douglas County Commission Chairwoman.
Dr. Romona Jackson Jones expressed pleasure at welcoming AWS to bring secure, resilient, and advanced cloud computing technology to the county.
Dr. Romona Jackson Jones noted the investment will bring hundreds of high-paying, high-tech jobs benefiting the community long-term.
Dr. Romona Jackson Jones described the initial investment as a game-changer that will result in the community being on the forefront of innovation.
Dr. Romona Jackson Jones stated it will further establish Georgia as a leader in technology, innovation, and artificial intelligence.

"We are pleased and proud that Amazon Web Services, a world technology leader, has chosen to locate new data centers in Butts County," said Russ Crumbley, chairman of the Butts County Board of Commissioners.
Russ Crumbley stated the investment will be the most significant in Butts County history.

Russ Crumbley noted it represents a collaborative effort for more than a year by multiple entities including the Butts County Board of Commissioners, Development Authority of Butts County, Butts County Water and Sewer Authority, and Butts County School System.
Each entity worked with AWS to ensure infrastructure improvements throughout the community will benefit citizens for decades.
Among local training programs AWS offers is a Fiber Optic Fusion Splicing Workshop.
The workshop trains individuals in new fusion splicing techniques and equipment.
In the fall of 2022, dozens of participants completed this program at Augusta Technical College.
Learners earn a fusion splicing certificate.
Participants are immediately connected to employers seeking talent in fiber broadband.
AWS will bring signature STEAM education programs to Georgia.
Programs include Girls’ Tech Day, which inspires girls to pursue technology careers.
Programs include Think Big Spaces for students to get hands-on STEAM education.
AWS chooses locations that provide robust utility infrastructure, a skilled workforce, and opportunities to support public services through increased tax revenue.
AWS is committed to making a positive impact in Georgia through job creation, educational partnerships, sustainability initiatives, and community reinvestment.
source_title: AWS plans $11 billion Georgia infrastructure investment, to support AI and cloud tech

entity_name: DOT and NHTSA Autonomous Vehicle Framework
canonical_name: Department of Transportation and National Highway Traffic Safety Administration Automated Vehicle Framework
facts: On April 24, 2025, US Transportation Secretary Sean P. Duffy announced a new NHTSA Automated Vehicle (AV) Framework as part of the DOT’s agenda to promote domestic innovation.

The AV Framework includes three key principles: prioritize safety of ongoing AV operations on public roads, unleash innovation by removing unnecessary regulatory barriers, and enable commercial deployment of AVs to enhance safety and mobility for the American public.
NHTSA is poised to explore further avenues to accelerate the domestic development of AVs.

Two initial actions under the Framework include the issuance of the Third Amended Standing General Order 2021-01 and the expansion of the Automated Vehicle Exemption Program to include domestically produced vehicles.

NHTSA issued a Third Amended Standing General Order 2021-01 (SGO) governing incident reporting for vehicles with Automated Driving Systems (ADS) and Level 2 or higher Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS).
The Third Amended SGO takes effect June 16, 2025.
The amended SGO eliminates the one-day timeline for incident reporting and now requires submission of an incident report no later than five calendar days after receiving notice of the incident.
The amended SGO eliminates the separate ten-day reporting requirement previously required under the SGO.
Under the amended SGO, incidents involving property damage only must be reported if the resulting property damage is reasonably expected to exceed $1,000.
Reporting is also required for property damage not expected to exceed $1,000 if the subject vehicle was the only vehicle in the crash or struck another vehicle or object.
If a property damage incident falls in either of these two scenarios, it must be reported by the 15th day of the calendar month following the month in which notice of the incident was received.
Monthly reports are now required only after the receipt of materially new or materially different information for particular fields in the incident report.

Fields requiring updated monthly reporting for new or materially different information include VIN, engagement status, source, highest severity alleged, subject vehicle damage, subject vehicle pre-crash movement, airbag deployment status for any vehicle involved, data availability, and narrative.
Reporting entities will no longer need to report when there is no new information to provide.

NHTSA Chief Counsel Peter Simshauser announced in an open letter to AV developers that the Agency is expanding the Automated Vehicle Exemption Program (AVEP) to include domestically produced vehicles.
Under 49 C.F.R. Part 591, NHTSA grants exemptions to allow research and demonstrations on imported non-FMVSS-compliant vehicles.
As of the announcement, NHTSA will now accept requests for vehicles built in the United States to receive these same exemptions for research and demonstration purposes.
NHTSA indicated that it seeks to streamline the process for receiving the broader 49 C.F.R. Part 555 exemptions, which can include commercialization purposes.
The announcements indicate a strong focus on accelerating the advancement and innovation of domestically produced AVs through the AV Framework.

Future initiatives are expected to include modifications to the Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS) to encourage safe commercial deployment of AVs and promote innovation under this framework.
source_title: DOT and NHTSA Announce Autonomous Vehicle Framework | Insights | Mayer Brown

facts: Rivian increases its Georgia plant production capacity by 50% to 300,000 vehicles annually in 2026.
The initial target for the Georgia facility was to produce around 200,000 vehicles annually.
A recent decision boosts the Georgia plant's expected annual output to 400,000 units per year.
The Georgia facility was initially planned as a second major assembly plant after Rivian's existing operations in Normal, Illinois.
The project focus has shifted from building an entirely new, separate facility to overhauling and expanding the already planned site.
Once fully operational, the single Georgia location is targeted to achieve an annual production rate of 400,000 vehicles.
The expansion involves upgrading the plant's physical footprint and integrating advanced manufacturing technologies, including robotics, automation, and smart factory systems.
These technological upgrades are designed to enhance efficiency, improve quality control, and drive down production costs.
Rivian is working closely with local authorities and partners to ensure the expansion aligns with environmental standards and community development goals.
The increased production capacity will primarily focus on Rivian's core models: the R1T electric pickup truck and the R1S electric SUV.
Expanding production is essential to reduce waiting times for customers and compete effectively with established automakers and other EV startups.
The increased production capacity will likely facilitate the introduction of new variants or potentially lower-cost models in the future.
Rivian's expansion is projected to create thousands of new jobs covering roles from assembly line workers and technicians to engineers and administrative staff.
The influx of employment opportunities will provide a substantial economic stimulus, increasing household incomes and consumer spending within the community.
The expansion will generate indirect economic activity through Rivian's reliance on a network of suppliers for parts and materials.
Investment in local infrastructure, including transportation networks and utilities, is expected to have long-lasting positive effects.
The development is expected to attract further investment into the area, potentially making Georgia a hub for advanced manufacturing and automotive innovation.
Rivian aims to capture a larger share of the burgeoning electric vehicle market by significantly boosting production capacity in Georgia by 2026.
Increased production allows Rivian to move beyond its current subscriber base and appeal to a broader demographic.
The scaling is critical for Rivian to compete effectively against established automotive giants like Ford and Tesla, as well as EV startups like Lucid.
Increasing output allows Rivian to work towards achieving economies of scale, potentially leading to more competitive pricing and improved profit margins over time.
The increased output is crucial for fulfilling backorders and reducing delivery timelines.
Analysts will be watching closely how Rivian manages the ramp-up, cost controls, and its ability to maintain quality at higher production volumes.
The expanded manufacturing capability provides flexibility to introduce future “R2” platform vehicles, which are expected to be more affordable and target a larger market segment.
Rivian's commitment demonstrates a strategic focus on consolidating and maximizing its manufacturing resources in the U.S. for greater control over production timelines and costs.
Rivian's facility in Normal, Illinois, has been its primary manufacturing hub with a stated annual capacity of approximately 150,000 vehicles.
The Normal, Illinois plant is responsible for producing the R1T, R1S, and the Amazon Electric Delivery Van.
The Georgia expansion is designed to significantly augment the existing capacity at the Normal facility.

The 50% increase in production capacity aiming for 400,000 units annually is expected to substantially shorten delivery times for customers by fulfilling existing orders faster and reducing the backlog.
source_title: Rivian (RIVN) Boosts Georgia Plant Capacity by 50% in 2026 | NEXUSVOLT

International companies accounted for 23% of the projects in Fiscal Year 2023.
Georgia shattered all previous global trade records with $196 billion in total trade, a 40% increase over five years.

Georgia has been named the No. 1 state for doing business for nine consecutive years.

facts: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing LLC is headquartered in Mt. Pleasant, Texas.
The company designs and manufactures trailers for a range of commercial and personal hauling needs.
Its products are used across industries such as construction, landscaping, agriculture, and transportation.
On February 5, 2026, Big Tex Trailers notified the Georgia Office of Workforce Development of its decision to conduct a mass layoff at its facility in Cordele, Georgia.

The company is being investigated for potentially laying off 100 employees without providing the required 60 days' prior written notice under the federal Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act.
The WARN Act requires employers with 100 or more employees to provide at least 60 days' notice of significant layoffs or plant closings.
Employees laid off from Big Tex Trailers in Cordele, Georgia may be entitled to 60 days of severance pay and benefits if the company violated the WARN Act notice requirements.
Employers who fail to follow WARN Act rules by giving notice too late or providing unclear notices may have to pay employees back pay and benefits for the violation period.

facts: Blue Ridge Manufacturing's revenues are gauged from an analysis of company filings.
Blue Ridge Manufacturing has an income statement based on industry averages.
A competitive analysis shows other companies are in the same general field as Blue Ridge Manufacturing, even though they may not compete head-to-head.
These companies are the largest by revenue but may not have the largest market share if they have diversified into other business lines.
Competitive advantage for similar companies comes from offering better pricing or superior products/service.
Some companies compete with neighboring businesses for customers, while others compete to attract skilled employees.
Future competition includes rapidly expanding companies that may grow organically or through acquisition.
In some cases apparently high growth rates may be caused by data that weren't available in previous years.
source_title: Annual Report on Blue Ridge Manufacturing's Revenue, Growth, SWOT Analysis & Competitor Intelligence - IncFact

facts: The Port of Savannah handled a record 5.4 million container units last year.

A multibillion-dollar expansion is underway at the Port of Savannah's Ocean Terminal to transform it into a dedicated container facility, improve gate and crane capabilities, and establish larger ship berths.
The Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) is accommodating this growth through infrastructure upgrades, harbor dredging, and a new container terminal on Hutchinson Island.
The GPA views its inland ports as a way to extend port services inland to help support industry and economic development in Georgia.
The $127 million Blue Ridge Connector inland port is 75% complete and expected to open in spring 2026.
The Blue Ridge Connector is funded entirely through GPA revenues rather than taxpayer dollars.
The Blue Ridge Connector is located near Interstate 85 about 50 miles northeast of Atlanta.
The terminal will serve a region of more than two million residents and a growing concentration of forest products industries, food processing, and manufacturing.
The location was chosen to better serve existing Georgia forest customers and manufacturers who currently have to send drivers all the way to the coast to pick up cargo.
With the new terminal, companies will be able to move hundreds of boxes by train instead of one container per truck, saving cost, time, and emissions.
Once operational, daily trains will bridge Gainesville and Savannah, delivering containers to the Blue Ridge site within 48 hours.
The terminal is anticipated to handle 26,000 containers in its first year.
The GPA invested $4.8 million in Hall County road improvements, with upgrades completed in summer 2025 to secure safer and more efficient local access.
No major expansions are planned beyond the initial footprint of the Blue Ridge Connector, but capacity could grow over time as demand increases.
The terminal will start with seven rubber-tire gantry cranes, with plans to add more as demand increases.
The Blue Ridge Connector will join the GPA’s inland port network alongside the Appalachian Regional Port and the Carolina Connector.
The terminal will strengthen Georgia’s position as a manufacturing and export powerhouse, serving companies like Williams Sonoma, Kubota, and SK Battery already operating in the area.
These industries move key commodities such as furniture, heavy equipment, and poultry.
The Blue Ridge Connector aims to make Georgia’s businesses more globally competitive by giving them more affordable and faster access to international markets.
The GPA is aiming to strengthen regional supply chains, lower costs for businesses, and guarantee that the prosperity generated at Georgia’s coast reaches industries across the state.
source_title: Expanding Georgia’s Gateway - The George-Anne Media Group

facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that Belgium-based Stow Group, a leader in industrial storage and automation solutions, will invest US$36 million (£26.8 million) to establish a new manufacturing facility in Gordon County, Georgia.
The strategic investment will result in a 240,000 square foot manufacturing site located along Highway 41 near Adairsville.
The facility is scheduled to commence operations in 2026.
The new facility is poised to create approximately 200 jobs in the local community.
Stow Group plans to hire for various production roles, including profiling, stamping, welding, powder coating, automation, and assembly.
Additional job opportunities will be available in logistics, administration, and management.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s reliable infrastructure network and strategic location ensure the state remains a gateway to opportunity, citing companies like Stow Group as a big reason why Georgia's robust logistics industry continues to deliver results across the state.
Governor Kemp emphasized looking forward to the impact of this job creator on the community and critical industries throughout Georgia.

Jos De Vuyst, CEO of Stow Group, stated that the new production facility in Georgia will further strengthen Stow’s presence and support network in North America and bring the company closer to existing and new customers.

Expanding its production footprint in the United States is a key milestone for Stow Group, with the new Gordon County facility allowing the company to serve customers faster, enhance local partnerships, and continue its commitment to innovation and sustainability.
source_title: Belgium’s Stow Group Invests $36 Million to Create US Manufacturing Hub in Georgia - Food and Beverage Business

facts: Georgia Ports Authority links importers and exporters to global markets via 37 weekly container ship calls.
Georgia’s ports are a key factor in the decision for automakers, their suppliers, other manufacturers, and logistics operations to bring jobs to the state.
Griff Lynch is Georgia Ports Authority President and CEO.
Griff Lynch stated that the authority's mission is to drive the success of the entire state, not just Savannah or Atlanta.
GPA and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced 350 projects from potential port users in Fiscal Year 2023.
These announcements will bring more than 33,000 manufacturing and logistics jobs and $21 billion in investment to 96 counties across the state.
Griff Lynch said GPA, GDEcD, and the Georgia Department of Transportation are working to foster responsible growth that delivers good jobs while preserving quality of life.
Georgia Ports is investing $29 million in an overpass that will route trucks leaving its Ocean Terminal in Savannah directly onto U.S. 17.

Georgia’s growing electric vehicle ecosystem has touched every corner of the state, led by announcements associated with the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America located in more than 10 counties across the state.
Pat Wilson is a Commissioner at the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Pat Wilson stated that companies in e-mobility are prioritizing speed-to-market solutions.
Pat Wilson noted that investing in site development and obtaining “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” certification are ways communities can prepare for new opportunities.
Anovion Technologies is a synthetic graphite manufacturer creating 400 jobs for Southwest Georgia in Decatur County.
Chip Dunn is Anovion Executive Chairman.

Chip Dunn described the state’s recruitment effort that swayed Anovion's decision to locate in rural Georgia, including meetings with Gov. Brian Kemp, GPA’s inland port system, and access to waterway and land transport.
The inland port system includes the Appalachian Regional Port in Crandall, Ga., and the Blue Ridge Connector rail yard now under construction near Gainesville, Ga.
In the Savannah area alone, the inventory of industrial property totals more than 124 million square feet, with more than 14 million square feet under construction at the close of Calendar Year 2023.
Stephen Ezelle is a broker for industrial real estate firm Cushman & Wakefield.
Savannah regional economy continues to experience expansion and strong absorption in the industrial sector despite a slowing economy across most U.S. markets.
Savannah is expected to eclipse 8 million TEUs of throughput by 2030.
The operational efficiency offered by the GPA and shift in import cargo from the West Coast and NY/NJ are key drivers in the growth of Savannah’s industrial market.
Savannah’s industrial sector ended 2023 with an overall vacancy rate of 7 percent.
Augusta is a short drive from Savannah, and a majority of its manufacturers are port users.
Cal Wray is president of the Development Authority of Augusta, Ga.

Augusta-Richmond County provides an ideal location for port users due to available, affordable industrial land, over 350 manufacturers already calling it home, and easy rail and highway connections to the port.
Aurubis Richmond, a metal smelting operation, is bringing 250 jobs and $600 million in investment to Augusta.
Denkai America, a copper film producer, is providing 250 jobs and $440 million in investment to Augusta.
Purecycle Technologies, which recycles plastic into like-new pellets for reuse, is creating 100 jobs and $400 million in investment in Augusta.
A stand-out industry ecosystem in Augusta is electric vehicle manufacturing, with EZGo Textron and Club Car and their related suppliers located in Richmond and Columbia counties.
Metals and chemical manufacturing are two other important industries in Augusta.
Stacy Watson is director of economic and industrial development at GPA.
Stacy Watson stated that being within 30 miles is great, but companies do not have to be in order to be competitive in drayage, lease rates, and overall cost of doing business.

Stacy Watson noted that locations 40 or 50 miles away from Savannah are an easy drive, attributed to the port’s location upriver of the Savannah population center and Georgia Department of Transportation efforts making truck routes less congested.
Direct truck routes from the Port of Savannah to Interstates 95 and 16, as well as on-terminal service from two Class I railroads, expedite cargo moving to and from the marine terminal.
Available properties in Bryan, Liberty and McIntosh counties – as well as points west – offer interstate and rail links to the port and to thriving inland markets.
GPA supports the efficient operation of existing industries, local economic development efforts, and serves as a key partner in ongoing recruitment of new industry.
source_title: Georgia Ports Highlights Site Selection Opportunities - Rome CEO

facts: Bosch completed the acquisition of assets of TSI Semiconductors Corporation on August 31, 2023.
The acquired assets form a new company called Robert Bosch Semiconductor LLC.
Thorsten Scheer is heading the new organization as plant manager of the Roseville site and regional president of the Bosch Automotive Electronics division in North America.
California Competes Tax Credit incentive of $25 million was approved to support a planned $1.5 billion investment into the Roseville site by Bosch.
In April 2023, Bosch announced its intention to acquire certain assets of TSI Semiconductors.
Starting in 2026, the first silicon carbide (SiC) chips will be produced on 200-millimeter wafers at the Roseville site.
Bosch plans to invest some $1.5 billion to transform the Roseville site into a facility that produces and tests silicon carbide semiconductors, which are key building blocks for electric vehicles.
Michael Budde, President of Bosch Automotive Electronics, stated the company is systematically reinforcing its global portfolio of SiC chips to advance electromobility.
SiC chips enable greater range and more efficient recharging in electric vehicles by using up to 50 percent less energy.
Paul Thomas will assume the role of president, Americas for Bosch Mobility beginning January 2024.
Approximately 250 local associates at the Roseville site are now part of the global Bosch family.
The Roseville location has nearly 40 years of extensive experience in the design and production of semiconductors for automotive and industrial applications.
Production at the Roseville site will be built up gradually, and the location is expected to grow.
Thorsten Scheer is a physicist with a doctorate in economics and brings 10 years of professional experience in the sensor business, along with expertise in post-merger integration.
Over the next few years, Bosch intends to invest more than $1.5 billion USD in the Roseville site to upgrade manufacturing facilities to state-of-the-art processes.
The full scope of the planned investment will be heavily dependent on federal funding opportunities.
Bosch intends to apply for federal funding under the CHIPS and Science Act.
The Governor's Office of Business & Economic Development (GO-Biz) approved the $25 million California Competes Tax Credit incentive to support redevelopment and investment in Roseville.
Thorsten Scheer stated that Bosch looks forward to a strong, long-term partnership with the City of Roseville, Placer County, and the State of California.
The semiconductor experts in Roseville and existing clean-room facilities will allow Bosch to manufacture SiC chips for electromobility on an even larger scale.
As of December 31, 2022, the Bosch Group employs roughly 421,000 associates worldwide across nearly every country.
Bosch generated $93.1 billion in sales in 2022, with $15.1 billion from the U.S., Canada, and Mexico combined.
Bosch employs approximately 85,500 associates in research and development at 136 locations globally, including nearly 44,000 software engineers.
Ninety-four percent of the share capital of Robert Bosch GmbH is held by Robert Bosch Stiftung GmbH, a charitable foundation.
The majority of voting rights for Robert Bosch GmbH are held by Robert Bosch Industrietreuhand KG, an industrial trust.
