View of Bosch semiconductor plant in Reutlingen, Germany
Photo of Bosch semiconductor production in Reutlingen, Germany

source_title: Bosch completes acquisition of assets of TSI Semiconductors

facts: The Vehicle Motion Division (VM Division) is one of the backbones supporting Bosch.
The development of braking systems that ensure the safety and security of automobiles is particularly important to Bosch.
The role of software control has become extremely important in recent years for automotive safety.
Yuji Tokumasu heads the Engineering Software Department within the VM Division.

The VM Division is responsible for ESP® (Electronic Stability Program, anti-skid device), ABS (anti-lock braking system, technology to ensure vehicle safety during sudden braking, etc.), iBooster (electro-mechanical brake booster), airbags, and on-board sensors.

The Software Engineering Department comprehensively conducts project management in software development, function development from specification analysis, software architecture design, network communication, integration, and software testing.
Yuji Tokumasu's department mainly develops software for braking systems.
In recent years, the importance of software technology has increased because hardware faces limits on how quickly technology can be updated and improved.
Customers have a vision for future technical improvements to be provided as software in their central units rather than hardware.
At Bosch, a software foundation called a platform is basically developed in Germany.
Various software components are prepared based on a common standard software architecture for automotive electronic control units, such as AUTOSAR.
These platforms serve as the core part that supports all customers.
Bosch listens directly to requirements from automobile manufacturers to finalize specifications.
Actual software development follows a style where Japan is the center, with bases in Germany, India, Vietnam, France, and the United States sharing the work.
Once specifications are decided, Bosch leverages the platform to design, code, test, integrate components, and conduct system tests including vehicle tests before releasing to customers.
The mainstream platform contains components required for products, including control and network technologies, and algorithms/interfaces for individual software like ESP.
Know-how about controlling and moving vehicles is consolidated in the platform, which is then customized by incorporating customer-specific specifications.
Bosch's Japan team serves Japanese automobile manufacturers as customers.
Development tailored to customer needs is carried out in Japan, requiring direct communication in Japanese.
Bosch often makes proposals rather than just responding to detailed requests from customers.
Bosch adopts a development system close to customers globally: German headquarters handles VW and Daimler; the North American team handles Ford, GM, and Tesla.
The technology of Japanese automobile manufacturers is highly regarded worldwide, supporting Bosch's world-leading technological capabilities.
Decision-making power for platforms remains with German headquarters, but Japan's influence has increased as orders from Japanese OEMs have grown.
Globally, the number of personnel in Japan's software development team is high.
Demand from Japanese automobile manufacturers for software has been increasing, with demands expanding and becoming more detailed.
Brake control specifications differ for each car model, requiring responses to individual company requirements.
Connected automobiles are vulnerable to cyberattacks via networks, making cybersecurity crucial for vehicle safety.
Cybersecurity requires software-based responses that comply with laws, regulations, and the latest technology.
The software development team plays an important role in addressing cybersecurity threats.
Both vehicle manufacturers and suppliers must protect safety as software evolves quickly.
Yuji Tokumasu has worked at Bosch for a period (third company), citing constant new missions like creating the future of automated driving using AI.
Development methods are constantly changing, such as adopting agile practices in software development.
The role of software in automated driving technology will be important in the future.
Responding to new technological concepts for automated driving is a key step for the Engineering Brake Systems Department.
Bosch has adopted a strategy presenting various portfolios to respond flexibly to any direction each vehicle manufacturer takes.
DevOps viewpoint, promoting flexible and speedy development and operations, will become important in creating future automobiles with manufacturers.
Project teams organize people according to concept regardless of nationality (e.g., Japanese or Indian).
Communication with engineers at overseas bases is basically in English.
Software terminology is basically the same worldwide.
Engineers from each country are Bosch colleagues with high technical capabilities.
Online communication became the mainstay over the past two years due to the coronavirus pandemic, replacing travel to Bengaluru (India base).
Japan has a number of excellent suppliers, and the entire Bosch Group often works closely with a single customer to develop software for them.
Advanced initiatives in development with Tesla have been accumulated globally, allowing each base to propose the essence to local customers.
Bosch's embedded software standard development language is C, with Python used for automation of business and data processing parts.
Basic knowledge of C and Python is desirable for software personnel.
A basic understanding of software structure and architecture is beneficial.
Experience with embedded software is not required; the door is open to anyone interested in software development (application, web, image processing).
Vehicle control software requires knowledge of hardware and physics, but the barrier is not high for those who find it enjoyable.
Bosch products keep people safe, leading to great pride and responsibility in safety standards and software quality.
The Bosch education system includes company-provided training and self-study opportunities for growth.
As a European company, Bosch has a rational organizational culture compared to Japan, with a very systematic training system.
The training curriculum is diverse, allowing free choice of subjects even if not directly related to work.
Leadership training curriculum was useful for career advancement, teaching how to think as a leader and manager.
This culture applies to career choices, offering opportunities to take initiative in jobs one wants to challenge.
Plenty of opportunities for self-improvement exist due to many different jobs.
Flexibility to absorb and ability to learn can lead to becoming an engineer even without automotive industry experience.
Information was current at the time of the interview, published on September 26, 2022.
source_title: Background of the VM Division's focus on recruiting SW associates and its future goals | Recruitment Site for Mid-Career | Bosch Corporation

facts: On April 1, 2025, Bosch Automotive Workshop Services LLC announced its expansion into the American Southeast, bringing high-quality and efficient automotive service opportunities to Georgia, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee, with plans to expand into additional states in the future.
Uli Jaschek serves as the CEO of Bosch Auto Service.

In August 2025, Bosch and CARIAD intensified their cooperation within the Automated Driving Alliance to develop software stacks for Level 2 and 3 assisted and automated driving by making full use of artificial intelligence.
Shawn Dupuie holds the title of Senior Director of Sales and Operations at Bosch Auto Service.
On July 1, 2025, Bosch Auto Service opened a new franchise location in Houston, Texas, led by long-time industry professional Nelson Ramos.
On August 1, 2024, Bosch Auto Service Alamo Ranch opened to the public as its first Texas franchise location in San Antonio, offering complete auto repair services.

On November 18, 2024, Bosch Automotive Workshop Services LLC announced a Development Rights Agreement with Larry Battison, co-founder of Global Dealer Services, to open 15 new franchise locations in Oklahoma over the next four years.
On July 22, 2024, Bosch Auto Service announced a collaboration with Shopgenie, an automotive technology company specializing in customer relationship management solutions.
In September 2023, Bosch began volume production of its fuel-cell power module at its Stuttgart-Feuerbach location and initiated a recycling plan to recover almost all the platinum in the stacks.
In July 2022, Bosch announced it would invest further billions into its semiconductor business for applications in cars, e-bikes, household appliances, and wearables.
Bosch has won orders worth billions for vehicle computers that provide intelligence for modern automotive electronics.
Bosch charging services are being deployed to support the ramp-up of electromobility worldwide.
In November 2022, Bosch and Mercedes-Benz reached a milestone with Germany’s Federal Motor Transport Authority (KBA) approving their driverless parking system for commercial use.
Over the past five years leading up to June 2023, Bosch filed patent applications for more than 1,000 AI inventions, making it one of the leading applicants for AI patents in Europe.
In May 2023, Bosch announced plans to realign its automotive-supply business to focus on software-defined mobility and further growth.
source_title: Bosch Auto Service Franchise News | Bosch Auto Service

facts: Over the past five years, Bosch has invested more than $2 billion in capital expenditures in the United States, representing more than 75 percent of its North American capital expenditures.
Since 2023, Bosch has announced plans to invest around $6 billion in U.S.-based acquisitions.
The company currently employs more than 20,000 associates and operates 15+ manufacturing sites in the United States, with both numbers expected to grow in 2025 following a major planned acquisition.

Bosch's Mobility business sector is its largest, expanding its product portfolio to match U.S. mobility trends including software-defined vehicles (SDV), advanced-driver assistance systems (ADAS), hybrid technology, and vehicle motion control.

The company continues to innovate on internal combustion engine (ICE) technology to support hybrids while also providing battery electric and hydrogen solutions, including fuel cell and hydrogen combustion technology.
Bosch maintains an experienced U.S. mobility manufacturing workforce across nine locations in California, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Minnesota, and South Carolina.
Plans are underway to manufacture second-generation silicon carbide (SiC) chips on 200mm wafers at a facility in Roseville, California beginning in 2026.
The transformation of the acquired Roseville facility represents an investment of up to $1.9 billion.

Nearly all 250 associates at the Roseville site have been retained during the transformation process to prepare for the 2026 SiC production launch, with advanced training provided from other Bosch global manufacturing sites.

The Cross-Domain Computing Solutions division is a major growth driver for Bosch Mobility in North America, delivering ADAS and vehicle computers to support U.S. automakers' shift toward software-defined vehicles.
Bosch has expanded its software and hardware capabilities for assisted and automated driving, particularly Level 2 and Level 2++ systems.

The company developed a new radar sensor with all core elements manufactured in-house, including a computing chip (SoC) using RF CMOS technology with a 22-nanometer transistor structure size, making Bosch the first Tier 1 supplier to introduce this technology in serial production.

Bosch signed a major award with the Southeastern Pennsylvania Transportation Authority (SEPTA) to install tram forward collision warning systems across its fleet via retrofit, following a successful 2022 pilot program on four vehicles.

In 2024, Bosch announced plans to acquire Johnson Controls' HVAC solutions business for residential and light commercial buildings, including the York and Coleman brands, with closure expected in 2025.

Through its subsidiary Bosch Ventures, the company is providing around $270 million USD for a new venture capital fund focused on stimulating growth in electromobility, hydrogen, and software-defined vehicles.
The Industrial Technology division's factory automation segment is focusing on growth areas including battery, semiconductor, and consumer goods production.
Chairman Stefan Hartung stated the company's ambition for the North American region to represent 20 percent of global sales by 2030, with the U.S. accounting for the majority.

Paul Thomas, president of Bosch in North America and Bosch Mobility Americas, confirmed the company is investing to grow in the U.S. market and has developed regionally relevant products and solutions to increase market share and achieve profitable growth.
source_title: Bosch focuses on growth in United States - Bosch Media Service US

facts: Bandag signed an agreement with Tegeta Motors to establish a state-of-the-art tyre retreading facility in Rustavi.
The facility marks the first Bandag factory in the Caucasus region.
The project is supported by Bridgestone Middle East and Africa (MEA).
The initial investment for the project is USD 2 million.
The initiative aims to expand sustainable mobility and circular economy practices across the region.
The Tegeta Bandag Factory is designed to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tyres annually in its initial phase.
Plans are in place to double production capacity within five years.
Construction of the facility is scheduled to begin in 2025.
Completion of the facility is targeted for late 2026.
The plant will introduce internationally recognised retreading standards to the Caucasus market.
The facility will initially focus on retreading tyres for buses and trucks.
The operation ensures compliance with global safety and performance benchmarks.
The project helps fleet operators reduce costs and environmental impact.

Jacques Fourie, President of Bridgestone Africa, Middle East Region, stated the project represents a major step in expanding high-quality retreading solutions in the Caucasus, combining global expertise, shared vision, and a strong commitment to sustainable mobility.
The factory will be equipped with advanced Bandag machinery sourced from Europe and the United States.
The retreading process reinforces used tyre casings with premium materials similar to those used in new tyre manufacturing.
The process delivers cost savings of 30–40 per cent compared to new tyres.
The retreaded tyres offer performance and service life of up to five years.
Ekaterine Kavtaradze, CEO of Tegeta Motors, stated the project will help advance fleet solutions, strengthen the company’s regional presence, and deliver long-term value to customers and communities.
Tegeta Motors will integrate the new retreading operations into its broader ecosystem of tyre, oil, battery, and recycling services.
The initiative underscores Bandag’s regional growth strategy and commitment to cleaner, more efficient mobility solutions through strategic partnerships and advanced technology.
source_title: Bandag to build state-of-the-art retreading factory in Georgia with Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone

facts: Bridgestone Americas celebrated the expansion of its Bandag manufacturing plant in Abilene, Texas with an official groundbreaking ceremony.
The 50,000-square-foot, $60 million expansion of the Abilene plant is aimed at meeting growing demand for tread rubber products.
The expansion includes an immediate increase in operational activity by running on six- and seven-day production schedules.
New mixing operations are being constructed at the facility.
The new mixing operations are expected to be completed and in operation by January 2025.
Since 1957, Bandag has been responsible for keeping approximately 300 million tires out of the waste system.
The retreading process helps reduce material emissions, water, and energy use associated with new tire production.
The company's retreading process has saved four billion gallons of oil since it began 50 years ago.
source_title: Bridgestone Announces Retread Tire Plant Expansion

facts: Bridgestone Americas, Inc. launched a new industry-wide campaign focused on educating small-to-medium sized fleets on the advantages of retreads.
The campaign was launched at the 2019 Technology & Maintenance Council (TMC) Annual Meeting & Transportation Technology Exhibition in Atlanta.
The initiative is called the “Fleets We Depend On” campaign and spotlights fleets such as fire departments, first responders, and school districts that rely on retreads.

Eric Higgs, president of truck, bus and retread tires for U.S. & Canada at Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations (BATO), stated Bridgestone is committed to making mobility more efficient and aims to inform small- and mid-sized fleets on the benefits of choosing Bandag.

LaTres Jarrett, director of marketing for Bandag in the U.S. and Canada, noted that while retreading is common in larger fleets, small to medium-size fleets retread less but could benefit from lower total cost of ownership.
Bridgestone research showed that retreads cost 30-50% less than buying a new tire.
Dealers who own a retread plant can gain a second stream of revenue by engaging fleet customers in a program.
The commercial tire industry has been affected by ultra-low-cost imported tires that are generally used only once and are less likely to be retreaded.
Bridgestone data estimates the surge in ultra-low-cost tires contributed to the closure of more than 450 independently-owned small and medium retread facilities between 2000 and 2016.
The decline of retreading may have environmental implications, including reducing CO2 emissions, air pollution, and natural resources.

The campaign includes a 27-stop roadshow at Bridgestone locations and dealer shops across North America, with cities including Atlanta; Nashville; Phoenix; Indianapolis; Akron, Ohio; Orlando, Florida; and Elizabeth, New Jersey.
The roadshow will run through July 2019.
The campaign includes a Fleets We Depend On Award presented by Bandag to recognize reliable individuals and fleets.
Nominees can be submitted through the Bandag website or at roadshow stops in North America.
The award winner will be announced in September and will receive a cash prize of $5,000 and a matching donation to a 501(c)(3) organization of their choice.

links.context: Bandag website for campaign details and award nominations

source_title: Bridgestone Launches 'Fleets We Depend On' Campaign in Push for Retreading

facts: Bandag (part of Bridgestone) and Tegeta Motors signed an agreement to build a tire retreading plant in Rustavi, Georgia.
The new facility will be Bandag's first plant in the Caucasus region.
Initial investments for the project are around $2 million.
Retreaded tires cost 30-40% less than new tires while maintaining comparable characteristics and a lifespan of up to five years.
The retreading process requires less raw materials and electricity, extends tire life through multiple cycles, reduces CO2 emissions, and promotes efficient natural resource use.
Construction is expected to begin before the end of this year and be completed by the end of 2026.
The plant will initially have a capacity to retread up to 5,000 tires per year, with plans to double capacity within five years.
Initially, the facility will retread tires for trucks and buses.
Jacques Fourie, President of Bridgestone MEA, stated that the collaboration combines technical expertise and a shared vision for sustainable mobility to benefit fleets and communities in the region.
Representatives from Bridgestone and Bandag will ensure the plant meets global standards.
The facility will be equipped with modern equipment manufactured in Europe and the US.
Tegeta Motors is involved in tire sales, car maintenance services, and waste recycling.

Ekaterine Kavtaradze, General Director of Tegeta Motors, noted the launch expands their fleet solutions portfolio, promotes eco-friendly mobility, and strengthens their presence in the Caucasus region.
source_title: In the Caucasus, the first Bandag tire retreading plant will appear

facts: Rising oil prices are contributing to a rapid escalation in new tire costs.
Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions (BBTS), a division of Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations, is launching a campaign to promote the wider use of retreads by truck fleets.
Tightness in the market supply for new replacement tires in 2011 has made retreading more attractive for all types of fleets.
Chris Ripani, BBTS director of marketing for strategic brands and channels, explained that fleets are extending tire casing age limits to realize maximum value from owned casings.
Fleets are returning to or evaluating retreads for the first time as a strategy to maximize their tire programs and keep their trucks rolling.
BBTS launched a new website designed to provide fleet managers with tools and information on how to get the most out of retreading.
The website includes a Wheel Position Analyzer tool, a cost-analysis example for considering extending current casing age limits, and an informational video on Shearography inspection technology.
Mike Bice, tire program manager for Crete Carrier Corp., extended his company's tire casing life from five to seven years.
Crete Carrier spent about five years tweaking its tire program and diagnosing scrap pile data to determine that a seven-year casing age limit provided the best return on investment.
By adding two years to the casing age limit, Crete gained one to two additional retreads per casing.
This strategy significantly lowered overall operating costs for maintaining over 172,000 wheel positions within Crete's fleet.

BBTS conducted studies of more than 13 million tires rejected for retreading and determined that 30% of those commercial truck-tire casings could have been retreaded at least one more time if the repair specs had been different.
The Retread Tire Association notes that every typical-size truck tire retread saves approximately 15 gallons of oil.
Retreading also results in one less tire going to overcrowded landfills.

links.context: Website for fleet managers on retreading tools and information

source_title: Bridgestone Bandag launches retread campaign | FleetOwner

facts: Last year, the Development Corporation of Abilene (DCOA) announced a $60 million expansion of Bridgestone Bandag, a Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations (BATO) division.
Bridgestone Bandag is a global leader of medium and heavy-duty truck tires for North America’s original equipment and replacement markets.
The company has been an integral part of the Abilene community for more than half a century.
The expansion adds 50,000 square feet to the existing 200,000-square-foot facility.
The project is expected to create 25 new jobs and retain 179 full-time employees.
The expansion will contribute approximately $21 million in taxes to the City of Abilene, Taylor County, and Abilene Independent School District.

Misty Mayo, President and Chief Executive Officer of the Development Corporation of Abilene, stated that having a company with a long history of manufacturing in Abilene make an additional commitment is a true highlight.
The DCOA serves as the economic development driver of Abilene, with a mission to attract and sustain industries that support job creation and foster strong business.
Over the next 10 years, the total estimated direct and indirect economic impact of the expansion is $1.81 billion.
The $60 million expansion marks the fifth largest project in the DCOA’s history.
Sam Vinson, DCOA Board Chair, noted that Bridgestone Bandag is a well-established company whose expansion brings immense value to the community.
Chris Daniel, Executive Director of Bandag Manufacturing at Bridgestone Americas, spoke at the groundbreaking ceremony regarding the decision to grow in Abilene.
Daniel stated the growth is driven by growing demand for products and the team’s commitment to delivering innovation and superior quality for customers globally.
The company plays a vital role in the transformation of mobility by building greener retreads that help maximize fuel efficiency for fleets.
The expansion involves developing an automated rubber mixing and production operation to meet burgeoning demand in the retread tire industry.
The investment aligns with Bridgestone’s E8 Commitment, a program to achieve carbon neutrality in mobility by 2050.
The E8 Commitment encompasses eight core values beginning with “E,” including Energy, Ecology, and Efficiency.
Since its inception in 1957, Bandag has diverted approximately 300 million tires away from landfills.
The retreading process significantly reduces material emissions, water consumption, and energy use associated with producing new tires.
The Bandag retreading process has conserved approximately four billion gallons of oil.
Abilene is located in Taylor County, Texas, directly on I-20, just two and a half hours from the Dallas/Fort Worth metro area.
Abilene’s metropolitan statistical area covers a three-county region with a population of 179,308.
The region offers reliable infrastructure, a pro-business environment conducive to economic growth, and progressive business incentives.

The project taps into Abilene’s skilled manufacturing labor force, bolstered by education initiatives from four universities, one technical college, one community college, and high school training opportunities.
The DCOA operates a program called NEXTUniversity (NEXTU) that prepares and trains students for degree programs through local independent school districts.
NEXTU assists young professionals in pursuing careers such as welding, industrial systems, aircraft maintenance, and nursing.
Through high school and beyond, NEXTU students receive scholarship dollars covering technical classes, books, supplies, and tools.
With career coach guidance, NEXTU students obtain industry-based certifications and career connections.
More than 13,000 students are actively being educated in Abilene through the program.
Primary manufacturing is a focus at local institutions like Texas State Technical College (TSTC) and Cisco College.
source_title: Abilene, Texas: A Manufacturing Powerhouse Backed by Robust Education Resources | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Carcoustics, a German automotive acoustic parts supplier, plans to create 200 new jobs in Buford over the next half-decade.
The Leverkusen, Germany-based company will invest $6 million in a new 130,000-square-foot manufacturing facility at 4620 Thompson Mill Road.
Carcoustics focuses on developing and producing solution-oriented automotive parts that are both acoustically and thermally effective.
Governor Nathan Deal stated that Georgia offers automotive industry suppliers like Carcoustics the resources necessary to compete worldwide.
Gov. Deal noted that Carcoustics will benefit from a well-trained workforce, a thriving automotive industry, and the nation's top business environment in Georgia.
Gov. Deal expressed pride in another German company locating in Georgia and welcomed Carcoustics to the state's growing community of auto industry suppliers.

The project announcement resulted from a joint effort between the Georgia Department of Economic Development's Global Commerce Division, Partnership Gwinnett, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Carcoustics will join numerous other international and German-based companies with locations in Gwinnett County.

Partnership Gwinnett Project Manager Deven Cason stated that Carcoustics was chosen as a global innovation leader and its new Buford facility is ideally located to serve its rapidly growing worldwide customer base.
Carcoustics will join nearly 100 other German companies operating in Gwinnett County, which also hosts 500 internationally owned and operated firms.

Carcoustics Project Manager Stefan Vallentin cited the facility's strategic location near Southeast U.S. car plants as a key factor, specifically naming facilities owned by Kia, Mercedes, BMW, Honda, Volkswagen, Nissan, Hyundai, and Volvo.
The proximity to these automotive production facilities provides Carcoustics with access to a large pool of potential customers.
Stefan Vallentin stated that Georgia provides excellent international transport connections and is logistically positioned between BMW's plant in South Carolina and Mercedes' plant in Alabama.
Vallentin noted that this logistical advantage offers new possibilities for Carcoustics' growth strategy, particularly in the U.S. market.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted continued economic development opportunities in Gwinnett County and noted Carcoustics joins a growing number of German automotive manufacturers in Georgia.
Buford City Manager Bryan Kerlin called the project a great win for the city and expressed confidence that Carcoustics will find necessary local support and resources to remain globally competitive.

Gwinnett County Board of Commissioners Chairwoman Charlotte Nash welcomed Carcoustics, noting it joins a strong base of international companies that found Gwinnett's mix of talent, resources, and location ideal.
Chairwoman Nash expressed anticipation for Carcoustics' continued success and growth within the county.
source_title: Deal: German car parts company creating 200 jobs in Buford :: WRAL.com

facts: Carcoustics specializes in the development and production of acoustic, thermal and electromagnetic insulation solutions for passenger cars and commercial vehicles.
The company focuses on four major areas of vehicle requirements: Interior Solutions, Powertrain Solutions, Exterior Solutions and New Enery Vehicles.

Carcoustics is independent from predefined raw materials and works with seven different production technologies: Thermoforming, Aluminumforming, PU Foaming, Die Cutting, Vacuumforming, Injection Molding and Blow Molding.
The company relies on a multicultural team of employees and managers and promotes individual employee development in all areas.
Carcoustics maintains a global footprint with production plants, development centers, and an international sales structure in Europe, America, and Asia.
Proximity to existing and potential new customers enables the company to make the benefits of new innovations available quickly and efficiently worldwide.
The company's focus is on sustainable and profitable growth in the relevant automotive markets.
In 1952, the Illbruck Company was founded.
In 2001, following a change of ownership, Illbruck's automotive division became a separate company called Carcoustics as part of a management buyout.
In 2005, the company opened its first Slovakian factory in Senec.
In 2008, Carcoustics expanded its business activities by applying its core competencies to the industrial and truck sectors.
In 2009, the first Carcoustics facility was opened in Querétaro, Mexico.
In 2012, the Langfang plant was opened, giving Carcoustics its own manufacturing facility in China.
In 2013, a second production facility was opened in Novaky, Slovakia.
In 2014, identical molded polyurethane foam production lines were established in China, Mexico and Slovakia to manufacture products to the same high standard worldwide.
In 2015, Thermoforming was introduced at the Querétaro plant, expanded through additional machinery capacity and the opening of a new location in Rankweil, Austria.
In 2016, Dare Group became the new investor.
In 2017, the Wroclaw plant in Poland was opened, along with a new plant in Buford, USA.
In 2018, new plants were opened in San Miguel de Allende (Mexico) and Shenyang (China), as well as a Test Center in Shanghai (China).
In 2021, Carcoustics started its 20th year with a new organizational structure and strategy focused on innovative solutions for customers and sustainable growth in all major automotive markets.
In 2023, the Innovation & Technology Center in Shanghai was officially opened, involving the relocation and extension of the former Test Center.
source_title: Carcoustics - Expert in Acoustics, Thermodynamics and electromagnetic shielding - Carcoustics International GmbH

The new facility will be located in the White Oak Industrial Park near an existing Club Car facility that opened in 2019.

Rick Evans, Chair of the Board of Directors for the Development Authority of Columbia County, highlighted Club Car's long-standing relationship with Columbia County and valued its continued investments to expand and manufacture golf cars and utility vehicles there.

facts: Club Car hosted a ribbon-cutting ceremony for its new Appling facility on Thursday morning.
Governor Brian Kemp attended the ribbon-cutting ceremony.
Columbia County Commissioners Connie Melear, Alison Couch, and Chairman Doug Duncan attended the ceremony and made remarks.
City Councilmember Richard Bowman attended on behalf of Grovetown.
Mayor Roxanne Whitaker attended on behalf of Harlem.
Georgia Senators Lee Anderson, Harold Jones, and Chuck Payne represented the state at the event.
State Representatives Mark Newton, Lynn Gladney, Trenton Howard, Gloria Fraser, Jodi Lott, and Gary Richardson represented the state at the event.
Club Car announced plans last October to launch a new manufacturing plant in a 105,391 square-foot building it is leasing in White Oak Industrial Park.
The Development Authority of Columbia County manages White Oak Industrial Park.
The new facility is located at 780 Innovation Pkwy.
The project represents an $8 million investment.
The new facility sits across the street east to Club Car’s existing half-million square foot distribution center at 750 Innovation Pkwy.
Both Club Car buildings are located across from the Amazon Fulfillment Center.
The plant will establish U.S. manufacturing of the Club Car Urban, a new street-legal, lightweight utility vehicle.
The Club Car Urban's design comes from Polish electric vehicle company Melex.
Club Car acquired Melex via its purchase of Danish EV manufacturer Garia in 2022.
Governor Kemp emphasized the manufacturer’s contribution to Georgia’s place in the growing regional electric vehicle sector during his address.

Governor Kemp stated: “We often tout these incredible investments that we’re seeing… in the auto manufacturer area, but we know that true e-mobility supply chains continue to include great products like those offered by Club Car.”
Club Car CEO and President Mark Wagner recognized Kemp’s pro-EV initiatives during his remarks.
Wagner highlighted the company’s reliance on lithium as a key element in the EV industry's supply chain that enabled Club Car’s growth.
Wagner stated: “It’s wild for us, because we’ve always been in this [electric vehicle] space.”

Wagner added: “This product [Club Car Urban] wouldn’t be possible without lithium. These electric vehicles are very capable, high range high torque. So it’s allowing us to build on that kind of EV purpose in the corridor.”
source_title: State and local leaders join Club Car in celebrating its new Appling facility - The Augusta Press

facts: Club Car, headquartered in Augusta, Georgia, announced the launch of its all-new CRU Lithium option for its street-legal CRU Neighborhood Electric Vehicle (NEV).
The CRU Lithium NEV features an advanced lithium-ion battery that delivers faster charging, lighter weight, longer range, and improved power.

Brant Mitchell, Senior Director of Product Management of Club Car, stated that the CRU Lithium combines style, comfort, and social design with lithium performance for a smarter, longer-lasting power solution.
The CRU Lithium NEV is available now through authorized Club Car dealers.
Club Car has over six decades of history in superior design and performance and is known for quality craftsmanship ensuring long-term reliability.
The Club Car product portfolio includes vehicles for commercial, personal, and street-legal low-speed use.
The Club Car Vehicle Group also includes the luxury electric vehicle brand Garia and the lightweight electric utility vehicle brand Melex.

facts: For more than 60 years, Club Car has set the standard in golf carts, utility vehicles, and personal transportation.
Club Car was founded in Augusta, Georgia.
Every detail from design and fabrication to assembly reflects a focus on durability, comfort, and performance.
Club Car earned recognition as one of EHS Today’s America’s Safest Companies.
Club Car operates a global network of over 400 authorized dealers and distributors.
Club Car is the Official Golf Car of the PGA of America, PGA TOUR’s TPC Network, and the European Tour.
Club Car vehicles are showcased at major events including the Ryder Cup, President's Cup, U.S. Kids Golf, and First Tee.
Club Car was originally founded in 1958 in Houston, Texas.
Club Car relocated to Augusta, Georgia in 1962 under Bill Stevens.
In 1979, a team of former EZGO executives known as “The Original 8” acquired Club Car and reshaped the industry with groundbreaking products.
In 1995, Club Car joined Ingersoll Rand.
In 2021, Club Car entered a new era under Platinum Equity.

facts: Club Car is headquartered in Augusta, Georgia.
Craig Scanlon was appointed President and Chief Executive Officer of Club Car, effective June 9, 2025.
Scanlon succeeds Mark Wagner, who announced his retirement in March.
Scanlon has over two decades of executive leadership experience in private equity-owned and Fortune 500 organizations.
Most recently, Scanlon served as CEO of K&N Engineering.
Scanlon's career spans the industrial, powersports and automotive aftermarket sectors, including 18 years at Polaris where he rose to Chief Marketing Officer.
Scanlon began his career in 1998 in commercial sales of Club Car Carryall vehicles while at Ingersoll Rand.
Jared Hutchins, Principal at Platinum Equity, which acquired Club Car from Ingersoll Rand in 2021, stated that Scanlon is a proven leader with operational expertise and deep industry experience.
Club Car is a global leader in golf cars, low-speed utility vehicles and personal-use transportation.
Club Car is poised to rapidly innovate with upcoming initiatives focused on new product and features, technology, and digital connectivity.
The Club Car product portfolio includes the luxury electric vehicle brand Garia and lightweight electric utility vehicle brand Melex.
Club Car has a history of superior design and performance that spans over six decades.

canonical_name: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. and Club Car LLC
facts: The world's top two golf cart manufacturers are headquartered in Augusta, Georgia.

The nonpartisan U.S. International Trade Commission announced that Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. and Club Car LLC, along with their domestic competitors, have been materially injured by unfairly traded imports of low-speed personal vehicles from China.
The Commerce Department stated in June its commitment to impose antidumping tariffs of between 119 percent and 478 percent on the affected imports.
Countervailing tariffs will be applied at rates between 31 percent and 679 percent.

As first reported by The Augusta Chronicle in December 2024, the U.S. Department of Commerce concluded that China's government engaged in antidumping and countervailing activities undercutting domestic vehicle makers.
Antidumping occurs when foreign manufacturers sell goods at less-than-fair value, while countervailing involves a foreign government subsidizing manufacturers to enable cheap sales.

Rob Scholl, president and CEO of Textron Specialized Vehicles, stated the ITC's decision will help protect the health of a uniquely American industry and the livelihoods of thousands of American workers who build golf cars, low-speed vehicles, and personal-transportation vehicles for global customers.

Craig Scanlon, president and CEO of Club Car, described the final determination from the U.S. International Trade Commission as a clear win for fair competition and the thousands of American jobs that power their industry.
Law firm Wiley Rein counseled the advocacy group American Personal Transportation Vehicle Manufacturers Coalition through its filings of several unfair-trade protests.
Wiley Rein announced the ITC's decision on July 17.
source_title: Golf cart companies Club Car, E-Z-Go applaud tariffs on Chinese products

facts: Police escorted trucks transporting scab parts through a picket line at Constellium Automotive in Van Buren Township, Michigan.
Workers on the picket line reportedly made each truck wait 15 minutes before allowing it through.
UAW Local 174 instructed members to allow scab trucks to enter and exit without delay instead of mobilizing solidarity action from Ford workers.
A picket reported that day shift held trucks for 15 minutes until the company called police and the union ordered them to be let through.
A co-worker stated that if Ford workers refused to take the scab parts, the strike would end by the next day.
The 160 workers at the plant were approaching their third week on strike.
Striking workers are demanding wage increases to offset the soaring cost of living, safe working conditions, and an end to the attendance points system.
Under the attendance points system, employees are fired after accumulating 10 points, with penalties ranging from half a point for being one minute late to 4 points for a no-call/no-show.
High turnover at the plant forces team leaders, who train new hires, to take on extra work.
The hazardous work environment includes tarps covering the plant to catch rainwater, which is directed via hose into a trash barrel.
Union bureaucracy collaborates with management to enforce speedups, often assigning one person to operate machinery designed for two.
New hires are frequently forced to work seven days a week for 12 hours a day.
The facility serves as a key regional supplier of aluminum engine rails and crash control systems to Ford Motor Company’s six flagship assembly plants.
Scab parts from this supplier are visibly identifiable on the shop floors of Ford's assembly plants.
On Tuesday, May 16, the union permitted the company to bring in approximately 40 temporary agency workers to be trained as strikebreakers.
Unqualified and uncertified management personnel are operating hi-los and other heavy equipment during the strike.
A Constellium spokesperson stated upon the strike's start that they do not anticipate production disruptions and will work with customers to ensure operational continuity.
The Paris, France-based corporation employs more than 12,000 workers internationally.
The global producer of aluminum rolled products, extruded products, and structural auto parts reported $8.73 billion in 2022 revenue, including $1.9 billion in automaker sales.
The company recorded a net profit of $332 million in 2022, an increase from $282 million in 2021.
CEO Jean-Marc Germain earned nearly $9 million in compensation last year.
The strike is part of a broader wave of autoworker resistance against automaker plans to force workers to fund the industry's transition to electric vehicles.
In Holland, Ohio, 525 UAW members have been striking for over three weeks against Clarios, the world's largest auto battery manufacturer.
Days after receiving a court injunction limiting picketing, Clarios began importing strikebreakers to its Toledo-area factory to maintain battery production for Ford, GM, Stellantis, and others.
Both Constellium and Clarios are collaborating with UAW leadership to secure wage settlements below inflation and mandate overtime at straight-time pay.
This trend threatens over 170,000 Big Three auto plant workers in the US and Canada facing contract negotiations in the fall.
Decades of auto industry concessions, including plant closures, pay cuts, temp labor, and eliminated COLA and personal days, largely originated in parts plants spun off from the Big Three.
After rejecting a UAW-backed contract with compulsory overtime and low pay, Clarios workers formed a Rank-and-File Committee under the International Workers Alliance of Rank-and-File Committees.
In a May 26 open letter, Clarios rank-and-file workers stated that merely saying "no" is insufficient and they must clearly define their strike demands.
The letter demands UAW President Shawn Fain utilize the union's $800 million strike fund to provide $1,000 weekly strike pay for Clarios and Constellium workers ahead of fall contract expirations.
Constellium workers face identical issues and are urged to connect with the Clarios Rank-and-File Committee to build mutual strike support.
Despite UAW leadership's attempts to isolate the strikes, rank-and-file workers from other facilities are joining the picket lines.
A picket reported that wages remain stagnant while costs rise, overtime requires 40 hours worked, and they are picketing 24/7 as maintenance workers and temps scab around the clock.
Constellium workers are encouraged to coordinate with the International Workers Alliance of Rank-and-File Committees at Clarios and Dana to develop a counter-strategy.
source_title: Police escort scab trucks through pickets at Constellium Automotive in Michigan - World Socialist Web Site

facts: Constellium operates a corporate office in downtown Baltimore, Maryland, located only a few minutes from the Inner Harbor.

Neuf-Brisach is one of Constellium’s largest plants with a capacity of 450,000 metric tons.

Paris is home to Constellium's Corporate Headquarters located in the heart of Paris, just a street away from the Champs-Elysées.
The Paris corporate office houses multiple teams including Finance, HR, IT, Legal, Marketing, Sustainability, Internal Audit, Communications and Public Affairs.
Constellium’s Automotive Structures plant in San Luis Potosí produces customized aluminium Crash Management Systems and structural components for automakers in Mexico.
The San Luis Potosí plant is centrally located to serve Mexico’s expansive auto industry.
The San Luis Potosí plant features a state-of-the-art quality lab.
Constellium’s plant at Singen is an aluminium manufacturing plant with over 1,500 employees.
The Singen plant features an integrated hot/cold rolling line, high-grade cold mills, and one of the largest extrusion presses in the world.
Sierre produces large profiles used in the transportation, industry, and general engineering sectors and has two of the largest extrusion presses in the world.
The Sierre facility also manufactures high value-added aluminium plates in a variety of widths dedicated to industry, defense, and aerospace.
Constellium Vigo is a state-of-the-art production facility which produces customized aluminium Crash Management Systems for OEM customers.
The Vigo facility employs more than 60 employees and is part of a network of Automotive Structures facilities serving automakers across Europe.

Zilina features advanced technologies for forming, machining, welding, and heat-treating aluminium automotive components along with a state-of-the-art quality lab to ensure products meet customer specifications.

Zurich is home to a Corporate Office located in the Altstetten quarter in district 9 of Zurich.

Hard copies of the audited financial statements included in the 2022 Form 20-F are available upon request to shareholders free of charge.
Constellium is a global sector leader that develops innovative, value added aluminium products for a broad scope of markets and applications, including aerospace, automotive and packaging.
Constellium generated €8.1 billion of revenue in 2022.

facts: Constellium is a world leader in transforming aluminum into innovative, lightweight products primarily for the aerospace, packaging, and automotive markets.
The company operates across North America, Europe, and Asia.
Constellium supplies aluminum plates, sheets, and extrusions to commercial, military, and space markets for global aerospace manufacturers.

Constellium is a world-leading supplier for the aluminum packaging market, designing, producing, and recycling aluminum coils and sheets for beverage and food cans, wine and spirits closures, and personal care products.

The company provides advanced aluminum rolled products and extrusion-based components to global automotive manufacturers to enable production of vehicles that are lighter, safer, more fuel-efficient, and have greater range.
Constellium develops specialty aluminum alloys for extrusions, sheets, plates, and semi-finished components used by the transportation and industry markets.
In the defense market, Constellium's advanced alloys offer outstanding impact resistance for armored vehicles, turrets, and military bridges.
Innovation is a driving force for the company, exploring ways aluminum can offer improved performance, enhanced manufacturability, and efficiency while minimizing its overall footprint.
The company promotes collaborative research among its teams, joint development with customers, and partnerships with universities, labs, and scientific organizations worldwide.
Constellium supports the circular economy of tomorrow while prioritizing the safety and well-being of employees and communities today.
Aluminum increases efficiency by reducing weight and can be recycled without losing its properties.
The company tracks its sustainability performance each year and transparently discloses it with independent verification.
Constellium (NYSE:CSTM) is a global sector leader that develops innovative, value-added aluminum products for a broad scope of markets including aerospace, automotive, and packaging.
Constellium generated $7.3 billion of revenue in 2024.
source_title: At a Glance 2025 - 2024 Digital Presentation

facts: Continental Tire is closing in 2026 and employees are currently being assisted by numerous local and regional work force resources.
The status of the plant itself is not yet determined as the company's priority is its employees until year end.

Based in Fort Mill, S.C., Continental Tire the Americas, LLC manufactures and distributes a complete premium line of passenger, light truck and commercial tires for original equipment and replacement markets.
Continental’s ultra-high, performance tires include award-winning technologies that focus on safety, help save money, and are #ForWhatYouDo.
Continental’s premium products are available at leading independent tire dealers, car dealers, and mass retail companies across North America.
Continental Tire of Barnesville manufactures tire cord fabric for Continental Tire.
The plant has been in operation since 1888 where it operated as a cotton mill, producing tire cord fabric in the early 1920s.
In 1930, the plant was sold to General Tire & Rubber Co., which was purchased by Continental A.G. in 1986.
Continental Tire the Americas, LLC was formerly known as Continental Tire North America, Inc. and changed its name to Continental Tire the Americas, LLC in February 2010.
The company was founded in 1983 and is based in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
Continental Tire the Americas, LLC operates as a subsidiary of Continental Aktiengesellschaft.
The Barnesville facility address is Packages: 160 Aldora St., Mail: PO Box 190, Barnesville, GA 30204, United States.
source_title: Continental Tire The Americas- Barnesville | Barnesville, GA

facts: DAS Corp. plans to establish a new manufacturing facility in Candler County.
DAS Corp.'s investment, coupled with collaborative indirect investments, will total more than $35 million.
The new Metter facility will create 300 jobs.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development representative confirmed the project is still active and that Georgia Quick Start is involved.
The Georgia Quick Start designation allows state officials to decline to release how much the state offered in incentives.
DAS Corp. designs and manufactures seating systems, structures, and safety seating components.
DAS Corp. is a supplier to Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia and Hyundai Motor Group.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated that logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners in all corners of the state and to markets across the world.
The company will primarily produce automotive seat structures at its planned Candler County Industrial Park facility.
Operations at the new facility should start toward the end of 2024.

Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated that the project's impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving the social economic wellbeing of its citizenry.
source_title: Automotive parts company to lead $35M Candler County 'investment' | Georgia | thecentersquare.com

entity_name: Continental Mount Vernon Tire Plant & Warehouse
facts: Continental is investing $76 million in a highly automated warehouse at its Mount Vernon, Illinois tire plant.

The investment highlights a broader shift where manufacturers are allocating more capital to distribution infrastructure to achieve faster delivery times, greater inventory visibility, and improved customer service.
The new facility is scheduled to begin operations in 2027.
The warehouse will store approximately 500,000 passenger vehicle tires.
It will serve as a key distribution hub for Continental’s North American tire business.
The project is being constructed adjacent to Continental’s largest U.S. tire manufacturing plant.
The primary goal of the investment is to strengthen logistics and fulfillment capabilities rather than increase production capacity.
The announcement underscores the growing importance of warehouse automation and network efficiency as customers increasingly expect rapid product availability and reliable delivery performance.
The project occurs amid a multi-year wave of distribution center investments across manufacturing and wholesale sectors.

Companies ranging from industrial suppliers and building products distributors to electrical, HVAC, and automotive firms are expanding warehouse networks and deploying automation technologies to improve inventory accuracy, reduce labor requirements, and accelerate order fulfillment.
Continental stated the Mount Vernon warehouse will be highly automated and designed to support growing North American demand while improving service levels.
The facility will provide additional storage capacity for finished goods and help move products more efficiently through the company’s distribution network.
Tansu Işık, chief executive officer of Continental Tires Americas, stated: “Our new highly automated finished-goods warehouse underscores our growth ambitions in North America.”
Manufacturers increasingly view logistics operations as a strategic asset rather than a back-office function due to elevated transportation costs and rising customer expectations for speed.
The project signals continued confidence in North American demand despite ongoing economic uncertainty.
Continental’s Mount Vernon plant produces approximately 11.4 million tires annually.
The plant employs more than 3,500 workers, making it the company’s largest tire manufacturing operation in the United States.

The warehouse project is expected to further integrate manufacturing and distribution activities at the site, reducing handling requirements and improving the flow of finished products from production lines to customers.

Advanced warehouse management systems, automated storage technologies, real-time inventory visibility, and faster fulfillment cycles are becoming standard expectations across industrial supply chains.
Investments in warehouse productivity, automation, and network efficiency are no longer optional initiatives but core components of long-term growth strategies for distributors.
source_title: Continental's $76 Million Warehouse Investment Signals Continued Push Toward Automated Distribution - Distribution Strategy Group

facts: Continental achieved its financial targets in the past fiscal year, taking the DAX company a step closer to its mid-term targets.
Geopolitical situation, additional inflation-related costs of around €1.4 billion, exchange-rate effects and high costs for special freight had a considerable impact on performance.
Continental presented its strategy for increasing value creation and achieving its mid-term targets at its Capital Market Day in December 2023.
For the current fiscal year, Continental is targeting consolidated sales of around €41.0 billion to €44.0 billion.
Continental is targeting an adjusted EBIT margin of around 6.0 to 7.0 percent for the current fiscal year.
Continental expects the margin to improve further in the Automotive group sector, thanks mainly to cost-cutting measures, price adjustments and efficiency improvements adopted last year.
The goal is also to achieve above-market growth.

Continental CEO Nikolai Setzer stated at the annual press conference in Hanover that the past fiscal year posed major challenges with geopolitical uncertainties, persistent strains on the supply of semiconductors, and inflation.
Nikolai Setzer added that Continental achieved its goals for 2023 and made improvements toward achieving its mid-term targets despite challenging conditions.
Nikolai Setzer thanked around 200,000 employees worldwide for making substantial progress.
Nikolai Setzer stated that in 2024, the company will tackle challenges head on and pursue goals with determination.
Adjusted operating result increased by 31.6 percent in 2023.
In the past fiscal year, Continental achieved consolidated sales of €41.4 billion (2022: €39.4 billion, +5.1 percent).
Its adjusted EBIT was €2.5 billion (2022: €1.9 billion, +31.6 percent), corresponding to an adjusted EBIT margin of 6.1 percent (2022: 4.9 percent).
All three group sectors contributed to the improved adjusted EBIT margin.
Net income increased to €1.2 billion in 2023 (2022: €67 million, +1,635 percent).
Adjusted free cash flow was €1.3 billion (2022: €200 million, +547 percent).
Continental CFO Katja Garcia Vila stated that an improvement in operating earnings and a reduction in inventories and receivables significantly increased adjusted free cash flow.
Katja Garcia Vila noted that the company slightly exceeded its cash flow target, speaking to financial strength.

Based on the updated distribution strategy of 20 to 40 percent of net income, the Continental Executive Board will propose a €0.70 increase in the dividend to €2.20 per share for the past fiscal year.
The distribution to shareholders is at around 38 percent, at the upper end of the defined range, and would amount to around €440 million in total.
In 2024, Continental expects the global production of passenger cars and light commercial vehicles to change by -1 to 1 percent.
Last year, global vehicle production increased by almost 10 percent compared with 2022 to more than 90 million vehicles.
For the global tire-replacement business, Continental expects sales volumes to develop by 0 to 3 percent.
Higher costs for wages and salaries, expected to amount to around €500 million, will weigh heavily on the earnings position in fiscal 2024.
Around half of the wage and salary cost increases are attributable to the Automotive group sector.
Continental anticipates consolidated sales for 2024 of around €41.0 billion to €44.0 billion and an adjusted EBIT margin of around 6.0 to 7.0 percent.
Continental expects the Automotive group sector to generate sales of around €20.0 billion to €22.0 billion and an adjusted EBIT margin of around 3.0 to 4.0 percent.
For the Tires group sector, Continental forecasts sales of around €14.0 billion to €15.0 billion and an adjusted EBIT margin of around 13.0 to 14.0 percent.
Continental anticipates sales in the ContiTech group sector of around €6.6 billion to €7.0 billion and an adjusted EBIT margin of around 6.5 to 7.5 percent.
Capital expenditure before financial investments is forecast to be around 6.0 to 7.0 percent of sales.
Adjusted free cash flow is expected to be around €0.7 billion to €1.1 billion.
Katja Garcia Vila stated that the company is targeting an increase in sales and adjusted EBIT margin for the Continental Group in the current year.
Despite high special expenses, Continental expects the corridor for adjusted free cash flow to be only slightly below that of the previous year.
Payments for the buyback of shares in ContiTech AG will impact free cash flow by around €500 million in 2024.
In 2009, the company sold 24.9 percent of its shares in ContiTech AG to Continental Pension Trust e. V. to strengthen liquidity during the financial market crisis.
The shares were bought back to restore the original ownership structure, meaning Continental AG now fully owns ContiTech AG, which operates under the name ContiTech Deutschland GmbH.
Continental anticipates additional special expenses of around €500 million related to the carve-out of Original Equipment Solutions (ContiTech) and User Experience (Automotive) business areas.
Additional special expenses also relate to measures already announced in the Automotive group sector to strengthen competitiveness.
In total, Continental expects special expenses of around €1 billion, which will impact adjusted free cash flow in fiscal 2024.
In the Automotive group sector, sales increased by 10.8 percent to €20.3 billion (2022: €18.3 billion).
Automotive significantly increased its adjusted EBIT margin compared with the previous year to 1.9 percent (2022: -0.3 percent).
The improvement in earnings was attributable to higher production efficiency thanks to a more stable material supply, strict cost discipline and a lower special freight volume.
Continental further increased order intake in the Automotive group sector, generating orders worth around €27.1 billion last year.
Year-on-year, the order volume increased by around 16 percent (2022: more than €23 billion).
The order from the exclusive partnership with Aurora contributed to the increased order intake.
Continental and Aurora will bring autonomous trucking systems to the mass market, starting with the USA by 2027.
Continental and Aurora recently defined the design and architecture of the future driverless system, reaching this development milestone less than a year after their partnership began.
The Tires group sector delivered stable profitability despite weak tire-replacement markets in Europe and North America, achieving sales of €14.0 billion (2022: €14.0 billion, -0.3 percent).
At 13.5 percent, the adjusted EBIT margin was higher than in the previous year (2022: 13.1 percent).
The margin improvement was mainly due to the continued high share of premium tires and a strong end-of-year business in Europe.
With the UltraContact NXT, Continental launched the most sustainable production tire currently on the market last year.
The UltraContact NXT has been available to tire dealers in Europe since July 2023.
The UltraContact NXT comprises up to 65 percent renewable and recycled materials.
It combines a high share of sustainable materials with maximum safety and performance, holding the highest possible rating (“A”) of the EU tire label in all performance categories.
Continental has established a strong position in the tire original equipment segment for electric vehicles.
The 10 highest-volume manufacturers of electric vehicles currently all place their trust in tires from Continental.
The ContiTech group sector performed positively despite a weak industrial environment, generating sales of €6.8 billion (2022: €6.6 billion, +3.7 percent).
ContiTech's adjusted EBIT margin was up significantly year-on-year at 6.7 percent (2022: 4.7 percent).
In May last year, ContiTech launched a strategic realignment aimed at enhancing impact and efficiency, improving customer and market proximity and further expanding its industrial business.
ContiTech strengthened its industrial business through company takeovers, fully acquiring the printing technology business of Trelleborg.
This acquisition expanded Continental’s range of surface solutions in printing technology while boosting business with industrial clients.
Continental also acquired the conveyor and maintenance company Vertech AB, a leading service provider for the mining industry in Sweden and Norway.

The Vertech AB acquisition marks an important next step toward ContiTech’s goal of offering industrial customers in Sweden and other northern European countries a comprehensive package of products, services and solutions.
At the end of 2023, Continental had around 200,000 employees (+1.9 percent compared with the end of 2022).
The number of software and IT specialists increased slightly to around 22,000 worldwide.
source_title: Continental increases earnings in 2023 and targets further improvement this year | Automotive World

facts: Continental is a major supplier of automotive parts to nearly every car producer in the world.
Continental is a leader in the autonomous driving intelligence space.
Its ADAS business unit began developing intelligent sensors and data-driven traffic safety solutions more than 20 years ago.
The ADAS business unit has been working to increase the speed of development using deep learning and training artificial neural networks.
Continental’s Vision Zero project aims to virtually eliminate deaths by traffic accident through the development of ADAS technologies.
Continental improved AI training time by 70% using IBM Storage Scale and NVIDIA DGX systems.
Continental has the ability to run at least 14x more deep learning experiments per month at the same time.
Continental’s ADAS Vision Zero initiative employs a test fleet equipped with sensors that drive 15,000 kilometers per day.
The test fleet generates and records over 100 TB of data daily.
Data is ingested, processed, selected, assessed, annotated, and used for training and validation of the system.
The team uses NVIDIA DGX systems for training and validation.
Continental needs high performance AI processing and access to data plus a powerful storage solution to analyze hundreds of thousands of images per second with NVIDIA GPU computing.
As automation of driver assistance and vehicle safety systems increased, software complexity grew, as did the number of safety requirements across multiple geographies.
Continental needed a powerful parallel file system to meet high-speed AI demands and protect sensitive data.
The solution needed to provide fast random access, support protocols such as SMB and Amazon S3, and provide several different access management options.
Robert Thiel is Head of Artificial Intelligence, Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), at Continental.
David Enenkel is Head of IT Operations, ADAS, at Continental.
Balazs Lorand, PhD, is Head of AI Competence Centre, ADAS@Budapest, at Continental.
Continental established comprehensive testing and evaluated top storage solutions with IBM Business Partner SVA System Vertrieb Alexander GmbH.
The IBM Storage for Data and AI with NVIDIA DGX solution provided required performance and advantages.
The parallel high performance architecture and simple-to-scale node deployment provided the AI infrastructure required, with resilience and scalability.
IBM Storage Scale solution ensures infrastructure will support growth in cloud or on premises.
IBM has extensive experience in the automotive industry.
With the new solution, Continental optimized for deep learning with multi-node training, increasing model accuracy for higher safety levels without impacting time to production.
Continental scaled to a cluster of DGX to handle 14 times more experiments per month.
The system can test millions of permutations in environmental conditions (rain, snow, sunlight, clouds) or transients (cars moving too close during lane change).
Continental can now run 20, 40, 80 GPUs simultaneously to speed up training.
Training time was reduced from weeks to days.
The solution supports growth in containerized hybrid cloud environments, on premises, and in multiple data centers.
Continental built a completely new infrastructure in the Frankfurt, Germany AI-ready data center at Equinix.
SVA implemented the overall integration of the storage solution in the cluster, including installation, deployment, configuration, commissioning, and training.
The new solution includes a multimode GPU cluster, non-blocking InfiniBand network infrastructure, IBM ESS with fast NVMe drives, NVIDIA DGX systems, and NVIDIA V100 Tensor Core GPUs.
Continental uses IBM Storage Scale software with its Kubernetes environment for modern application development.
To meet Euro NCAP five-star rating requirements, Continental needs to keep developing more intelligent products and establish data environments.
These improvements translate to a strong competitive position for Continental, enabling faster development of new, safer autonomous driving solutions.
Continental develops pioneering technologies and services for sustainable and connected mobility.
Founded in 1871, Continental offers safe, efficient, intelligent, and affordable solutions for vehicles, machines, traffic, and transportation.
The Autonomous Mobility and Safety business area develops and produces integrated active and passive driving safety technologies as well as products that support vehicle dynamics.

source_title: Continental Automotive AG | IBM

facts: Beginning in 2006, Kia made one of the biggest single foreign investments in the history of Georgia by establishing a state-of-the-art production facility in West Point.
Kia and its suppliers have helped create more than 14,000 jobs since the facility's establishment.
The facility has produced more than 4.6 million vehicles to date.
Kia Georgia has an annual production capacity of more than 350,000 vehicles.
The facility manufactures the Telluride SUV, Sorento SUV, Sportage SUV, and the all-electric EV9 and EV6 SUVs.
The advanced manufacturing facility represents a $3.2 billion investment.
The facility is located on a 2,200-acre site.
The facility operates 24 hours a day.
Kia Georgia’s goal is to be the best and most innovative automotive company in the world.
Kia Georgia is driven by the belief that customer satisfaction is rooted in quality.
Stuart Countess serves as the President and CEO of Kia Georgia, Inc.
President and CEO Stuart Countess stated: "We are extremely proud to call Georgia home, a place where Southern hospitality is alive and well."

Stuart Countess noted: "Our biggest responsibility and greatest honor is to continue to develop Kia Georgia’s production operations, maintain a focus on our people and to provide a clear vision for the future."
Kia Georgia team members strive to achieve ambitious production goals.
source_title: About Kia Georgia - Kia Georgia

facts: Continental Tires unveiled sensor-ready tires to simplify digital tire monitoring on June 24, 2026.

Continental Tire appointed Torben Tiersch as Director of Original Equipment, Truck Tires, Americas on January 29, 2026.
Continental Tire added a size extension to its Regional Drive and Steer Tires on January 7, 2026.
Continental’s Mount Vernon, Illinois plant, its largest U.S. tire facility, earned ISCC PLUS Sustainability Certification on December 9, 2025.

Dr. Jochen Etzel will retire as CEO of Continental Tire the Americas, LLC after a more than 30-year career with Continental, and Tansu Isik will assume the CEO role on May 1, 2025.
Continental and Samsara debuted a digital tire monitoring integration at ATA's Technology & Maintenance Council Meeting on March 10, 2025.

Continental announced the market launch of two new ContiConnect Lite and Pro versions for customized fleet management at the IAA Transportation 2024 in Hanover from September 17 to 22 on September 6, 2024.
Continental will place an EV compatible checkmark on some new truck and bus tires to indicate they meet special requirements of EV/hybrid vehicles as well as combustion engines on August 29, 2024.
BestDrive Tire, a commercial tire dealer and retreader focusing on Continental tire offerings, celebrated the grand opening of its new location in Charlotte, NC on August 22, 2024.

Continental Tire released a size extension of its Conti Hybrid HT3 in size 255/70R22.5 in load range H featuring a 16/32nds tread depth for better performance in high scrub situations on July 15, 2024.

General Tire officially announced a summer rebate promotion for RA 2 and RD 2 19.5-inch tires that kicks off on June 3 and lasts through August 31 on May 31, 2024.

In 2024, Continental’s U.S. tire plants hit anniversary milestones: 5 years in Clinton, MS, 10 years in Sumter, S.C., and 50 years in Mount Vernon, IL, operating with over 16 million annual tire production capacity and 6,700+ local employees while investing more than $1.5 billion USD in its American tire manufacturing operations.
Continental Tire opened a new Retread Solutions Development Center focusing on retread process improvements, innovation, virtual and augmented training on March 21, 2024.
Continental introduced a new digital tool to check truck tires on February 27, 2024.

Continental introduced a new valve cap sensor compatible with its entire range of digital tire monitoring products and services, including ContiConnect LIVE Truck and Trailer, ContiConnect Yard, and ContiPressureCheck on November 27, 2023.
Continental expanded its truck tire portfolio with the addition of new 19.5-inch rim size drive and steer tires specifically designed for Class 4 and 5 vehicles on November 2, 2023.
Bill Morgan Tire and Continental announced a partnership to open the first retread facility in Lexington, Kentucky in 40 years on August 4, 2023.

Continental introduced the all-new Conti Scandinavia HD3 regional drive axle tire designed to handle extreme weather conditions such as rain, mud, and snow, equipped with multiple sipe technologies and 3PMSF certification on April 3, 2023.

Continental Tires introduced new ContiConnect Live cloud-based solutions and next generation 2 tire sensors to assist fleets in real-time, intelligent digital tire monitoring of all vehicles in the fleet on February 26, 2023.

Continental Tires introduced its Generation 5 Regional Truck Tires for the original equipment and replacement market for fleets that want to optimize their total cost of ownership operation in regional applications, and added the Conti Scandinavia HD3 to its winter tire portfolio on February 26, 2023.

facts: A textile plant in Barnesville, about 60 miles south of Atlanta, is laying off 235 people and closing by the end of the year.

Continental Tire The Americas will lay off all its employees at Aldora Mills, its Lamar County facility.
The layoffs were announced via a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) filed with the state at the end of January.

Continental Tire The Americas is a subsidiary of Germany-based Continental AG.
