facts: Continental has announced the retirement of Dr. Jochen Etzel, Chief Executive Officer of Continental Tire the Americas, LLC, effective May 1, 2025.
Tansu Işık will succeed him in this role and will be the new Head of Continental’s Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas.
He will report directly to Christian Koetz, Executive Board Member of Continental and Head of Continental Tires.
The leadership transition announcement was issued from Hanover, Germany, and Fort Mill, S.C., USA on March 18, 2025.
Jochen Etzel has served as CEO of Continental Tire the Americas since 2011.
Christian Koetz stated that Jochen’s impact on Continental’s tire business has been immeasurable and thanked him for his leadership, dedication, and outstanding contributions throughout his tenure.

Tansu Işık brings more than 20 years of international experience in a wide variety of markets and functions within Continental’s tires business, including marketing, supply chain, strategy, and key account management.
He began his career as a Business Development Specialist in Istanbul, Türkiye.
Tansu Işık moved to Continental Tires’ Headquarters in Hanover, Germany, to become Head of Pricing for the region Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA).
He then moved to Shanghai, China, as Director and then Vice President for Key Account Management (OE) China and the Asia-Pacific region (APAC).
From 2017, Işık worked as Head of Marketing for the company’s replacement tire business in the EMEA region.
He previously led the Strategic Business Development and Global Marketing organization of Continental Tires.
Currently, Işık serves as the Head of Region South & Channels Fleet and OE Trailer for Continental replacement tire business in the EMEA region.
In this role, he oversees business operations in multiple countries, leads Continental’s truck tire business in EMEA, and is responsible for its retread and digital solutions business in the region.
Işık holds an Executive MBA from Koç University and trainings at institutions such as INSEAD, Duke University, and Ashridge University.
Continental was founded in 1871 and develops pioneering technologies and services for sustainable and connected mobility of people and their goods.
In 2024, Continental generated sales of €39.7 billion and currently employs around 190,000 people in 55 countries and markets.
The Tires group sector offers a premium portfolio encompassing car, truck, bus, two-wheel, and specialty tires as well as smart solutions and services for fleets and tire retailers.
In fiscal 2024, the Tires group sector generated preliminary sales of 13.9 billion euros.
Continental's tire division employs more than 57,000 people worldwide.
Continental's tire division has 20 production and 16 development sites globally.
Allison Zora serves as Head of Brand and Public Relations for Continental Tires – The Americas.
Continental Tires – The Americas is located at 1794 MacMillan Park Drive, Fort Mill, SC 29707.
ContiConnect Tire Monitoring Integration is seamlessly integrated with leading telematics provider Samsara to deliver actionable tire insights for every trailer in a fleet.
ContiConnect digital tire management provides valuable insights into truck or bus tires to allow for safer, cost-efficient fleet operations.
source_title: Continental Announces Leadership Transition in its Replacement Tires Business in the Americas | Continental tires

facts: Daechang Seat Corp. USA is an automotive supplier specializing in seat frames.
The company will locate at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center in Chatham County.
The facility will be situated within reach of the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center megasite.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the new facility.
The investment is expected to create over 500 jobs in Chatham County.
This marks the third supplier to establish operations in Chatham County.
No production start date or timetable was provided for the new facility.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the news highlights the broader impact of projects like the Hyundai Metaplant beyond their initial investment.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA was founded in 1979 in South Korea.
The company has grown to become one of the largest seat frame manufacturers globally.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA currently employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.

Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA) President and CEO Trip Tollison stated that Daechang Seat Corp. USA is a well-established global company and expressed excitement about welcoming them to the community.
A job fair will be scheduled in the future to recruit for the new positions.
Open positions at the facility include assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers.
The announcement comes weeks after Governor Kemp's office celebrated Georgia being ranked the No. 1 state to do business for 10 consecutive years.
Georgia has attracted more than $25 billion in investments and created thousands of jobs in EV manufacturing over the last five years.
Savannah has become a main player in the state's aggressive approach to EV manufacturing.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that countless partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry.
Pat Wilson highlighted that Chatham County has leveraged Savannah's status as an established global gateway to create a bustling community for residents and workers.
source_title: Hundreds of jobs are coming to Chatham County through auto supplier

facts: A new Korean supplier is set to invest $72.5 million in Savannah.
The facility will hire more than 500 workers.
The facility will make seat frames for cars with an automated process outfitted with robotic welding machines.
Daechang Seat Corp. will set up shop in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The location is near the Hyundai Meta Plant, where the South Korean auto maker is constructing its first dedicated electric vehicle factory in the United States.
Another two suppliers have already located in the industrial park.
Hyundai will soon mark one year since it broke ground on the 2,900-acre site in Bryan County.
Daechang works already with key suppliers Hyundai Mobis and Hyundai Transys.
Jinsuk Lee, CEO of the local operation, Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., stated the company hopes to work on "even more projects in the future."
Daechang is now one of the largest seat frame producers globally.
Daechang started in the city of Gyeongju in the Gyeongchang-buk province of Korea in 1979, where it still has factories.
It now employs more than 5,000 people across Korea and seven other countries, including China, India, Turkey, Poland, Indonesia, Czech Republic and Slovakia.
Along with Korean OEMs Kia and Hyundai, Daechang counts as customers global automakers like Toyota, Ford, Chrysler, Volvo and Volkswagen.
The corporate headquarters is in Dongtan, south of Seoul.
In 2022, the company had 1.4 trillion won in sales, about $1 billion.
In the U.S., the company known as DSC has a factory in Phenix City, Ala., just across the state line from Columbus, Ga.
The Phenix City facility was established in 2020.

It was a latecomer to a troop of Korean firms that set up shop in the western part of Georgia or eastern Alabama to serve the Hyundai and Kia plants in Montgomery, Ala., and West Point, Ga., respectively.
Daechang’s initial Phenix City investment was $9 million.
The company followed with a reported $40 million expansion in 2022.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp said suppliers for the Hyundai Meta Plant are present in nine counties.
The Hyundai Meta Plant represents a $5.5 billion investment along Interstate 16.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development was recently recognized by The Korea Society for its prowess in welcoming Korean firms, particularly those participating in the energy transition in the United States.

The Southeast U.S.-Korea Chamber of Commerce on Nov. 2 will host The Next Generation Workforce Development Forum in partnership with the Georgia Automotive Manufacturers Association, or GAMA, at the Kia plant in West Point.
source_title: New Korean Supplier to Land in Savannah, Investing $72M and Hiring 500 - Global Atlanta

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on October 23 that Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center in Chatham County, Georgia.
The investment will create over 500 jobs at the new facility located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
Daechang Seat Corp. USA specializes in automotive seat frames and serves as a supplier for HMGMA.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea, and has become one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company's production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
DSC recently ventured into the electric vehicle sector and continues to expand its scope.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project alone reach at least nine counties, adding to a growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Jinsuk Lee, CEO of Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, local communities, and the City of Savannah for their support.
DSC currently holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
The new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will hire for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers through local job fairs and website postings in the future.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, noted that the site is only minutes from the Port of Savannah, providing ideal connectivity for U.S. supply chains.

Trip Tollison, President and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority, highlighted that DSC is the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team, represented the department on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that countless partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry.
Since 2018, Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs as it pursues the entire electric vehicle supply chain.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in fiscal year 2023.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Automotive Supplier Daechang Seat Corp. USA to Create 500 Jobs in Chatham County - Automotive Industry Today - EIN Presswire

facts: Daesol Ausys, a South Korean auto supplier, plans to build a USD 72 million factory in Georgia to make parts for electric vehicles.
The company will hire more than 140 workers for the new facility.
Daesol Ausys announced its plans on Tuesday to build a factory in West Point, Georgia.
The plant will manufacture luggage boards and covers for compartments in electric vehicles.
The company supplies parts to Hyundai, Kia, and General Motors.
This will be the third factory in the same West Point industrial park for members of the company's corporate family.
Daehan Solution Co. opened a million factory in the same West Point industrial park in 2008 to make headliners and other sound insulation parts for the nearby Kia plant.
Daehan Solution Co. currently employs 350 full-time workers.
Daesol Material opened a USD 35 million factory in the same West Point industrial park in 2017 to make automotive fiber padding.
Daesol Material currently employs 50 workers.
These employment figures were reported by Meghan Richardson, an economic developer for the city of West Point.
Daehan Solution Co. also operates a plant near Montgomery, Alabama, which supplies parts to the Hyundai assembly plant there.

The announcement follows a wave of electric vehicle suppliers announcing plants in Georgia after Hyundai said in 2022 that it would build a USD 5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
The Hyundai site in Ellabell could grow to 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
EV suppliers have pledged to invest nearly USD 2.2 billion and hire 5,000 people for the Georgia projects.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that the state's automotive industry employs over 50,000 Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose locations across the state.
Daesol Ausys is scheduled to begin production in December 2024.
The company will pay workers USD 15 to USD 20 an hour.
The state of Georgia will pay to train workers for the facility.
The state gave USD 500,000 last year to help Harris County clear and grade the site and build a road, which the county matched using sales tax collections.
The city of West Point and Harris County are granting eight years of property tax breaks worth a projected USD 5.7 million.
Daesol Ausys could qualify for USD 875,000 in state income tax credits, calculated at USD 1,250 per job over five years.
To qualify for the USD 875,000 in state income tax credits, workers must make at least USD 31,300 a year.
source_title: Daesol Ausys Auto: SK auto supplier, Daesol Ausys plans USD 72 million plant in Georgia to build EV parts, ETAuto

facts: More than 140 new jobs will be created in the Columbus area from an automobile parts supplier opening a new manufacturing facility in Harris County.
The $72 million project is located in West Point, Georgia.

Daesol Ausys Georgia joins other automotive suppliers in the area, including Kia Motors (which opened its only U.S. manufacturing plant in 2010), Adient, Daehan Solutions, Daesol Materials, Kopla, and Hyundai Glovis.
Governor Brian Kemp stated in a news release that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right and expressed excitement about Daesol Ausys joining other innovators in Harris County.
Georgia's automotive industry currently employs over 50,000 Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose locations across the state.

Daesol Ausys is a South Korean company established in 2017 that designs and manufactures motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories for carmakers such as Kia, Hyundai, and General Motors.

CEO Min Ho Kwon stated the company is committed to delivering world-class services and is excited about the project at the Northwest Harris Business Park, noting a partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County.
The new facility is expected to open in December 2024.
At the new facility, Daesol Ausys will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
The company plans to hire personnel for roles in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.
Harris County Commission Chairman Robert Grant stated that Daesol Ausys' decision to locate in the Northwest Harris Business Park represents a commitment to Harris County.

West Point and Harris County have a strong relationship with Daesol Ausys' sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials, and officials have worked closely with company officials to bring this new venture to West Point.
Harris County Development Authority Chairman Craig Greenhaw welcomed Daesol Ausys as a new corporate citizen and noted the venture brings jobs and capital investment to the region.
source_title: $72 million Harris County auto parts plant project to create 140 jobs in Columbus area

facts: A South Korean automobile parts supplier announced Tuesday it will build a new manufacturing facility near Kia’s factory in West Point.
Daesol Ausys Georgia, a supplier for Kia, Hyundai Motor Group and General Motors, will build a $72 million factory in Harris County.
The new factory is located roughly 80 miles southwest of downtown Atlanta.
The plant will employ more than 140 workers.
The plant will manufacture luggage boards and covers for electric vehicles.
The manufactured components will be used for Kia’s new electric EV9 SUV.
Kia’s EV9 SUV is expected to roll off the West Point production lines next year.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated in a news release that the project continues the ring of growth around Kia’s auto plant.
The state aims to recreate this ring of growth with two other massive EV factories.
Gov. Brian Kemp said, “West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right.”

Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future.
Daesol Ausys was founded in 2017.
Daesol Ausys specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and EV interior components and accessories.
Daesol Ausys plans to open its new facility at the Northwest Harris Business Park off Ga. 103 in December 2024.
Kia expects to launch its three-row SUV called the EV9 next year.
Launching the EV9 requires a $200 million expansion to Kia’s existing factory.
Kia’s West Point plant opened nearly 15 years ago and became Georgia’s only vehicle manufacturing plant.
Hyundai Motor Group, Kia’s parent company, is building its $5.54 billion “Metaplant” near Savannah.
EV startup Rivian expects to begin construction soon on its $5 billion EV factory in southern Morgan and Walton counties.
Rivian’s new factory is located an hour east of Atlanta.
Georgia has emerged as a leader in EV production.
Since 2018, Georgia has attracted $25 billion in investment and 30,000 jobs in the EV industry.
This fiscal year, suppliers flocking to locate near the forthcoming Georgia factories represent $2 billion of that investment.
Daesol Ausys will likely qualify for job creation tax credits and worker training through Georgia Quick Start.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development spokesperson said negotiations involving discretionary incentives, such as grants, remain active.
source_title: Korean auto parts supplier to open factory near Kia’s West Point plant - Hefei Auto Parts Group

facts: In May 2022, Hyundai Motor Group announced it would open Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), a state-of-the-art U.S. smart manufacturing and battery facility in the Savannah region that would produce electric and hybrid vehicles.
The HMGMA project involves a $7.6 billion investment.
HMGMA will create 8,500 new jobs.
HMGMA has a production capacity of 300,000 vehicles with the ability to produce up to 500,000 annually.
Production at HMGMA began in October 2024.
HMGMA is currently producing the IONIQ9 and IONIQ5 models.
Hyundai Motor Group cited speed-to-market and workforce as key reasons for choosing the Savannah region for HMGMA.
HMGMA suppliers are locating in the Savannah region, including Tier 1 suppliers Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., USA, Seoyon E-HWA and PHA in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center is located 8.6 miles (13.8 km) from HMGMA.
Supplier sites at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center are still available.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced in early September that DAS Corp. (DAS), an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The facility will create 300 new jobs.
The facility will generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.

facts: DAS North America announced an advanced manufacturing facility in 2023.
The DAS North America facility held its ribbon-cutting ceremony in August 2024.
The DAS North America facility is expected to create hundreds of jobs and attract workers to the Metter area.
Candler County voters approved a Floating Local Option Sales Tax (FLOST) by 1,027 votes to 368 to fund infrastructure improvements such as roads and utilities.
The FLOST measure supports economic incentives through enhanced public works and aligns with local leadership priorities for fostering growth and diversity.
Metter's population reached an estimated 4,021 as of July 2024, showing modest recovery partly attributed to recent industrial developments.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The announcement was made in Atlanta, GA on September 6.
The new facility will create 300 new jobs.
The project will generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
The initiative will involve collaborative indirect investments.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.
Kemp noted that Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners across the state and global markets.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987.
DAS specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS, stated that the strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process.
Kim noted that Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub due to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations at the new facility are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will recruit for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.
Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated that the impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving social economic wellbeing.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as their new home and stated they will provide a boost to spur more economic development.

Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state by driving economic activity and jobs.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is well-positioned for Hyundai’s supplier network due to years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.
Wilson noted that DAS is another addition to Georgia’s automotive community and welcomed the company along with partners in Metter and Candler County.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DAS Corp. maintains a focus on innovation and quality to provide premier automotive seating solutions tailored to evolving customer needs.
Strategic collaborations with Hyundai Motor Group and Kia Motors continue to drive progress within the constantly evolving automotive industry for DAS.
DAS Corp. garnered total revenue of approximately $900 million USD during fiscal year 2022.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution are investing another $2 billion in the planned “Metaplant” west of Savannah.
The project is already the largest economic development project in Georgia history.
The two companies announced an expansion of the original $5.5 billion electric vehicles and battery cell manufacturing joint venture on Thursday.
The expansion brings the total investment to nearly $7.6 billion.
The expansion adds 400 jobs on top of the 8,100 originally planned for the next eight years.
Gov. Brian Kemp said the project will go a long way toward making Georgia the e-mobility capital of the U.S.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated: “These types of major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure, and more.”

José Muñoz, president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, said: “This incremental investment in Bryan County reflects our continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.”
When fully built out, the Metaplant will turn out 300,000 EVs per year.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant.
Hyundai Mobis will supply the battery packs to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for the production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
The facility is located west of Savannah in Bryan County.
The state Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team worked on the project in partnership with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority.
Partners included the Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and the Technical College System of Georgia’s Quick Start program.

facts: Georgia's automotive industry is experiencing significant growth as demand rises for electric vehicles (EVs) and automotive parts.
The state has a strategic focus on fostering a robust mobility ecosystem.
Automotive industry companies are establishing new manufacturing centers in Georgia to meet the increasing demand for EV cars and parts.
Doowon Climate Control America Inc. has invested millions in new facilities.
DAS Corp. has invested millions in new facilities.

facts: Auto parts manufacturer Doowon Climate Control America will build a $30 million factory in Metter, Georgia.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the project in a Feb. 6 state release.
The Metter facility will be the South Korea-based company’s first U.S. site.
The facility will manufacture climate control systems for gas-powered and electric vehicles.
The investment will create over 200 jobs, including injection molding technicians, assembly operators, quality, materials, and production specialists and engineers.
Operations at the Metter facility are expected to begin in 2026.
Doowon is the second Hyundai supplier to establish a facility in Metter to serve the automaker’s upcoming $5.5 billion EV manufacturing site in Ellabell, Georgia.
In September, car seat maker Das Corp. unveiled plans to establish a $35 million plant in Metter.
Das’ facility will produce automotive seat structures when operations begin in the second half of 2024.
Georgia has welcomed more than $2.5 billion in investment from a total of 15 manufacturers that will supply Hyundai’s plant in Ellabell.
The growing manufacturing network is creating over 6,000 jobs, according to Gov. Brian Kemp in November.
Hyundai is building a massive EV manufacturing campus in Georgia.
In September, Hyundai and LG Energy Solution invested an additional $2 billion in their joint battery cell factory, bringing the total investment to $6.3 billion.
The joint battery cell plant will be adjacent to Hyundai’s EV campus.
The push for domestic EV manufacturing has attracted a host of foreign auto suppliers to the U.S. to serve major automakers.
Ford’s upcoming EV manufacturing campus in Tennessee has attracted several auto parts manufacturers.
Canada-based Magna International plans to invest $790 million to build three facilities in Tennessee, two of which will be part of Ford’s BlueOval City supplier park.
Germany-based auto part supplier ZF Group is investing $500 million to expand its transmission facility in Gray Court, South Carolina, to serve customers like BMW, Tesla and Mercedes-Benz.

facts: The Japanese manufacturer of copper foil used in printed electronics will build its North American headquarters in Georgia, as announced by Gov. Brian Kemp.
Denkai America is making an initial investment of $150 million on a 115-acre site in Richmond County.
Ultimately, the company will spend $430 million on a headquarters and manufacturing plant.
The project will create 250 jobs over the next five years.
Denkai America’s products have traditionally been used in printed circuit board applications primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's copper foil products are now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening the state’s status as a leader in emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
Gov. Brian Kemp emphasized the state's focus on bringing quality jobs to highly trained Georgians and highlighted Georgia’s top-ranking pro-business environment.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s president and CEO, stated the company is thrilled to expand its manufacturing presence into Augusta due to the skilled workforce needed for growth.
Construction is expected to begin in September as part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
The company will be hiring skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.

The state Department of Economic Development worked with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and the Technical College System of Georgia’s Quick Start program to secure the project.

Georgia Commissioner of Economic Development Pat Wilson stated that as electric mobility adoption becomes widespread, Georgia is committed to ensuring Georgians in the automotive industry have access to future jobs at home.
Pat Wilson noted the investment demonstrates Georgia’s commitment to building lasting international relationships and driving economic partnerships across the state.
source_title: Japanese manufacturer to build North American headquarters in Augusta - The Christian Index

facts: Denkai America will move its North American headquarters and a new manufacturing facility to Augusta-Richmond County, Georgia.
Denkai America is the only North American manufacturer of high-quality electrodeposited copper foil used in printed electronics.
The initial investment will be $150 million to establish an ED copper foil production facility focused on electric vehicle battery foil.
The project aims for a total investment of $430 million across three phases.
The new facility will be located on a 115-acre site at the Augusta Corporate Park.
The move is expected to add 250 new jobs over the next five years.
Construction is expected to begin in September 2022 as part of the three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
Denkai America's materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board applications for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's copper foil is now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Operating for more than 60 years, Denkai has team members worldwide with operations in Japan and North America.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project, stating Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America.
Governor Kemp emphasized Georgia's focus on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained Georgians and its top-ranking pro-business environment.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO, stated the investment highlights their commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO, said the company is thrilled to expand its manufacturing presence into Augusta due to the skilled workforce needed for growth.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.
Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, welcomed Denkai America to the Augusta Corporate Park and highlighted the creation of high-skilled jobs.

Barton Lowrey, Global Commerce Director of Corporate Solutions and Cyber Security at the Georgia Department of Economic Development, represented the state on this competitive project in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
The Technical College System of Georgia’s Georgia Quick Start program has provided specialized workforce training tailored to business needs for more than 40 years.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Denkai America plays a vital role in the EV ecosystem and highlighted Georgia's commitment to harnessing electric mobility opportunities for automotive industry workers.

The investment serves as evidence of Georgia’s commitment to building lasting international relationships and driving economic partnerships across the state, including recruiting the wider electric mobility value chain.
Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate have made it attractive for industries focused on a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain to help companies increase efficiencies by reducing reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $12.6 billion in investments and more than 17,800 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
Augusta Corporate Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific requirements prior to a prospective business visit.
source_title: Copper Foil Manufacturer locates facility, North American Headquarters, to Augusta-Richmond County adding 250 new jobs | Sponsored | wfxg.com

facts: A Japanese company announced plans for a $150 million plant to make copper foil for electric vehicle batteries in Augusta, Georgia.
The plant location was shifted from a previously announced site near an existing Camden, South Carolina, facility.
Nippon Denkai's American subsidiary said it would hire more than 100 people initially.
The company could eventually triple production at the Georgia site from an initial 9,500 metric tons (10,500 tons) to 28,500 metric tons (31,500 tons).
Tripling production would raise investment to $430 million and create 250 jobs over the next five years.
Denkai America spokesperson Michael Coll stated: “Our goal is to triple the capacity in Augusta; market conditions will determine timeline.”
The company raised its initial investment by $10 million from what it had announced in December.

The 115-acre (47-hectare) Augusta site was chosen over South Carolina because there is room for expansion, it can host an “advanced” equipment layout, and it offers a “key cost competitive edge in electricity price.”
State and local officials will give the company what could be more than $76 million in tax breaks and incentives.
Nippon Denkai said it will start construction in September and hopes to be shipping samples to manufacturers by the summer of 2024.
The company sees a big opportunity to make copper foil for electric vehicle battery makers, noting many battery plants are being built in the United States but few copper foil plants are.
Nippon Denkai said companies will want domestic sources to avoid supply chain problems including risks of politically driven supply disruptions.
The company projects an annual 21.4% growth in copper foil demand through 2035.
The ability to triple production in Augusta will allow the company to pursue that projected growth.
Combined with the 30-year-old Camden plant, the company would have an American production capacity of more than 35,000 metric tons.
In June, Denkai announced a smaller $14 million expansion at the 80-worker Camden plant, also related to expansion in the electric battery and vehicle market.
Augusta-Richmond County will give property tax breaks valued at a projected $68.7 million over 25 years.
The company will pay $30 million in taxes under the deal, according to the Augusta Economic Development Authority.
The company gets free land valued at $2.25 million, said Cal Wray, the authority's president.
Georgia officials said they will spend an undisclosed amount of money to train workers.

Nippon Denkai could claim various state tax breaks, including an income tax credit allowing it to annually deduct $4,000 per job from state income taxes, up to $5 million over five years, as long as workers make at least $28,000 a year.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson pointed to the announcement as more fruit of the state's focus on what he calls a “generational opportunity” in electric vehicles.
Wilson cited the $5 billion Rivian Automotive plant east of Atlanta and the $5.5 billion Hyundai Motor Group plant near Savannah as part of this focus.
Wray said a $347 million, 125-job copper smelter announced by Aurubis AG in Augusta is another development driven by the growth in electric vehicles.
source_title: Japanese firm shifts copper foil plans from SC to Georgia | Taiwan News | Jul. 21, 2022 02:17

facts: Denkai America, a manufacturer of copper foil used in printed electronics, will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American headquarters in Richmond County.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the company's selection on Wednesday.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an “electrodeposited” copper foil production facility on a 115-acre site.
Denkai America aims to invest a total of $430 million and create 250 jobs over the next five years.
The plant will focus foil for electric vehicle batteries.
Denkai America’s materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board applications, primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's materials are also now used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Construction is expected to begin in September at the Augusta Corporate Park in south Augusta.
The Augusta facility will be the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
Wednesday’s announcement switches the location for the Nippon Denkai plant from one originally announced next to the company’s existing plant in Camden, S.C.
The company says it could eventually triple production at the Georgia site.
Denkai says the Augusta site has room for expansion, allows for more advanced equipment and has cheaper electricity prices.
Expanding battery manufacturing will drive a big increase in demand for copper foil.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.
Operating for more than 60 years, Denkai has team members worldwide with operations in Japan and North America.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated: “Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening our state’s status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.”

Steven Kendrick, chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, said the authority is excited to welcome Denkai America and its North American Headquarters to the Augusta Corporate Park and expects them to bring high-skilled jobs.
The Augusta Corporate Park has been a magnet for new industry in the past couple of years.
Metal recycler Aurubis is breaking ground on a plant at the park that will bring 125 jobs.
Plastic recycler PureCycle Technologies is planning a plant at the park that will create 80 to 100 jobs.
Georgia officials are close to finalizing a deal with automaker Hyundai to build a $5.5 billion electric car plant near Savannah.
An economic development agency representing four Savannah-area counties approved its portions of the Hyundai agreement, including an economic incentives package.
Details of the Hyundai deal won’t be released until state officials and Hyundai also sign it.
source_title: Copper foil maker to build Augusta plant with 250 jobs

facts: Nippon Denkai has decided to close and liquidate its copper foil manufacturing plant in Augusta, GA.
The facility is the last remaining copper foil factory in the United States.

Denkai America Inc., the US-based subsidiary of the Japanese parent company, will close just two years after the parent heralded the site as the future of foil production, with tens of millions of dollars in investments planned and hundreds of new jobs expected.

The company cited financial problems stemming from semiconductor shortages, a decrease in exports of batteries manufactured domestically, lower demand for smartphones, and other factors for the decision.
Continued losses coupled with loan payments coming due and dried-up sources of new capital have rendered the company insolvent.
In 2022, Denkai America announced the planned investment of some $150 million in the plant to make copper foil.
The company took combined losses in the 2023 and 2024 fiscal years of about $23 million tied to the plant.

For the fiscal year ending March 2025, decreased sales and a rapid increase of copper prices, plus an impairment loss related to the Augusta plant, led to first-half consolidated ordinary losses of approximately 1.5 billion yen ($9.9 million).
The same period resulted in a consolidated net loss of approximately 5 billion yen ($33 million).

Cash flow gradually deteriorated due to reconstruction of manufacturing facilities at the subsidiary's previous plant in Camden, SC, and investments in foil manufacturing facilities in the Augusta plant.
Attempts to find additional funding have stalled.
The company filed a petition for commencement of civil rehabilitation proceedings last month.
Under the supervision of the Tokyo District Court, Nippon Denkai will continue its business while seeking a buyer.
Sumitomo Mitsui Banking is providing up to 2 billion yen as DIP financing for the company’s future fund-raising.
Nippon Denkai is being delisted from the Tokyo Stock Exchange.
source_title: Printed Circuit Design & Fab Online Magazine - Nippon Denkai to Close Last Remaining US Copper Foil Plant

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced that Georgia sustained economic development momentum during the first half of Fiscal Year 2023 (July 1 through December 31, 2022).
The 218 locations or expansions supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce division are expected to create approximately 17,500 new jobs and more than $13 billion in investment for the state.
Both the number of supported locations and total investment amount increased over the same period last year.
Areas outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region accounted for 85 percent of jobs created and 92 percent of investments from economic development projects during the period.
Four of Georgia’s largest industries – advanced manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, and food processing – accounted for nearly 75 percent of new jobs and 84 percent of investments.
Planned investments resulting from these projects increased by more than 38 percent compared to the same time last year.
The automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment, after advanced manufacturing.

In October, state and local officials joined company representatives, including Korean Ambassador to the U.S. Tae-yong Cho, for the groundbreaking of the new Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) EV facility.
The HMGMA EV facility will support more than 8,100 direct jobs in Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham Counties.
Three off-site HMGMA suppliers announced locations following the groundbreaking, swiftly surpassing the initial $1 billion in investment predicted to come from such additions.
International companies accounted for 23 percent of state projects since July 1, 2022, acting as valued partners in creating a closed-loop electric mobility supply chain.
The partnership between SK On and Hyundai Motor Group in Bartow County is a historic project example in the top five largest projects by investment in the state’s history.
Next-generation developer FREYR Battery’s Coweta County facility is also among the top five largest projects by investment in the state’s history.
Battery recyclers Sung-Il Hitech and Denkai America joined Georgia’s growing electric mobility ecosystem.
The aerospace and defense industries created more than 1,500 new jobs by benefiting from Georgia’s logistics infrastructure and engineering talent pool.

Archer Aviation Inc., an aerospace company advancing sustainable urban air mobility, announced a facility near airports that will benefit from nearby top-ranked technical colleges and four-year universities.
Anduril Industries, a defense technology company, also announced a facility near airports with similar benefits from nearby educational institutions.
Advanced manufacturing is a cornerstone industry sector in Georgia’s middle and coastal regions, announcing more than 7,700 new jobs in the state since July 1, 2022.
Major advanced manufacturing investments or expansions announced in FY 2023 include YKK AP in Macon and Beretta Holding S.A.’s new facility in Bryan County through its subsidiary Norma Precision Inc.

Germany-based Haering Precision celebrated the grand opening of its Lavonia facility in November with a two-day event including a “Future Day” for approximately 400 local high school students and community members.
Companies in Georgia’s food processing industry announced more than $695 million in investments, largely through expansions, supported by the state’s agricultural strength and logistics capabilities.
King’s Hawaiian®, a Georgia partner for more than a decade, announced another expansion of its Oakwood facility, marking its second expansion in less than a year.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner for Global Commerce Kristi Brigman stated that companies choose to grow in Georgia because they are connected with community and state partners to solve workforce challenges.
Brigman noted that companies utilize the Georgia Quick Start program to build talent from the ground up, helping Georgians upskill amid strong inbound domestic migration trends.
Georgia Tech hosted a groundbreaking for its Science Square purpose-built district in August.
The Rowen Foundation’s 2,000-acre science and learning campus broke ground in early December.

Athena Studios announced it will add an additional 350,000 square feet to its Athens facility, including a structure dedicated to training the local workforce through a partnership with the University of Georgia and Georgia Film Academy.

BlueStar Studios announced more than 600,000 square feet of new stage space and an additional 45,000 square feet dedicated to technical resources and amenities at the former Fort Gillem outside of Atlanta.
source_title: Key Industries Sustain Momentum for State's Economic Development - Valdosta CEO

facts: Dinex Emission, Inc. posted a full-time Production Planner position for its team in Dublin, Georgia 31021.

The role requires planning production in accordance with customer orders and stock management policy, including creating, planning, releasing, closing, and cost accounting of production orders.

facts: Dana Incorporated announced a definitive agreement with Eaton Corporation plc to combine with Eaton's Mobility business in a transaction valued at approximately $5.1 billion.
The transaction is structured as a Reverse Morris Trust, resulting in Eaton shareholders owning at least 50.1% and Dana shareholders owning approximately 49.9% of the combined company at close.
Under the agreement terms, Eaton will receive a cash distribution of approximately $1.1 billion (subject to adjustments for cash and indebtedness), funded through new debt raised prior to closing.

The combined company is expected to achieve $250 million in annual run-rate synergies within 24 months following closing, driven by reduced structural costs, purchasing scale, manufacturing optimization, and engineering efficiencies.

On a fully synergized, pro forma 2026 estimated basis, the combined company will have approximately $11 billion in sales and approximately $1.7 billion adjusted EBITDA (approximately 15% adjusted EBITDA margin).

The transaction values Eaton Mobility at approximately $5.1 billion on a cash-free, debt-free basis, representing approximately 8.3x estimated 2026 pro forma adjusted EBITDA before synergies, or approximately 5.9x including run-rate synergies.
Dana's 2030 strategy targets are increased to $14–$15 billion in sales, approximately 18% adjusted EBITDA margin, and an 8%–9% adjusted free cash flow margin by 2030.
Prior to the combination, Dana's 2030 targets included approximately $10 billion in sales, 14% to 15% adjusted EBITDA margins, and a 6% adjusted free cash flow margin.

Following closing, the combined company is expected to maintain a strong balance sheet with net leverage of approximately 1.2x on a fully synergized pro forma 2026 estimated basis, and Dana is expected to maintain its current credit rating.

R. Bruce McDonald will serve as Executive Chairman of the combined company with responsibility for integration and synergy realization, and Byron Foster will serve as Chief Executive Officer, with both assuming their roles on July 1, 2026.
Timothy Kraus will continue as Chief Financial Officer, and Eaton's Erin Rowse, Senior Vice President Human Resources, Industrial, will serve as Chief Human Resources Officer at closing.
The combined company's Board of Directors will be comprised of all the members of Dana's Board of Directors and three Eaton designees.

The combination will integrate Dana's global powertrain, thermal, and sealing technologies with Eaton Mobility's commercial vehicle transmissions, engine and emissions products, and advanced electrification capabilities.

Byron Foster, Dana's incoming Chief Executive Officer, stated the transaction marks an important milestone in Dana's transformation and positions it as a leading, scaled provider of powertrain solutions.

Paulo Ruiz, Eaton Chief Executive Officer, stated the combination delivers significant value to Eaton and its shareholders, aligns with powerful megatrends, supports Eaton's 2030 growth strategy, and creates a leading global engineering solutions partner for commercial and light vehicle markets.
Timothy Kraus, Dana's Chief Financial Officer, stated the transaction meaningfully enhances long-term financial outlook and enables significantly increasing Dana 2030 targets.
The combined company will continue to operate under the Dana Incorporated name and retain its listing on the New York Stock Exchange.

The transaction is expected to be tax-free to Dana and Eaton shareholders for U.S. federal income tax purposes and is expected to close in the first quarter of 2027, subject to approval by Dana shareholders, receipt of regulatory approvals, and other customary closing conditions.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor, Kirkland & Ellis LLP is serving as legal counsel to Dana, and Ernst and Young LLP is serving as transaction advisor.
Goldman Sachs Bank USA is serving as sole underwriter of the committed financing for the transaction.
Dana will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. EDT on Thursday, June 11, to discuss the transaction.
Dana Incorporated is headquartered in Maumee, Ohio, USA, reported sales of $7.5 billion in 2025, and employs 27,000 people in 24 countries across six continents.
The company supplies leading vehicle manufacturers and related aftermarkets with industry-defining drive systems, electrodynamic technologies, and thermal and sealing solutions.

facts: Eaton Corporation's Van Wert, Ohio facility received certification to a new technical specification published by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO).
The Van Wert facility is among the first facilities in North America and the second at Eaton Corporation to be certified.
The certification covers the Manufacturing and Central Distribution Facility at Van Wert, Ohio.
The facility was certified to ISO/TS 16949:2002, an international standard that defines quality system requirements in the automotive supply chain when coupled with customer-specific requirements.
The certification also covers design functions at Eaton's Technical Center in Maumee, Ohio.
The certification covers applications engineering, customer service, marketing, strategic purchasing, and supply resource management at Eaton Hydraulics Inc. headquarters in Eden Prairie, Minnesota.
The Amtec Plant in Rivarolo, Italy is also certified to the standard within Eaton Corporation.
ISO/TS 16949:2002 was jointly developed by International Automotive Task Force members to ensure global consistency of quality processes.
The standard includes all requirements of ISO 9000 and combines AVSQ (Italian), EAQF (French), QS-9000 (North American), and VDA 6.1 (German) automotive requirements.
For customers that still honor QS-9000, the new standard will replace it as of December 14, 2006.
Employees at Eaton's Van Wert, Eden Prairie, and Maumee facilities began pursuing certification five months before the standard was officially released in April 2002.
The pursuit of certification was led by Quality Assurance Engineer Karen Adkins and a team of internal auditors.
Don Waggener serves as plant manager for Van Wert Manufacturing.
Don Waggener stated that success depends on pursuing certification to the toughest industry standards to drive process and performance improvements.
Don Waggener noted that Eaton is one of only a few companies in North America fulfilling ISO/TS 16949:2002 requirements at the time.
Don Waggener expressed pride in Karen Adkins, the internal audit team, and all Van Wert employees for reaching high performance levels.
Scott Etzler serves as plant manager for Van Wert Central Distribution Facility.
Scott Etzler stated that the certification will bring improved processes and continuous improvement to operations.
Scott Etzler noted that the certification will create additional customer opportunities as more customers adopt the automotive business model or require the certification in the future.
DNV Certification, an independent certifying body, confirmed that Van Wert's quality systems link all functions and comply with customer-specific requirements.

The certification process included a nine-day location audit where employees demonstrated results across eight clauses covering quality management system, resource management, product realization, and measurement, analysis, and improvement.
Eaton Corporation is a global $7.2 billion diversified industrial manufacturer.

Eaton is a leader in fluid power systems; electrical power quality, distribution, and control; automotive engine air management and fuel economy; and intelligent drive train systems for fuel economy and safety in trucks.
Eaton employs 51,000 people and sells products in more than 50 countries.
source_title:
Eaton Corporation's Van Wert, Ohio, Facility Receives
Certification to New Automotive Supply Chain Standard

facts: Eco Plastic, a company specializing in plastic automotive parts, is experiencing delayed profitability for its U.S. subsidiary established approximately four years ago.
The U.S. subsidiary has continued to incur financial deficits since its establishment.
Initial expectations for profitability starting last year were delayed due to external factors including an EV demand slowdown ("chasm") and adjustments to the prime contractor's production system.
ECOPLASTIC AMERICA reported sales of 191.7 billion won and a net loss of 28.2 billion won in the previous year.
Sales increased by 1,051.3% (11.5 times) to 16.6 billion won compared to the prior year, but the company continued to incur deficits with a net loss of 21.3 billion won in 2024.
The U.S. subsidiary, established in 2022, operates an EV parts (bumper) factory in Savannah, Georgia.
Eco Plastic was selected as a primary bumper supplier for Hyundai Motor Company's North American EV factory, leading to a joint expansion that included establishing a local production base.
The U.S. EV parts factory underwent reviews in late 2022, broke ground in June 2023, and started full-scale mass production in October 2024.
A client-tailored production system is in place, but capacity utilization rates have only shown a slow upward trend.
The Georgia plant's capacity utilization rate reached 55.6% last year.
In 2024, the factory's first year of production, the utilization rate was 10.8%.
The low initial utilization rate has hindered the subsidiary from achieving stable profitability.
At groundbreaking, Eco Plastic anticipated performance growth through long-term overseas supply contracts alongside its domestic business.
The facility was planned to supply parts to Metaplant America (HMGMA), a dedicated EV factory under construction by Hyundai Motor Group with an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
Projected revenue and operating profit for 2025 were set at 313.9 billion won and 6.3 billion won, respectively.
The 2025 projections were based on an annual production target of 140,000 units.
For 2030, the company projected revenue of 738.2 billion won and operating profit of 34.8 billion won, based on an annual production of 330,000 units.
Changes to the prime contractor's production system have disrupted the supplier's original plans.
HMGMA was originally planned as a dedicated EV factory but announced plans to introduce hybrid models alongside EVs upon its completion last year.
HMGMA began producing the Ioniq 5 in October 2024 and started mass production of the Ioniq 9 electric SUV in March 2025.
Starting in June, Kia models will be added to the lineup for a total of three EV types, with plans to mass-produce two hybrid models next year.
Changes to the production system were not reflected in EcoPlastic's 2023 annual profit and loss forecast for its U.S. subsidiary.
The timing for the U.S. subsidiary's profitability is expected to be repeatedly delayed until HMGMA's hybrid model production begins.
Sequential startup of production lines for three EV models is further affecting initial utilization rates for parts suppliers.
The slow performance improvement of the overseas subsidiary has increased the financial burden on the parent company.
Eco Plastic holds a 100% stake in its U.S. subsidiary and provides annual additional capital contributions.
Capital contributions totaled 6.7 billion won in 2022, 12.9 billion won in 2023, 13.4 billion won in 2024, and 34 billion won last year, reaching a cumulative total of 67 billion won.
The parent company provided loan support during the subsidiary's first year of mass production to help establish the overseas business.
Loans to the U.S. subsidiary totaled 102.9 billion won in 2024 and stood at 100.4 billion won as of last year.
The scale of debt guarantees provided by Eco Plastic for its U.S. subsidiary is significant.
As of last month, Eco Plastic's total outstanding debt guarantee balance was 274.7 billion won, representing 82.5% of its total capital (333 billion won) at the end of 2025.
The largest portion of these guarantees, 187.5 billion won, is allocated to the U.S. subsidiary.

An Eco Plastic official stated that HMGMA's fixed capacity and changing production system prevent suppliers from achieving sufficient utilization rates with only two EV models, but they expect the U.S. subsidiary to meet original performance and sales targets once hybrid models are mass-produced next year.
source_title: Eco-plastic When will the US production base be monetized?

entity_name: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in the United States
canonical_name: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in the United States
facts: Year-end foreign direct investment (FDI) numbers for 2022 show strong interest in expansion within the United States.
Top investing countries for FDI in the US include Japan, Germany, Canada, the United Kingdom, Ireland, and France.

International companies are attracted to invest in the United States due to overall positive business climates, availability of diverse workforce and higher education programs, reasonable regulatory environments and business-friendly tax structures, efficient freight options and solid transportation infrastructures, access to a strong and robust consumer market, and strong concentration of industry clusters.
Global events including the COVID-19 pandemic, Brexit, US tensions with China, and the Russia-Ukraine war created significant supply chain backlogs across several industries.
Many decision makers planning to invest in or expand in the United States are doing so to achieve better proximity to customers and overcome current supply chain and logistics issues.

Continued increases in FDI announcements could help communities struggling with workforce concerns by creating more opportunities in higher education, technical skills, workforce training, and certification programs as expanding companies provide details on labor needs requiring specialized skills.
Effective tools for attracting foreign direct investment include international trade missions, targeted conferences, and communications that highlight regional advantages and incentives.

Economic development organizations are advised to incorporate international prospecting strategies into their budgets, such as attending the SelectUSA Investment Summit, to better identify companies looking to invest in the United States.
Canada-based IKO Industries, a roofing products manufacturer, is establishing its first South Carolina operations in Chester County.
Finland-based Kempower, a manufacturer of EV charging equipment, announced it would open its first US facility this year in Durham County, NC.

Finland-based Nokian Tyres, a global manufacturer of vehicle tires, announced it will double the production capacity of its eastern Tennessee plant and established its first US plant in Dayton, TN, in 2019.
France-based Tageos, a manufacturer of high-tech RFID inlays and tags, announced it will establish its North American headquarters and first manufacturing site in the US in Fletcher, NC.
Japan-based SkyDrive Inc., an electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft manufacturer, has plans to enter the US market and establish its home base in South Carolina.

Portugal/Brazil-based MSS Steel Tubes, a manufacturer of steel tubing owned by Portuguese industrial company Metalogalva Group and Brazilian steelmaker Soufer Group, announced plans to establish its first US operations in Memphis, TN.
South Korea-based Sewon America, a subsidiary of Sewon Precision Industry Co., will establish a production facility in Rincon, GA.
South Korea-based Samkee Corp., a manufacturer of aluminum automotive components, plans to establish its first US operations in Tuskegee, AL.

South Korea-based Seoyon E-HWA, a global auto parts supplier that manufactures both interior and exterior components, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Chatham County, GA, which is its second facility in the state.

South Korea-based Ecoplastic Corporation, a company that manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and other suppliers, will invest in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County, GA.
source_title: Foreign Direct Investment in the US on the Rise

facts: Fouts Bros Inc. hosted its inaugural “Home Day” event at the company’s corporate headquarters in Milledgeville, Georgia, on Thursday, November 7th, 2024.

The event was attended by state and federal government officials, industry leaders, and local community members to highlight the company’s long-standing commitment to manufacturing life-saving equipment for fire service and emergency responders across North America.
Fouts Bros Inc. is a valued member of the Fire Apparatus Manufacturers’ Association (FAMA).

The Honorable Austin Scott, U.S. Congressman for District 8, delivered the keynote address recognizing Fouts Bros Inc.’s significant contributions to Georgia’s workforce and its ongoing dedication to keeping first responders equipped with necessary tools.
Attendees participated in guided tours of the Fouts Bros manufacturing facility to witness state-of-the-art fire apparatus, emergency vehicles, and specialized equipment being manufactured on-site.

Nearly 1,000 Georgia residents are employed by the company in the manufacturing and distribution of fire and emergency service equipment, underscoring its role as a major local employer and economic contributor.

Scott Edens, Fouts Bros Inc. President and CEO, stated that the event showcased the company’s progress over the past seven decades as a family-owned business and expressed pride in the dedicated team manufacturing safety equipment.

A key focus of Home Day was federal and state funding for fire departments, emergency service agencies, and first responder training facilities, with special attention given to the Assistance to Firefighters Grants (AFG) and SAFER Grants.

Fouts Bros Inc. leadership shared recent advocacy efforts during “Hill Day” in Washington, DC, where they worked alongside nearly 200 fire chiefs and industry peers to ensure continued funding for AFG and SAFER programs.

Gary Pacilio, Executive VP of Operations at Fouts Bros Inc. and current President of FAMA, noted that more than 70% of firefighters and first responders across the country are volunteers and advocated for congressional appropriation of critical grant funding to support their equipment and assets.
source_title: Fouts Bros Inc. Home Day - FAMA/FEMSA GAC

facts: Fouts Bros Inc. announced that it has been awarded contracts through the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA).
The contracts are to supply a total of 14 FB-94 fire apparatus to support U.S. military operations.
The contract allocation includes eight units designated for the U.S. Army and six units for the U.S. Navy.
Fouts Bros Inc. is located in Milledgeville, GA.
source_title: Fouts Bros Inc. Awarded U.S. Army and U.S. Navy Contracts - NewsBreak

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. designs, engineers, manufactures, and markets performance-defining products and systems in North America, Europe, Asia, and internationally.
The company operates through Powered Vehicles Group (PVG), Aftermarket Applications Group (AAG), and Specialty Sports Group (SSG) segments.

It offers powered vehicle products for off-road vehicles and trucks, side-by-sides, on-road vehicles with and without off-road capabilities, all-terrain vehicles, snowmobiles, specialty vehicles and applications, and motorcycles.
The company provides aftermarket truck suspension components, including two-inch bolt-on shocks and position sensitive internal bypass shocks.

It also offers lift kits and components with shock products, aftermarket accessory packages for trucks, suspension tuning services, wheels, off-road tires, and accessories, superchargers, interior accessories, tires, lighting, and body enhancements.
The company provides mid-end and high-end front fork and rear suspension products.

It offers mountain and gravel bike wheels; cranks, chainrings, pedals, bars, stems, and seat posts; and baseball and softball products comprising metal and wood bats, apparel and accessories, batting and fielding gloves, and bags and protective equipment.
Further, it provides aftermarket applications products, upfitting products, and mountain bikes, e-bikes, and gravel bikes.

The company sells its products to OEMs through dealers, distributors, and direct-to-consumer channels under brands including FOX, Marzocchi, Race Face, Easton Cycling, Marucci, BDS Suspension, Zone Offroad, JKS Manufacturing, RT Pro UTV, 4x4 Posi-Lok, Ridetech, Fox Factory Vehicles, Black Widow, Rocky Ridge, Outside Van, Custom Wheel House, Method Race Wheels, Badlander, Black Ops, Harley-Davidson, and Shelby American.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. was incorporated in 2007 and is based in Duluth, Georgia.
The company has 3,700 employees.
Michael Dennison serves as the Chief Executive Officer.
Dennis Charles Schemm is the Chief Financial Officer.
Brendan R. Enick is the Chief Accounting Officer and Treasurer.
Toby D. Merchant J.D. is the Chief Legal Officer, Chief Compliance Officer and Secretary.
Paul W. Stecher is the President of Powered Vehicles Group and Chief Information Officer.
Janet Wong is the Chief Human Resources Officer.
Christopher J. Tutton is the President of Specialty Sports Group.
The company's headquarters address is 2055 Sugarloaf Circle, Suite 300 Duluth, Georgia 30097 United States.
source_title: Fox Factory Holding (FOXF) Company Profile & Description

facts: Willett Engineering Company is a structural design firm that has been in business for over 30 years.

The company specializes in tilt-up concrete design, erection engineering for long-span structural steel framing systems, connection design for the steel fabrication industry, and preservation of historical structures.

WEC serves markets including manufacturing, distribution, K-12 education, retail & restaurant, multi-family residential, office, healthcare, theater & film, government & civic, and prototypical design.
The firm is located in the metro Atlanta area with a satellite office in Port St. Joe, Florida, and its engineers are licensed in 47 states and territories.
Over 95% of WEC's business comes from repeat clients.
WEC provided structural design for Fox Factory's 350,000-sf vertically integrated manufacturing and distribution facility in Gainesville, Georgia.
The Fox Factory facility design includes a large office mezzanine, anodizer room, clean rooms, truck canopy, and fuel storage area.

WEC conducted structural renovation and historic preservation on the 1928 Historic Ford Auditorium at Berry College in Rome, Georgia, creating 3D models of original roof trusses to install a new acoustic ceiling.
The Historic Ford Auditorium renovation won the Architecture MasterPrize 2020 award in the Restoration & Renovation Category.
WEC designed a new building for Midtown High School in Atlanta, Georgia, spanning 65,000 sf with a construction cost of $17 million.

WEC completed a new building design and structural renovation for the Barack & Michelle Obama Academy in Atlanta, Georgia, including a 10,000 sf heavy renovation, 3,000 sf new media addition, 8,050 sf new gymnasium addition, and 400 sf new entrance addition at a construction cost of $11 million.
Willett Engineering is currently hiring Structural Engineers at multiple experience levels in Tucker, Georgia (metro Atlanta area).
Job qualifications require a BS in Civil Engineering with an emphasis in Structural Engineering from an ABET-accredited program and proficiency with Revit, Enercalc, and RISA software.
Advanced level engineering positions prefer an SE license and extensive relevant design experience.
The company offers competitive salary based on experience, comprehensive benefits, flexible PTO, professional development opportunities, and a collaborative work environment.
source_title: Willett Engineering 2025 | Structural Engineers, Designers, and Project Managers

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. is headquartered in Duluth, GA.
The company reported a net loss of $544.6 million for fiscal 2025.
The net loss was driven by a $557.3 million non-cash goodwill impairment charge.
$295.2 million of the goodwill impairment charge occurred in the fourth quarter alone.
Adverse changes to U.S. tariff policies and a sustained decline in the company’s stock price triggered the goodwill write-down.
The non-deductible nature of the impairment negatively impacted the company's effective tax rate.
Full-year net sales increased by 5.3% to $1.467 billion, compared to a $6.6 million net income in the prior year.
CEO Mike Dennison cited persistent cyclical and tariff headwinds affecting the business.
The Specialty Sports Group (SSG) includes high-performance bicycle components and the Marucci baseball brand.
The bicycle industry faces ongoing tariff pressures and high inventory levels squeezing OEM partners.
Marucci's profitability was impacted by tariffs and strategic investments aimed at future growth.
The Powered Vehicles Group (PVG) saw nearly flat sales, with Marzocchi acquisition contributions offsetting a slowdown in demand for automotive original equipment (OE) products.
High interest rates and elevated vehicle costs have dampened consumer appetite and created inventory challenges for dealers in the powersports sector.
The Aftermarket Applications Group (AAG) grew sales by 12.5% in the fourth quarter, driven by strong demand for upfitting product lines and aftermarket suspension products.
Fox Factory is launching Phase 2 of its turnaround plan, targeting an additional $40 million in savings for fiscal 2026.
The company aims for a total realized savings of approximately $50 million for the year, building on $25 million saved from Phase 1 in 2025.
Cost-cutting initiatives target business line rationalization, supply chain and materials cost productivity, and operating expense reductions.
The Board of Directors is reviewing strategic alternatives for Marucci and other non-core assets to shed unprofitable business lines.
Fox Factory plans to divest its Phoenix, Arizona-based AAG operations, including the Upfit UTV, Geiser, and Shock Therapy businesses.
The company expects to complete the Phoenix operations sale by the end of the first quarter of 2026.
All proceeds from the asset sales will be dedicated to paying down $673.5 million in total debt.
Fox Factory's stock declined in after-hours trading following the announcement.
Q4 revenue of $361.1 million slightly beat analyst expectations, but adjusted earnings per share of $0.20 fell short of consensus estimates.
The company projects fiscal 2026 net sales between $1.328 billion and $1.416 billion, reflecting divestitures and a subdued market outlook.
Fox Factory expects a 200 basis point improvement in its adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal 2026.
source_title: Fox Factory's Reckoning: Huge Loss Triggers Aggressive Turnaround Plan - BriefGlance.com

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. is located in Duluth, Georgia.
The company designs, engineers, and manufactures shocks, suspension, and components for leading powered vehicle original equipment manufacturers.
It supplies premium products that deliver championship-level performance for specialty sports and on- and off-road vehicles.
The company acquires complementary businesses to integrate engineering and manufacturing expertise beyond its core shock and suspension segment.
Products are distributed in the aftermarket through a global network of retailers and distributors, as well as direct-to-consumer channels.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. will announce first quarter 2026 earnings results on Thursday, May 7, 2026, after the market close.
The company will host an executive management conference call to discuss Q1 2026 results at 4:30 p.m. ET on Thursday, May 7, 2026.

facts: FOX Factory decided to move its headquarters and plant from California to Georgia.
The new facility is FOX’s first “vertically integrated” factory.
The building layout allows products to move linearly from materials arriving on one side to finished products and shipping on the other.
The facility was designed to be energy efficient and make optimal use of the site topography.
Productivity increased by more than 25% within the first two months of the new facility’s operation.
The linear flow of materials and product through the building enhances predictability, planning, and worker safety.
The plant will support current and future local employees, with a capacity of up to 800 workers.
FOX Factory served as the client/owner for the project.
Carroll Daniel Construction Co. acted as the design-build firm.
BRPH Architects-Engineers, Inc. served as the architect.
Rochester & Associates, Inc. served as the engineer.
Specialty contractors for the project included Lawson HVAC, Simco Interiors, Simpson Grading, T&M Tilt Up, and Titan Electric – Georgia.
The construction duration for the new headquarters was 16 months.
The total project cost was $47,172,103.00.

facts: Fox Factory is based in Duluth, GA.
On October 19, 2022, Fox Factory announced a partnership with the Society of Women Engineers (SWE).
The company will attend the SWE National Conference in Houston, Texas, from October 20-22.
The partnership aims to recruit talented professionals for Fox’s summer internship program.
Fox Factory plans to interview and hire experienced engineers to help develop next-level performance products.

Jackie Martin, Fox Factory’s Chief Purpose and Inclusion Officer, stated that engineering remains a heavily male-dominated field and emphasized the importance of having role models, a good support system, and a diverse network to help women professionals develop and thrive in the STEM workforce.

As part of the partnership, Fox Factory is investing in women in engineering and other technical roles by offering SWE annual memberships to enhance their individual development and professional networks.
The goal of this investment is to provide more opportunities for mentoring, training, and professional growth to support career trajectories at Fox.
Kayleigh Hreha serves as Fox Factory’s Design Engineer and is the company's first female Design Engineer.
Cat Vachon holds the title of Inclusion, Diversity, and Engagement Manager at Fox Factory.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. designs, engineers, manufactures and markets performance-defining products and systems for customers worldwide.

The company's premium products and systems are used primarily on bicycles, side-by-side vehicles, on-road vehicles with and without off-road capabilities, off-road vehicles and trucks, all-terrain vehicles, snowmobiles, specialty vehicles and applications, motorcycles, and commercial trucks.

Freudenberg strategically expanded its business fields to include the energy economy alongside medical technology as a key success factor for earnings growth.
