facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies produces sealing products for the automotive market alongside aerospace, agriculture, appliance, chemical processing, industrial equipment, outdoor power, and recreation markets.
The company was founded in 1989 as a joint venture between NOK Corporation and Freudenberg & Co. KG.
Freudenberg-NOK is headquartered in Plymouth, Michigan, with offices and facilities throughout Brazil, Malaysia, Mexico, Canada, and the United States.
The parent Freudenberg Group was originally founded in 1849 as Heintze & Freudenberg, a tannery employing 50 people in Müllheimer Tal near Weinheim, Germany.
The firm employs over 6,200 people globally and provides career advancement opportunities through apprenticeships, entry-level professional positions, and experienced professional roles.

As an international enterprise, the company offers employees opportunities to enhance their skills and gain professional perspective through assignments in Germany and other countries outside the United States.
Freudenberg-NOK rewards employees with competitive wages and benefits that vary based on experience and field of endeavor.
The company prioritizes diversity as a core pillar of its vision, actively recruiting qualified candidates with unique backgrounds and skill sets.
The highest-paying position at Freudenberg-NOK is Sales Manager, offering an annual salary of $110,000.

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced a new Simmerring® Shaft Seal Ring for vehicles with electric drive on October 17, 2013.
The new seal features electrically conductive nonwoven material to help protect electric drive vehicle systems from damage.
The seal reliably seals shafts while safely draining off build-up of static electricity.
It was especially developed for vehicles with electric drives to allow axle drives to be sealed extremely reliably.
The seal drains off electrical charge that normally builds up on the drive shaft, conducting it safely to the housing.
This prevents static charge from traveling along the shaft into the transmission and damaging machine elements due to dielectric breakdown.
Traditionally, transmission input shaft seals are enhanced with a conductive nonwoven on the air side to create a conductive bridge between the shaft and the housing or grounding.

The new seal provides manufacturers of drive components in electric vehicles with a proven method to seal shafts while conducting away electrical charges without needing additional discharge measures.
Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies is the Americas joint venture between Freudenberg & Co. in Germany and NOK Corp. in Japan.

Freudenberg-NOK operates through Automotive, Merkel, Process Seals, and Simrit sales channels to produce advanced sealing technologies for markets including aerospace, agriculture, appliance, automotive, construction, diesel engine, energy, food and beverage, heavy industry, and pharmaceutical.
The company was founded in 1989 under the legal name Freudenberg-NOK General Partnership.
Freudenberg-NOK is headquartered in Plymouth, Mich., and operates more than 20 facilities across the Americas.
For nearly 90 years, the Freudenberg Simmerring® Shaft Seal has been a synonym for reliable shaft sealing.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies Announces New Simmerring® Shaft Seal Ring for Vehicles with Electric Drive - EDN

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies has been named the Rear Axle Supplier of the Year by Meritor Inc.
The award ceremony took place on Nov. 12 at Meritor’s headquarters in Troy, Michigan.
Executives cited Freudenberg-NOK as the “real seal deal” during the ceremony.
Freudenberg-NOK’s pinion seals and other seals are used by Meritor in its commercial vehicle business.
Freudenberg-NOK products can be found on the majority of commercial truck brands on the road today.
This is the second consecutive year that Freudenberg-NOK has been recognized for innovation and technology contributing to Meritor’s success.
Meritor is a leading global supplier of drivetrain, mobility, braking and aftermarket solutions for commercial vehicle and industrial markets.
Freudenberg-NOK was the only seal company Meritor named as one of its top suppliers in 2015.

Tim Guzman, senior sales director for Freudenberg-NOK’s Power Sector, said they are honored to receive Rear Axle Supplier of the Year recognition from Meritor, one of their largest general industry customers.

Guzman stated the award represents their steadfast dedication to technology, innovation, quality and customer service to ensure they are meeting all of Meritor’s business needs, calling it a collaborative relationship.
Meritor executives noted that Freudenberg-NOK consistently met demanding record volumes and competitiveness targets during 2015 while maintaining performance and quality levels of the seals supplied.
Freudenberg-NOK’s Northfield, New Hampshire, competence center switched from compression molding to state-of-the-art injection molding in manufacturing pinion seals.
The switch to injection molding improved quality, increased component volumes, and reduced engineered waste at the Northfield facility.
Meritor recognized a total of 18 companies at its awards ceremony.
Jon Rose, vice president, procurement for Meritor, said each of the 18 honored companies demonstrated a commitment to quality and excellence through the North America Supplier Partner Award.
Rose noted their performance contributes to Meritor’s strategic goal of meeting and exceeding customer expectations and strengthens mutual businesses.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Named Meritor Rear Axle Supplier Of Year

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced plans to expand its automotive sealing facility and hire new employees to meet increasing customer requirements for quality components.

Freudenberg-NOK’s Findlay, Ohio, plant produces approximately 700 high-performance plastic parts for customers including vehicle manufacturers and Tier One and Two suppliers using state-of-the art manufacturing processes.
Company executives officially announced plans to pursue a 43,000-foot factory expansion that could result in retaining 148 jobs and creating 25 new jobs at the plant.
Final plans are contingent upon formal approval of all State of Ohio workforce and incentive programs.
Freudenberg-NOK leaders have worked with JobsOhio, state and local officials on incentives to support the expansion.
The company expects to invest approximately $8.6 million in construction and equipment upgrades.
The expansion and construction will be complete by winter 2015.

Dr. Theodore Duclos, president of Freudenberg-NOK, stated that the Findlay facility is a benchmark for lean manufacturing and production of quality components, and escalating customer demand offers tremendous new opportunities for business expansion in Ohio.

Dr. Duclos expressed happiness about investing in the success of the facility and the greater Findlay community, anticipating the expansion will help better serve existing and new customers with quality sealing products.
Freudenberg-NOK and its Findlay operations are considered a top employer in Hancock County.
The plant was a finalist in the IndustryWeek 2014 Best Plants competition last year.
The IndustryWeek competition annually recognizes the best manufacturing plants in North America.
The Findlay plant maintains nearly perfect statistics in quality, delivery, and customer retention.
Findlay has become the company’s lead facility in the successful adoption of a new breakthrough manufacturing approach called Single Cavity Net Shape Molding.
The single cavity approach uses compact, custom-engineered one cavity injection and compression molding machines rather than multi-cavity machines to produce crucial thermoplastic parts.
This process improves part quality and reduces scrap and waste.
Roy Schroeder, Lead Center manager for Fluid Power Automotive in Findlay, said the project is a testament to the skills of their people and the success of their processes.
Schroeder noted the plant expansion will allow them to continue pursuing world-class manufacturing processes and products, expand markets, and look forward to completion and adding new employees.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Plans To Expand Facility In Findlay, Ohio

facts: Freudenberg-NOK and International Seal Co., Inc. announced an agreement on June 9, 1998, to merge their respective O-ring businesses.
The companies signed a letter of intent and expect to complete the agreement by July 1, 1998.
The new company will be conducted under the name of International Seal.
The new company will be headquartered in International Seal offices in Henderson, Nevada.
The new company will have manufacturing facilities in Santa Ana, California, Henderson, Nevada and LaGrange, Georgia.
The new company will have affiliated plants in Cuautla, Mexico and Sao Paulo, Brazil.
The new company will be an integral part of the global O-ring business of the $6 billion Freudenberg and NOK Group Companies.
The Freudenberg and NOK Group Companies have annual O-ring revenues in excess of $300 million.
The group has O-ring plants in the United States, Mexico, Europe, Japan, Southeast Asia and China.
The group has distribution capabilities for O-rings in 24 countries around the world.
The new company will represent over 20 percent of the group's global O-ring sales.
Joseph C. Day serves as CEO of Freudenberg-NOK.
Harry Railton serves as Chairman of International Seal Co.
Harry Railton will continue to serve as CEO of the new company.
Skip Marvick will continue to serve as president of the new and expanded International Seal.
International Seal is a leading supplier of industrial O-rings for the aerospace, semiconductor, chemical process and other related industries.
International Seal is the fastest growing O-ring company in America with special focus on unique materials and low-cost processes.
International Seal also has operations in Virginia, England, Canada, Mexico and Australia.
Freudenberg-NOK is headquartered in Plymouth, Michigan.
The Freudenberg and NOK Group Companies have total annual sales of more than $6 billion.
The supplier group operates through a global network of facilities spanning 24 countries with some 21,500 employees worldwide.

The group offers an extensive portfolio of precision-molded products for the aerospace, appliance, business machine, fluid power, marine, medical, off-highway equipment and recreational vehicle markets.
The Freudenberg and NOK Group also offers its automotive customers globally integrated products, including total sealing packages for engines, transmissions and brakes.
The group provides NVH components and modules to automotive customers.
The group supplies all rubber, plastic and PTFE components for suspension, steering, electrical and fuel systems to automotive customers.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK, International Seal Co. to Merge O-Ring Businesses

facts: On Monday, October 19th, 2020, the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced the five companies that earned the 2020 Georgia Emerging Automotive Recognition (GEAR) Awards.
Award presentations were held in the winners’ communities throughout the week in accordance with state executive orders and public health guidelines.

The GEAR Awards were previously known as the Georgia Automotive Awards and are updated for 2020 and beyond to recognize forward movement in the state’s automotive and mobility industries through innovation and emerging technologies.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the state’s automotive and mobility industries are major drivers of jobs and innovation, and congratulated the winners on their resilience and commitment to creating jobs and opportunities for Georgians.
Nominations for companies from across the state were reviewed and decided by a diverse committee of experts with deep understanding of the automotive and manufacturing industries.
Nifco KTW America won Supplier of the Year (employing 200+ people) and is located in Toccoa.
Sangsin Technology America won Supplier of the Year (employing 199 or fewer people) and is located in McDonough.
Kubota Manufacturing of America Corporation won Transportation Equipment Manufacturer of the Year and is located in Gainesville.
Panasonic Automotive Systems won the Automotive Innovation Award and is located in Peachtree City.
Freudenberg NOK Sealing Technologies won the Citizenship Award and is located in Cleveland.
Socially distanced award presentations were conducted by leaders from the GDEcD’s Global Commerce division and community economic development partners, including one completely virtual ceremony.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner of Global Commerce Scott McMurray noted that the winners’ dedication to employees and customers has been critical to strengthening the state’s diverse automotive and mobility ecosystem.
Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities, and the automotive industry contributes more than $3 billion to the state’s economy.

The state’s skilled workforce, strong logistics network, and central location among Southeastern Original Equipment Manufacturers continues to attract automotive and mobility suppliers from across the U.S. and internationally.
Nifco’s Georgia location in Toccoa currently employs more than 350 Georgians.
Nifco is a globally known Tier 1 supplier of automotive parts whose customers include BMW, Mercedes, Volkswagen, and others.
Sangsin Technology America supplies to companies like Vanguard, Great Dane, and others, and its McDonough manufacturing facility currently employs approximately 30 people in Georgia.
Kubota’s North American headquarters are located in Gainesville, Georgia, where approximately 3,000 Georgians help make top-of-the-line Georgia Made™ RTVs, Tractors, Lawn Tractors, Mowers, and more.
Kubota recently broke ground on its North American Research and Development Center, marking its fourth major expansion within the state.

Kubota Manufacturing of America President Hirohiko Arai stated that the company is honored to be recognized by the State of Georgia, noting that their strength comes from their products, processes, and people.
Panasonic’s Georgia facility is located in Peachtree City and employs roughly 1,500 people.

Panasonic Automotive President Scott Kirchner stated that developing innovative products for safer roadways and a transformed mobility experience is the company's core mission, relying on remarkable talent and culture as their most sustainable competitive advantage.
Freudenberg NOK Sealing Technologies has had operations in Georgia for more than 30 years, with its Cleveland facility employing roughly 1,200 Georgians.

Freudenberg’s dedicated ‘Culture Team’ serves the community through initiatives including engineers assisting with ‘Rocket Day’ at White County Middle School, donating funds to the school’s engineering program, and planting trees with the 4-H.
source_title: GDEcD Announces 2020 Georgia ‘GEAR Award’ Winners - Columbus CEO

facts: GEDIA has existed for over a century.
Since 1955, the company has produced for the automotive industry.
Today, GEDIA employs over 4,700 professionals across nine production plants globally.
The company is involved in joint ventures and research projects worldwide.
In the 1950s, GEDIA entered plastic handle production and began parallel production of pressing, stamping, and deep-drawing parts for automobiles.
In the late 1990s, production plants were established in Nowa Sól (Poland) and Sta. Margarida (Spain).
Production facilities were later added in Hungary, a second plant in Poland, and new plants in China and Mexico.
Engineering centers were established in France, the US, and Sweden.
In 2003, GEDIA sold its handle division to focus on developing lightweight technologies and automotive chassis components.
In 2016, the GEDIA Automotive group expanded with new headquarters in Michigan (USA) and Pune (India).
Construction of the "Askay II" production plant began in June 2018 and became operational in 2019.
In 2022, a new production plant in Georgia (USA) was integrated into GEDIA's production network.
The Georgia plant supplies body components to both German and American OEMs.
source_title: GEDIA. One Brand - Worldwide. Nuestra empresa.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp of Georgia visited the GEDIA Automotive Group in Attendorn, Germany, last Friday with a high-ranking delegation.
The visiting delegation was led by Commissioner Pat Wilson and included representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
The visit underscored the close and successful partnership between GEDIA and the U.S. state of Georgia.
GEDIA is a leading automotive supplier with approximately 4,700 employees worldwide.
GEDIA has operated a state-of-the-art thermoforming plant in Dalton, Georgia since 2021.
GEDIA employs approximately 700 people across North America.
Approximately 65 of GEDIA's North American employees work at the Dalton site.
GEDIA maintained close cooperation with local Georgia government officials even before construction began on its Dalton plant, which was a key factor in the site's smooth development.
The purpose of the Attendorn visit was to further strengthen relations and emphasize the importance of transatlantic cooperation.
The delegation received a comprehensive tour of GEDIA's Attendorn thermoforming plant, which is comparable to the Dalton site in many respects.
Plant employees conducted the tour and explained individual processes to the delegation.
Governor Kemp asked specific questions about the manufacturing processes and gained insight into practical workflows alongside the employees.
Construction of the Dalton plant began during the Covid-19 pandemic, facing uncertainties, material shortages, and travel restrictions.
GEDIA overcame a major cyber attack shortly before completing the Dalton plant construction.
Despite these challenges, GEDIA completed the Dalton plant on schedule without delays.
The successful completion illustrates the company's determination and professionalism in achieving excellence under adverse circumstances.
Helmut Hinkel, CEO of the GEDIA Group, stated: “The political environment is changing rapidly, and to remain successful, we need to orient ourselves well and strengthen the right networks.”
The visit concluded with an evening reception at Schnellenberg Castle where participants discussed the future development of GEDIA in the USA.
Governor Kemp stated: “The relationship between GEDIA and Georgia is a prime example of a successful international partnership.”
Helmut Hinkel stated: “We are proud of the trust and support we receive from the Georgia government. This is the basis for our success and future growth.”
GEDIA has the opportunity to triple production capacity in Dalton on its already developed site.
GEDIA expressed optimism about its future following discussions that highlighted Georgia's commitment and business-friendly environment.
Governor Kemp emphasized his openness and determination to attract new investors to Georgia and actively support companies.
GEDIA praised the comprehensive support received from local government throughout the entire investment and development phase.
The visit underscored Georgia's position as a preferred location for international companies due to its clear focus on economic development, growth, and innovation.
Both GEDIA and Governor Kemp expressed confidence in a long-term and successful cooperation.
source_title: Governor Kemp visits GEDIA in Attendorn: A sign of successful transatlantic cooperation

facts: GPS is a 220,000 sq. ft., state-of-the-art remanufacturing facility near Atlanta.
The company combines over 50 years of experience with lean manufacturing principals and the latest technology.
GPS produces high-quality remanufactured transmissions delivered on time at the best value.
GPS houses one of the largest inventories of remanufactured units in North America.
The company provides a one- to two-day turnaround for most units virtually anywhere in the U.S. and Canada.
GPS Transmissions aims to set the automotive aftermarket apart in transmission repair with better quality, faster turnaround, and affordable pricing.
source_title: About Us - GPS TRANSMISSIONS

facts: Mercedes-Benz plans to go fully electric by the end of the decade.
Mercedes-Benz will become an electric-only brand where market conditions allow by 2030, with all new vehicle platforms designed for the electric era.
At the end of June, Mercedes announced it was ready for the “rapid scaling of electric vehicle volumes” as it began preparing its production network for transitioning to luxury EVs.
Mercedes began delivering its flagship EQS sedan last year, followed up with the EQE and EQB.
With the EQS and EQE SUVs that debuted in October, drivers can choose a fully electric luxury vehicle in every category.
Following the successful launch of the EQ series, Mercedes is taking the next steps toward achieving its goal of an EV-only future.
For Mercedes-Benz to transition its entire portfolio to fully electric by 2030, it will also need to adapt its supply chain.
The automaker revealed new plans to retool its global production network for electric powertrains.
Jörg Burzer is a Member of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz Group AG.
Jörg Burzer stated that today the global powertrain production network is already supplying the Mercedes-EQ vehicle plants with state-of-the-art batteries and electrified axles.
With the new production setup, powertrain plants are now optimally positioned for the new vehicle architectures from 2024.
Jörg Burzer noted that sustainable, digital and flexible, highly qualified and motivated colleagues around the world are now preparing for the rapid ramp-up of electric drive systems.
The automaker will invest around 1 billion euros in realigning its production setup for electric powertrain systems, including batteries, drive units, and axles, from 2024.
Two German plants (Kamenz and Brühl) and the Mercedes Beijing plant in China will produce batteries for new EQ models based on the MMA and MB.EA platforms.
Romania and Beijing locations will start ramping up electric powertrains in 2024.
Hamburg and Mettingen will produce electric axles for Mercedes EQ models.
The new setup results in increased flexibility and efficiency at these facilities and paves the way for securing the plant’s future.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz realigning powertrain network for electric future

facts: The global powertrain machinery market size was approximately USD 129.04 billion in 2024.
The global powertrain machinery market is projected to reach around USD 223.38 billion by 2034.
The market is estimated to grow annually at a CAGR of around 7.10% over the forecast period (2025-2034).
Powertrain machinery encompasses all mechanical components and equipment involved in generating and delivering power in vehicles, including engines, transmissions, driveshafts, and axles.
The automotive industry is undergoing a transformation toward electrification, automation, and efficiency.
Advancements in robotics, CNC machining, and precision manufacturing are enhancing production efficiency and reducing operational costs for automotive manufacturers.
Market growth is projected to be driven by rising vehicle production in emerging economies, government incentives for clean energy transportation, and stringent global emission regulations.
Based on type, the CNC machining equipment segment is expected to lead the market.
The casting and forging machinery segment is expected to grow considerably.
Based on component, the engine machinery segment is the dominant segment.
The transmission machinery segment is projected to witness sizable revenue growth over the forecast period.
Based on application, the automotive industry segment is expected to lead the market compared to the electric vehicles segment.
Based on region, Asia Pacific is projected to dominate the global market during the estimated period, followed by Europe.
Growth drivers include increasing demand for passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and two-wheelers globally.
Growth in electric vehicles (EVs) and hybrid vehicles is boosting demand for specialized powertrain components.
Advanced manufacturing techniques, lightweight materials, and automation are improving efficiency.
Policies promoting fuel efficiency and emissions reduction drive innovation in powertrain machinery.
Maintenance, repair, and replacement of engines and transmission systems fuel aftermarket growth.
High capital investment is a market challenge, as manufacturing powertrain machinery requires significant financial investment.
Supply chain disruptions from raw material shortages and geopolitical tensions can affect production.
Traditional internal combustion engine (ICE) powertrain machinery demand may decline in the long term due to the shift to EVs.
Opportunities include expanding vehicle production and automotive infrastructure in Asia-Pacific and Latin America.
There is an increasing need for specialized machinery in EV and hybrid powertrains.
Adoption of AI, IoT, and Industry 4.0 solutions in powertrain production presents opportunities.
Development of aluminum, magnesium, and composites for efficient powertrain systems is a key opportunity.

Key players in the global market include Robert Bosch GmbH, Magna International Inc., Daimler AG (Mercedes-Benz Powertrain Division), Continental AG, ZF Friedrichshafen AG, AVL List GmbH, Fives Group, Mahle GmbH, Toshiba Machine Co., Ltd., and Nidec Corporation.
These companies focus on R&D, automation, precision engineering, and collaborations with automotive OEMs to maintain competitive advantage.
Key market trends include the shift from ICE to hybrid and EV powertrain systems.
Industry 4.0 adoption involves IoT, AI, and automation in powertrain production enhancing efficiency.
Lightweight materials usage includes aluminum, magnesium, and composites to improve fuel efficiency.
Growing requirement for repair and maintenance machinery for legacy vehicles drives aftermarket and refurbishment demand.
Sustainability initiatives focus on developing energy-efficient manufacturing processes to reduce carbon footprint.
By 2034, the market is expected to witness steady growth supported by the rise of EV and hybrid vehicles, increasing vehicle production, and adoption of advanced manufacturing technologies.
Manufacturers investing in electric powertrain solutions and smart manufacturing will benefit from long-term opportunities.
Companies focusing on innovation, automation, electrification, and sustainable manufacturing will emerge as leaders over the next decade.
North America has significant demand for advanced powertrain machinery due to EV and hybrid vehicle development.
Europe growth is driven by stringent emissions regulations, high adoption of EVs, and advanced manufacturing practices.
Latin America is experiencing gradual market expansion with increasing automotive assembly plants.
Middle East & Africa market growth is fueled by rising automotive demand and infrastructure development.
source_title: Powertrain Machinery Market Size to Grow from USD 129.04 Billion in 2024 to USD 223.38 Billion by 2034, at a 7.1% CAGR - EIN Presswire

entity_name: Global Powertrain Hydraulic Mounting System Market
facts: The global powertrain hydraulic mounting system market is valued at approximately $2.5 billion in 2024.
The market is projected to reach around $4.1 billion by 2034.
The forecast period from 2025 to 2034 is expected to exhibit a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.1%.

Market growth is primarily driven by increasing demand for lightweight and efficient automotive components as manufacturers transition to electric vehicles (EVs) and enhance fuel efficiency in internal combustion engines.
Advancements in materials science are enabling the development of more durable and effective hydraulic mounts.
Regulatory pressures aimed at reducing vehicle emissions are prompting automotive companies to invest in innovative powertrain solutions.
Challenges include fluctuating raw material prices and potential supply chain disruptions that could hinder growth.
The expansion of the automotive sector in emerging markets presents significant opportunities for industry players.
Corporate strategies focusing on sustainability and the adoption of automation technologies are expected to reshape competitive dynamics through 2034.
Active mounts use sensors and actuators to minimize vibrations and provide enhanced isolation, particularly for luxury vehicles requiring superior comfort levels.
Demand for active mounts is increasing as consumers favor advanced vehicle technology, accounting for a substantial market share of around 30%.
Passive mounts continue to dominate due to lower costs and sufficient performance for most applications, particularly in the commercial vehicle segment where cost-efficiency is a priority.
Passive mounts hold a share near 50% of the overall market, driven by high volume production needs.
Semi-active mounts are gaining traction as manufacturers look to improve vehicle dynamics without the complexity of active mounts.
The semi-active segment is estimated to capture about 15% of the market, driven by innovation in EV technology.
The automotive application segment contributes approximately 60% of the total market, influenced by increasing automotive production and a focus on reducing emissions and improving fuel efficiency.
The automotive segment's expansion is supported by the rise in electric and hybrid vehicles, which often utilize advanced powertrain technologies.

The non-automotive application segment holds around 40% of the market share, reflecting growth in sectors like industrial machinery and aerospace leveraging hydraulic mounting systems for performance optimization.
Rubber is the most widely used material due to its favorable properties such as durability and vibration absorption, holding over 50% market share across various vehicle types.
Elastomeric materials contribute about 20% of the market and are increasingly adopted for superior damping properties and greater longevity compared to rubber.
Metal mounts constitute around 10% of the market, primarily used in specialized applications due to their robustness and ability to withstand heavy-duty demands.

The transportation end-user industry segment dominates the market at nearly 70%, owing to wide-ranging applications in cars, trucks, and public transport systems impacted by global vehicle production surges.
The construction segment utilizes hydraulic mounting systems for heavy machinery, contributing roughly 15% to the overall market with promising growth prospects from infrastructure projects.
The aerospace segment is niche but crucial, contributing about 5% of the market, with increasing demand for lightweight and efficient components in aircraft design presenting opportunities.
Light vehicles comprise approximately 65% of the market share, driven by high consumer demand for passenger cars incorporating advanced technologies and vibration control solutions.
Heavy vehicles account for around 25% of the market, largely due to the need for durability and strength in commercial applications.
Electric vehicles are experiencing rapid growth and are projected to capture around 10% of the market share as manufacturers embrace new technologies and transition toward EVs.

The Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) sales channel captures the largest portion of the market at around 70%, due to partnerships between manufacturers and automotive suppliers for integrated systems.
The aftermarket sales channel holds approximately 30%, vital for maintaining existing vehicles and reflecting trends toward enhanced durability and performance.

North America currently dominates the market, contributing about 36% of total revenue in 2024, driven by a robust automotive industry, high vehicle production rates, advanced technology adoption, established manufacturing base, high R&D investment, and regulatory support for emissions reduction.

Asia-Pacific holds an estimated market share of 30% with a projected CAGR of 7.5% until 2034, fueled by rapid urbanization, increasing disposable incomes, rising automotive sales in China and India, and ongoing infrastructure development.
Europe accounts for around 28% of the market share with a CAGR of 6% through 2034, influenced by stringent environmental regulations promoting cleaner technologies and sustainability investments.
Latin America is projected to expand at a CAGR of 8.2%, driven by increasing automotive manufacturing and expanding middle-class populations seeking personal vehicles.

The Middle East shows promise with a CAGR of around 7%, fueled by infrastructure investment and enhanced logistics capabilities, though challenges include political instability and fluctuating oil prices.

Continental AG unveiled its latest range of powertrain hydraulic mounting systems in October 2023, incorporating advanced dampening technologies to improve noise and vibration control for quieter, more comfortable driving experiences.

Trelleborg AB entered a strategic partnership with an electric vehicle start-up in August 2023 to co-develop innovative hydraulic mounting solutions specifically for EV powertrains, highlighting a commitment to sustainable transport.

ZF Friedrichshafen AG announced the acquisition of a specialized technology firm focused on innovative dampening technologies for hydraulic systems in September 2023, positioning itself to enhance its product portfolio and streamline R&D processes.

LORD Corporation announced the expansion of its manufacturing facilities in India in July 2023 to increase production capacity of hydraulic mounting solutions, catering to rising demand in Asia and potentially increasing regional competitive pressure.

Vibracoustic GmbH launched a new simulation tool in June 2023 aimed at enhancing the design and efficiency of hydraulic mounts, allowing engineers to predict real-world performance and accelerate design cycles.

The transition towards electric vehicles necessitates the redesign of powertrains, which experiences an increasing reliance on hydraulic mounting systems to ensure performance stability and durability.
Innovations in composite materials combining strength with reduced weight represent a key R&D investment hotspot for developing lightweight components that enhance vehicle efficiency.

Changing consumer behavior towards sustainability is amplifying demand for eco-friendly automotive solutions, prompting manufacturers to scrutinize supply chains and production processes to adhere to sustainable practices.

The growing adoption of automation technologies, including artificial intelligence and machine learning within manufacturing processes, is expected to enhance production efficiency, reduce costs, and optimize resource allocation and maintenance schedules.
Integration of smart technologies into hydraulic systems, such as real-time performance monitoring, represents an emerging trend poised to redefine powertrain capabilities over the next decade.
source_title: Global Powertrain Hydraulic Mounting System Market Analysis

facts: Hyundai Glovis is ramping up its logistics presence across the US by opening major new facilities on both the East and West Coasts as it seeks to bolster its supply chain competitiveness amid changing market conditions.
The logistics arm of Hyundai Motor Group established a new logistics center in Los Angeles and an integrated warehouse in Savannah, Georgia, earlier this year.
The expansion is aimed at proactively responding to the increased local production in the country.

The LA logistics center spans 12,000 square meters — roughly the size of two soccer fields — and is located in downtown LA and about 25 minutes by car from both the Port of Long Beach and Los Angeles International Airport.
The proximity of the LA facility is optimized for multimodal transportation operations linking sea and air freight.
Hyundai Glovis plans to use the LA facility as a West Coast hub to consolidate various logistics functions, including transloading, air freight, warehouse and distribution services.
The new LA center will strengthen its ability to respond quickly to urgent shipments and manage costs more flexibly amid changing market conditions.

On the East Coast, the company established an integrated warehouse in Savannah to accommodate rising cargo volumes tied to the opening of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the carmaker’s new EV production facility in Georgia.
The Savannah warehouse will also support cargo from other customers in the surrounding region.

The Savannah integrated warehouse spans 69,000 square meters — approximately the size of 10 soccer fields — and is expected to give the company a strategic base for further expansion across North America.

A Hyundai Glovis official stated: “By continuing to expand logistics infrastructure at key hubs on the US East and West coasts, we are proactively responding to the rapidly changing global supply chain environment.”
The company added: “Through logistics competitiveness based on local operational bases, we will flexibly respond to the diverse needs of global clients.”
source_title: Hyundai Glovis steps up US logistics network with new hubs in LA, Savannah - THE INVESTOR

facts: Haering Precision USA LP is located in Lavonia, Georgia.
Haering Precision USA LP welcomed Georgia’s Consular Corps for an exclusive visit on March 20.
The visit was hosted in partnership with the Hart County Industrial Building Authority (IBA).
The event provided consular representatives an opportunity to explore the region’s manufacturing strengths, workforce development initiatives, and pro-business environment.
The visit reinforced Hart County’s position as a hub for international investment.

The delegation consisted of diplomats representing various countries who engaged in discussions with Haering Precision's leadership about the company’s state-of-the-art manufacturing capabilities, commitment to innovation, and role in the local economy.
Guests toured Haering’s facility to gain firsthand insight into its high-precision production processes and advanced technology.
Terri Partain, executive director of the Hart County IBA, participated in the visit as a key leader from Hart County.
Amanda Brown, president and chief executive officer (CEO) of the Hart County Chamber of Commerce, also participated.
Steve Burton, CEO of the Hart County College and Career Academy, was present at the event.
Executives from Hart County and the City of Hartwell provided an overview of the county’s economic landscape during the visit.
Local leaders highlighted strong collaboration between local government, industry, and education to support workforce development and attract new business.

Dustin Chambers, commercial plant manager at Haering Precision USA LP, stated that hosting Georgia’s Consular Corps reflects the company’s commitment to innovation and workforce development as well as the strength of Hart County’s industrial sector and opportunities for international partnerships.
The Hart County IBA participated in discussions on expanding industrial opportunities, attracting investment, and enhancing infrastructure.
Workforce development was a focal point of the visit, with Haering Precision’s apprenticeship and training programs serving as a model for industry-education collaboration.
Partnerships with institutions like the Hart County College and Career Academy ensure a pipeline of skilled workers to strengthen the region’s appeal to international businesses.
Haering Precision USA remains a leader in manufacturing excellence and workforce development, reinforcing Hart County’s position in Georgia’s industrial landscape.
source_title: Georgia’s Consular Corps visits Haering Precision USA LP | Hartwell Sun, Hartwell, Georgia

facts: Haering Precision USA LP is a leading manufacturer of high-quality automotive components.

The company took part in National Manufacturing Week by hosting and participating in key events that emphasize the value of manufacturing and its connection to education, innovation, and community engagement.
As part of its commitment to inspiring the next generation of manufacturing professionals, Haering Precision actively welcomes students for exclusive plant tours.

During plant tours, students explore the company’s cutting-edge production processes, interact with skilled professionals, and see firsthand the technology behind the manufacturing of automotive components.

The visits aim to expose students to the vast possibilities of a career in manufacturing and highlight the role of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) in shaping the industry’s future.
Dustin Chambers, Commercial Plant Manager, stated that engaging with students at this stage is critical to building a strong pipeline of future talent.

Haering Precision participated in Camp STEMPowerment, an empowering program hosted by North Georgia Technical College specifically designed for middle school girls to explore STEM fields through interactive workshops and real-world applications.
The company’s involvement in Camp STEMPowerment underscores its dedication to fostering diversity and inclusion in STEM-related careers.
Nathan Lindler, Technical Plant Manager at Haering Precision, stated that seeing students dive into STEM with enthusiasm is a reminder of why the company invests in education outreach.
Haering Precision participated in Career Fairs at North Georgia Technical College’s Clarkesville and Currahee campuses to reinforce its leadership in industry collaboration and workforce development.
The company is dedicated to continued involvement in educational and industry events that promote STEM education, workforce development, and community engagement.
Haering Precision looks forward to further strengthening its ties with local schools, technical colleges, and industry leaders in the years to come.
source_title: Haering Precision Celebrates National Manufacturing Week with Community Engagement and Industry Leadership - WNEG

facts: Hexpol Group has acquired an 80 per cent stake in nylon compounder Piedmont Resin Supply LLC.
The acquisition deal is valued at US$86 million.
Piedmont is headquartered in Cartersville, Ga.
Piedmont was founded in 2013.
Piedmont has grown to approximately 60 employees.
Piedmont services a variety of customers within automotive, transportation, and the furniture industries.
Piedmont is said to be one of the largest independent nylon compounders in the U.S.

Jan Wikström, president of Hexpol Thermoplastic Compounding, stated in an Oct. 31 news release that “the acquisition of Piedmont adds new capabilities, application knowhow and a new customer base to Hexpol Thermoplastic Compounding in the U.S.”
Hexpol Group is headquartered in Malmo, Sweden.
Hexpol Group is said to be the world’s largest rubber compounder.
Hexpol is organized into two business areas: Hexpol Compounding and Hexpol Engineered Products.
source_title: Hexpol Acquires Majority Stake In Nylon Compounder Piedmont - Canadian Plastics

facts: HEXPOL Compounding, the rubber compounding arm of the HEXPOL Group, announced an investment in a devulcanization line in Europe.
The investment enables in-house mechanical devulcanization of rubber to support the transition to a circular economy.
For years, HEXPOL Compounding has worked on utilizing cured compound scrap and waste generated as unavoidable side products of rubber part production.
Previously, the converter paid for disposal of rubber waste that often ended in landfills or was incinerated, or was ground and reused mainly as a filler in low-quality applications.
Devulcanization transforms cured rubber parts back into an uncured state while retaining the original polymer, carbon black or other fillers, and plasticizers.
The process creates a raw material base for producing new rubber parts, demonstrating that rubber can be recycled.
Carsten Rüter serves as President of HEXPOL Rubber Compounding Europe & Asia.
HEXPOL's target is to reprocess cured rubber from post-industrial sources and reuse it for typical applications in the automotive or building and construction industries.
The company supports customers in transforming production offcuts into new raw material with a reduced product carbon footprint instead of charging for disposal.
The devulcanization process adds no additional chemicals, relying solely on mechanical forces like shear, compression, and temperature via a specific extruder to break down crosslinking bonds.
The investment will enable HEXPOL Compounding in Europe to produce several thousand tons of compounds with devulcanized rubber content per year.
Ralph Wolkener serves as President of HEXPOL Compounding Europe & Asia.
HEXPOL already offers a variety of compound solutions with recycled content and will soon add rubber recycling to its services.
Customers act as business partners in the process by collecting and sorting production side products until HEXPOL transforms them into devulcanized raw materials.
HEXPOL believes the devulcanization process could potentially be extended to post-consumer rubber recycling.
The new extruder will be installed at the HEXPOL Compounding site in the Czech Republic by year-end 2024.
The Czech facility will provide devulcanization services to other European HEXPOL Compounding sites to ensure a broad customer base can participate.
Norbert Niemand serves as Managing Director at HEXPOL Lesina.
Adjusting compound recipes based on devulcanized rubber and other renewable raw materials to achieve performance matching standard compounds is part of HEXPOL's service.
source_title: HEXPOL Compounding invest in devulcanization - Cable Technology News

facts: HEXPOL Compounding Americas (HCA), under the leadership of President and CEO Gary Moore, announced key changes to its Executive Leadership Team.
Bruce Wynd was promoted from Director of Automotive to Chief Commercial Officer.
Wynd will be supported by four Commercial Directors responsible for the Automotive, Specialty, Industrial, and Silicone/Performance Additives markets.
Saul Reyes will replace Wynd as the Director of Automotive.
Chris Airhart will continue as the Director of Industrial.
Chris Poirier will continue to direct the Silicone and Additives business.
Newly hired Brad Donat will serve as the Director of Specialty.

Gary Moore stated that Bruce Wynd is a terrific leader with depth of experience in technology, supply chain, and commercial management, and has unparalleled industry knowledge at HEXPOL Compounding Americas.
Brad Donat has over 30 years of industry experience and has served in various leadership roles within the Chemical Marketplace.
Brad Donat most recently served as Senior Account Manager, Carbon Black, for Cabot Corporation based in Boston, MA.
Bruce Wynd stated that Brad Donat's background of commercial success, market knowledge, and experience will benefit HEXPOL’s customers and drive growth in the Specialty marketplace.
Current Purchasing and Supply Chain Director Ellen Clunk was promoted to Chief Procurement Officer to manage all aspects of the HCA supply chain.
Ellen Clunk has been with the organization for over 13 years, starting as a development chemist before shifting into various purchasing roles within the company.
Andrew Chan was promoted from Director of Operations of the South and West regions to the newly formed position of Chief Operations Officer.

Chan will be supported by two Operations Directors: Ernesto Gutierrez overseeing HCA’s three Mexico sites, and newly hired Kevin Brown overseeing HCA’s five Northern sites along with its two West Coast sites.
Chan will also lead the Business Systems, Continuous Improvement, and Engineering/Maintenance teams.
Kevin Brown joins HCA from Morgan Truck Body, where he served as Sr. Regional Director of Operations.
Kevin Brown has over 25 years of industry experience including leadership positions in Plant Management, Quality Control, and Operations.

Andrew Chan stated that Kevin Brown’s exceptional leadership and organizational skills will benefit the Operations Team and noted he is a hands-on manufacturing professional who understands continuous improvement, culture development, and problem-solving.
Gary Moore noted that these organizational changes will allow HEXPOL Compounding Americas to have clear visibility on the current and future needs of its customers.
source_title: HEXPOL Compounding Americas Announces Leadership Changes - Modern Plastics India

facts: HEXPOL Group develops advanced compounds designed for industrial applications facing high regulatory demands, 24/7 production, and increasing productivity goals.
The company's compounds provide thermal stability, chemical and oil resistance, as well as resistance to weathering, oxygen, and ozone to ensure long service life.
HEXPOL can develop compounds with efficient shock absorption, insulation, or containment properties to meet specific process or application requirements.
The company provides engineering expertise and thorough material and application know-how to help customers improve the sustainability and longevity of their applications.
Customers can contact HEXPOL's skilled staff representatives for detailed information on how the compounds can make a material difference in their business.

facts: Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. will create 100 new jobs and invest more than $24 million in a new manufacturing facility near Newnan in Coweta County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that partners like Kia and Hyundai are attracting a large network of suppliers, creating jobs and opportunity for Georgians across the state.
Suppliers have committed to bringing over 5,300 jobs to Georgia, mostly to communities outside of large urban areas, since last year's Hyundai Metaplant announcement.
Hyundai Industrial was established in 1978 and is headquartered in Ulsan, Korea.
The supplier specializes in manufacturing car seats, arm rests, and head rests for Hyundai and Kia.

Mr. Woosuck Kim, Team Leader at HDI America Inc., stated that Hyundai Industrial has more than four decades of experience providing comfortable and safe experiences for drivers and passengers with some of the world’s biggest automotive brands.

Hyundai Industrial expressed excitement about being part of the e-mobility transition in Georgia and looking forward to working with Coweta County to create mutually beneficial growth for the community and future employees.
Hyundai Industrial has purchased an existing building located at 83 Amlajack Way in Coweta County.
The purchased building will be retrofitted to produce arm and head rests for OEMs, including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Hyundai Industrial will focus on filling manufacturing production roles, with job postings to be made available when positions open.
Coweta County Board of Commissioners Chairman John Reidelbach welcomed Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. to Coweta County.
The new facility will have close proximity to I-85, making transport to nearby automobile plants effortless, and is expected to increase workforce opportunities in the community.

Yoonie Kim, Director of Korean Investment, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project in partnership with the Coweta County Development Authority and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that with Kia in West Georgia and Hyundai on the eastern coast, suppliers like Hyundai Industrial can locate almost anywhere in the state while remaining central to at least two major manufacturers.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted that this project exemplifies Georgia’s partnership approach to economic development by matching a community's assets and plans with a company's aligned goals.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered industry practices, collaboration, and innovation, positioning itself as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
Since 2020, Georgia has created more than $25.1 billion in investments and 29,000 jobs by pursuing the entire electric vehicle supply chain as the market grows.

In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021, and suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility OEMs resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state.
source_title: GA: Hyundai, Kia Supplier to Invest in Manufacturing Facility in Coweta Co., Creating 100 Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Hyundai Motor Group confirmed it is going forward with previously announced plans to expand its Georgia plant, just weeks after an immigration raid delayed the startup of an electric vehicle battery plant at the site.
As part of a broader investment strategy, Hyundai said it would spend $2.7 billion to increase production capacity at the Ellabell site by 200,000 over the next three years.
The expansion will bring total production capacity at the Ellabell site to 500,000 vehicles a year.
The company first announced the expansion in March at the grand opening of the plant west of Savannah.
In August, Hyundai had said it would invest an additional $5 billion in the United States overall.
An immigration raid occurred at the site, which included arrests of more than 300 South Korean citizens.
The raid led to questions about the wisdom of South Korea investing in the U.S.
Hyundai plans to produce 10 models of electric and hybrid gas-electric vehicles in Georgia, up from the current two the plant has been assembling as it ramps up production.
Hyundai says it's still on track to expand production worldwide to 5.6 million vehicles a year by 2030.
The automaker pledged that 60% of those worldwide vehicles will be electric or hybrid powered, targeting sales in South Korea, North America and Europe.
Hyundai said that it plans to make more than 80% of vehicles sold in the United States domestically by 2030.
Total domestic content for these vehicles will increase from 60% to 80%.
For the first time, the expanded production will include a mid-sized pickup truck, which is a key vehicle class in the U.S. market.
The company already makes the Santa Cruz model, a four-door compact pickup that it started selling in 2021.
Hyundai CEO José Muñoz has said the immigration raid will delay opening the battery plant by at least two to three months.
Spokesperson Michael Stewart said Thursday that the facility will open in the first half of 2026.
Both Hyundai executives and Georgia officials have been trying to calm the situation since the raid, which mushroomed into a diplomatic dispute between South Korea and the United States.

Republican Gov. Brian Kemp told reporters Tuesday at a ceremony marking the groundbreaking of a Rivian Automotive electric vehicle plant that he remains confident Georgia's business advantages would "win out" in terms of foreign investment.
Gov. Kemp said the raid may even wind up smoothing the way for South Korean employees to more easily obtain legal permission to help build and operate facilities in the United States.
Gov. Kemp stated he had good conversations with companies that are here doing business in Georgia, as well as companies looking to do business there.
Gov. Kemp also said he had good conversations with people in the White House about the visa issue.
Brent Stubbs, the chief administrative officer of the Ellabell site, wrote in an opinion piece published Wednesday in The Atlanta Journal-Constitution that the company is still committed to Georgia.
Stubbs wrote that the situation doesn't change plans to continue expanding and localizing in the United States.
Stubbs stated that investments in America are part of a long-term strategic plan.
The $2.7 billion investment confirmed Thursday will go to expanding capacity at the plant and group affiliates, according to spokesperson Michael Stewart.
This investment brings total investment in the auto plant to $10.3 billion.
The $10.3 billion total does not count the $4.3 billion that Hyundai and its joint-venture partner, LG Energy Solution, are putting into the battery plant.
Hyundai and its onsite affiliates currently have 3,129 employees in Ellabell, according to Stewart.
Hyundai pledges to hire at least 8,500 workers by the end of 2031.
State and local governments have promised $2.1 billion in tax breaks and other incentives for the project.
The boost at the Ellabell site is the biggest part of Hyundai's plan to raise production by 1.2 million vehicles a year worldwide.
The worldwide production increase includes another 250,000 vehicles out of Pune, India.
It also includes 200,000 more at Hyundai's electric vehicle plant in Ulsan, South Korea.
The automaker said it would also deliver parts for assembly into an additional 250,000 vehicles at plants in Saudi Arabia, Vietnam and North Africa.
Hyundai underlined a previous announcement to deepen its investment in robotics.
By 2027, Hyundai will launch extended-range electric vehicles with gasoline motors to extend the range of its electric batteries to more than 600 miles (960 kilometers).
source_title: Hyundai says it will spend $2.7 billion expanding part of the Georgia complex raided by ICE

canonical_name: SK-Hyundai Joint Battery Plant in Bartow County
facts: An electric vehicle battery plant set to be built by two Korean giants in Bartow County by 2025 will hit $5 billion in investment value.
The plant will generate $40 million in tax revenue per year.
Details were outlined in a Development of Regional Impact filing submitted to the Georgia Department of Community Affairs.
SK Battery America Inc., which already operates twin battery plants in Commerce, logged the filing to outline how its investment will impact the local community.
The state’s community affairs department oversees the granting of tax incentives that help facilitate inbound investments.
SK is partnering on the new plant with Hyundai Motor Group.
Hyundai Motor Group is developing its own $5.54 billion “meta plant” in Bryan County and has already begun attracting a parade of suppliers from Korea.
Hyundai’s facility near Savannah is set to turn out 300,000 electric models per year at launch.
Hyundai’s production volume is expected to increase to 500,000 vehicles shortly after launch.
All those cars will require batteries built in the United States using materials sourced domestically or from free-trade partners to qualify for federal sales tax incentives of $7,500.
Hyundai and SK signed a memorandum of understanding to work together on a U.S. battery plant in November.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced in December that the investment would come to Cartersville and total between $4 billion and $5 billion, creating 3,500 jobs.
The February filing disclosed new details about the plant, which at the outlined spending level will be the third $5 billion project in Georgia’s history.
Previous $5 billion projects in the state include Rivian’s Covington factory and Hyundai’s Bryan County plant, both announced within the last 18 months.
The factory will occupy 3.3 million square feet.
The site is located on Highway 411 on a 1,119-acre plot owned by an entity called Two Run Ranch LLC.
According to the filing, 60 percent of the plot will be covered in impermeable surfaces when construction is completed.
The plant is expected to use 3.6 million gallons of water per day.
The plant is expected to generate 1 million gallons per day of sewage flow.
The water and sewage requirements necessitate plans for a new treatment plant and service lines that will be built by the county.
source_title: SK, Hyundai Joint Battery Plant in Bartow to Hit $5B, Filing Shows

facts: Officially named Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
Represents an approximate $7.6 billion investment for the initial factory.
Total planned investments reach $12.6 billion in Georgia.
Located near the port of Savannah.
Facility covers about 697,000 m² (more than 7.5 million ft²).
Aims for an annual production capacity of up to 500,000 electric vehicles (per IEEE Spectrum).
Production will serve Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands.
Features a high level of automation and digital integration.
Hundreds of industrial robots on-site handle welding, stamping, and body assembly.
Automated guided vehicles (AGVs) navigate the factory floor to move parts and finished vehicles autonomously.
AGVs are integrated with AI-based logistics and just-in-time supply-chain management.
Human workers are referred to as “Meta Pros” and occupy roles focused on craftsmanship and quality.
Repetitive heavy work is handled by machines rather than human labor.
Sets sustainability targets including solar roofs covering part of the parking lots.
Uses hydrogen-fuel-cell trucks for parts delivery.
Aims to source 100% of the plant’s energy from renewables.
Supported by a large battery-production joint venture with LG Energy Solution.
The battery production joint venture ensures the EV supply chain is logically co-located with the factory.
Building the EV factory from scratch allows for optimized line layout, digital twins, scalable production, and lean logistics.
Underscores a manufacturing shift from human labor to smart systems and from internal-combustion legacy to an electric-vehicle future.
The facility’s success depends on workforce training, resilient supply chains, and policy support in a competitive global market.
Serves as a blueprint for next-generation mobility manufacturing in the United States.
source_title: Metaplant in Motion: Hyundai’s Ultra-Modern EV Factory in Georgia - ENGtechnica

facts: Hyundai is constructing an EV manufacturing facility on a Bryan County megasite along Interstate 16.
Bomee Oh, senior associate for the design division of Haeahn Architecture, presented details on Hyundai's sustainability commitment at a Savannah Joint Development Authority meeting.
The plant design features solar panels covering the employee parking area to honor environmental sustainability.
The site will include displayed artwork, walking trails, recreation fields, a pond, and a greenspace larger than Forsyth Park.
Hyundai officials declined to provide artist renderings of the designs for public view at the time of the meeting.
Commissioner Chairman Carter Infinger stated excitement for the public to see the design, noting it addresses fears about an industrial appearance.
Suppliers relocating to the region are bringing more than 5,000 jobs and have invested $2.093 billion.
There are currently 10 suppliers in the region with more expected to arrive.
In April, Gulfstream announced plans to add 1,600 jobs and invest $150 million in capital investment to expand its facilities.

Maria Whitfield, director of workforce for the Savannah JDA, noted that some organizations are discussing enhanced incentives like quarterly bonuses, onsite childcare, and large referral bonuses to attract talent.
Whitfield is collaborating with regional workforce development professionals to create action plans preparing the local labor force for manufacturing roles.
The Savannah JDA is working with local youth to break manufacturing career stigmas and conducting industry tours.
Workforce development professionals are helping identify candidates who may not pursue traditional university pathways but want to enter the local hiring market directly.
The Savannah JDA hired Wadley Donovan Gutshaw Consulting, LLC to conduct a workforce study and draft a comprehensive plan to address regional labor supply challenges.
An electronic survey was distributed to regional companies, and roundtable interviews were held with staffing agencies, the military, transportation, and higher education sectors.

Trent Thompson, vice president of infrastructure for Savannah-based engineering firm Thomas and Hutton, stated the new water and sewer system will transform regional infrastructure beyond just the megasite.
The water and sewer infrastructure project costs $343 million and will support new developments along the I-16 corridor from New Hampstead to Bulloch County.
Blue pipes visible along I-16 are 18-inch force mains forming part of the phase two sewer connection between the megasite and Savannah.
The entire water and sewer system is scheduled for completion in 2025.
A wastewater treatment plant across I-16 from the megasite is nearing completion and features a membrane bioreactor technology.
Thompson described the effluent from the membrane bioreactor as very high quality and reusable for applications like irrigating the EV factory's greenspaces.
The plan includes sending reclaimed water back to Hyundai to irrigate its park area and landscape across the entire site.
source_title: Not just an ugly industrial site: Hyundai officials unveil site design for EV factory - AOL

facts: FREYR Battery is building a $2.6 billion clean-battery manufacturing plant in Coweta County.
The FREYR Battery facility is expected to produce 724 jobs when fully operational in 2026.
Hyundai Industrial Co., a global auto parts supplier, was selected to build a manufacturing facility in Coweta County.
Hyundai Industrial Co. will invest more than $24 million to retrofit an existing building for its new Coweta County facility.
The Hyundai Industrial Co. facility will produce arm and head rests for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplant in Bryan County and Kia’s West Point plant are identified as OEM customers for the Hyundai Industrial Co. project.
The Hyundai Industrial Co. project will create 100 new jobs.
Automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea is joining a new industrial park project in Henry County alongside Renewal by Andersen.
The NVH Korea project will create over 160 new jobs.
The NVH Korea project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
Henry County's manufacturing job growth has ranked fourth in the state and fifth highest across all industries over the last four years.
Reshoring efforts have attracted manufacturers to Henry County due to its location and existing supply-chain infrastructure.
Coweta County's economic development strategy is focused almost exclusively on advanced manufacturing.
Porsche’s North American headquarters is located in Hapeville near Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
source_title: South Metro Atlanta | Sophisticated Growth - Georgia Trend Magazine

Team members at the plant assemble front and rear bumpers in a just-in-sequence manufacturing environment.

The company plans to occupy the new space at Belfast Commerce Park in Midway on or about June 1, 2024.
Hyundai MOBIS Technical Center of North America serves as the Research & Development Center for Hyundai MOBIS.
Hyundai MOBIS is a Tier 1 supplier currently ranked #7 globally and an affiliate under Hyundai Motor Group.

Hyundai MOBIS produces and supplies a wide variety of automotive components to customers including component modules (chassis, cockpit and front-end module), core system, eco-friendly and DAS-mechatronics.

The Technical Center is one of five globally located in Plymouth, MI and delivers the design and development of advanced engineering, autonomous driving, infotainment, component modules, safety, brake and steering systems, lamp, ICS/electronics and software development solutions.
Hyundai MOBIS is growing quickly and looking for passionate and engaged Engineers and Technical Professionals to join its team.
James Lee serves as President & Chief Sales Officer for the company.

facts: The position is for a Paralegal that collaborates closely with attorneys and internal stakeholders to perform advanced legal and compliance-related tasks.

The role supports the organization’s compliance program by monitoring regulatory updates, managing compliance training, assisting in the development and implementation of the Code of Conduct, and providing support for whistleblower matters.
This position requires occasional travel to multiple MOBIS entities in the United States to conduct compliance training sessions and support on-site compliance activities.
The job position is part of MOBIS Corporate Center America (MCCA) and is located in Atlanta, GA.
Responsibilities include supporting attorneys with contract management including reviewing, editing, tracking, and archiving agreements.
The incumbent must ensure contracts comply with internal standards and legal requirements, escalating issues to legal counsel when necessary.
The role maintains centralized records and reporting for legal matters affecting multiple entities.
The position serves as a liaison between legal departments of different MOBIS entities to ensure consistency in legal practices and documentations.
Responsibilities include tracking and analyzing regulatory updates relevant to the organization’s operations regarding labor, immigration, tariffs, data privacy, and anti-corruption.
The role monitors and reports on regulatory changes affecting MOBIS operations including labor, employment, trade, and data privacy laws.
Duties involve developing, implementing, and monitoring compliance training programs for employees.
The position maintains training records and ensures timely completion of mandatory courses.
Responsibilities include assisting in drafting, updating, and promoting the company’s Code of Conduct to ensure alignment with legal requirements and industry best practices.
The role administers and monitors whistleblower reporting mechanisms and provides support for any ongoing whistleblower cases.
Duties include ensuring confidentiality and proper handling of reports in compliance with applicable laws.
The position supports internal audits and compliance reviews and assists in implementing corrective actions.
Responsibilities include maintaining compliance documentation and reporting systems and assisting with internal audits and risk assessments.
The role collaborates with legal, HR, and other operational teams to address compliance issues.
The position has supervisory responsibilities.
Required education includes a Bachelor’s degree in business, legal studies, paralegal studies, or related field.
Candidates must have 3-5 years of experience in compliance, legal, or regulatory roles.
Required knowledge includes familiarity with compliance frameworks such as FCPA and GDPR and regulatory reporting.
The role requires the ability to communicate clearly in writing and verbally and to explain legal issues to all levels of management.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail are required.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint is required.
Familiarity with Microsoft Web-based Platforms such as SharePoint is required.
Experience in policy development and training program management is required.
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work cross-functionally with legal, HR, and other operations teams are required.
The candidate must be willing to collaborate, share knowledge, respond quickly to urgent matters, and execute proactively.
The role requires the ability to work independently as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.
Knowledge of manufacturing facility and/or warehouse operations and regulations is preferred.
Experience in risk assessment and internal audit processes is preferred.
A Paralegal Certificate is preferred.
Professional certifications such as Certified Compliance & Ethics Professional (CCEP) or similar are preferred.
Working conditions include an office setting, some walking within and between buildings, and some traveling to Mobis entities in the U.S.
Job tags indicate the role is full time, contract work, and requires working at an office.
source_title: Paralegal job at Hyundai Mobis Savannah, GA, US - sheriff.co.in

facts: Seoyon E-HWA announced it will invest $76 million in a new plant near Savannah, Ga.
The facility will manufacture interior and exterior parts for Hyundai Motor Group, including door trim, headlining, and tailgate trim.
Parts will be supplied to Hyundai's upcoming in-plant located in nearby Ellabell.
Seoyon E-HWA plans to hire more than 500 workers for the new facility.
The company plans to begin production at the plant in October 2024.
source_title: Hyundai supplier plans $76 million Georgia plant, hiring 500 | Chattanooga Times Free Press

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Mobis plans to invest $926 million in an Electric Vehicle (EV) Power Electric system plant in Bryan County.
The new plant will eventually employ at least 1,500 people.
This announcement marks the second supplier to locate in the Bryan County area in just two weeks.
The EV Power Electric system plant will expand Hyundai Mobis’ current footprint in Georgia.
Hyundai Mobis has operated a manufacturing facility with almost 1,200 employees in West Point since 2009.
The West Point facility is the largest Tier 1 supplier to Kia Georgia.
The plant supplies automotive manufacturers with complete cockpit modules, chassis modules, as well as bumper assemblies.
H.S. Oh, Vice President of Hyundai Mobis Electric Powertrain Business Unit, stated the project reflects an acceleration in the development of the EV supply chain in Georgia’s auto industry.
Once it reaches full production, the 1,200,000-square-foot facility will be able to supply over 900,000 EV Power Electric systems and 450,000 Integrated Charging Control Units annually.

The annual output will be supplied to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) factory in Bryan County, the Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) factory in Montgomery, and the Kia Georgia plant.
Construction on the new facility is located at the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified Belfast Commerce Park in Richmond Hill.
Construction is expected to begin as early as January 2023.
Production is expected to begin in 2024.

Jon Seagraves, Chairman of the Development Authority of Bryan County, noted the site was purchased about two years ago and highlighted its proximity to available housing in nearby Heartwood, a top-performing public school system, interstate access, and rail.
The project involves nearly $1 billion of investment and the creation of over 1,500 good-paying jobs.
Support for the project came from Bryan County, the City of Richmond Hill, and Raydient Places.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Development Authority of Bryan County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that suppliers like Hyundai Mobis are transitioning to the jobs of the future and becoming part of Georgia’s growing EV ecosystem.
In May, GDEcD predicted that non-affiliated HMGMA suppliers in connection with the project would invest an additional $1 billion into Georgia.
That prediction was already exceeded in about two weeks following the announcement.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have contributed over $13.3 billion in investment and announced more than 18,100 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers connected to the project are predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
source_title: Second Global Automotive Supplier for Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan Co. to Create 1,500 Jobs • The Georgia Virtue

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced Monday that Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC will invest $9 million in expanding their seating manufacturing operations in West Point, Georgia.
The expansion will create more than 150 jobs in Troup County.

Governor Kemp stated: “Georgia’s highly skilled workforce continues to attract manufacturing jobs in every corner of the state, and Hyundai TRANSYS’ continued investment in West Point shows that the hardworking people of west Georgia have proven they’re up to the task.”
Governor Kemp added: “I thank Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System for continuing to invest in Georgia, and I look forward to seeing the opportunities this expansion creates in Troup County.”
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System manufactures seats and seat foam pads for the Hyundai Santa Fe, KIA Sorento, and other models.
The company provides seating structures for automobile manufacturers.
The company also supplies seats to leading electric vehicle makers.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System’s President and CEO Sangkil Jung said: “We are excited for the new opportunity ahead as we expand our presence in the West Point community.”
Sangkil Jung stated: “We are proud of our partnership with the State of Georgia.”
source_title: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System LLC expands manufacturing operations in Georgia | Business | griffindailynews.com
