facts: A group of students and staff from Inspire Academy visited the Hyundai-Transys Georgia Seating System, LLC on Wednesday.
Human Resources Director Robert Carlson led the tour of the Hyundai-Transys plant in West Point.
Hyundai-Transys Georgia Seating System, LLC is a specialized car seat manufacturer that uses eco-friendly materials to satisfy customer needs.
The company produces seats for automobiles including the Genesis series, K9, Grandeur (Azera), and Sonata.
Job titles introduced to students included multi-craft maintenance technicians, production team members, quality team members, logistics team members, and foam team members.
The plant employs over 600 workers running three different assembly lines on multiple shifts.
Students viewed assembly line production and automated seat retrieval systems operated by robots.
Robots are used to manufacture foam production at the facility.
Chambers County School District Superintendent Casey Chambley emphasized developing local business partnerships to support students in pursuing meaningful careers after graduation.
Dr. Travis Smith and UNITE, Inc provided funding for out-of-state travel for the Inspire Academy field trip.

Inspire Academy Principal Dr. Tyler Nelson noted that students learned about internship opportunities offering work-based learning potential with Hyundai-Transys while reinforcing career technical education.
source_title: Inspire Academy students tour Hyundai-Transys

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on July 25, 2023 that Bradshaw Home will build a new distribution center in Effingham County.
The project will create more than 230 new logistics jobs.
Bradshaw Home is a leading designer and marketer of kitchen and cleaning products.
The company's product portfolio includes kitchen tools, gadgets, bakeware, cookware, food storage, and cleaning products.
Products are marketed under brands including GoodCook®, Casabella®, and Evercare®.
More than 75% of Bradshaw's customers are located east of the Rocky Mountains.
Fifty-five percent of Bradshaw's customers are located east of the Mississippi River.
CEO Tony Hair stated that opening an East Coast location expands the distribution network to service customers more effectively while significantly reducing freight miles.
Bradshaw will partner with NFI to operate the new facility.
NFI brings over 90 years of supply chain expertise and maintains a strong presence in Savannah with warehouses, transload facilities, and dedicated and port drayage terminals.
NFI recently partnered with the Georgia Ports Authority to build a new transload facility at the Garden City Terminal, set to open in 2023.
The new distribution center will be 1.2 million square feet.
The facility is located at the Savannah Portside International Park near Meldrim in Effingham County.
The facility is projected to open in November.
Hiring positions include forklift operators for loading and unloading, material handlers for kitting, warehouse operation leads, and other roles.

Speaker of the House Jon Burns thanked local public and private sector partners, particularly the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority and the Georgia Department of Economic Development, for making the announcement possible.

Matt Saxon, Chairman of the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority, thanked development partners at Panattoni Development Company for their investment in attracting companies to Effingham County.
Griff Lynch, Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the facility is ideally positioned to reach domestic markets spanning from Dallas to Chicago.
Georgia's logistics network features 35 weekly containership calls, Class I rail service from CSX and Norfolk Southern, and interstate connectivity.

Dorie Bacon, Senior Regional Project Manager for the GDEcD Global Commerce team, represented the department on the project alongside the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority and Georgia Ports Authority.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that counties near the Port of Savannah are experiencing unprecedented growth, attracting companies with national and global footprints in sectors ranging from automotive to logistics.
Bradshaw Home is headquartered in Rancho Cucamonga, California.
Bradshaw maintains the number one market share for kitchenware in grocery stores.
Bradshaw currently distributes its products in more than 100,000 retail store outlets in North America.
NFI is a fully integrated North American supply chain solutions provider headquartered in Camden, New Jersey.
NFI has been privately held by the Brown family since 1932.
NFI generates more than $3.9 billion in annual revenue and employs over 16,800 associates.
By 2023, NFI will operate a 100% zero-emission drayage fleet.

NFI's business lines include dedicated transportation, warehousing and distribution, eCommerce fulfillment, brokerage, transportation management, port drayage, intermodal, global logistics, and industrial real estate.
source_title: Bradshaw Home to Create More than 230 Logistics Jobs in Effingham Coun | Georgia

facts: Georgia and Atlanta provide a welcoming, business-friendly environment that serves as an ideal backdrop for Cargill’s investment in technology and innovation.
The state created 6,389 jobs during the second quarter of 2024.
The state recorded $2.9 billion in capital expenditures (CapEx) during the second quarter of 2024.
Since January 2024, Georgia's Economic Development team supported 61 companies to establish or expand operations in the state, creating nearly 9,000 new jobs and $5.5 billion in capital investment.
Job creation and investment in projects located outside of metro Atlanta represent 64% of new jobs and 75% of investment compared to the previous year.
Manufacturing locations remain the majority contributor to job activity at nearly 60%, even as fiscal stimulus begins to wane.

facts: Nextran Truck Centers announced today that it has acquired Quincy Mack, Decatur Mack, and H&L Mack.
Quincy Mack, Decatur Mack, and H&L Mack is a well-respected Mack Trucks dealer with five locations across Illinois, Missouri, and Iowa.
This strategic acquisition further expands Nextran's footprint from 26 to 31 locations across the United States.
Founded over 40 years ago, Quincy Mack, Decatur Mack, and H&L Mack have built a strong reputation in the Midwest.
The acquisition will enhance Nextran's industry-leading sales, parts, service and rental & leasing operations.
Jon W. Pritchett is President and CEO of Nextran Truck Centers.

The integration of these new locations into Nextran's operations will bolster its position as the world's top-selling Mack Truck dealer, driving the total annual units sold to over 5,500 new and used commercial trucks.
The acquisition expands Nextran's rental and lease fleet to nearly 1,000 units in operation.
This growth is supported by a robust network of over 530 service bays and nearly 1,400 employees nationwide.
This acquisition marks a significant step in Nextran's strategic growth, bringing the total to 12 new locations added over the past three years.
Jack Sharkey is Owner of Quincy Mack, Decatur Mack, and H&L Mack.

Established in 1993, Nextran Truck Centers is a full-service commercial truck dealership network located throughout Florida, Georgia, Alabama, Missouri, Colorado, Kansas, Wyoming, and now Illinois and Iowa.
Nextran's 31 locations are strategically positioned along key transportation routes throughout the United States.
Nextran sells and services medium and heavy-duty trucks from top manufacturers such as Mack Trucks, Volvo Trucks, Isuzu, HINO, Kalmar Ottawa, GMC, and Ford.

entity_name: Developing Markets for Zero Emission Vehicles in Short Haul Goods Movement Report
canonical_name: Developing Markets for Zero Emission Vehicles in Short Haul Goods Movement
facts: The report 'Developing Markets for Zero Emission Vehicles in Short Haul Goods Movement' was published on 12/10/2020.
It examines the potential market share for electric heavy duty trucks (EHDTs) via simulation modeling, case studies, interviews, and a survey.
It assesses the impacts of EHDTs on freight operations.
It compares the costs and benefits of using diesel, natural gas hybrid, and all-electric vehicles for 2020, 2025, and 2030.
It presents recommendations for promoting and increasing the market share of EHDTs and hybrids.
The report was authored by Giuliano, G., Dessouky, M., Dexter, S., Fang, J., Hu, S., and Steimetz, S.
The copyrighted publication can be accessed through the University of California, Davis National Center for Sustainable Transportation website.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Publications

facts: HL Mando America Corporation will invest $11.1 million to upgrade its current Opelika facility.
The investment will create 26 new jobs at the company's Opelika operations.
HL Mando’s Opelika operations produce brake calipers and suspension systems.
The company supports a customer base located throughout the U.S.
Mando Corporation is one of the leading automotive parts manufacturers in the world, specializing in internationally competitive state-of-the-art chassis components and systems.

Through extensive research and development, Mando became the first manufacturer in Korea to develop advanced products such as ABS (Anti-Lock Brake System), ESP (Electronic Stability Program), CDC (Continuous Damping Control), ESC (Electronic Stability Control) and EPS (Electric Power Steering).
Headquartered in South Korea, the company operates 20 manufacturing plants and 18 R&D centers in 10 countries.
Mando employs over 11,700 associates worldwide.
Opelika Mayor Gary Fuller stated, “We are extremely honored by HL Mando’s continued confidence in Opelika and the additional investment in our area.”
Mayor Fuller noted that HL Mando is one of Opelika's largest manufacturing employers and added, “we appreciate the employment opportunities for our citizens.”
City of Opelika Economic Development Director Lori Huguley stated, “We are appreciative of HL Mando’s additional investment in Opelika.”
Huguley described HL Mando as a valued community partner and added, “we are glad to support their continued growth and success.”

entity_name: Georgia Clean Vehicle Related Tax Credits
canonical_name: Georgia Clean Vehicle Related Tax Credits
facts: Historic Low Emission/Zero Emission Vehicle (LEV/ZEV) and Medium Duty/Heavy Duty Vehicle (MDV/HDV) Tax Credits in Georgia were discontinued effective July 1, 2015.
Tax credits for vehicles converted to alternative fuels (Converted Vehicle) and Electric Vehicle Chargers are still available.
The tax credit allowed for electric vehicle chargers is 10% of the cost of the charger or $2,500, whichever is less.
The electric vehicle charger tax credit is available only to businesses; individual homes do not qualify.
A qualified charger must be rated greater than 130 volts and designed to charge on-road vehicles.
The charger must be installed in Georgia and remain in Georgia for 5 consecutive years.
The tax credit allowed for converted vehicles is 10% of the cost of conversion, not to exceed $2,500 per converted vehicle.
The converted vehicle must be fueled solely by an alternative fuel; a vehicle capable of operating on an alternative fuel and gasoline or diesel does not qualify.
Natural gas (compressed or liquefied) qualifies as an alternative fuel for the converted vehicle tax credit.
Battery electric qualifies as an alternative fuel, but hybrids operating on gasoline or diesel do not qualify.
Propane qualifies as an alternative fuel for the converted vehicle tax credit.
Hydrogen fuel cell qualifies as an alternative fuel for the converted vehicle tax credit.
The Georgia Environmental Protection Division (EPD) is responsible for certifying electric vehicle chargers and converted vehicles that meet the requirements.
The Georgia Department of Revenue is responsible for administering the electric vehicle charger and converted vehicle tax credits.
EPD certification is required to apply for the applicable tax credit.
Applicants must download and read EPD’s instructions before applying.
Applicants must download either the Electric Vehicle Charger Certification Form or the Converted Vehicle Certificate Form.
Applicants must complete the certification form in accordance with EPD’s instructions and submit the signed form and required documentation to EPD electronically or by US mail.
EPD will review the completed certification form and supporting documents, and if all requirements are met, EPD will sign the form and send it back.
Patrick Brand leads the Certification Program at Georgia EPD for charger or converted vehicle certification inquiries.
The Georgia Department of Revenue handles inquiries on how to obtain the tax credit after receiving an approved certification form.

links.context: EPA requirements for alternative fuel conversions
Email to submit signed certification forms and documentation to EPD

links.url: Georgia Environmental Protection Division, Air Protection Branch, Mobile and Area Sources, 4244 International Parkway, Suite 120, Atlanta, GA 30354
links.context: Mailing address for submitting certification forms to EPD
Email to contact Georgia Department of Revenue for tax credit information

source_title: Clean Vehicle Related Tax Credits | Environmental Protection Division

facts: Mercedes-Benz USA LLC and Penske Automotive Group announced they have started discussions to transition the distribution rights, management, sales and marketing activities of smart USA from Penske Automotive Group to Mercedes-Benz USA.
The transition is expected to occur by the end of the second quarter of 2011.

The proposed change marks a further step in implementing the future growth strategy of the smart brand and reflects last year’s reorganization at smart headquarters in Germany, where smart is now managed as an independent brand fully integrated into Mercedes-Benz Cars business unit of Daimler AG.

Mercedes-Benz President and CEO Ernst Lieb stated excitement about working toward integration of smart into the MBUSA organization and looking forward to working with dealer partners to exceed customer expectations for this unique vehicle.
The addition of smart, combined with the Autohaus dealer initiative, will help build upon the tremendous momentum the Mercedes-Benz brand had achieved in the U.S.
Penske Automotive Group Chairman Roger Penske noted that introducing the smart brand to the U.S. delivered over 45,000 vehicles over the last three years.

Aligning smart distribution with Daimler is described as the logical next step for the smart brand in the U.S., enabling growth through the Mercedes-Benz dealership network and leading in conservation and meeting future fuel economy standards.

The addition of the fuel efficient smart fortwo and smart fortwo electric vehicles to MBUSA’s product portfolio underscores the luxury brand’s commitment to meet even more stringent fuel economy standards in the future.
The smart fortwo achieves EPA estimated 41 mpg in highway driving.
Manufactured by Daimler AG, smart is currently sold in more than 40 countries, and more than 1.2 million smart fortwo vehicles have been sold worldwide since 1998.

Smart has established itself as the think tank for urban and electric mobility for Daimler AG and plays an important role in plans to make innovative future mobility concepts such as car2go available in Europe and the United States.
Mercedes-Benz USA (MBUSA), headquartered in Montvale, New Jersey, is responsible for distribution, marketing and customer service for all Mercedes-Benz and Maybach products in the United States.
MBUSA offers drivers the most diverse line-up in the luxury segment with 12 model lines ranging from the sporty C-Class to the flagship S-Class sedans and the SLS AMG supercar.
MBUSA is also responsible for distribution, marketing, and customer service of Mercedes-Benz Sprinter Vans in the U.S.
Penske Automotive Group, Inc., headquartered in Bloomfield Hills, Michigan, operates 325 retail automotive franchises representing 40 different brands and 25 collision repair centers.
Penske Automotive sells new and previously owned vehicles, finance and insurance products, replacement parts, and offers maintenance and repair services on all brands it represents.
Penske Automotive has 172 franchises in 17 states and Puerto Rico and 153 franchises located outside the United States, primarily in the United Kingdom.
Penske Automotive is a member of the Fortune 500 and Russell 1000 and has approximately 14,800 employees.

The press release includes forward-looking statements regarding the parties' ability to complete the transaction, noting risks such as completion of binding documentation, regulatory approvals, and other conditions outside their control.
Hughes Telematics Inc. (HTI) is a Delaware corporation with its principal address at 41 Perimeter Center East, Suite 400, Atlanta, Georgia 30346.
HTI is in the business of providing telematics equipment and services for the automotive industry.
The Telematics Services Agreement between HTI and Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC (MBUSA) was executed on October 31, 2007.
MBUSA will factory-install telematics equipment capable of receiving services in MCG Vehicles and authorize HTI to deliver those services.
MCG Vehicles are defined as vehicles manufactured by or for MBUSA during calendar year 2010 or thereafter for sale or lease within the Territory.
The HTI Telematics Communicator (TCU) is a device capable of receiving PASS Services and MBTA Services, provided through an Authorized Manufacturer and/or Authorized Suppliers.
Hughes Network Systems, LLC is deemed an Authorized Supplier of HTI with respect to the HTI Telematics Communicators.
LCT Enabled Vehicles are vehicles sold or leased by MBUSA equipped with a Low Cost Tele Aid (LCT) Communicator or successor devices.
Embedded Cellular Technology refers to transmission and reception via a device installed as original equipment using wireless signals based on communication networks.
The agreement outlines standards and service levels for PASS Services and MBTA Services in Exhibit B.
Technical specifications for the HTI Telematics Communicator are detailed in Exhibit G.
MCG Vehicle models and planned production volumes are listed on Exhibits D-1 and D-2, respectively.
HTI must meet specific milestone requirements reflected on Exhibit N of the agreement.
The first year of subscription service to PASS Services and MBTA Services is provided free to subscribers.
Subscription fees for the second and subsequent years are charged by HTI to subscribers for the telematics services.
Vehicle and subscriber data collected by both parties is summarized in Exhibit V, which serves as a living document subject to mutual written modification.
Personal information, data protection, and security requirements are governed by Exhibit AA of the agreement.
An Executive Steering Committee and an Operating Committee will be established to oversee the telematics services relationship.
The agreement covers intellectual property ownership for prior developed, contributed, and jointly created technology between HTI and MBUSA.
Indemnification obligations regarding intellectual property infringement and general liabilities survive the termination or expiration of the agreement.
The Territory covered by the agreement includes the fifty states of the United States, the District of Columbia, and Puerto Rico.
source_title: Telematics Services Agreement - HUGHES Telematics Inc. & Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy released a report showing hundreds of U.S. coal power plant sites could convert to nuclear power plant sites.
The coal-to-nuclear transition could add new jobs, increase economic benefit, and significantly improve environmental conditions.
The transition could add substantial clean electricity to the grid, helping the U.S. reach its net-zero emissions goals by 2050.
The study investigated the benefits and challenges of converting retiring coal plant sites into nuclear plant sites.
After screening recently retired and active coal plant sites, the study team identified 157 retired coal plant sites and 237 operating coal plant sites as potential candidates for conversion.
Of the identified sites, 80% are good candidates to host advanced reactors smaller than the gigawatt scale.
A coal-to-nuclear transition could significantly improve air quality in communities around the country.
A case study found greenhouse gas emissions in a region could fall by 86% when nuclear power plants replace large coal plants.
This emissions reduction is equivalent to taking more than 500,000 gasoline-powered passenger vehicles off the roads.
The transition could also increase employment and economic activity within those communities.
When a large coal plant is replaced by a nuclear power plant of equivalent size, jobs in the region could increase by more than 650 permanent positions.
Long-term job impacts from the case study could lead to additional annual economic activity of $275 million.
This implies a 92% increase in tax revenue for the local county compared to operating coal power.
Assistant Secretary for Nuclear Energy Dr. Kathryn Huff stated this is an important opportunity to help communities preserve jobs, increase tax revenue, and improve air quality.
Huff emphasized delivering place-based solutions and ensuring an equitable energy transition that does not leave communities behind as the U.S. moves to a clean energy future.
The reuse of coal infrastructure for advanced nuclear reactors could reduce costs for developing new nuclear technology by saving 15% to 35% in construction costs.

Coal-to-nuclear transitions could save millions of dollars by reusing electrical equipment such as transmission lines and switchyards, cooling ponds or towers, and civil infrastructure like roads and office buildings.
Argonne National Laboratory, Idaho National Laboratory, and Oak Ridge National Laboratory conducted the study.
The study was sponsored by the Department of Energy’s Office of Nuclear Energy.
source_title: DOE Report Finds Hundreds of Retiring Coal Plant Sites Could Convert to Nuclear | Department of Energy

entity_name: HB-Line Facility at Savannah River Site
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Environmental Management announced the decision to restart HB-Line operations at the Savannah River Site in South Carolina.

Restarting HB-Line provides the capability to power America’s nuclear future by recycling surplus plutonium and partnering with industry to produce uranium-plutonium mixed oxide (MOX) fuel for advanced nuclear reactors.
The facility is an integral part of H-Canyon, the only chemical separations facility of its kind in the United States.

EM Assistant Secretary Tim Walsh stated that restarting HB-Line is the right decision for taxpayers, national security, and America’s energy future, restoring a unique capability to accelerate the mission, strengthen the domestic nuclear industrial base, and deliver fuel for advanced reactors.
The restart decision is the first step in a multi-year restart plan.
Once operational, HB-Line will accelerate EM’s plutonium disposition mission by 10 to 13 years while reducing existing costs and saving American taxpayers up to $350 million.

Restarting HB-Line creates an opportunity to recover valuable isotopes currently available in limited quantities domestically, supporting critical needs in scientific research and commercial applications.
HB-Line is a specialized processing facility within the H-Canyon complex that has supported critical national nuclear missions.
Following completion of its last mission in 2018, the facility was placed in a managed layup state, preserving its one-of-a-kind capability for future use.

Savannah River Operations Office manager Edwin Deshong noted that the site has been integral to America’s nuclear mission for more than 70 years and that the workforce has the expertise, experience, and dedication to execute the mission safely and successfully.

The decision directly advances President Trump’s executive orders, Reinvigorating the Nuclear Industrial Base and Deploying Advanced Nuclear Reactor Technologies for National Security, which call for jumpstarting America’s nuclear industrial base to ensure national and economic security.
source_title: DOE’s Office of Environmental Management Restarts One-of-a-Kind Facility in South Carolina to Fuel America’s Nuclear Future | Department of Energy

entity_name: Alabama Alternative Fuels Laws and Incentives
canonical_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center Alabama State Summary
facts: The page lists incentives, laws, and regulations related to alternative fuels and advanced vehicles for Alabama.
Local Clean Cities and Communities coalitions, specifically the Alabama Clean Fuels Coalition, can provide information about grants and other opportunities.
Information in the list is updated throughout the year and comprehensively reviewed annually after Alabama's legislative session ends.
The last comprehensive review of the incentives and laws was conducted in February 2026.
State incentives include Alabama's National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Planning.
State incentives include Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger and Medium- and Heavy-Duty Diesel Vehicle Replacement Rebates.
State incentives include Biofuel Research and Development Funding.
State incentives include a Biofuel Production Jobs Tax Credit.
State incentives include an Idle Reduction and Natural Gas Vehicle (NGV) Weight Exemption.
State incentives include Electric Vehicle (EV) Chargers Grants.
Laws and regulations include an Electric Vehicle (EV) Chargers Grant Program.
Laws and regulations include Autonomous Vehicle (AV) Operation Requirements.
Laws and regulations include a Public Utility Definition.
Laws and regulations include an Electric Vehicle (EV) Fee.
Laws and regulations include Alternative Fuel Taxes.
Laws and regulations include a Fuel-Efficient Green Fleets Policy and Fleet Management Program Development.
The page provides access to a list of expired, repealed, and archived laws and incentives in Alabama.
Points of contact information is available for Clean Cities and Communities coalitions or agencies that can help with transportation laws, incentives, and funding opportunities.
Alabama is home to Clean Cities and Communities Coalitions.
The Alabama Legislature meets annually from February through May.
During the legislative session, the governor must sign or veto legislation within six days of transmittal (except Sundays) or it becomes law without signature.
If legislation is passed during the last five days of the session, the governor must sign or veto within 10 days of session adjournment or the legislation is pocket vetoed.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Alabama Laws and Incentives

facts: Murata will be attending CES 2025 January 7-10 in Las Vegas.
The booth in the Vehicle Technology Pavilion (West Hall Booth #6500) will serve as a showcase for products enabling innovation throughout the electronics industry.
With a focus on mobility and connectivity, attendees are invited to meet with solution experts to see how Murata is unlocking the future of connected mobility.
Potential and existing customers, partners, and fellow innovators will see an extensive line-up of product demos and technologies, as well as those still under development.
The SCH16T is a high-performance MEMS-based 6DoF sensor in a single package, offering low drift over temperature and a wide gyro measurement range for centimeter-level accuracy in flight.
Murata's integrated lens cleaning device ensures clear optical camera vision to meet the need for safety in adverse weather for AD/ADAS-equipped vehicles.

In-Cabin/Out-of-Cabin Radar Sensing Solutions can minimize the need for traditional sensors in vehicles while enhancing safety for in-cabin use and providing added convenience for out-of-cabin applications.
Matter™ is a new smart home connectivity standard for IoT devices, enabling seamless, secure connections to home networks, supported by Murata's miniaturized, high-performance modules on display.

The Type 1SC-NTN is the first ultra-compact resin mold module recognized by an NTN service provider, fully certified from Skylo for its dual-mode cellular and non-terrestrial network capabilities, integrating the latest Rel-17 NTN standard to enable satellite data communication without hardware changes.

LF antennas transmit and receive magnetic field energy to enable high accuracy positioning, low power, and stable communication without blind spots, with Murata applying the position detection function to an auto landing system for drones in a precision landing demo.
Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. is a worldwide leader in the design, manufacture and sale of ceramic-based passive electronic components and solutions, communication modules and power supply modules.
Murata is committed to the development of advanced electronic materials and leading edge, multi-functional, high-density modules.
The company has employees and manufacturing facilities throughout the world.
source_title: Unlocking the Future of Connected Mobility: Murata Heads to CES 2025

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis is constructing a new greenfield rolling and recycling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama.

Total capital expenditures for the first three months of fiscal year 2026 were $386 million, primarily attributed to strategic investments in new rolling and recycling capacity under construction at the Bay Minette facility.
In June 2025, Novelis issued $400 million of tax-exempt bonds with a mandatory tender for purchase in 2032 and maturation in 2055 to finance a portion of the Bay Minette construction costs.
During the quarter ended June 30, 2025, Novelis incurred $5 million of start-up costs related to the Bay Minette plant construction, recorded in Selling, general and administrative expenses.
Novelis is executing a comprehensive cost reduction program expected to deliver run-rate cost savings exceeding $100 million by the end of fiscal year 2026.
The company's aluminum products serve customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage packaging, and specialties industries across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy through supplier and customer partnerships.
Steve Fisher serves as president and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Dev Ahuja serves as executive vice president and CFO of Novelis Inc.
Net sales for Q1 FY2026 increased 13% year-over-year to $4.7 billion, driven by higher average aluminum prices and a 1% increase in total rolled product shipments.
Rolled product shipments reached 963 kilotonnes in Q1 FY2026, up 1% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 FY2026 was $416 million, down 17% year-over-year due to higher aluminum scrap prices, unfavorable product mix, and a net negative tariff impact.
Net income attributable to common shareholders decreased 36% year-over-year to $96 million in Q1 FY2026, primarily driven by restructuring charges and lower operating performance.
Restructuring and impairment expenses for Q1 FY2026 totaled $85 million, including $83 million related to the 2025 Efficiency Plan.
Novelis reported a net leverage ratio of 3.2x at the end of Q1 FY2026.
Total liquidity stood at $3.0 billion as of June 30, 2025, consisting of $1.1 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $2.0 billion in availability under committed credit facilities.
Novelis had net sales of $17.1 billion in fiscal year 2025.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group.

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading global provider of sustainable aluminum solutions and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis broke ground on a new US recycling and rolling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama last month.
Net income attributable to common shareholders decreased 23% year-over-year to $183 million for the second quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Net income from continuing operations was $184 million, down 23% year-over-year; net income from continuing operations excluding special items was $203 million, down 17% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA was $506 million, down 8% year-over-year, with Adjusted EBITDA per tonne at $514.
Total shipments reached 984 kilotonnes, representing a 2% year-over-year increase.
The company maintained a net leverage ratio of 2.3x and total liquidity of $2.8 billion.
Net sales increased 17% to $4.8 billion for the second quarter of fiscal year 2023, compared to $4.1 billion in the prior year period.

Shipment growth was primarily driven by higher automotive shipments as semiconductor shortages impacting the automotive industry began to ease, alongside slightly higher beverage can and aerospace shipments.

The 8% decrease in Adjusted EBITDA was primarily due to higher energy and operating costs from geopolitical instability, inflation, and global supply chain disruptions, partially offset by higher product pricing, volume, and favorable product mix.
Adjusted free cash flow from continuing operations was an outflow of $90 million for the first six months of fiscal year 2023.
Steve Fisher serves as President and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Devinder Ahuja serves as Executive Vice President and CFO of Novelis Inc.
Novelis provides innovative aluminum solutions to customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis reported net sales of $17.1 billion for fiscal year 2022.
Novelis Inc. is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group.
Novelis will discuss its second quarter fiscal year 2023 results via a live webcast and conference call at 7:00 a.m. EST on Tuesday, November 8, 2022.
The company's forward-looking statements highlight risks related to the impact of the global semiconductor shortage on automotive production and demand for automotive aluminum sheet.
source_title: Novelis Reports Second Quarter Fiscal Year 2023 Results

facts: High-pressure tanks operating at 3,600 psi have been used safely in compressed natural gas vehicles (NGVs) for many years.
Improved high-strength composite tanks now store hydrogen at higher pressures of 5,000 and 10,000 psi to achieve greater driving range in hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
High-pressure hydrogen tanks are designed not to rupture and must meet rigorous performance requirements through extensive testing.
Standards compliance for 25 MPa (3.6 ksi) storage pressure follows NGV2-2000 (modified) and DOT FMVSS 304 (modified).
Standards compliance for 35 MPa (5 ksi) storage pressure includes E.I.H.P./Rev 12B ISO 15869, EU 97/23/EG, NGV2-2000 (modified), DOT FMVSS 304 (modified), and Reijikijyun Betten 9.
Standards compliance for 70 MPa (10 ksi) storage pressure includes E.I.H.P./Rev 12B ISO 15869, EU 97/23/EG, DOT FMVSS 304 (modified), and Betten 9 (modified).
During testing, tanks are subjected to more than twice the maximum pressure they experience under normal service conditions to ensure they do not fail.
At the end of their life, tanks are tested to approximately twice their working pressure according to SAE Journal 2579.
Hydrogen filling stations utilize numerous redundant overpressure protection systems to prevent vehicle fuel system over-pressurization.
Millions of high-pressure composite tanks are estimated to be in use worldwide across commercial and industrial applications with an excellent overall safety record.
Tanks undergo cycling tests where they are pressurized and depressurized far more times than during a vehicle's lifetime.
Advanced carbon-composite tanks have been cycled more than 500,000 times to maximum operating pressure without leaking.
A typical vehicle tank filled once a week for 20 years undergoes slightly more than 1,000 cycles.
Tanks are exposed to pressures above normal to simulate fault management and are dropped 6 feet when empty, shot with a rifle, burned, and exposed to acids, salts, and other road hazards.
In the unlikely event of a leak, an advanced composite tank can be removed from service without incident and is highly unlikely to fail in a way that endangers vehicle occupants.
These tanks have remained intact during collisions and vehicle fires, passing various pressure tests after such events.
A tank's pressure relief device activates when temperatures exceed a set point of typically 102°C (~216°F), safely releasing hydrogen gas.
This safety procedure is validated through performance tests conducted per the NGV2-2000 standard.

Required manufacturing quality pressure tests include hydrostatic burst, drop test, extreme and ambient temperature pressure cycles, permeation, hydrogen cycle, chemical exposure, softening temperature, bonfire, tensile properties, gunfire penetration, resin shear, flaw tolerance, hydrogen-compatible material testing, accelerated stress, and numerous internal tests.
Additional standards SAE J2579 and ISO 15869 are being developed and validated to further improve safety standards for high-pressure hydrogen tanks.
Domestic and foreign vehicle manufacturers are collaborating with tank manufacturers and others to develop robust test procedures ensuring tank and vehicle safety.

Key organizations providing hydrogen codes and standards include the American National Standards Institute/Canadian Standards Association (NGV2-2000), European Integrated Hydrogen Project (Rev 12B), Society of Automotive Engineers (J2579), and H2 Tools.
source_title: High-Pressure Hydrogen Tank Testing | Department of Energy

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. announced it will change its company name and brand to Mobitera to better represent its commitment to creating a safe, comfortable and sustainable mobility experience.
The corporate name and brand change will be effective April 1, 2027.
CEO Masashi Nagayasu stated that Mobitera reflects the organization's vision to power the future of mobility with high-quality technologies that elevate and enrich the human experience.

In December 2024, Panasonic Automotive Systems began a strategic partnership with the Apollo Group to strengthen software development capabilities for the in-vehicle cockpit space and optimize their overall business portfolio.
The rebrand to Mobitera is positioned as the next step in positioning the organization for success following this partnership.
The name "Mobitera" fuses "mobility", the Latin word "iter" (path), and the Japanese word "terasu" (to illuminate) to express a wish to shine light upon the future.
The central "i" in Mobitera highlights a vow to keep individuals the focus of all they do.
The new corporate logo features an initial "M" symbolizing the aspiration to illuminate the future and a central "i" symbolizing keeping individuals at the forefront.
The corporate color, Mobitera Blue-Green, was developed to symbolize the harmonization of people, society, and the planet.
The company's mission is to create a sustainable mobility society to promote a better life for everyone.
The company's vision is to be the "Joy in Motion" design company.
Domestic and overseas consolidated subsidiaries will change their corporate names to align with the new Mobitera brand name, also effective April 1, 2027.
Panasonic ITS Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera ITS Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Electronics Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Solutions Inc.
Panasonic System Networks Evaluation Technology Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Evaluation Technologies Inc.
Harima SANYO Industries Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Wel Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC will become Mobitera America, LLC.
Panasonic Automotive Systems de Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera De Mexico, S.A de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Reynosa Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Reynosa Mexico, S.A de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Monterrey Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Monterrey Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Sales Company de Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Sales Company de Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Shared Services Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Shared Service Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Europe GmbH will become Mobitera Europe GmbH.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Czech, s.r.o. will become Mobitera Czech, s.r.o.
OpenSynergy GmbH will see no name change.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Asia Pacific Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Asia Pacific Co., Ltd.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Malaysia Sdn. Bhd. will become Mobitera Malaysia Sdn. Bhd.
Panasonic Automotive Systems India Pvt. Ltd. will become Mobitera India Pvt. Ltd.

Details regarding name changes for Panasonic Automotive Systems (China) Co.,Ltd., Panasonic Automotive Systems (Dalian) Co., Ltd. and Panasonic Automotive Systems (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. will be announced after completing internal procedures.

facts: The DOE Vehicle Technologies Office announced a $60 million Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA) for batteries and electrification, new mobility systems, and other advanced vehicle technologies.
The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced up to $60 million in new and innovative advanced vehicle technologies research funding on December 10, 2020.
The funding supports research aimed at developing more affordable, efficient, and secure transportation energy.
The funding is provided through the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE).
Up to $35 million is allocated for Batteries and Electrification priorities.

Research topics under Batteries and Electrification include advanced liquid electrolytes for lithium-ion cells under extreme conditions like extreme fast charging, mechanical, thermal, or electrical abuse.
Research topics under Batteries and Electrification include novel liquid electrolytes for lithium-sulfur cells to improve overall cell stability and performance.
Research topics under Batteries and Electrification include lithium-sulfur and lithium-air battery cell development.
Research topics under Batteries and Electrification include high-power-density traction inverters for light-, medium-, or heavy-duty vehicle applications.
Up to $5 million is allocated for Advanced Combustion Engines and Fuels priorities.

Research topics under Advanced Combustion Engines and Fuels include developing simulation tools that couple engine combustion with aftertreatment systems to optimize near-zero exhaust emissions while maintaining or improving engine efficiency.
Up to $11.5 million is allocated for Materials Technology priorities.
Research topics under Materials Technology include the production demonstration of lightweight multi-material passenger vehicle glider systems.
Up to $17.5 million is allocated for New Mobility Systems priorities.
Research topics under New Mobility Systems include cooperative driving automation in vehicles enabled by low-cost infrastructure upgrades or novel applications.
Research topics under New Mobility Systems include demonstrating new mobility systems technologies or practices in real-world transportation systems.
Up to $1.2 million is allocated for Transportation and Energy Analysis.

Some funded topics support the DOE Energy Storage Grand Challenge, leveraging DOE National Laboratories, universities, and industry to accelerate energy-storage technology development and sustain American global leadership.
The application process consists of two phases: a concept paper and a full application.
Concept papers are due on February 5, 2021, and full applications are due on April 7, 2021.
source_title: DOE Announces $60 Million to Accelerate Advanced Vehicle Technologies Research | Department of Energy

canonical_name: Qualified Plug-in Electric Drive Motor Vehicle Credit (IRC 30D)
facts: The Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (Public Law 117-169) amended the Qualified Plug-in Electric Drive Motor Vehicle Credit (IRC 30D), now known as the Clean Vehicle Credit.
A new requirement for final assembly in North America took effect on August 17, 2022.
The final assembly requirement applies to new electric, fuel cell electric, and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles acquired, delivered, and placed in service after August 16, 2022.
For vehicles placed in service on or after January 1, 2023, Clean Vehicle Credit provisions are subject to updated guidance from the IRS and the U.S. Department of the Treasury.
Beginning January 1, 2024, buyers can reduce the vehicle's upfront purchase price by transferring their Clean Vehicle Credit to the dealer.
Before 2024, eligible vehicle buyers could only receive the credit amount after filing their tax return.
Starting January 1, 2024, dealers must submit information to the IRS through IRS Energy Credits Online to determine vehicle eligibility and credit amount at the point of sale.
Without dealer submission to the IRS, buyers cannot claim a tax credit on their return nor transfer it to a dealer.
A dealer must provide the buyer a copy of the IRS's approval of the dealer's submission.
The list of EVs with final assembly in North America applies only to vehicles purchased and delivered between August 17 and December 31, 2022.
Model Year 2022 and 2023 vehicle eligibility is based on Fuel Economy labeling information and Part 583 data submitted by manufacturers to the EPA and NHTSA as of December 31, 2022.
Verification of final assembly for vehicles purchased beginning January 1, 2023, should use the VIN decoder.
Manufacturer sales caps apply to vehicles placed in service before January 1, 2023.

Vehicles with met manufacturer sales caps include the Chevrolet Bolt EUV, Chevrolet Bolt EV, GMC Hummer EV Pickup, GMC Hummer EV SUV, Tesla Model 3, Tesla Model S, Tesla Model X, Tesla Model Y, and Cadillac Lyriq for their respective model years.

Eligible vehicles for 2022 include the Audi Q5, BMW 330e, BMW X5 xDrive45e (PHEV), Chrysler Pacifica PHEV, Ford E-Transit, Ford Escape PHEV, Ford F-150 Lightning, Ford Mustang MACH E, Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe, Jeep Wrangler 4xe, Lincoln Aviator PHEV, Lincoln Corsair PHEV, Lucid Air, Nissan Leaf, Rivian EDV, Rivian R1S, Rivian R1T, and Volvo S60 Recharge.

Eligible vehicles for 2023 include the BMW 330e, BMW X5 xDrive45e (PHEV), Ford E-Transit, Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe, Jeep Wrangler 4xe, Lincoln Aviator PHEV, Lucid Air, Mercedes EQS SUV, Nissan Leaf, Rivian R1S, Rivian R1T, and Volkswagen ID.4.
The vehicle list will be updated as manufacturers submit Fuel Economy labeling and Part 583 information to government agencies.
Manufacturers can use the website contact form to request changes to the vehicle list.
Final assembly in North America is not required for tax credit eligibility for vehicles purchased before August 17, 2022.
The build location of a particular vehicle should be confirmed by the VIN or an information label affixed to the vehicle.
NHTSA provides a VIN decoder to identify a vehicle's build plant and country of manufacture.
North America for final assembly purposes includes the United States, Puerto Rico, Canada, and Mexico.
The information on the page is not an official or legally binding document; full details are available through IRS.gov/cleanvehicles.

links.context: Dealer portal for submitting vehicle eligibility and credit amounts at point of sale
Full details and eligibility requirements for the Clean Vehicle Credit

source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Electric Vehicles with Final Assembly in North America

canonical_name: Battery Monitor 2023 (Roland Berger & RWTH Aachen University)
facts: Global battery market is set to grow at 34% per year until 2030.
Electric vehicles will account for around 80% of demand for lithium-ion batteries over the coming decades.
Competition is growing from new European and US manufacturers as well as massively expanding Chinese companies.

Despite the recent deceleration in automotive manufacturers' electric vehicle projections, the global battery market continues to grow at an enormously fast pace and the demand for lithium-ion and sodium-ion batteries is still rising.
Between 2020 and 2030 alone, demand will increase more than eighteenfold at an annual growth rate of 34%.
The main reason behind this development is the automotive industry's switch to battery electric vehicles.
This is having a corresponding impact on the industrial landscape, particularly in the USA and Europe.
New battery production hotspots are emerging in these regions, as well as new companies to add to those already existing in Asia.
These conclusions were reached by Roland Berger and the Chair of Production Engineering of E-Mobility Components (PEM) of RWTH Aachen University in their 'Battery Monitor 2023' publication.
The focus of current developments lies primarily on technical innovations towards more efficient production and alternative battery materials.

Professor Heiner Heimes, Member of the Institute Management of RWTH's Chair of PEM, states that to thrive on the international market, battery manufacturers need to optimize their production processes and that developing new production technologies is the key to efficient, cost-effective and sustainable battery production.
Demand for batteries is expected to reach around 4,900 gigawatt hours (GWh) in 2030, significantly up on last year's forecast of about 4,000 GWh.
This expected demand will be exceeded by announced global production capacities of around 8,900 GWh in 2030.
It is doubtful whether these capacities will all be realized.
Wolfgang Bernhart, Partner at Roland Berger, states that they do not expect to see overcapacity outside China.
The lack of materials, available talent and secured sales is impairing the performance of battery manufacturers and production processes worldwide.
Market imbalances are arising and are exacerbated by national legislation.
The development of production capacities and requirements differs starkly between countries, particularly the USA, China and Europe.
Wolfgang Bernhart states that in North America, new relevant market players will emerge.
In Europe, large amounts of battery capacity have been announced, but many of the companies are still very new, so the market is expected to consolidate.
China is building up tremendous overcapacities, resulting in low capacity utilization alongside export pressure.

Goods from China are unlikely to be imported into the USA due to a sizeable tax hit of 25.4% on battery imports from China and barriers Chinese manufacturers and suppliers face as a result of the Inflation Reduction Act.
Chinese manufacturers are increasingly focusing on the European market, which in turn increases the cost pressure on local producers.
Innovative product and process technologies have become increasingly important in the battery sector to counteract the years of experience that Asian factories already have.
There is a growing trend in patents coming out of the USA and Europe.
Companies must strike a balance between efficient, cost-effective and sustainable battery production.
In the medium-term future, striking this balance will be the only way for firms to secure their position in this dynamic market.
The Chair of Production Engineering of E-Mobility Components (PEM) of RWTH Aachen University was founded in 2014 by StreetScooter co-inventor Professor Achim Kampker.

PEM focuses on all aspects of the development, production, and recycling of battery systems and their components as well as fuel cells and the production of the electric drive train and entire vehicle concepts.
PEM employs a total of 78 researchers, 35 non-scientific staff and around 130 student assistants.
PEM collaborates with numerous renowned industrial partners with a focus on sustainability and cost reduction to aim for a seamless 'Innovation Chain' from basic research to large-scale production.
source_title: A sustained boom: Global battery market continues on a growth trajectory | Roland Berger

facts: NETL prepared a Draft EA (DOE/EA-2237) for the Solvay Battery-Grade Polyvinylidene Fluoride (PVDF) Manufacturing Facility project in Richmond County, Georgia.
The Draft EA evaluates the potential environmental consequences of DOE providing financial assistance to Solvay Specialty Polymers USA, LLC (a member of the Syensqo Group).
The project involves the construction of a chemical manufacturing facility for the production of PVDF (a highly non-reactive thermoplastic) for use in electric vehicle batteries.
The Draft Environmental Assessment was published on November 14, 2024.
source_title: DOE/EA-2237: Draft Environmental Assessment (November 2024) | Department of Energy

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider.
The company announced a new plan expected to expand its domestic manufacturing capacity to 24 Gigawatts.
The plan focuses on increasing production across its 11 U.S. manufacturing and battery component plants.
More than 2,500 people currently produce batteries at these facilities for multiple applications.
Battery applications include military and government, data centers, grid storage, automotive, and material handling.
The new investment is supported by existing advanced manufacturing production tax credits.
These tax credits contribute to the company's ability to accelerate capacity growth while supporting the domestic industrial base.
Mike Judd serves as President and Chief Executive Officer of Stryten Energy.
Mike Judd stated that investments will significantly increase gigawatt capacity across the company's footprint to support growing energy demand and achieve national goals of true energy resilience.
Mike Judd emphasized that American energy security must rely on domestic companies like Stryten manufacturing batteries for supply chains and critical infrastructure backup power.
Energy security requires continuous access to reliable power, necessitating scaling up production of all reliable battery chemistries.
Stryten advances energy storage in the U.S. with a unique suite of domestically manufactured battery solutions using advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow battery technology.
The company's plants are located in Kansas, Georgia, Iowa, Virginia, and Arkansas.
Plans for increasing gigawatt production capacity will bring hundreds of high-paying manufacturing jobs to surrounding communities.
Stryten operates a domestic, vertically integrated supply chain that provides battery components to its factories and the broader U.S. battery manufacturing industry.
Plants in Pennsylvania, New York, Indiana, Missouri, and Wisconsin ensure a reliable U.S. source for critical materials needed to manufacture batteries.
The investment plan includes increasing domestic processing capacity of recycled plastic from spent batteries.
Recycled plastic is used to produce new battery components for automobiles, forklifts, and locomotives.
This initiative contributes to the sustainable circular economy of the U.S. battery industry.
Stryten recently opened a lithium battery assembly plant in Georgia that supports military and industrial battery needs with proprietary lithium module technology.
The company is partnering with Dragonfly Energy, headquartered in Reno, Nevada, to bring advanced lithium batteries to automotive aftermarket and battery distributor partners.
Stryten announced the launch of Storion Energy LLC through its affiliate Stryten Critical E-Storage LLC.
Storion Energy was formed with a subsidiary of Largo Inc. (NASDAQ, TSX: LGO) and Largo Clean Energy Corp. (LCE).

Storion Energy focuses on removing barriers to entry for battery manufacturers to domestically source price-competitive electrolytes used in vanadium redox flow batteries (VRFB) for long-duration energy storage (LDES) applications in the U.S.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The company's energy storage technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
Stryten powers applications ranging from submarines and subcompacts to microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
source_title: Stryten Energy Unveils Plan to Add 10 Gigawatts of New U.S. Energy Storage Manufacturing Capacity

facts: Largo Inc. subsidiary Largo Clean Energy Corp. and Stryten Critical E-Storage, LLC affiliate of Stryten Energy LLC finalized the formation of Storion Energy LLC effective Friday, January 31, 2025.

Storion Energy is a joint venture focused on removing barriers to entry for battery manufacturers to domestically sourced, price-competitive components for the flow battery industry, including vanadium electrolyte.
The partnership leverages access to Largo’s high-quality vanadium supply, Largo Physical Vanadium Corp.’s innovative electrolyte leasing model, and Stryten’s U.S.-based manufacturing expertise.
Francesco D’Alessio, President of LCE, stated the closing represents a transformational step for Largo as it advances its investment strategy into the fast-growing vanadium flow battery market.

Storion Energy provides a direct channel for Largo’s high-quality vanadium products into the long duration energy storage sector, reinforcing Largo's position as a leading and reliable supplier of vanadium.

Through Storion, the partners are establishing a fully integrated vanadium electrolyte supply chain in North America to address a key challenge for vanadium flow battery adoption while unlocking new growth opportunities for Largo.

The combination of Stryten’s battery manufacturing expertise, Largo’s vanadium expertise, and LPV’s innovative electrolyte leasing model positions Storion Energy to deliver innovative, cost-competitive solutions for long duration energy storage.
Each of LCE and Stryten contributed certain vanadium flow battery-related assets and liabilities to Storion.
Stryten paid US$1 million in cash directly to Largo and will contribute US$6 million over time to fund Storion’s operations.
LCE and Stryten each hold a 50% equity interest in Storion, with customary pre-emption rights and anti-dilution protections.
Storion’s board composition will be generally proportional to ownership, with Stryten holding one additional seat.
Largo amended its Safekeeping and Supply Agreements with LPV and assigned its Safekeeping Agreement to Storion.
Largo and Storion entered a separate supply agreement granting Storion a right of first offer for vanadium products.
Required third party consents, which were closing conditions, were received.

Storion Energy intends to bring energy resilience and security to the U.S. by removing barriers to entry for battery manufacturers to domestically sourced, price-competitive electrolyte used in vanadium redox flow batteries for long-duration energy storage.
Storion will support scalable domestic electrolyte production to establish a fully integrated vertical supply chain for utility-scale VRFB LDES solutions.
VRFB technology is a safe and reliable option for Battery Energy Storage Systems that need to provide energy storage of four or more hours.
Storion has locations in Alpharetta, Georgia and Wilmington, Massachusetts.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, and partners with recognized companies to meet growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten Energy's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.

Largo Physical Vanadium Corp. aims to provide an exchange-traded investment alternative for investors interested in direct exposure to physical vanadium, a metal essential to achieving a greener world in key industries such as steel, aerospace and energy storage.
LPV's strategy includes owning and actively supplying vanadium to end users of vanadium flow batteries to advance integration of renewable energy in long duration storage.
Largo is a globally recognized supplier of high-quality vanadium and ilmenite products sourced from its Maracás Menchen Mine in Brazil.
As one of the world's largest primary vanadium producers, Largo produces critical materials that empower global industries including steel, aerospace, defense, chemical, and energy storage sectors.
Largo is strategically invested in the clean energy storage sector through its 50% ownership of Storion Energy.

links.context: More information on Largo Physical Vanadium Corp.

source_title: Largo and Stryten Energy Finalize Formation of Storion Energy to Strengthen U.S. Energy Resilience and Address Long Duration Energy Storage Needs

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG).
The TSLOTS line of aluminum structural framing systems is expanding operations into the Midwest region of the United States.

The TSLOTS aluminum framing system allows manufacturers to create light, durable, attractive, strong, versatile, and functional structures using custom T-slotted aluminum extrusions and connecting hardware.
Manufacturing and distribution for TSLOTS occurs from the Clearfield, Utah site.
A new leased facility is operated in conjunction with Bonnell Aluminum’s existing Niles, Michigan location.
The new leased facility provides 12,550 square feet of warehouse space for TSLOTS inventory.
This additional storage capacity will improve customer service levels for the Eastern and Midwestern United States.
Expansion aims to reduce shipping costs and shorten lead-times for customers in the Eastern and Midwestern United States.
Steve Silvester serves as the TSLOTS Sales Manager.
Customers in the Midwest and East requested a location closer to them over the years.
Shipping materials from the Midwest provides cost and lead-time benefits to customers.
TSLOTS has experienced growth over the past two decades.
Bonnell Aluminum operates locations across the country to service customers effectively.
TSLOTS is a full-service operation providing project design assistance, fabrication, custom packaging, and full assembly.
Bonnell Aluminum serves the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, electrical and distribution markets.
Branded product lines include TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum and Futura Transitions by Bonnell Aluminum.
Bonnell Aluminum has manufacturing facilities located in Newnan GA, Carthage TN, Niles MI, Elkhart IN, and Clearfield, UT.

facts: FREYR Battery announced the closing of its acquisition of Trina Solar’s U.S. solar manufacturing assets.
The transaction closed in accordance with a previously communicated timeline.
FREYR acquired Trina Solar’s 5 GW solar module manufacturing facility in Wilmer, Texas.
The Wilmer, Texas facility commenced production on November 1, 2024.
The facility is expected to ramp up to full production by H2 2025.
30% of estimated production volumes at the Wilmer facility are backed by firm offtake contracts with U.S. customers.
The transaction creates a commercial and operating platform to establish a leading integrated U.S.-owned and operated solar and battery storage company with a pathway for value enhancing growth.

The transaction leverages Trina Solar’s global leadership in the solar and renewable energy industries, established U.S. commercial presence, global supply chains, advantaged technology, and a strong track record of manufacturing and project execution for U.S. customers.

Total consideration to Trina Solar at closing comprised $100 million of cash, $50 million repayment of an intercompany loan, $150 million loan note, 9.9% of FREYR outstanding common stock, and an $80 million convertible loan note that would convert into an additional 11.5% of FREYR outstanding common stock after certain conditions are satisfied.
In addition to the consideration, FREYR acquired $235 million in indebtedness in connection with the Wilmer, Texas facility.
FREYR reiterates initial 2025 EBITDA guidance of $75 - $125 million.
FREYR expects to exit 2025 at full-year run rate EBITDA of $175 - $225 million.
Integrated solar module/solar cell production annual run rate EBITDA is expected to be $650 - $700 million.
Ramp up activities at the Wilmer, TX solar module plant continue as scheduled.
Line 1 of the Wilmer facility has been completed.
Commissioning on Line 2 commenced in November 2024.
FREYR expects the seven-line Wilmer facility to reach full production in H2 2025.
FREYR intends to submit transaction documentation in Q1 2025 to secure U.S. regulatory consents from relevant organizations, including CFIUS.
FREYR received $50 million from Encompass Capital Advisors LLC (“Encompass”) in exchange for the issuance of preferred stock in connection with this closing.
FREYR may receive an additional $50 million from Encompass upon proceeding to start of construction on a solar cell manufacturing facility.
FREYR is proceeding with its site selection process for a planned U.S. solar cell facility.
Start of construction for the planned U.S. solar cell facility is anticipated in Q2 2025.
FREYR is progressing with the implementation of a multi-phase strategic plan to establish a vertically integrated U.S. solar manufacturing footprint.
Site selection for the planned 5 GW U.S. solar cell manufacturing plant is underway.
The company is evaluating and pursuing debt and equity solutions to fund construction of the solar cell facility.
FREYR is still targeting a start of construction in Q2 2025 for the solar cell facility.
Anticipated first solar cell production at the new facility is H2 2026.
The creation of a U.S.-owned and operated company providing a turnkey solar technology solution is expected to solve a bottleneck for developers.
The project is expected to create up to 1,800 direct jobs.
The project is expected to satisfy local content requirements for U.S. solar projects.
The project is expected to competitively differentiate FREYR.

Daniel Barcelo, FREYR’s Chairman of the Board and CEO, stated that the closing marks the start of a new chapter as they execute their strategic plan to build a U.S.-based leader in the solar and storage markets.

Barcelo highlighted key objectives to create shareholder value and enhance competitive position in 2025, including the planned start of construction of the solar cell manufacturing facility and other emerging project development opportunities for FREYR and Trina to mutually pursue.

Total consideration to Trina Solar consists of $100 million cash, $50 million intercompany loan repayment, a $150 million loan note, 9.9% of FREYR outstanding common stock, and an $80 million convertible loan note converting to an additional 11.5% of FREYR outstanding common stock after certain conditions are satisfied.
FREYR secured a $100 million commitment for the issuance of preferred stock to certain funds/accounts managed by Encompass.
$50 million in preferred stock has been issued to Encompass-managed funds/accounts in connection with this closing.

$14.8 million was allocated for a private placement of 7.0% of FREYR outstanding common stock to Ms. Chunyan Wu, a co-founder and significant shareholder of Trina Solar, subject to certain conditions.
The transaction funds will be used for general operational and working capital purposes.
Santander served as financial advisor to the transaction.
Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom (UK) LLP served as legal advisor to the transaction.
Arnold & Porter, Ernst & Young, Clean Energy Associates, and Rystad Energy served as advisors to FREYR in support of the transaction.
Dorsey & Whitney LLP served as U.S. legal advisor to the transaction.
CICC served as financial advisor to Trina Solar.
Deloitte served as tax advisor to Trina Solar.
FREYR is a clean energy solutions provider building an integrated U.S. supply chain for solar and batteries.

In November 2024, FREYR announced the transformative transaction positioning it as one of the leading solar manufacturing companies in the U.S. with a complementary solar and battery storage strategy.
FREYR is based in the U.S. with plans to expand its operations in America.
The company is exploring value optimization opportunities across its portfolio of assets in Europe.
Forward-looking statements cover FREYR’s ability to establish a commercial presence in the U.S. solar market and potential benefits of acquiring Trina Solar US Holding Inc.

Projections include 2025 EBITDA guidance, run-rate EBITDA figures, timelines for post-closing activities, securing financing for the solar cell facility, ramping up Wilmer production by H2 2025, and estimated production volumes backed by firm offtake contracts.
Projections include start of solar cell manufacturing in Q2 2025 and construction targeting start of production in H2 2026.

Statements cover integration of U.S. solar module and solar cell capacity, timeline for regulatory consents, ability to become a leading U.S. solar module producer, establishment of a domestic manufacturing footprint, creation of 1,800 local jobs, and integration of U.S. solar and battery energy storage system manufacturing.

Risk factors include potential legal proceedings post-closing, transaction disruption risks, inability to timely secure regulatory consents, transaction costs, changes in laws/regulations, economic/competitive factors, risk that Chinese equity ownership may impact U.S. solar cell facility development, increases to commodity pricing or US tariff/countervailing duty levels, and operational risks from commissioning/ramp-up.

Additional information on risk factors is set forth in SEC filings including Form S-3 filed Jan 4, 2024; Form S-4 filed Sept 8, 2023 with amendments Oct 13, 19, 31, 2023; Form 10-K filed Feb 29, 2024; and Forms 10-Q filed May 8, Aug 9, Nov 12, 2024.
source_title: FREYR Battery Closes Transformative Acquisition of Trina Solar’s U.S. Manufacturing Assets

facts: Automotive parts company Hwashin announced it will build a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The project will create more than 460 new jobs.
The project involves an investment of more than $176 million.
The initiative resulted from three years of planning and 15 months of ongoing site preparation by the local community.
Dr. Fred Williams serves as Chair of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority (DLCDA).
Hwashin will be the seventh international company to call Dublin and Laurens County home.
The facility is situated on a 331-acre site at the intersection of U.S. Highway 441 and Georgia State Route 257.
Sixty acres of the parcel have been secured for Hwashin's use.
The DLCDA prepared the property to undergo development reviews required for state-approved certification through the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) Program.
The GRAD program markets pre-qualified, “ready for development” industrial sites to business and site selectors as a one-stop shop.
The Dublin site was the first GRAD-certified site to earn the "Select" status.
The Authority hired an engineering firm to continue site improvements after receiving detailed engineering questions from prospective companies.
Ryan Waldrep serves as President of the DLCDA.
The Authority obtained permits for water access and collaborated with the Georgia Department of Transportation to conduct traffic studies.
A mass grading project moved enough cubic yards of material to fill a line of dump trucks stretching over 130 miles from the Dublin site to the 17th Street Bridge in Atlanta.
During a site visit, a company consultant noted that the ongoing clearing and grading made it easy to envision the facility on the property.
The DLCDA will install water, sewer, a road, and a retention pond on the site to service future developments.
GRAD Certified sites have been critical in securing key projects, including about half of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s suppliers and numerous battery supply chain projects.

facts: TDK Components USA, Inc. was recognized by EPA Regional Administrator Jimmy Palmer as a recipient of the 2008 Performance Track Environmental Performance Award.
TDK is one of two companies in the Southeast and just five nationwide to receive this award.
TDK was also recognized for acceptance to the highest level of the Partnership for a Sustainable Georgia, an environmental leadership program for Georgia businesses.

Jimmy Palmer stated that TDK's outstanding commitment to consistently exceed environmental requirements allowed it to continuously improve on its environmental performance and reduce its environmental footprint.

Suzanne Burnes, Manager of the Partnership for a Sustainable Georgia, noted that members like TDK lead in implementing sound environmental practices while recognizing that what is good for the environment can also be good for the bottom line.
TDK manufactures multi-layer ceramic chip capacitors at its Peachtree City, Ga., facility.
From 2003-2006, TDK increased production by 28 percent while achieving specific environmental results.
During that period, TDK reduced total water use by 38 percent, or more than 1.1 million gallons, by analyzing electroplating process line rinse flow rates.

TDK reduced non-transportation energy use by 17 percent and associated greenhouse gas emissions by nearly 700 tons of carbon dioxide through the installation of a more efficient lighting system, upgrading to more efficient equipment, and consolidating operations to two buildings.
TDK reduced the use of copper paste by 36 percent, nearly 3,500 pounds, and the use of caustic soda by 53 percent, about 21,600 pounds.

TDK President Masatoshi Fujimoto stated that being selected among peers to receive the award represents confirmation of numerous improvement activities implemented at TDK toward reducing their environmental footprint.
Since its inception in June 2000, Performance Track membership grew to more than 500 facilities in 49 states and Puerto Rico, with members making more than 3,500 commitments to the environment.

Through combined environmental efforts, Performance Track member facilities reported cumulative reductions in water use of 5.2 billion gallons, greenhouse gas reductions of 309,780 metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent, increases in recycled materials use by 559,991 tons, and conservation of 16,809 acres of habitat.

The Performance Track program encourages facilities with strong environmental records to exceed legal requirements, typically setting four public, measurable goals to improve air, water, and land quality.

The Partnership for a Sustainable Georgia was established by the Pollution Prevention Assistance Division of the Georgia Department of Natural Resources to foster environmental leadership and recognize superior environmental performance.

The Partnership offers incentives including cost savings through increased efficiencies, potential regulatory flexibility, free technical assistance and training, and access to networking and mentoring among peers, state and local officials.

links.context: Program information, summary of member benefits, application guidelines, and facility listing for Performance Track
More information on the Partnership for a Sustainable Georgia

source_title: 08/19/2008: TDK Receives EPA Award for Reducing Its Metro Atlanta Manufacturing Facility’s Environmental Footprint
