facts: Polk County has lost only one employer of significant size since the recession began.
Rockmart-based Meggitt Polymers & Composites is the county's largest employer, manufacturing aircraft fuel tanks and doubling in size from about 600 employees to more than 1,200 over five years.
Meggitt Polymers & Composites announced plans to add another 56 new jobs in Rockmart.
Meggitt Polymers & Composites was recognized as the Large Manufacturer of the Year in 2011 by the Technical College System of Georgia and the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Polk County is located 50 miles from Atlanta, 85 miles from Birmingham, Ala., and 83 miles from Chattanooga, Tenn.
Interstates 20 and 75 are accessible from Polk County via four-lane highways U.S. 27 and U.S. 278.

Manufacturing is Polk County's largest industry, with The HON Company (furniture), Jefferson Southern Corporation (auto parts), Can Am Yarns (yarn), and Advance Storage Products (storage systems) among the top employers.
Jefferson Southern Corporation occupies 250,000 square feet in a dual-rail industrial park.
Nordic Logistics & Warehousing occupies 300,000 square feet in the same dual-rail park served by Norfolk Southern and CSX.
The $55-million Paulding Northwest Atlanta Airport opened in late 2010 just across the county line.
The Paulding Northwest Atlanta Airport is expected to have a major impact on economic development in Polk County over the next five to 10 years.
The Paulding Northwest Atlanta Airport has available land around it to attract large aerospace companies.
Polk Medical Center, a new $25-million, 25-bed critical access hospital, will break ground between Rockmart and Cedartown by the end of this year (2012).
The Polk Medical Center project will bring about 30 new healthcare jobs to the county, mostly in surgical nursing.
A medical office building and medical mall are planned for the Polk Medical Center campus, projected to be complete by January 2016.
Two publicly owned industrial parks in Rockmart and Cedartown encompass 298 acres of prime industrial land.
The Cedartown North Business Park has 115 acres of light industrial land just off the U.S. 27 bypass, one mile from U.S. 278.
The Development Authority of Polk County purchased an additional 220 acres adjacent to the Cedartown North Business Park's eastern boundary.
A 105,000-square-foot spec industrial building will break ground on a 15-acre pad in the park by the end of this year (2012).
The Rockmart 101 Business Park contains an additional 183 acres of industrial land.
Jefferson Southern Corporation occupies 250,000 square feet in the Rockmart 101 Business Park.
Nordic Logistics & Warehousing occupies 300,000 square feet in the Rockmart 101 Business Park.
Cedartown is a Department of Community Affairs-designated Main Street City following a redevelopment plan that includes $1.6 million invested in new downtown streetscaping.

The city of Cedartown offers an incentive package including county and city enterprise zones that double property tax relief for restaurants and opportunity zone tax credits worth up to $3,500 per new employee.

A group of 16 community leaders from throughout Polk County has been working with the Development Authority of Polk County and the Fanning Institute at the University of Georgia since last September on a countywide strategic economic development plan.
The strategic economic development plan aims to address workforce challenges, attract new industries, and create a world-class community.
Georgia Northwestern Technical College in Rockmart offers the Quick Start program, providing customized training to students at no cost to businesses.

Coosa Valley Technical Institute partnered with The HON Company and the Polk County School District to create Project Success, launching in the fall to give 30 at-risk high school students a job to prepare them for a career after graduation.

Southern Company and Georgia Power partnered with Georgia Northwestern Technical College to create the Instrumentation and Controls Academy in Polk County, which trains 32 people every year for high-tech jobs in the power generation industry.
A student with Instrumentation and Controls Academy training can earn up to $60,000 a year two years after high school graduation.
Polk County passed a Special Purpose Local Option Sales Tax (SPLOST) in 2008 and renewed it through 2018.
$165,000 was recently approved by the Polk County Commission for infrastructure improvements to help existing industries expand.
Polk County's population was 41,475 according to the 2010 Census, with Cedartown at 9,750 and Rockmart at 4,199.
Polk County's per capita income in 2009 was $25,185 compared to Georgia's state average of $35,215.
The unemployment rate in Polk County was 9.0 percent in April 2012, compared to Georgia's 8.9 percent.
Top employers in Polk County include Meggitt Polymers & Composites, The HON Company, Tip Top Poultry, AT&T Communications, and Angelica Textile Services.

facts: JTEKT North America is expanding its operations in Greenville County, South Carolina.
The company will invest $1.75 million to establish an operational headquarters at CU-ICAR.
The expansion will create 27 new jobs in Greenville, S.C.

JTEKT Corporation was established in January 2006 through the merger of Koyo Seiko Co., Ltd., a world-class bearing manufacturer, and Toyoda Machine Works, Ltd., a machine tool manufacturer excelling in world-leading technologies.
JTEKT is now a trusted systems supplier of JTEKT brand automotive steering and driveline components, Koyo brand bearings, and Toyoda machine tools.
JTEKT employs more than 44,000 workers worldwide.
JTEKT North America employs more than 7,000 associates at 14 manufacturing facilities plus additional technical and sales centers in North America.
The company will locate its North American operational headquarters alongside its Koyo bearing product technical center at CU-ICAR, an advanced-technology research campus for the automotive industry.
The expansion will occupy facilities at both 1 and 7 Research Drive in Greenville, S.C.
The expansion is expected to be fully operational in the first quarter of 2016.
Hiring for the 27 new positions has already begun.
The Coordinating Council for Economic Development has approved job development credits related to the project.

Mike Davidson, COO of JTEKT North America, stated that their operational headquarters in Greenville County and CU-ICAR will provide an efficient base to support their extensive footprint in North America.
Mike Davidson cited the availability of professional and technical talent, proximity to the majority of their production facilities, and the quality of life in the Upstate as reasons for the move.
Greenville County Council Chairman Dr. Bob Taylor stated that seeing a company like JTEKT grow beyond their initial investment confirms they are doing things right in Greenville.
Dr. Bob Taylor highlighted Greenville's business climate, infrastructure, and talented workforce as factors making it a globally-recognized destination for the automotive industry.

Clemson University President James P. Clements stated that at CU-ICAR, their goal is to provide a dynamic environment where global leaders in the automotive industry can be located together and work together.
James P. Clements noted that it is exciting to see JTEKT call the CU-ICAR campus home, calling it a win for Clemson students, faculty and staff, JTEKT, and South Carolina’s economy.
The company's new North American Operational headquarters will be housed at 7 Research Drive, Greenville, S.C.
source_title: JTEKT North America expanding Upstate operations - Who's On The Move

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG (Kautex), a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company, announced it has received an order from a leading automotive OEM for a full-BEV thermoplastic composite lower battery housing unit.
The lower housing is part of the company’s Pentatonic battery enclosure portfolio, facilitating battery electric and hybrid electric vehicle production.
The Kautex Pentatonic product line offers lightweight, customizable EV solutions made from thermoplastic composite or composite metal hybrids.
These solutions enhance the thermal management of the battery pack and contribute to overall lightweight design, boosting vehicle performance and extending driving range.

The lower housing seals and protects the vehicle battery while meeting extensive structural and stringent thermal requirements, such as crush and bottom impact testing, and external and internal fire tests.
It will be produced for a major BEV platform, spanning across multiple vehicle brands.

This Kautex Pentatonic solution offers advantages over conventional metal counterparts, with lower cost through a reduction in components due to its high functional integration and thus a leaner bill of material.
The thermoplastic composite material helps to lower the carbon footprint of the part, while the lightweight housing contributes to reducing carbon emissions while driving.
This multi-material approach provides corrosion resistance with no compromise in performance throughout the lifespan of the vehicle.
Joerg Rautenstrauch serves as President & CEO of Kautex.
This order marks Kautex’s second Pentatonic business win, following its initial success in January 2023 with a full-BEV underbody protection skid plate that entered serial production in 2024.
The Pentatonic product line has garnered numerous awards, including the SPE Grand Innovation Award in Europe, as well as the Lingxuan and Gasgoo Innovation Awards in China.
Kautex has deployed three on-road test vehicles in the US, Japan and Europe to validate the performance of full-BEV and hybrid Pentatonic enclosures across various terrains and weather conditions.
Collectively, these test vehicles have been road-tested for over 100,000 miles.
Kautex operates as a Tier One automotive supplier with 30 plants in 13 countries.
Kautex is a pioneer in the design and manufacture of traditional and hybrid fuel systems, engine camshafts and plastic industrial packaging solutions.

For the era of new mobility, Kautex is expanding its portfolio to offer thermoplastic composite and composite-metal hybrid battery systems as well as assisted and autonomous drive vehicle cleaning systems.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
Textron owns powerful brands including Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO and Textron Systems.
source_title: Textron Inc.  - Kautex Textron Wins Significant BEV Battery Housing Contract with Leading Automotive OEM

facts: Manufacturers and retailers had to quickly find scalable solutions to create safer environments for employees and customers during the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic.
Employers required solutions to facilitate social distancing and automate occupancy monitoring and reporting due to varying and shifting guidance.
Employers reviewed common spaces like shared workstations, locker rooms, bathrooms, and breakrooms through a new lens for safety.
Kautex Textron partnered with Banner Engineering to implement an occupancy monitoring solution that alerts employees when capacity is reached and common areas are safe.
Banner’s Occupancy Monitoring System automates counting and notifications using Q45 paired sensors.
The sensors detect individuals entering and exiting at single-door and multiple-entry locations.
The system was installed quickly without alterations to existing infrastructure.
It provides notifications when traffic meets or exceeds a programmed maximum capacity.
A Banner DXM wireless controller collects data from each sensor, logs it, and communicates status updates to a Banner Direct Select operator interface.
Employee and customer lights trigger based on set occupancy limits.
The system can be configured for real-time text and email notifications to management with an added cell plan or network connection.
Aggregated data automatically uploads to Banner’s Cloud Data Services platform, which provides visualization and analysis tools.

Customers can use the platform to track behavior at locations, monitor and compare behaviors across different plant areas and multiple locations, compare customer traffic to sales, and make data-driven decisions about employee scheduling.
Highly visible LED lights alert employees and customers on safe access to common areas.
Green and red indicators can include icons, with custom light selections offering various color options and animations.
The solution is scalable and flexible, allowing devices to be easily added, moved, or removed to meet changing needs.

The solution is available as a kit containing a DXM Series wireless controller, wireless Q45 directional detection sensor pair, Direct Select wireless operator interface, high-visibility indicator light, power supplies, and mounting brackets.
Kautex Textron successfully provided its employees with a safer working environment and increased peace of mind through the new system.
Vince Vlaminck serves as Director EHS Global at Kautex Textron.

Vince Vlaminck stated: 'As we, along with other industries, battle against COVID-19 and work to provide our employees a safe return to work, we’ve partnered with Banner Engineering to bring Industry 4.0 solutions to social distancing and regular disinfection processes. The results bring a level of professionalism and increased visual management—and support a message to our employees that we are committed to your safety and willing to invest accordingly.'
Banner Engineering partnered with Digi-Key Electronics to stock several versions of their new Occupancy Solutions Kits for purchase.
source_title: Banner Engineering Partners with Kautex Textron for COVID-19 Safe Work Solutions | Industrial Equipment News

facts: Mack Trucks manufactures the all-electric Mack LR Electric for refuse and eMobility applications.

Mack's vehicle lineup spans regional haul, highway, refuse, and vocational applications across multiple models.
Mack Pioneer is designed for highway and regional haul applications, positioning itself as a new standard for long-haul operations.
The Mack Pioneer features cab configurations, driver comfort enhancements, and new efficiency technology aimed at saving thousands in fuel costs.
Mack TerraPro is purpose-built for vocational demands, supporting specifications for concrete pumpers, refuse bodies, or specialty applications.
source_title: Product Lineup | Mack Trucks

canonical_name: Mack Trucks Lehigh Valley Operations plant
facts: Mack’s Lehigh Valley Operations plant is a more-than-century-old manufacturing site that employs roughly 3,000 workers and remains a cornerstone of the region’s industrial economy.
The facility has long been a symbol of heavy-duty truck manufacturing in the area and a major source of local jobs.
In 2025, Mack Trucks announced layoffs of between 250 and 350 workers at the plant, citing declining demand and market instability linked in part to tariffs and broader trucking industry conditions.
The company has also warned that trade policy shifts could continue to affect production and orders.
Union representatives, workers, and plant leadership participated in the rally, emphasizing the importance of manufacturing jobs in the Lehigh Valley.
Speakers referenced ongoing contract negotiations and workforce concerns while noting the plant’s continued role in the regional economy.
Trump repeatedly pointed to Mack Trucks as an example of American industrial strength, saying the company has been “building the heavy-duty machinery that keeps our economy rolling.”
Trump promoted tariffs on imported trucks and vehicles, arguing they are necessary to protect domestic manufacturers and keep production in the United States.

Trump cited ongoing and proposed projects in Pennsylvania, including manufacturing and pharmaceutical investments, as examples of economic growth, even as layoffs at Mack highlight continuing volatility in the industry.
Trump addressed energy policy, calling for expanded oil, gas, and coal production, particularly in Pennsylvania.
source_title: Trump rally at Mack Trucks in Macungie highlights GOP support, manufacturing push amid local uncerta – Lehigh Valley Press

facts: The Mack Anthem has been redesigned and repurposed to serve as the company’s new regional haul tractor.
The Anthem relinquished its title of on-highway flagship to the recently introduced Mack Pioneer.
The new Anthem was introduced June 25.
The new Anthem shares design similarities with the all-new Pioneer both internally and externally.
The Anthem's bumper-to-back-of-cab (BBC) measurement has been shortened from 117 inches to 113 inches to afford greater maneuverability.
The cab has been widened by nine inches to increase interior space and driver comfort.
A 45-degree wheel cut improves maneuverability.
A more sloped hood improves forward visibility by about 12%.
The original Mack Anthem was available in only two sleeper configurations: a short 48-inch service sleeper and a 70-inch studio.
The lack of intermediate sleeper options previously cost Mack business in applications like bulk haul.
The new Anthem offers more configurations including low-roof sleeper, day cab, mid-roof sleeper in 44- and 64-inch lengths, and rigid day cab.
Jonathan Randall is president of Mack Trucks North America.
Combined with the Pioneer's 76-inch sleeper, Mack now has every sleeper configuration between the Pioneer and Anthem to address all application needs.
Regional customers can dress down the truck’s aerodynamics to prioritize maneuverability over fuel economy.
The mid-roof sleeper is more than seven feet high, providing ample headroom for taller drivers.
Brian Wright is a Mack Trucks consultant.
Customers can opt to remove the chassis fairing and air dam to allow for greater ride height to navigate city streets, potholes, curbs, and rugged terrain.
Canadian customers may forego small fuel economy gains in wintry conditions for greater ground clearance due to snow and ice buildup.
The Anthem's BBC is about a foot shorter than the Pioneer’s, with four fewer inches between the bumper and front axle for a better turning radius.
The hood is now made of composite material rather than fiberglass and comes in three sections for easier and more cost-effective repairs.
The composite hood is lighter, stronger, and has better paint adhesion.
A larger radiator opening improves cooling capabilities.
Six different wheelbases are available ranging from 16 to 19 feet in length.
Blake Routh is senior product manager of on-highway trucks at Mack.
The Pioneer features a significantly longer 125-inch BBC and availability of a 76-inch sleeper, while the Anthem is limited to a 64-inch sleeper.
Prioritizing fuel efficiency points toward the Pioneer, which offers up to an 11% improvement over the old Anthem.
Prioritizing maneuverability points toward the Anthem.
The new Anthem features the newly named Mack Protect suite of active safety systems combining camera and radar with braking capabilities.
Mack Protect includes frontal collision and rollover protection, adaptive cruise control, automatic emergency braking, pedestrian detection, and road sign detection.
Side safety features include blind spot assistance, lane departure assistance, side pedestrian detection, lane centering, and oversteer guidance through Command Steer.
All lights are LED throughout and heated for colder climates, with additional LED fog lights and cornering lights.
The Anthem takes new electronic air brakes from the Pioneer, which are easier to control and reduce driver injury risk upon exiting before brake application due to automatic activation.
Power is supplied by the newly named MP13 (previously MP8) 13-liter engine in two variations.
The variable geometry turbo (VGT) version produces up to 515 hp and 1,900 lb.-ft. of torque.
The MP13 HE (high efficiency) uses turbocompounding to convert previously wasted heat to engine torque, available with power ratings up to 505 hp and 1,950 lb.-ft. of torque.
Engine design improvements include a shorter seven-wave piston, a new injector, and a longer connecting rod for almost total combustion in the cylinder.
More thorough combustion leaves less soot for the aftertreatment system to deal with.
Todd Shakespeare is head of leasing and service solution sales at Mack.
Most software updates can be done over the air to reduce downtime.
In ideal conditions, oil change intervals are stretched from 75,000 to 90,000 miles (120,000 to 144,000 km).
Chassis lube intervals are stretched from 15,000 to 30,000 miles (24,000 to 48,000 km).
These maintenance interval improvements offer a maintenance savings of about US$685 a year.
The truck typically requires less maintenance than other models even in harsher environments.
Kent Worthington is sales development manager for powertrain solutions at Mack.
The Mack powertrain learns routes and optimizes shifting based on terrain and load conditions.
Field testing proved better performance in stop-and-go driving and less downshifting required on hills due to increased torque available at lower RPMs.
Increased torque at lower RPMs reduces driver fatigue as well as component wear.
The new Anthem will be publicly shown for the first time in October at the American Trucking Associations’ annual Management Conference & Exhibition.
Orders will be taken as early as August when the price book is released.
Production will begin in January 2026 with customer deliveries commencing soon thereafter.
The truck is available in Mack’s Truck Builder configurator for customers to play with specs and configurations.
source_title: Mack’s Anthem to live on, with completely new design and purpose - Truck News

facts: NHTSA Campaign Number 21E039000 addresses Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) modules that may short circuit.
An electrical short circuit increases the risk of an engine compartment fire while parked or driving.
Mando America Corporation is the manufacturer of the recalled ABS modules.
The ABS module may leak brake fluid and cause a short circuit.
Vehicle manufacturers have developed and will perform the remedy procedure for the recall.

June 2018: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) notified Mando America Corporation of a Quality Information Report concerning 2014 Hyundai Santa Fe Sport vehicle’s ABS module overheating, issued by Hyundai Motor America.
Mando requested KMMG to recover the parts for analysis and received three ABS modules from incident vehicles listed in Hyundai’s QIR.
Mando did not observe any significant failure in the ABS modules that could lead to a fire and informed KMMG that a potential root cause cannot be determined by component inspection alone.
Mando suggested KMMG conduct a vehicle inspection to identify a root cause of the issue.

March 2019: Mando conducted a joint inspection with HMA and Hyundai Motor Company on a repurchased 2015 Santa Fe Sport vehicle to identify a possible source of an engine compartment fire, but severe damage prevented determination.
Hyundai Motor Company recovered the ABS module for further study by Mando.
From additional inspection, Mando observed a leakage of brake fluid inside the recovered ABS module but found no quality deviation in its manufacturing process or history relative to this issue.
April 2019—December 2019: Mando received three additional incident ABS modules from HMC and inspected those recovered parts.

Mando observed brake fluid leakage inside the three additional modules but could not find a correlation between the leakage and a compartment fire, leaving an approximate failure mechanism or root cause undetermined.
June 2020: Kia Motors Corporation sent Mando Corporation of Korea an ABS module involved in an electrical failure for a 2015 Kia Sorento.
Mando Korea evaluated the recovered ABS module with KMC and observed brake fluid leakage and internal damages.
August 27, 2020: Kia Motors America filed a Part 573 defect information report identifying a defect in certain Kia Optima and Kia Sorento vehicles manufactured at KMMG (Recall No. 20V-519).
HMA filed a Part 573 defect information report identifying a defect in certain Hyundai Santa Fe Sport vehicles manufactured at KMMG (Recall No. 20V-520).
September 2020—Jan 2021: Mando proactively informed General Motors about the HMA and KMA recalls on Sep 3, 2020.
GM conducted a safety investigation concerning their vehicles equipped with the same HECU.
In January, GM informed Mando that a field action is not necessary for GM vehicles equipped with the Mando HECU.
January 2021—Present: Mando Korea received four ABS modules from KMC recovered through KMA’s recall inspection procedure and found signs of internal leaks.

Mando Korea and Mando are continuing an investigation with KMC to determine the root cause of the leakage, including verifying component failures, brake system configurations, and user driving conditions.
April 2021: NHTSA requested Mando to submit a 573 Defect Report to identify purchasers of the recalled HECU equipment.
On April 30, 2021, Mando filed a 573 Defect Report to NHTSA to identify vehicle manufacturers who purchased the recalled HECU equipment, although Mando has not identified a defect in their HECU.
To date, Mando has not yet determined the presence of a defect in the HECU/ABS modules.
The affected equipment is listed as MANDO ANTI-LOCK BREAK SYSTEM with no part number or production dates provided.

facts: Opelika Mayor Gary Fuller recently announced that two existing Opelika manufacturing companies will be expanding and creating more jobs.
Car Tech will invest more capital to build and expand its current facility.
Car Tech is a Tier 1 supplier for BMW Group.
Car Tech is located in Opelika’s Fox Run Business Park along the I-85 corridor.
Car Tech's current facility has over 388,000 square feet for stamping and assembly operations.
Car Tech plans to invest $18.3 million for an additional 85,800 square feet of warehouse and production space.
Car Tech plans to purchase additional manufacturing equipment as part of the expansion.
Car Tech Plant Manager Shea Phillips stated that continued growth has come from both new customers and existing customers.
Car Tech currently employs 115 employees.
The Car Tech expansion will create 15 new team member positions filling warehousing, production, and technical roles.
Mayor Gary Fuller stated that Opelika is clearly focused on providing opportunities for current businesses to grow and bring additional good-paying jobs to the community.
City of Opelika Economic Development Director Lori Huguley called Car Tech a great community partner and a testament to the local workforce and business climate.
HL Mando America Corporation will invest $11.1 million to upgrade its current facility.
The HL Mando expansion will create 26 new jobs.
HL Mando’s Opelika operations produce brake calipers and suspension systems for customers throughout the U.S.
HL Mando's parent company, Mando Corporation, is a leading global automotive parts manufacturer specializing in chassis components and systems.
Mando Corporation was the first manufacturer in Korea to develop advanced products including ABS, ESP, CDC, ESC, and EPS.
Mando Corporation is headquartered in South Korea and operates 20 manufacturing plants and 18 R&D centers in 10 countries.
Mando Corporation employs over 11,700 associates worldwide.
Mayor Fuller noted HL Mando is one of Opelika's largest manufacturing employers and expressed appreciation for the employment opportunities provided to citizens.
Economic Development Director Lori Huguley added that the city appreciates HL Mando's investment and supports their continued growth and success.
A publisher's note corrected a previous article's error, clarifying Car Tech's investment is $18.3 million and HL Mando's investment is $11.1 million.
source_title: Opelika Preparing For Multi-Million-Dollar Industry Expansions - The Observer

facts: Stemco and its parent company — EnPro Industries — have sold a Rome, Georgia brake business to MAT HD, a subsidiary of MAT Holdings.
The sold business produces brake shoes, brake kits and lined shoes for medium- and heavy-duty truck and bus applications.
It will now become part of the brake products group at MAT, which manufactures and distributes products for the automotive, hardware, and power equipment sectors.

Stemco — a producer of commercial wheel end, braking and suspension components, as well as tire and mileage solutions — has facilities in Texas, Michigan, Kentucky, Tennessee, Ohio, Canada, Australia, China and Mexico.
EnPro manufactures sealing technologies and engineered products.
MAT is headquartered in Long Grove, Ill., and has 15,000 employees around the world.
Craig Gordon, MAT HD president, stated: 'We are thrilled with the opportunity to grow our presence in the commercial vehicle braking segment.'
Craig Gordon added: 'We believe the acquisition of the Rome manufacturing facility provides a great complement to our existing product offering in our heavy-duty portfolio.'
Terms of the deal were not disclosed.
source_title: MAT HD acquires Stemco brake business - Truck News

entity_name: DHL Group / Fraunhofer Institute for Material Flow and Logistics (IML)
facts: Introduction of battery-electric trucks faces significant challenges including shortage of charging stations, limited available space, and narrow operational windows.
Growing interest exists in automated battery swapping technology that decouples charging from vehicle operation.
Fraunhofer Institute for Material Flow and Logistics (IML) produced a study identifying situations where battery swapping creates added value and remaining obstacles.
Battery swapping involves automated replacement of discharged traction batteries with fully charged ones at specially equipped stations.
Swappable battery modules are removed from vehicles from below or the side and replaced.
Key benefit: vehicle spends short time at service station while batteries charge separately, reducing grid strain and costs.
Decoupling charging from operation is especially beneficial for heavy-duty road freight transport, enabling high vehicle availability while keeping grid load manageable.

Fraunhofer IML produced white paper titled “Battery Swapping for Heavy-Duty Commercial Vehicles as a Complement to Wired Charging” on behalf of logistics and waste management companies including DHL Group.
Project supported by Working Group on CO₂-Free Logistics of the Federal Association For Freight Forwarding and Logistics Germany (DSLV).

Study integrates technological, business, and energy system perspectives based on 22 expert interviews across value chain participants including users, station operators, battery manufacturers, grid operators, and vehicle manufacturers.
Heavy-duty vehicles account for more than a quarter of greenhouse gas emissions from road transport in the EU.
Heavy-duty vehicles account for over six percent of the EU’s total emissions.

In 2024, stricter CO₂ regulations took effect implementing staggered reduction targets for new heavy-duty vehicles: minus 45% by 2030, minus 65% by 2035, and minus 90% by 2040 compared to 2019 reference year.

Electrifying heavy-duty trucks poses greater technical and operational challenge than passenger cars due to high daily mileage, tight time windows, large energy required per stop, and lack of space at high-traffic locations.
Industry feedback indicates depot charging remains cornerstone of operations; widespread adoption of electric trucks will likely be driven primarily by fast wired charging.
Battery swapping is beneficial when three factors converge: time-sensitive processes, predictable routes, and limited grid or space resources.
Study identifies hub-to-hub transport in multi-shift operation as application area with fixed, predictable routes and daily driving ranges of 400 to 500 kilometers.
Six- to ten-minute swap replaces wired charging session of about 50 to 70 minutes for hub-to-hub transport.

Study identifies 24/7 internal transport operations with just-in-time requirements as application area where exchange occurs concurrently with loading dock and yard operations without additional downtime.
Potentially autonomous 24/7 logistics can use battery swapping to ensure continuous power supply without driver or operational downtime.
Swapping stations have large storage capacities and controllable energy consumption, relieving pressure on power grid.
Swapping stations require significantly less space than conventional charging stations for heavy-duty trucks.
Cost-effectiveness of battery swapping depends heavily on usage profile, system parameters, and business model.
Key cost factor is interplay between energy price, swap time, and resulting opportunity costs rather than energy price alone.
Quick battery swap significantly reduces costly downtime.
European model calculation concludes swap-capable trucks can currently offer cost advantage of up to 17% over vehicles charged at stations.
Cost advantage may persist through 2035 (up to 10%).
Data on actual operating costs of heavy-duty battery-swap fleets is currently limited, and study acknowledges uncertainties in parameterization.
Most significant barrier for newcomers is lack of cross-manufacturer mechanical interoperability.
Currently no standardized interchange or interface format exists for different vehicle and battery types.
Outstanding issues include ownership and liability, investment needs for automated swap stations and battery pools, and invoicing compliance with legal calibration requirements.
Station mechanics pose single-point-of-failure risks, necessitating redundancy and fallback wired charging option.
Europe is lagging behind by international standards; China uses battery swapping on industrial scale.
In 2024, approximately 28,700 swap-capable heavy-duty trucks were registered in China.
Roughly one in three newly registered heavy-duty electric vehicles in China was swap-capable in 2024.
Faster implementation in China attributed to standardization initiatives, government regulation, and stronger market dynamics.
Local conditions in China cannot be directly applied to Europe.
Recommendations include launching open-standard corridor pilot projects with anchor fleets as complement to high-power charging rollout.
Future expansion can include megawatt charging systems to address technical, regulatory, and business issues under real-world conditions.
Findings support measures to promote testing of battery swapping outlined in 2030 Charging Infrastructure Master Plan of German Federal Ministry of Transport.
Plan requires vehicle manufacturers and battery producers to collaborate with freight forwarding and logistics companies to develop binding standards.
DHL Freight is examining ways to accelerate electrification of heavy-duty vehicles in practice using battery swapping for specific use cases.
Battery swapping is not intended to replace plug-in charging but serve as potential component for well-defined, predictable transport operations.
DHL promotes emissions reductions through currently available solutions including using battery-electric trucks for distribution and testing new technologies.
DHL offers GoGreen Plus Flex sustainable offer allowing customers to reduce shipments’ carbon footprint through insetting measures.
Contributing to studies like this white paper is part of DHL’s approach to developing practical solutions where need for action is urgent.
Wired charging remains primary path for widespread adoption, but battery swapping has place in high-frequency, schedule-driven applications.
Battery swapping reduces downtime to just a few minutes and enables grid- and cost-optimized charging at station.
Technology requires resolution of interoperability, invoicing, ownership, and liability issues to reach full potential.
Timing is crucial; if Europe addresses issues too late, non-European standards could set pace.
Decarbonizing road freight requires combination of technologies including battery swapping, fast-charging infrastructure, and consistent emissions reductions.
Battery swapping currently takes about five to ten minutes for heavy-duty commercial vehicles.
Main advantage is significant reduction in downtime compared to 50 to 70 minute cable charging sessions.
Decoupling energy consumption from route planning increases vehicle availability.
Battery swapping considered supplementary option to depot and fast charging, particularly useful for predictable routes with frequent traffic.
Station utilization rate of over 43% represents tipping point for economic viability.
All calculations on operating costs are subject to uncertainty due to limited data.
source_title: Battery Swapping in Road Freight: Opportunities and Obstacles

facts: Susanne Reynolds has served as the director of the Development Authority of Early County for two years as of January 2, 2023.
The Development Authority office had been vacant for approximately six years prior to her appointment.

Since 2023, Susanne Reynolds and the Georgia Economic Development Region 10 representative have conducted site visits to Georgia-Pacific, Nucor, Brewers Manufacturing, Early Trucking Company, Southern AgComm, Nantze Springs, Blakely Barns, Olam Edible Nuts, and Golden Peanut Company.
The authority plans site visits with existing industries every other month to share local and state incentive information.
According to MEAG Power, the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced that for the third consecutive year, Georgia broke records for economic development during Fiscal Year 2023.
In FY23, total investments in facility expansions and new locations across Georgia totaled more than $24 billion.
FY23 investments resulted in 38,400 new jobs through 426 projects supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team.
Prior to 2023, the only local incentive available was the Early County Revolving Loan Fund (RLF).
The RLF allows the Development Authority to recommend businesses for gap financing to assist with economic development projects that create and retain jobs.
The Early County Board of Commissioners makes the final decision on each RLF application.

As of December 31, 2024, additional entities and incentives created to promote economic development in Blakely and Early County include the Downtown Development Authority, Local Historic Preservation Ordinances, Local Historic District & Commission, Rural Zone Designation, Enterprise Zone Ordinances & Designations, Alumni of Georgia Initiative for Community Housing, and a GA Main Street Start-Up Community.
The Blakely DDA developed branding for Downtown Blakely and commissioned a professional website to showcase the downtown area.
Early County and Blakely are entering their 200th year as Susanne Reynolds begins her third year in office.
source_title: Building a Strong Foundation

facts: Mercedes-Benz is expanding its presence in Metro Atlanta by moving up to 500 corporate, technical, and high-tech roles to its existing “1MB” facility in Sandy Springs, Ga.
The company is making a multi-million dollar investment in a new Research & Development hub in Metro Atlanta for technical teams from various U.S. locations.
Today’s announcement establishes Atlanta as Mercedes-Benz’s headquarters in North America, centralizing and uniting key corporate functions driving the success of the U.S. market.
Additional corporate roles will move to Sandy Springs from Farmington Hills, Mich.
Financial services teams and corporate functions based in Farmington Hills will be co-located with current sales teams in Sandy Springs.
The move of up to 500 roles further expands Mercedes-Benz’s longstanding presence in the Atlanta area.
Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, stated that thanking the State of Georgia for its support deepens roots and brings talented team members to the city.

Hoff noted the expansion strengthens their position for continued growth, reinforces commitment to the U.S. market, enables agility, increases speed to market, and ensures the best customer experience.
Mercedes-Benz opened its 1MB facility in 2018, making it an integral part of the Atlanta community.
There are currently approximately 800 Mercedes-Benz employees in Atlanta.
The move of up to 500 additional roles to Georgia is expected to be completed by August 2026.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia continues to lead in mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies driving the automotive industry forward.
Kemp expressed excitement that a job creator with close ties to Georgia is doubling down on its presence in the best state for business and opportunity.
Mercedes-Benz is co-locating its Research & Development and customer lifecycle-focused activities in the U.S. as part of a broader strategy to enhance product excellence and customer experience.
The strategic investment in Atlanta will allow the company to leverage the engineering talent pool around institutions like Georgia Institute of Technology.
The investment will create new opportunities to collaborate with the region’s thriving startup ecosystem and established tech sectors.
This announcement comes weeks after Mercedes-Benz announced it will bring production of the GLC SUV to its Tuscaloosa, Ala. facility.
The U.S. is a vital market for Mercedes-Benz, with sales of 374,100 vehicles (passenger cars and vans) last year.
The company is responsible for 11,100 direct jobs in the U.S., of which around 8,000 are in manufacturing.
There are approximately 112,000 jobs across Mercedes-Benz’s business suppliers and service providers.
Mercedes-Benz has a strong heritage dating back to 1888 in Long Island City, NY.
As a major local investor, Mercedes-Benz has committed over $10 billion to production, infrastructure, R&D, design, and the dealer network.
This commitment includes $7+ billion at its Tuscaloosa, Ala., site and $560+ million at its Charleston, S.C., plant.
The company supports more than 163,000 jobs across the country, including 11,100 direct jobs (around 8,000 in manufacturing) and 28,000 dealership employees.
Mercedes-Benz’s nationwide presence spans 385 dealer partners and 24 corporate locations in 13 states.
More than 4.5 million vehicles have left the Tuscaloosa plant over the past 28 years.
Roughly sixty percent of annual output from Tuscaloosa is exported.
The United States is the second-largest global market for Mercedes-Benz Sprinter vans.
Since 2006, Mercedes-Benz Vans, LLC (MBV Charleston) has assembled more than 400,000 vans for delivery across North America.
Mercedes-Benz works with around 400 suppliers in the United States.
The company is sourcing local demand for steel and aluminum almost exclusively in the United States.
The Tuscaloosa operation is the global hub for SUVs including GLE, GLS, GLE Coupe, Mercedes-Maybach GLS, EQS SUV, EQE SUV, and Mercedes-Maybach EQS SUV.
Mercedes-Benz is making electric vehicle infrastructure investments of approximately $1.2 billion to expand a high-speed charging network in North America over the next six to seven years.
The company launched its first charging location in Sandy Springs, Ga. in November 2023.
In 2024, Mercedes-Benz sold 324,500 passenger cars and 49,600 vans in the U.S.
The United States is the second-largest global market for Mercedes-Benz cars and vans.
Mercedes-Benz currently supports over 700,000 finance and lease customers in the United States with a portfolio of over $40 billion.
The company has filed nearly 100 technology patents from the U.S.
Mercedes-Benz introduced DRIVE PILOT, the first SAE-Level 3 system for conditionally automated driving approved for Nevada and California.
The company actively supports communities with nearly 40,000 hours of company-supported volunteer work in 2024 alone.
Mercedes-Benz AG is part of the Mercedes-Benz Group AG with around 175,000 employees worldwide.
Ola Källenius serves as Chairman of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz AG.
The company focuses on development, production, and sales of passenger cars, vans, and vehicle-related services.
Mercedes-Benz aspires to be the leader in electric mobility and vehicle software.
The product portfolio includes the Mercedes-Benz brand with Mercedes-AMG, Mercedes-Maybach, G-Class, all-electric models, and the smart brand.
In 2024, Mercedes-Benz sold around 2.4 million passenger cars and vans worldwide.
The company is continually expanding its worldwide production network with more than 30 production sites on four continents.
Mercedes-Benz is constructing and extending its global battery production network on three continents.
Sustainability is the guiding principle of the Mercedes-Benz strategy, focusing on creating lasting value for stakeholders through a sustainable business strategy of the Mercedes-Benz Group.

facts: Mercedes-Benz announced plans to establish its North American headquarters in metro Atlanta.
The automaker will expand its existing U.S. corporate hub by adding hundreds of jobs relocated from other cities.
The change will add about 500 jobs to the Georgia facility that has served as Mercedes-Benz's U.S. headquarters since 2018.
The Georgia facility currently employs about 800 people.
Mercedes-Benz plans to add a new, multimillion-dollar research and development center nearby.
Most of the additional jobs coming to Sandy Springs north of Atlanta are being moved from the Detroit area.
Mercedes-Benz is closing its financial services headquarters in the Detroit area.
The headquarters expansion in Georgia should be completed by August 2026.
Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, stated that bringing teams closer together will enable them to be more agile, increase speed to market, and ensure the best customer experience.
The headquarters expansion bolsters Georgia's reputation for luring automotive jobs.
Porsche also has its North American headquarters outside Atlanta.
Hyundai and Kia both have large manufacturing plants in Georgia.
Electric SUV maker Rivian is also building a factory in Georgia.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Georgia continues to lead the way in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz that are driving the automotive industry forward.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz will establish its North American HQ in Georgia, moving hundreds of jobs | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Mercedes-Benz USA is using “Sandy Springs” in its street address for its new headquarters.
The decision serves as a nod to city concerns that Sandy Springs is home to several major corporations that often brand themselves “Atlanta” instead.
MBUSA’s website lists the address for its Customer Assistance Center as “1 Mercedes-Benz Drive, Sandy Springs GA 30328.”
Whether the address would say “Sandy Springs” or “Atlanta” was a touchy and unanswered point in last year’s controversial City Council decision to rename part of Barfield Road for the company.
City officials have long been sensitive to their own branding issue, as other major corporations headquartered in the city such as UPS and Intercontinental Exchange use “Atlanta” in their materials.
MBUSA opened its new headquarters in March.
MBUSA did not respond to questions about the address decision.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz uses Sandy Springs street address - Rough Draft Atlanta

facts: The state and local incentive package offered to Mercedes-Benz to move its U.S. headquarters to metro Atlanta could total more than $27 million, including a grant and certain tax breaks.
Georgia and Fulton County officials on Tuesday further outlined their offerings to Mercedes-Benz USA.
The company last week announced a move here from New Jersey and is said to be close to picking a site in Sandy Springs or Alpharetta.

The state offered an incentives package valued at $23.3 million, and the Development Authority of Fulton County estimated that property tax savings for the company’s headquarters campus could total $4 million over 10 years.

entity_name: Georgia Consortium for Advanced Technical Training (CATT)
canonical_name: Georgia Consortium for Advanced Technical Training (CATT)
facts: Manufacturers are worried they will not have enough skilled workers in the future, so they have started to create apprenticeship programs to train high school students.
Factory workers are aging and manufacturers are worried there won’t be enough young people with the technical skills to do their jobs.
10 states have embraced Germany’s apprenticeship model to help fill manufacturing positions.
In Georgia, high school students are training for factory jobs in a German-style apprenticeship program.
Seventeen-year-old Northgate High School student Cole McKeehan wakes up at 3 a.m. on Fridays to work at E.G.O. North America.
E.G.O. is a radiant heating elements and electronics factory that mostly makes parts inside electric stoves, located in Newnan, about an hour’s drive south of Atlanta.
The state of Georgia wants more teens to get exposure to manufacturing while in high school by expanding the Georgia Consortium for Advanced Technical Training (CATT).
Students start the three-year program when they’re 15 and are matched with a nearby technical college and a factory where they learn technical skills firsthand.
Cole McKeehan splits his time between high school studies, classes at a technical college, and work experience shaping metal at E.G.O., where he makes $10 an hour.
Cole McKeehan expects to graduate with a high school diploma, an associate’s degree in precision manufacturing, and a full-time job offer.
Cole McKeehan is currently one of 27 students in the apprenticeship program.
The program began with just 10 students in the fall of 2016 in Coweta County, Georgia.
Georgia Lt. Gov. Casey Cagle said the program is a great win-win for not only the student but obviously for industry.
The state launched the program in 2016 after a German company, Grenzebach, which makes manufacturing equipment for other factories, had trouble finding workers.
A report from The Manufacturing Institute and Deloitte found U.S. manufacturers need to fill more than 3.5 million jobs over the next decade.

Starting with Tennessee in 2011, the German American Chamber of Commerce helped set up German-style apprenticeship programs in ten states: Georgia, Illinois, Wisconsin, Michigan, Indiana, Virginia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama and Tennessee.
There are plans underway to help expand the program to six more states.

Tony Wilding, a manager at the Michelin Tread Technologies plant in Covington, Georgia, said they have a hard time filling manufacturing or reliability technician jobs and have to recruit outside the area.
Michelin Tread Technologies recently recruited a 15-year-old high school sophomore.
Companies train and mentor high school students but also commit to $25,000 to fund student salaries.
In Georgia there are 20 manufacturers that are part of the program.
Kenny Adkins with the Technical College System of Georgia said hundreds of students apply and the state wants to accept more of them.
The state relaunched Georgia Piedmont Technical College’s manufacturing program in Covington with a new Advanced Manufacturing Center.
The Advanced Manufacturing Center includes five labs and more than $1 million of new equipment.
The center provides training in machine tools, electronics, mechanical training, automation and process control, as well as industrial wiring and motor controls.
Kenny Adkins said updating the labs at technical colleges is part of the state’s effort to address occupational training needs.
Training at technical colleges is a key part of the program since child labor laws in the U.S. prevent apprentices from working at manufacturing plants until they turn 16.
Lt. Gov. Casey Cagle expects the number of high school students in the Georgia CATT program to double in 2018.
The state of Georgia aims to offer this kind of opportunity to every high school student by 2020.

Lt. Gov. Casey Cagle set a goal so that by 2020 every student in Georgia will have access to a College and Career Academy, with all high school students having the opportunity to become a GA CATT apprentice within the next few years.
source_title: Georgia embraces Germany’s apprenticeship model

The expansion will double the production volume at the site.

facts: Michelin is announcing the complete rollout of TreadEye, a new technology designed to improve the retreading process for commercial fleets.
After several stages of testing and implementation, TreadEye is now fully operational within the Michelin Retread Technologies network across the United States.
TreadEye is an advanced tool that measures tread depth, helping fleets get the most out of their tire investments.
The technology collects detailed information from 1,200 measurement points to ensure accurate readings of tread wear and casing conditions.

Nate Kirian, Vice President of Sales and Distribution for Michelin North America, stated that TreadEye is 'a revolutionary addition to our retread services and a significant benefit for the fleets we support' and 'a great demonstration of Michelin’s ongoing commitment to innovation for our customers.'
TreadEye identifies optimal removal points based on fleet location.
The technology extends tire life by preventing unnecessary removals.
TreadEye reduces the risk of casing damage and DOT violations.
The tool improves the overall efficiency of tire removal, positively impacting a fleet’s finances.
Michelin continues to pioneer tire technology, staying focused on delivering quality and reliability.
Based in Greenville, S.C., Michelin is a world leader in manufacturing tires and other diverse materials for over 130 years.
Michelin North America employs around 23,500 people.
Michelin North America operates 36 manufacturing facilities in the U.S. and Canada.
source_title: MICHELIN Launches TreadEye Tech Nationwide in Retread Network - India Bulletin

facts: The Georgia Center of Innovation, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), hosted hundreds of attendees at the 2025 Georgia Logistics Summit.

Established in 2009 as the first state-led event of its kind, the Georgia Logistics Summit is one of the Southeast’s key logistics and supply chain events connecting industry professionals for networking and knowledge-sharing.
The economic impact of Georgia’s transportation and logistics industry was $107 billion in 2023, according to an economic impact study by the University of Georgia’s Selig Center for Economic Growth.
Georgia’s transportation and logistics industries supported more than 578,000 jobs, representing one in nine jobs in the state.
From 2010 to 2023, transportation and logistics jobs in Georgia grew by 68%, outpacing the national growth rate of 52%.
In 2023 and 2024 alone, new logistics and distribution sector investments, including cold storage and ecommerce fulfillment centers, totaled $3.8 billion and created over 9,000 new jobs.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that decades of strategic investment in ports, rail lines, highways, and air cargo capabilities have led to record-breaking economic investments and trade.

The Logistics Summit brings together private sector, government, and education leaders to learn from experts, exchange best practices, and explore opportunities in the rapidly evolving logistics landscape.
Georgia Department of Transportation Commissioner Russell R. McMurry highlighted strategic investments and how GDOT is leveraging technology to improve freight flow.
Governor Brian P. Kemp and the General Assembly allocated $1 billion to the newly created Georgia Freight Program over the past two years.
Additional transportation infrastructure investments are advancing the timing for key planned projects to maintain and improve Georgia’s interstate highway system, roads, and bridges.
Georgia’s multimodal transportation network carried nearly half a billion tons of freight in 2019, valued at $673 trillion.
Projections show that freight volume is expected to nearly double to 900 million tons by 2050, with freight value more than doubling today’s value by the same year.
Working with partners including the Center of Innovation and the Georgia Institute of Technology, GDOT is focusing on enhancing safety and efficiency.
GDOT projects include adding 50% more truck parking and installing fiber internet on Georgia interstates.

Georgia Ports Authority Vice President of Operations Susan Gardner provided updates on strategic investments to expand port capacity and leverage live data to improve safety, track vessel productivity and containers, and eliminate congestion.

The Georgia Ports Authority is investing $4.5 billion in improvements over the next decade as part of its port master plan to expand cargo handling capabilities and support future supply chain requirements.

A feature panel titled “AI and Beyond: Embracing Digital Transformation in Logistics” included leadership from The Home Depot, Havertys Furniture, TOTO USA, and research perspectives from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Panelists highlighted a three- to five-year outlook for the industry and provided insights on how AI and automation are transforming supply chains.

Center of Innovation Executive Director David Nuckolls stated that digital technologies are reshaping supply chains and the Center’s specialized logistics team works alongside the industry to position businesses for growth.

University System of Georgia Chancellor Sonny Perdue and Technical College System of Georgia Commissioner Greg Dozier provided updates on how state post-secondary institutions are developing a globally competitive workforce.
Discussion focused on ensuring taught skills match logistics company needs, including creative problem-solving and effective use of new AI and automation tools.
The breadth of Georgia’s technical college programs was discussed, including the High Demand Career Initiatives program.

A pilot program called “Dual Achievement” enrolls students who withdrew from high school in a technical college program, enabling them to earn a high school diploma alongside a technical college certificate, diploma, or degree.
Panelists emphasized helping students progress faster, upskilling the existing workforce, and fostering connections between industry leaders and educators.

The Center of Innovation’s Logistics Industry Advisory Board recognized winners of the inaugural Future Innovators in Supply Chain competition, which invited college students to create videos about supply chain careers.
Led by professor Parisa Pooyan, student team “The Masters of Logistinomics” from Kennesaw State University won first prize and a $3,000 grant for the university.
The winning video was created by Eli Hampton, Angeline Harris, Joe Johnson, and Dana Pazhouhesh.
Participants in the 2025 Georgia Logistics Summit also included leaders from S&P Global Market Intelligence, Boost Phase Ventures, and M.D. Livingstone Consulting.

The Center of Innovation helps Georgia businesses find solutions by connecting them with experts, external partners, and independent mentors across six key industries: Aerospace, AgTech, Energy Technology, Information Technology, Logistics, and Manufacturing.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging industry expansion, and locating new markets for Georgia products.

links.context: 2025 Georgia Logistics Summit information and next year's plans
Contact for Allie Dean, Communications Manager, GDEcD

source_title: Georgia Center of Innovation Hosts 2025 Logistics Summit | Physical Internet Center

canonical_name: Hyundai Metaplant America (Joint Venture with LG Energy Solution)
facts: South Korea’s foreign minister is considering a trip to the U.S. to meet with the Trump administration after hundreds of South Korean nationals were arrested in Georgia this week at an electric vehicle battery plant.
Foreign Minister Cho Hyun expressed deep concern and a heavy sense of responsibility over the arrests of South Korean nationals during an emergency meeting in Seoul.

South Korean President Lee Jae Myung called for all-out efforts to swiftly resolve the matter, stressing that the rights and interests of South Korean nationals and the business operations of South Korean companies investing in the United States must not be infringed upon.

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents arrested 475 people, most of them South Korean nationals, in a raid Thursday at an electric vehicle battery plant under construction in Ellabell, Ga., near Savannah.
The plant is a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution.

ICE released video showing officers shackling workers and loading them onto Georgia prison buses during what officials say is the largest single-site enforcement operation in the history of the Homeland Security Investigations.

Steven Schrank, special agent in charge of homeland security investigations for Georgia and Alabama, stated the operation underscores a commitment to protecting jobs for Georgians and Americans and sends a clear message that those who exploit the workforce or violate federal laws will be held accountable.
Schrank clarified the arrests were the result of a months-long investigation, not a random roundup, and that the workers were either illegally present in the U.S. or working unlawfully.
President Trump characterized the arrested individuals as "illegal aliens" and stated that ICE was just doing its job during a White House press conference on Friday.
Democratic U.S. Sen. Raphael Warnock of Georgia raised questions about the president’s immigration strategy and how the firms were able to allegedly employ workers without proper documentation.

Warnock stated companies should not be able to get rich in Georgia by exploiting immigrant workers, calling the situation bad for the economy, Georgia’s workers, and law-abiding families living in fear of this administration.

Hyundai Motor Co. stated it is working to understand the specific circumstances of the arrests and noted that based on current understanding, none of those detained are directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company.
Hyundai promised an internal investigation into employment compliance and reiterated its zero tolerance policy for those who don't follow the law.
LG Energy Solution suspended all business trips to the U.S. and instructed workers visiting the U.S. to stay in their accommodations or return to South Korea immediately.

LG Energy Solution's chief human resources officer is heading to the U.S. to address the issues on site, with the company promising to make every effort to promptly resolve the situation and ensure the speedy and safe return of all employees.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp has touted the Hyundai Metaplant America site as a game changer for the state that has provided historic benefits and new opportunities for workers.

A spokesperson for Gov. Kemp stated the state will always enforce the law, including federal immigration law, and that all companies operating within Georgia must follow the laws of the state and nation.

Republican U.S. Senate candidate and Congressman Buddy Carter, whose district includes the Ellabell plant site, applauded the Trump Administration and law enforcement for taking bold action to put American workers first and protect communities from illegal immigration.
The arrests occur at a time when President Trump has been pushing for trade deals and more South Korean investment in the U.S.
Hyundai also operates an auto plant in Montgomery, Ala., and has announced plans to invest more than $5 billion in a steel plant in Louisiana.
source_title: ICE arrests at a Georgia Hyundai plant create new tension with South Korea - TPR: The Public's Radio

facts: Rivian Automotive will receive a total of $1.5 billion in state and local incentives and tax credits for the new EV plant it plans to build in Georgia.
The incentive package is by far the largest Georgia has ever offered to a company investing in the state.
Documents outlining the incentives were signed by Rivian and the state on May 2.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson described the size of the package as being "appropriate" for the largest single industrial announcement in Georgia history.
The $5 billion plant is expected to employ some 7,500 people by 2028.
The average annual salary will be $56,000.
All employees will also receive equity in Rivian.
The two parties estimate that the new plant will create 7,978 additional indirect jobs.
Together, these 15,478 jobs are expected to generate over $1 billion in labor income annually.
The Economic Development Agreement (EDA) was between the Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties, the State of Georgia, and Rivian.
The EDA was approved and signed by all parties on May 2, marking a milestone in the automaker's progress towards the development of its second US manufacturing plant.
Rivian stated that the "long-term economic partnership promises to deliver value to Rivian, the people of Georgia and their kids' kids' kids."
Rivian released the site plan of the future campus as well as an exterior rendering of the front of the plant building.
The rendering shows planned green spaces filled with native plants.
Over 50% of the site's acreage will remain unpaved when the plant reaches maximum production capacity.
Rivian also operates a plant in Normal, Illinois.
Approximately 5,000 people at the Illinois plant build Rivian R1T electric pickups, R1S electric SUVs, and electric vans for Amazon.
Rivian expects to break ground at the new Georgia site as early as this summer.
Rivian forecasts that production will start in 2024 at its Georgia plant.
The project has faced opposition from residents east of Atlanta who say that the factory will spoil their rural quality of life.

A press statement from Georgia state leaders notes that the agreement "ensures that the project follows locally required standards pertaining to water quality, groundwater recharge and runoff, and local ordinances."
source_title: Rivian To Get $1.5 Billion In Public Incentives For Georgia Plant

Through its Redistribution Center, the company provides after-service parts to dealerships, auto parts stores, and other parts distribution centers.
James Jeon, program manager for Mobis US Alabama, stated the company is excited about opening its second facility in Alabama and utilizing the Jefferson County workforce.

facts: Hyundai MOBIS forms the parts and service arm for South Korean automakers Hyundai Motor Company, Genesis Motors, and Kia Motors.

The company offers chassis, cockpit, and front-end modules; safety products including airbags, headlights, anti-lock brake systems, and electronic stability control products; steering parts; multimedia systems; UVO systems; injection-molded plastic parts such as instrument panels, carriers, and bumpers; and steel wheel rims and decks.
Hyundai MOBIS supplies after-sales service parts for vehicles and concentrates its resources on after-sales parts, module parts manufacture, and parts export.
Hyundai MOBIS has firmly established its position as one of the leading auto parts specialist companies in the world.
Hyundai MOBIS’s Module Division, which includes MOBIS Alabama, LLC, supplies the chassis module, cockpit module, and front-end module to automakers using a just-in-sequence (JIS) system.

The division participates in the initial stages of new vehicle development processes and performs auto-parts research and development, including designing and testing for production applications based on module specifications.
Through modularization system integration, Hyundai MOBIS reduces total weight and the number of parts needed, enabling easier assembly, effective inventory control, and cost reduction.
MOBIS Alabama, LLC was established in 2002 and operates on nearly 83 acres in Montgomery, Alabama.
MOBIS Alabama, LLC is the largest Tier 1 supplier to both Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) and KIA Motor Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG).
The company holds ISO14001 and IATF16949 certifications for Environmental Management and Quality Management.
Production begins in a 600,000 square-foot Plastics plant that uses injection molding to produce front and rear bumpers, instrument panel frames, and after-service parts.
Bumpers are molded and painted in-house at the Alabama facility.
Bumpers destined for HMMA are assembled in-house before being transferred to HMMA for final vehicle assembly.
Bumpers destined for KMMG are sent to a sister plant in Georgia for assembly.
After molding, instrument panel frames move to an adjacent Module plant where they receive additional parts to form complete Cockpit (dashboard) assemblies.
The Module building also houses front and rear Chassis (vehicle suspension) assembly lines that assemble parts from Tier 2 and 3 suppliers into completed Chassis modules.
Completed Chassis modules are transported to nearby HMMA for final vehicle assembly.
A Redistribution Center (RDC) supplies genuine Hyundai and KIA after-service parts to dealerships, auto parts stores, and other distribution centers across North America.

The company offers competitive wages and benefits including paid vacation and personal time off, 14 paid holidays, a 401(k) with company match, health/dental/vision insurance, life insurance, and disability insurance.

Lucid reported a 19 percent sales gain and announced leadership changes.
Rivian is cutting 2% of its workforce, targeting sales and service teams, as it works toward profitability following the launch of its R2 SUV.
