facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America announced on May 4 that it will establish the Panasonic Automotive Innovation Center in the Technology Square area of Midtown Atlanta on the campus of the Georgia Institute of Technology.
The center will be an extension of the Design Engineering Centers located in Peachtree City, Ga., and Southfield, Mich.
The center is expected to begin operations at the end of May.
The facility will serve as an incubator for next-generation automotive infotainment technologies.
The new Panasonic Automotive Innovation Center facility will be located in Centergy One, a LEED Silver-certified office tower.
The Panasonic offices are designed to maximize natural light and enable open collaboration for projected 40 software, electrical, systems, mechanical, and other additional support personnel.
The center expects to offer at least 15 co-op positions a year to Georgia Tech students.
Automakers and suppliers like Panasonic prioritize developing sophisticated connected car systems with internet and smartphone connectivity due to consumer demand for seamless in-vehicle lifestyles.

Tom Gebhardt, President of Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, stated the Atlanta location allows the company to tap into the engineering talent of one of the fastest growing high-technology areas in the nation.
Atlanta currently hosts over 13,000 technology companies employing nearly 200,000 technology workers.
A primary driver for locating the Innovation Center in Technology Square was Panasonic's interest in enhancing its partnership with the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Panasonic aims to develop a strong partnership with the Georgia Tech Enterprise Innovation Institute and the University’s Division of Professional Practice for employing co-ops.
Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed stated that Panasonic's decision to open an office in Atlanta testifies to the city's strength as a technology hub, citing its educated workforce and high quality of life.

Stephen E. Cross, executive vice president for research at Georgia Institute of Technology, noted collaboration with state and local economic development organizations to identify Georgia Tech resources supporting Panasonic's objectives.
A key goal of the partnership is involving Georgia Tech students in Panasonic’s research and development program to provide real-world industrial experience.

Hans Gant, senior vice president of economic development for the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated the R&D center showcases Atlanta’s strengths as a global technology hub and congratulated Panasonic on new jobs and collaborative efforts with Georgia Tech.
The Metro Atlanta Chamber collaborated with partners at the Georgia Department of Economic Development, Invest Atlanta, Georgia Power, and Georgia Tech to help bring about the project.

Chris Cummiskey, commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, highlighted how Georgia’s pipeline of knowledge-based talent helps global businesses like Panasonic grow and remain competitive.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is based in Peachtree City with Detroit-area operations located in Southfield, Mich.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Secaucus, New Jersey-based Panasonic Corporation of North America, the principal North American subsidiary of Panasonic Corporation of Osaka, Japan.
source_title: Panasonic to establish auto innovation center in Atlanta | The Citizen

facts: On Jan. 9, 2012, AT&T announced at CES that it is partnering with Panasonic and QNX to bring its Watson speech-recognition technology to cars.
The technology will be integrated into cloud-connected vehicles to respond to voice commands for controlling GPS or changing radio channels.
Initial testing of the technology is currently taking place in Peachtree City, Georgia.
AT&T is providing the network services for the project.
Panasonic and QNX are supplying the hardware for the prototype.
The initiative operates within the Peachtree "Connected" City, GA collaboration framework.
AT&T's Watson speech engine is a multimodal and multilingual speech and language engine with best-in-class performance across applications.
AT&T holds more than 500 speech-related patents that can process speech, text, and gesture inputs for calls, navigation, and web browsing.
Glenn Lurie, president of emerging devices, resale and partnerships at AT&T, stated that wireless innovations in the automotive space continue to expand and are great news for consumers.
The prototype aims to demonstrate a connectivity strategy for automotive virtual assistance and limit driver distraction by focusing on customer needs and system interface usability.
Derek Kuhn, vice president of sales and marketing at QNX Software Systems, noted that natural and intuitive user experiences are key to consumer adoption of connected vehicles.
AT&T and Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America formed a working agreement in 2011 to explore and test connectivity solutions for consumers and commuters.
The collaboration framework allows the two companies to jointly create customized products for global automotive manufacturers in North America.
Initial concept testing for in-car infotainment systems and emerging mobile devices interface is currently taking place in the Peachtree City testing community.
source_title: AT&T's Watson coming to cars: won't mind if you ask it if you're there yet

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co. of America
canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co. of America
facts: Atlanta has 13,000 technology companies employing 200,000 people.

Tom Gebhardt, president of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co. of America, announced the company's decision to establish the Panasonic Automotive Innovation Center in Technology Square in Atlanta on the campus of the Georgia Institute of Technology.
The center began operations at the end of May.
The center is expected to become an incubator for next-generation automotive infotainment technologies.
Panasonic Automotive Systems is Panasonic's second largest division.
The company is expected to add many new jobs in metro Atlanta.
The company aims to develop a strong partnership with the Georgia Tech Enterprise Innovation Institute and the University's Division of Professional Practice for employing co-ops.
The company worked closely with Georgia Tech to identify research and education resources within the university to support its objectives.

Stephen E. Cross, executive vice president for research at the Georgia Institute of Technology, stated a key goal is to involve Georgia Tech students in Panasonic's research and development program to provide real-world industrial experience and connect the company to innovative young minds.
The Atlanta region has 120,000 students within city limits and 270,000 within the broader region.
The area is home to 57 colleges and universities, plus seven technical colleges each year.
Invest Atlanta's CEO Brian McGowan describes the local talent pool as a "technology feeder system of talent."
Atlanta holds the No. 4 spot for recent college grads according to Bloomberg BusinessWeek (July 2010).
This readily available talent source contributes to Atlanta's #4 ranking in terms of Fortune 500 headquarters, behind New York, Houston, and Dallas.
There is a trend of manufacturing companies returning to the city.
Advanced manufacturing is growing significantly in Atlanta's urban core, according to McGowan.
A Brookings Institution report states that 80% of the nation's manufacturing jobs are located in and around cities.
Metro Atlanta holds 150,000 manufacturing jobs, representing a gain of 9,000 since early 2010.
A decade prior to the article, the region had 186,300 manufacturing jobs.
The Atlanta region posted a 4.3% increase in manufacturing jobs over the past two years.
U.S. cities overall saw only a 2.7% increase in manufacturing jobs during the same period, according to The Atlanta Journal Constitution.
The Technology Association of Georgia works on the economic advancement of the state's technology industry through policy, capital, education, and giving.
Atlanta has attracted technology giants including AT&T, IBM Corp., General Electric, Verizon Wireless, Cisco, Google, Kimberly Clark, Equifax, and UCB.
source_title: Hi-Tech's Growth in Atlanta Stems from Workforce | IndustryWeek

facts: UK-based holographic technology startup Envisics debuted with the intention to revolutionise the in-car experience with its technology.

During the ongoing Consumer Electronics Show (CES), Envisics announced a partnership with Panasonic Automotive Systems to develop and commercialise a new generation of head-up displays for cars, SUVs and trucks.
Panasonic Automotive Systems is a tier 1 automotive supplier and an arm of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
Envisics' head-up displays are units integrated in vehicles’ dashboards that project images into the windshield to help drivers with navigation and other alerts.
As a result of the partnership, Panasonic HUDs will use Envisics' holographic technology.
The new partnership combines Panasonic’s expertise in optical design and its global reach to bring holography technology into the mainstream.
Envisics intends to begin mass production of its technology in 2023.
Envisics was founded by Jamieson Christmas in 2018.
Envisics secured $50M (nearly £37M) Series B funding in a round led by Hyundai Mobis, General Motors’ GM Ventures, SAIC Capital, the venture arm of SAIC Motors, and Van Tuyl Companies (VTC).
The investment was raised in October last year.
The raised investment is supposed to be used for continued development of its technology and to bring fresh iterations of the same into the market.
Hyundai Mobis, a branch of the Korean car maker, will integrate Envisics’ AR HUDs as part of its autonomous driving development.
At the time of investment, it was announced that the fund will be used to integrate the technology in the upcoming Cadillac Lyriq electric vehicle.
Envisics focuses solely on the automotive applications of holography technology.
The first generation of this tech is already integrated in over 150,000 Jaguar Land Rover vehicles.
Jamieson Christmas stated that the Series B funding round was about scaling the business and enabling them to move forward as they enter the market.
Part of the plan included a commitment to engage in partnerships with Tier ones to deliver products to market.
Envisics will develop head-up displays with high resolution, wider color gamut and larger images that can be projected upon reality.
The technology can simultaneously project information at multiple distances.
This capability can be used for night vision applications such as overlaying enhanced information upon a dark road to clearly show where the road is heading and the obstacles on the path.
source_title: This UK startup empowers car dashboards to display information, directions and points of interest as holograms - UKTN

facts: Apollo funds acquired an 80% stake in Panasonic Automotive Systems in 2024, with Panasonic Holdings retaining 20%.
The deal was the largest sponsor-led corporate carve-out in Japan by revenue, spanning 20 subsidiaries across more than 20 countries, with roughly $8 billion in revenue.
As a standalone company, Panasonic Automotive has set a clear goal to become the global number one in integrated cockpit systems and advanced in-vehicle solutions.
CEO Masashi Nagayasu noted that the partnership with Apollo has helped change how the company operates by enabling faster decisions, sharper priorities, and greater accountability.
Following its separation, the company has been streamlining operations, investing in technology, and improving EBITDA minus capex as a key performance indicator.
Panasonic Automotive is targeting new business to become an integral supplier to all original equipment manufacturers in Japan.
Early signs of success include improving margins and building new order momentum across a wider group of Japanese manufacturers.
The company will soon operate under the new name Mobitera, underscoring its transition from a division to a standalone entity with its own identity, strategy, and ambitions.

Tetsuji Okamoto, Partner at Apollo, stated that focused ownership can unlock the next phase of growth for world-class Japanese businesses and that Apollo's role is to partner with management to translate strategic ambition into operational execution.
source_title: PE Case Study: Panasonic AutomotiveApollo Global Management

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
facts: On October 04, 2022, Capgemini Group announced the successful creation of a data ecosystem for longstanding client Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America.
The new platform will improve Panasonic Automotive's capabilities in data-driven decision making and innovation to drive efficiencies and more reliable extracts.
Panasonic Automotive is a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America specializing in innovations that are revolutionizing the driving experience.
Panasonic Automotive streamlined its data processes with the Microsoft Azure platform with Power BI (Business Intelligence) for data visualization.
Panasonic Automotive built a centralized, stable foundation for its data team to promote innovation and capture more reliable results across the business.

Scott Kirchner, President of Panasonic Automotive, stated: “Data really enables our people... If people can see how their piece of the business or another area they are related to is working, they can have more innovation and accountability as to how they can impact operations. It is empowering.”
Capgemini established an aggressive six-month timeline to migrate Panasonic Automotive’s existing data reporting platform to Microsoft Azure, built on a single-instance software system.

The new data lake architecture solution provides a solid, stable foundation for the data team and utilizes data from across Panasonic Automotive’s business units to centralize their wealth of information.
The migration results in more reliable data extracts in a much shorter timeframe, setting the organization up for future artificial intelligence and machine learning use cases.

Venkat Padmanabhan, Account Executive for Panasonic at Capgemini, stated: “This collaboration shows Capgemini’s commitment to understanding our clients’ goals and then making them a reality... We’re glad to have a strong relationship with Panasonic Automotive, and to utilize our skills in data migration and automotive connectivity to successfully complete this project and support Panasonic Automotive’s dedication to passion, quality, and innovation.”

Since migrating to the data lake, the Panasonic Automotive Business Intelligence team spends less than 20% of their time on support-related tasks, down from approximately 50% on the previous platform.
The transformed ecosystem can present well-informed data in significantly less time with increased accuracy and reliability.

Panasonic Automotive and Capgemini have worked together for more than five years, streamlining Panasonic Automotive’s applications with automation and ongoing support from Capgemini’s full stack of application data management capabilities, ADMnext.

links.context: Client story link for the data platform project

source_title: Capgemini Group fuels collaboration with Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America through transformative new data platform - Sogeti Ireland

facts: U.K.-based holographic technology startup Envisics reached an agreement with Panasonic Automotive Systems to jointly develop and commercialize a new generation of head-up displays for cars, trucks and SUVs.
Panasonic Automotive Systems is a Tier 1 automotive supplier and a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
The head-up displays are units integrated in the dash of a vehicle that project images onto the windshield to aid drivers with navigation and provide other alerts.
The Panasonic HUDs will use Envisics holographic technology.
The deal was announced ahead of the virtual 2021 CES tech trade show.
The announcement follows Envisics’ $50 million Series B funding round and news that its tech will be integrated in the upcoming Cadillac Lyriq electric vehicle.
The funding round brought Envisics a valuation of more than $250 million.
Investors in the funding round included Hyundai Mobis, GM Ventures, SAIC Ventures and Van Tuyl Companies.
Envisics’ technology foundation originated from founder and CEO Dr. Jamieson Christmas’ PhD studies at Cambridge University more than 15 years ago.
The technology electronically manipulates the speed of light to enable images to appear three-dimensional.
The company has secured more than 250 patents and has another 160 pending certification.
Envisics is solely focused upon the automotive application of holography.
The company's first generation technology is already integrated in more than 150,000 Jaguar Land Rover vehicles.
The new agreement aims to combine Panasonic’s expertise in optical design and its global reach as a Tier 1 supplier with Envisics’ technology to bring holography into the mainstream.
Mass production of vehicles using the technology is slated for 2023.

Founder and CEO Dr. Jamieson Christmas stated: 'This is very much about part of our business plan, you know the Series B funding round we undertook was about scaling the business and enabling us to move forward as we enter the market.'
Christmas added that engaging in partnerships with Tier 1 suppliers is part of the plan to deliver products to market, noting this agreement is the first of its kind.
The head-up displays will feature high resolution, wide color gamut and large images that can be overlaid upon reality.
The technology can project information at multiple distances simultaneously.
Short-term applications include augmented reality navigation, highlighting the lane the driver is supposed to be in, and safety applications.
Future applications for autonomous driving could include entertainment and video conferencing.
The technology could also be used for night vision applications by overlaying enhanced information upon a dark road to clarify the road path and obstacles.
source_title: Holographic startup Envisics partners with Panasonic to fast-track in-car AR tech | TechCrunch

canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America (PASA)
facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America (PASA) is an industry-leading global supplier to Automotive Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) for infotainment systems and advanced connected car solutions.
Clients include Ford, GM, Chrysler, Daimler, Fiat, Tesla, Honda, Toyota, Nissan and many others.
The company enhances consumer experience through audio enhancements or safety enhancements inside the vehicle.
Headquarters are located at 776 Highway 74 South, Peachtree City, Georgia, United States.
The company has 2434 employees.
Annual revenue is $250 - 500M.
Legal name is Panasonic Automotive Systems.
NAICS code is 441310.
Industry classification is Electrical/Electronic Manufacturing.
President is Scott Kirchner.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems - Phone, Email, Employees, CEO, VP, 2024

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems is a global Tier 1 automotive supplier.
The company achieved a Bronze Medal in the sustainability assessment conducted by EcoVadis, an international organization headquartered in France that evaluates corporate sustainability.
The Bronze Medal recognition is granted to companies ranked within the top 35 percent of all organizations assessed.
EcoVadis evaluates the sustainability performance of more than 150,000 companies across 185 countries.
Assessments cover four categories: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement.
Scores are on a 100-point scale based on 21 indicators across these themes.
In this assessment cycle, Panasonic Automotive Systems received the Bronze Medal, placing the company within the top 35 percent overall.
The automotive industry faces rising environmental, social, and governance (ESG) expectations due to its ecological footprint and complex global supply chains.
Companies are expected to address social responsibility issues including human rights, working conditions, ethical sourcing of minerals, coexistence with local communities, and supply chain safety.
Governance structures must support implementation of these initiatives across group operations while strengthening risk management.
ESG initiatives have become a key driver of long-term sustainability and competitiveness in the automotive sector.
Panasonic Automotive Systems’ efforts in the Sustainable Procurement category were highly recognized.

The evaluation cited the company’s promotion of responsible procurement across its supply chain, including conflict mineral management and due diligence using the Conflict Minerals Reporting Template (CMRT).
The evaluation also cited systematic management of environmental and social considerations.
Overall, the company’s policies, initiatives, and support for international standards were assessed as being extremely strong.
Efforts in Environment, Labor & Human Rights, and Sustainable Procurement were acknowledged as demonstrating a high level of execution and due diligence.
Recognition against international standards marks a significant step toward achieving the company’s mission to “create a sustainable mobility society to promote a better life for everyone.”
The company stated it will continue to strengthen sustainability initiatives toward realizing a sustainable mobility society.
Hisayoshi Nakao serves as Director and Executive Officer in charge of ESG Promotion at Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

Hisayoshi Nakao said the assessment provided a valuable opportunity to reassess sustainability efforts from an international perspective and that the company will use it as a foundation to further improve initiatives and contribute to industry sustainable development.
The company remains committed to accountability and transparency in business practices through dialogue with stakeholders, starting with shareholders and customers.
By acting swiftly, fairly, and with integrity, the company aims to boost its corporate value further.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. is headquartered in Yokohama, Kanagawa, Japan.
The company operates globally with subsidiaries in eight other countries.
As a Tier 1 automotive supplier, it provides advanced proprietary technologies, including infotainment systems, to automakers in Japan and overseas.
Panasonic Automotive Systems has announced it will change its company name and brand to Mobitera Inc., effective April 1, 2027.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems Earns EcoVadis Bronze Medal

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Malaysia Sdn Bhd
canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Malaysia Sdn Bhd
facts: Panasonic has announced that its automotive systems factory in Penang, Malaysia is now operating as a net-zero carbon emission plant.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Malaysia Sdn Bhd manufactures audio and navigation systems for vehicles.
The company achieved net-zero CO2 status earlier this year.
It is the first facility in Penang to declare achievement of net-zero CO2 status.
The ISO 14001-certified plant covers a land area of 100,700 sqm with a build-up area of 28,430 sqm.
The facility has been actively working to reduce emissions across various aspects of its operations.
Key emission reduction measures include the replacement of water pumps, lighting, air-conditioning, and cooling systems' equipment with energy-efficient alternatives.
Additional emission reduction measures include the installation of motion sensors, air leak surveys, and implementing corresponding countermeasures.
source_title: Panasonic becomes first in Penang to operate net-zero carbon emission plant | NEWS | Reccessary

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. announced it will change its company name and brand to Mobitera to better represent its commitment to creating a safe, comfortable, and sustainable mobility experience.
The corporate name change is effective April 1, 2027.
Masashi Nagayasu serves as the chief executive officer (CEO) of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

In December 2024, Panasonic Automotive Systems began a strategic partnership with the Apollo Group to strengthen software development capabilities for the in-vehicle cockpit space and optimize its overall business portfolio.
The rebrand to Mobitera is positioned as the next step for accelerated growth following the Apollo Group partnership.
The new corporate name will be Mobitera Inc.
The brand name 'Mobitera' fuses 'mobility,' the Latin word 'iter' (path), and the Japanese word 'terasu' (to illuminate).
The central 'i' in the brand name highlights a vow to keep individuals at the focus of all operations.
The corporate color, Mobitera Blue-Green, symbolizes the harmonization of people, society, and the planet.
The company's mission is to create a sustainable mobility society to promote a better life for everyone.
The company's vision is defined as the 'Joy in Motion' design company.
Domestic and overseas consolidated subsidiaries will change their corporate names to align with the new Mobitera brand, effective April 1, 2027.
Panasonic ITS Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera ITS Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Electronics Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Solutions Inc.
Panasonic System Networks Evaluation Technology Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Evaluation Technologies Inc.
Harima SANYO Industries Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Wel Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC will become Mobitera America, LLC.
Panasonic Automotive Systems de Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera De Mexico, S.A de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Reynosa Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Reynosa Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Monterrey Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Monterrey Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Sales Company de Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Sales Company de Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Shared Services Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Shared Service Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Europe GmbH will become Mobitera Europe GmbH.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Czech, s.r.o. will become Mobitera Czech, s.r.o.
OpenSynergy GmbH will undergo no name change.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Asia Pacific Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Asia Pacific Co., Ltd.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Malaysia Sdn. Bhd. will become Mobitera Malaysia Sdn. Bhd.
Panasonic Automotive Systems India Pvt. Ltd. will become Mobitera India Pvt. Ltd.

Details regarding Panasonic Automotive Systems (China) Co., Ltd., Panasonic Automotive Systems (Dalian) Co., Ltd., and Panasonic Automotive Systems (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. will be announced after internal procedures are completed.

source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems Unveils New Brand Reflecting Vision to Drive Future of Mobility

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (PAS) and Arm
canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. and Arm
facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (PAS) and Arm recently announced a strategic cooperation to jointly promote the standardization of the software-defined vehicle (SDV) architecture.
Both parties share a common vision to create a flexible software stack that meets the needs of automobiles.
PAS and Arm will promote standardized cooperation in software development through participation in the SOAFEE industry initiative.
In this new cooperation project, PAS and Arm will adopt and expand the VirtIO device virtualization framework.
The adoption of VirtIO aims to achieve decoupling of automotive software development from hardware and accelerate the development process of the automotive industry.
As the automotive industry integrates electronic control units, hypervisors and advanced chip sets are becoming increasingly important.
Automakers and Tier 1 suppliers face the challenge of inconsistent proprietary interfaces of suppliers.
PAS and Arm recognize the need to shift from hardware-dominated to software-first development mode.

The companies aim to standardize software stack interfaces, hypervisors and chip sets so automotive partners can more easily adopt latest technologies optimized for specific needs and application scenarios.
The cooperation includes promoting a unified human-machine interface based on VirtIO to achieve standardization of the regional controller architecture.
Both parties use VirtIO technology to virtualize devices connected to central and regional ECUs.
Through PAS's open source remote GPU technology Unified HMI, a concept verification is demonstrated.
The SOAFEE blueprint and reference implementation are launched to promote the standardization of the regional controller architecture.
The cooperation aims to realize environment equivalence from the cloud to the vehicle end.
PAS's vSkipGen runs on a cloud server based on the Arm Neoverse platform.
By maintaining the same architecture and virtualization framework, the gap between virtual and physical automotive systems is narrowed.
The cooperation promotes the standardized expansion of VirtIO.
Currently, the expansion is focused on applying the VirtIO standard to cockpit scenarios.
PAS and Arm plan to expand VirtIO to more fields, including developing standardized interfaces for real-time operating systems to achieve decoupling of ADAS software from hardware.

Masashige Mizuyama, executive vice president and chief technology officer of PAS, said that the cooperation will promote the standardization of VirtIO and lay a foundation for building future automotive technologies for SDV.
Dipti Vachani, senior vice president and general manager of Arm's automotive business unit, said that SDV is an opportunity for automakers.
Dipti Vachani added that the cooperation will reduce fragmentation through standardization and accelerate the automotive development cycle.
source_title: Arm Neoverse、VirtIO、ECU、PAS

facts: Panasonic has defined cockpit high performance computing (HPC) and cabin user experience (UX) as important factors for planning strategies for the evolution of a mobility society and the diversification of people’s values.
The company identifies reducing environmental impact and contributing to a society that requires the reduction of CO2 emissions as key market opportunities.
Quick charging to shorten EV charging time is identified as a critical issue facing the widespread use of EVs.
Panasonic possesses industry-leading technologies that support high-voltage, high-powered chargers and skills in power conversion efficiency.
The speed of software evolution determines the value of automobiles with the progress of SDV (Software Defined Vehicle) and integrated HPC.
Panasonic holds a large share of IVI (In-Vehicle Infortainment) by leveraging enhanced operability acquired from know-how on digital AV and development capability of large-scale software.
The company has the ability to propose a platform that integrates various types of large-scale software necessary for HPC.
With the shift to EVs and autonomous driving, the added value of automobiles is shifting to the value of the experience in the mobility space.
Panasonic leverages knowledge of people acquired from housing and consumer electronics businesses and the ability to design experiences that enhance the value of mobility.
Top management’s commitment to environmental contribution and the ability to execute tasks steadily together with all employees are cited as core strengths.
source_title: Market Opportunities and Our Strengths

facts: Panasonic's multi-billion, roughly five million-square-foot facility is just months away from opening in De Soto, Kansas.
Kansas Gov. Laura Kelly has lauded the project as a first-of-its-kind investment and the largest economic development project in the state's 161-year history.
The plant, with an estimated April 2025 opening date, is expected to be the largest battery factory in the world.
The project started with Senate Bill 347, the Attracting Powerful Economic Expansion Act (APEX) in 2022.
The goal of the now-expired APEX legislation was to create tools for success in Kansas when competing for large economic development projects.
According to the bill, the APEX funding mechanism provides tax incentives for firms that agree to invest at least $1 billion in Kansas within a five-year period.
The legislation allowed the state to enter into one agreement in 2022 and 2023, with all APEX incentives utilized for Panasonic.

Expert researchers from the economic development nonprofit Good Jobs First said data from the IRS shows local tax incentives are only a small portion of the average major company in America's total cost structure.
Good Jobs First researchers believe local incentives don't usually determine where companies go but governments overspend in mega deals like Panasonic to be competitive.

Greg LeRoy, executive director for Good Jobs First, stated that individual battery plants will get more than $1 billion per year, per factory from the federal government for several years early in their production cycles, which alone could pay for the factories and payrolls before revenue from selling batteries.
Panasonic's capital investment into the De Soto facility totals $4 billion.
The plant is receiving far more than its initial investment from state, local and federal governments, a rare occurrence according to Good Jobs First.
Greg LeRoy noted that Uncle Sam is already paying these companies a great deal of money for every battery they take off the loading dock.
Panasonic could earn $6.8 billion in federal tax incentives from the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
The IRA's Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit, the 45X credit, is expected to pay over $1 billion per year to Panasonic and seven other factory deals.
Kansas' total APEX investment is $829.2 million, indicating a huge stake in the success of the plant.
The incentives are not guaranteed but are provided on a reimbursement basis after Panasonic hires workers and makes its own investments.

Kansas Department of Commerce breakdown includes: Investment Tax Credit of $500 million (12.5%) over 5 years; Payroll Rebate of $234 million (10%) over 10 years; Training and Education of $5 million per year over 5 years; Relocation Investment of $1 million per year over 10 years; Sales Tax Exemption of $60.2 million over 5 years.
The Department of Commerce believes every $1 of Kansas APEX incentives will generate $26.06 of private investment.
The De Soto City Council unanimously approved a development agreement with Panasonic in October 2023.
The city offered a 25% reduction in Panasonic's water and sewer utilities since the company will be the highest water purchaser.
The city offered a 50% reduction in building permit fees.
The city offered a 20-year Tax Increment Financing incentive.
The city's TIF incentive for the company is in exchange for more than $200 million in public infrastructure projects.
TIF financing uses property tax collected from the increased value of the development to reimburse the developer for costs of infrastructure improvements needed to make the development possible.

De Soto Mayor Rick Walker stated that through the TIF, they are forgoing roughly $40 million in property tax over 20 years and will receive $248 million worth of infrastructure all at once upon completion.

Mayor Walker explained the company will still pay taxes every year but will be reimbursed because of the initial investment, noting they have paid roughly $200 million on city projects to collect back over 20 years.

City projects being supported by Panasonic include the redevelopment of the former Sunflower Army Ammunition Plant and significant infrastructure and services to support the new facility and future growth.

Supported project breakdown includes: $40 million sewer treatment plant expansion; $28 million sewer line extension; $47 million water treatment plant expansion; $19 million new fire station and truck; $84.2 million road improvements; $11 million new water tower.

According to the city's development agreement, Panasonic must remit annual payments of $800,000 to fund operation of a new fire station, police services and road maintenance via a community improvement district.
Twenty-eight mills of property tax for education are protected per Kansas law, with 20 mills going to the state of Kansas and eight mills to USD 232.
The city's latest estimate is a tax payment of approximately $14 million to USD 232 and an estimated $35 million to the state of Kansas for education over the 20-year life of the TIF.

Greg LeRoy suggested local neighbors ask who the building crews are, if construction companies are local or union, if they have apprenticeship programs for women and people of color, or if it is mostly itinerant workers sending money back to Texas and Tennessee.

Mayor Walker said Panasonic is already making a tangible difference and return on investment, as the city was able to cut six mills out of the 2025 budget, reducing city property taxes by roughly a third for taxpayers.
Mayor Walker added that quality of life enhancements will be seen while maintaining a low property tax burden on citizens of De Soto.
Panasonic is required to make a minimum $1 billion investment in the project and employ at least 2,500 full-time employees to maintain the incentive level.
The incentive agreement will be measured every year, with Panasonic required to provide documentation showing the commitment is being met annually.
Researchers with Good Jobs First have been tracking economic development deals like Panasonic for 27 years.
Good Jobs First stated that when a package crosses the $1 billion mark, financial risks are much higher for states and communities.
Good Jobs First believes local governments take on the biggest risk in these deals since they are obligating property taxes over a long period of time.

Greg LeRoy emphasized it is up to residents to remain educated and officials to ensure company obligations are met, stating accountability falls on the mayor, county executive, Congress member, and senators regarding who gets jobs, local hiring, and job quality.
source_title: How much is the government investing in Panasonic? Explaining tax incentives

facts: Peterson Spring is a privately held manufacturer of engineered metal products that designs, manufactures and supplies springs, snap-rings, wire forms, clips, clamps, stampings and a variety of sub-assemblies.
The company traces its corporate history back 100 years to 1914, when Norwegian blacksmith August Peterson arrived in Michigan to produce goods for the burgeoning railroad and automotive industries.
Three generations of Petersons ran the company through 2008, when a six-member management team was brought in.
All but the chief financial officer on the new management team had backgrounds in engineering.
The new management team centralized operations, created standardization in quality systems, cost-estimating systems, and communications to share efficiencies across divisions.
Don Lowe serves as Senior Vice President for sales and marketing at Peterson Spring.
Ron Check is the vice president for engineering and technology at Peterson Spring.
The company currently manufactures engine valve springs for General Motors and has expanded its customer base to other OEMs, marine engines, snowmobiles, and Jet Skis.
Peterson Spring has expanded with plants in the United Kingdom, Canada, and Mexico.
Agricultural and automotive opportunities in South America and/or Asia may be next on the company's expansion agenda.
Lowe stated that current market dynamics have shifted back onshore after years of off-shoring, as rising wages and transportation costs in Asia eroded previous cost advantages and quality declined.
Some of the company’s pre-recession competition is no longer around, validating Peterson Spring’s mantra of innovation, technology investment and in-house manufacturing discipline.
The company's corporate headquarters is located in Southfield, Michigan.
source_title: Peterson Spring - Manufacturers of Engineered Metal Products | Business View Magazine

facts: Global auto parts manufacturer PHA is building a $67 million facility in Chatham County.
The Tier 1 supplier will make door modules, tailgate latches and hood latches for the nearby Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County.
Operations are scheduled to start in 2024.
The project will create 402 new jobs.
Massachusetts-based Ascend Elements recently celebrated its grand opening in Covington.
The 150,000-square-foot lithium-ion battery recycling facility can shred 30,000 metric tons of used batteries and scrap per year.
The facility employs 100 people.
It is the largest lithium-ion battery recycling plant in North America.
Sustainable technology material manufacturer Hanwha Advanced Materials Georgia (HAGA) is investing $147 million to build a manufacturing facility in Cartersville.
The project is the only facility in the U.S. to make encapsulant film used in solar cells for long-term durability.
The facility will support the nearby Qcells solar panel facility.
The project will create more than 160 new jobs.
The facility is expected to be operational next summer.
source_title: Economic Development Around the State - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: In March 1937, started business with Ford Japan Ltd., delivering prototype parts through Ford Japan to Ford Motor Company in the U.S., passing strict specification tests to be supplied as genuine Ford parts.
In October 1969, established a technical tie-up with FT Fasteners Inc. (present TRW Inc.) and manufactured plastic fasteners for the first time as a spring manufacturer.
In June 1972, established the Overseas Department after receiving inquiries from overseas companies.
In October 1972, concluded an annual export contract with GE Company and Cumins Engine Company in the U.S.
In October 1973, established K.P. Products Corporation as a joint venture with Peterson American Corporation, a major U.S. spring manufacturer.
In November 1986, established KHK of America Inc. in Illinois, U.S., later moving to Michigan in August 1988 to launch strategies for yen appreciation.
In May 1988, established Piolax Corporation in Canton, Georgia, U.S., starting production of metal springs for home electric appliances and automobiles.
In November 1991, completed construction of a second plant in the U.S. and started manufacturing plastic fasteners for automobiles in Canton, Georgia, shifting from export to local production.
In October 1998, started business with Toyota at the newly established Fuji Plant production base in Shizuoka.
In December 2000, reinforced U.S. bases by merging KHK of America Inc. and Piolax Corporation, consolidating operations in Michigan and Georgia.
In August 2001, obtained QS9000 international quality certification, allowing PIOLAX to meet the requirements of the U.S. Big 3 automakers.
source_title: History – PIOLAX, INC.

facts: The Company will start producing lithium-ion battery components at Piolax Corporation PA (Georgia), starting in the second half of 2012.
The products will be used in the battery packs of the Nissan Leaf EVs sold in the U.S.A.

Piolax has already been supplying components for the Leaf batteries in Japan, but the Georgia facility will become its first plant among Piolax' existing facilities in Europe, the U.S.A and China and other Asian countries outside Japan to manufacture battery parts.
In line with automakers' move to step up EV production overseas, the company aims to boost sales in the area related to EVs by switching to local production.
The Company will accelerate development and production of automotive components for hybrid and electric vehicles.
Piolax' major automotive businesses include precision springs for drivetrain systems and opening and closing devices, as well as plastic fasteners for interior and exterior products.
In the area of hybrid and electric cars, the company has already been manufacturing battery terminals, battery spacers, and insulators and cover terminals of motors.
These components are used in vehicles such as the Leaf and the Fuga Hybrid manufactured by Nissan, the company's major customer.
Nissan said it is aiming to increase its global sales of hybrid and electric vehicles to 700,000 units by fiscal year 2015.
Piolax is going to launch into a so far untapped inverter business to broaden its business opportunities.
The Company developed low-cost materials for coil springs used in transmissions and engines.

The Company developed and started mass-producing smaller, lighter, and more cost-efficient transmission units by using technology to analyze product stress and behavior in order to optimize the design.
The Company put increased emphasis on developing composite springs designed to raise efficiency when being installed in transmission units.
The Company also worked closely with assembly plants of its customers in order to develop products that best meet the requirements of the customers, while offering the Company's designing expertise.
The Company increased joint development activities with and deliveries to automakers in emerging nations, while stepping up development programs with Japanese automakers.
The Company developed fasteners designed for sheets that run in size from thin to thick.

Its new products include fasteners which require a minimum level of strength to be installed in vehicles, those with strong fastening power, and those that make dismantling of scrapped vehicles more efficient.
The Company is working to develop lightweight products that contribute to improving fuel efficiency.
The Company developed user-friendly net hooks for vehicle interiors.
The Company developed resin parts for lock handles, side locks and dampers for glove boxes.
In the area of valves for plastic tanks, the Company enhanced the performance and reduced the manufacturing costs of rollover valves and inlet check valves.

The Company's R&D activities in the area of valves for metal fuel tanks resulted in the development of standard products and as well as multi-functional products too, which both exceeded the performance of conventional ones at less cost.
The Company has won increased orders for its fuel supply system components, in line with growing use of fuel injection systems on motorcycles.
The Company is working on developing products for environmentally friendly vehicles such as those that run on alternative fuels and hybrids.
The Company is developing products (for batteries and motors) made from copper and frame-resistant resin materials. These products are for use on electric and hybrid electric vehicles.

The Company has been working on developing environmentally friendly technologies compliant with the Japan Automobile Manufacturers Association's directive calling for voluntary reductions of volatile organic compounds and other environmentally harmful substances.
It is also following both the End-of-Life (ELV) Vehicle Directive and the Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) in effect in Europe.
The Company has been working closely with its European operations in developing technologies that meet European REACH (Registration, Evaluation and Authorisation of CHemicals).

Automotive Products sales were 43,667 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2012, up 3.1% year-on-year due to accelerating global expansion plans to meet increased demand in emerging countries especially in Asia.
R&D expenditure for Automotive Products was 390 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2012.
Capital investment for Automotive Products was 3,548 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2012.
The Company spent 1,882 million yen in Japan and 1,728 million yen outside Japan on capital investment.

Planned capital investments for production facilities and dies by the company office are 1,431 thousand yen from Apr. 2012 to Mar. 2013, focused on updating equipment mainly to improve production efficiency with only a minor increase in production capacity.

Subsidiaries outside Japan have planned capital investments of 2,353 thousand yen for production facilities and dies from Jan. 2012 to Dec. 2012, with an unclear level of increase in production capacity due to the wide range of products made.
source_title: PIOLAX, INC. Business Report FY2012 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Piolax is a leading metal and plastic manufacturer that designs, produces, and tests fasteners, fuel systems, and open/close parts for the automotive industry.
The company was founded in Japan in 1939 with five employees.
Piolax has expanded over the years to operate in nine countries.
The company established a location in Canton, Georgia in 1988.
Piolax currently employs 2,883 people globally.
The Canton manufacturing complex employs 343 employees and spans 279,000 square feet.
Piolax has developed environmental and sustainability policies as part of its commitment to sustainability.
The company has achieved ISO 14001:2015 certification.
The company has achieved ITAF 16949:2016 certification.
Since 2016, Piolax has engaged the Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP) at Georgia Tech on various projects.
GaMEP assisted Piolax with their ISO 14001:2015 certification.
In December 2024, Betty Wurzel, Piolax compliance coordinator, earned her certification for completing the Sustainability Cohort.
The Sustainability Cohort was led by Sandra Enciso, GaMEP energy and sustainability project manager.

The year-long cohort assisted with developing and implementing prevention strategies to achieve cost savings and reduce environmental pollution through interactive education, implementation support, individual coaching, and technical assistance.
The Sustainability Cohort began with an energy assessment where participants identified the top 10 electricity- and gas-consuming equipment in their facilities.
Piolax initially assumed their 129 plastic injection machines consumed the most energy but discovered their seven chillers used more after analyzing the data.
With guidance from GaMEP, Piolax invested in energy consumption monitors that use sensors to measure and record electricity usage.
Real-time data revealed their chillers were working against each other.
Piolax started experimenting by raising the initial temperature of 50/55 degrees by five degrees on one set of chillers.
When they reached the maximum temperature allowed without experiencing issues, GaMEP helped them install a chiller sequencer that optimizes energy efficiency and performance.
Throughout the cohort, Piolax identified waste reduction opportunities such as improper fan installation and gas burnoff during heat treatment.
Piolax engaged GaMEP to help find solutions, including reversing airflow and developing a safe filtration system that conserves heat and water.
The company considered looking into an evaporation system to eliminate shipping wastewater offsite.
Kelly Grissom, GaMEP energy and sustainability project manager, introduced Piolax to the Industrial Training and Assessment Center (ITAC) Implementation Grant.
The ITAC Implementation Grant helps manufacturers implement onsite assessment recommendations.
In June 2023, Piolax initiated a solar project.
One year later, Piolax installed their first solar array.
The first solar array produced 306 MWh, equating to offsetting 246.79 k of coal or planting 3.69 trees.
Piolax is planning to install a second solar array.
Piolax applied for and received the ITAC Implementation Grant for the second solar array installation.
The second solar array installation is scheduled to begin in May 2025.
Without the grant, the solar arrays would have taken eight to 10 years to pay for themselves.
With the grant, Piolax anticipates a payback period of just four years for the solar arrays.
Since participating in the Sustainability Cohort two years ago, Piolax has significantly reduced their energy consumption and operational costs.
Betty Wurzel stated that the cohort fostered a community for sharing concerns and ideas without judgment and was enlightening, motivating, and worth the time investment.
source_title: Piolax Success Story- GaMEP at Georgia Tech

facts: Piolax, Inc. reported FY2007 sales of 48,820 million JPY, representing a 15.1% increase from FY2006.
Operating income for FY2007 was 4,074 million JPY, up 74.1% from FY2006.
Ordinary income for FY2007 was 4,877 million JPY, up 56.6% from FY2006.
Current net income for FY2007 was 3,281 million JPY, up 65.6% from FY2006.
Piolax enhanced its production capacity mainly in Asia to cope with rising demand and launched commercial operations at a new plant in China to strengthen its regional supply network.
In the precision spring segment, Piolax has alliances with Korean and Japanese carmakers to conduct joint development activities.
Following joint development activities in Thailand and Mexico, Piolax completed similar activities with a Japanese carmaker in China and launched commercial production.
Major carmakers in and outside Japan are inquiring about Piolax's side locks for glove boxes, expecting an increase in vehicle models equipped with these locks.
Piolax's share of new dampers grew due to increased joint development activities with carmakers in Japan, Europe, the U.S.A., and Korea.
A greater number of carmakers outside Japan are adopting Piolax's in-tank evaporation systems for use in metal tanks equipped on their vehicle models.
Piolax will begin local production of automotive metal parts in China in fiscal 2008 to meet growing needs from local Japanese car manufacturers, including Nissan and Honda.
The company will study the possibility of adding a new facility to its existing plant in Guangdong Province, China.
Producing metal parts in addition to resin parts will allow Piolax to triple its automotive sales in China to 2.2 billion yen by 2010.
The supplier will add production lines on the premises of the new Chinese plant to make metal parts.
Piolax plans to expand its business with US automakers and has already acquired a new order from Ford Motor Company for pickup truck components.
Piolax will send engineers to Ford R&D departments to win more orders by getting directly involved with development activities.
The company will begin supplying fasteners to clip exterior and interior parts to bodies of new Ford pickup trucks planned for release in 2010.
Fastener production for Ford will begin in the latter half of 2009 at Piolax's Thai plant.
Piolax has a plan to expand its capacity to produce glove box side lock components for General Motors Corporation.
By fiscal 2010, Piolax aims to triple its sales to GM to 600 million yen compared to the current level.
These efforts will allow Piolax to raise its ratio of sales to US makers to 7 to 8% in ten years, which is about 2.5 times the current level.
R&D expenses were 544 million yen in FY2007.
R&D expenditure on automotive products was 427 million yen.
Piolax developed compressed coil spring materials used in engines and gears.
The company commercialized products that are more compact and lighter in weight.
Piolax strengthened its development activities on complex springs, enabling them to be installed easier.
The company worked on developing surface coatings for spring materials that do not contain hexavalent chromium.
Piolax started development activities on products that comply with the new REACH Regulation in Europe.

The company is developing versatile fasteners that can be used on both thin and thick metal plates, fasteners that require less force when being installed, fasteners with very powerful fastening capabilities, and fasteners that are easier to disassemble during car scrapping.
Piolax is considering migrating to materials that emit less odor in vehicle interiors while working to develop environmentally friendly products that enhance interior comfort.
The company is currently developing high-strength, user-friendly net hooks used in car interior parts.
Piolax developed side locks for glove boxes and released them in January 2003, which are now being equipped on an increasing number of vehicle models to improve performance and workability.
The company worked on developing new dampers.
Piolax is currently developing hinge parts to open/close the console box lid and the floor board in the rear luggage compartment.
In the area of valves for plastic tanks, Piolax enhanced the performance and reduced the manufacturing costs of rollover valves and inlet check valves.

As of March 2008, Piolax has licensing-in agreements with Norma Germany GmbH for automotive fuel delivery components and plastic fuel tank components, with contracts renewing annually or covering terms from September 18, 1995 to December 12, 2006.

As of March 2008, Piolax has licensing-out agreements for manufacturing technology to Sanjia Industrial Co., Ltd. (Taiwan) for metallic and plastic fasteners, Taiwan Houmu Industrial Co., Ltd. for automotive clutch pressure springs, torsion springs, and engine valve springs, and various Piolax affiliates in the USA, UK, Korea, Thailand, and China for precision plastic slips, precision springs, and related assembly products.
In FY2007, Piolax's capital investments totaled 3,567 million yen, mainly spent on automotive parts production facilities.
The company spent 2,469 million yen in Japan and 1,098 million yen outside Japan for capital investments.

Piolax Inc. Yokohama Technical Center in Yokohama City, Japan is a design and development facility with an expected investment of 37 thousand yen for the plant and 391 thousand yen for molds, starting in April 2008 and completing in March 2009.

Piolax Inc. Mooka Plant in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan is a plant facility with an expected investment of 159 thousand yen for the plant, 474 thousand yen for production facilities, and 593 thousand yen for molds, starting in April 2008 and completing in March 2009.

Piolax Inc. Fuji Plant in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan is a plant facility with an expected investment of 9 thousand yen for the plant, 361 thousand yen for production facilities, and 85 thousand yen for molds, starting in April 2008 and completing in March 2009.

Piolax Inc. Kyushu Plant in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan is a plant facility with an expected investment of 13 thousand yen for the plant, 142 thousand yen for production facilities, and 38 thousand yen for molds, starting in April 2008 and completing in March 2009.

Piolax Medical Devices, Inc. in Yokohama City, Japan is a plant facility with an expected investment of 13 thousand yen for the plant, 56 thousand yen for production facilities, and 1 thousand yen for molds, starting in April 2008 and completing in March 2009.

Piolax Corp. Head Office/Plant in Georgia, USA is a plant facility with an expected investment of 4 thousand yen for the plant, 275 thousand yen for production facilities, and 57 thousand yen for molds, starting in January 2008 and completing in December 2009.
Piolax Ltd. Head Office/Plant in Lancashire, UK is a production facilities facility with an expected investment of 55 thousand yen, starting in January 2008 and completing in December 2009.

Piolax Co., Ltd Head Office/Plant in Inchon, Korea is a production facilities facility with an expected investment of 92 thousand yen for the plant and 65 thousand yen for molds, starting in January 2008 and completing in December 2009.

Piolax (Thailand) Ltd. Plant in Rayong, Thailand is a buildings facility with an expected investment of 175 thousand yen for buildings, 115 thousand yen for production facilities, and 70 thousand yen for molds, starting in January 2008 and completing in December 2009.

Dongguan Piolax Co., Ltd. in Dongguan, China is a plant facility with an expected investment of 234 thousand yen for the plant, 669 thousand yen for production facilities, and 190 thousand yen for molds, starting in January 2008 and completing in December 2009.
source_title: PIOLAX, INC. Business Report FY2007 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Overseas, Piolax Corporation manufactures and sells automotive parts in Georgia, USA.
The main products in the automotive-related, etc. business are industrial fasteners (parts used for fastening interior and exterior automobile trim) and precision springs.

The automotive industry, which is the Piolax Group's main customer, continues to face a severe business environment, with both production and sales volumes declining year-on-year, along with revisions to supply chains and production systems, sluggish sales by Japanese automakers in the Chinese automotive market, a rapid shift toward EVs, and sluggish growth in the U.S. automotive market.
Net sales for the automotive-related segment decreased by 1,407 million yen (△2.4%) year-on-year to 56,770 million yen due to production cuts at major Japanese automaker customers.

Operating profit for the automotive-related segment decreased by 727 million yen (△23.5%) year-on-year to 2,368 million yen, primarily due to a decrease in marginal profit accompanying production cuts at Japanese automakers.

Domestically, Piolax HFS Co., Ltd., PNS Co., Ltd., Piolax Kyushu Co., Ltd., and K&K Co., Ltd. handle manufacturing, selling primarily through Piolax to Nissan Motor Co., Ltd., other automobile manufacturers and parts manufacturers, and others.
Piolax forecasts consolidated net sales of 63,000 million yen for the fiscal year ending March 2027.
The assumed exchange rate for the fiscal year ending March 2027 forecast is 1 USD = 150 yen.
source_title: [Piolax 5988.T] Fiscal Year Ended March 2026 Consolidated Financial Results [Japanese GAAP] — BigGo Finance

facts: Piolax is a leading supplier of plastic and metal fastening products with global marketing, knowledge, and technological capabilities.
To meet the demands of the global automotive industry, Piolax established a U.S. presence in Canton, Georgia, in August 1988.
The company's in-house manufacturing expertise enhances its ability to deliver high-quality products to the automotive market.
Piolax is a major manufacturer of precision automotive springs and industrial fasteners.
Automotive products account for 95% of Piolax's sales.
The company partners with all major domestic automobile and truck manufacturers, as well as housing equipment manufacturers.
Piolax holds a leading market share in each product category it serves.

As the industry evolves, especially with the rise of electric vehicles, Piolax remains committed to developing and providing innovative products to meet the needs of a wide range of electronic and automotive applications.
Piolax’s product offerings fall into three main categories: Fasteners, Fuel Systems, and Open/Close Mechanisms.
Piolax’s fasteners are produced using advanced elastic microfabrication technology, material processing techniques for metals and synthetic resins, and applied technology for shape-memory alloys.
source_title: Piolax – Global Distributor of Electronic Components

facts: PIOLAX Corporation maintains an office at 139 Etowah Industrial Court Canton, GA 30114.
The company also operates a Michigan office located at 47075 Five Mile Rd. Plymouth, MI 48170.
source_title: Contact Us | PIOLAX

facts: FY2006 sales were 42,418 million JPY, a 3.2% increase from FY2005, supported by strong overseas sales.
Operating income was 2,340 million JPY (0.1% increase), with gains offset by soaring material prices and increased depreciation costs from vigorous capital spending.
Ordinary income was 3,114 million JPY (1.1% increase).
Current net income was 1,981 million JPY (20.4% increase).
Japan sales amounted to 34,176 million JPY (up 679 million JPY), with operating income down 3 million JPY to 1,920 million JPY due to material price hikes and higher fixed costs.
North America sales increased 79 million JPY to 6,215 million JPY, driven by U.S. subsidiary marketing targeting major Japanese carmakers, the Big Three of the USA, and Tier-1 suppliers.
North America operating income was 199 million JPY (up 68 million JPY), impacted by soaring material prices and special costs for expanding the Mexico plant.
Other regions sales reached 4,682 million JPY (up 989 million JPY), with operating income up 102 million JPY to 177 million JPY.
Production structure strengthening in Japan involved integrating precision spring production and augmenting fuel system components and open-close mechanism parts volume.
Major overseas investment activities included a plant expansion in Mexico and land purchase in China prior to an expansion/relocation project starting in the first half of 2007.
Sales to Hyundai Motor Co. (Korea) and Ford Motor Co. (U.S.A.) increased by 1.5-fold compared to the previous term.
Overseas sales have more than doubled since the Nissan Revival Plan period, when 90% of sales were from Nissan and the domestic market.
In FY2006, 15% of sales came from North America, while Europe and Asia sales reached two-digit percentages for the first time.
Overseas sales amount doubled the 2000 results.
The medium-term business plan aims to expand Asian region sales by 2.2-fold from 2005 to 4 billion JPY over three years.
The company targets large demand increase in Korea, marking the 10th year of business there, with a target of 1 billion JPY sales for the term ending March 2007.

In February 2007, Piolax decided to acquire all stocks of Sankyo Hatsujo Co., Ltd. (Oume City, Tokyo), making it a wholly-owned subsidiary and merging it with P.N.S Co., Ltd. (Yokohama City, Kanagawa Pref.) on April 1, 2007, to consolidate precision spring production.
R&D expenses were 421 million JPY in FY2006, with 306 million JPY allocated to automotive products.

Automotive R&D focused on precision springs (compression coil springs for engines/transmissions), composite springs, and hexavalent-chromium-free surface treatments to meet EU End-of-Life Vehicle Directive, RoHS, REACH, and Japan Automobile Manufacturers Association regulations.

Joint development activities with Japanese carmakers were reinforced, along with technological ties to a Korean car maker and joint development with Japanese automakers in China leading to mass production.
Industrial fasteners R&D developed products for various sheet thicknesses, easy assembly to auto bodies, high strength, easy scrapping, and odor-reducing interior materials.

Compact units R&D included new lock mechanisms for glove boxes (mounted on vehicles launched January 2003), new dampers (developed with domestic and overseas manufacturers in Europe, U.S., Korea), hinge mechanism parts for console box lids/floor boards using oil dampers, and better performance filtering elements for transmissions.

Fuel systems R&D developed in-tank evaporator systems for metal tanks to meet USA evaporator regulations, valves (rollover and inlet check valves) for plastic tanks, and products for alternative fuels like bio diesel and alcohol fuel.

Licensing of technology from Norma Germany GmbH covers automotive fuel delivery components and plastic fuel tank components via approved patents, renewed every 5 years starting August 18, 1995 and December 13, 2001.

Licensing of technology to other companies includes Sanjia Industrial Co., Ltd. (Taiwan) for metallic/plastic fasteners, Taiwan Houmu Industrial Co., Ltd. for automotive clutch/torsion/valve springs, and various Piolax subsidiaries in USA, UK, Korea, Thailand, and China for precision plastic slips/springs/assembly products under indefinite or annual renewal terms.
FY2006 capital investments totaled 3,986 million JPY, mainly for automotive parts production facilities, with 3,006 million JPY in Japan and 980 million JPY overseas.

New facility investments include Yokohama Technical Center (49,811 thousand JPY, April 2007-March 2008), Mooka Plant (100,750 thousand JPY, November 2007-March 2008), Fuji Plant (1,000 thousand JPY plant + 188,950 thousand JPY facilities, April 2007-March 2008), and Kyushu Plant (114,400 thousand JPY plant + 150,555 thousand JPY facilities + 53,900 thousand JPY molds, April 2007-March 2008).

Piolax Corp. Head Office/Plant in Georgia, USA has an expected investment of 9,900 thousand JPY for the plant, with production facilities at 185,460 thousand JPY and molds at 91,850 thousand JPY, scheduled to start in January 2007 and complete in December 2007.

Additional new facility investments include Lancashire UK (88,400 thousand JPY plant + 19,700 thousand JPY facilities), Inchon Korea (55,200 thousand JPY facilities + 83,000 thousand JPY molds), Rayong Thailand (360 thousand JPY buildings + 106,607 thousand JPY facilities + 112,226 thousand JPY molds), and Dongguan China (30,339 thousand JPY plant + 280,610 thousand JPY facilities + 193,943 thousand JPY molds), all starting January 2007 and completing December 2007.
source_title: PIOLAX, INC. Business Report FY2006 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

Harpak-ULMA Packaging Inc. (Spain) operates in Cherokee County focusing on Industrial Machinery and Equipment.
Inalfa Roof Systems (Netherlands) supplies Automotive Roofing Systems in Cherokee County.
Jaipur Living (India) manufactures Rugs, Throws, Pillows, and Poufs in Cherokee County.
Meyn America (Netherlands) operates in Cherokee County focusing on Industrial Machinery and Equipment.
MSK Covertech (Germany) operates in Cherokee County focusing on Industrial Machinery and Equipment.
Pilgrims (Brazil) operates in Cherokee County focusing on Poultry Processing.
