facts: PTNA stands for Pirelli Tire North America (Rome, GA).
Pirelli Tire North America is listed among companies headquartered in Georgia.
Pirelli Tire North America, New Haven, CT, has started construction of a tire manufacturing plant in Rome, GA.
source_title: PTNA - Pirelli Tire North America (Rome, GA) | AcronymFinder

facts: The plant, featuring Pirelli’s MIRS production system, supplies OE tires for various Ford vehicles.
Francesco Gori, managing director of Pirelli Tire, stated the Q1 award is a prestigious indication of the company's rapidly developing capability in the U.S.
Pirelli Tire North America is focused on selective growth in both the replacement and original equipment markets in the U.S., with Ford being very much part of that strategy.
Q1 Accreditation is a set of quality and manufacturing guidelines developed by Ford for its suppliers, which Pirelli met or exceeded each criteria for.
source_title: Pirelli Gets Q1 Status from Ford - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Pirelli is continuing its expansion in the United States with a $15 million investment that will boost manufacturing capacity by 20 percent at its factory in Rome, Ga.
The plant produces high performance tires for the North American market using its MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) technology.
Rome’s MIRS production lines will increase from four to five.
The facility manufactures tires for both the original equipment and replacement segments.
According to the company, MIRS is the world’s most advanced robotized system for manufacturing high performance tires.
Hugh Pace, chairman and CEO of Pirelli Tire North America, states that the Rome-based MIRS facility supports Pirelli’s strategic growth plan for the North American market.
The company has achieved significant share of market gains and a 30 percent sales increase over the past three years.
The new expansion of the MIRS manufacturing facility will bring high-impact jobs to the Rome area.
The expansion demonstrates Pirelli’s strategy of investing in its ongoing partnership with the state of Georgia and its office of economic development.
Pirelli’s decision to increase production of high performance tires in Georgia is part of its North American growth strategy begun at the start of the decade.
The company now supplies its ultra-high performance original equipment tires to some of General Motors’ and Ford’s highest performing car models, including the new Camaro and Mustang respectively.
Pirelli also supplies tires to Mercedes Benz and BMW transplant models.
MIRS was inaugurated in 2000 and is a highly automated system that allows for flexible and rapid response to market demand.
The MIRS system makes it possible to operate efficiently even in markets where the cost of labor is high.
Robots manage the entire production cycle without interruption and without having to move semi-finished products.
There is no need for intermediate warehousing phases, thereby maximizing energy efficiency during the manufacturing process.
Integrated software coordinates all steps of production starting with product design, thus cutting costs and production time.
Pirelli works in conjunction with Georgia Quick Start – a state-of-the-art employee training program.
Quick Start is a state run program that customizes training for qualified businesses in Georgia, including Pirelli.

The program delivers proprietary on-site instruction to Pirelli workers, equipping them with the latest skills and strategies needed to operate the MIRS manufacturing facility and manage its expansion.
Andrew Kearton, Pirelli’s vice president of manufacturing and original equipment sales, expressed pride in Pirelli’s commitment to growing its Rome-based MIRS manufacturing facility.

The additional workforce positions being added to support the expansion are high-skill jobs that will operate a state-of-the-art automation process recognized as the most advanced in the tire industry.
Pirelli looks forward to continuing its relationships with local colleges and the Georgia QuickStart program to help realize this growth.
source_title: Pirelli invests $15 million for fifth manufacturing line, cites 30 percent sales increase | Fender Bender

facts: Pirelli Tire North America designs, develops, manufactures and markets tires for passenger vehicles in both the original equipment and replacement markets.
Pirelli Tire North America markets and distributes tires for motorcycle and motorsports.
Pirelli's manufacturing facility is located in Rome, Georgia.
The Rome, Georgia facility utilizes Pirelli’s Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS).
The MIRS system employs state-of-the-art technology to manufacture tires for both export and domestic markets.
source_title: PIRELLI RACE REPORT: Pirelli Goes One-Two In South America - Motocross Action Magazine

facts: The Motor Enclave announced a sponsorship agreement with Pirelli Tire North America to become "The Official Tire" of the venue.
The partnership branding includes naming the pit lane "The Pirelli Pit Lane".
The Motor Enclave is a 200-acre experiential motorsports venue located at the corner of I-4 and I-75, less than 15 minutes from downtown Tampa, FL.
Pirelli will receive exposure in key locations throughout the development, including trademark yellow and red branding throughout the "Pirelli Pit Lane".
The "Pirelli Pit Lane" is where all events on the 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke-designed performance circuit begin and end.
The pit lane will incorporate a "Pirelli Podium" featuring authentic Pirelli Formula 1 tires for guest photos.

Brad Oleshansky, Founder and CEO of The Motor Enclave, stated that Pirelli is synonymous with innovation and quality and aligns with their mission to build the premiere motorsports venue in the world.
Andre Oosthuizen, Chief Marketing Officer of Pirelli Tire North America, noted they have tracked The Motor Enclave's development since its 2019 announcement.

Oosthuizen cited the success of Oleshansky's initial project in Michigan, M1 Concourse, as a reason for choosing this platform to highlight Pirelli's full product line across driving surfaces including the performance circuit, vehicle dynamics pad, and off-road experience.
Pirelli Tire North America includes a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
Pirelli also operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico, with sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications.
Pirelli was founded in Milan in 1872 and develops tailor-made tires through partnerships with prestigious manufacturers.

The Motor Enclave development includes a 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke-designed driving circuit, a 2-acre vehicle dynamics pad, a 100-acre off-road experience with purpose-built trails, a 37,000-square-foot corporate event center, and over 300 privately owned luxury garages.
Complete details about The Motor Enclave are available on its official website.
source_title: The Motor Enclave Tampa Announces Partnership With Pirelli Tire North America

facts: Michelin North America Inc. (MNA) is raising prices for the fourth time this year, announced on 1 Oct.
MNA is based in Greenville, South Carolina.
Effective 1 Dec, the company is increasing prices up to 4.9% for Michelin- and BFGoodrich-brand truck tires and Michelin Retread Technologies and Oliver-brand retread products in the US and Canada.
Additional pricing details will be provided directly to MNA's North American fleet account customers.
On 1 Sept, MNA increased prices on selected Michelin- and BFGoodrich-brand passenger and light-truck tires in the US and Canada by up to 5%.
On 1 July, the South Carolina-based tire maker increased prices on Uniroyal-brand commercial tires and select Michelin-brand off-highway products by as much as 6%.
The July increase followed a 1-Jan price hike of up to 5% on passenger and light-truck tire prices in the US and Canada.
The January price increase also applied to Uniroyal-brand consumer tires and Michelin-brand two-wheeler tires sold in the US and Canada.
Yokohama Tire Corp. is raising prices in the US by as much as 5% on its consumer tires, including passenger, high performance and light-truck tires, effective 1 Nov.
Pirelli Tire North America LLC raised prices on replacement market passenger and light truck tires in the US on 1 Oct but declined to disclose the amount.
Pirelli's price increase will vary by line and tire size and will be communicated directly with its customers.
Pirelli also raised prices by an average of 6% on 1 Jan.
Cooper Tire & Rubber Co. raised prices on its Cooper- and Roadmaster-brand truck tires to compensate for elevated US government import duties on truck tires from China.
Cooper's price increases were effective after the start of the second quarter, though the company declined to disclose the scale of the increase.
source_title: Michelin raising North America prices for fourth time | European Rubber Journal

facts: Tire manufacturer Pirelli is planning to invest between $1 billion and $1.2 billion in its U.S. operations over the coming years.
The investment is expected to further strengthen production at Pirelli's Rome manufacturing facility.
Pirelli's newly appointed board of directors has been informed of the multi-year investment plan.
The investment plan will be presented for formal approval at an upcoming board meeting.
The investment aims to increase Pirelli's manufacturing capacity in the United States.
The investment includes expanding production of Pirelli's Cyber Tyres.
In May, Pirelli announced that Cyber Tyres would be produced at its Rome, Georgia, plant.
The Rome, Georgia, facility is positioned at the forefront of the company's next generation of tire technology.
Cyber Tyre technology integrates sensors embedded inside the tire with software capable of transmitting real-time data to vehicles.
The Cyber Tyre system can monitor information such as tire pressure, temperature, and road conditions.
The system provides drivers and vehicle systems with enhanced safety and performance data.
The planned investment underscores the strategic importance of Pirelli's U.S. manufacturing operations as demand grows for advanced tire technologies.
Earlier this year, the Italian government stepped in to limit the influence of Pirelli's largest shareholder, Chinese state-owned chemical company Sinochem.
The Italian government cited concerns that Sinochem's ownership structure could affect Pirelli's business opportunities in the United States.
Shareholders approved a new board of directors last week that is controlled by Italian investor Camfin.
Camfin is the investment company led by longtime Pirelli executive Marco Tronchetti Provera.
Camfin owns a 26.2% stake in Pirelli, while Sinochem remains the company's largest shareholder with a 34.1% stake.
Under the new board structure, Sinochem secured just three seats on the 15-member board.
On Tuesday, the board confirmed Andrea Casaluci as Pirelli's chief executive officer.
The board appointed Marco Tronchetti Provera as executive chairman after he previously served as executive vice chairman.
Pirelli has operated one of its largest North American manufacturing facilities in Rome for decades.
The investment signals continued confidence in the Rome plant's future.
The company's decision earlier this year to manufacture Cyber Tyres in Rome places the local facility at the center of Pirelli's U.S. growth strategy.
The Rome facility is positioned to play a key role as the automotive industry increasingly adopts connected vehicle technologies.
source_title: Pirelli Plans Up to $1.2 Billion U.S. Investment, Expanding Focus on Rome Plant - Coosa Valley News

facts: Pirelli & C SpA is based in Milan, Italy and is the world’s fifth largest tyre manufacturer.
The company operates 22 factories in 13 countries around the world.
North America is one of its fast growing markets for both OEM and replacement tyre sales.
Pirelli increased production of tyres within North America to improve customer service rates and reduce logistics costs.
John Godfrey serves as vice-president of logistics and purchasing at Pirelli Tire North America.
The company shifted to more land-based transport, especially intermodal, while noting a need to expand logistics capacity from truck drivers to warehouses and rail transport.
Pirelli re-engineered its logistics services around direct deliveries and faster response times for retail customers in the US and Canada.
There is higher demand for smaller and more frequent deliveries, including next day and second-day delivery, for US and Canadian retail customers.
Increased sourcing within North America improved logistics cost and delivery performance.
An increase in Mexican production capacity and exports to the US decreased overseas container ocean imports relative to overland imports.
The reduction of transit time increased replacement tyre customer fill rates from 60% to 85%.
Within the NAFTA region, Pirelli has a tyre plant in Rome, Georgia – near Atlanta.
Pirelli serves two primary distribution channels for finished products: OEMs for vehicle production and the replacement tyre segment for the aftermarket.
The company saw strong sales growth in both retail and OEM segments in North America in recent years.
Pirelli expects close to double digit growth compared to 2013 sales.
Pirelli’s tyre sales of approximately $871.4m in North America last year accounted for 11% of its global sales.
Despite regional production expansion, Pirelli still imports most of its tyres from overseas, about double the amount compared to regional production.
Most finished tyre imports arrive in the NAFTA region by ocean container from Brazil, China, Romania, and Germany.
The company has a large share of raw material suppliers in Europe and Asia.
For the Silao plant, the majority of rubber used in production is from Southeast Asia.
Pirelli is working to develop more regional suppliers, including natural rubber in Mexico and Guatemala, and carbon black from Mexico and China.
The company's desire is to have everything in North America.

Potential re-introduction of tariffs on Chinese tyre imports to the United States could reach as high as 90%, making it very expensive to import Chinese tyres and likely requiring a change in sourcing.
Both North American and imported finished tyres currently move through seven distribution centres in North America.
In the US, Pirelli has distribution centres in Rome and McDonough, Georgia, and Fontana, California.
Two of the US distribution centres are operated by Kuehne + Nagel.
In Canada, Pirelli has warehouses in Brampton, Ontario and Vancouver, British Columbia, operated by Kuehne + Nagel and DB Schenker respectively.
In Mexico, facilities are located in Silao and Mexico City.
An upcoming tender may result in another warehouse in the US midwest or northeast.
For customers in the US and Canada, Pirelli ships mainly by full truckload from its in-country distribution warehouses.
Outbound OEM shipments go to vehicle assembly plants, fitters that attach tyres to wheels, or sequencers.
For Ford’s Kansas City plant, Pirelli ships tyres to Ceva Logistics, which sequences them for the carmaker.
From the Rome, Georgia plant, approximately 60% of finished tyres are destined to OEM plants.
The majority of tyres from the Rome plant go to Mercedes-Benz and BMW, as well as Chrysler and Ford.
From Mexico to the US, Pirelli mainly uses Kansas City Southern Railway through Texas, then ships to its warehouse in McDonough.
Ocean freight coming to McDonough arrives at the ports of Savannah, Georgia and Charleston, South Carolina.
For the Fontana warehouse, shipments use the ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach.
For Brampton, shipments use Montreal, Vancouver, and Philadelphia primarily.
Pirelli aims to maximize direct delivery volumes to North American customers.
The company ships directly from its Rome plant to OEM customers whenever possible.
In August, Pirelli began shipping directly from its Silao plant to Ford’s Flat Rock, Michigan plant.
Pirelli does not ship directly to Mercedes-Benz in Alabama or BMW in South Carolina from its plant; many of their tyres come from the UK, Germany, Romania, and Mexico.
The company builds trucks with multiple sequenced SKUs at its McDonough warehouse for these OEMs.
Some replacement tyre volume ships from overseas factories directly to US customers’ distribution centres.
Pirelli strives to balance costs with service levels, particularly for retail customers demanding shorter transit times compared to wholesalers.
The company is pushing more business to retailers rather than wholesalers for its aftermarket business due to shorter cycle times.
Pirelli delivers to four national crossdocks operated by Exel for its largest replacement tyre customer, Discount Tire, shipping one or two trucks per day.
In 2012, Pirelli introduced a ‘Tier 1’ programme, a make-to-order system for delivering replacement tyres to Dealer Tire and Tire Rack.
The Tier 1 programme includes a collaborative planning forecasting and replenishment system to reduce warehouse and pipeline inventories.
Pirelli hopes to expand the Tier 1 initiative to other North American customers, requiring sufficient volume such as one container per month.
Production and OEM customer growth in Mexico led to a higher share of intermodal transport from Mexico to the US, currently about 75% by intermodal container and 25% by truck.
Passenger and light truck tyres require special pallets or racks for transport.
Pirelli is investing in new pallets in the NAFTA region and designing an optimal pallet using specialised steel and plastic globally.
The product portfolio has shifted toward premium tyres with larger diameters, requiring effective racking accommodations.
In Canada, some deliveries use racks that fit into 53ft trailers.
Intermodal transportation is half the cost of truck transportation, with a target intermodal percentage of 80-90%.
Mexico’s growth increased the number of intermodal carriers used along with Mexico’s two Class-A railways: Ferromex and Kansas City Southern Mexico.
Transport in Mexico presents challenges ranging from capacity to security issues.

Across North America, there is a general driver shortage and gaps in transport capacity, partly attributed to increased US government regulations over hours of service and monitoring of driver safety records.
Labour disputes with the longshoremen union have threatened strikes at west coast ports, making long distance just-in-time shipments more difficult.
There is a shortage of team drivers available for expedited shipments.
Pirelli monitors shipments to OEM customers around the clock and limits the number of carriers handling just-in-time business to ensure quality service.
The company is organizing a 3PL tender and network re-engineering for its US and Canadian warehousing and distribution system.
Network re-engineering may demonstrate that a third US warehouse in the midwest or northeast could be more efficient and enable better customer service.
Pirelli generally consolidates to a single drayage carrier for each distribution centre.
For replacement tyre shipments, preferred drayage companies transfer containers from ports.
Threats of west coast port strikes and congestion at the port of Los Angeles caused problems picking up containers, leading to delays in direct deliveries to some customers in southern California.
Pirelli foresees more direct deliveries from Mexican production and overseas imports to OEMs and wholesale customers in North America.
The company is arranging more direct deliveries and switching to building more make-to-order tyres with total cycle times of around 90 days.
In the United States, making-to-order saves Pirelli millions of dollars per year.

Pirelli delivers more small shipments to tyre retailers using a special order management system based on larger retailers' systems, operating on a no-backorder policy with demands for delivery within 48 hours.
The company is seeing an evolution between using distributors to handle finished products and using its own distribution centres.
Distributors can bundle Pirelli tyres with those of other manufacturers for faster delivery within a few hours.
In Mexico, Pirelli switched from using distributors to opening its own facility in Mexico City four months ago due to delays, stocking it with 1,000 tyres and replenishing it three times per week.
The increase in local production and sourcing supports the company's aim for speedier, more direct delivery while cutting inventory costs.
source_title: A stronger grip on the market | Feature | Automotive Logistics

facts: Kuehne + Nagel will manage inbound receipt, storage and distribution of Pirelli tires to customers across America.
The logistics selection is part of Pirelli’s new ‘Logistics and Customer Care Action Plan’ targeted to enhance its total client service.
Pirelli chose Kuehne + Nagel for its optimization capabilities, transition expertise, and proven industry experience.
Guy Mannino, President and CEO of Pirelli Tire North America, stated that the company listened intently to trade customers to better understand their needs, particularly regarding delivery service.
Guy Mannino commented that the new arrangement with Kuehne + Nagel will represent a major step forward in logistics service and a major contribution to expanding business in North America.
Pirelli Tire North America specializes in the manufacture and marketing of high performance car, light truck and motorcycle tires.
Pirelli currently has three highly advanced MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) modules in operation in its new factory in Rome, Georgia.
The Rome, Georgia factory operates to better serve the American OEM and Replacement markets.

Kuehne + Nagel provides an in-place network of 40 multi-client distribution centers with 11 million square feet of space in North America for companies needing high-performance, flexible distribution infrastructure.
source_title: Pirelli Tire North America selects Kuehne + Nagel to optimize and manage North American logistics

facts: Pirelli North America announced it will manufacture its Cyber Tyre in its Rome plant in the near future.
The Cyber Tyre is a combined hardware and software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires.
The system processes and communicates collected information in real time with the vehicle’s electronics.
Pirelli North America CEO Claudio Zanardo stated that the start of Cyber Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country.

Claudio Zanardo noted the move reflects Pirelli's commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber Tyre closer to the market and strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
Specific investment and expansion details will be communicated in the coming months following finalization of development plans, according to Pirelli officials.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the Modular Integrated Robotized System production process at the Rome plant.
The new manufacturing process will be the most advanced for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group and is exclusive to the Rome factory.
Floyd County Commissioner Scotty Hancock stated that the announcement signals Pirelli’s commitment to Rome and Floyd County and called them an important community partner.
The initial investment for the project is approximately $20 million.
Pirelli expects to create as many as 20 new jobs initially.
The Rome plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility hosts a dedicated R&D center to strengthen its role in technological development and product innovation.
The facility is located at 100 Pirelli Drive in the Floyd County Industrial Park in Lindale.
The Rome plant currently employs approximately 225 people.
source_title: Pirelli to manufacture Cyber Tyre in Rome - NewsBreak

facts: Pirelli announced that it is withdrawing as race tire supplier for the Grand-Am Rolex Sports Car Series after the 2010 racing season.
Pirelli plans to redeploy resources into other motorsport activities.
Pirelli, a Rome, Ga.-based tiremaker, signed a three-year exclusive supply deal with the series, beginning with the 2008 season.
Continental signed a five-year pact with Grand-Am Road Racing to become title sponsor and exclusive tire supplier for its Sports Car Challenge.

Bill Caldwell, CTNA consumer tire vice president of sales and marketing, stated that the entitlement sponsorship adds an entirely new dimension to their marketing strategy to promote the Continental brand.
Bill Caldwell noted that Grand-Am Road Racing is one of the highest-caliber racing series in the world and expressed pride and excitement about being a part of it.
Bill Caldwell mentioned looking forward to the start of the 2010 season.
source_title: Continental In, Pirelli Out of Grand-Am Series - AftermarketNews

facts: Pirelli Tire North America has announced that Claudio Zanardo will replace outgoing CEO Pietro Berardi as CEO, effective November 1.
Outgoing CEO Pietro Berardi is leaving the company to pursue a new professional challenge.
New CEO Claudio Zanardo joins the North American team from Pirelli’s Milan headquarters, where he most recently served as senior vice president, Moto and Velo.
Zanardo has extensive experience in the automotive industry, including working for Ducati Motor Holding.
As CEO of the North American region, Zanardo will be responsible for the car, motorcycle, and cycling businesses.
Zanardo will oversee manufacturing facilities in Rome, Georgia, and Silao, Guanajuato, Mexico.
In 2015, Zanardo joined Pirelli overseeing marketing and controlled retail for Region Europe.
He assumed responsibility for Driver Italia (Pirelli’s franchise network) in 2016.
He was appointed country CEO of Italy before moving to the role of senior vice president, Moto, in January 2020.
In February 2021, he was given responsibility for Business Velo.
source_title: Movers & Shakers: Pirelli Tire North America Names New CEO | Specialty Equipment Market Association (SEMA)

facts: Pirelli Tire North America is based in Rome, Ga.
Pirelli Tire North America announced that it is increasing prices on all of its passenger and light truck/SUV tires by between 3 and 5 percent.
The price increase will take effect this December.
The increase was due to escalating raw material costs.
Pirelli SpA announced a global 3 to 5 percent price increase on all types, sizes and brands of tires the company makes around the world.
Yokohama Tire Corp. is increasing prices across the board on its consumer, commercial and OTR equipment tires effective Dec. 31.
Prices on passenger and light truck/SUV tires from Yokohama will increase 3 to 4 percent, while commercial lines and OTR tire lines will increase by 3 to 5 percent.
Yokohama cited rising business costs, particularly rising raw material costs, for the price adjustments.
source_title: Pirelli, Yokohama Announce Price Increases - AftermarketNews

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced a new multi-million dollar investment to further develop its North American headquarters and U.S. footprint in Georgia.
The new development is located at the northeast corner of Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The 33-acre development adds to PCNA's existing 27-acre headquarters.
Construction for the first stage of the development began in September 2020.
The first stage consists of a standalone Porsche Service Center independently developed, owned, and operated jointly by Hennessy Porsche North Atlanta and Porsche Atlanta Perimeter.
PCNA will invest in later stages over the next three years, including a new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility and a track extension for the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta.

Kjell Gruner, newly appointed President and CEO of PCNA, stated that Porsche is committed to the United States and sees a prime opportunity to enhance the customer experience by expanding its campus next to the world's busiest airport.
The Porsche Experience Center Atlanta has welcomed over 270,000 visitors since opening in 2015.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia’s automotive industry is a $3 billion economic driver in the state and praised Porsche's continued commitment to Georgia.
Mayor Alan Hallman of Hapeville expressed excitement for Porsche breaking ground and expanding its footprint within the city, symbolizing tremendous growth and opportunities.
Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms stated that the administration looks forward to working with Porsche to strengthen the local economy through highly skilled and middle-wage opportunities.
The Porsche Service Center South Atlanta will be a 20,815 square foot facility situated on 2.5 acres of land.
The service center will include 13 lift bays to service customer vehicles, a Porsche Driver’s Selection boutique, and a reception area with a waiting lounge for customers.
The Porsche Service Center South Atlanta is one of two standalone service centers for Porsche in the U.S.
The expected completion date for the Porsche Service Center South Atlanta is the third quarter of 2021.
Phase two of the development is expected to begin in 2021 with the construction of a parking structure.

Phase two will also include construction of the new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility and the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta track extension, with an expected completion date of mid-2023.
source_title: Porsche to Build Multimillion-Dollar Project in Atlanta - Savannah CEO

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. is conducting a safety recall for certain 2020-2022 Taycan vehicles under campaign ANA5 / NHTSA ID 22V-278.
The issue involves the driver and passenger seat wire harness potentially becoming damaged during horizontal or longitudinal seat adjustment.
Damaged wiring harnesses may deactivate air bags and seat belt pre-tensioners, causing the red air bag warning light to illuminate.
Approximately 9,570 units (or 9,500 VINs) are affected by this recall.
Dealers will inspect the seat wire harness for damage, add fabric tape to secure the harness, and repair damaged wires as necessary, free of charge.
Owner notification letters were mailed on July 22, 2022.
The remedy was not immediately available at the time of the initial notice; customers were instructed to wait for a follow-up letter once remedy parts were obtained.
Porsche decided to conduct the safety recall as a precautionary measure on April 20, 2022.
Between January 2022 and April 2022, field analysis identified 3 instances outside the United States of wire harness damage resulting in the activation of the red air bag warning lamp.
No incidents or injuries associated with this issue were reported by Porsche as of the recall announcement.
The recall remedy takes up to 1 business day to perform at an authorized dealer.
Vehicles affected by a stop delivery were to be remedied prior to vehicle delivery.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. customer support is located at One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354.

The technical repair procedure involves removing front seats, checking the drive spindle for trapped fibers, and reworking or repairing the wiring harness using textile fabric tape (approx. 40-50 mm width) and commercially available connecting sleeves/repair cables.

Warranty processing labor times vary by scope: Scope 1 (undelivered vehicles) is 110 TU; Scope 2 (delivered vehicles) is 126 TU; Scope 3 (1-9 lines repaired) is 197 TU; Scope 4 (10-18 lines repaired) is 269 TU.
Required parts for warranty include external hexagon round-head bolt M10 x 28 (8 pieces) and textile fabric tape (ANA50000001, costing $1.88).
For Scope 3 and 4 repairs, additional materials include connecting sleeve, repair cable, and heat shrink hose (ANA50000002), costing $1.88 for Scope 3 and $3.75 for Scope 4.
Porsche requires the use of specific tools including a battery charger with at least 90 A current rating, PIWIS Tester 3, and torque wrenches (40-200 Nm).
The recall applies to Taycan models Y1A, Y1B, and Y1C from model years 2020 through 2022.
source_title: ANA5 - Driver And Passenger Seat Wire Harness Damage - 2020-2021 Porsche Taycan

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced third-quarter retail deliveries of 18,403 cars.
Overall sales for the first nine months of the year totaled 57,099 cars, up 5.6 percent year-to-date over 2024.
The 718, Macan, and Panamera posted significant gains year-to-date.
Third-quarter sales of the Taycan and Macan Electric performed well.
Porsche Approved Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) sales in the U.S. for Q3 reached 12,232 units, a new PCNA quarterly record.
Year-to-date CPO sales stand at 35,439 units, representing a 13 percent increase compared to the same point last year.
Timo Resch, President and CEO of PCNA, stated that Porsche remains strong in the United States due to customer support and loyalty during unprecedented change.
Timo Resch noted that choice is vital for customers and highlighted that the new Cayenne Electric will join the Cayenne gasoline and plug-in hybrid models by year's end.
Compared to the same period in 2024, Q3 sales saw a decrease of 4.9 percent, driven in part by restricted supply.
The Macan was the top seller in the third quarter with 6,599 deliveries.
The Cayenne followed as the second top seller with 4,294 deliveries.
The 911 was the third top seller with 3,448 deliveries.

facts: Porsche Cars North America is hiring a Front Desk Admin Assistant at its Atlanta, GA headquarters located at One Porsche Drive.
The position handles all inquiries at the company's front desk and provides primary phone coverage for the main telephone line, excluding the 1-800 call center.
The role acts as a brand ambassador for visitors to the Experience Center and assists with guest service areas.

Responsibilities include answering the main switchboard, transferring calls, documenting customer complaints in the internal C@P tracking system, and coordinating with security for guest access and passes.
The employee will assist the facilities team with common area office needs and support the Experience Center and Customer Relations teams with project work.
Staff will escort guests through the facility and provide occasional facility tours.
Qualifications require a high school diploma or higher, a minimum of two years of experience as a receptionist or related role, and some automotive knowledge is considered a plus.

Candidates must have an outgoing personality, warm demeanor, intermediate knowledge of MS-Windows and MS-Office applications, strong organizational skills, and the ability to occasionally move equipment, boxes, and luggage weighing up to 40 lbs.
The regular shift schedule runs from 9:00 am to 6:00 pm for approximately 95% of the year.
Saturday shifts are required during peak periods of the year, with occasional early evening shifts scheduled for key corporate events.

facts: Ayesha Coker serves as Vice President of Marketing for Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
Coker has led marketing for the sports car maker's largest market since 2022.

Timo Resch, President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America, stated that Coker's work on campaigns for new models such as the Macan and Taycan, along with large-scale events like Rennsport Reunion, has elevated marketing at Porsche.
Coker is a graduate of the University of Maryland Eastern Shore and the Fashion Institute of Technology.
Coker began her career with PCNA in 2010 as an experiential brand event manager responsible for planning, developing, and managing major event marketing programs.
During her tenure at PCNA, she held various marketing roles including manager and senior manager of events and sponsorships, and director of experiential marketing.
Coker joined the Executive Committee at PCNA in January 2022 as Vice-President of Marketing, making her the first African American and first woman of color to join the leadership team.

The 2025 class of Automotive News' 100 Leading Women in the North American Auto Industry was selected through a months-long nomination and judging process that attracted hundreds of entries from the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

Mary Beth Vander Schaaf, Automotive News senior director of editorial operations, noted that the selection committee made many difficult decisions to identify talented executives at the forefront of thousands of successful women in the auto industry.
Automotive News has published the list of the industry's top female executives every five years since 2000.

Porsche Cars North America's Ayesha Coker recognized among 100 leading women in the North American auto industry

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced it will become The Official Car of WeatherTech Raceway Laguna Seca in Monterey, California.
Since 2011, Laguna Seca has been home to the Rennsport Reunion, the world’s largest ticketed gathering of Porsche enthusiasts.
As part of the sponsorship, Porsche will support Friends of Laguna Seca in its commitment to revitalize the Raceway and Recreation Area.
A Porsche Driving Center (PDC) will launch at the raceway in 2026, offering introductory, intermediate, and advanced vehicle control training courses.

Timo Resch, President and CEO of PCNA, stated that many greatest moments in motorsport history have happened at Laguna Seca and expressed excitement about allowing the next generation to drive Porsches.
Friends of Laguna Seca board member Bruce Canepa welcomed Porsche as the official car, noting the partnership brings together two iconic brands.
Porsche has hosted four Rennsport Reunions in the last 15 years at Laguna Seca and earned its 600th and 601st IMSA victories there last year.
In February 2024, the Taycan Turbo GT set a track record of 1:27.87min, earning the title of fastest electric series-production car at Laguna Seca.
Rennsport Reunion 7 in 2023 hosted over 91,000 attendees over four days, marking the biggest attendance for any Rennsport Reunion to date.
The sponsorship launches with Porsche serving as the official safety car for the TireRack.com Monterey Sportscar Championship IMSA race weekend from May 9-11.
The race will be broadcast live on Sunday, May 11, at noon PDT/3 p.m. EDT on NBC.
PCNA is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, at One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354 USA, and has been based there since 1998.
Established in 1984, PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera, and Taycan.
The Atlanta campus houses the first Porsche Experience Center in North America, featuring two module-based 1.6-mile driver development tracks, a business center, and Restaurant 356.
The Atlanta campus also serves as the U.S. headquarters for Porsche Classic.

PCNA operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles with an 8-module, 4.1-mile driver development track, a business center, Restaurant 917, and Porsche Motorsport North America headquarters.
PCNA supports 203 independently owned Porsche Centers in the U.S., four Porsche studios, and five satellite stores, supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.
The Porsche Car Configurator can be used by anyone to customize and pre-order any vehicle in that lineup at any time.

Porsche boasts a racing heritage with over 30,000 motorsport wins to date.
Photos and video footage are available to accredited journalists on the Porsche Press Database and on the Porsche Cars North America Newsroom.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced the addition of the Macan Electric to driving programs at the Porsche Experience Centers in Atlanta and Los Angeles.

facts: Porsche originally planned for 80% of its cars to be EVs by the end of the decade, including an all-electric Macan and replacements for the 718 Boxster and Cayman.
Porsche has backed away from its ambitious electrification plans due to the general EV market not growing as expected and luxury brands like Mercedes and Audi seeing EV sales plummet.
Taycan EV sales slipped in 2024, which Porsche attributed to supply chain issues and customers waiting for the updated Taycan.
Porsche unveiled the new Taycan alongside the new gas-powered 911 Carrera T to meet driver demand for both performance-oriented EVs and gas-powered sports cars.
Porsche North America product specialist Frank Wiesmann stated that a Porsche is meant to be an engaging, practical driver's car and that an electric car can fulfill those roles.
Wiesmann noted Porsche remains passionate about analog cars like the 911 with manual transmissions and gas engines.
The new 911 Carrera T features lightweight glass, less sound-deadening material, standard rear-wheel steering, a lower stance, better brakes, and is manual transmission only.

Porsche reported that 70% of US buyers of the prior Carrera T selected it with a seven-speed manual transmission; the new model uses a six-speed shifter with a sportier short-throw design and new linkages.
Wiesmann stated the US is Porsche's biggest market for manual transmission vehicles, as purist drivers value the engaging element of shifting themselves.
The Taycan GTS features Porsche Active Ride suspension to smooth road bumps, new aero and design elements, and nearly 100 more horsepower, totaling 690 hp.
The Taycan starts at $99,400, with the new GTS trim starting at $149,895.
The Taycan competes against EVs like the Tesla Model S and Lucid Air, as well as gas-powered cars and hybrids.
Porsche has adopted a multi-energy strategy including hybrid Cayenne SUVs and Panamera sedans, with the 911 also available in a hybrid Carrera GTS variant.
Wiesmann explained Porsche's flexible powertrain strategy allows customers to choose EVs, hybrids, or gas engines based on their home charging capabilities and preferences.
The 2025 911 Carrera T and Taycan GTS will arrive in dealerships next year (2025), with the Carrera T starting at $135,995.
source_title: Mixing it up: Porsche unveils a new gas-powered 911 Carrera T and electric Taycan GTS - AOL

facts: Rivian Automotive has already begun public deliveries of its lower-priced R2 mid-size SUV from its Normal, Illinois plant.
Rivian is extending its AT&T partnership to add built-in 5G connectivity.
Rivian is planning additional capacity at a future Georgia facility.

Management is targeting gross margin positive R2 units.
Rivian is leveraging software-enabled features to support longer-term financial resilience.
Rivian plans to add up to 300,000 units of annual capacity at its future Georgia plant starting in 2028.
The Georgia expansion could reinforce the R2 platform as the center of Rivian's growth story.
The expansion amplifies execution and capital intensity risks if demand, pricing, or regulatory support fall short of expectations.
The biggest risk remains high cash use and the possibility of further capital raises.
Rivian Automotive's narrative projects $19.1 billion revenue and $478.9 million earnings by 2029.
This projection requires 51.3% yearly revenue growth and an earnings increase of about $4.0 billion from -$3.5 billion today.
Some optimistic analysts are modeling revenue to reach about US$37.2 billion by 2029, assuming the R2 platform plus autonomy deliver far stronger growth than consensus.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Sewon America will invest over $300 million in a new manufacturing facility creating 740 new jobs in Georgia.

Most of the Company's capital investment was devoted to the automotive component business.
The Company improved facilities in order to improve productivity and reduce cost and prepared the facilities to produce products for new vehicle models.
The Company has completed consolidation of its production operations in Aichi Prefecture, Japan.
Manufacturing operations in Aichi Prefecture have been integrated into two plants of Toyokawa Plant, Toyokawa City, and Nagoya Plant, Toyota City.
By shutting down the former Toyota Plant, the number of Shiroki's production bases in Japan has been reduced from six to five.
Sales declined 9.3% year-on-year to 111,924 million JPY in FY2009 because the Company's major customers (automakers) reduced their production volumes.

Operating income improved to 2,680 million JPY as a result of consolidating production sites in Japan, improving manufacturing processes by implementing a one-shift, fixed-working-hour system, and engaging in cost reduction programs.
R&D expenditure for the automotive components business was 2,154 million JPY in FY2009.
Recent R&D achievement for the automotive components business includes next-generation power seats (next generation manual seats).
Recent R&D achievement for the automotive components business includes new wire-type regulators.
Recent R&D achievement for the automotive components business includes new arm-type regulators.
Recent R&D achievement for the automotive components business includes new door frames.
The Company holds a technical partnership (licensing-in) with Keiper GmbH & Co. in Germany for seat recliners from April 1, 1988 to March 31, 2011.
The Company has a licensing-out partnership with NHK Spring (Thailand) Co., Ltd. in Thailand for seat recliners until December 9, 2010.
The Company has a licensing-out partnership with Summit Laemchabang Auto Body Co. in Thailand for door frames until December 31, 2010.
The Company has a licensing-out partnership with Asan Co., Ltd. in Korea for automotive door frames until October 31, 2010.
The Company plans to increase supplies to Japanese automakers in India through Technico Industries, Ltd., a parts supplier in Gurgaon, Haryana, India, in which it holds equity.
Shiroki will provide Technico with facilities for the three lines of stamping, sub-assembly and assembly from Japan to start production in 2010.
Orders were received from Maruti Suzuki India Ltd., New Delhi, for seat recliners and seat adjusters, which will be Technico's first seat parts.
Shiroki's major client Toyota Motor Corp. has placed parts orders for its newly developed small car, the EFC (Entry Family Car), targeted at emerging countries.
source_title: Shiroki Corporation Business Report FY2009 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: The SK Battery America plant in Commerce, Ga. (Jackson County) already employs 250 people.
The company plans to eventually hire 10,000 workers to provide batteries for American-built vehicles like the electric Ford F-150 pickup and a new Volkswagen electric SUV.
On Feb. 10, the US International Trade Commission issued a ban on much of the technology SK plans to use at the Commerce plant.
The ITC agreed that SK intentionally destroyed evidence after rival company LG Energy Solution complained SK stole its trade secrets.
The ruling threatens the future of the plant and all associated green energy jobs.
Deputy Secretary of Transportation nominee Polly Trottenberg promised to speak to President Joe Biden about the importance of the SK Battery plant.
Georgia Sen. Raphael Warnock won a pledge from a Transportation Department nominee to explain to Biden what is at stake regarding the plant.
The president has 60 days to either amend the ban or let it move forward.

During a confirmation hearing for Deputy Secretary of Transportation Polly Trottenberg, Warnock stated that losing 2,600 clean energy jobs in the short term and 10,000 in the long term would be absolutely critical for the people of Georgia.
Warnock pointed out that Biden had pledged to create 10 million clean energy jobs.
Trottenberg agreed the issue was important and committed to explaining it to the senator, noting the department is already aware of the issue with the plant and starting to look into it.
The ITC released public findings stating SK’s destruction of evidence in the case was extraordinary, ordered at a high level, and carried out by department heads throughout SK.
The ITC found that even after receipt of the complaint, SK scrambled to destroy more records.
The ITC noted it was not enough for SK to destroy or hide records once, as SK then collected more LG proprietary information only to destroy or hide those records as well.

LG Energy Solution, a rival Korean battery manufacturer, accused SK of stealing their battery secrets and publicly urged SK to negotiate a financial settlement that would guarantee the Jackson County plant opens on schedule.
In a statement, SK argued that no judicial process or legal authority has ever found that SK stole any technology, and urged the president to undo the damage from the ITC decision.
Neither company confirmed whether settlement negotiations have begun.
LG’s intellectual property attorney Song Jung stated they must first address the issue of stolen technology to compensate LG before SK can maintain the plant in Georgia.
The ITC decision allows SK to produce Ford electric F-150 batteries for four years and VW batteries for two years as a temporary reprieve.
source_title: Georgia Battery plant's destruction of evidence 'extraordinary,' still pinning hopes on Biden | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: The construction site of the Hyundai–LG battery plant (part of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America) in Ellabell, Bryan County, Georgia was the location of a massive immigration raid dubbed "Operation Low Voltage."
On September 4, 2025, nearly 500 federal, state, and local law enforcement officers stormed the site and arrested 475 construction workers.
The operation marked the largest raid at a single location in Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) history.

The raid was led by ICE and HSI, supported by the FBI, DEA, ATF, and Georgia State Patrol, as part of a months-long criminal investigation into alleged illegal hiring practices and other serious federal violations.
Roughly 300 or more of the detained individuals were South Korean citizens.
Detainees also included individuals who entered the U.S. illegally, overstayed their visas, held non-work visas (e.g., tourist visas, visa waiver entries), or otherwise violated visa terms.
Agents lined up workers to inspect documents, including dates of birth and Social Security numbers, to determine detention status.
Detainees were transported by bus to the ICE processing center in Folkston, Georgia, over 160 km away from the plant site.
Some workers attempted to flee by hiding in ventilation ducts or jumping into a sewage pond on-site, with one attempting to flip an agency boat before being arrested.

The South Korean government voiced "concern and regret," reiterating that the rights and business interests of its citizens and companies should not be unjustly harmed during U.S. law enforcement actions.
South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Hyun traveled to Washington for urgent negotiations regarding the detainees.
A charter flight was arranged to fly more than 300 detained Korean workers home once administrative procedures were complete.
Hyundai stated that none of those arrested were direct employees but rather hired through subcontractors.
Hyundai pledged full cooperation and announced an internal probe into compliance across its entire supply chain.
LG Energy Solution affirmed its cooperation with authorities and stated it was gathering relevant information about the incident.
The Hyundai–LG joint venture represents a $7.6 billion investment including a battery facility modeled to serve 300,000 EVs annually.
The project was previously hailed as Georgia’s largest economic development project.
The raid halted construction at the site and introduced delays and cost concerns for the facility.
The incident shook investor confidence and strained diplomatic relations between the U.S. and South Korea.
The raid occurred shortly after South Korea agreed to invest $350 billion in U.S. energy and manufacturing projects.
President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social urging companies to respect U.S. immigration laws and emphasizing the importance of hiring and training American workers.

The operation highlighted an uncompromising U.S. immigration enforcement stance toward allies, raising questions about America's credibility as a stable investment destination under "America First" policies.
source_title: Massive Immigration Raid at Hyundai-LG Plant Sparks Diplomatic Fallout

facts: SKC operates a large capacity of film plant in Covington, Georgia, United States.
In February 2017, SK acquired the polyethylene acrylic acid business of Dow Chemical Company for $370 million.
SK planned to increase battery production capacity from 1.9 to 3.9 GWh per year at the end of 2018, supplying Kia and Mercedes.
In July 2022, SK Group announced a $22 billion investment in the United States semiconductor, green energy bioscience and other technology industries.
SK Group will invest over $52 billion in the United States by 2025.
SK Innovation is doubling its battery production for electric vehicles to 4GWh/year to support demand from Mercedes and others.
SK Siltron is expanding its business portfolio to include emerging areas such as SiC wafers.
A SKC-led consortium bet $80 million for next-generation EV battery materials.
source_title: SK Group - Wikipedia

facts: Ford and SK On have broken ground at BlueOval SK Battery Park.
Their joint venture – BlueOval SK – has invested $5.8 billion to produce advanced batteries for future Ford and Lincoln electric vehicles.
The factory is slated to start production in 2025.
The project will create 5,000 new jobs in Kentucky.
Construction is on schedule at the two massive battery manufacturing facilities.
The facilities are capable of collectively producing more than 80-gigawatt hours annually.
Jee Dong-seob, SK On president and CEO, stated: “BlueOval SK Battery Park will be at the core of the electrification of the North American auto market.”
Jee Dong-seob added: “We expect SK On and Ford’s leadership in the global electric vehicle market to be solidified through BlueOval SK.”
Significant construction progress brings Ford closer to its target of producing an annual run rate of 2 million electric vehicles globally by the end of 2026.

Lisa Drake, vice president of Ford EV Industrialization, stated: “Ford’s roots run deep in Kentucky, and BlueOval SK is going to help Ford to lead the EV revolution, bringing thousands of new, high-tech jobs to the Bluegrass State.”
Lisa Drake added: “Ford is building on more than a century of investment in Kentucky and its incredible workforce.”
BlueOval SK Battery Park will train 5,000 new workers at the new Elizabethtown Community and Technical College (ECTC) BlueOval SK Training Center.
The training center is located on the 1,500-acre BlueOval SK Battery Park site.

David Hahm, BlueOval SK CEO, stated: “At BlueOval SK Battery Park, we’re building the future – state-of-the-art batteries for future Ford and Lincoln electric vehicles and a bright future for the workforce in Kentucky.”
David Hahm added: “In order to produce these batteries, we need a premier workforce that is trained in the latest battery and advanced manufacturing technology.”
The ECTC BlueOval SK Training Center is the only co-branded learning facility within the Kentucky Community College System.
The training center represents a $25 million investment by the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear stated: “The partnership between Kentucky and Ford dates back more than a century, to the very dawn of the automobile industry.”
Gov. Beshear added: “That bond grows even stronger today as we break ground on Ford and SK On’s BlueOval SK Battery Park in Hardin County.”

Gov. Beshear noted: “As both the largest economic development project in our state’s history and part of the biggest investment ever by Ford, this project cements Kentucky’s status as the electric vehicle battery production capital of the United States.”
The curriculum within the 42,000-square-foot training facility will support battery knowledge, roles, and skills.

BlueOval SK will train employees in SK On’s proprietary technical, quality and manufacturing processes in the ECTC BlueOval SK Training Center’s virtual reality labs, industrial maintenance lab, work simulation lab, and ergonomics techniques classrooms.
The Kentucky Community and Technical College System (KCTCS) will begin construction of the ECTC BlueOval SK Training Center in 2023.
The training center is scheduled to be completed in 2024.
Significant progress has been made at the 2.3-square-mile battery manufacturing campus.
Crews have moved 4.3 million cubic yards of soil, enough to fill 200 American football stadiums.
Crews have laid 283,000 tons of stone, equivalent to the weight of nearly 1,350 locomotives.
Crews have poured 66,000 cubic yards of concrete – enough to fill 356 backyard swimming pools.
Crews have installed 3,300 tons of rebar ties to reinforce the concrete, equivalent to the weight of more than 470 elephants.
Crews have installed 1,300 deep foundations, equal to the height of nearly 60 Empire State Buildings, stacked end to end.
Crews have erected 7,900 tons of structural steel, equivalent to the weight of nearly 400 fire trucks.
Through the campus construction process, Ford and its construction partners continue to engage with communities in Kentucky through donations, sponsorships, and volunteer efforts.
Ford Motor Company provided nearly $40,000 and manpower from its construction partners, Barton Malow Company and Gray Construction, to the city of Glendale, home to BlueOval SK Battery Park.
The team refreshed many of the city’s parks and sidewalks, some of which had not been replaced in more than 100 years.

Ford’s construction partners installed handicap-accessible ramps throughout Glendale and supported various projects locally with United Way of Central Kentucky, Feeding America and Habitat for Humanity throughout Central Kentucky.
Ford donated $42,000 to the Glendale Volunteer Fire Department, increasing the department’s budget by more than 50 percent.
Ford sponsored this year’s Glendale Crossing Festival.
Lisa Slaven, Hardin County Schools Transition Readiness Director, stated: “Ford has been a pillar of the community in Kentucky for more than a century, and now Glendale will write the next chapter.”

Lisa Slaven added: “As an educator and member of this community, I view the building of BlueOval SK Battery Park as synonymous with creating new learning opportunities and skill sets for the careers of tomorrow for our students, our community, and Kentucky.”
source_title: Ford, Partner Make Significant Progress At BlueOval SK Battery Park

facts: SK Battery America, Inc. announced March 6 it would lay off 958 workers from its electric vehicle battery plant in Commerce, Georgia.
The Associated Press reports that the laid-off workers make up roughly 37% of the company’s entire workforce.
The workers finished their last day of work on March 6 but will receive pay through May 6.
The layoffs were disclosed via a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification notice filed with the state.
The Georgia plant cost $2.6 billion to build and opened in January 2022.
The plant initially supplied batteries for the Ford F-150 Lightning electric pickup truck.
SK Battery America, Inc. was formed as a joint venture with Ford.
Ford discontinued its partnership with SK On.
Ford announced it would cease production of the F-150 Lightning in December 2025.
Pratt & Whitney is spending $200 million to expand its Georgia aircraft engine factory.
The Pratt & Whitney expansion is expected to initially create 15 new jobs.
Preciball USA announced a new $17 million factory in Georgia.
The Preciball USA factory will produce industrial balls for bearings and valves and create 65 new jobs.
Dongwon Autopart Technology is building a new $30 million factory in Georgia that will create 200 new jobs.
Dongwon Autopart Technology, a Korean auto parts manufacturer, already has about 500 employees in the region.
source_title: SK On cuts 37% of its Georgia workforce | Plant Services

facts: The Biden administration avoided one of the more challenging early tests of its climate agenda Saturday when two South Korean battery giants feuding over trade secrets reached a settlement to keep a massive Georgia factory complex open.

The White House faced a Sunday deadline to decide whether to veto a U.S. International Trade Commission decision from February that barred SK Innovation from importing materials needed to build electric vehicle batteries at a $2.6 billion pair of plants in Commerce, Georgia.

The case illustrates the new administration’s dueling priorities as it seeks to jumpstart efforts to eliminate the U.S. economy’s planet-heating pollution while going head-to-head with China over intellectual property protections.

As late as Friday night, SK Innovation was far from a deal with Seoul-based rival LG Energy Solutions, which convinced the federal trade court that SK Innovation destroyed evidence of stolen trade secrets.
In February, the ITC imposed a 10-year import ban on SK Innovation.
The import ban jeopardized the supply of batteries for Ford’s electric F-150 pickup and Volkswagen’s Crossover series.
The ruling threatened 2,600 jobs the company planned to fill in Georgia over the next few years.
The ruling also risked delaying President Joe Biden’s plans to electrify the nation’s 276 million some-odd automobiles, which are the top U.S. source of climate pollution.

The $1.8 billion cash deal will end other ongoing lawsuits between SK Innovation and LG Energy Solutions in the U.S. and South Korea, including one in a federal court in Delaware that had been on pause until the ITC saga reached its completion.

SK Innovation CEO Jun Kim and LG Energy Solution CEO Jong Hyun Kim issued a joint statement Sunday morning saying: “We have decided to settle and to compete in an amicable way, all for the future of the U.S. and South Korean electric vehicle battery industries.”

The CEOs also stated: “In particular, we will work together to strengthen an EV battery domestic supply chain and support the U.S. Biden Administration’s effort to advance green energy policies and environmental responsibility.”
SK Innovation’s plant will comprise upward of 35% of the U.S. production capacity of electric vehicle batteries by the end of Biden’s first term.
By the end of Biden’s first term, the country is expected to have roughly 11 big powerpack-making factories online.
The settlement details were starting to be disclosed Sunday morning and will likely help reverse the ruling and allow SK Innovation to keep its plant open.
The agreement is a political win, with leaders across the partisan spectrum pressing for an outcome that would allow the plant to remain open.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp (R) pleaded with Biden to veto the decision if a settlement wasn’t reached.
In recent weeks, Sen. Jon Ossoff (D-Ga.) held at least one meeting seeking to broker a deal.
The looming deadline and high stakes of a decision that should define the future of a U.S. set for near exponential growth in the next decade led to an eleventh-hour agreement.

Losing the factory without a deal or veto would have sent a chill through the U.S. battery market as it looks to start attracting more players, according to Caspar Rawles, an analyst at Benchmark Mineral Intelligence, a London-based research firm that specializes in lithium-ion batteries and electric vehicle supply chains.

Caspar Rawles stated before the deal was announced: “The message that you’re sending to those companies by essentially shutting down a plant in the U.S. due to legal reasons does not send a good message.”

Rawles added: “It’s not the most inviting investment environment for someone looking to spend several billion dollars, and then there’s a risk something happens and there’s a legal intervention and they lose it all.”

A presidential veto would have been a rare move, with the last time the power was used occurring in 2013 when President Barack Obama blocked an ITC ruling that would have prevented Apple from importing some iPads.
Ronald Reagan set the record for vetoing four ITC decisions, including one that coincidentally dealt with batteries.
source_title: Biden Avoids Big Test As Battery Giants Reach Deal To Save Georgia Factories | HuffPost Latest News

facts: Construction workers at the SK Battery America plant in Commerce, Ga. (Jackson County) report that government contractors are ignoring safety concerns to finish the job on schedule.
Kimel Brantley worked as a safety officer for Eastern Corporation, one of the major contractors at the electric car battery plant.
Brantley stated: “They’re sacrificing people’s safety for production.”
Brantley previously held the same safety officer position at MiDong, a subcontractor at the plant, before quitting earlier in the month.
Brantley filed a complaint with the Department of Labor Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA).
Randy Gregory works as an elevator installer on the multi-billion dollar facility.
Gregory remarked: “It’s a wonder someone hasn’t been killed already.”
SK Battery, a Korean-owned company, received $300 million in tax breaks and free land to establish the electric car battery plant in Jackson County.
SK Battery promised more than 2,000 good jobs when the facility opens in 2022.
Most workers at the construction site were from overseas performing tasks that union leaders stated unemployed American pipefitters or electricians could easily handle.
David Cagle of Local Union 72 complained: “All I hear is 2,000 jobs... Does the construction industry not matter? The average common man that works with his hands?”
U.S. Customs caught 33 Koreans attempting to enter the US with fake papers to work at the battery plant.
American workers have complained that Korean workers do not understand American safety standards on a construction site.
In May, Customs reported catching 33 Koreans trying to enter the country with fake papers for the battery plant project.
SK Battery stated it has hired 1,000 Americans to build the plant so far and instructed contractors from the start to prioritize hiring local American workers.
Brantley alleged he repeatedly saw unqualified Korean workers hired instead of qualified American workers.
Brantley noted that Korean workers were willing to work for $2 a day compared to his rate of $6,000 a month.
Brantley, who is half Korean, attempted to enforce safety on site but could not communicate with Korean workers due to a language barrier and received no support from bosses at Eastern or MiDong.

In his OSHA complaint filed this month, Brantley wrote he witnessed many workers exposed to fall hazards and general safety hazards, but companies prioritized staying on track for the 2022 completion deadline.
Brantley noted in his complaint that the majority of employees are Korean Nationals and Latino workers who showed a lack of understanding of American safety standards.

An Eastern Corporation spokesman stated they cannot imagine Brantley’s comments being true, citing Eastern’s strict safety policy regardless of project schedule, and emphasized that safety is their first priority.
FOX 5 Atlanta contacted MiDong for comment but received no response.
Jackson County EMS responded to four emergency calls at the site since April, transporting two workers with no life-threatening injuries.
Workers were observed using a forklift to remove equipment from a flatbed truck, which safety experts warn against because loads can shift or fall when used as a crane.
Gregory provided photos showing a worker driving a forklift onto a platform suspended 30 feet in the air by a crane to lift pallets of equipment.
Gregory explained the danger of this practice: “The slings break and that platform comes down with that guy in the forklift on it.”
The photos are evidence in a separate OSHA complaint filed by the union on behalf of Gregory and other workers, which does not name a specific contractor.
Gregory stated he took the pictures to leave proof for his wife in case he was killed on the job.

The union’s OSHA complaint states: “The consensus of the workers that care about leaving the job at the end of the day in the same condition as they were when they walked in that morning is that there is little regard for proper safety procedures and hazard prevention on the job.”
Gregory, a 26-year veteran of the construction industry, described the SK Battery job site as one of the worst he has ever seen regarding safety.

In a written statement, SK Battery said although on-site safety management is under general contractors' responsibility, SK Battery America prioritizes construction personnel safety and is working with contractors to ensure all safety standards and procedures are followed.
Eastern Corporation insisted it regularly trains employees and subcontractors to provide the safest possible work environment for all workers on site.

Download the FOX 5 Atlanta app for breaking news and weather alerts.

source_title: Construction workers tell feds they don't feel safe at SK Battery site | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: Commerce, Georgia-based SK Battery America, a subsidiary of SK On that produces high-energy-density lithium-ion battery cells for major automotive partners, has announced a major reduction in force at its Commerce plant.
The workforce reduction impacted 958 employees.
A company statement cited the need "to align operations to market conditions" as the reason for the reduction.
SK Battery America stated it appreciates employees' contributions and will work with those affected during the transition.
The company remains committed to Georgia and to building a robust U.S. supply chain for advanced battery manufacturing.
SK Battery America is pursuing a range of future customers, including in the Battery Energy Storage System arena.
BlueOval SK Battery Park is a joint venture between Ford and SK On located in Glendale, Kentucky.
The joint venture has invested $5.8 billion to produce advanced batteries for future Ford and Lincoln electric vehicles.
Production at the site is slated to start in 2025.
The project will create 5,000 new jobs in Kentucky.
BlueOval SK Battery Park consists of two massive battery manufacturing facilities capable of collectively producing more than 80 gigawatt hours annually.
The site covers 1,500 acres and spans a 2.3-square-mile campus.
Ford aims to reach an annual run rate of 2 million electric vehicles globally by the end of 2026, with this project bringing it closer to that target.
SK On president and CEO Jee Dong-seob stated that BlueOval SK Battery Park will be at the core of the electrification of the North American auto market.
Jee Dong-seob added that they expect SK On and Ford’s leadership in the global electric vehicle market to be solidified through BlueOval SK.
Ford EV Industrialization vice president Lisa Drake said BlueOval SK will help Ford lead the EV revolution by bringing thousands of new, high-tech jobs to Kentucky.
Lisa Drake noted that Ford is building on more than a century of investment in Kentucky and its workforce.
BlueOval SK CEO David Hahm stated that the park is building state-of-the-art batteries for future Ford and Lincoln electric vehicles and a bright future for the Kentucky workforce.
David Hahm emphasized the need for a premier workforce trained in the latest battery and advanced manufacturing technology to produce these batteries.
The ECTC BlueOval SK Training Center will be located on the 1,500-acre site to train 5,000 new workers.
Kentucky Commonwealth invested $25 million into the training center.
The ECTC BlueOval SK Training Center is the only co-branded learning facility within the Kentucky Community College System.
The training facility spans 42,000 square feet and will support battery knowledge, roles, and skills.
BlueOval SK will train employees in SK On’s proprietary technical, quality, and manufacturing processes at the center.
Training facilities include virtual reality labs, an industrial maintenance lab, a work simulation lab, and ergonomics techniques classrooms.
The Kentucky Community and Technical College System (KCTCS) began construction of the training center in 2023, with completion scheduled for 2024.
Kentucky Governor Andy Beshear stated that the partnership between Kentucky and Ford dates back more than a century to the dawn of the automobile industry.
Gov. Beshear noted that breaking ground on BlueOval SK Battery Park in Hardin County cements Kentucky’s status as the electric vehicle battery production capital of the United States.
Gov. Beshear described the project as both the largest economic development project in Kentucky’s history and part of the biggest investment ever by Ford.
Construction crews have moved 4.3 million cubic yards of soil at the site, enough to fill 200 American football stadiums.
Crews have laid 283,000 tons of stone, equivalent to the weight of nearly 1,350 locomotives.
Crews have poured 66,000 cubic yards of concrete, enough to fill 356 backyard swimming pools.
Crews have installed 3,300 tons of rebar ties to reinforce concrete, equivalent to the weight of more than 470 elephants.
Crews have installed 1,300 deep foundations, equal to the height of nearly 60 Empire State Buildings stacked end to end.
Crews have erected 7,900 tons of structural steel, equivalent to the weight of nearly 400 fire trucks.
Ford and its construction partners provided nearly $40,000 and manpower to the city of Glendale through donations, sponsorships, and volunteer efforts.
Construction partners include Barton Malow Company and Gray Construction.
The team refreshed many of Glendale’s parks and sidewalks, some not replaced in over 100 years.
Partners installed handicap-accessible ramps throughout Glendale.
Ford supported local projects with the United Way of Central Kentucky, Feeding America, and Habitat for Humanity.
Ford donated $42,000 to the Glendale Volunteer Fire Department, increasing its budget by more than 50 percent.
Ford sponsored this year’s Glendale Crossing Festival.
Hardin County Schools Transition Readiness Director Lisa Slaven stated that Ford has been a pillar of the Kentucky community for over a century.
Lisa Slaven viewed the building of BlueOval SK Battery Park as synonymous with creating new learning opportunities and skill sets for students, the community, and Kentucky.
