facts: SK On's North American battery shipments in the second quarter are expected to more than double compared to the same period last year, significantly narrowing its operating loss.
The amount of Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (AMPC) received under the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) is projected to reach a quarterly record high.
SK Battery America (SKBA) in Georgia, USA, shipped approximately 4.3 gigawatt-hours (GWh) in the second quarter of this year.
The second quarter shipment volume represents an increase of more than 115% compared to the estimated 2.0GWh in the same period last year.
Under the IRA, batteries produced and supplied within the United States are eligible for a tax credit of $35 (about 47,526 KRW) per kWh for cells and $45 per kWh for modules.
SKBA's AMPC received in the second quarter is estimated at approximately 241.3 billion KRW, marking the largest quarterly amount since the program was implemented.

KB Securities forecast that SK On’s operating loss in the second quarter, including AMPC, would be around 35.6 billion KRW, representing an 88.1% improvement from the previous quarter’s 299.3 billion KRW.
Hana Securities projected the second quarter operating loss at 117.8 billion KRW, expecting a significant reduction compared to the previous quarter.
SKBA has a total production capacity of 22GWh and serves as the exclusive battery supplier for Hyundai Motor’s electric vehicles produced in the United States.
Analysts attribute the increase in battery cell production to the start of full-scale electric vehicle production and wholesale sales at Hyundai’s U.S. plant.
KB Securities researcher Jeon Wooje estimated that SK On’s U.S. plant reached 100% full operation between March and April.
From May, inventory buildup for high-performance electric vehicles such as the Ioniq 9 and EV9 began, with battery demand exceeding 1.5GWh based solely on Hyundai’s April production.
SKBA’s supply to Hyundai currently accounts for as much as 75% of its total output.
SK Innovation recently withdrew the planned IPO of its lubricant subsidiary SK Enmove, citing market conditions and corporate value enhancement.
Industry insiders view SK Innovation's withdrawal of the SK Enmove IPO as part of a rebalancing strategy to focus capital and resources on SK On.
SK Innovation CEO Jang Yongho declared since the beginning of this year that portfolio rebalancing is an essential task for survival.
Jang Yongho added that in the short term, the company will concentrate on areas and markets where it can secure competitiveness to grow into a global top-tier battery company.

An industry official stated that demand for SK On’s U.S. plant is expected to continue growing due to expanded U.S. electric vehicle production, IRA tax credit effects, and automakers’ local battery procurement strategies.
The same industry official explained that SKBA’s utilization rate is expected to remain high under these conditions.
source_title: SK On Signals Q2 Turnaround... North American Battery Shipments Double - The Asia Business Daily

facts: Fulton County, Georgia, moved from #11 last year to #4 in the national Top 20 Best Counties in America rankings.
Fulton County achieved the #1 position in the I-85 Corridor Report featured in Site Selection Magazine's July issue.
Site Selection Magazine, published by Conway Data, Inc., evaluated all 3,142 U.S. counties using its proprietary Conway Data Project Database based on new investments and job creation.
Robb Pitts, Chairman of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners, stated that the recognition is a testament to collaborative work with partners at the State of Georgia, the region, and 15 cities.

Kristi Brigman, Deputy Commissioner of Global Commerce for the Georgia Department of Economic Development, noted that diverse, well-educated talent and a collaborative ecosystem are fostering growth in tech and life sciences.
Kristi Brigman highlighted that Georgia’s global gateways, such as Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, facilitate access to U.S. and global markets.

Recent major corporate wins for Fulton County include Adobe, SK Battery America, Amazon Music, Cummins, Resia Manufacturing, Microsoft, GCI Restaurant Group, Mastercard, Newegg Commerce, Nike, PrizePicks, and Cargill.
These recent projects span technology, manufacturing, and finance sectors, strengthening the regional economy and creating job opportunities for Georgians.
Fulton County is the most populous county in Georgia and is home to 15 distinct cities, including Atlanta.

Samir Abdullahi, Director of Economic Development and Select Fulton, stated that moving up from #11 to #4 is a notable accomplishment and emphasized Select Fulton's mission to cultivate new job prospects and investment opportunities across all communities.
SK Battery America is listed among the high-profile companies securing recent major wins in Fulton County.

links.context: Reference to Site Selection Magazine July issue rankings and I-85 Corridor Report

source_title: Fulton County Soars to #4 Nationally for Economic Development Projects - Metro Atlanta CEO

facts: Tremendous growth in electric vehicle and sustainability ecosystems in Georgia is a key driver behind the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s announcement of 35,400 new jobs and $12.9 billion in investment for the state so far this fiscal year.

Through the third quarter of Fiscal Year 2022 (July 1, 2021-March 31), job creation and investments resulting from economic development projects have already surpassed Fiscal Year 2021’s year-end totals.
GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 251 project locations that will result in the creation of more than 35,400 new jobs and $12.9 billion in investment for the state.
Key industry sectors include automotive, advanced manufacturing, logistics and distribution, software and technology, and food processing.
Georgia is continuing to recruit the entire EV supply chain to ensure an effective, efficient and affordable environment for EV production.
Rivian has ongoing plans to develop a $5 billion campus at Stanton Springs North.
Rivian also plans an electric vehicle service center in Atlanta that will provide training on EVs.

SK Battery America partnered with American lithium-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup Ascend Elements to recycle both cell and module lithium-ion battery manufacturing scrap from SK’s Jackson County facility.
Ascend Elements announced its first commercial-scale battery recycling plant in Covington in January.
Aurubis will invest $340 million in a “first-of-its-kind in the U.S.” state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multimetal recycling in Augusta.
Aspen Aerogels announced plans to locate its new 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Statesboro.
Aspen Aerogels will produce PyroThin materials at its new Georgia facility, providing thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that targeting new strategic industries for growth and supporting existing businesses’ expansions is central to the Department’s mission to create hope and opportunities for Georgia communities.
Gov. Brian Kemp emphasized Georgia's strong partnership and pro-business approach, expressing gratitude to job creators choosing to locate or expand operations in the state.
source_title: Growth In Electric Vehicle And Sustainability Ecosystems In Georgia Are Key Drivers Of Jobs – Livable Buckhead

facts: Former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms is running for Governor of Georgia and laid out her platform at a Democratic gubernatorial forum in Savannah on January 8, 2026.
Bottoms proposed creating the Center for Workforce Innovation at Atlanta Area Tech to train workers for corporate roles.
The proposed workforce training program would train individuals as coders for Truist, plumbers for Home Depot, mechanics for Delta Airlines, and linesmen for Georgia Power.
Bottoms stated that the Center for Workforce Innovation program should be scaled statewide across Georgia.
She proposed finding a pathway to free technical and community college in Georgia to support workforce development.
Bottoms emphasized scaling workforce training programs throughout Coastal Georgia and the state as a significant game changer for career advancement.
On January 8, 2026, Bottoms toured the Port of Savannah alongside ILA 1414 to discuss economic development and logistics infrastructure.
During the port tour, Bottoms observed approximately 14,000 trucks that go in and out of the Port of Savannah, many owned by small businesses.
Bottoms stated that economic development and workforce development must go hand in hand to ensure people are trained for incoming jobs in Georgia.
She emphasized holding corporate partners accountable when they receive state incentives to ensure they give back to local communities.
A study by Georgia Tech identified a shortage of 41,000 homes in Coastal Georgia, which Bottoms cited as an immediate infrastructure challenge affecting economic development and affordability.
Bottoms proposed investing in Coastal Georgia infrastructure to make it easier for residents to build homes and access grants.
As mayor of Atlanta, Bottoms created an affordable housing trust fund to help homeowners avoid rising property taxes.
source_title: Bottoms at Savannah Democratic Gubernatorial Forum: ‘This Election is About the Future’

facts: Stryten Energy maintains operational locations in Alpharetta, Georgia, and Cumming, Georgia.

Stryten Energy’s Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) provides power resiliency to the Atlanta community through a lead BESS installation at the VICARS Community Center, which serves the Southwest Atlanta community as part of Community Church Atlanta.

Stryten Energy introduced new E-Series AGM Multi-Terminal Batteries designed for harsh environments, offering superior performance and reliability in long-duration discharge applications for telecommunications and utility sectors.
Stryten Energy offers a Mobile Microgrid solution engineered to deliver remote power and disaster relief by restoring electricity to communities after storms disrupt grid access.
At CES 2026, Stryten Energy convened industry experts to discuss how batteries support data center infrastructure as AI adoption accelerates energy demand across hyperscale facilities.
Stryten Energy emphasizes domestically sourced and manufactured battery energy storage solutions to address increasing U.S. power demand and enhance energy security.

Stryten Energy showcased lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow battery technologies at CES 2026, designed for grid resilience, renewable energy integration, data centers, manufacturing, and EV charging applications.
Stryten Energy partnered with Georgia Tech to install a lead battery energy storage system that serves as a critical component in the institute’s carbon-neutral initiatives.

Stryten Energy delivered advanced energy storage solutions to BCI Flow Batteries North America to ensure uninterrupted power for commercial, industrial, and utility-scale applications amid grid disruptions and peak demand.

The Inflation Reduction Act provides incentives for domestic clean energy manufacturing, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (AMPC), which encourages companies to invest in U.S.-based production.

Stryten Energy formed a joint venture with Storion Energy and Largo to establish a domestic supply chain for vanadium flow battery components and deliver price-competitive vanadium electrolyte via a leasing model to accelerate commercialization.

Stryten Energy promoted a technology-agnostic, battery-first approach to U.S. energy resilience at NAATBatt 2025 in Orlando, Florida, highlighting the need for innovative storage solutions as power demand reaches record highs.
U.S. power demand is projected to reach 4,185 billion kWh in 2025, surpassing the previous record of 4,067 billion kWh set in 2022.
Scott Childers serves as Vice President of Essential Power at Stryten Energy.
Dr. Vick Singh holds the position of Chief Operating Officer at Dragonfly Energy.
Melissa Floyd is the VP of Communications and Digital Marketing at Dragonfly Energy.

Lithium batteries, originally developed in the 1970s and commercialized in the 1990s, offer higher energy density, higher energy efficiency, longer cycle life, and longer calendar life compared to earlier battery technologies.

canonical_name: Georgia Institute of Technology / Stryten Energy LLC
facts: Georgia Institute of Technology partnered with Stryten Energy LLC, a U.S.-based leader in energy storage solutions, to install a Lead Battery Energy Storage System (BESS).
The BESS was installed at Georgia Tech’s Carbon Neutral Energy Solutions Laboratory (CNES).
CNES is located in the North Avenue Research Area of Georgia Tech’s campus.

The facility houses Georgia Tech’s Strategic Energy Institute (SEI), an interdisciplinary research hub focused on accelerating discoveries in energy systems, sustainable practices, and resilient infrastructure.
Faculty, postdocs, and students working on renewable energy systems, microgrids, storage technologies, and decarbonization strategies conduct research at CNES.
The installed system will support a wide array of research initiatives related to Distributed Energy Resources (DERs), energy reliability, peak load management, and microgrid design.
The system simultaneously provides functional value by enhancing the building’s own energy infrastructure.
Stryten’s Lead BESS offers Georgia Tech researchers the ability to simulate, model, and monitor real-time energy storage and discharge behaviors in a campus-integrated environment.
The system was selected for its well-documented advantages in cost-efficiency, safety, recyclability, and operational performance.
Lead batteries are described as a mature, proven, and circular solution specifically suited for medium-duration energy storage.
The installed BESS features bi-directional charging capabilities to absorb electricity from the grid or renewable sources like solar panels and release stored energy for peak loads or backup power.
The system performs critical functions including load shifting, frequency regulation, and demand response to balance renewable energy integration.
The BESS is designed to seamlessly integrate with Georgia Tech’s existing solar photovoltaic (PV) array.
Excess energy during high solar generation periods is stored and deployed later during late afternoon hours or overnight to advance energy independence and emissions reduction.
Christine Conwell serves as interim executive director of the Strategic Energy Institute at Georgia Tech.

Christine Conwell stated that Georgia Tech’s strategic plan envisions the campus as a dynamic laboratory where sustainable practices are integrated into operations, creating mini ecosystems for situational data to guide innovative energy research beyond the campus.
Scott Childers holds the title of vice president of essential power at Stryten Energy.

Scott Childers noted the deployment signifies an evolution in viewing energy storage as scalable solutions for commercial, institutional, and behind-the-meter use cases rather than just utility-scale grid components.

Scott Childers stated that with the introduction of this lead battery BESS, behind-the-meter applications are getting their day in the sun, expressing particular excitement about deploying units in commercial and industrial microgrids paired with EV charging stations to help the U.S. achieve its energy goals.
Richard Simmons is the director of research and studies at SEI.

Richard Simmons explained the system allows researchers to precisely control and study charging and discharging cycles in close coordination with the building’s rooftop solar PV array and a new electric vehicle (EV) charging test bed.
Researchers can time-shift solar input to align with demand patterns like late-afternoon building loads and store renewable electricity to charge vehicles at night.
Georgia Tech is planning a broader suite of energy storage technologies as part of its living lab ecosystem.

Richard Simmons stated that regional partners are exploring R&D and testing projects involving flow batteries for longer-duration storage and lithium-ion BESS integrating second-life EV battery modules for grid resilience.
The multi-technology approach aims to position Georgia Tech as a national leader in battery innovation, systems integration, and sustainable energy design.
The project trains students and supports cutting-edge research while delivering tangible environmental and operational benefits.
source_title: Georgia Tech, Stryten Launch Lead Battery Research System

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The company provides advanced vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) technology that delivers medium and long-duration energy storage (LDES).
VRFB technology helps ensure grid stability and facilitates increased utilization of solar and wind for businesses and consumers across the U.S.

The Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) includes vital Investment Tax Credits (ITC) for domestically sourced and manufactured standalone clean energy storage intended to lower costs, increase energy efficiency, and strengthen energy security.
Tim Vargo, Chief Executive Officer of Stryten Energy, welcomed the legislation’s long-term, standalone energy storage investment tax credits and its ten-year runway.
Vargo stated that these credits will help customers incorporate medium and long-duration storage such as VRFB batteries into their operations more economically than before.

Vargo noted that leveraging domestic VFRB technology and other long-term energy storage solutions will enable reliable access to clean power and help the U.S. achieve energy security as it transitions to a clean energy economy.

Scott Childers, Vice President of Stryten Energy’s Essential Power Division, stated that Stryten’s patented flow battery technology is uniquely positioned to provide safe, long-term, reliable on-demand energy needed to build a new economy powered by clean energy.

Childers added that the Inflation Reduction Act is expected to accelerate demand for domestically sourced energy storage, especially for commercial applications such as charging stations, manufacturing plants, and businesses with 24/7 energy requirements.
Stryten’s VRFB technology combines multiple innovations including domestically sourced vanadium electrolyte that is easily recycled.
The VRFB technology features increased power density and reduced battery downtime.
The technology offers the potential of limitless cycle life with proper maintenance.
The batteries have high-temperature ratings and are thermally safe.
The system is scalable to meet varying energy storage capacity needs.
Stryten’s VRFB technology is ideal for applications that require more than four hours of storage capacity to deploy clean energy on demand from renewable sources such as solar and wind energy.
Target applications for the VRFB technology include utility-scale storage, microgrids, and military bases.
Stryten Energy provides energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
The company partners with some of the world’s most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
The company's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
Stryten is an industry leader backed by more than a century of expertise.
source_title: Stryten Energy Applauds US Investments in Energy

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The company features its Enhanced Flooded Battery (EFB) technology along with a full lineup of energy storage solutions for the automotive aftermarket.
Nearly 30% of new start-stop vehicles sold in the U.S. now come equipped with an EFB.
According to the EPA, half of new vehicles produced in 2021 included start-stop technology.
Independent third-party testing found Stryten Energy’s EFB offers three times the cycle life and twice the charge acceptance compared to standard flooded batteries.
Testing showed Stryten Energy’s EFB yields more than two times the cycle life of a competing premium flooded battery.
The EFB contains a more robust alloy for higher heat tolerance at a lower grid thickness.
The EFB has an average capacity output 50% greater than Absorbed Glass Matt (AGM) for an equally sized battery.

The EFB outperforms AGM in under-hood applications for vehicles equipped with a flooded battery from the factory, given its greater energy availability, increased battery life and high heat tolerance.
Stryten Energy will feature these technologies at AAPEX 2023, which takes place October 31 - November 2 at the Venetian Expo in Las Vegas.
Bill Nonnamaker serves as Vice President of Sales, Transportation for Stryten Energy.

Bill Nonnamaker stated that batteries need more energy to support start-stop technology alongside conveniences and advanced safety features, and choosing the right battery will become increasingly important as vehicle power demands evolve.
Nonnamaker noted the Stryten EFB is specifically designed to resist cycling-related failures associated with increased electrical demands of modern vehicles.
The company will make a $100 donation (up to $10,000) to the University of the Aftermarket Foundation for every selfie taken with the Stryten Jeep at booth 2620.
The University of the Aftermarket Foundation funds scholarships and educational grants to help talented individuals maximize career potential in the automotive aftermarket industry.

Bob Egan, MAAP, chairman of the University of the Aftermarket Foundation, stated: 'We appreciate the creative way that Stryten Energy is supporting the University of the Aftermarket Foundation and encourage all AAPEX attendees to visit booth 2620 to take a selfie.'
Stryten Energy implemented LED lighting and will power down its booth during non-show hours for efficient energy use.
The company is using QR Codes instead of printed literature to minimize paper waste and replacing branded plastic water bottles with a water cooler and recycled cups.

Stryten Energy partners with recognized companies to meet demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity across Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
The company's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.

facts: Largo Inc. signed a non-binding letter of intent with Stryten Energy LLC to establish a 50:50 joint venture combining Largo Clean Energy Corp. with Stryten's vanadium redox flow battery business.

The joint venture aims to combine decades of VRFB technology expertise, access to raw vanadium supplies from friendly sources, and high-volume electrolyte production capabilities to transform the long-duration energy storage sector in North America.
The estimated market in North America for VRFB LDES solutions is hundreds of Gigawatts in size, requiring a vertically integrated vanadium supply chain.
The joint venture would provide access to U.S.-produced vanadium electrolyte needed for VRFB manufacturers to accelerate commercial deployment of vanadium battery solutions.

Daniel Tellechea, Director and Interim Chief Executive Officer of Largo, stated the agreement aims to unlock and maximize the value of LCE through its patented vanadium flow battery stack technology, electrolyte purification technology, and access to vanadium via Largo Physical Vanadium Corp.

Stryten's ability to produce electrolyte in large volumes is expected to help reduce the overall cost for VRFB solutions, a critical factor in catalyzing commercial adoption and meeting DOE LCOS targets.
The proposed transaction remains subject to negotiation of definitive agreements, completion of due diligence by both parties, and receipt of required Board and regulatory approvals.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, and provides energy storage solutions across Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
Stryten's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
Largo Physical Vanadium Corp. aims to supply physical vanadium to end users of VRFBs to advance the integration of renewable energy in long duration storage.
Largo is currently ramping up production of its ilmenite concentrate plant and evaluating its U.S.-based clean energy business, including advanced VCHARGE vanadium battery technology.
The announcement coincides with Enel Green Power España and LCE going live with a 5.5-megawatt hour VRFB in Spain, deploying one of the largest utility-scale vanadium systems in Europe.
source_title: Largo Announces a Proposed Joint Venture with Stryten Energy to Bring Innovation and Scale to North American Vanadium Flow Battery Market | INN

facts: Stryten Energy LLC announced plans to expand its domestic manufacturing capacity by an additional 10 gigawatts, bringing the total to 24 gigawatts.
The expansion aims to enhance the supply of batteries for military, long-duration energy storage, transportation, and material handling applications.
The effort involves increasing production across Stryten Energy’s 11 manufacturing and battery component plants in the United States.
Over 2,500 employees currently work at these facilities.
The facilities produce batteries used by military and government entities, data centers, grid storage systems, automotive industries, and material handling operations.
The planned investment is supported by existing advanced manufacturing production tax credits.
Mike Judd serves as President and Chief Executive Officer of Stryten Energy.
Mike Judd stated that Stryten Energy is committed to growing its domestic energy storage capacity to serve the needs of customers and partners in the United States.
Mike Judd emphasized that American energy security must rely upon domestic companies manufacturing batteries that keep supply chains running and critical infrastructure supplied with backup power.
Mike Judd noted that investments to significantly increase gigawatt capacity across their footprint will help support growing energy demand and achieve the nation’s goal of true energy resilience.
The company is scaling up production of various battery chemistries, including advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries.
Stryten Energy emphasizes energy security, which entails continuous access to reliable power sources.
The expansion includes facilities in Kansas, Georgia, Iowa, Virginia, and Arkansas.
These facilities are expected to create hundreds of high-paying manufacturing jobs and provide economic benefits to local communities.
Stryten Energy operates a vertically integrated supply chain with plants in Pennsylvania, New York, Indiana, Missouri, and Wisconsin.
The expansion plan includes increasing capacity for processing recycled plastic from spent batteries.
Recycled material will be used to produce new battery components for automobiles, forklifts, and locomotives.
The use of recycled materials contributes to a sustainable circular economy within the U.S. battery industry.
Stryten Energy recently opened a lithium battery assembly plant in Georgia to support military and industrial battery needs using its proprietary lithium module technology.
The company is partnering with Dragonfly Energy of Reno, Nevada, to introduce advanced lithium batteries to automotive aftermarket and battery distributor partners.

Stryten Energy launched Storion Energy LLC, a subsidiary focused on eliminating barriers for battery manufacturers to source competitively priced electrolytes for vanadium redox flow batteries used in long-duration energy storage applications in the U.S.
source_title: Stryten Energy Expands U.S. Battery Manufacturing by 10 GW - Battery-Tech Network

facts: Stryten Energy LLC announced at AAPEX 2022 the launch of Stryten Energy Components on November 9, 2022 in Las Vegas, Nevada.
The company is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The new division provides engineered-to-order battery components for lead, lithium, flow and other emerging battery technologies for the automotive and industrial markets.
Stryten Energy invested in automation in each of its four facilities to enhance quality, productivity and service to all customers.

Jeremy Furr, Components Division Leader for Stryten Energy, stated that these investments helped scale up operations to provide U.S.-manufactured and sourced components required for the emerging domestic battery market.
The division provides engineered plastic components from natural and recycled polypropylene, including battery containers, covers, vents, handles and terminal protectors.
Stryten Energy Components offers cold-formed lead battery terminals designed to prevent corrosion and utilizes a patented SuperLock terminal to provide superior sealing.
The company provides value-added technical services and expertise to help customers with product development, design, mold making and injection molding processing.
Jeremy Furr noted an increase in market demand for critical battery components in the U.S. and internationally.
Stryten Energy is continuing to invest in its component plants to expand its product offering and availability to serve international customers.

The division combines the company's existing custom plastic injection molded battery component operations with the recent acquisition of Tulip Richardson Manufacturing’s plastic injection molding, lead terminal and reprocessed polypropylene operations.
Stryten Energy Components operates manufacturing facilities in Lampeter, PA; Columbus, IN; Niagara Falls, NY; and Milwaukee, WI.
The company provides energy storage solutions and components across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
Stryten Energy powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains and trucks.
The company's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.

facts: Largo Inc. signed definitive agreements with Stryten Critical E-Storage, LLC (an affiliate of Stryten Energy LLC) to establish Storion Energy, LLC as a joint venture.
The transaction establishing Storion Energy is subject to customary closing conditions.
Francesco D’Alessio serves as Chief Commercial Officer of Largo and President of LCE.
The partnership aims to maximize the value of Largo’s vanadium products and utilize Storion’s patented purification process to accelerate vanadium electrolyte manufacture.
Accelerated vanadium electrolyte manufacturing is expected to increase demand for vanadium from Largo Physical Vanadium Corp. (LPV) and the broader vanadium sector.
Storion Energy intends to provide domestically sourced, price-competitive electrolyte for vanadium redox flow batteries (VRFB) used in long-duration energy storage (LDES).
Storion will operate locations in Wilmington, Massachusetts and Alpharetta, Georgia.
Storion Energy is a joint venture between a Stryten Energy affiliate and Largo Clean Energy Corp., a subsidiary of Largo Inc.
The JV aims to support scalable domestic electrolyte production to establish a fully integrated vertical supply chain for utility-scale VRFB LDES solutions.
VRFB technology provides long-duration energy storage greater than four hours to ensure grid stability and facilitate increased utilization of renewables.
Gallatin Capital LLC acted as financial advisor to Largo, and Linklaters LLP acted as legal counsel for the transaction.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, and provides energy storage solutions across Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
Stryten Energy's technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
Largo Physical Vanadium Corp. (LPV) aims to provide direct exposure to physical vanadium, a metal essential for steel, aerospace, and energy storage industries.
LPV's strategy includes owning and actively supplying vanadium to end users of vanadium flow batteries to advance renewable energy integration in long duration storage.
Largo produces critical materials from its Maracás Menchen Mine in Brazil and is one of the world’s largest primary vanadium producers.
Largo holds a 50% ownership stake in Storion Energy.
Largo Inc. common shares trade on the Nasdaq Stock Market and Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol LGO.
The press release containing these details was published on December 19, 2024, via Business Wire and on July 7, 2026, via Green Stock News.

Stryten Energy's booth number at MODEX 2026 is #B9325.
Matt Gould, Vice President of Sales, Industrial Power at Stryten Energy, stated that material handling operations are demanding more choice, flexibility, and performance from their energy systems.
Matt Gould stated that the Li610 launch reflects Stryten Energy's commitment to providing advanced, reliable solutions that help customers achieve higher uptime and greater productivity.

Matt Gould stated that combined with its lead and lithium battery portfolio, next-generation chargers, and fleet intelligence tools, Stryten Energy is delivering a complete motive power platform that supports the evolving needs of warehouses and distribution operations.
Stryten Energy will feature its Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) offerings at the event.
The BESS solutions integrate both lead and lithium technologies to deliver dependable backup power and grid-support capabilities.
The BESS solutions provide strong performance under high discharge rates.
The BESS solutions offer the benefits of mature technology, simplified maintenance, and high surge-current capabilities.
The BESS solutions are well-suited for short- to medium-duration energy storage and high-power applications.
source_title: Stryten Energy Launches New M‑Series Li610 Lithium‑Ion Battery

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy provides a broad range of energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
Stryten partners with some of the world's most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains and trucks.
Stryten's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries.
Stryten's stored energy technologies include intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.
Stryten Energy is backed by more than a century of industry expertise.
source_title: Energy, Automobile, EV, Renewable News | Stryten Energy Expands Support of Rivers of Recovery

facts: Dragonfly Energy Holdings Corp. signed a licensing agreement with a potential value of up to $30 million to license its Battle Born Batteries® brand to Stryten Energy for global distribution.
The agreement grants Stryten Energy exclusive rights to market and distribute Battle Born Batteries for military, automotive, marine, power sports, lawn and garden, and golf cart applications.
These licensed markets represent business-to-business (B2B) end markets where Dragonfly Energy currently has no presence.
The partnership encompasses a mix of existing Battle Born Batteries products with a strong focus on developing new battery models tailored to Stryten Energy’s target markets.
Dragonfly Energy will receive an initial upfront licensing fee of $5 million.
Dragonfly Energy is entitled to mid-single digit royalty payments based on Stryten Energy’s product volume using the licensed branding.
The royalty payments follow a tiered structure reaching up to an additional $25 million.
Once the tiered royalties reach the maximum, Stryten Energy will be required to pay a nominal annual license fee.
Additional fees will apply for battery design and contract manufacturing services provided outside of the licensing agreement.
The agreement was announced on July 30, 2024, in Reno, Nev.
Stryten Energy is a leading U.S.-based manufacturer of energy storage solutions headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy provides advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium batteries to customers across the transportation, industrial, military, and government sectors.
Stryten Energy has a workforce of over 2,500 employees and operates ten manufacturing plants.

Dragonfly Energy will initially contract manufacture all batteries distributed by Stryten Energy at its facility in Northern Nevada until Stryten Energy establishes its own domestic manufacturing footprint.
Dragonfly Energy will continue independent operations in existing markets including recreational vehicles (RV), off-grid properties, heavy-duty trucking, stationary storage, and oil and gas.
Dragonfly Energy specializes in cell manufacturing, battery pack assembly, and full system integration using a patented dry electrode manufacturing process for chemistry-agnostic power solutions.
Dragonfly Energy's overarching mission includes the future deployment of proprietary, nonflammable, all-solid-state battery cells.

Stryten Energy plans to leverage its vast distributor and customer network, including automotive aftermarket retailers and battery distributors, to introduce Battle Born Batteries branded products to new B2B markets.
Dragonfly Energy remains committed to providing exceptional technical support for all Battle Born Batteries products and will continue to grow its support team along with increasing demand.
Dr. Denis Phares serves as chief executive officer of Dragonfly Energy.
Mike Judd serves as chief executive officer of Stryten Energy.
Tyler Bourns serves as chief marketing officer at Dragonfly Energy.
“At Dragonfly Energy, we’re incredibly proud to see the Battle Born Batteries brand take this leap forward into new end markets,” said Dr. Denis Phares, chief executive officer of Dragonfly Energy.
“At Stryten Energy, we’re focused on providing premium energy storage solutions to our customers,” said Mike Judd, chief executive officer of Stryten Energy.

“The impact of this partnership with Stryten Energy for the wider proliferation of the Battle Born Batteries brand cannot be understated,” said Tyler Bourns, chief marketing officer at Dragonfly Energy.
source_title: Dragonfly Energy Enters into $30M Licensing Agreement for Battle Born Batteries Brand with Stryten Energy, a Leading North American Battery Manufacturer | Dragonfly Energy

facts: Stryten Energy LLC announced executive leadership changes and appointments to support its long-term strategy for growth.
Tim Vargo will transition from Chief Executive Officer to Chairman of the Board before the end of the company’s fiscal year on March 31, 2023.
Mike Judd will be promoted from President and Chief Operating Officer to Chief Executive Officer and President, effective before the end of the company’s fiscal year.

Petar Oklobdzija joins the company as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective February 1, 2023, and will lead Finance, Accounting, Treasury, Information Technology, and Strategic Sourcing functions.
Dan Autey joined the company as Executive Vice President, Commercial, on January 3, 2023, and is responsible for leading commercial strategies across the Stryten Energy business.

Tim Vargo stated that Mike Judd’s appointment to CEO is the culmination of a succession plan developed over the last several years and expressed confidence in his ability to achieve increased profitability and growth.

Mike Judd noted he is proud to take on the CEO role and excited to welcome two seasoned leaders to the senior management team to meet the increased demand for sustainable, clean energy storage solutions.
Mike Judd highlighted that Dan Autey’s automotive and industrial battery expertise will strengthen the company’s ability to serve customers’ evolving energy needs.
Mike Judd noted that Petar Oklobdzija’s tenure in operations and finance in the automotive and battery industries will help accelerate growth strategy into new and existing energy storage markets.

The past year marked a significant period of successful transformation for Stryten Energy with the integration of three strategic acquisitions that added advanced lithium-ion and vanadium redox flow battery technologies to its energy storage chemistry portfolio.

These acquisitions combined with ongoing capital investments in manufacturing plants position the company to meet increased demand for domestically produced, clean and sustainable energy storage solutions.
Stryten Energy provides energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
The company is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, and partners with recognized companies to meet growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten Energy’s stored energy technologies power applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
The company’s technology portfolio includes advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
Stryten Energy is an industry leader backed by more than a century of expertise in delivering top-performing energy solutions.
source_title: Stryten Energy Announces Executive Leadership Appointments for Next Phase of Growth

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, LLC., will locate its first U.S. recycling facility in Georgia.
SungEel Recycling Park Georgia is a subsidiary of the Korea-based global industry leader SungEel HiTech Co., Ltd.
The company will invest more than $37 million in the new facility.
The project will create 104 jobs in Stephens County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia recently announced record-breaking numbers for fiscal year 2022.
Governor Kemp noted companies like SungEel are evidence that the rapidly growing electric mobility ecosystem continues to generate new jobs for hardworking Georgians across the state.
Governor Kemp highlighted Korea has been a key partner in Georgia’s growing sustainable technology industry.
Governor Kemp welcomed SungEel to the Peach State for their first U.S. facility.
SungEel has more than 10 years of patented technology specializing in complete recycling technology using a 100 percent full circulatory system.
The company's system recycles end-of-life batteries and battery manufacturing scrap with a metal recovery rate of more than 95 percent.
Recovered metals from the process include nickel, cobalt, and lithium.

Suk Jae Yim, Representative of SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, stated that SungEel HiTech's entry into Georgia is the last piece of the puzzle to build a sustainable ecosystem of Georgia’s electric vehicle supply chain.
SungEel Recycling Park Georgia will conduct its full responsibility to build a U.S. eco-friendly industrial ecosystem in line with the expectations of the State of Georgia and Stephens County.
The new facility will be located at the Hayestone Brady Business Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site, in Toccoa.
The GRAD Program is a proactive effort by Georgia’s economic development community to develop a portfolio of available sites ready for fast-track development.
Operations are expected to begin in early 2024.
Over the next few years, the company will be hiring for technicians, operators, administrative, and sales roles.
Brian Akin, Chairman of the Stephens County Development Authority, stated it has been a pleasure working with SungEel HiTech over the past eight months.
Akin noted this is an exciting time for Toccoa-Stephens County and a solid boost for the local job market.
Akin highlighted that investments in infrastructure, workforce development, and education were all significant factors in the selection process.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Stephens County Development Authority and Georgia EMC on this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated SungEel is a company at the cutting edge of sustainable technology.
Wilson believes SungEel’s proximity to other members of Georgia’s electric mobility ecosystem will lead to amazing success.
Wilson noted creating the jobs of the future and protecting the opportunities of today means preparing key industries, such as the auto industry, for the next technological revolution.
Wilson expressed gratitude to partners in Stephens County for investment in speed to market solutions like site preparation.

Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate have made it an attractive location for a diverse array of rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain that will help companies increase efficiencies by reducing the reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $13 billion in investments to Georgia since 2020.
EV-related projects have contributed more than 18,100 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Korean Lithium-Ion Battery Recycler SungEel HiTech to Build First U.S. Recycling Facility, SungEel Recycling Park Georgia - EIN Presswire

facts: Stephens County is one step closer to adding another international company to its roster of industrial/manufacturing facilities in the Haystone Brady Business Park.

At a recent Stephens County Development Authority meeting, a motion was approved to authorize the SCDA chairman to sign a Memorandum of Understanding with Korean lithium-ion battery recycling company SungEel HighTech.
SCDA President and CEO Brittany Ivey is working to bring the community’s MOU in line with the MOU that SungEel has already signed with the State of Georgia.

The MOU between SungEel and the State of Georgia, signed April 17 of this year, stipulates that a REBA Grant in the amount of $700,000 will be awarded to SungEel through the Stephens County Development Authority.

REBA Grant funding comes from the Georgia Department of Community Affairs and is an incentive program used to help close deals when companies consider Georgia versus another state or country for location or expansion.
REBA funds may finance various fixed-asset needs including infrastructure, real estate acquisition, construction, or machinery and equipment.
The state is only legally allowed to offer incentives for projects when the company is also considering locations in at least one other state.
Local incentives included in the SCDA and SungEel MOU will not be made public until after both parties sign it.
The facility will operate under the name SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, LLC.
Stephens County has changed its land sale strategy since 2017 by asking for full price for the land in Haystone Brady instead of giving it away.
Under the current MOU terms, the company will be placed on the tax digest within the first two years of operation.
The project will bring additional jobs to Stephens County with higher than average wages.

An August 2022 press release from Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, LLC will locate its first U.S. recycling facility in Georgia as a subsidiary of Korea-based global industry leader SungEel HiTech Co., Ltd.
The company will invest more than $37 million in the new facility and create 104 jobs in Stephens County.
The SCDA has been working on bringing this facility to Stephens County since November of 2021.

After a site visit by SungEel company representatives, a contract was signed for the company to purchase Haystone Brady lots 3 and 4, located behind the NIFCO KTW facility and next to the SKAPS building on Haystone Brady Road.
The lots were chosen because they are pad ready and already have sewer utility access, which will result in a quicker project completion timeline than undeveloped parcels.
The SungEel facility will be part of the company’s electric vehicle supply chain according to company executives.

Suk Jae Yim, Representative of SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, stated that SungEel HiTech’s entry into Georgia is the last piece of the puzzle to build a sustainable ecosystem of Georgia’s electric vehicle supply chain.
SungEel Recycling Park Georgia will conduct its full responsibility to build a U.S. eco-friendly industrial ecosystem in line with the expectations of the State of Georgia and Stephens County.
WNEG will continue to follow the story and report on details of the MOU between the SCDA and SungEel once the document is signed and made public.

links.context: Source for more details on the pending MOU and project

source_title: Progress Being Made On Bringing New Lithium-Ion Battery Plant - 92.1 WLHR

facts: SungEel’s groundbreaking for its first U.S. recycling facility is scheduled for March 2023.
Stephens County Development Authority President and CEO Brittany Ivey stated the South Korea-based company anticipates breaking ground in March with project completion by the end of 2023.

Governor Brian Kemp announced in August 2022 that lithium-ion battery recycler and raw materials provider SungEel Recycling Park Georgia will locate its first U.S. recycling facility in Stephens County.
The company is expected to invest more than $37 million in the new facility.
The project is expected to create 104 jobs in Stephens County.
SungEel utilizes over 10 years of patented technology specializing in complete recycling via a 100 percent full circulatory system.
The recycling system processes end-of-life batteries and battery manufacturing scrap with a metal recovery rate exceeding 95 percent.
Recovered metals from the process include nickel, cobalt, and lithium.
SungEel Recycling Park Georgia’s new facility will be located at the Hayestone Brady Business Park in Toccoa.
The site is certified under the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) program.
The GRAD Program is a proactive effort by Georgia’s economic development community to develop sites ready for fast-track development.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in early 2024.
Over the next few years, the company will hire technicians, operators, administrative staff, and sales personnel.
source_title: SungEel's Stephens County facility's groundbreaking set for March 2023 - WNEG

facts: TCI has been manufacturing powder coatings for more than 35 years.
TCI aspires to be the premium brand supplier for OEM coatings.
TCI was founded in Ellaville, GA with a modest plant and a portable building that served as the lab and office space.
TCI has expanded to become an international company with multiple warehouse locations.
TCI operates a state of the art product development center.
TCI employs a staff of several hundred employees focused on servicing customers.
TCI's highly trained technical service and sales teams extend across North America.
TCI provides expert advice and assistance to customers ranging from large multinational corporations to independent custom coaters.
TCI maintains an expansive network of distribution centers, either certified or approved external providers, that services customers throughout the United States, Canada, Mexico, Europe, and Asia.
TCI focuses on environmental consciousness and reliable service response at all levels.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings - Powder Coating Institute

facts: TCI Powder Coatings announced the opening of a new office in Americus, GA.
The new office will function as corporate headquarters for the business and house the administrative, customer service, finance, and information systems departments.
Doug Greene is President of TCI Powder Coatings.
In the near future, the company plans to add laboratory and training facilities, sample product preparation, and light warehousing in the new facility.
This expansion is part of a multi-year strategy to improve capabilities and provide innovative, premium solutions and service to customers.
The Americus-Sumter Payroll Development Authority assisted in making the new facility possible for TCI.
TCI Powder Coatings has been part of the community for 28 years.
The company anticipates opening the office in February 2016 with approximately 20 employees.
Additional functions will be added as growth allows.
The facility is expected to accommodate up to 75 full-time employees.
Barbara Grogan is Executive Director of the Payroll Development Authority.
Grogan stated that supporting local and regional industry expansion is critical to Sumter County’s economic development strategy.
Growth of long-term companies such as TCI Powder Coatings benefits Sumter County, particularly in their ability to hire locally from the existing workforce.
The Development Authority partnered with TCI and will continue to support their expansion needs.
TCI has an outstanding reputation, providing a positive work environment for citizens and a product that is used by many industries worldwide.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings Expands in Georgia | 2016-01-17  | PCI Magazine

facts: TCI has been manufacturing powder coatings for more than 35 years with a strong commitment to people, products, and service.
TCI was founded in Ellaville, GA, starting with a modest plant and a portable building that served as the lab and office space.
TCI has expanded to become an international company with multiple warehouse locations.
TCI operates a state-of-the-art product development center.
TCI employs a staff of several hundred employees focused on servicing customers.

TCI's highly trained technical service and sales teams extend across North America and provide expert advice and assistance to customers of all sizes, from large multinational corporations to independent custom coaters.

TCI maintains an expansive network of distribution centers, consisting of either certified or approved external providers, allowing it to service customers throughout the United States, Canada, Mexico, Europe, and Asia.
TCI ensures stock powders and products are readily available at a moment's notice.
TCI focuses on being environmentally conscious and providing reliable service response at all levels under the motto 'Where Your World Matters'.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings - Powder Coating Institute

facts: TCI Powder Coatings is located in Americus, Georgia.
TCI Powder Coatings has maintained its Committed Badge from EcoVadis for a third consecutive assessment cycle.
The EcoVadis framework evaluates 21 sustainability criteria across four core themes: environment, labor and human rights, ethics and sustainable procurement.
Tom Whalen serves as vice president of commercial operations at TCI Powder Coatings.

Tom Whalen stated the company is excited and proud to receive an EcoVadis sustainability badge for a third submission in a row, emphasizing that environmental impact is central to their innovation, service, and performance.
The company lives out its "Your World Matters" commitment by demonstrating care for the planet.
All of TCI's powder coating products are inherently VOC-free.
TCI's research and development teams continue to work on formulations free of PFAs and TGIC.
Technical service staff provide training and troubleshooting support aimed at reducing product use and waste in the field.
All shipping boxes used by the company remain 100% recyclable.
The company is continuing to implement digital initiatives across its business operations.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings Maintains EcoVadis Committed Badge > Powder Coated Tough Magazine

facts: TCI Powder Coatings is a subsidiary of RPM International.
TCI Powder Coatings and BASF entered into an agreement that allows TCI to sell the BASF CathoGuard electrocoat technology to specified TCI market segments.
Jack Bostock, TCI vice president of sales and marketing, stated that the alliance builds on TCI’s commitment to market differentiation.
Bostock noted that pairing the CathoGuard technology series with premium powder coatings is a win-win for customers looking for performance.
The partnership aims to deliver an innovative solution meeting global standards to make a significant difference for customers’ production lines.
The alliance will help customers increase throughput while reducing costs.
The BASF CathoGuard coating combines several unique performance qualities for parts that are more difficult to paint efficiently, including those with multiple angles and crevices.
CathoGuard improves the application process as well as coverage and corrosion edge protection.
CathoGuard lowers total material costs by nearly 20 percent.
TCI market segments with access to this technology through the new alliance include off-road and heavy equipment, general finishing, office furniture, and appliance.
CathoGuard will also be available to custom coaters that supply major subassemblies and modular components for the automotive market.
Thaddeus Lepkowski, BASF tier metals market manager, stated they are very pleased that TCI can deliver their innovative product line more broadly to customers.
Lepkowski noted that the chemistry in CathoGuard coating achieves a flawless coat on hard-to-paint parts found in many of TCI’s markets where finish quality and durability are critical.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings, BASF Announce Strategic Alliance - Coatings World

facts: The Jeep brand achieved its 75th anniversary milestone in 2016.
To commemorate the milestone, the Jeep marketing team rolled out Anniversary Special Edition models of several vehicles.
Exclusive details for the 2016 Jeep Wrangler Unlimited fleet included distinct vehicle colors and trim packages.
A specialized bronze-colored trim package along with off-road bumpers are painted and protected with TCI Powder Coatings.
TCI Powder Coatings collaborated with Chrysler to develop a coating that met manufacturing process demands of Flex-N-Gate and Powder Cote II as well as styling expectations.
The successful metallic coating received positive feedback, prompting Chrysler’s styling team to expand its use to the tow hitch in the trim package.
The metallic bumper's success led the broader Chrysler styling team to extend it as an option for higher-end Jeep Sahara and Wrangler anniversary edition models.

About TCI Powder Coatings company information

source_title: TCI Powder Coatings Provides Innovation for Jeep’s 75th Anniversary - Coatings World

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that TEKLAS will invest $6.5 million in opening their first North American facility and headquarters in Gordon County.
The new facility will deliver 120 jobs to Calhoun, Georgia.
TEKLAS is a Turkish advanced research and development, manufacturing company and supplier of electric vehicle (EV) parts.
Founded in Turkey in 1971, TEKLAS designs, develops, and manufactures fluid circulation systems for cooling and heating applications.
The company produces hoses and pipes from rubber, plastic, and metal for the global automotive industry.
TEKLAS uses highly innovative and automated manufacturing processes using advanced water molding technology.
TEKLAS USA will include assembly lines to serve the most prominent original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in the industry.
Operations at the new Georgia facility are scheduled to start in the spring of 2021.
TEKLAS is headquartered in Turkey and maintains locations in Bulgaria, Serbia, Mexico, and China, along with sales offices in Germany and France.
Regional Director Arcan Ergur stated that the Calhoun location will help the company be closer to its main customers.
The 200,000-square-foot facility and headquarters will be located at 320 South Industrial Boulevard in Calhoun, Georgia.
TEKLAS will hire for positions at a variety of skill levels, including advanced manufacturing, management, and supervision.
Larry Roye, chairman of the Development Authority of Gordon County, applauded the efforts of state and local partners who helped encourage the company to come to this decision.

Kathy Johnson, president and CEO of the Gordon County Chamber of Commerce, commended TEKLAS for its research and advancements in electric vehicle manufacturing and for its contribution to the expansion of the automotive sector of the local manufacturing base.
GDEcD Project Manager Fernanda Kirchner represented the Global Commerce division on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Economic Development Authority of Gordon County and Georgia Power.
The Center of Innovation for Logistics, a division of GDEcD, provided freight analysis to support the project and company planning.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the State of Georgia is focused on attracting and growing the jobs of the future and welcomed TEKLAS to the diverse family of EV suppliers in Georgia.
TEKLAS serves major car original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) with more than 45 years of experience in design, development, and production capabilities.
The company provides innovative solutions for thermoplastics, rubber, and metal products to its customers.
source_title: Electric Vehicle Manufacturer TEKLAS to Open North American Headquarters in Georgia - Expansion Solutions

facts: Teklas is one of the leading suppliers to prominent car manufacturers.
Teklas operates 11 plants in 5 different countries.
Teklas is a leading global supplier of automotive fluid systems.
Teklas provides Thermal Management Systems.
Teklas develops production constructions for OEMs, including rubber, plastics, metal solutions, and water injection technology (WIT) to reduce vehicle weight and boost recycling rates.
Teklas is one of the largest manufacturers of connectors in Europe.
Teklas constantly develops innovative hose structures to follow emerging automotive technologies and adapt to changing regulations.
Teklas has more than 50 years of experience in rubber compound and high pressure hose production.
Teklas develops air conditioning hoses with considerably low permeability values (<0.3 g/m*day).
Teklas develops rubber hoses and metal tubes resistant to fluids and refrigerants for customers at given temperatures.
Teklas leads the future of the automotive industry through its R&D centers staffed by competent engineers and experts.
Teklas's R&D team develops ideas based on specific automotive industry needs, enabling flawless production after validation.
Teklas develops and deploys Robotic Automation Process at scale in its production process.
Teklas's vision includes an automation-based approach in manufacturing to achieve a sustainable future and business.

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles is a United States-based company located in Augusta, Georgia.
The company operates as a subsidiary of Textron Inc., a Fortune 500 company.
Textron Specialized Vehicles employs 1,166 people.
The company generates $1 billion in annual revenue.
Textron Specialized Vehicles was founded in 1954.
The company's industry is classified as Other Vehicles with SIC Code 5012 (Automobiles and Other Motor Vehicles).
Textron Specialized Vehicles manufactures golf cars, ground support equipment for the aviation industry, industrial utility vehicles, and recreational side-by-side UTVs.
The company's products are sold globally to diverse customer segments including golf courses, airports, industrial facilities, and recreational enthusiasts.
Textron Specialized Vehicles operates under several prominent brands: Cushman®, E-Z-GO®, Arctic Cat®, Jacobsen®, Ransomes®, TUG™, Douglas™, Premier™, and Safeaero™.
As of June 2, 2025, the company's RPM Program has surpassed 400 graduates since its founding.
On April 3, 2025, the company enabled consumers to build and buy their dream E-Z-GO® vehicle from home.
On February 23, 2025, Textron Specialized Vehicles was associated with an issue regarding insufficient side and rear reflectors/FMVSS 108.
On February 4, 2025, the company introduced the Cushman Hauler XL Long-Bed Utility Vehicle.
On January 22, 2025, the company introduced Pace Technology Walk™ to enhance golf-course management and track walking golfers and caddies.
On December 18, 2024, Textron weighed options for its off-road vehicles business and paused production.
source_title: Textron Specialized Vehicles - Company Overview, Contact Info, Competitors | Connexy

facts: On February 19, Textron Specialized Vehicles announced a deal to purchase a new manufacturing plant and 235 acres of land from Procter & Gamble Co.
The purchased facility is located where Mike Padgett Highway meets Marvin Griffin Road in the southern portion of Augusta, Georgia.
This location is in the same city where Textron Specialized Vehicles is headquartered and EZGO golf buggies are manufactured.
The new facility comprises manufacturing and administrative space totaling 600,000 square feet.
The facility will be used to augment current Augusta operations, including the production of EZGO golf buggies.
New offices and manufacturing processes will be installed in the plant, located approximately one mile from the current headquarters.
Textron Specialized Vehicles has been headquartered in Augusta since Textron Inc. purchased the original manufacturer of EZGO golf buggies in 1954.

The company manufactures more than 90 models of golf carts, low-speed vehicles (LSVs), neighborhood electric vehicles (NEVs), and utility task vehicles (UTVs) for consumer, commercial, and industrial uses.
Other brands under Textron Specialized Vehicles include Cushman, Bad Boy, Douglas, and TUG, most of which are manufactured or assembled in Augusta.
Kevin Holleran serves as the president of Textron Specialized Vehicles.
The expansion will enable rapid increases in manufacturing throughput and flexibility to meet growing customer demands from individual consumers to commercial enterprises.
The total purchase amount for the expansion is more than $40 million.
The Augusta Economic Development Authority issued $20 million in revenue bonds backed by a payment agreement with the company to fund the purchase and capital improvements.
Walter Sprouse serves as the executive director of the Augusta Economic Development Authority.

Walter Sprouse stated that Textron Specialized Vehicles’ decision to expand is proof of Georgia’s great infrastructure for manufacturing and that the authority was excited to assist with creating new jobs in Augusta.
The State of Georgia provided a $600,000 project-development grant and $1.6 million in job-training services through the Georgia Quick Start program.
In exchange for state assistance, Textron Specialized Vehicles has five years to create 400 new jobs and complete its $40 million investment.
The company currently employs approximately 1,000 Georgia residents.
The company's workforce has steadily grown by 13 percent each year.
Nathan Deal serves as the Governor of Georgia.

Governor Nathan Deal stated that the expansion reinforces the advantages of Georgia’s business resources for a leading company looking to expand operations, noting their commitment to job creation and economic development is evident in the number of companies who’ve expanded or relocated there.
Adela Kelley is a senior project manager representing the Global Commerce division at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Region 7.
Chris Carr is a Commissioner at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Region 7.
Adela Kelley and Chris Carr are major contributors to the partnership between Georgia and Textron Specialized Vehicles.
Existing industry accounted for 75 percent of total investment to the state and 63 percent of total jobs created among the 329 projects located in Georgia.

Chris Carr stated that existing industry accounted for 75 percent of total investment to the state and 63 percent of total jobs created, and expressed a desire to work with local partners in Augusta to continue supporting future growth.
Kevin Holleran stated that the city of Augusta, surrounding region, and state of Georgia have been valuable partners for Textron Specialized Vehicles since its earliest days.
source_title: Everything You Need to Know About Texron's August Expansion

facts: Textron Inc. operates in the aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses worldwide.
The company operates across six segments: Textron Aviation, Bell, Textron Systems, Industrial, Textron eAviation, and Finance.

The Textron Aviation segment manufactures, sells, and services business jets, turboprop and piston engine aircraft, and military trainer and defense aircraft, while offering maintenance, inspection, repair services, commercial parts, and advanced flight training devices.
The Bell segment supplies military and commercial helicopters, tiltrotor aircraft, and related spare parts and services.

The Textron Systems segment offers unmanned aircraft systems, electronic systems and solutions, advanced marine crafts, piston aircraft engines, live military air-to-air and air-to-ship training, weapons and related components, and armored and specialty vehicles.

The Industrial segment provides blow-molded solutions including conventional plastic fuel tanks, pressurized fuel tanks, plastic tanks for catalytic reduction systems, lightweight and composite pentatonic battery systems for use in electric vehicles primarily to automobile original equipment manufacturers, golf cars, off-road utility vehicles, powersports products, light transportation vehicles, aviation ground support equipment, and professional turf-maintenance equipment.

The Textron eAviation segment manufactures and sells light aircraft and gliders with electric and combustion engines and provides research and development initiatives related to sustainable aviation solutions.
The Finance segment offers financing services to purchase new and pre-owned aviation aircraft and Bell helicopters.
Textron Inc. was founded in 1923 and is headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island.
The company employs 34,000 full-time employees.

Key executives include Executive Chairman Mr. Scott C. Donnelly (born 1962), Executive VP & CFO Mr. David Rosenberg (born 1977), Executive VP General Counsel Mr. E. Robert Lupone (born 1960), Executive VP & Chief Human Resources Officer Ms. Julie G. Duffy (born 1966), CEO President & Director Ms. Lisa M. Atherton (born 1975), VP & Chief Information Officer Mr. Todd A. Kackley, Vice President of Investor Relations & Treasurer Mr. Scott P. Hegstrom, CEO & President of Industrial Segment and Kautex and Textron Specialized Vehicles Mr. Jorg Rautenstrauch, Senior Vice President of Government Affairs & Washington Operations Ms. Shannon H. Hines, and Chief Commercial Officer of Bell Mr. Rob Scholl.
Upcoming corporate events include a Textron Inc. Earnings Date on July 28, 2026 at 12:30 PM UTC.
Recent corporate events include a Dividend Date on July 1, 2026 and an Ex-Dividend Date on June 12, 2026.
The company filed an SD: Specialized Disclosure Report pursuant to Section 1502 of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act relating to the use of conflict minerals (Rule 13p-1).
source_title: Textron Inc. (TXT) Company Profile & Facts - Yahoo Finance

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company, introduces closed-loop electronic fuel injection technology on all E-Z-GO RXV and TXT golf car models and for Cushman’s Hauler, Refresher and Shuttle vehicles.
Closed-loop EFI technology adjusts the fuel-to-air ratio in the engine to improve fuel economy, reduce engine service needs and lower emissions.

Michael R. Parkhurst, Vice President, Golf & Turf North America for Textron Specialized Vehicles, stated that the new engine will run more efficiently, offer customers increased performance, lower costs of operation and a greener ecological footprint.
Closed-loop EFI uses an oxygen sensor to read exhaust levels, enabling sensors in the new engine to adjust the fuel-to-air ratio for better performance in all weather conditions.
The new EFI technology increases the number of operating hours between service intervals, resulting in less maintenance.

Exclusive sound-deadening techniques on the E-Z-GO TXT include an extended air intake hose and sound-absorbing foam under the seat and rear-body access panels to reduce engine noise and create a more pleasing engine pitch.
Closed-loop EFI is now available on all gas-powered E-Z-GO RXV and TXT vehicles, as well as Cushman Hauler, Refresher and Shuttle models.
E-Z-GO was founded in 1954 in Augusta, Ga., became part of Textron Inc. in 1960, and today operates as part of Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc.

Products sold under the E-Z-GO brand include RXV and TXT fleet golf cars, Freedom RXV and TXT personal golf cars, E-Z-GO Express personal utility vehicles, and the 2Five street-legal low-speed vehicle.
E-Z-GO’s newest offerings include the ELiTE series of lithium-ion powered golf cars and PTVs, and the exclusive 72-volt AC electric powertrain found in its latest Express series vehicles.

Cushman vehicles are designed and manufactured by Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., with products ranging from Hauler utility vehicles to the Titan fully electric material-handling vehicle for indoor environments with a load capacity of 3,000 pounds, and the Minute Miser for transporting people and small loads.

Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of golf cars, utility and personal transportation vehicles, snowmobiles, side-by-sides, all-terrain vehicles, professional turf-care equipment, and ground support equipment.
Textron Specialized Vehicles markets products under the E-Z-GO, Cushman, Arctic Cat, Jacobsen, Ransomes, TUG, Douglas, Premier and Safeaero brands.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
source_title: Textron Delivers Closed-Loop EFI Tech for E-Z-GO - The Golf Wire

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., a Textron Inc. company, is based in Augusta, GA.
The company announced the transition of its Bad Boy Off Road brand to Textron Off Road.
Side-by-side models in the current line remain the same during the brand transition.
Vehicles with the new Textron Off Road branding will begin to appear in authorized dealerships by April 2017.
The Textron Off Road product line features gasoline-powered Stampede and Stampede XTR side-by-sides.
The Textron Off Road product line includes all-electric Recoil, Recoil iS, and Recoil iS Crew vehicles.
The Textron Off Road product line includes the hybrid Ambush iS vehicle.
Textron Off Road pioneered electrically powered 4x4 vehicles for hunting and outdoor recreation due to their silent, odorless operation and emissions-free drivetrains.
John Collins, vice president of consumer for Textron Specialized Vehicles, stated the brand change reflects detailed design, precision engineering, manufacturing expertise, and high performance.
Changes to the company's website and social media platforms are complete and online.
Textron Off Road is a brand of Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company.

Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of golf cars, utility and personal transportation vehicles, snowmobiles, side-by-sides, all-terrain vehicles, professional turf-care equipment, and ground support equipment.
Textron Specialized Vehicles markets products under the E-Z-GO, Cushman, Arctic Cat, Textron Off Road, Jacobsen, Dixie Chopper, Ransomes, TUG, Douglas, Premier, and Safeaero brands.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
source_title: New Textron Off Road Brand Reflects Heritage of High Performance - ATVILLUSTRATED

facts: Textron E-Z-GO LLC announced a new "Reserve Now" online feature that allows customers to customize and order E-Z-GO golf cars, personal-transport vehicles, and street-legal low-speed vehicles without an initial dealership visit.
The online ordering system enables buyers to select from multiple color, seating, lighting, and accessory options to customize their vehicle.
E-Z-GO designs and manufactures its vehicles at a facility in Augusta, Ga.
Rob Scholl serves as the president and CEO of Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc.
The "Reserve Now" ordering process requires customers to use an online vehicle builder on the E-Z-GO website and place a $500 deposit.
Custom vehicles are built to order and delivered to the nearest Authorized E-Z-GO Dealer for final preparation, payment, and customer pickup.
Most custom orders will be fulfilled within eight weeks, while some models and option packages may require longer lead times.
Customers can search nearby dealer inventories for available vehicles and request online quotes for new and used stock.
The "Reserve Now" feature is applicable to E-Z-GO Freedom®, Liberty, street-legal Liberty LSV, Express® and Valor® models.
Throughout 2025, E-Z-GO will release new special-edition vehicles featuring new colors and special options.

The new Phantom limited edition vehicle is a pure-white Freedom RXV equipped with black premium seats, a modular top, tinted windshield, LED lights, a premium steering wheel, soundbar, and the IntelliGauge™ smart gauge system.
E-Z-GO was founded in Augusta, Ga. in 1954.
The company is recognized for its use of innovative sustainable electric-vehicle and powertrain technology.
E-Z-GO's product lineup includes RXV® fleet golf cars; Freedom® and Valor personal golf cars; and E-Z-GO Express™ personal utility vehicles.
The Liberty™ model is the industry's first vehicle to provide four forward-facing seats within a compact, golf-car-sized footprint.
E-Z-GO became part of Textron Inc. in 1960 and currently operates as a Textron Specialized Vehicles business.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry corporation that utilizes a global network of aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses to provide customer solutions.
