facts: Enplas America is located in Marietta, GA.
Enplas America is a leading provider of precision injection molded plastics products and services.
The company operates a state-of-the-art facility that utilizes cutting-edge technologies to deliver high-quality products.
Enplas specializes in custom injection molded plastic components known for their precision, durability, and functionality.
The company excels in producing intricate small parts and large complex assemblies tailored to specific client requirements.
Enplas employs a collaborative approach to provide design recommendations and custom solutions that exceed client expectations.
Enplas serves the automotive industry by providing components for interior and exterior applications, lighting systems, and electrical connectors.
The company's products meet stringent automotive standards for durability, heat resistance, and compliance.
Enplas's products are designed to enhance vehicle performance and safety.
The company possesses extensive knowledge of engineering-grade plastics and assists clients in selecting suitable materials based on strength, chemical resistance, and regulatory compliance.
Enplas partners with trusted suppliers to source high-quality materials for client needs.
Enplas America is recognized as a trusted partner delivering injection molded plastic solutions across various industries.
source_title: Enplas America, Inc. Distributor | Connectors, Sockets | IBS Electronics

facts: Enplas is a leader in the development and manufacturing of custom mechanical plastic parts for applications across the automotive, consumer electronics, medical, and other industries.
Enplas engineers utilize scientific and performance-driven processes to design reliable plastic gears for applications.

Thorough DFM, in-house fine tool making, scientific injection molding, and automated assembly are conducted for all mechanical parts and devices to ensure mechanical performance in manufactured components.
Enplas custom-designs and manufactures scientifically validated gears and gear trains to meet performance and reliability standards.
Engineers utilize simulation and modeling technology following an empirical gear performance database and rigorous testing procedures to ensure reliable design and manufacturing.
Enplas supplies spur gears, helical gears, worm and worm wheel gears, planetary gear systems, and many more custom and standard formats.
Customers develop and manufacture other plastic components that conduct critical mechanical functions in automotive, medical, and other industries.

Enplas delivers a steady supply of high volume, high quality, and high-performance parts with extensive DFM, performance-focused engineering, problem-solving, fine tool making, and deep expertise in injection molding.
Automotive parts require special features such as heat tolerance and longevity.
Enplas conducts extensive tests during product development (e.g., stress tests) to ensure consistent features in mass-produced parts.
Enplas produces reliable parts for fuel pumps, ABS/ESC, seat belts, motor insulators, electric throttle valves, and more.
Cam injection molding is used to provide contour precision for accurate and efficient mechanical cam systems.
Enplas’s expertise in precision cam tool building and injection molding allows provision of high-quality plastic cams in mass volumes.
source_title: Gears and Mechanical Parts - Enplas Global

facts: EVCO Plastics is a globally recognized leader in custom plastic injection molding, providing comprehensive solutions from concept to production.
The company has a rich history dating back to 1964 and operates as a family-owned business founded by Don Evans.
EVCO specializes in innovative design, engineering, advanced tooling, and high-quality manufacturing of plastic components.
EVCO serves a diverse range of industries, including medical, automotive, packaging, and consumer goods.
The company operates multiple state-of-the-art facilities in the United States, Mexico, and China to meet global demand.
The corporate headquarters and Advanced Technology Center house administrative, engineering, R&D, and advanced manufacturing operations.
The Advanced Technology Center is ISO 9001 and IATF 16949 certified, showcasing advanced molding technologies and automation.
EVCO emphasizes innovation, precision, customer collaboration, and continuous improvement, fostering a team-oriented environment focused on employee development and technical excellence.
The headquarters serves as the central hub for strategic decision-making, engineering innovation, new technology adoption, and coordination of global operations.
EVCO supports its global customer base through strategically located manufacturing facilities in the United States (Wisconsin, Georgia), Mexico (Juarez), and China (Dongguan).
These facilities provide comprehensive services including custom plastic injection molding, advanced tooling, engineering support, assembly, and global supply chain management.
EVCO Plastics operates a facility in Calhoun, Georgia, located at 101 EVCO Drive, Calhoun, GA 30701, USA.
The Calhoun, Georgia facility caters to the manufacturing needs of clients in the Southeast, offering logistical advantages and regional support.
In May 2023, custom injection molder EVCO Plastics Inc. invested $3 million in new automation and a 30,000-square-foot expansion at its manufacturing facility in Calhoun, Ga.
The Calhoun, Georgia expansion aims to enhance production capacity and efficiency.
As of April 2025, EVCO Plastics' leadership includes current executives, with no major executive new hires or exits widely reported in the last 12 months due to its privately held status.
EVCO Plastics marks its 60th anniversary in 2024, celebrating six decades of leadership, innovation, and growth in the custom plastic injection molding industry.
The company has been backed by several prominent investors over the years.
source_title: Where is EVCO Plastics Located? HQ, Global Offices & Company Insights

facts: EVCO Plastics was founded in 1964 by Donald Evans with a half-ounce injection press in a basement.
EVCO Plastics is now a $130 million company.
The company operates 148 injection machines and employs 900 people across nine plants in the U.S., Mexico, and China.
Donald Evans, age 87, serves as chairman of EVCO Plastics.
Dale Evans, Don Evans' son, is the company president.
Don Evans has two other sons who work at the company.
Anna Bartz, Dale Evans' daughter, serves as marketing director.
Spencer Wright is the Oshkosh plant manager; his father previously held that position.
EVCO Plastics has pursued an aggressive expansion program, averaging a major addition about every three years, including during the 2001 and 2009 recessions.
The original 1,200 ft² headquarters plant in DeForest, Wis., has grown to three facilities totaling 156,000 ft².
EVCO Plastics operates a 100,000 ft² facility in Oshkosh, Wis.
EVCO Plastics operates a 70,000 ft² facility in Calhoun, Ga.
EVCO Plastics operates 188,000 ft² across three plants in Monterrey and Juarez, Mexico.
EVCO Plastics operates a 73,000 ft² facility in Shenzhen, China.
EVCO started globalizing with a Chinese moldmaking joint venture in 1989 and a molding plant in 2000.
The Chinese moldmaking and molding operations were combined into a wholly owned molding and moldmaking operation in 2011.
EVCO Plastics de Mexico opened three plants in 2001, 2005, and 2007.
Dale Evans states the company quotes the same jobs for production in the U.S., China, and Mexico to give customers a choice.
The company's philosophy is to locate relatively small plants with 15 to 20 machines close to customers.
Some customers have parts molded in both the U.S., Mexico, and China.
China tends to have the lowest price but longest lead times.
As China raises taxes and labor rates, Mexico becomes increasingly competitive for some jobs that start out in China.
EVCO targets three primary markets: packaging, medical parts, and large parts.
Packaging is a target market due to customers' need for quick time to market and the cost of shipping air in hollow parts.
Medical parts are targeted because the market is heavily regulated by the FDA.
EVCO MED (Medical & Electronic Devices) started in 1996.
EVCO MED has 15 injection presses, all under 200 tons, located in a 34,000 ft² Class 100,000 clean room.
EVCO plans to more than double its medical facility size with a 50,000 ft² clean-room addition next year.
EVCO's Shenzhen plant has a 1,500-ton press capable of molding sizeable parts.
EVCO also has presses up to 3,000 tons in Mexico.
EVCO is qualifying a medical clean room at the Shenzhen plant to Class 100,000 standards.
EVCO plans to move one medical molding and assembly cell from DeForest to Shenzhen.
EVCO plans to seek ISO 13485 medical quality certification for its Shenzhen plant.
EVCO states that 70% of a part's cost is determined by the design.
EVCO emphasizes early involvement in project development and has ample design resources at its technical center in DeForest.
The firm uses over 100 materials, primarily engineering resins like PPS, PC, ABS, PC/ABS, nylon, TPE, and acrylic, as well as PP, PE, and PS.
EVCO prides itself on expertise in metal-to-plastic conversions.
One conversion example is a foot well for construction equipment converted from metal to 23 lb of glass-filled nylon.

Another example is a rotary debris-removal filter for agricultural equipment originally requiring an assembly of 15 metal and plastic parts and 144 operations, now reduced to four parts and one operation.
A robot loads two halves of a 52-in.-diam. metal screen and a metal hub into a mold where they are overmolded and bonded together by glass-filled PP.
EVCO uses rapid-prototyping technologies through outside service bureaus and its own 3D printer at the tech center.
The company's 3D printer is a Projet3500 HD Max from 3D Systems that uses inkjet technology to deposit droplets of UV-curable liquid polymer.
EVCO builds 30% of its own molds in Wisconsin and Georgia, plus another 20-30% in China.
Dale Evans states the company controls the steel to ensure good quality.
EVCO can build a wide variety of injection molds for large and small parts up to 300 cavities.
The company builds hot-runner, insert, two-shot, gas-assist, thin-wall, and family molding molds, as well as stack molds.
EVCO can build quick-turn prototype molds from aluminum using the same steel, gating, and side actions as multi-cavity production molds.
Prototype molds are instrumented with cavity-pressure sensors and engineered with Scientific Molding techniques for scalability.
More than a dozen EVCO employees have received training in Scientific Molding from RJG Inc. in Traverse City, Mich.
EVCO has at least one mobile RJG monitoring system at every plant that can be moved from press to press for mold setup and qualification.
The company incorporates provisions for sensors on every mold rather than putting cavity-pressure sensors on every mold.
In some cases, EVCO uses in-mold temperature sensors to set up tools for glass-filled materials requiring a smooth resin-rich surface.
EVCO keeps an eye on processes with Syscon-PlantStar monitors on every machine in North America.
Dale Evans can monitor production from his office desk, including remotely checking the Georgia plant's machine status.
The Syscon-PlantStar system is used for scheduling and monitoring cycle times, rejects, downtime, job completion, utilities (amperage, water temperatures), and chiller/compressor alarms.
The system sends emails to appropriate personnel if a problem arises and allows maintenance staff to log in to check status.
Operators can use the touchscreen to page supervisors, material handlers, or quality inspectors.
At Oshkosh, the system is currently monitoring part weight, with plans to upgrade this year to include fill time, cavity pressure, and mold temperature monitoring.
EVCO is certified to ISO 9001 at six plants in the U.S., China, and Mexico.
EVCO is certified to the ISO 13485 medical quality standard at EVCO MED.
EVCO is certified to the ISO/TS 16949 automotive quality standard at DeForest and Mexico.
Dale Evans notes that in China, the company had its first external reject in six years.
EVCO has 10 coordinate measuring machines (CMMs), leak and pressure testing equipment, and instruments for measuring melt flow, volume, tensile properties, gloss, color, and ash content.
Every plant has a color spectrophotometer to maintain precisely matched molded-in color.
All QA people undergo yearly testing and training to determine their ability to see color differences caused by varying heat histories on different machines.
At the Oshkosh plant, robots drop parts onto weigh scales sensitive to 0.01 lb to check part weight as a key quality variable.
EVCO has more than 104 robots, including three-axis Cartesian types, six-axis articulated units, SCARA pick-and-place units, and high-speed side-entry models.
Dale Evans built EVCO AMP (Automated Molding Plant) in DeForest in 1989 to compete with Japanese plants.
A fully automated cell at EVCO AMP uses a Husky Hylectric hybrid press and CBW Automation robotics to mold Redbox DVD cases with in-mold labeling.
Another machine molds small round containers in a 48-cavity mold, with a six-axis robot grabbing 64 at a time for layer packing.
At EVCO MED, a SCARA robot picks up 16 cannulas, submits them to a laser scan, and loads them into a rotary-table vertical press for overmolding with PS.

Another cell with 26 automated stations produces a testing device that enters an enclosed assembly system after molding, where feeders add o-ring seals and apply adhesive labels without human handling.
A third cell produces an electronic water control valve of PPS converted from stainless steel, using a robot to load six metal inserts of brass and stainless steel.
After molding, parts pass through a cooling conveyor to automated inspection stations for optical flash checks and pressurized air gauge orifice diameter checks.
Dale Evans states that exact tolerances cannot be achieved with hand labor in China, necessitating automation.
EVCO uses its 3D printer to build fixtures for automation cells and robot end-of-arm tooling (EOAT).
Plastic EOAT is lighter than metal, allows faster robot movement, channels air/vacuum without tubing, prevents mold damage during crashes, and can be built quickly and inexpensively.
EVCO offers capabilities for product design, material selection, mold design, mold building, prototyping, and full contract manufacturing including assembly and decorating.
Multicomponent molding example: EVCO made toothbrush handles by molding a rigid body, pad printing, then overmolding handles with two TPE materials in a second tool.
Insert molding example: A large PC part for an ice machine inner door panel was molded on a 2,000-ton press with a robot loading 39 brass inserts at once.
Another door assembly part was molded with 31 inserts.
IMD/IML is a core competency available at U.S. and Chinese plants for packaging, medical devices, warning labels, and decoration on large parts.
A glass-filled PET frozen-food tray application uses a four-sided in-mold label that provides an oxygen barrier and is selectively metalized for microwave reheating.
A high-speed side-entry robot with a linear motor inserts the label and removes the box in under 9 seconds.
EVCO is building its third cell for the frozen-food tray job.
EVCO has 23 presses from 1,000 to 3,500 tons at seven plants in DeForest, Oshkosh, Calhoun, Mexico, and China.
Large presses are used for appliances, agricultural equipment, off-road vehicles, outboard marine motor covers, riding lawnmower hoods, pallets, and more.
The stable of big machines includes several Husky presses (1,000, 1,500, and 3,000 tons), a 750-ton Toyo press, and an Engel 3,500-tonner that arrived at Oshkosh last April.
A 2,500-ton Engel is coming in June.
The Engel 3,500-tonner features a robot with 300-lb capacity that travels the length of the machine to save floorspace.
Magnetic platens are standard on big machines at Oshkosh for faster and safer mold mounting.
Mold changes on a 3,300-ton press using quick-connects can be accomplished in as little as 1 hour.
EVCO believes in continuing education and cycles people through its tech center to improve process understanding.
The company sent eight people to the K 2013 show in Dusseldorf in October.
EVCO has sent as many as 120 staffers to NPE shows.
Dale Evans' philosophy is to keep learning, stating, "Today is the dumbest I'm ever going to be."
source_title: EVCO Plastics at 50: Still Thinking Big | Plastics Technology

facts: Ken Stewart, commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, called fiscal year 2007 "a significant year," noting that 281 projects were announced domestically and internationally.
The Georgia economy was projected to grow at 2.4 percent, which is higher than the national average.
Kia auto assembly facility in West Point was announced with a commitment to hire 2,500 workers and begin operations next year.
A number of Tier-One suppliers have chosen to locate in Georgia following the Kia announcement.
Korea-based Sewon Precision is locating its first U.S. plant in LaGrange, creating 700 jobs to manufacture stamped chassis, body components, and decorative trim pieces for the Kia plant.
Kia supplier Sejong Georgia LLC anticipates 250 jobs at its new LaGrange muffler and exhaust systems manufacturing facility, planned to begin production in October 2009.
Canada is Georgia's largest trading partner, with $4.7 billion of exports.

At the end of 2007, Governor Sonny Perdue signed a declaration to charter the SEUS (South East United States)-Canadian Provinces Alliance to promote trade and tourism between Canada and the southeastern states.
The SEUS-Japan alliance, started in 1975, has been a factor in attracting more than 300 Japanese companies currently operating in Georgia.
China is Georgia's second-largest trading partner.
In April, the governor opened the Beijing Georgia Business Advisory Center in Beijing to encourage foreign direct investment and find new export markets and tourism opportunities.
Point-of-sale manufacturer Pax Technology Ltd. of China announced an Atlanta venue for its U.S. headquarters, sales, and support center.
General Protecht announced a Barnesville assembly and distribution facility for electrical devices in 2007.
Sany Heavy Industry Company, Ltd. established its U.S. headquarters and assembly center in Peachtree City.
Transformer company Group EFACEC of Portugal is creating 600 jobs in Georgia.
Compressed air technology firm BEKO of Germany is adding 35 jobs in Georgia.
Wipro of India is establishing a software development center expected to hire 500 people over the next three years.
source_title: Georgia Attracts Foreign Direct Investment for Automotive, Life Sciences - Area Development

entity_name: Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) International Trade Division
facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s International Trade division earned a President’s “E Star” Award for Export Service from the U.S. Department of Commerce on October 23, 2020.
The award recognizes continuing significant contributions to an increase in U.S. exports and is the highest honor given to providers of services to U.S. exporters.
GDEcD is the first entity in the nation to earn a fourth “E” Award for export services.
The division first earned the President’s “E” Award for Exports in 1975, with subsequent awards received in 2007, 2014, and 2020.
U.S. Secretary of Commerce Wilbur Ross called earning a fourth award “a feat not achieved by any other entity in the 59-year history of the program.”
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia’s International Trade team provides personalized support to small and medium-sized businesses to help them grow in global markets.
Georgia was ranked the Top State for Business for seven years running as of October 2020.
GDEcD achieved more than 1,170 client trade successes since receiving the President’s “E Star” Award in 2014.
These export achievements have contributed to national export expansion efforts that support the U.S. economy and create American jobs.

GDEcD supports export development through representation in 12 strategic international markets: Brazil, Canada, Chile, China, Colombia, Europe, Israel, Japan, Korea, Mexico, Peru, and the United Kingdom.
Approximately two-thirds of Georgia trade involves markets where the state has representation.
Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the recognition testifies to Georgia’s role in the global economy, the quality of its goods and services, and the commitment of its in-state and abroad teams.
In September 2020, GDEcD’s International Trade division was awarded a grant from the U.S. Small Business Administration to bolster export assistance for small businesses statewide.
The Go Global Georgia initiative will help small business exporters stretch international marketing budgets and create new global opportunities.
Georgia’s logistics network is anchored by two deepwater ports, inland ports, road, and rail, providing exporters with a direct connection to the world.

On October 22, 2020, the Georgia Ports Authority reported that the Port of Savannah handled an increase of 1.6% in twenty-foot equivalent container units (TEUs) during the first quarter of fiscal year 2021, which began on July 1.
The Port of Savannah is the top export gateway and fastest-growing container port in the U.S., providing cost-effective connections for Georgia exporters.

GDEcD serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging industry expansion, locating markets for Georgia products, promoting tourism, and planning economic development resources.

links.context: Go Global Georgia eligibility and application guidelines

source_title: GA is first state to receive four Export Services Awards

facts: Flambeau Inc. has invested significantly in its operations recently due to surges in demand for plastic parts.
Flambeau added new presses at three of its nine North American plants: Columbus, Indiana; Middlefield, Ohio; and Madison, Georgia.
The press additions represent a company-wide expansion of capabilities for Flambeau’s manufacturing capacity.
A worldwide economic slowdown last year due to the Covid-19 pandemic led to a rapidly increasing company-wide backlog of orders for Flambeau.
Just months into the slowdown, Flambeau’s supply chain began to reach its limits, and demand for vital parts began to overwhelm supply.
In Columbus, a new 750-ton press is primarily dedicated to specific proprietary products, including Flambeau Brute tool boxes, gun cases, and tackle boxes.
In Madison, new 200-ton and 900-ton presses are utilized to increase production of a variety of stock and custom products for Flambeau’s fluids and packaging divisions.
In Middlefield, three new presses ranging from 330- to 730-ton are primarily dedicated to manufacturing stock boxes and cases for Flambeau Outdoors, ArtBin and other packaging lines of products.

Flambeau implemented several creative solutions to reduce backlog, such as 24-hour operations and new production scheduling methodology to improve forecasting practices and address various constraints on production.

Along with further expansion of injection and blow molding at its several plants, Flambeau is implementing a modern, company-wide ERP system that will continually improve customer support and the company’s standing as a supply chain system.
source_title: Flambeau Inc. Invests in Operations | Medical Product Outsourcing

facts: Scrapped plans to build a US$2.6 billion battery factory in Newnan, Georgia.
Opted instead to focus on solar panel manufacturing in Texas.
Initially announced the Georgia project in 2023.
Informed local officials last week that it would not proceed with construction.
The facility was expected to hire over 700 workers.
The facility was expected to manufacture batteries for storing renewable energy.
Never broke ground on the 368-acre (149-hectare) site.
Is a Norwegian-founded startup.
Moved its headquarters to the U.S. to maximize tax benefits from President Joe Biden's climate law.
Purchased a $340 million solar panel factory in Texas from China's Trina Solar last year.
Spokesperson Amy Jaick expressed gratitude for the support and partnership found in Coweta County and throughout Georgia.
Stated that the company is focusing at the moment on the solar module manufacturing facility in Texas.
Decision to pivot was influenced by rising interest rates, declining battery prices, leadership changes, and a shift in company goals.
Coweta County Development Authority President Sarah Jacobs provided the reasons for the pivot.
Georgia Department of Economic Development previously granted $7 million to assist Freyr in securing land for the project.
Department spokesperson Jessica Atwell stated that Georgia and Freyr are working together to ensure the grant funds are repaid quickly.
The company may also owe additional funds to Coweta County.
Stated that Georgia's incentives process protects the Georgia taxpayer, and when a company's plans change, that process ensures discretionary incentives are repaid.
Initially planned to build battery factories in Norway and Finland.
In November, announced plans to sell its European business.
Terminated its battery technology license, paying $3 million in settlement fees.
Former CEO Tom Einar Jensen previously warned investors that Chinese manufacturers were producing batteries at lower costs, making it difficult for startups to secure funding.
Company's cash reserves fell from $253 million at the end of 2023 to $182 million by September 30.
Georgia remains a significant hub for EV and battery manufacturing despite Freyr's withdrawal.
SK Innovation invested $2.6 billion in a battery plant in Commerce, though it later furloughed some workers.
Hyundai Motor Group has begun production at its $7.6 billion electric vehicle and battery facility near Savannah and expects to hire 8,500 workers.
Electric truck maker Rivian revived its $5 billion factory project near Atlanta after securing a $6.6 billion federal loan.
State officials remain confident in Georgia's position as a leader in clean energy manufacturing despite Freyr's exit.
source_title: Battery firm abandons plan for a $2.6 billion plant in Georgia

facts: Norway-based FREYR Battery acquired a site in Coweta County, Georgia, for its planned $1.7 billion Giga America battery plant.
The company plans to launch detailed project engineering in the coming months.
The first phase is planned to be a cell production module with around 34 GWh of capacity.
The plant's production will be based on the next-generation of 24M’s U.S.-based SemiSolid production platform.
FREYR is evaluating other cell production lines that could bring total capital investments to more than $2.6 billion through 2029.

Co-founder and CEO Tom Einar Jensen stated that the recent passage of the Inflation Reduction Act raised expectations that U.S. demand for ESS, passenger EV, and other electric mobility applications will “grow rapidly over the next decade.”
The SemiSolid electrode technology was invented in the MIT lab of Dr. Yet-Ming Chiang.
The technology mixes electrolyte with active materials to form a clay-like slurry.
The resulting slurry enables the manufacture of thick electrodes with less volume, mass, and cost while enabling a simpler manufacturing process.
In September, 24M licensed its platform to Fujifilm, which agreed to invest $20 million in the company.
The partnership between 24M and Fujifilm combines the 24M manufacturing platform with Fujifilm’s precision coating and production technologies.
This partnership is expected to enable mass production of large-area SemiSolid batteries with high energy density.
The total package of financial incentives for Giga America includes assistance provided by the state of Georgia and Coweta County.
The incentive package also includes expected federal production tax credits.
FREYR plans to seek a Department of Energy grant and/or direct loans to help with the plant’s development.
According to Rystad Energy, annual collective storage demand in the U.S. and Europe is expected to reach 1.4 TWh by 2030, a 34-fold increase from 2022 demand.
source_title: FREYR Battery picks Georgia site for $1.7 billion cell plant

facts: Freyr Battery has announced its rebranding as T1 Energy Inc., marking a shift away from battery manufacturing to solar PV production and storage.
The company cancelled its $2.6 billion Georgia battery gigafactory plans.
T1 Energy will trade under the ticker “TE” on the NYSE starting March 3, 2025.
The abandonment of the Georgia battery factory plans follows the company's previous aims of 200 GWh of battery production by 2030.
The company is selling the site for the abandoned Georgia battery factory.
In late 2024, T1 Energy acquired a 5 GW solar cell facility in the U.S. from Trina Solar for $340 million.
T1 Energy continues to advance a battery facility in Norway following previous cutbacks to its size and scope.
The company has not provided significant new information on the Giga Arctic project beyond its October investor presentation.

In the October investor presentation, the company detailed that it has continued to advance value optimization with leading strategic, industrial, and financial stakeholders based on less capital-intensive business models.

These value optimization efforts include high-value adjacencies to the battery value chain such as digital battery platforms and data center opportunities for Giga Arctic balanced by Battery Energy Storage Solutions.
Multiple industry moves highlight difficulties in entering the competitive battery manufacturing sector while competing with China's rapidly growing sector.
According to Benchmark Mineral Intelligence, 2024 saw record global battery gigafactory cancellations exceeding 312 GWh.
T1 Energy's new operational focus centers on its solar module facility in Wilmer, Texas, dubbed “G1 Dallas,” which employs over 1,000 people.
The company is planning a second U.S. solar cell manufacturing facility, “G2,” with site selection expected in Q1 2025.
T1 Energy will have its headquarters in Austin, Texas.
The company is positioning itself to leverage U.S. tax benefits under the Inflation Reduction Act while focusing on domestic solar manufacturing capacity.
Daniel Barcelo serves as T1’s chairman of the board and CEO.
Daniel Barcelo stated that the rebirth as T1 Energy is about American energy, jobs, and advanced manufacturing, emphasizing the need for domestic supply chains to harness abundant solar resources.
Barcelo noted that more than 80% of new U.S. electricity capacity in 2024 was solar and batteries.

T1 Energy plans to lead in the solar and battery markets by enabling energy supply growth to support AI, data centers, reshoring, and electrification to restore American industrial capacity and leadership.
source_title: Freyr Battery rebrands as T1 Energy - Energy Storage

facts: FREYR Battery withdrew plans to establish a nearly $2.6 billion battery manufacturing plant in Coweta County, Georgia.
The abandoned project was forecast to generate more than 700 jobs in the region.
FREYR Battery sent a letter to the Coweta County Development Authority in January detailing the withdrawal.
The company stated the decision was necessary to realign its near-term strategic goals.
FREYR Battery noted that the Coweta County Development Authority, Coweta County, and the State of Georgia provided extraordinary assistance and support.
Shifting market forces and internal changes influenced the cancellation.
Contributing factors to the cancellation included rising interest rates, declining battery prices, and corporate leadership adjustments.
The cancellation is a setback for Coweta County's economic expansion prospects.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development spokesperson stated the state's incentive arrangement is designed to protect taxpayers.
Efforts are underway to recuperate funds promptly allotted for the project's inception.
FREYR Battery is currently focusing on a solar module manufacturing facility in Texas.
The Coweta County project was initially announced in 2022.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp had previously heralded the project as a major boost to Georgia's sustainable technology ecosystem.
The facility was intended to produce lithium-ion battery cells for vehicles and energy storage solutions.
Coweta County officials stated that the incentives were structured with safeguards to protect taxpayers.
Coweta County officials noted that the property will remain valuable for creating jobs and investment to benefit the community.
source_title: FREYR Battery Halts Plans for $2.6B Georgia Plant, Coweta County's Job

facts: Norway-based Freyr Battery SA assembled and successfully charged its first semi-automated lithium-ion battery unit cells at a Norwegian facility.
CEO Tom Einar Jensen said the successful tests pave the way for the company to ramp up process automation, battery cell production, and testing of cells it produced.
The company anticipates supplying customers with samples in the second half of this year.
Jensen noted an “increasingly robust pipeline of customer opportunities and use cases for our products.”
Freyr presented its first “capital markets” day at the New York Stock Exchange as a pitch to attract further investment.
Jensen stated the company is moving toward a totally automated process, which will increase production speed and yields.
Battery applications include transportation, heating, and industrial processes, which are in the process of moving from fossil fuel-based systems to electric-powered systems.
The company has yet to show any revenue, as its operations are financed through post-IPO offerings, additional rounds of private equity, and grants from Norwegian governmental entities.
In his NYSE presentation, Jensen mentioned the company’s planned U.S. gigafactory that will be built in Coweta County, Georgia.
Batteries produced at the U.S. site will qualify for subsidies under the Inflation Reduction Act.
The company is also exploring a potential gigafactory site in Vaasa, Finland.
Partner companies revealed include investor and miner Glencore plc (financial backer), Nidec Corp., Siemens AG, and Caterpillar Inc.
The company ended the first quarter with $475 million in cash.

2023 capital expenditures are focused on three main projects: its customer qualification plant in Norway, the Georgia Giga America plant, and maintaining progress at its Giga Arctic facility in Norway.
The Georgia facility will be the company’s first full-scale battery manufacturing facility.
Freyr went public in February 2021 via a SPAC merger.
source_title: Freyr Battery Powers Up After Successful Tests, NYSE Presentation — TradingView News

facts: Co-Founder Tom Jensen will return to the Chief Executive Officer role.
Current Director Daniel Barcelo has been appointed Chair of the Board.
Evan Calio, a recognized financial expert in the energy sector, has been appointed as the Company’s new CFO.
Birger Steen stepped down from his CEO and Board positions at FREYR and is leaving the Board effective immediately.
Oscar Brown notified the Company of his intention to resign as CFO for personal reasons.
Both departing executives will remain with the Company temporarily to ensure seamless transitions.
The leadership changes support FREYR’s accelerated strategic shift to the U.S. market and key initiatives to drive revenues and profitability.
The Board believes the new management team is aligned with pursuing organic and inorganic opportunities in the U.S. to accelerate timelines to revenues and EBITDA.
The company plans to advance its next-generation 24M SemiSolidTM technology through operations at the Customer Qualification Plant (CQP).
FREYR intends to finalize a conventional technology agreement with an established cell producer.

Tom Jensen stated that the company is in the final stages of finalizing an accretive partnership with a well-established battery producer globally to provide cost-competitive products to its large customer base.

Todd Kantor, a Director and early investor who joined the Board last month, expressed confidence that the new team will translate FREYR’s technology into value-enhancing partnership transactions in the near term.
Kantor noted that Encompass Capital Advisors LLC first invested in FREYR in 2020.
Evan Calio has more than 30 years of energy experience on Wall Street, including working with Freyr management on its IPO and $704 million capital raise.
Calio has led more than $60 billion in capital raises and executed over $75 billion of M&A transactions.

Prior to his current role at BTIG, Calio spent 14 years at Morgan Stanley as a Managing Director within Equity Research covering U.S. exploration and production companies, refiners, and integrated oils.
FREYR Battery is operating its Customer Qualification Plant (CQP) for technology development in Mo i Rana, Norway.
The company is commencing development of the Giga America battery manufacturing project in Coweta County, Georgia, in the U.S.
FREYR seeks to serve primary markets of energy storage systems (ESS) and commercial mobility, with an ambition to serve the passenger electric vehicles market.
The Company’s mission is to accelerate the decarbonization of global energy and transportation systems by producing sustainable, cost-competitive batteries.

facts: FREYR Battery will invest $2.57 billion into Georgia’s sustainable technology ecosystem.
FREYR Battery will create 723 new jobs over the next seven years.
The investment and job creation are for a manufacturing facility in Coweta County.
Governor Brian Kemp welcomed FREYR to Georgia, citing the state's No. 1 ranking for business.
FREYR is dedicated to supporting a domestic supply chain for renewable power sources.
FREYR's battery cells can be used for stationary energy storage (ESS), electric mobility, and additional applications.
FREYR Co-Founder and CEO Tom Einar Jensen stated the company is committed to decarbonizing transportation and energy sectors with U.S. partners.
Tom Einar Jensen noted that as FREYR advances its U.S. expansion plan, it expects to make meaningful investments to spur job creation and develop localized, decarbonized supply chains in the U.S.
FREYR's new facility will be located at the Bridgeport Industrial Park site.

FREYR will hire technicians, operators, engineers, executives, and administrative staff.

The facility will utilize the U.S.-based 24M Technologies SemiSolidTM platform to simplify production and increase efficiency.
FREYR is currently evaluating clean power supply solutions for the new facility, including potential renewable energy from solar installations.
Coweta County Development Authority Chair John Daviston welcomed FREYR Battery to the community.
John Daviston stated the venture will bring quality, highly-skilled jobs and provide a significant stimulus to the local economy.
Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick welcomed FREYR Battery to metro Atlanta, noting the region's rapid growth as a hub for battery cell research and production.
Katie Kirkpatrick stated FREYR will benefit from the region's vibrant research ecosystem and diverse talent pool.
Project Manager John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Coweta County Development Authority, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that companies like FREYR are driving a global revolution based on sustainable technology and solutions.

Pat Wilson noted that electrification is changing how cities are powered and that Georgia is developing a battery ecosystem with manufacturers, recyclers, and customers to support home and commercial storage.
Georgia's prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate attract diverse industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain to help companies increase efficiencies and reduce reliance on imported materials.
FREYR Battery aims to provide industrial-scale clean battery solutions to reduce global emissions.
FREYR Battery is listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
FREYR's mission is to produce green battery cells to accelerate the decarbonization of energy and transportation systems globally.
FREYR has commenced construction on its first factory in Mo i Rana, Norway.
FREYR has announced potential industrial-scale battery cell production developments in Vaasa, Finland, and the United States.
FREYR intends to install 50 GWh of battery cell capacity by 2025.
FREYR plans to reach 100 GWh of annual capacity by 2028.
FREYR targets 200 GWh of annual capacity by 2030.

facts: Freyr Battery is a U.S.-based battery manufacturer.
Freyr agreed to acquire Trina Solar's U.S. solar manufacturing assets for $340 million.
The acquired assets include a 5 GW, 1.35 million square foot solar module manufacturing facility in Wilmer, Texas.
The Texas facility started production in November 2024.
The Texas facility is expected to ramp up to full production in 2025.
Thirty percent of the Texas facility's capacity is already contracted with U.S. customers.
This acquisition is the first step in Freyr's plan to build out a vertically integrated domestic manufacturing footprint.
The next phase of the footprint will include a 5 GW solar cell facility.
Freyr is currently selecting a site for the new solar cell facility.
Construction on the solar cell facility is targeted for Q2 2025.
First Freyr cells are anticipated to roll off the line in the second half of 2026.
The buildout plans include creating up to 1,800 direct jobs.
The expansion will provide domestic content for the U.S. solar industry.
Daniel Barcelo is Freyr's newly appointed chief executive officer.
Tom Einar Jensen, Freyr's co-founder, will assume the role of CEO of Freyr Europe to oversee optimization and monetization of the European portfolio.
Since going public in July 2021, Freyr set up a giga-factory in Georgia with a $1.7 billion initial capital investment.
Plans for the Georgia facility include producing 34 GWh of batteries in 2025.
Production at the Georgia facility is planned to scale up to 100 GWh by 2028.
The Georgia facility will bring more than 720 jobs.
Investment in the Georgia battery facility is projected to exceed $2.6 billion through 2029.
Freyr anticipates closing the transaction at the end of 2024.
Solar module production at the Texas facility is expected to begin in Q4 2024.
Freyr initiated 2025 EBITDA guidance of $75 to $125 million.
Freyr expects to exit 2025 at a full-year run rate EBITDA of $175 to $225 million.

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. operates a manufacturing plant in Social Circle, Georgia.
The Georgia plant faces about $70,000 in fines for failing to follow federal workplace safety laws that endangered workers.
Federal OSHA investigators imposed seven serious citations against the company in January after inspecting its Social Circle plant last year.
Federal officials found the company failed to provide proper protective gear to workers processing rubber through hot metal presses with temperatures exceeding 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Investigators discovered hazards from unguarded machines, rainwater and leaking equipment that put workers at risk of slipping on the production floor.
William Fulcher, the OSHA area office director in Atlanta, stated that the inspection found multiple safety deficiencies putting employees at risk of serious injury or death.
Goodyear has contested the proposed fines.
Laura Duda, a Goodyear spokeswoman, stated the company cooperated with OSHA during its inspection and is following the standard administrative process for responding to citations.
A Reveal investigation from The Center Investigative Reporting in December showed how Goodyear’s lax approach to safety contributed to the deaths of motorists on the road and workers in its plants.
Goodyear ranked among the top five manufacturers in the United States for worker deaths since 2009, according to Reveal’s analysis.
At least four motorists over the last seven years have died in accidents after tires made at Goodyear plants failed.
Tires were manufactured in plants where intense production demands and leaks in the roof endangered both workers and consumers.
Court documents and former workers in Goodyear’s plants in Danville, Virginia, and Fayetteville, North Carolina, detailed how water leaked through the roof and onto machines.
Workers are trained to avoid moisture in tire production because it can prompt treads to separate, causing a blowout.
Former employees of Goodyear’s Danville and Fayetteville plants said they felt pressure to put production before workplace safety.
Some former employees recalled a quota-driven motto invoked on the shop floor: “Round and black and out the back.”
Hours after Reveal’s investigation was published, a Goodyear spokeswoman issued a statement regarding the findings.

Barbara Hatala, Goodyear’s communications manager for Americas operations, stated that over the past two years the company fell short of its own expectations for safety and mourned the loss of valued coworkers at two U.S. manufacturing plants.

The story detailed how protections for factory workers are being dismantled under the Trump administration, which rolled back and postponed Obama-era protections in keeping with goals laid out by the National Association of Manufacturers.
Richard Kramer, Goodyear’s chairman, chief executive officer and president, serves on the National Association of Manufacturers’ board.
Since October 2008, Goodyear has been fined more than $1.9 million for more than 200 health and workplace safety violations, far more than its four major competitors combined.
Besides the most recent proposed OSHA fines of $69,058, Goodyear’s plant in Social Circle, Georgia, has been previously cited for three workplace safety violations, two of them serious.
Jordan Barab, former deputy assistant secretary of OSHA under President Barack Obama, called the most recent fines “deeply discouraging.”

Barab stated that even with multiple preventable deaths and serious injuries in plants across the country and multiple OSHA citations, Goodyear never seems to learn that they are legally and morally required to provide safe workplaces for their employees.
source_title: OSHA: Georgia Goodyear plant lapses put employees at serious risk - Reveal

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company has entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Off-the-Road (OTR) tire business to The Yokohama Rubber Company, Limited for $905 million in cash.
The sale is part of Goodyear's broader Goodyear Forward transformation plan.
Yokohama will acquire Goodyear’s OTR tire segment, which provides high-quality tires for mining, construction, and industrial markets globally.
Mark Stewart, Goodyear Chief Executive Officer and President, stated that the sale marks an important milestone in executing the Goodyear Forward transformation plan.
Goodyear will continue to supply OTR tires for U.S. military and defense applications following the transaction.
Through a Product Supply Agreement, Goodyear will manufacture certain OTR tires for Yokohama at specific facilities for up to five years post-transaction.
The transaction is pending regulatory and customary approvals.
The deal is expected to close by early 2025.
Proceeds from the sale will help Goodyear reduce debt and support initiatives under the Goodyear Forward transformation plan.
GT shares closed lower by 1.71% at $12.10 on Friday.
source_title: Goodyear Sells OTR Tire Business To Yokohama For $905M: Details - Goodyear Tire & Rubber (NASDAQ:GT) - Benzinga

facts: Goodyear and Gatik announced a multi-year collaboration to advance mobility solutions for autonomous B2B short-haul logistics.
The partnership aims to improve safety and sustainability in goods movement while reducing costs for Gatik's customers nationwide.
Goodyear Ventures participated in Gatik's Series B funding.
Joint projects will utilize Goodyear SightLine technology to monitor tire performance in real-time.
Gatik operates the first autonomous middle mile logistics network in North America.
Gatik is expanding its fleet of Class 3-6 autonomous box trucks across Texas, Arkansas, Louisiana, and Ontario.
Goodyear is one of the world's largest tire companies with over 120 years of experience developing technology and performance standards for the industry.
The collaboration expands service levels and capacity for both organizations across the U.S.
Gatik's medium-duty fleet will use tires equipped with tire intelligence technology powered by Goodyear SightLine.
The tire intelligence technology aims to improve stopping distances in autonomous commercial vehicles and monitor tire pressure in real time.

Use of tire intelligence solutions is expected to reduce fuel and maintenance costs while increasing operational efficiencies for retailers, e-commerce companies, and distributors employing Gatik's autonomous solution.

Chris Helsel, Goodyear's senior vice president, global operations and chief technology officer, stated that leveraging advanced tire intelligence solutions can improve vehicle performance and safety in autonomous transportation.
Arjun Narang, CTO and co-founder of Gatik, noted that the collaboration aligns with Gatik's mission to enhance safety, increase efficiency, and reduce costs.
Abhijit Ganguly, managing director of Goodyear Ventures, said Gatik's mission aligns with their investment strategy and includes key autonomous delivery players in long-haul and last-mile markets.
Goodyear employs about 72,000 people and manufactures products in 55 facilities across 23 countries.
Goodyear operates two Innovation Centers in Akron, Ohio, and Colmar-Berg, Luxembourg.
Gatik delivers goods using light and medium duty trucks for Fortune 500 retailers like Walmart and Loblaw.
Gatik was founded in 2017 by veterans of the autonomous technology industry and has offices in Palo Alto and Toronto.
Gatik was recognized on the 2021 Forbes AI 50 list and as a World Economic Forum Technology Pioneer.
source_title: Goodyear Invests In Autonomous Trucking Company Gatik And Collaborates To Enhance Safety And Efficiency For Autonomous Middle Mile Logistics | GT Stock News

facts: Great Dane LP, Savannah, Ga., has acquired a production facility in Scott County, Tenn.
The anticipated start-up date of the new facility is the first half of 2005.
Sam Gupta, recently promoted to senior vice president of manufacturing, will lead preparation of the plant for the production of Great Dane trailers.
Gupta has more than 26 years of truck trailer manufacturing experience, including 14 years as Great Dane’s vice president of manufacturing for the Van Division.
The Scott County facility, located in the northeastern part of Tennessee, was previously owned by Wabash National Corp.

facts: Great Dane Trailers is the No. 1 trailer manufacturer in the world.

Great Dane manufactures dry vans, refrigerated trailers, and platform trailers.
The company's corporate headquarters is located in Chicago, IL.
Great Dane maintains an additional corporate office in Savannah, GA.
The company operates a parts distribution center.
Great Dane has nine strategically located manufacturing plants throughout the U.S.
The company distributes trailers and aftermarket parts across North and South America through an extensive network of company-owned branches and full-line independent dealers.

Dean Engelage serves as President of Great Dane Trailers.
Rick Mullininx holds the title of Executive Vice President of Engineering at Great Dane Trailers.
Bob Brege holds the titles of Vice President and Vice President Procurement at Great Dane Trailers.
Tony Wiese works as a Plant Manager for Great Dane Trailers.
Brad Harvey also serves as a Plant Manager at Great Dane Trailers.

facts: Great Dane Trailers has begun building a new manufacturing plant in Statesboro, Ga.
The project represents an investment of $33.5 million by the company.
The project is expected to create more than 400 jobs over the next four years.
The new plant will produce single- and multitemp refrigerated trailers to support the food service industry.

The 450,000-sq.-ft. facility is located on a 118-acre site.

Great Dane Trailers produces dry vans and refrigerated and platform trailers.

facts: Great Dane Trailers broke ground on the site of its new refrigerated trailer manufacturing plant on Oct. 18.

Georgia Gov. Sonny Perdue, Statesboro Mayor Joe Brannen and Bulloch County Commission Chairman Garrett Nevil hailed cooperation among the state, local economic developers and Great Dane as key reasons for the success of the selection process.

facts: Great Dane Trailers announced the addition of Superstition Trailers to its North American distribution network.

facts: Georgia Southern University’s City Campus in Statesboro will house a FabLab business incubator for engineering entrepreneurs featuring state-of-the-art equipment such as 3-D printers, scanners, and CNC machines.

Statesboro Mayor Jan Moore oversaw the approval of a tax allocation district overlay to incentivize redevelopment for the Blue Mile project.
The Blue Mile is a grassroots revitalization effort focused on the mile-long South Main Street corridor stretching from Georgia Southern University’s main entrance to the county courthouse.
The South Main Street Revitalization Committee, led by Keely NeSmith Fennell and Darron Burnette, oversees the Blue Mile project which gained momentum in 2012.
New construction within the Blue Mile corridor must meet uniform design guidelines established for the area.
The Downtown Statesboro Development Authority is investing in residential redevelopment through its Homes for Heroes initiative to rehabilitate abandoned houses for public safety workers.

Statesboro was named one of 15 semi-finalists and the only Georgia community in America’s Best Communities competition sponsored by Frontier Communications, Dish Network, CoBank, and The Weather Channel.
Eight finalists in the America’s Best Communities competition will each receive $100,000 to launch projects, with the winning community announced in April 2017.

Statesboro implemented Right Start meetings through its planning and development office to provide potential business operators with coordinated views from engineering, fire, licensing, planning, and water/sewer departments.
The city partnered with Birmingham-based Retail Strategies to create a Retail Recruitment Strategic Plan to identify appropriate businesses for its retail mix.
A Krispy Kreme doughnut shop was recently permitted in Statesboro as part of ongoing retail development driven by the Georgia Southern University student population.
The multi-jurisdictional Statesboro-Bulloch County Crime Suppression Team was formed in January 2014 through a city and county task force.
Georgia Southern University contributed two campus police officers to the crime suppression team, covering all associated costs for officers and vehicles after Board of Regents approval.
Interim Georgia Southern University President Jean Bartels actively supports economic development partnerships between the university, city, and county.
South Georgia Tormenta FC was announced in fall 2015 as a new professional league soccer franchise that will join the Premier Development League in May 2016.
The team will play seven home games on a 14-match schedule for 2016, with its season opening May 13 and first home game May 21 at Georgia Southern University.
Darin Van Tassel, co-owner and president of South Georgia Tormenta FC, projects the team will generate a $5.5-million impact on the local economy.
source_title: Statesboro | Bulloch County: History Meets High-Tech - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Hall County's advanced manufacturing sector accounts for nearly 30% of local employment.
The Gainesville-Hall County area is home to more than 330 manufacturing and processing facilities.
The region hosts 60 international companies from 21 foreign countries.

Tim Evans, senior vice president of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce, stated that advanced manufacturing, healthcare, and education services provide diverse economic drivers that help withstand national or global recession headwinds.
The planned Northeast Georgia Inland Port received National Environmental Policy Act approval in May 2023.
Port activity currently supports 9,682 jobs in the Gainesville-Hall County area.
The Georgia Ports Authority anticipates the inland port rail hub will open after its 2026 completion with volumes of 60,000 containers per year.
The inland port will serve a key area for the production of heavy equipment, food, and forest products.
Griff Lynch, president and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, noted the inland port will deliver more economical supply chain alternatives and extend Georgia’s rail advantage.
The inland port project is expected to reduce approximately 36 million truck miles in the terminal’s first year of operation.
The inland port will be located in the heart of the I-85 manufacturing and logistics corridor with rail connections to Norfolk Southern and proximity to the National Highway Freight Network.

Hall County Commission Chair Richard Higgins stated the inland port will cement Hall County's legacy as a leading manufacturing hub for the state and region while providing jobs and an overall boost to the local economy.
The 1,300-acre Gainesville 85 Business Park contains 20 lots sized from 14 to 112 acres for industry and office use.
Car hauler manufacturer Cottrell purchased two lots in the Gainesville 85 Business Park before its April 2021 groundbreaking.
A sixth lot in the Gainesville 85 Business Park was pending sale in late summer 2023.
Bryan Lackey, city manager for Gainesville, stated the business park was developed with environmental design features including nature trails and existing youth sports facilities.
The Gainesville-Hall County Development Authority partnered with the city of Gainesville on the development of the Gainesville 85 Business Park.
Swiss firm medmix will start production in November 2023 at a new 300,000-square-foot facility in Flowery Branch.
The medmix facility will use high-tech injection molding and assembly to manufacture devices used during surgery and dental procedures, as well as EpiPen-like drug delivery systems.
James Kirk, president and site lead for medmix Healthcare U.S., stated the plant will expand through five phases over the next 10 years.
The medmix facility is expected to employ over 250 workers with competitive wages and climate-controlled environments.
Medmix cited proximity to Atlanta, Hartsfield-Jackson airport, the Port of Savannah, and the region’s interstate system as key logistics advantages for its U.S. expansion decision.
Flowery Branch offers cost savings on the cost of doing business compared to major metro areas.

Medmix has initiated conversations with area colleges including Kennesaw State University, Georgia Tech, and the University of Georgia regarding an injection molding European-style apprenticeship program to encourage trade skills in the region.
The area was recognized as a rapidly growing trades workforce region by medmix leadership.
source_title: Gainesville | Hall County: Crowd Favorite - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Hanon Systems is a Hankook Company.
Hanon Systems has declared a goal to be carbon neutral by 2040 and net-zero by 2050.

Hanon Systems supports the need to protect the planet for future generations and joins other companies in the worldwide effort to mitigate the effects of carbon dioxide (CO₂) emissions on the earth’s climate, resources and its people.
source_title: Hanon Systems | Automotive Thermal Solutions

facts: Hanon Systems is an automotive parts supplier.
Hanon Systems is investing $155 million to build a new facility in Woodbridge, Ontario.
The new facility will be 284,200 square feet.
The new facility will manufacture e-compressors, which are essential components for electric vehicles.
The project will create 300 jobs.
The investment aims to strengthen Ontario’s growing EV supply chain.
Vic Fedeli is the minister of economic development in Ontario.

Vic Fedeli stated that Ontario has all elements necessary to build future electric vehicles, including leading global parts manufacturers like Hanon Systems who are continuing to choose Ontario to expand and grow their operations.

Vic Fedeli noted that Hanon Systems' investment in Woodbridge will create hundreds of local jobs for auto workers and strengthen Ontario's reputation as a North American leader in automotive manufacturing.
Hanon Systems currently employs over 970 employees across its Belleville and Concord facilities.
Hanon Systems supplies automakers throughout North America.
The new Woodbridge facility will be North America’s first e-compressor plant.
The facility will have the capability to produce up to 900,000 e-compressors annually.
Ontario is providing Hanon Systems with $10 million through its Invest Ontario Fund in support of the investment.
Nurdal Kücükkaya is president and co-CEO of Hanon Systems.
Nurdal Kücükkaya stated that the company is pleased to expand its manufacturing presence in Ontario to increase proximity to vehicle manufacturers and better meet customer demand.
Nurdal Kücükkaya noted that the expansion demonstrates a commitment to growth and proves customer confidence in the ability to deliver electric compressors to the market.
Subu Nagasubramony is president and co-CEO of Hanon Systems.
Subu Nagasubramony stated that the company investigated several locations for the new plant.
Subu Nagasubramony noted that the partnership with the Government of Ontario and Invest Ontario was a significant element in the decision to locate the plant there.
source_title: Hanon Systems To Invest Over $155M In Ont. Facility - Clean Technology Canada

facts: Global automotive thermal and energy management solutions supplier Hanon Systems announced its board of directors has approved the appointment of Nurdal Kücükkaya and Ramachandran (Subu) Nagasubramony as co-chief executive officers with immediate effect.
Nagasubramony was appointed co-CEO along with a promotion to President, leading alongside President and co-CEO Nurdal Kücükkaya.
Kücükkaya has been serving as President and representative executive officer of Hanon Systems.
Nagasubramony served as the company's chief financial officer (CFO) since Jan. 1, 2019, and will retain his role as CFO.
Both executives have a long tenure serving in executive roles with the company and more than three decades of automotive industry experience.
Hanon Systems is a full-line supplier of automotive thermal and energy management solutions for electrified and conventional vehicles.
Its offering includes a wide range of solutions in heating ventilation and air conditioning, powertrain cooling, compressor, fluid transport, and electronics and fluid pressure.
The company currently operates 50 manufacturing sites and three regional innovation centers.
Hanon Systems employs more than 22,000 people across 21 countries.
source_title: Hanon Systems Appoints Nurdal Kücükkaya and Ramachandran (Subu)

facts: Hanon Systems is a leading global automotive thermal management supplier for electrified mobility.
Hanon Systems announced a new manufacturing plant in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada.
The new Woodbridge facility includes approximately 284,200 square feet (26,400 square meters) of manufacturing space.
The facility has room for future expansion based on business needs.
The Woodbridge plant will be Hanon Systems' first North American location to produce electric scroll compressors.
Electric scroll compressors are essential components for thermal management in hybrid and battery electric vehicles.
Depending on customer demand, the facility can produce up to 900,000 electric compressors annually.
At full production, the plant expects to employ nearly 300 hourly and salaried employees.
President and co-CEO Nurdal Kücükkaya stated the expansion increases proximity to vehicle manufacturers and meets customer demand.
Kücükkaya noted the expansion demonstrates commitment to growth and proves customer confidence in delivering electric compressors.
President and co-CEO Subu Nagasubramony stated the company investigated several locations for the new plant.
Nagasubramony cited partnership with the government of Ontario and investment development agency as a significant factor in the location decision.
The company expressed pride in fostering local community development and economic growth in Woodbridge.
Ontario Minister of Economic Development, Job Creation and Trade Vic Fedeli stated the province has all necessary elements to build future electric vehicles.
Fedeli highlighted that leading global parts manufacturers like Hanon Systems continue to choose Ontario for expansion.
Minister Vic Fedeli stated the investment will create 300 new local jobs for auto workers.
Fedeli noted the project strengthens Ontario's reputation as a North American leader in automotive manufacturing.
The new Canadian facility joins Hanon Systems' four existing electric compressor manufacturing operations in Pyeongtaek (Korea), Dalian and Changchun (China), and Portugal (Europe).
Expanding production at the new site reinforces the company's competitiveness by producing key components for hybrid and battery electric vehicles.
Company executives and local dignitaries gathered at a media event to announce the new plant.
The new Woodbridge plant is expected to start production in the first half of 2025.
Hanon Systems has a 35-year history in Canada.
Hanon Systems' Belleville plant was established in 1989.
The Belleville plant produces fluid transport products, HVAC modules, and electronic coolant valves for the North American market.
The new Woodbridge plant is located 200 kilometers from the Belleville plant.
The Woodbridge facility will create synergies with Hanon Systems' Americas Innovation Center in Novi, Michigan.
Proximity to major automakers will allow the company to respond quickly to customer demands.
source_title: Hanon Systems Expands Manufacturing Capability in Canada

facts: HELLA Automotive Sales, Inc. is celebrating its 40th anniversary of operations in North America.
The company was founded in Germany in 1899.
HELLA has become a global leader in automotive lighting and electronics technology.
The company has been a key player in the North American market since 1983.
HELLA Automotive Sales, Inc. first established its North American presence by opening a headquarters in Cranford, New Jersey.
In 1991, the company moved its North American headquarters to Peachtree City, Georgia.
Over the past 40 years, HELLA has expanded its operations throughout the United States, Canada, and Central and South America.
Alejandro Caballero serves as the President of HELLA Automotive Sales, Inc.
Caballero stated that over the past four decades, the company has grown its business and established itself as a trusted partner to customers in the automotive industry.
HELLA has been recognized with numerous awards for its products and services, including the Automotive News PACE Award.
The company maintains partnerships with some of the world’s leading automotive manufacturers.

To commemorate its 40th anniversary in North America, HELLA will host a series of events and activities throughout the year, including employee recognition programs, customer appreciation events, and community outreach initiatives.
source_title: HELLA Automotive Sales, Inc. celebrates 40 years of Success in North America - Motortopia - EVERYTHING Automotive!

facts: Automotive supplier Hella operates under the umbrella brand Forvia.
Hella is expanding its European R&D network with two new facilities in the Romanian cities of Iași and Oradea.
The focus of the new facilities will be on autonomous driving and electromobility.
The Technical Center in Oradea will primarily concentrate on developing products for electric vehicles, including battery management systems and voltage converters.
In Iași, software development and testing for 77 GHz radar sensors is initially entrusted to the new location.
The 77 GHz radar sensors are described as a central key technology for assisted and automated driving.
Both locations have already started operations in autumn 2022 with a core team of around ten engineers each.
Hella plans to create a total of up to 300 new jobs at the two locations over the next three to four years.
The planned jobs are not broken down between the topics of electromobility and autonomous driving.
Forvia is the name of the group of companies since the takeover of Hella by the French supplier Faurecia last year.
Björn Twiehaus serves as Managing Director Electronics at Hella.
Björn Twiehaus stated that Romania has already been represented for many years with essential high-performance electronics sites where highly sophisticated control units are developed.
Björn Twiehaus said it was obvious to build on this network and further strengthen Hella's presence in the country.
source_title: Hella opens e-mobility development centre in Romania - electrive.com

facts: In January 2021, Hitachi Automotive Systems, Keihin, Showa, and Nissin Kogyo completed a business merger to form the global mega-supplier Hitachi Astemo.
Hitachi Astemo is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and operates as a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd. and Honda Motor Co.
Brice Koch serves as President & CEO of Hitachi Astemo, Ltd.
Gary Plover of Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. has been appointed to CAWA's Manufacturers' Advisory Council.
Gary Plover was named Head of Aftermarket Division for Hitachi Astemo Americas in April 2022.
Hannah Hua was appointed as Marketing Manager of Aftermarket Sales at Hitachi Astemo Americas.
On September 26, 2022, Hitachi Astemo received EV e-Axle orders from Honda and announced plans to further expand its electrification business.
On October 31, 2022, Hitachi Astemo announced it will provide inverters and motors to JATCO Ltd., which will supply e-Axles to Nissan's future e-POWER and battery electric vehicles.
For Nissan's electric axles, Hitachi Astemo will deliver efficient, low-loss motors using square wire technology, along with efficient inverters.
Hitachi Astemo develops in-house electric axles intended for sale to various automobile manufacturers globally.
The company developed a Steer-by-Wire prototype with advanced steering and failsafe functions, announced on October 3, 2022.
On October 20, 2022, Hitachi Astemo developed a prototype of a new steer-by-wire steering device that expands cabin space.
On October 21, 2022, Hitachi Astemo developed a prototype 360-Degree Stereo Vision system with multi-camera 3D sensing.
Hitachi Astemo's product segments include engine management systems, electric powertrain systems, drive control systems, and car information systems.
The company is committed to achieving carbon neutrality at its business sites (factories and offices) by FY2030.
Hitachi Astemo reduced CO2 emissions at factories and offices by 11% in FY2021 compared to FY2010.
A facility named Hitachi Astemo Rock Spring, Inc. is located at 11782 in Rock Spring, Georgia, United States, with job listings for a Machining General Associate (3rd Shift) role.
Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. maintains an office at 10833 Valley View St. Suite 400, Cypress, CA 90630 U.S.A., formerly known as Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc.
Hitachi America, Ltd. was established in 1959 as a regional subsidiary, with its first U.S. manufacturing facility opening in 1977.
Hitachi Astemo's entry into the Americas began in 1972 with a subsidiary established in Brazil.
The company expanded ignition coil coverage to 260 million VIO in April 2022.

facts: Japanese automotive group Astemo is investing £100m in its local factory in Horwich, Greater Manchester.
The investment will create a new production line to manufacture inverters for electric vehicles following a “significant” contract win.
Astemo will be the only company in the UK with that specific EV inverter production capability.
The project is expected to create around 220 new high-skilled manufacturing jobs.
Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham and Manchester City Council leader Bev Craig visited Astemo’s Tokyo base last month during a trade mission to Japan.
During the visit, they met with Astemo executive officer Mathieu Devillard.
Andy Burnham stated the investment puts Greater Manchester at the forefront of the electric vehicle revolution and creates high-quality, long-term jobs in advanced manufacturing.
Burnham noted that international engagement has made Greater Manchester the number one UK destination for foreign direct investment outside London.
Bolton Council leader Coun Nick Peel said the project will create hundreds of jobs in Horwich and ensure a new production line to manufacture inverters for electric vehicles.
Business and Trade Secretary Jonathan Reynolds stated Astemo is investing over £100 million into its UK site, creating and safeguarding more than 200 jobs.
Reynolds emphasized the government is working hand in hand with investors to build a globally competitive zero-emission vehicle supply chain in the UK.
Joe Manning, managing director at inward investment agency MIDAS, confirmed over £100m in confirmed investment supports 220 jobs and noted MIDAS has worked closely with Astemo for several years.
Ahead of the Japan trade mission, Japanese vinegar manufacturer Mizkan announced it was investing £17m in its Middleton base.
Mizkan will be the headline sponsor for the 50th annual Japan Week cultural festival in Manchester this September.
Cllr Bev Craig stated Greater Manchester's economy is worth approximately £80 billion annually and provides a foundation for Japanese companies to establish and expand UK operations.
source_title: Japanese automotive group Astemo investing £100m and creating 200 jobs in Greater Manchester | Business Live
