Delivery Method: Design-Build
Hwashin Georgia Corporation is expanding its US presence by building a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, GA.

This facility will include various buildings that will support the warehousing, fabrication, painting, and logistics for automotive parts for electric vehicle market that is rapidly expanding in Eastern Georgia.
source_title: Hwashin Georgia | Dunn Building Company

facts: Hwashin plans to build a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Laurens County.
The facility will create around 460 jobs and bring more than $176 million investment to Laurens County.
Nearly 500 new jobs will come to the Laurens County area following the opening of the facility.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Hwashin is the latest company to join Georgia's growing ecosystem of companies in the e-mobility industry.
The new facility will be located at the Georgia Highway 257 Rail Served Site.
Production at the new facility is scheduled to start in late 2025.
The site is the first Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Select site in the state, following years of planning and months of work.
Hwashin will be the seventh international company to establish operations in Dublin and Laurens County.
The facility will mainly focus on manufacturing chassis components for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Key OEM customers for the chassis components include Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America and Kia Georgia.
Hwashin is a South Korean-based Tier-1 supplier that specializes in producing chassis and body parts for automobile makers.
Hwashin currently operates manufacturing facilities in the U.S., Asia, and South America.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority successfully pitched the region to Hwashin as a prime location for business operations.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that the new facility will serve as an economic engine for the region, driving employment opportunities and quality growth.
source_title: More than 450 jobs are coming to Laurens County as auto parts company opens facility

facts: Hwashin, an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin.
The project will create over 460 new jobs.
The project involves more than $176 million in investment in Laurens County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the goal to become the e-mobility capital of the nation is bringing opportunity to communities all across the state.
Hwashin is the latest company to join Georgia's growing ecosystem of companies bringing good jobs in this industry, according to Governor Kemp.
Hwashin stands as a Tier-1 supplier with a specialization in crafting chassis and body parts for Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, and international original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The company operates from its headquarters in Korea and has established production facilities spanning the United States, Asia, and South America.
Seo Jin Jung is CEO of Hwashin.
Jung stated Hwashin aspires to shape a future global city focused on electric vehicles (EVs), drawing from 50 years of expertise and collaboration with Georgia and Laurens County.
The alliance seeks to position the region at the forefront of the evolving automobile industry, according to Jung.
Jung stated Hwashin will take on the role of a reliable partner to customers and an engaged contributor to the local community.

Jung expressed belief in the company's capacity to contribute to community prosperity and the American auto parts industry, extending gratitude to the State of Georgia and Laurens County for ongoing cooperation and support throughout the project.
Hwashin’s new Georgia facility is scheduled to begin production in late 2025.
The facility will be located at the Georgia Highway 257 Rail Served Site, the first Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Select site in the state.
A “GRAD Certified Select” status indicates a site has met or exceeded more rigorous certification requirements.
The facility will be primarily focused on manufacturing chassis components for OEMs, including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America and Kia Georgia.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated the City of Dublin is pleased to welcome Hwashin to the community.
Mayor Kight praised the staff and board of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority for demonstrating why Dublin-Laurens County is a great place to do business.
Mayor Kight stated the manufacturing facility will become an economic engine for the region, leading to more employment opportunities and quality growth.
Jeff Davis is Chairman of the Laurens County Board of Commissioners.
Chairman Davis welcomed Hwashin as the seventh international company to call Dublin and Laurens County home.
Davis cited the partnership and “can-do attitude” between Laurens County, the City of Dublin, and the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority.

Davis stated the project will change the landscape for Dublin-Laurens County by providing new career opportunities for area residents while developing increased opportunities in housing and retail development.
Griff Lynch is President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority.
Lynch stated significant investments from Hwashin and other automotive suppliers are delivering career opportunities that will allow Georgians to thrive at home.
Lynch expressed pride in supporting economic development throughout the state and welcomed Hwashin to the manufacturing community.

Lynch stated the Georgia Ports Authority's mission is to efficiently connect companies like Hwashin to international markets, noting the announcement as evidence of Georgia’s position as a global business center.
Alyce Thornhill is Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team.
Thornhill represented GDEcD on this competitive project in partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
Pat Wilson is a GDEcD Commissioner.
Commissioner Wilson stated Hwashin will have direct interstate access to at least two long-term automotive partners from its chosen site.
Wilson noted the company can pursue an accelerated construction timeline by locating on a GRAD Select site.
Wilson cited decades of elected leadership supporting infrastructure and workforce development as areas where Georgia excels against competition.
Wilson praised local officials and teams for their role in attracting jobs of the future.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility OEMs resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state.
As the electric vehicle market continues to grow, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25.1 billion in investments and 29,000 jobs since 2020.
Since its inception in 1975, Hwashin Co., Ltd. has held a prominent position in the Korean auto parts industry for nearly five decades.
The company maintains a specialized focus on chassis and body parts and actively engages in research and development.
Hwashin continuously expanded its product offerings to encompass lightweight aluminum components and eco-friendly solutions aligned with the global automotive industry’s eco-conscious direction.
Hwashin Co., Ltd. employs more than 4,200 personnel worldwide.
source_title: Auto Parts Supplier Hwashin to Bring 460 Jobs to Middle Georgia - AllOnGeorgia

facts: The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority serves as the primary conduit for business incentives offered to qualified industrial prospects relocating or expanding in Dublin-Laurens County.
Incentives require signed performance agreements mandating companies to meet specific job and investment expectations.
Manufacturers, distributors, and qualifying businesses may receive local property tax exemptions for a negotiated period based on commitments to new jobs and capital investment.
Development Authority staff assists companies in structuring tax incentives to comply with local, state, and federal law.
The Authority acts as a conduit for low-interest Industrial Revenue Bonds (IRBs) for qualifying projects that provide external letters of credit for collateral.
Local leadership commits to expedite permitting and plan review for certain projects as allowed by law.
Qualifying projects are eligible for grant funds through the Laurens County Closing Fund (LCCF) for real property improvements and other Georgia-law-allowed expenditures.
Qualifying companies may receive donations of land in established industrial parks based on projected jobs and investment.
Companies generating significant economic impact may receive assistance with building pad preparation.
In 2005, Georgia became the first Southeast state to adopt a Single Factor Gross Receipts apportionment formula for corporate income tax.
Georgia's corporate income tax rate is six percent, and it is one of only 13 states using Single Factor Apportionment.
The Single Factor formula treats gross receipts in Georgia as the only factor for determining taxable income, significantly reducing effective rates for companies selling outside Georgia.
Georgia does not use a Throw Back Rule to tax income from sales to out-of-state customers whose states lack the corresponding tax.
Companies in strategic industries (manufacturing, warehousing, distribution, processing, telecommunications, broadcasting, tourism, and R&D) may qualify for the Job Tax Credit Program.
Qualifying companies must create between five and 25 net new full-time jobs in year one depending on the community tier.
Job Tax Credits accrue for additional jobs created in years two through five.
The Job Tax Credit value ranges from $750 to $3,500 per job per year starting in the first taxable year.
An additional $500 credit is available in counties participating in a multi-county Join Development Authority (JDA).
Increased tax credits equal to Tier 1 credits are allowed for jobs created in Less Developed Census Tracts, Opportunity Zones, Military Zones, and Georgia's 40 least developed counties.
Job Tax Credits may offset 100 percent of state corporate income tax liability in Tier 1 and 2 counties, or 50 percent in Tier 3 and 4 counties.
Unused Job Tax Credits may be carried forward for 10 years from the close of the taxable year qualified jobs were established.
In Tier 1 counties, excess Job Tax Credits may offset Georgia payroll withholding taxes with a $3,500 per job per year limitation.
The Port Tax Credit Bonus is available to taxpayers increasing imports or exports through a Georgia port by 10 percent over the base year.
Base year port traffic must be at least 75 net tons, five containers, or 10 TEUs, or these figures are used as the base if actual traffic is lower.
Port Tax Credits can offset up to 50 percent of corporate income tax liability and may be carried forward for 10 years.

The Port Tax Credit Bonus cannot be utilized with the Quality Jobs Tax Credit and is limited to Opportunity Zones, Military Zones, and Less Developed Census Tracts for existing large distribution centers.
An additional $1,250 per job credit is added to the Job Tax Credit for taxpayers with qualified increases in port shipments.
The Quality Jobs Tax Credit requires creating at least 50 jobs in a 12-month period paying at least 110 percent of the county average wage.
The Quality Jobs Tax Credit ranges from $2,500 to $5,000 per job per year for up to five years, with new jobs within seven years qualifying.
Quality Jobs Tax Credits may offset payroll withholding after other tax liability is exhausted and can be carried forward for 10 years.
Georgia offers a 10 percent tax credit on increased research and development expenses subject to a base amount calculation.

The R&D base amount equals current year Georgia gross receipts multiplied by the average ratio of qualified Georgia R&D expenses to gross receipts for the preceding three years, or 0.300, whichever is less.
For new companies or those with no prior Georgia R&D expenditures, the base amount is 30 percent of current year Georgia gross receipts.
The R&D credit applies to 50 percent of net Georgia income tax liability after other credits and can be applied to state payroll withholding in the first five years for new entities.
Unused R&D credits may be carried forward for up to 10 years.
Companies employing at least 1,800 new employees and investing a minimum of $450 million or having a $150 million annual payroll qualify for the Mega Project Tax Credit.
The Mega Project Tax Credit provides $5,250 per job per year for the first five years, applied to corporate income tax with excess eligible for payroll withholding.
Employers purchasing or building qualified child care facilities receive Georgia income tax credits equal to 100 percent of construction costs, spread over 10 years at 10 percent annually.
Unused child care construction credits can be carried forward three years, and the facility must be state-licensed.
Employers providing or sponsoring child care receive a credit against Georgia income tax equal to 75 percent of direct operating costs, with unused credits carried forward five years.
All child care credits can be used against 50 percent of a taxpayer's income tax liability in a given year.
The Work Opportunity Tax Credit Program (WOTC) is coordinated by the Georgia Department of Labor for hiring individuals from nine target groups facing employment barriers.
WOTC reduces federal income tax liability by $1,200 to $9,000 per qualified employee, with $2,400 being the most frequent credit for adult new hires.
Employers must receive Georgia Department of Labor certification before claiming WOTC on federal returns.
Qualified equipment purchases or leases used in manufacturing are exempt from sales tax.
Primary material handling equipment, computer equipment, and Class 100 (or less) clean room machinery, equipment, and materials can also be exempted under certain conditions.
Effective January 1, 2011, business inventory is exempt from state property taxes at 0.25 mills.
Many Georgia counties exempt up to 100 percent of qualified raw material, work-in-process, and finished goods inventory under the local-option Freeport law.
Distribution and warehouse inventories are exempt if destined to be shipped out of state.
Georgia's Foreign-Trade Zone (FTZ) program allows companies to defer, decrease, or eliminate duties on imported materials used in products assembled in Georgia.
The Georgia Department of Labor assists recruitment by posting job notices, screening applications, providing interview space and scheduling, and hosting job fairs.
GDOL works with private employment agencies that list jobs with the state.
Quick Start is a nationally-ranked employee training program providing customized skill-based job training at no cost to qualifying companies.
Quick Start provides training space, instructors, and materials, potentially saving companies millions in training costs.
A retraining tax credit allows companies to claim 50 percent of direct training costs, up to $500 per full-time employee per approved program, totaling a maximum of $1,250 per employee per year.
Retraining programs must be approved by the Technical College System of Georgia for quality and productivity enhancements or software technologies.
The retraining tax credit offsets up to 50 percent of state corporate income tax liability and can be carried forward 10 years or combined with other credits.
The HOPE Scholarship provides tuition assistance at one of Georgia's 35 public colleges or universities for graduating high school seniors with a B or better average.
The HOPE Grant allows all Georgians to receive degrees or certificates at low cost through technical colleges and schools, aiding relocating families and company training initiatives.

Worksource Heart of Georgia covers 17 counties including Appling, Bleckley, Candler, Dodge, Emanuel, Evans, Jeff Davis, Johnson, Laurens, Montgomery, Tattnall, Telfair, Toombs, Treutlen, Wayne, Wheeler, and Wilcox.
The Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) funds provide On-The-Job training, Youth Services, and Individual Training Accounts across the 17-county area.

facts: Uline, Inc. is a private, family-owned company offering shipping and business supplies throughout North America.
The company was founded in 1980 by Richard and Elizabeth Uihlein from their basement in Lake Forest, Illinois, using start-up funds from Edgar Uihlein.
Its first product was the H-101 carton sizer.
Operations expanded to Minnesota, California, and New Jersey during the 1980s and 1990s.
In the 2000s, Uline opened distribution centers in Illinois, Texas, Georgia, Wisconsin, and Pennsylvania.
The company's catalog grew to 450 pages offering over 17,000 products before later expanding further.
In 2008, Uline announced construction of a new headquarters in Pleasant Prairie, Wisconsin, motivated by the founders' ties to the state.
Wisconsin Governor Jim Doyle expected 1,000 jobs would move to Southeastern Wisconsin and pledged $6 million to support the headquarters move.
The 200-acre headquarters opened in summer 2010, featuring offices and a 1-million square foot warehouse supplying distribution centers.

An expansion with a second office building and warehouse was announced in 2014, with construction beginning in early 2016 and completing in 2017, bringing about 800 additional jobs to Pleasant Prairie.
In 2019, Uline began considering a second major expansion with two more distribution centers totaling over 1.7 million square feet to complement existing centers in Pleasant Prairie.
In 2021, Uline opened a warehouse in Reno, Nevada, to service Nevada and Northern California.
Elizabeth Uihlein serves as president and chief executive officer, while Richard Uihlein is chairman of the board of directors.
All three of their children serve as company executives: Duke Uihlein (Minnesota branch manager), Freddy Goldenberg (corporate planning manager), and Brian Uihlein (vice president of merchandising).
Richard Uihlein's brother Steve also serves as a vice president.
The headquarters consists of two buildings on 200 acres, including a 279,000 square foot facility and a newer 2017 building of about the same size.
Plans announced in January 2024 to begin work on a third corporate campus building of 325,000 square feet.
The company moved from Waukegan, Illinois, to Pleasant Prairie, Wisconsin, in 2010 in return for up to $18.6 million in state incentives.
Uline regularly holds career fairs at its warehouses and uses workers on H-1B and temporary work visas.

In 2015, Uline began using hydrogen-powered forklifts in its Pleasant Prairie facility, expanding their use to five other facilities between then and 2024, with four more facilities announced in 2024.
The company's product catalog is mailed twice a year, is 800 pages long, and advertises over 37,500 products.
In 2014, the company was estimated to have $2 billion in revenue, with ProPublica estimating nearly a billion dollars in profit in 2018.
As of February 2020, the company reported over 6,700 employees and over $5.8 billion in revenue.
In 2023, Uline reported US$11 billion in revenue and had 9,392 employees.
The company donated $3 million to the city of Kenosha, Wisconsin, in 2023 to help build housing.
Elizabeth and Richard Uihlein are major donors to far-right conservative and Republican causes, with the company and employees contributing over $31 million to political campaigns before June 2020.
source_title: Uline - Wikipedia

facts: Dublin-Laurens County is known throughout Georgia as one of the most pro-business communities in the state.
The region has experienced a resurgence of manufacturing and logistics jobs.
The region has one of the nation’s lowest costs of living which translates into lower costs for companies.
Laurens County has the third lowest property tax rate out of 159 counties in Georgia.
The community provides 100% exemption on Freeport which includes a company’s inventory and work in process materials.
These cost advantages allow companies leaving California, the Northeast and other high cost areas to see more bottom line efficiencies.
Millions of dollars are being invested in the region’s utility infrastructure to meet future demands of the area’s businesses, industries and residents while maintaining very competitive rates.
The City of Dublin has over 1.5 million gallons per day of excess permitted water and wastewater capacity with significant pressure.
The City also has one of the most reliable natural gas networks in the country and significant excess gas capacity.
Customers in Georgia with over a 900 kilowatts per hour connected load can require electric companies to bid for their service.
Georgia Power, five Electric Membership Corporations, and the City of Dublin provide electricity and natural gas throughout the region.
These utility providers are in the midst of developments designed to significantly increase the power generation capacity over the next decade.
Companies in Middle Georgia typically enjoy electric rates priced at 15%-20% below the U.S. national average.
Laurens County is a Tier One county as selected by the Georgia Department of Community Affairs.
Tier One communities receive the full state statutory incentives which include a per job state income tax credit equivalent to $4,000 per job over five years.
Companies creating jobs here can use the $4,000 per job against 100% of their State of Georgia corporate income tax liability.
Any remaining credits, up to $3,500 per job, can be applied to the company’s withholding tax liability which is sent as a cash reimbursement to the company.
The region is located halfway between Atlanta and Savannah with direct access to major highways and interstates.
The area has direct access to two international airports and the fastest-growing port in North America.
This strategic location provides the ability to transport goods and materials to 80% of the consumer market in the Unites States in less than a 2-hour plane ride, or a 48-hour truck drive.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority is comprised of a small staff and a volunteer board that represent the professional and personal integrity of the community.
Dublin is governed by a mayor and 7-person city council, and Laurens County is governed by a 5-person county commission board.
Matt Hatchett, a small business owner in our community, represents the interests of Dublin-Laurens County as a state representative in the Georgia House of Representatives.
State Senator Larry Walker, III represents the interests of Dublin-Laurens County in the other chamber of the Georgia General Assembly.
The low cost/high quality of life in Dublin-Laurens County is paired with the availability of large industrial job opportunities, good schools, and major healthcare providers.
source_title: Competitive Advantages | Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority

NEXO Fuel Cell vehicles are available at certain Hyundai dealerships, which are also certified for NEXO Fuel Cell service and maintenance.
Evolve+ is a month-to-month car subscription service that comes with 1,000 miles, insurance, maintenance, registration, and roadside assistance, and can be canceled at any time.

Most Hyundai dealerships accept credit cards for certain payments, though details should be confirmed with the specific location.

facts: Up to 450 workers at a Hyundai Motor facility under construction in Georgia have been detained in a major raid by U.S. authorities that has led to a pause in the project to build a car battery factory.
The raid on Thursday dealt a setback to the project, which is part of what would be the biggest investments in the state.
The incident illustrates the increasing crackdown by the Trump administration on immigrants and its impact on businesses.

An agent at the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said multiple U.S. agencies conducted a judicially authorized enforcement operation, as they are actively conducting an investigation into unlawful employment practices.
Steven Schrank, special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations for Georgia, stated in a news briefing aired on U.S. television that arrests are being made.
A Korean news report said about 30 South Korean nationals had been detained.
The Atlanta office of the U.S. Justice Department agency ATF reported the raid in a post on X.

The battery production facility is a joint venture between South Korean battery maker LG Energy Solution (LGES) and Hyundai Motor, and was due to start operations at the end of this year according to LGES.
A spokesperson at the Hyundai-GA battery company stated it was cooperating fully with appropriate authorities regarding activity at the construction site and had paused construction work to assist.
Hyundai Motor said its production of electric vehicles at the sprawling site was not affected.

LG Energy Solution stated it is closely cooperating with the South Korean government and relevant authorities to ensure the safety of employees and staff at contractors, and to secure their swift release from detention, adding it is providing all necessary support including interpreters and legal assistance.
LGES shares dropped 2.3%.
In 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution announced a $4.3 billion venture to produce EV battery cells, with each company holding a 50% stake.
The plant will supply batteries for Hyundai, Kia and Genesis EV models.
The battery factory is part of Hyundai’s $12.6 billion investments in the state, including the automaker’s just-opened car factory.
The project would be the largest economic development project in the state’s history.

Social media video footage showed a man wearing a vest with the letters HSI telling workers in yellow safety vests that they have a search warrant for the whole site and need construction to cease immediately.

Under President Donald Trump, the Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, which is part of the Department of Homeland Security, has become the driving force of the Republican leader’s sweeping crackdown on migrants, bolstered by record funding and new latitude to conduct raids.
Trump has said he wants to deport the worst of the worst criminals but ICE figures have shown a rise in non-criminals being picked up.
Rights advocates have denounced such raids.

The U.S. is adding pressure on Korea Inc, one of the major investors in the United States, at a time when Washington and South Korea are at odds over details of a trade deal which includes $350 billion of investments.
source_title: Work paused at Hyundai’s U.S. site after hundreds of workers detained in raid | The Asahi Shimbun: Breaking News, Japan News and Analysis

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) are partnering on an electric vehicle battery cell manufacturing joint venture in Savannah, Georgia.
The joint venture’s battery production will have an annual production capacity of 30 gigawatt hours.
The 30 gigawatt hour capacity is enough power to support the making of 300,000 electric vehicles.
Battery packs will be assembled by Hyundai Mobis using cells from the plant.
The battery packs will be supplied to the group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for the production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis electric vehicles.
The facility will help create a steady supply of batteries in the region and allow the company to respond more quickly to the increasing demand for electric vehicles in the U.S. market.
LGES now has seven battery plants operating or under construction in the U.S.
The U.S. is where LGES is focusing most of its resources to expand production capacities.
By increasing local production, LGES aims to provide products in scale and with speed, aiding the transition of clean energy in the United States.
The companies have been partners in the field of electrification for many years.
They have worked on supplying batteries for electric vehicles including the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and IONIQ 6 dedicated EV.
Hyundai Motor Group's first electrified model, the Hyundai Elantra Hybrid LPi hybrid, was introduced in 2009.
Both companies started construction of an Indonesia battery cell joint venture in 2021.
The Indonesia battery cell joint venture is scheduled to start production in the first half of 2024.

Jaehoon Chang, president and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, said the company is focusing on electrification efforts to create a strong foundation to lead the global electric vehicle transition by establishing the new battery cell plant.
Both Hyundai Motor Group and LGES will hold a 50% stake in the joint venture.
The joint venture involves an investment of over $4.3 billion.
They aim to strengthen ties with the EV battery cooperation going forward.
Construction for the joint venture will start in the second half of 2023.
Battery production is aimed to start at the end of 2025 at the earliest, according to the companies.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group, LG Energy Solution to Invest $4.3 Billion in U.S. Battery Plant - Environment+Energy Leader

entity_name: Hyundai-LGES Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: LGES-Hyundai Metaplant America Battery Cell Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) will invest an additional $2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County.
This expansion raises the joint venture's total investment value to more than $4.3 billion.
The expansion will create another 400 new jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that they landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai’s Metaplant.
Governor Brian Kemp said they welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Georgia is being positioned as the e-mobility capital of the nation through these major investments.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure.

José Muñoz, president and global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated the incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.

In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) onsite battery cell manufacturing joint venture.
With this announcement, the EV manufacturing facility and the battery joint venture represent an estimated $7.59 billion in investment.
The combined facilities will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.

Dong-Myung Kim, president and head of the Advanced Automotive Battery Division of LG Energy Solution, stated the investment underscores dedication to driving America’s EV transition while bolstering the local economy through quality jobs.
The facility will have a capacity of 30 GWh.
At full operations, the facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant.
Hyundai Mobis will supply the assembled battery packs to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.

Carter Infinger, Chairman of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA), stated the announcement will bring 400 additional well-paying jobs for those in the Savannah region.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA), Georgia Power, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start on the project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the United States.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $25 billion in investments and the creation of over 30,000 jobs.
Battery-related projects have accounted for at least $11 billion of that investment.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.

facts: Hyundai announced the temporary halt of production for its Ioniq 5 and Kona Electric vehicles in South Korea in April 2025.
The automaker cited sluggish demand and U.S. tariffs as immediate triggers for the production shutdown.
U.S. tariffs currently add $10,000 to the cost of imported EVs.
Domestic sales in South Korea for the Ioniq 5 dropped to just 75 units in January 2024.
Hyundai slashed vehicle prices as a short-term fix that risks long-term brand equity.
Analysts warn that lithium prices could spike by 2026, squeezing automakers reliant on external suppliers.
Hyundai announced a $21 billion U.S. investment for a Georgia plant dedicated to Ioniq 5 production.
The Georgia plant is scheduled to reach full capacity in 2026.
The delayed timeline leaves Hyundai exposed to tariffs and lithium price volatility in the interim.
Electrovaya reported Q2 2025 revenue of $15 million, a 40% year-over-year surge, with net profit turning positive at $0.8 million.
Electrovaya's Jamestown, NY facility is backed by a $51 million EXIM loan and is set to begin commercial lithium-ion production by mid-2026.
Electrovaya secured over $25 million in new orders and achieved a 31% gross margin.
Mullen Automotive reported Q2 2025 revenue of $5 million, which is 143 times its 2024 output, driven by orders for Class 3 EVs from logistics firms and municipalities.
Mullen Automotive held cash reserves of $2.3 million as of March 2025 amid liquidity struggles.
Mullen Automotive partnered with Enpower Greentech to produce solid-state batteries by 2026.
LG Energy Solution reported a Q1 2025 operating profit of $375 million, supported by $458 million in U.S. tax credits.
LG Energy Solution shifted production to Michigan and prioritized lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries for energy storage systems (ESS).
LG Energy Solution maintains a 20% EBITDA margin and holds contracts with Delta Electronics and PGE.
source_title: Hyundai's EV Stumbles: A Buying Opportunity in the Battery Revolution?

facts: Hyundai is reportedly rushing the construction of its $7.6 billion electric vehicle and battery production facility in Georgia due to EV tax incentives tied to the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
The IRA rewards domestic EV and battery production, prompting foreign automakers to establish roots in North America or any country with a free trade agreement with the United States.
Hyundai stated that the IRA might be unfair to automakers that import electric vehicles and batteries from outside North America.
The IRA provides up to $7,500 in EV tax credits to vehicles and batteries made in North America.
Jose Munoz, Hyundai’s President and Global Chief Operating Officer, shared sales and lease figures after signing a partnership with Georgia Tech.
The Hyundai group sold or leased the second-most electric vehicles in the United States during the first half of 2023, behind only Tesla.
Hyundai still receives some benefits from the IRA since it provides tax credits to leased electric vehicles regardless of the country that produced them.
The South Korean company admits that the IRA is pushing it to set up production lines in North America faster.
Jose Munoz stated, “What we decided is to double down,” and noted the company is trying to accelerate the project as much as possible.
Hyundai is confident that the original project date of January 2025 would be pulled ahead, possibly by three months or more.
Hyundai initially aims to assemble 300,000 electric vehicles annually at the Georgia plant.
Hyundai has partnered with South Korean battery supplier LG Energy Solution (LGES) to produce cells at the Georgia plant, qualifying its EVs for more tax incentives under the IRA.
LGES and Hyundai increased their investment in the Georgia plant by $2 billion to increase its battery cell production capacity.
Jose Munoz stated, “We would like to ensure that the sourcing of the batteries is 100% USA to comply with the IRA.”
The increased investment will also ensure Hyundai and LG use “the best possible technology” to make batteries.
source_title: Hyundai rushes construction on $7.6B EV factory due to IRA incentives

facts: Steve Morrey, President and CEO of JAC Products, a leading designer and manufacturer of functional automotive exterior trim, announced the consolidation of manufacturing operations to improve productivity for the competitive automotive industry.
Manufacturing programs from the Monclova, Ohio plant will be transferred in an orderly fashion to other JAC Products facilities.
The company will close the Monclova plant during the spring of 2009.
It is estimated that 120 positions will move to the company's facilities in either Georgia or Michigan.
JAC Products' manufacturing operations in Europe will not be affected by this consolidation.

Steve Morrey stated that the consolidation is part of ongoing efforts to continually improve productivity, a prerequisite for success within the global automotive industry and a mandate at JAC Products.
Product innovation is a priority at JAC Products, which recently launched production of new cargo management systems for pickup trucks.
Founded in 1967, JAC Products Inc. is a leading designer and manufacturer of original equipment roof racks, functional exterior trim, and cargo management systems supplying vehicles worldwide.
Owned by Citi Group, JAC Products operates as a full-service global supplier with manufacturing at ISO/TS Registered locations in the U.S., Germany, and Portugal.
source_title:
Automotive Exterior Trim Manufacturer JAC Products Announces Plant
    Consolidation Plan

facts: JAC initiated an R&D process tailored for the Australian market three years prior to the article's publication.
The development plan included calibration of the J9’s suspension and driver assistance systems, plus work on local accessories.
An off-road trip with six JAC J9 pickups to Mount Disappointment north of Melbourne in the Victorian high country was one of the final hurdles before the brand’s arrival in Australian showrooms.
Danny Lenartic serves as the deputy managing director of JAC Motors.
One J9 test vehicle has covered more than 20,000 kilometres in various conditions.
Six additional J9s are equipped in top-level Oasis specification.
The development effort includes durability testing to ensure vehicle changes align with local preferences and withstand Australian conditions.
Balancing innovation with timelines and resources is cited as the biggest challenge in the development process.
The development team consists of engineers from JAC in China, engineering staff from local importer LTS Auto, and contractors.
JAC and LTS Auto are working in a continued partnership to commit resources toward improving products for Australian conditions and standards.
The test program began in the final quarter of last year and has been ramping up since then.
JAC has put special emphasis on driver assistance systems calibration to meet Australian road safety standards.
Despite the extensive J9 test program, there is no update from JAC about other models for Australia, including SUVs and passenger cars from its Chinese lineup.
The company’s current focus remains solely on the T9 ute.
Future models are scheduled to arrive later this year.
Heavy-duty accessories are being developed and built locally in Australia.
source_title: How the JAC T9 ute is being prepared to cope with Australian conditions | CarExpert

facts: Auto supplier JAC Products Inc. struck a deal to lease a 46,500-square-foot warehouse on Woodland Drive in Saline.
The deal expands JAC Products' presence in Saline.
The company was acquired in late 2010 by Chicago-based private equity firm Wynnchurch Capital Ltd.
JAC Products employs 412 workers at its 180,000-square-foot warehouse on Industrial Drive.
The leased warehouse space will accommodate off-site storage of older model year parts and tools needed to service aftermarket needs.
JAC Products moved its warehouse from Manchester to Saline to save cost and convenience.
Signature Associates' Jay Chavey, Brad Viergever, Joe Hamway, and Steve Gordon represented the parties in the transaction.
The warehouse property owner is auto supplier DURA Automotive Systems Inc., represented by Signature's Jay Chavey and Brad Viergever.

facts: South Korea’s Hyundai Motor Group announced in May 2022 its intention to build a $5.4 billion plant to produce electric vehicles in Bryan County.
The plant will be built on the 2,923-acre megasite located less than 31 miles from the Port of Savannah, with access to I-95 and I-16.
The plant will have capacity to produce 300,000 vehicles a year and offer 8,100 jobs.
The state’s original prediction of $1 billion in additional investment has already been exceeded by $600 million.
Georgia Department of Transportation and county governments are working on highway and local road improvements, including redesigning the I-95 and I-16 interchange.
Water and sewer infrastructure is being upgraded in some counties to support project growth.
A task force has been set up to identify strategies to increase the regional skilled workforce pipeline.

In 2014, Bryan, Chatham, Effingham, and Bulloch counties established the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA) to attract a landmark investment to the Bryan County site.
All four JDA counties have an equal voice and work together to secure Hyundai-related projects, while competing independently for non-Hyundai projects.
The JDA structure discourages potential investors from playing one county off against the other.
Bryan County was the fastest-growing county in Georgia and the sixth-fastest-growing county in the nation as of the 2020 census.
St. Joseph’s/Candler health system plans to add two new medical facilities in Bryan County to accommodate population influx.
Plug Power announced an $84 million investment to open a green-power production plant in Kingsland.
Plug Power’s zero-emission fuel production plant will help serve industrial customers across the Southeastern U.S. and will create 24 jobs.
Green Power EMC reached an agreement to bring an additional 252 megawatts of solar energy online to serve 16 subscribing electric membership corporations across Georgia.
Construction of the first of three South Georgia Green Power sites will begin in Jeff Davis County later this year.
The three South Georgia Green Power sites together will produce enough low-cost, renewable power to help serve more than 44,000 EMC households.
Effingham County has seen strong growth in logistics over the last six years due to Port of Savannah expansion and commercial property scarcity.
Trammell Crow Company located a 477-acre site in Effingham County to build Coastal Trade Center, featuring 4.7 million square feet of logistics space.
Bulloch County has attracted two Hyundai suppliers: auto parts manufacturers Joon Georgia and Ecoplastic.
Chatham County’s economy is supported by the port, tourism, and an entertainment production industry that generated $207 million in direct spend in 2022.
Hyundai supplier Seoyon E-Hwa announced it will build a second $76 million facility in Chatham County, where it already has a plant in LaGrange.
Commercial real estate company Jones Lang LaSalle reported that warehousing rents rose 28% in the fourth quarter of 2022 due to strong demand.
Brunswick’s port in Glynn County is the nation’s second-busiest hub for Ro/Ro cargo like cars and tractors.
A $150 million development plan at the Brunswick port will be completed this year, raising annual capacity from 1.2 million vehicles to 1.4 million.
Glynn County is fielding requests for information from Hyundai suppliers.
Tourism in Glynn County was up 23% compared to pre-pandemic 2019 in 2022, bringing $229 million in lodging revenue.
Downtown Brunswick is experiencing growth with new restaurants and a historic hotel undergoing restoration.
Broadband is being installed in rural sections of Glynn County that still lack it.
A serious worker shortage exists in the region, according to Ralph Staffins III, CEO of the Brunswick-Golden Isles Chamber of Commerce.
Camden County hosts Naval Submarine Base Kings Bay, which generates a former-military workforce of skilled engineers and technicians.
Camden County hosts two green hydrogen production plants owned by Latham, NY-based Plug Power.
The Camden County Joint Development Authority provided land and local site support to attract Plug Power’s $84 million investment.
The Camden County hydrogen plants are capable of producing 15 tons of liquefied hydrogen a day, with plans to expand to 30 tons.
A 721-acre, $500 million coastal project called Cumberland Inlet is being developed in St. Marys, featuring an ecotourism park, marina, boutique hotel, retail, restaurants, and vacation rentals.
Atlanta-based Jacoby Development Inc. broke ground on the Cumberland Inlet project in August 2022.
The real estate market in Camden County has exploded with people moving from the West, Northeast, and Florida, attracted by lower land and insurance costs compared to Florida.
Liberty County shares Fort Stewart-Hunter Army Airfield with Bryan County and can offer businesses an ex-military workforce.
Liberty County has a strong industrial base in distribution, e-commerce, and manufacturing, with four new industrial zones recently designated.
Foreign direct investment accounts for 70% of Liberty County’s industrial companies.
Coffee County aims to diversify its economy by adding value to its agricultural base alongside new industry.
Pilgrim’s Pride recently expanded its poultry operations in Douglas with a $75 million facility to produce pet food ingredients.
Water, sewer, and natural gas expansions at Barrington Agri-Business Park are intended to generate $45 million in private investments.
Coffee County faces tight available housing, especially for low-income and middle-income families, prompting calls to encourage residential construction.
Ware County has several planned expansions and new industrial projects in the works, some in the solar area.

Alabama-based Twin Pines Minerals plans to mine titanium on the edge of the Okefenokee National Wildlife Refuge, a plan that claims no harm to the swamp but faces opposition from residents and environmentalists.
The Twin Pines Minerals mining plan could impact neighboring Charlton County’s economic fortunes.

facts: Kumho Tire U.S.A. appointed Carolina Wagner as Vice President of Marketing.

Wagner will oversee brand strategy, product marketing, integrated communications, digital marketing, demand generation, and sales support for the tire manufacturer’s U.S. operations covering passenger, light truck, and commercial vehicle segments.
The executive brings more than 25 years of experience in the tire industry, having held positions at Continental Tires the Americas and Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.
At Continental, Wagner served as Executive Marketing Manager for passenger, light truck, and commercial tire segments across the Mercosur region in São Paulo, Brazil.
She later held Director of Marketing roles at Continental Tires Americas, leading teams for the commercial vehicle segment across the United States, Canada, and Latin America.
Wagner began her career at Goodyear in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, advancing through executive leadership positions in Brazilian and Latin American markets.
Ed Cho serves as CEO of Kumho Tire U.S.A.

"Carolina’s arrival marks a pivotal moment for Kumho Tire," said Ed Cho. "Her deep expertise in the tire industry, combined with her proven ability to build integrated marketing strategies that deliver measurable results, makes her the ideal leader to drive our next chapter."
Wagner holds a bachelor’s degree in business administration from Universidade Santa Úrsula and a Master of Business Administration from IBMEC, both in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.
Kumho Tire Co. Inc., headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, serves as the U.S. sales, marketing, product development, and distribution arm of the South Korea-based tire manufacturer.

facts: Kumho Tire USA held its 2012 dealer meeting at the close of 2011 rather than waiting for the start of the new year.
The event featured a "Let's Go" theme to reflect the tiremaker's eagerness to continue momentum from 2011 into 2012 and beyond.
Kumho used the event to communicate that successful moves made in 2011 would continue into 2012, including progress in dealer communication, product availability, and brand awareness.
The meeting was held just outside of Liberia, Costa Rica, and included 38 key dealers in attendance.
Kumho officials addressed challenges faced going into 2011, including a sluggish economy, financial troubles of parent company Kumho Asiana Group, and an industry-wide supply shortage.

J.H. Kim, Kumho Tire Co. president and CEO, stated that the company continued to progress through its debt workout situation in 2011 with dealer help, noting good progress was made despite remaining work.
J.H. Kim predicted more change in 2012 that would bring challenges and opportunities, emphasizing the importance of working together for future success.

J.B. Kim, Kumho Tire USA president and CEO, attended the meeting and reported greatly improved fill rates through consolidating warehouses to three locations: Rancho Cucamonga, Calif., Chicago, and Atlanta.
J.B. Kim noted that during the year, Kumho's fill rates were among the best of any supplier for passenger tires.

Armand Allaire, national vice president of sales, stated that supply was a bright spot for Kumho in 2011 and that moving from five to three warehouses allowed concentration of the supply pipeline and replenishment schedules on three locations.
Armand Allaire reported that as a result of the warehouse consolidation, fill rates were quite good and the company continues to work toward a 100% fill rate goal.
J.B. Kim announced a new marketing direction focusing on sports marketing to place the Kumho brand among well-known and popular sports teams in the U.S.

Through sponsorships of the NBA’s Los Angeles Lakers and Miami Heat, NFL’s New York Jets and Buffalo Bills, USC Trojans football, and U.S. men’s and women’s soccer teams, Kumho’s overall brand awareness climbed from 21% to 25%.
In the New England area, brand awareness increased to 29% due to NFL sponsorships.
Rick Brennan, vice president of marketing, stated Kumho will continue building on sports marketing momentum in 2012 with more initiatives.
The Ecsta 4X tire, launched in July, earned impressive marks in testing, with 8.6 out of 10 drivers who purchased it saying they would consider purchasing it again.
Charles van Heusden, manager of training and product planning, noted Kumho added 12 more sizes to the Ecsta 4X lineup and plans to add sizes to the Ecsta LE Sport and Ecsta LX Platinum lines.
An improved winter tire program will be offered in 2012, with the entire line of I’zen studless and studdable tires for passenger cars, light trucks, SUVs, and CUVs seeing improved fill rates.
Charles van Heusden noted Kumho plans to inventory some winter tires in its warehouses to save dealers space in their inventories.
The consumer rebate on the Ecsta 4X and LX Platinum was extended through Jan. 31 due to its success, with similar rebates paired with sports tie-ins planned between April and October of 2012.

David Koh, marketing communications manager, stated Kumho’s TV and consumer magazine advertising campaigns will return even larger in 2012, with an estimated 140 million impressions planned during the April to October timeframe.
The advertising will target the age 18-49 male demographic, which is Kumho’s strongest.
David Koh added that Kumho will continue focusing on Internet presence and social media campaigns, which brought success in 2011.

David Koh noted Kumho extended an invitation for dealers to develop exclusive sports tie-ins for markets where Kumho holds sponsorships, citing the Dunn Tire co-branding initiative with the Buffalo Bills as an example.
Armand Allaire stated these adjustments will offer dealers better tools to improve their businesses and added that Kumho Tire USA is going to be much easier to do business with in 2012.
source_title: Ring in the New: Kumho Holds Dealer Meeting Early - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Broe Group’s Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub (SGIH) added global automotive parts supplier Kyungshin America Corporation (KAC) to its 2,600-acre multimodal logistics park near Georgia’s Port of Savannah.
KAC is a division of the South Korean Kyungshin Corporation.
KAC’s new operation will import and distribute automotive components to Hyundai Motor Group’s new $7.5B electric vehicle plant in Savannah.
KAC’s new operation will also distribute automotive components to the growing automotive manufacturing network throughout the southeastern United States.
Broe Real Estate Group Senior Vice President Sean Fitzsimmons stated that the Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub provides tremendous geographic and multi-modal benefits for park tenants.
Sean Fitzsimmons noted that KAC’s new facility is strategically positioned with the Port of Savannah and the new Hyundai Metaplant, both within 15 miles.
Sean Fitzsimmons added that this positioning creates exceptional supply chain efficiency and connectivity to global markets while leveraging Effingham County’s strong work force and education system.
The South Korean-based company produces wire harnesses for automotive companies.
KAC is a certified Tier One supplier to Hyundai.
KAC’s new facility will be 136,000 SF.
KAC’s new facility is expected to open in February.
The move supports Kyungshin’s continued North American production expansion.
Effingham County Industrial Development Authority CEO Brandt Herndon stated they are excited to welcome KAC to Georgia.

Brandt Herndon described the addition as a great example of the dynamic partnerships that will serve Hyundai’s new Georgia production plant and expressed pride in world-class companies bringing new jobs and new investment to Effingham County.

SGIH is a public-private partnership between the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority (ECIDA), The Broe Group’s real estate affiliate Broe Real Estate Group, and its transportation affiliate OmniTRAX.
SGIH is located 12 miles from the Port of Savannah.
SGIH provides dual rail access to the Class I rail networks of CSX and Norfolk Southern.

facts: Lear Corporation was founded in 1917 as American Metal Products in Detroit, Michigan.

During the 1980’s and 1990’s the company expanded to become a supplier of complete interior automotive systems, including seating, electrical, flooring, interiors, and instrument panels.
Lear supplies its components to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Today Lear has 221 manufacturing facilities in 36 countries globally.
The company’s largest customers include BMW, Ford, General Motors, Fiat, and Volkswagen.
Lear Corporation trades publicly on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol LEA.
Lear is ranked #242 in the Fortune 500.

facts: Lehigh Technologies is headquartered at 120 Royal Woods Court SW, Tucker, Georgia, 30084, United States.
The company was founded in 2003 and has reached the acquired stage.
Lehigh Technologies raised a total of $99.5M in funding.
Investors include Michelin Group, Horizon Technology Finance, Kleiner Perkins, Index Ventures, Florida Gulfshore Capital, and five additional investors.
The company manufactures green materials by converting end-of-life tire materials and post-industrial rubber into sustainable powders for industrial and consumer applications.
Lehigh Technologies has filed 26 patents, including a patent granted on March 15, 2022, related to polymers, thermoplastics, elastomers, rubber, and copolymers.

The company is listed as a key player in the tire recycling market alongside Liberty Tire Recycling, GENAN HOLDING A/S, ResourceCo, GRP LTD, Entech Inc., Emanuel Tire LLC, BDS Tire Recycling, Contec, and CRM.
Lehigh Technologies' recycled materials support circular economy practices by enabling the reuse of end-of-life tires instead of disposal.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies - Products, Competitors, Financials, Employees, Headquarters Locations

facts: Lehigh Technologies Inc., a sustainable materials company, is located in Atlanta, Georgia.
The company announced the expansion of its state-of-the-art research and development capabilities at its Application and Development Center (ADC).
Lehigh added Chad Jasiunas as Technical Manager for Functional Materials to its ADC team.
Jasiunas brings more than a decade of international experience in the development of solution-SBR systems.
Prior to joining Lehigh, Jasiunas worked at Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. and Synthos S.A., Europe’s largest manufacturer of emulsion rubber polymers.
Lehigh made significant investments in new mixing and analytical equipment for its ADC.
The expansion aims to drive further adoption of sustainable micronised rubber powder (MRP) material in green tire applications.
Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh Technologies, stated that the global green tire market is expected to grow significantly over the next five years.
Barton noted that Lehigh has positioned itself to support this growth by establishing best-in-class research and development facilities and a strong technical team.

Adel Halasa, Ph.D., a Lehigh Advisory Board member, stated that Jasiunas’s background in solution and functional SBR technologies will help Lehigh deliver MRP-based functional systems for Green Tire applications.
Lehigh invested in advanced silica-silane mixing capabilities, including a new internal mixing unit custom-built to its specifications.
The new internal mixing unit includes precision speed and temperature control as well as capability for mixing high viscosity silica-silane-SBR compounds.
Lehigh also acquired a Metravib model DMA+ 1000 dynamic mechanical analysis (DMA) unit.
The new DMA unit measures predictive parameters critical to green tire performance such as rolling resistance, traction, and handling under a wide range of conditions.
Tom Rosenmayer, Lehigh’s vice president of technology, stated that having advanced mixing and DMA testing capabilities is critical to the development of functional materials based on MRP.
Rosenmayer claimed that among its peers, Lehigh is unique in having this capability in-house, enabling more efficient work with customers to develop innovative solutions.
MRPs lower costs, increase the sustainability profile of end products, and deliver performance without sacrificing the reliability and performance offered by traditional raw materials.
Lehigh’s customer base already includes five of the top ten tire companies in the world.
More than 250m tires have been manufactured using its MRP technology.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies expands R&D capabilities | European Rubber Journal

facts: In 2022, Georgia-based Lehigh Technologies collaborated with Michelin to resurface 6.3 miles of Georgia’s Lawrenceville Highway with Lehigh’s Rubber Modified Asphalt, made with recycled tire rubber powder.
Jason Stravinski serves as President and CEO of Lehigh Technologies.
The paving project began in September 2022 and was in its final stages at the time of publication.
Kara Fulcher serves as Director of Sustainability and Government Affairs for Michelin North America, Inc.
The Rubber Modified Asphalt uses tires at the end of their first life, which are already available in the North American market.
The recycled material replaces a polymer derived from the oil-refining process.
RMA is a locally sourced replacement for an ingredient that is generally imported from Asia.
The use of RMA provides a highly resilient response to a long, complex supply chain.

Michelin and Lehigh are proud to take end-of-life tires and give them new life in other products through innovation to keep valuable materials in service as long as possible with progressively less ecological impact.
Over one million tons of US asphalt mix have been paved utilizing the Rheopave + MicroDyne 400-TR system which is comprised of all US sourced materials.
Lehigh’s rubber powder alone over the past 10 years has been employed to produce over 5,000 miles of asphalt.
Lehigh’s sustainable product blend can be used to modify liquid bitumen at an asphalt terminal or directly added at an asphalt contractor’s site via the Dry Process.
Lehigh is one of several companies that have been at the forefront of developing a unique, cost-saving modification process to produce sustainable and long-lasting roadways.

Rubber Modified Asphalt produced via the Dry Process with Lehigh’s state-of-the-art technologies provides a paving solution that is equally effective as oil-derived polymer modified asphalt, yet it’s cheaper and more friendly to the environment.
With oil and gas prices growing higher each day, importing polymer is less attractive compared to utilizing better, more sustainable options available in the U.S.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies Taking Rubber Modified Asphalt Round the Curve - Scrap Tire News: Tire and Rubber Recycling News and Information

facts: Lehigh Technologies is a leading specialty chemicals company that produces high-performance micronized rubber powders (MRP).

facts: ICF has provided 2.5 million euros in financing to Linde + Wiemann Spain.
The financing is for the construction of vehicle structural parts at the company’s plant in La Garriga.
The work drives the global launch of the new 100% electric Mercedes-Benz van.
Linde + Wiemann España is a German group based in La Garriga dedicated to the manufacturing of industrial vehicle components.
The company specializes in producing parts that form the structural components of vehicles.
The collaboration reflects the company’s commitment to financing alternatives beyond traditional banking, enabling a more agile financial structure adapted to the industrial timeline of the program.
The process with ICF has been marked by its operational simplicity and a high degree of cooperation between teams.

José Carlos López Pérez, manager of Linde + Wiemann España, stated: “We wish to express our sincere gratitude to the ICF for its support. Its proximity and flexibility have been key to securing the financing for these projects and accompanying a strategic challenge such as the launch of the new electric van.”

Vanessa Servera, CEO of ICF, stated: “this is an industrial project that combines innovation, sustainability, and growth — the kind we are proud to be part of and help make possible through the Generalitat’s public promotional bank.”

With this operation, Linde + Wiemann España strengthens its commitment to electrification, industrial competitiveness, and collaboration with public financial partners to spur innovation across the automotive value chain.
source_title: Linde + Wiemann Espanya receives 2.5 million euros from ICF for the manufacturing of the new Mercedes-Benz electric van

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Salesforce, Inc. will be investing $15 million to expand its presence in Georgia.
The expansion is expected to create over 250 new jobs by the end of 2028.
The investment will bring economic growth and job opportunities to Fulton County.
Governor Kemp stated the decision is a direct result of the state's commitment to fostering innovation and economic opportunity.
Governor Kemp highlighted Georgia's pro-business environment and investments in workforce development as key factors in attracting companies like Salesforce.
Salesforce is a global leader in AI-powered Customer Relationship Management (CRM) known for cloud-based technology that helps businesses connect with customers.
The company currently employs over 2,000 people in Georgia.
Salesforce serves customers across various industries including financial services, healthcare, retail, manufacturing, logistics, and the public sector.
Tanya Counter serves as Site Lead for Salesforce Atlanta.
Tanya Counter emphasized the company's confidence in Atlanta's talent and its commitment to creating an inclusive workplace.
Tanya Counter stated the expansion reflects their dedication to helping customers, employees, and communities thrive.
Tanya Counter expressed pride in continuing to grow and invest in a city they call home.
Salesforce Tower Atlanta, located at 950 East Paces Ferry Rd NE, will serve as the hub for this expansion.
Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens praised the city's diverse talent pool, global connectivity through Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, and welcoming community.
Mayor Dickens thanked Salesforce for their continued investment and looked forward to the opportunities this expansion will bring for residents and the local economy.
Fulton County Board of Commissioners Chairman Robb Pitts welcomed the expansion as a win for the county driven by premier companies' confidence in the community.
Chairman Pitts expressed pride in welcoming this growth and the opportunities it will bring for residents and the local economy.
Katie Kirkpatrick serves as President and CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber.
Katie Kirkpatrick highlighted metro Atlanta's deep pool of talented professionals and thriving communities as key drivers for growing companies seeking quality jobs and economic growth.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development's (GDEcD) Global Commerce team, represented by Senior Regional Project Manager Caroline Kinchler, worked closely with Invest Atlanta, Fulton County, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power to secure the expansion.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson congratulated Atlanta and Fulton County on attracting companies like Salesforce.
Commissioner Wilson emphasized Georgia's support for industries that drive technology and innovation as a means to boost local economies.
With Salesforce's expansion, Georgia continues to solidify its position as a leading state for business and innovation.
The investment will contribute to the overall economic growth of Fulton County and the state as a whole.

links.context: Company website for more information on Salesforce and its services

source_title: Georgia Chron - Gov. Kemp: Salesforce to Expand Georgia Footprint - 10318911

facts: Magna International Inc. is a Canadian parts manufacturer for automakers and the largest automobile parts manufacturer in North America by sales of original equipment parts.
The company produces automotive systems, assemblies, modules, and components supplied to General Motors, Ford, Stellantis, BMW, Mercedes, Volkswagen, Toyota, Tesla, and Tata Motors.
Magna is headquartered in Aurora, Ontario, with its chief executive officer being Swamy Kotagiri.
As of 2023, the company has 179,000 employees across 342 manufacturing operations and 91 product development, engineering and sales centres in 27 countries.
The company's corporate constitution requires consensus from management, shareholders, and employees to make changes, and it shares 10 percent of pretax profits with its employees.
In February 2021, the office of the governor of Michigan announced that Magna plans to open a facility in St. Clair, Michigan to build battery enclosures for the 2022 GMC Hummer electric pickup.
The new St. Clair facility is expected to cost $70 million and create over 300 jobs.
Battery enclosures for the GMC Hummer EV will be produced at General Motors' facilities in Detroit and Hamtramck.
An electric version of the Ineos Grenadier off-road utility vehicle is to be developed by Ineos and Magna, scheduled to enter production in Graz, Austria in 2026.

In March 2018, Magna announced a partnership with Lyft to supply high-tech kits that turn vehicles into self-driving cars, investing $200 million into the project while jointly owning the developed intellectual property as Lyft's exclusive supplier of self-driving kits.
In June 2018, Magna announced a partnership with BAIC Group to develop next-generation smart electric vehicles for Chinese consumers.
In February 2015, Samsung SDI agreed to purchase the battery pack business from Magna Steyr for $120 million.
The company partnered with BMW on electrification to produce electric cars on a contract basis.
Magna has produced more than 46 million automotive components and opened a $66.5 million plant to produce cameras and driver-assistance components.
In 2018, Magna introduced an Icon radar system to help automakers reach Level 5 autonomy as well as automatic emergency braking systems.
The company uses mobility technology to develop customized computing for fully automated driving systems and is known for using new materials for lightweighting vehicles.
Magna acquired Boston startup Optimus Ride and its more than 120 employees in January 2022 to advance autonomous vehicle technology.
In December 2022, Magna announced the acquisition of the Stockholm-headquartered Veoneer Active Safety business from SSW Partners for US$1.52 billion.
As of 2024, Magna International consists of seven groups: Cosma, Magna Electronics, Magna Powertrain, Magna Exteriors, Magna Seating, Magna Steyr, and Mechatronics, Mirrors & Lighting.
The company's United States operations are headed out of Troy, Michigan with its European head office in Vienna, Austria.
source_title: Magna International - Wikipedia

facts: Canadian mobility technology company Magna International has broken ground for a $70-million manufacturing facility in St. Clair (south of Port Huron).
The new facility will build complex battery enclosures for the 2022 GMC Hummer EV.
The 345,000-square-foot facility is named Magna Electric Vehicle Structures.
Production at the facility is expected to begin in early 2022.
The project will bring more than 300 new jobs to the area over the next five years.
The battery enclosures contribute to the structural and safety aspects of a vehicle’s frame and protect high-voltage batteries from damage and water.
Magna can develop these advanced assemblies in steel, aluminum, and multi-material configurations, including lightweight composites.

John Farrell, president of Cosma International (Magna’s body and chassis group), stated that bringing this battery enclosure technology to market demonstrates Magna’s ability to deliver full-system solutions supporting a lower emissions future.
Farrell noted that the move toward electrification allows the company to leverage its core strengths to bring customers innovative solutions.
The new business with General Motors Co. marks a point in Magna’s evolution as it expands its capabilities in the vehicle electrification space.
Magna has been an industry leader in vehicle structures, providing comprehensive engineering solutions globally.
Magna currently operates three technical facilities in Troy.
The company is hiring for managers, engineers, operators, and other roles at the new facility.

facts: German automotive supplier MollerTech announced it will invest $46.3 million to build a manufacturing facility in Bibb County, Alabama.
The project will create 222 jobs in Alabama’s auto sector.
Governor Robert Bentley stated that attracting investment and new jobs to rural counties is a key priority in the state's economic development strategy.
Governor Robert Bentley noted that MollerTech’s facility represents the type of project the state has been targeting.
Governor Robert Bentley mentioned new tools are available to help rural counties overcome economic development obstacles and expressed commitment to using them.
MollerTech plans to construct a 150,000-square-foot facility in the Scott G. Davis Industrial Park.
The facility site is located near Interstate 20/59 and close to the Woodstock community in northern Bibb County.
The facility will manufacture interior parts for Mercedes-Benz’s next-generation SUVs.
These SUVs will be produced at Mercedes-Benz’s Alabama assembly plant following a $1.3 billion expansion.
MollerTech North American President and CEO Steve Jordan stated the Bibb County facility will serve as the company’s flagship U.S. manufacturing facility.
The new facility will incorporate new ideas and best practices from other operations in the company’s global manufacturing footprint.
Steve Jordan said the company spent many months visiting various states and numerous counties in Alabama before deciding to settle in Bibb County.
Jordan stated MollerTech's culture is centered on people and local communities, finding a "good fit" with Bibb County.
Jordan noted that financial and personal help from the state and county helped influence the decision.
Jordan thanked the State of Alabama and Bibb County for their patience and support during the venture.
The project is codenamed "Helios".
The Birmingham region competed for the Helios project against other sites across the Southeast.

The Bibb County Commission, Alabama Department of Commerce, Economic Development Partnership of Alabama, Alabama Power Co., and Birmingham Business Alliance collaborated to bring MollerTech to the Birmingham region.
Greg Canfield, secretary of the Alabama Department of Commerce, joined company leaders and local officials at an announcement ceremony at the Bibb County Board of Education.
Bibb County provided 20 acres of land in the Scott G. Davis Industrial Park for the facility.
Bibb County provided an abatement of non-educational sales and use taxes and property taxes.
The state provided jobs and investment credits and AIDT workforce training, all valued at $12.3 million over a 15-year period.
Bibb County Commissioner Ricky Hubbard said the project will be a "great boost" for the county and its workforce.
Hubbard stated MollerTech will be the second largest manufacturing employer in the county and the third largest overall.
Hubbard expressed excitement that the investment will impact the lives of many local people.
MollerTech originated as a family copper trading and processing business in 1730.
This project represents the 18th German corporate investment in the seven-county Birmingham metro area, according to the Birmingham Business Alliance (BBA).
These 18 companies will employ approximately 1,700 people once MollerTech is operational.
BBA president and CEO Brian Hilson expressed excitement about MollerTech's future workforce and the impact of the 222 job opportunities on employees and their families.
MollerTech is the automotive arm of Germany-based MollerGroup, which remains a family-owned company with 2,300 employees worldwide.
Besides Mercedes-Benz, MollerTech develops and produces interior parts for automakers including Audi, BMW, General Motors, Honda, and Toyota.
MollerTech has existing U.S. facilities in Elberton, Georgia, and Shelby Township, Michigan.
The company hopes the Alabama facility, located just six miles from the Mercedes plant in Vance, will reach full production by the end of 2019.
source_title: MollerTech plans $46M Alabama auto parts plant with 222 jobs - Yellowhammer News

facts: A 42-year-old auto parts worker was killed at the Mollertech LLC plant in Shelby Township, Michigan when a five-ton piece of equipment fell on him.
The incident occurred while the worker was reportedly working on an overhead crane and was trapped underneath by a five-ton die.
Fire Chief James Swinkowski stated the cause is still under investigation regarding potential process failure.
Mollertech produces injection molded parts and accessories, such as dashboards, for car and truck interiors.
The plant opened in 1998 and operates as a non-union company employing approximately 140 people.
The facility is owned by MollerGroup GmbH, based in Bielefeld, Germany.
Mollertech supplies parts for Chrysler, BMW, Toyota, Honda, and Volkswagen.
The plant is located about 30 miles north of Detroit in an industrial park near Chrysler’s Sterling Heights assembly plant.

OSHA records show a single unannounced inspection opened in October 2006 citing four 'serious' violations and ten 'other' violations, with initial fines reduced from $3,600 to $1,800 after litigation.
In a 2003 lawsuit, Mollertech fired former employee Paul Bennett for requesting a 40-hour workweek under the ADA due to Crohn’s disease, claiming the request was unreasonable.

Bureau of Labor Statistics data shows automobile and light duty motor vehicle manufacturing workers experience higher injury and illness rates than most other industries, with a 2011 incidence rate nearly 40 percent higher than private industry overall.
Auto parts plants supplying Chrysler, General Motors, Ford, and other automakers face worse conditions due to cost-cutting pressures and just-in-time delivery schedules from Detroit automakers.
The 2009 auto industry restructuring under the Obama administration led to tens of thousands of job losses, dozens of factory shutdowns, and slashed wages, particularly for new hires.
Factories are running at full tilt with scheduled 10-12 hour shifts, eliminated break times, and increased assembly line speeds.
Cuts to OSHA under the Obama administration in 2011 included plans to sharply cut corporate regulations to eliminate constraints on profit margins.

The UAW's role in abandoning basic safety includes isolating strikes by parts workers since the 1980s, notably betraying 450 members of UAW Local 14 at AP Parts in Toledo, Ohio in 1984-85 after wage slashes from $12 to $6 an hour.
By 2000, auto parts workers earned 31 percent less than assembly workers at Big Three plants due to the rise of non-union plants and lowered wages for new hires.
source_title: Detroit area worker killed in auto parts plant - World Socialist Web Site
