facts: Nifco Inc. is a global leader in specialized plastic components, particularly for the automotive sector.
The company focuses on creating innovative solutions that enhance vehicle functionality and aesthetics.
Nifco's operations are centered around the design, manufacturing, and sale of high-quality plastic fasteners and precision components.
These products are essential for various applications, from vehicle interiors and exteriors to under-the-hood systems, contributing to lighter, more efficient, and durable vehicles.
The company also serves other industries like home appliances and industrial equipment.
For the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025, Nifco reported a notable 12% increase in operating profit, reaching 49,200 million yen.
This profitability was achieved despite a 5% decrease in net sales, which totaled 353,038 million yen.
Nifco produces a wide range of plastic fasteners and components, including trim clips, snap fit clips, and specialized items like breakaway grommets for pedestrian safety.
The primary focus is the automotive sector, supplying major manufacturers for interior, exterior, and under-the-hood applications.
Nifco utilizes high-precision molding technology for rapid development and mass production to meet diverse customer requirements efficiently.
With over 35 production plants in 17 countries, Nifco maintains a significant global presence that facilitates proximity to key customers and robust supply chain management.

The company's business model emphasizes continuous innovation to create plastic solutions offering reduced manufacturing labor, lower overall costs, decreased vehicle weight, enhanced product performance, and streamlined assembly processes.
Nifco differentiates itself through lightweight, rust-free, and easy-to-handle plastic components that improve product functionality and streamline manufacturing processes for customers.
Synthetic resin molded products form the backbone of Nifco's revenue and encompass a wide array of industrial plastic parts crucial for various manufacturing sectors.
A smaller business line focuses on products for the home furnishings market, diversifying income streams.
A significant portion of synthetic resin products caters to the automotive industry, driven by demand for lightweight, high-performance plastic parts.
Nifco maintains a global presence with significant revenue contributions from America, Japan, South Korea, and China.
The company employs a 'Co-Creation' approach, fostering close collaboration with customers to develop customized, high-value solutions.
Nifco monetizes through offering products with 'universal value,' such as enhanced safety, environmental benefits, and comfort, justifying premium pricing.
For the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025, consolidated net sales were 353,038 million yen.
The synthetic resin molded products business was the dominant revenue contributor, accounting for 315,942 million yen (approximately 89.49% of total revenue).
The bedding and furniture business added 37,100 million yen, representing 10.51% of sales.

Revenue breakdown by region for FY2025: Japan at 104,750 million yen (29.67%), America at 91,680 million yen (25.96%), South Korea at 44,830 million yen (12.70%), China at 41,310 million yen (11.70%), Other Asian regions at 39,830 million yen (11.28%), and Europe at 28,910 million yen (8.19%).
In September 2024, Nifco invested in LexxPluss, a company focused on automated transport systems, to develop advanced automation solutions for automotive maintenance.
Nifco is developing a biodegradable plastic fastener set for display at the 2025 Osaka-Kansai Japan Expo.

The company commercialized electromagnetic wave absorption panels in July 2024, designed for millimeter wave radar compatibility to address growing demand for advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS).
Strategic business transfers included the exclusion of Nifco Germany GmbH and Nifco KTW America Corporation to refine its global operational footprint and enhance efficiency.
Nifco has decades of expertise dating back to its pioneering role in plastic fasteners in 1967.
The company maintains a competitive edge through an extensive global network and advanced manufacturing capabilities.
Nifco holds a continuous 'AAA' rating in MSCI ESG Ratings as of May 2024 and has established self-delivering solar power plants.
The plastic fasteners market is expected to grow at a 6% CAGR from 2024 to 2031, fueled by demand for lighter vehicles and increased electronic integration.

Nifco faces risks including potential impacts from global automobile production uncertainties in late 2024, a subdued business environment in China, and fluctuations in raw material and electricity prices.
The 'Nifco Glocal Strategy' for FY2025-FY2027 focuses on strengthening existing businesses and pursuing new growth through 'Co-Creation.'
The company aims to deliver products that enhance safety, environmental responsibility, and comfort.

Nifco's strategic direction is guided by its Mid-Term Management Plan for FY2025-FY2027, emphasizing core business strengthening and cultivation of new growth avenues through strategic investments and collaboration.
The company's overarching vision is to 'Nifco makes Ideas a Reality' by addressing customer-specific needs and broader societal challenges.
For the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, Nifco forecasts consolidated net sales of 348,000 million yen and an operating profit of 49,500 million yen.
source_title: How Does Nifco Company Work? – MatrixBCG.com

facts: Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas’ Monroe, GA plant was recognized by The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) on December 1, 2016 as the inaugural winner of the Community Impact Award.

The first-ever Georgia Automotive Awards were awarded to companies and individuals in five unique categories: Company of the Year, Leader of the Year, Innovation Award, Supplier of the Year and Community Impact Award.

Paul Carroll, President and CEO of Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, stated that Georgia is a key market for Hitachi Automotive Systems and its work there is paramount to the overall success of the organization.

Carroll noted that the robust automotive industry and network in the state, combined with business-friendly tax incentives and an overall low cost of doing business, make Georgia an ideal location for Hitachi Automotive Systems to continue on its growth path.
The Community Impact Award was conferred upon Hitachi for strongly demonstrating a high level of corporate citizenship in and around the local community and/or within the state of Georgia.

The award cited Hitachi’s contributions and involvement with two community impact programs including F.I.S.H (Faith in Serving Humanity) and the Communities in Schools program, which are focused on combating child hunger and ensuring at-risk children remain in school.

Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. manufactures, remanufactures and markets a wide range of automotive systems including engine management systems, electric power train systems, drive control systems and car information systems for all major automotive original equipment manufacturers and aftermarket customers worldwide.
The company provides regional leadership in the Americas within the global operations of Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd.

Hitachi Automotive Systems maintains sites throughout North America with sales and engineering in Michigan serving as its headquarters and manufacturing facility in Kentucky, along with additional sales and manufacturing facilities in Kentucky, Georgia, California and Mexico.
Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd. is a wholly owned subsidiary of Hitachi, Ltd., headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. is a subsidiary of Hitachi America, Ltd.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and the lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging the expansion of existing industry and small businesses, aligning workforce education and training with in-demand jobs, locating new markets for Georgia products, attracting tourists to Georgia, and promoting the state as a destination for arts and location for film, music and digital entertainment projects.

facts: Fred Taylor relocated from Ellsworth, Michigan to Rome a little more than three decades ago.
Taylor opened his own business in the tillage tool industry providing items such as plows and disks upon moving to Rome with his wife, Mary Taylor, in 1983.
OTR Wheel Engineering is located at 6 Riverside Industrial Park in Rome.
The company expects to make more than $200 million in sales in 2017.
OTR has offices across the U.S. and the globe, including China, England, Sri Lanka, and France.
The business employs 125 workers in Floyd County and more than 600 worldwide.
After four years in Rome, Taylor purchased an Illinois-based company that made wheel and rim parts.
Taylor started the business with $5,000 in savings alongside Charles Jackson from Cave Spring.
In its first year, the company did $50,000 in business, made $10,000 in profit, and split $5,000 each.
During the second year, OTR did $300,000 in sales and hired its first employee, a truck driver.
In 1990, OTR hit $1 million in sales.
The company grew as much as 50 percent a year during the first few years.
Taylor and Jackson's goal was to reach $30 million a year in sales with a 10 percent pretax net profit, which they achieved in 1998.
In its 30th year (2017), OTR and its affiliate Green Carbon expect to do $220 million in sales.
The company reported annual double-digit growth until 2016, which was flat, but returned to double-digit growth in 2017.
Growth is attributed to developing new products and innovating industry-specific wheels, parts, and components for distribution to tire dealers throughout the country.
In 1994, OTR began working with tires, developing the 'Outrigger' tire for aerial work platforms (construction lifts), which is now on 90 percent of them around the world.
The number of wheel assembly manufacturers decreased from over a half-dozen to four.
In 1999, OTR partnered with Kubota Tractor to develop lawn mower tires, supplying the company's North American branch.
Supplying the all-terrain vehicle industry has been the primary driver of OTR's growth.
Honda is OTR's second-biggest customer behind Kubota for their side-by-side ATVs.
OTR holds about 20 percent of the lawn and garden market and only 5 percent of the RTV and ATV industry, leaving room for growth.
OTR secured a contract with Oshkosh for a series of newly developed tire and wheel assemblies.
The company also struck a deal with Arctic Cat.
OTR facilities in China make large forged wheels used on massive mining trucks.
Rome-based affiliate Green Carbon recycles mammoth tires into their original steel, carbon black, and petroleum products.
The headquarters plant is located in the old Celanese Industrial Park.
OTR purchased most of the old Galey & Lord mill in Shannon, with production set to begin later in 2017.
About a year and a half prior to 2017, OTR bought the old Spears Mattress plant in the Celanese complex to use as a warehouse for after-market sales products.
After-market sales doubled in the two years leading up to 2017.
Charles Jackson was elected to the Cave Spring City Council in November and makes sales calls 8-10 times a month across the country and Europe.
source_title: Rome’s OTR Wheel Engineering on a roll | AP News

facts: PPG Industries Inc. is hiring for a Plant Operations Manager position at its Aerospace Transparency manufacturing site in Huntsville, Alabama.

The manager will provide back-up support to the plant manager from a global operation perspective as needed by the business.

facts: The Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) Shielding Market is gaining momentum as the rapid proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs), advanced consumer electronics, aerospace systems, and industrial automation increases the need for reliable electromagnetic compatibility (EMC).
The global EMI shielding market was valued at USD 8.04 billion in 2026 and is projected to reach USD 10.90 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 5.2% during the forecast period.

Leading companies such as Parker Hannifin, Laird Performance Materials, 3M, Henkel, PPG Industries, and RTP Company are advancing innovative shielding materials, conductive coatings, gaskets, tapes, and metal-based solutions that protect sensitive electronic components from electromagnetic interference.

Manufacturers are focusing on lightweight composite materials that deliver high shielding effectiveness without increasing product weight—an increasingly important consideration in electric vehicles and aerospace systems.
The transition toward electric mobility is creating substantial opportunities for EMI shielding manufacturers.
Electric vehicles contain numerous high-voltage components and electronic control systems that generate electromagnetic emissions.
Effective EMI shielding ensures reliable operation of these systems while preventing signal disruption and improving passenger safety.
With global EV production expected to increase significantly over the coming decade, automotive applications are becoming one of the fastest-growing segments within the EMI shielding market.
Electrification is driving adoption of shielding technologies across automotive electronics and battery systems.
Fluctuations in the prices of metals such as copper, aluminum, and silver can affect production costs.
Miniaturized electronics require increasingly sophisticated shielding solutions capable of balancing performance, size, and weight.

Emerging technologies such as conductive nanomaterials, graphene-based composites, lightweight metallic foams, and multifunctional shielding materials are expected to improve shielding performance while reducing weight and manufacturing costs.
Companies investing in advanced materials, sustainable manufacturing processes, and application-specific shielding technologies will be well positioned to capitalize on future market opportunities.
source_title: EMI Shielding Market Growth Driven by 3M, Parker Hannifin & Laird Performance Materials

facts: PPG Industries, Inc. is the largest coatings company in the world by revenue.
The company operates a facility in East Point, Georgia that specializes in small batch production of water and oil-based coatings.
The East Point plant pre-assembles, mixes, fills, packages, and ships 3 million gallons of coatings each year.
The plant has the potential to produce above current levels and reduce its order delays.
The facility's current customer satisfaction rate is below the company-wide target of 98%.
PPG East Point identified pre-assembly (PA) as the process bottleneck of the plant.
Pre-assembly is the first step in the paint manufacturing process, and delays in this stage contribute to 47% of delayed batches.
The PA bottleneck is caused by limited indoor raw material storage capacity, which necessitates using external trailers for raw material storage.
Frequently used raw materials are often stored in hard-to-access locations at the facility, leading to delays and errors.
A Georgia Tech ISYE senior design capstone project was initiated to decrease the cycle time of the pre-assembly process and the plant's entire production chain.
The project team conducted three site visits to assess the capacity of the current raw materials warehouse and identify additional storage locations.
Increasing indoor storage capacity allows operators to access and retrieve materials quickly while reducing holding costs for existing inventory.
The team used an optimization model to create storage guidelines based on each material’s frequency of use.
Materials used most commonly are designated to be stored in the most easily accessible locations to reduce retrieval time and establish a consistent storage policy.
The team used Simio software to create a simulation model of the raw material warehouse and pre-assembly process to validate design strategies.

Three versions of the simulation were created: (1) the current system, (2) the proposed system after implementing storage redesign recommendations, and (3) the proposed system after additionally implementing an optimal storage policy.
The simulation model was validated by comparing outputs from the current system to data collected from the plant.
Key metrics compared across simulation versions included the time to pre-assemble a batch and the number of batch pre-assemblies completed per day.
The team identified four locations within the warehouse that could serve as additional bay storage for raw materials, assigning each a specific number of pallets.
Four separate maps were created detailing how to design each new location to hold its assigned pallet count and how to allocate materials from external trailers into the new rooms.
The warehouse redesign eliminates time-consuming raw material pulls from external storage, thereby reducing pre-assembly time.
An optimization tool was built to reallocate materials based on frequency of use using a cost-free, continuous Google Colaboratory model.
The optimization tool inputs consist of Python code and an Excel sheet that can be updated with locations and materials.
The optimization tool requires no technical knowledge to run and does not store client data on the cloud, mitigating privacy concerns.
The optimization tool contributes to reducing pre-assembly time by decreasing the time operators spend searching for raw materials.
source_title: PPG Warehouse Layout and Operations Design | Senior Design Capstone Projects

facts: PPG Industries is a global supplier of paints, coatings and specialty materials that serves industrial, transportation, consumer and construction markets.

Founded in 1883 as the Pittsburgh Plate Glass Company, PPG has evolved from its origins in glass manufacturing into a diversified coatings and materials company headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.
The company develops and manufactures a broad array of products used to protect and enhance surfaces, from consumer paints to highly engineered coatings for demanding industrial applications.

PPG’s product portfolio includes architectural and decorative paints, automotive original equipment and refinish coatings, industrial coatings for machinery and equipment, protective and marine coatings, aerospace and defense coatings, and packaging coatings and materials.
The company also provides color and design services, technical support, application systems and coatings-related services for manufacturers, distributors, contractors and retailers.
Its operations encompass research and development centers, manufacturing facilities and distribution networks aimed at delivering tailored coating solutions and color consistency for customers.
PPG operates on a global scale, with manufacturing, sales and technical support spanning the Americas, Europe, Asia, the Middle East and Africa.
The company sells to a wide range of end markets including automotive, aerospace, marine, industrial manufacturers, commercial builders and retail consumers.
As of mid-2024, PPG is led by Chief Executive Officer Michael H. McGarry.

Strategically, PPG places emphasis on innovation, sustainability and application expertise, investing in new coating chemistries, color technologies and formulation improvements that address durability, appearance and environmental regulations.

Its offerings target performance and lifecycle benefits such as corrosion resistance, chemical and UV protection, weight reduction opportunities in transportation, and efficient application systems for contractor and industrial customers.
source_title: PPG Industries (PPG) Stock Price, News & Analysis

facts: Rivian received conditional approval for an up to $6.6 billion loan from the U.S. Department of Energy.
The loan is likely to be deployed towards a planned factory in Georgia.
Rivian Automotive Inc (NASDAQ:RIVN) received conditional approval from the DOE’s Advanced Technology Vehicle Manufacturing (ATVM) loan program.
The loan consists of a principal amount of $6 billion and $600 million in capital interest.
The loan is set to offer more support for the company’s upcoming midsize platform, which consists of smaller, less expensive SUVs including the R2 and R3 crossovers.
The midsize vehicles will be produced at the Georgia facility.
Production at the Georgia facility is expected to start from 2028.
Rivian will build the facility in two phases.
Each phase will provide an annual production capacity of 200,000 units.
The EV maker is grappling with a cash crunch amid sluggish sales, disrupted production and rising expenses.
Rivian had paused construction of the Georgia plant earlier this year.
The DOE’s ATVM program has provided funding to several other major automakers, including bigger EV rival Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA).
Tuesday’s announcement comes just days after Rivian clinched a $5.8 billion investment from German automaker Volkswagen (ETR:VOWG_p).
Volkswagen attempts to use Rivian’s technology in making its own EVs.
source_title: Rivian gets conditional approval for $6.6 bln govt loan to build Georgia factory

facts: Five years after its first vehicle drive, Rivian has delivered a new model.
Rivian’s first two vehicles are the R1T electric truck and R1S SUV.
The 2027 Rivian R2 Performance is smaller and cheaper than its predecessors.
The R2 benchmarks against bestsellers like the Tesla Model Y, Toyota RAV4, and Subaru Outback.
The R2 benchmarks against sport luxury models like the BMW X3.
The R2 benchmarks against hardcore off-roaders like the Jeep Wrangler and Ford Bronco.
Rivian expects to conquest sales from these benchmarked vehicles.
Three variations of the R2 are planned for the next year.
Options across trims are generally limited to colors, wheels, and software.
The base R2 Standard starts in the mid-$40K range.
The R2 Standard offers between 275 and 345 miles of range.
The R2 Standard comes standard with a single rear motor producing 350 hp.
The midgrade R2 Premium features a 400-volt, 87.9-kWh battery.
The R2 Premium offers 330 miles of range.
The R2 Premium includes dual-motor all-wheel drive.
The R2 Premium provides 100 more horsepower than the base model.
The R2 Premium includes slightly ritzier interior materials.
The R2 Premium features a Toyota 4Runner–style lowerable rear glass.
The R2 Performance trim is currently on sale.
The R2 Performance produces 656 hp and 609 lb-ft of torque.
The R2 Performance includes a semi-active electronically adaptive suspension.
The R2 Performance features unique off-road drive modes.
All-terrain tires are available for the R2 Performance.
Installing all-terrain tires drops the R2 Performance's range from 330 to 307 miles.
RAD-tuned versions of the R2 will hit the trails later.
All early 2027 R2 Performance models include the Launch package.
The Launch package includes Rivian’s Autonomy+ driver assist system, a tow hitch, and a physical key fob.
The R2 Performance with the Launch package is priced at $59,485.
The R2 is considered important to Rivian's bottom line.
The EV marketplace is described as starved of real choice.
source_title: 2027 Rivian R2 First Drive: Smaller, Cheaper, Still a Rivian

facts: Rivian Automotive plans to raise nearly $15 billion in debt to help build an electric vehicle manufacturing plant in Georgia.
The taxable bonds will be issued by the Georgia Department of Economic Development and the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton and Walton Counties per an agreement on Nov. 9.
Rivian has agreed to purchase the bonds as they are issued.
The company has agreed to pay a minimum of nearly $300 million in property tax payments through 2047.
Property tax payments will increase if the carmaker exceeds its $5 billion investment.
Rivian announced in 2021 that it plans to set up the Georgia plant.
At the time of the 2021 announcement, Rivian stated the plant would be commissioned by 2024.
The new plant will employ more than 7,500 people.
The new plant will eventually build 400,000 vehicles a year.
source_title: Rivian to raise nearly $15 billion in debt for EV plant in Georgia

facts: Rivian delivered 12,194 vehicles in the second quarter, exceeding its own Q2 outlook of 9,000 to 11,000.
Rivian raised its full-year delivery target to 65,000 to 70,000 vehicles, up from a previous range of 62,000 to 67,000.
Rivian delivered 10,365 vehicles in the first quarter, bringing total first-half deliveries to 22,559.
Reaching the low end of the new full-year delivery range requires approximately 42,000 deliveries in the second half, nearly doubling the first-half pace.
The company is simultaneously scaling an entirely new R2 model alongside this steep second-half delivery ramp.
Rivian's R2 is a new, lower-priced vehicle designed for volume sales, distinct from its premium-priced R1 trucks and SUVs.
In the first quarter, Rivian's revenue rose 11% year over year to $1.38 billion.
Rivian generated $119 million in gross profit in the first quarter, representing a 9% gross margin.
The software and services segment produced $181 million in gross profit in the first quarter.

The automotive segment ran a $62 million gross loss in the first quarter, primarily due to a $100 million year-over-year decline in sales of automotive regulatory credits and lower production volumes.
Rivian's first-quarter operating loss widened to $881 million from $655 million a year earlier.
Rivian raised the initial production capacity planned for its Georgia plant by 50%, to 300,000 vehicles annually.
The Georgia plant project is backed by an up to $4.5 billion Department of Energy loan.
A completed testing milestone in March unlocked a $1 billion investment from Volkswagen Group.
Rivian currently loses money on every vehicle it sells, with automotive gross profit expected to improve as R2 volumes build.
The second-quarter financial report is due July 30 and will serve as the first checkpoint for automotive gross profit improvement.
source_title: Rivian Just Raised Its 2026 Outlook While Tesla Stock Stumbled. Is the Electric Vehicle Underdog Finally a Buy? | The Motley Fool

facts: Rivian plans to cut around 600 jobs, representing approximately 4.5 percent of its workforce.
The layoff figure comes from an internal memo sent to employees that was seen by various US media outlets.
The internal memo did not give a specific number, but the Wall Street Journal reported the layoffs would affect more than 600 employees.
At the end of 2024, Rivian employed just under 15,000 people, meaning 4.5 percent equals 675 jobs.
The job cuts will mainly be achieved through restructuring in the marketing, vehicle operations, sales/delivery, and mobile operations teams.
It is not yet known how the job cuts will be distributed among those teams.
Development and production are not expected to be affected by the layoffs.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated the cuts were made due to a changing operating backdrop and to rethink how the company is scaling its go-to-market functions.
Scaringe noted the changes aim to ensure the company can deliver on its potential by scaling efficiently toward building a healthy and profitable business.
The new and slightly smaller R2 model is expected to go into production in 2026.
Until the R2 contributes to rising sales and potentially profits, the start of production will initially cost more money.
In the second quarter, Rivian had a deficit of $1.1 billion on its balance sheet.
In the third quarter, Rivian recorded rising sales of 13,201 vehicles, which is a 32 percent increase.
The Q3 sales increase is likely due to a one-off effect surrounding the end of the US tax credit.
In Q3 2025, many car manufacturers set new electric vehicle records due to subsidy expiry and resulting uncertainty in the US market for electric cars.
Scaringe cited the subsidy expiry and resulting uncertainty as the likely 'changing conditions' prompting the layoffs.
Despite strong Q3 sales, Rivian now expects 2025 sales of only 41,500 to 43,500 vehicles.
The revised 2025 sales forecast is down from Rivian's previous forecast of 46,000 vehicles.
source_title: Rivian to cut around 600 jobs - electrive.com

facts: Previously, Rivian offered two battery options: Large with 135 kWh and Max with 149 kWh.
Rivian has introduced two new entry-level battery options called Standard and Standard+.
The Standard battery pack offers 106 kWh of capacity with a range of up to 270 miles (435 kilometres).
The Standard+ battery pack offers 121 kWh of capacity with a range of up to 315 miles (507 kilometres).
The published range figures are Rivian's values and not official EPA figures.
The entry-level price for both EVs has been reduced by about $3,100 due to the new battery options.
The Rivian R1T pickup truck starts at $69,900 with a dual-motor AWD and the Standard battery pack.
The Rivian R1T pickup truck costs $73,000 with the Standard+ battery pack.
The Rivian R1S electric SUV now starts at $84,000.
Customers can choose the “Performance Dual-Motor AWD” option for an extra $5,000 (plus $3,100) when selecting the Standard+ battery pack, though this option is not available for the Standard variant.
Rivian recently started offering the R1S for leasing in the US, allowing customers to benefit from the full $7,500 tax credit which is unavailable when purchasing the vehicle.
The US carmaker sold 57,232 electric vehicles in 2023, exceeding its revised annual target of 54,000 units.
Rivian currently builds the R1T Pickup, the R1S SUV, and the Electric Delivery Van (EDV) for Amazon on a separate production line.
Rivian received final approval from relevant authorities in November 2023 to build its second plant.
The new factory in Georgia will be able to produce 200,000 electric vehicles per year in its first expansion stage and is scheduled to go into operation in 2024.
An expansion of the Georgia plant to 400,000 electric vehicles per year will be completed by 2030.
source_title: Rivian introduces new entry-level battery options - electrive.com

facts: Rivian announced receiving an incentive package totaling $827 million from the US state of Illinois to expand its existing plant in Normal.
The expanded Normal plant will produce premium vehicles including the R1T pickup, R1S SUV, Rivian delivery vans, and the new electric mid-range SUV R2.
Rivian is putting the construction of a planned second plant in Georgia on hold for the time being.
Following the Normal plant expansion, total production capacity is expected to reach 215,000 vehicles per year.
Of the expanded capacity, the new R2 model will account for 155,000 units annually.
The current plant capacity prior to expansion is expected to be 150,000 units per year.
Rivian did not specify a completion date for the Normal plant expansion in its Q1 report.
The electric mid-range R2 SUV is scheduled to launch on the market in 2026.
Rivian continues to pursue a goal with Amazon to put 100,000 “EDV” electric delivery vans on the roads for the online delivery giant.
Rivian is also selling its delivery van to third parties under the name ‘RCV’.
Rivian's turnover in Q1 2024 totaled $1.2 billion, primarily driven by the delivery of 13,588 electric cars.
The company reported a net loss of approximately $1.45 billion for January to March 2024 due to high investments, representing an increase of about $100 million compared to the same period last year.
Rivian announced in April 2024 that it produced significantly fewer electric vehicles in Q1 2024 than in the previous quarter.
Rivian maintains its production forecast of 57,000 units for all of 2024.
This 2024 forecast matches the 2023 full-year output of 57,232 vehicles.
In Q1 2024, Rivian produced 13,980 vehicles and delivered 13,588 units.
Vehicle deliveries fell by 384 units in Q1 2024 compared to Q4 2023.
Production output declined by 3,561 vehicles in Q1 2024 compared to Q4 2023.
In Q4 2023, Rivian produced 17,541 electric cars and delivered 13,972 units.
In Q2 2023, Rivian recorded 13,992 vehicles produced and 12,640 deliveries.
In Q3 2023, Rivian recorded 16,304 vehicles produced and 15,564 deliveries.
Production declined by 20.3% from Q4 2023 to Q1 2024.
Deliveries decreased by 2.7% from Q4 2023 to Q1 2024.
Rivian currently produces the R1T pickup, R1S SUV, and EDV for Amazon on a separate production line.
The company does not provide a breakdown of production volumes by model series in its reports.
Financially, 2024 is expected to be a challenging year for Rivian.
In 2023, Rivian's net loss totaled $5.4 billion (€4.98 billion).
Within three years, Rivian has accumulated total losses of $16.8 billion.
The manufacturer has expanded drive options for the R1 models downward over time and introduced the smaller R2 in March.
Rivian teased an even more compact R3 model alongside the R2 launch.
Increasingly affordable vehicles are expected to pave the way for Rivian to enter the mass market.
source_title: Rivian plans to produce 155,000 units of the R2 per year - electrive.com

facts: Rivian Automotive renegotiated a $6.57 billion loan from the U.S. Department of Energy down to $4.5 billion.
Rivian is adjusting production expectations at an under-construction plant in Georgia.
The DOE loan was previously set to support two phases of production for a total of 400,000 units annually.
The amended loan covers one phase of production with a total capacity of 300,000 vehicles.
The changes enable Rivian to draw on the loan sooner and have greater initial production but lowers its total production capacity for the plant amid uncertain demand for all-electric vehicles.
The initial loan terms were negotiated under the Biden administration.
It had been in limbo under the Trump administration, which has taken action to cut or reduce such loans and has pulled back government investments to promote EVs.
Rivian plans to tap into the loan in 2027, a year ahead of previously scheduled.
Production of the company’s upcoming R2 electric vehicle is on track to begin at the facility in late 2028.
The automaker recently started production at its current facility in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe stated that any future expansion of the Georgia plant would be funded by the company.
Rivian has been raising capital through partnerships with companies such as Volkswagen and Uber.
The EV maker announced the new loan details in connection with its first-quarter results, which included a net loss of $416 million, or 33 cents per share.
The first-quarter net loss compares to a loss of $541 million, or 48 cents per share, a year ago.
Those per-share results were not comparable to Wall Street expectations.
Rivian’s revenue for the quarter was $1.38 billion, up from $1.24 billion a year earlier and slightly ahead of the $1.36 billion expected by analysts.
The company’s gross profit was $119 million, down $87 million during the first quarter compared with a year earlier.
Gross profit included a $62 million loss for its automotive segment and a $181 million profit for its software and services division.
The decline in automotive profit was primarily due to a $100 million slump in sales of automotive regulatory credits and lower production volumes.
source_title: Rivian renegotiates DOE loan down to $4.5 billion, adjusts capacity plans for Georgia plant – Metapress

facts: Rivian is an electric truck maker whose IPO in November was the largest of 2021 and the sixth-largest in U.S. history.
Less than two weeks ago, Rivian was responsible for quarterly losses reported by both Amazon and Ford Motor Co., each of which had invested billions only to see Rivian's value crash early this year.
This week, Rivian's stock price tumbled further on a report that Ford was getting rid of 8 million shares in the company.
Rivian is newly caught up in the Georgia governor's race over a record incentive package for a new manufacturing plant.
Rivian has an order from Amazon for 100,000 delivery vans, and more than 80,000 other orders.
As it burst onto the scene at an event tied to the L.A. Auto Show in 2018, Rivian was seen as a potential rival to Tesla.
Ford has been interested in battery development with Rivian, and at one point planned to jointly develop a pickup.
In March, Rivian acknowledged significant production challenges because of supply chain problems that have plagued automakers.
Rivian said it would be able to make only 25,000 vehicles this year.
Rivian's initial public offering last year raised $11.9 billion.
The share price hit a peak of $172.01 on Nov. 16.
At the end of the year, that was down to $103.69.
The stock has tumbled further since then, closing the first quarter at $50.24, costing Amazon $7.6 billion in the quarter and Ford $5.5 billion.
Rivian closed Tuesday at $22.64, down 21% for the week.
The "lockup" period for early investors and company insiders expired Sunday, and CNBC reported that Ford was set to sell 8 million of its 102 million shares.
Ford declined to comment Monday regarding the share sale.
Ford would sell its shares through Goldman Sachs, the report said, while JPMorgan Chase also planned to sell between 13 million and 15 million shares for an unknown investor.
Amazon owns about 17.7% of Rivian, while Ford owns 11.4%.
Amazon was not expected to sell, Bloomberg reported.

After Georgia and local governments this month approved $1.5 billion in incentives for Rivian to build a 7,500-job, $5 billion electric vehicle plant east of Atlanta, former Sen. David Purdue criticized the deal, the Associated Press reported.
Purdue is running against Gov. Brian Kemp in the May 24 GOP gubernatorial primary, with the winner likely facing Democrat Stacey Abrams for the governor's office in the general election.
Perdue has emphasized that "liberal billionaire George Soros" is an investor in Rivian.
“Think about how many small businesses in Georgia could be helped for this kind of money, instead of padding George Soros’ pockets,” Perdue said.
“Kemp gave away the farm to a woke corporation for something the locals don't even want, and hardworking Georgians are left footing the bill.”
Kemp, during a debate Sunday, reiterated his backing for Rivian.
“I support 7,500 great-paying jobs going to rural Georgia, to an automobile manufacturing facility,” Kemp said.
source_title: Onetime EV darling Rivian hit by report of Ford stock sale, Georgia governor's race fight

facts: Rivian recently began selling its new R2 vehicle.
The R2 lineup consists of smaller SUVs that are part of a larger plan to gradually roll out a lower-priced vehicle to expand Rivian's customer base.
The new R2 starts around $58,000 right now.
By late 2027, a base version of the vehicle will be on sale for just $45,000.
The $45,000 price means the R2 will cost roughly $4,000 less than the average new vehicle price.
Rivian needs to sell a lot of R2s, share parts and technology across its vehicle lineup, and produce vehicles efficiently to generate recurring profits.
Rivian has retooled its R1S and R1T vehicles to reduce parts and equipment.
Rivian has improved sourcing to lower costs and lowered other expenses.
Rivian formed a joint venture with Volkswagen in 2024.
Under the joint venture, Rivian provides software to Volkswagen in return for funding.
The joint venture deal helped Rivian report $1.6 billion in sales from its software and services segment last year.
The joint venture deal helped Rivian report $576 million in gross profit from its software and services segment last year.
The software and services segment performance helped Rivian achieve two quarters of gross profitability.
R2 sales and production are currently underway.
Rivian delivered 12,194 vehicles in the first quarter.
First-quarter deliveries outpaced Rivian's guidance of 9,000 to 11,000 vehicles.
Rivian raised its production guidance for 2026 from a previous estimate of between 62,000 and 67,000 to a new range of 65,000 to 70,000.
Many traditional automakers have backed away from ambitious EV plans because government tax incentives for buying an electric vehicle were prematurely eliminated.
Rising consumer costs due to tariffs and high interest rates are dampening sales.
Many automakers are releasing more hybrid models, which have proved appealing to car buyers and have likely cut into some EV sales.
source_title: Should You Buy Rivian While It's Below $20? | The Motley Fool

facts: Saft America, Inc. operates a manufacturing production facility located at 711 Gil Harbin Industrial Blvd., Valdosta, Georgia 31601, United States.
The company produces nickel-cadmium (Ni-Cd) and lithium-ion (Li-ion) aircraft battery solutions.
Saft's batteries provide high-peak-power for engine or auxiliary power unit (APU) starting and emergency backup power.

The company maintains in-house development and production capabilities.

Each battery design is optimized for energy and power.
The batteries guarantee integration with the electrical architecture of all fixed-wing and helicopter aircraft.

facts: Saft America Inc. is based in Valdosta, Georgia.
Saft America Inc. has won a contract worth up to $7.5 million to provide storage batteries for H1, Seahawk, and CH-53 helicopters.
The contract is a firm-fixed-price, indefinite-quantity agreement, meaning the price is set but the number of batteries needed can vary.
This deal was open to competition, and two companies submitted offers.

Work must be finished by April 24, 2028.

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia was designed and built on a 64-acre property with the intent to be expanded.
The construction team conducted mass grading onsite, added five retention ponds, concrete paving for the loading dock and truck court, asphalt parking lots, a fire road loop, and landscaping.

The facility includes two 3,000-amp main distribution panels and a robust fire loop system to support the current building and future expansion.
Manufacturing facility staff and visitors are greeted by a two-story open-concept lobby with an eye-catching glass staircase.
The spacious office area includes two multi-function conference rooms, open office spaces, a cafeteria, bathrooms, and locker rooms.

The manufacturing project includes six production lines, consisting of two for fabrication and four for assembly.
There are seven loading docks designated for the company's fleet of vehicles.
The team constructed racking systems equipped with full automated picking systems.
Dedicated electrical circuits were installed specifically for charging EV forklifts and tuggers.
An Early Suppression Fast Response (ESFR) fire sprinkler system was installed with a dedicated transformer for the fire pump.
A gas generator was installed to serve as a backup for emergency systems.
The team constructed a processed water system for cooling and cleaning all products and equipment.
A compressed air system was built to service the production lines and paint shop.
source_title: Seohan Auto Georgia Manufacturing Plant | Projects | Choate Construction

entity_name: Georgia Right to Work Economic Development & Job Creation

facts: Meissner Corporation is investing $250 million in order to expand and create 1,700 new jobs in Athens-Clarke County, Georgia.
According to Area Development Magazine, Meissner provides critical filtration equipment that benefits other key industries in Georgia, including life sciences and food processing.
The addition of Meissner is expected to complement the state's industry mix while diversifying the local economy in Athens-Clarke County.
Seohan Auto Georgia is investing $72 million and creating 180 new jobs in order to add a new facility in Liberty County, Georgia.
According to Area Development Magazine, Seohan Auto Georgia believes Georgia will be the center of the EV industry and a new frontier for its future with limitless opportunities.
Officials note that the ongoing transition to e-mobility and sustainable technology creates a window of opportunity for companies and states that remain on the forefront of this developing industry.

State officials stated they are pleased that Seohan Auto Georgia selected Liberty County as their location, describing the company and its affiliated enterprises as responsible, thorough, and progressive organizations striving to advance innovative technology.
Zoetis is investing $100 million in order to add a new location and create 150 new jobs in Douglasville, Georgia.

According to Area Development Magazine, the Douglasville facility provides an opportunity to expand production and supply capacity while benefiting the local economy by adding new jobs to serve veterinarians, livestock producers, pet owners, and their animals.

Local economic development leaders state that Zoetis choosing Douglasville for its new southeast facility is a testament to their commitment to attracting new investment and quality jobs, opening new avenues for recruiting additional life science and health technology companies.
Altogether, the three announced investments will create 2,030 new jobs in Georgia.
The page attributes the creation of these jobs to incentives created by Georgia's Right to Work laws.
source_title: Thousands of Jobs for Right to Work Georgia - NRTWC

facts: South Korean automotive supplier Kamtec Auto USA announced this week it will open its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Auburn.
The new operation represents a $9.2 million investment.
The new operation will create about 50 jobs over the next three years.
The facility will provide advanced technical components for engine control and electric management in hybrid vehicles.
Alabama Secretary of Commerce Ellen McNair said, “We are excited to welcome Kamtec to Auburn and honored to be its first U.S. site.”
Alabama Secretary of Commerce Ellen McNair stated, “We’re confident Kamtec will find the skilled workforce and ready community partners needed to be successful.”

Alabama Secretary of Commerce Ellen McNair said, “This is a win for Alabama and a great example of how automotive manufacturers in our state create opportunities for our communities by capturing follow-up investments by their suppliers.”
Kamtec was established in 2001.
Kamtec is an affiliate of the Seohan Group headquartered in Seoul.
The Seohan Group has operated in Auburn since 2007.
Kamtec will initially operate from a leased facility in Auburn.
Kamtec plans to relocate to a newly constructed dedicated manufacturing facility to be built by Seohan Auto USA.
Auburn Mayor Ron Anders said, “On behalf of the Auburn City Council, I want to thank Kamtec leadership for selecting Auburn as the home for its new manufacturing operation.”
Mayor Ron Anders stated, “We recognize that private investment and the creation of quality jobs are essential to the long-term economic health of our community.”

Mayor Ron Anders noted, “Kamtec’s commitment to innovation and eco-friendly solutions aligns well with Auburn residents’ values around sustainability as well as the engineering expertise at Auburn University that is helping to shape our future.”
Mayor Ron Anders said, “It is my pleasure to welcome the Kamtec team to Auburn.”

Kamtec Auto USA President Chang-Yeong Seo said Auburn’s proximity to Hyundai Motor’s plant in Montgomery and Kia’s plant in West Point, Georgia, will allow the company to manufacture and supply its products locally for the U.S. market.
Kamtec Auto USA President Chang-Yeong Seo said, “We look forward to partnering with Auburn University to identify talent for our technical positions.”

Kamtec Auto USA President Chang-Yeong Seo said, “We are deeply grateful for the unwavering support of the City of Auburn and the State of Alabama, and our goal is to contribute to the balanced, sustainable growth of our customers, partners, employees and the communities in which we operate.”

links.context: Future job postings for the new facility

source_title: Kamtec Auto USA to bring $9.2 million investment, 50 jobs to Auburn - Yellowhammer News

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia plans to invest $72 million in a new manufacturing plant in Midway, Georgia.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp called Hyundai’s plant the “largest economic development project in state history.”

Georgia officials had predicted Hyundai’s Metaplant would attract offsite suppliers to invest an estimated $1 billion.

Gov. Kemp stated: “The Hyundai Metaplant is and will continue to be an economic driver for the entire region, creating more jobs for hardworking Georgians in communities like Liberty County.”

Since January, Ecoplastic Corporation, Seoyon E-Hwa, Sewon America and PHA have each announced plans for manufacturing plants to supply Hyundai’s manufacturing hub.

facts: SKF was founded in Gothenburg, Sweden, in 1907 by Swedish engineer Sven Gustaf Wingqvist, who invented the self-aligning ball bearing.
The company provides an extensive range of products and services in rolling bearings, seals, lubrication, and mechatronics.
SKF supports customers across aerospace, medical, semiconductor, automotive, and energy sectors.
SKF maintains a US manufacturing site in Sumter, South Carolina.
At the Sumter site, complete production of thin-section bearings was implemented on three automated DMG MORI DMU 340 FD machines.
DMG MORI integrated grinding and gear milling by means of gearSKIVING at the facility.
Autonomous and process-reliable tool handling is provided by TH-AGV at the Sumter site.
Marcus Jakob, Director of Operations at SKF in Sumter, stated that their products enable precise, low friction operation of rotating equipment and machine elements.
Jakob noted that a focus on reduced energy consumption for customers puts exciting challenges ahead.
SKF continuously invests in the further development of its rolling bearings, slewing rings, and seals.
Customer demands include tighter tolerances, reduced cost, and higher performance, requiring continuous production adaptation.
To stay ahead of competition, SKF depends on high-precision multi-tasking machining centers and efficient manufacturing processes.
The three DMU 340 FDs were selected for their machining versatility through technology integration.
The XXL machining centers are equipped with 5-axis milling and turning in one workspace, plus exclusive DMG MORI technology cycles for Grinding and gearSKIVING.
This allows all machining steps to be performed on any of the models for which at least two additional machines were previously needed.
There has been a drastic reduction in throughput times in grinding and gear milling.
SKF maximizes machine tool utilization by eliminating waiting times that were previously unavoidable when changing to different machines.
Setup of components was accelerated with the help of robotic automation from Fastems.

The automation model was based on a plant established by Jakob in his previous role as Aerospace Process Development Manager involving robot-assisted production of smaller bearing rings for the aerospace sector.
In Sumter, SKF extended this principle to XXL machining while adding best in class workholding and automation principles.
Automation ensures continuous manufacturing operation and maximizes machining center utilization, allowing the team to fully concentrate on preparing new orders and quality control.
Tool management addressed a challenge of 60 tool changes per shift, which is a huge, time consuming task.
The TH-AGV ensures autonomous transport of all tools to the respective machine.
The TH-AGV can accommodate tools weighing up to 50 kg with diameters of 400 mm and lengths of 380 mm.
The maximum loading weight with 16 tools is 480 kg.
Safety for people and machines is ensured by scanners and sensors that permanently monitor the AGV travel path.
SKF uses RFID chips in central tool management to assign tools to orders and machines.
End-to-end digitization from tool presetting to machining ensures no incorrect tools are changed nor incorrect offsets entered into the controller.
The optimized processes at the Sumter site serve as a good role model within the entire SKF Group, with potential future implementation on a DMU 210 FD in France.
SKF USA Inc. is located at 925 Corporate Circle, Sumter, 29154 South Carolina, USA.
source_title: SKF – Customer Story | DMG MORI - DMG MORI Australia and New Zealand

facts: SKF USA Inc. announced it will be a major associate sponsor of the Penske Racing teams beginning with the 2012 racing season.
The SKF brand will be featured in the NASCAR Sprint Cup Series, the NASCAR Nationwide Series, and the IZOD IndyCar Series in 2012.
SKF is entering its 15th year as a major associate sponsor of auto racing in North America.
In 2012, SKF will be featured on the No. 2 Miller Lite Dodge driven by Brad Keselowski and the No. 22 Shell Pennzoil Dodge driven by Kurt Busch in the Sprint Cup Series.
In 2012, SKF will be featured on the No. 12 Alliance Truck Parts Dodge driven by Sam Hornish Jr. in the Nationwide Series.
In 2012, SKF will be featured on the No. 3 Team Penske IndyCar driven by three-time Indianapolis 500 Champion Helio Castroneves.
Poul Jeppesen, president of SKF North America, stated that Penske Racing's reputation for quality, preparation, and consistency matches SKF's products and services.
Roger Penske stated that SKF's technical knowledge and specialized mechanical expertise will significantly benefit the teams' ability to win across all racing categories.
Since 1947, SKF has partnered with Formula One’s Scuderia Ferrari, creating the longest technical supplier relationship in the sport.
The Scuderia Ferrari F1 machines contain over 150 SKF components designed and manufactured exclusively by SKF.
SKF maintains a close racing partnership with Ducati.
source_title: SKF to Sponsor Penske Racing Teams in 2012 - AftermarketNews

facts: Mohawk closed its Dahlonega carpet fiber plant in spring, where some 366 were employed, including two for more than 50 years.
The former Mohawk facility covered 35 acres and occupied a 365,000-square-foot building now available for $3.5 million.
Lumpkin County formed the North Georgia Network (NGN) with Forsyth, Dawson, White, and Union counties to run fiber-optic cable up Highway 400 to the North Carolina state line.
The NGN received a $240,000 grant from Gov. Perdue on Dec. 1 to fund a feasibility study for the fiber-optic project, with Lumpkin serving as the lead applicant due to its high poverty rate.
Lumpkin County is pursuing a strategy to build a fiber-optic network to attract tech-dependent businesses and retain its trained manufacturing workforce.
Georgia Tech is contracted as a contractor for the fiber-optic infrastructure project, alongside a national consulting firm assisting with infrastructure development and recruiting programs.
North Georgia College and State University (NGCSU) is partnering in creating the workforce to support the region's tech-dependent economic shift.
NGCSU currently has an enrollment of 5,500 students and graduates between 65 to 80 computer science majors annually.
In August, the NGCSU Mike Cottrell School of Business opened with undergraduate courses in accounting, finance, management, and marketing, plus an MBA program in Forsyth County.
Mike Cottrell, whose company Cottrell Inc. is the nation’s largest manufacturer of car-haulers, donated $10 million to establish the NGCSU business school.
Dr. Max Burns was named dean of the NGCSU Mike Cottrell School of Business.
The U.S. Army has charged NGCSU with graduating 100 commissioned lieutenants per year.

New manufacturing companies relocating to or operating in Lumpkin County include RefrigiWear, The Louver Shop (shifting its manufacturing line to the county), and Polly Portables (which manufactures 15 to 20 percent of all portable toilets and chemicals).
Timken U.S. Corporation, Southern Switches, Walmart, Chestatee Regional Hospital, and The Home Depot are listed as major employers in Lumpkin County.
Lumpkin County passed a Special Purpose Local Option Sales Tax (SPLOST) last Spring to fund water and sewer treatment capacity upgrades, with revenues holding steady despite the economic downturn.
The city of Dahlonega is constructing a new water treatment plant to increase capacity for future residents.
Lumpkin County's population has grown by approximately 1,000 new residents annually since 2000, reaching 26,554 in 2007.
The county's per capita income was $25,591 in 2006, compared to the Georgia state average of $30,914.
Unemployment in Lumpkin County stood at 7.8 percent in October 2008, slightly above the Georgia state rate of 7 percent.
Former Mohawk manufacturing jobs paid approximately $10 per hour, highlighting the wage gap the county aims to address with tech and higher-paying industries.
The Dahlonega-Lumpkin County Chamber of Commerce holds an Entrepreneur Friendly Community designation, one of only 80 in the state, aimed at attracting small businesses and IT-type companies.
Tourism remains a major economic engine for Lumpkin County, with Gold Rush Days drawing a record 200,000 visitors and hotel/B&B occupancy increasing by 9 percent.
Five wineries have opened in Lumpkin County, leveraging the region's climate and land slope for grape cultivation and wedding tourism.
Liquor by the drink was passed last November in the county to support potential boutique hotel development along Hwy. 16 and Ga. 400.
source_title: Lumpkin County: From Tourism To High Tech - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Southern Switches is a division of Piedmont Automotive Products.
The company has been manufacturing quality switches, wire harnesses, and related products since 1964.
Manufacturing operations are located in Dahlonega, Georgia.
Together, Piedmont and Southern serve the Automotive, Truck, Outdoor Power, Marine, and LED Lighting Industries.

facts: Members of Atlanta’s Foreign Trade Representative Group recently visited Rome and Floyd County to observe their success in attracting foreign-based manufacturers.
The region has successfully attracted foreign-based manufacturers producing a variety of products, including all-terrain vehicles and auto parts.
The region's economic development success is attributed to a commitment by city, county, and corporate officials to bring technology-based manufacturers to the area.
