source_title: GEDA August Newsletter - Georgia Economic Developers Association

facts: Georgia Tech and Hyundai Motor Group signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on research and applications to support the future of sustainable mobility.
The collaboration includes developing a hydrogen economy, workforce development, and making cities smarter and more sustainable.
Hyundai Motor Group is investing $12.6 billion in Georgia's new dedicated electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facilities.
The investment includes the $7.59 billion Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) site located in Bryan County, Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group claims the HMGMA site will create more than 8,500 direct jobs.
The group is also investing $5 billion in a battery manufacturing facility joint venture with SK On in Bartow County, Georgia.
The SK On joint venture facility is expected to employ around 3,500 people.
Proximity to Georgia Tech was cited as one of the important considerations for selecting the locations of these facilities.
Hyundai's collaboration with Georgia Tech builds upon its long history in Georgia.
Kia Georgia's West Point assembly plant began vehicle production in 2009.
University System of Georgia Chancellor Sonny Perdue participated in the HMG-Georgia Tech MOU ceremony.
Sonny Perdue was the Governor of Georgia at the time of the Kia Georgia plant's opening.
A recent study by the Center for Automotive Research reveals that Hyundai Motor's automotive operations contributed $20.1 billion and 190,000 jobs to the US economy in 2021 alone.
According to the study, Hyundai's investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 jobs.
The study projects that Hyundai's investments in Georgia will generate $4.6 billion in annual earnings.
Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera stated that Hyundai is a global brand synonymous with quality, innovation, and a commitment to advancing technology to make a positive difference in the world.
Cabrera noted that as they have gotten to know each other, the alignment of their values has become obvious.

Jay Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, expressed thrill at creating a partnership with Georgia Tech covering research, sustainable mobility, hydrogen economy, workforce development, and smart cities.
Chang stated that today is the beginning of a partnership that will last for decades.
Chang cited this partnership as one of the reasons Hyundai chose Georgia for its EV investments.

José Muñoz, President and Global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and President and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, said Hyundai's investments in Georgia will create tens of thousands of jobs and bring tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades to come.
Muñoz stated that proximity to institutions like Georgia Tech was one of the many reasons Hyundai selected Georgia for its new EV manufacturing facility.
Hyundai Motor Group is described as a global enterprise that has created a value chain based on mobility, steel and construction, logistics, finance, IT, and service.
source_title: Hyundai, Georgia Tech To Invest $12.6bn To Boost Electric Mobility

facts: Georgia Commissioner of Economic Development Pat Wilson expressed disappointment with Rivian's decision to delay building a new plant east of Atlanta but said the company remains committed to Georgia.

Pat Wilson told members of a state Senate budget subcommittee that "It stings," and noted that Rivian has said they will live up to all their promises and commitments, calling it an active project until heard otherwise.
Rivian executives announced last Thursday a pause in the company’s plans to invest $5 billion in an EV plant near Covington that would create 7,500 jobs.
Instead of building in Georgia, Rivian will manufacture its new R2 midsize SUV model at an existing plant in Normal, Ill.

To land the deal, the state and the local development authority offered Rivian $1.5 billion in incentives including tax credits, a 25-year no-cost lease, and $198.1 million in site and road improvements on 1,978 acres.
The state and the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton Counties have spent $141 million to acquire the property, prepare the site and drain wetlands.
Rivian has made two payments in lieu of taxes (PILOT) to the development authority totaling $3 million.
Sixty percent of those PILOT payments will go to local school systems.
Pat Wilson stated that the payments don’t stop because they’re paused, and the community will receive a benefit even though there’s no active construction.
Under an agreement Rivian signed off on in 2022, the company must fulfill at least 80% of its investment and jobs commitments by the end of 2030.
Rivian has committed to maintaining those timelines.
Several senators expressed discomfort with letting the site sit empty potentially until the end of 2030 waiting for Rivian to fulfill its part of the deal.
Pat Wilson expects Rivian’s intentions for the site likely will become clear well before 2030.

If Rivian doesn’t come through on the project, Wilson said the site is attractive enough that it won’t be hard to find another manufacturing tenant, stating, "We would have no trouble at all filling that spot."
Sen. Jason Anavitarte, R-Dallas, chairman of the Senate Republican Caucus, noted there is a distinct difference between a pause and a pullout.
Senate Majority Whip Randy Robertson, R-Cataula, expressed less hope, saying he would be surprised if Rivian comes back to Georgia and starts making good on their promises.
source_title: Senators question economic development chief on delayed EV plant | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: HMGMA announced that its second production vehicle will be the all-electric 2026 IONIQ 9 three-row SUV.
The IONIQ 9 will be the first all-new Hyundai model assembled at the Metaplant.
In June, Metaplant authorities announced that the first production vehicle would be the 2025 IONIQ 5, an updated version of the award-winning EV.
HMGMA is starting to build the all-new IONIQ 9 at the Metaplant, expanding the locally manufactured IONIQ lineup.
HMGMA is the only facility building IONIQ 9s in North America.
Oscar Kwon serves as the CEO of HMGMA.
The IONIQ 9 was revealed today at AutoMobility LA 2024 in Los Angeles and simultaneously unveiled by HMGMA at AutoNation Hyundai Savannah.
Meta Pros who assembled the preproduction vehicle on display were present for the unveiling.
The IONIQ 9 features a 12.3-inch panoramic curved touch display and available first- and second-row Relaxation Seats.
The IONIQ 9 includes an impressive array of standard Advanced Driver Assistant Systems.
The 2026 IONIQ 9 targets 300+ miles of range for all trims and features a standard NACS port capable of ultra-fast 800V charging, charging from 10 to 80% in just 24 minutes with a 350-kW DC charger.
Production of the 2026 IONIQ 9 starts in Q1 2025, and the SUV will go on sale in Q2.
The IONIQ 9 is expected to qualify for the full $7,500 federal tax credit thanks to U.S. assembly and battery sourcing.
The Metaplant will produce Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia electrified vehicles.
Additional vehicle models will continue to be announced as the Metaplant ramps up to full production status.
source_title: HMGMA Reveals 2026 IONIQ 9 as Newest Production Vehicle - HMGMA

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture (Metaplant America)
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture (Metaplant America)
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will invest an additional $2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County.
This expansion raises the joint venture’s total investment value to more than $4.3 billion.
The expansion will create another 400 new jobs.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai’s Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Georgia is continuing to make itself the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that these major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure, and more.
Hyundai Motor Group thanked LGES for recognizing Georgia as the No. 1 state for business.
The incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.

In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) onsite battery cell manufacturing joint venture.
With this announcement, the EV manufacturing facility and the battery joint venture represents an estimated $7.59 billion in investment.
The facility will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.

Dong-Myung Kim, president and head of the Advanced Automotive Battery Division of LG Energy Solution, stated the investment underscores dedication to driving America’s EV transition while bolstering the local economy through the creation of quality jobs.
Dong-Myung Kim thanked the State of Georgia for support to bring top-quality products and operational experience to grow with its communities.
The facility will have a capacity of 30 GWh.
The facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant.
Hyundai Mobis will supply the assembled battery packs to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
The announcement brings 400 additional well-paying jobs for those in the Savannah region.

Carter Infinger, Chairman of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, stated the continued investment by Hyundai Motor Group and announced suppliers like LGES is truly remarkable and will be transformative for the region.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA), Georgia Power, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start on this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the United States.
Pat Wilson noted Georgia continues to add to industry-leading success by building an ecosystem that supports the entire EV supply chain, bringing jobs home to Georgians in every corner of the state.

Pat Wilson credited state and community partners like the Savannah-area JDA, utility providers, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start for creating a collaborative, business-friendly environment.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $25 billion in investments and the creation of over 30,000 jobs.
Battery related projects have accounted for at least $11 billion in investment.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Invest Additional $2B in Bryan County - HMGMA
